### [SearchWP vs Relevanssi: Which is the Best WordPress Search Plugin?](https://searchwp.com/searchwp-vs-relevanssi-which-is-the-best-wordpress-search-plugin/)

**Published:** March 12, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Out of the box, WordPress search is notoriously basic. For sites relying on complex data structures, custom taxonomies, and advanced custom fields, relying on the native search query is a massive bottleneck.

When it comes to replacing it, two heavyweights dominate the conversation: **SearchWP** and **Relevanssi**.

Both plugins promise to completely overhaul your search experience, digging deep into your custom post types and delivering the highly accurate results your users expect.

But under the hood, they take very different approaches to indexing, algorithm control, and performance.

In this article, weâll compare them to help you find the right fit for your site and give your visitors the best search experience.

Hereâs a quick look at SearchWP vs Relevanssi, so you can pick the best solution:

Category[SearchWP ](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")â­Relevanssi**Core Features**Theme Integrationâ Supports all themes & page buildersâ Limited compatibility with some themesCustom Fields & PDF Indexingâ Easy setupâ PDFs require extra configurationFuzzy Search & Voice Searchâ Searches for partial matches for typos.â Not availableWooCommerce Integrationâ Seamless, automatic integrationâ Not available**Ease of Use**Setup & Interfaceâ Setup wizard, beginner-friendlyâ No wizard. Better suited for advanced users**Customer Support**Support ChannelsTicket system, live chat, and docsContact form + WordPress.org forum**Pricing**Free Versionâ No free versionâ AvailableStarting Price$99/yr â 1 site$119.61/yr â unlimited sites## Overview

To begin with, letâs take a quick overview of 2 WordPress search plugins weâre comparing today.

### SearchWP

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the most powerful and easiest to use WordPress search plugin on the market.

It comes with tons of useful features and allows you to get complete control over how search works on your site.

Using it, you can [create custom search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), impact search results order, [track searches of your visitors](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"), and more.

This plugin also has a huge extension base, extending its search functionality. You can install extensions right from your WordPress dashboard.

**More than 50,000 WordPress site owners** already use SearchWP to give their visitors the most relevant search results.

Hereâs what they say about their experience with this plugin:

![SearchWP reviews](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-reviews-1030x612.png)Given the number of features and reviews of satisfied users, SearchWP deservedly holds the title of the **\#1 WordPress search plugin**.

### Relevanssi

![Relevanssi official website](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-official-website.png)[Relevanssi](https://www.relevanssi.com/ "Relevanssi") is another WordPress search plugin that helps you to make searches on your site better.

For example, like SearchWP, it allows you to make searchable PDF files, custom fields, custom post types, and more.

Relevanssi has 100,000+ active installations and comes in free and paid versions that differ in functionality.

Even though this plugin isnât as powerful as SearchWP, itâs still a good option if you want to make search on your WordPress site a bit smarter.

## Features

Now that you know both plugins better, letâs take a closer look at some of their features.

### 1. SearchWP

SearchWP has many features that you can use to deliver more relevant search results to your siteâs audience.

#### Automatic Theme Integration

After you install SearchWP, it automatically integrates with your existing search forms and search results template.

Thatâs super convenient and ensures instant on-site search improvement.

The best thing is that there is no difference between using a custom theme or one of the default WordPress themes.

You donât have to write a single line of code to replace your current search fields with new smart ones. SearchWP perfectly works with any theme out-of-the-box.

Besides that, it supports some of the most popular website theme builders like Elementor, Divi, Oxygen, WPBakery, and more.

You get dedicated blocks or elements for these landing page builders, which makes it super easy to display your search form anywhere on your site.

![Add SearchWP form block in Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-form-block-in-elementor-1-1030x679.png)#### Multiple Search Engines and Custom Search Forms

Using SearchWP, you can control how search works on your site and manage specific search fieldsâ behavior.

Thatâs possible because SearchWP allows you to add and use multiple search engines on your WordPress site.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)You can fully customize a search engine to meet your needs and then link it to a specific search field on your site.

For instance, SearchWP lets you add multiple sources to the engine. You can include products, documents, downloads, and other sources in the search process.

![Only select product as source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/only-select-product-as-source-1030x500.png)Besides that, you can [limit a search field to specific post types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/), adjust the relevance weight of search attributes, and more.

This SearchWP feature can help your visitors find the content theyâre looking for faster.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Selecting attributes in the search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/set-attribute-relevance-1030x542.jpg)#### Make Anything on Your Site Searchable

By default, WordPress ignores a lot of valuable information when performing a search.

For example, it doesnât consider any data within custom fields. Thatâs a huge disadvantage, as data inside these fields can make it much easier to find the right content.

Using SearchWP, you can [make custom fields searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") on your site in a few clicks.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)In addition, the native WordPress search engine doesnât index text within [PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") and office documents.

With SearchWP, you can include these files in search results on your site by ticking a couple of checkboxes.

![Making PDF and documents file searchable in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/selecting-media-source-as-document-pdf.jpg)This way, visitors can find documents that you upload to your site using a search bar.

#### **Voice Search**

With SearchWP, you can now take the search experience to the next level using its Voice Search feature.

Simply enable the option in your search forms, and the microphone icon will appear in the search bar.

This allows your visitors to speak the search query, and it will automatically fill in the search bar.

![Enable voice search option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-voice-search-option-1030x492.png)#### Search Results Order Customization

Would you like to change the order of the search results on your site? WordPress doesnât have such a feature by default.

But with SearchWP, you can put any page at the top of the search results on your site with a couple of clicks.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Configure Custom Results Order: Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step5-1030x556.jpg)Plus, SearchWP allows you to easily exclude any redundant pages from search results on your site.

All you need to do is open a page youâd like to hide from search results and then tick the âcorresponding âExclude from SearchWPâ checkbox.

![Check exclude from search checkbox](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-specific-post-1030x984.jpg)This way, you can clean up your search results and help visitors navigate your site more easily.

#### Fuzzy Search

The default WordPress search engine only searches for exact matches.

If your visitors make a typo, there is a great chance that they wonât find anything relevant on your site.

Thankfully, you can solve this problem by enabling the [fuzzy search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/) feature in the SearchWP settings.

![Enable partial matches and closest match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-matches-and-closest-match-1030x573.png)WordPress searches for partial matches when search terms show no results when you turn on this feature.

#### WooCommerce Integration

Are you an eCommerce site owner? Then there is great news for you.

SearchWP has seamless integration with WooCommerce, the most popular WordPress eCommerce plugin.

Using SearchWP, you can [make WordPress search for product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), start considering product tags, categories, and more.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)The best thing is that you donât need to take any additional steps. SearchWP automatically integrates with your online shop after its installation.

### 2. Relevanssi

Relevanssi also has some nice features that can help you to power up search on your WordPress website.

#### Automatic Theme Integration

First, this plugin has automatic theme integration, just like SearchWP.

But there is one notable difference. Relevanssi has either limited compatibility or doesnât work at all with some popular WordPress themes and plugins.

![Relevanssi doesn't support some themes and plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-doesnt-support-some-themes-and-plugins.png)#### Better Control Over Search Results

Like SearchWP, Relevanssi allows you to enrich search results on your site.

First, you can control what types of content WordPress indexes and shows in search results with this plugin.

![choose type content your want WordPress to index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-type-content-your-want-WordPress-to-index.png)Also, with Relevanssi, you can adjust the weight of relevancy multipliers.

This way, you can control what parts of posts or pages WordPress will prioritize when performing a search.

![adjust the relevance weight in Relevanssi](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adjust-the-relevance-weight-in-Relevanssi.png)For example, it might be a title, content, excerpt, or something else.

In addition, using Relevanssi, you can exclude specific categories or posts from search results.

![exclude a category from search with Relevanssi](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-a-category-from-search-with-Relevanssi.png)But thatâs not as easy and quick to do as with SearchWP. Since Relevanssi divides the indexing and searching options into 2 different tabs, this may seem confusing to WordPress newbies.

In general, Relevanssi offers all the basic features for search control, but lacks the advanced features youâd find in SearchWP.

#### Multisite Support

You can install Relevanssi as a network plugin if you have multiple sites.

This way, you can combine your sites into one network and perform searches across them at once.

Itâll save time for your visitors and make it easier for them to find what theyâre looking for since they wonât have to switch between your sites.

![Relevanssi has the multisite mode](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-has-the-multisite-mode.png)Sadly, the network mode has several disadvantages. First, some Relevanssi features wonât work in multisite mode, such as filtering methods.

In addition, if your network contains too many sites, there is a good chance that youâll face performance issues.

#### PDFs indexing

Just like SearchWP, Relevanssi allows you to make PDF files searchable in WordPress.

![enable PDF indexing in Relevanssi](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-PDF-indexing-in-Relevanssi.png)But, there are some plugin restrictions. For example, Relevanssi canât see PDF files that donât contain any text.

So if you upload PDFs that consist only of images, they wonât be searchable on your site.

Plus, Relevanssi considers PDF files as post attachments. It means that to make them searchable, you first need to enable attachment indexing.

![Relevanssi indexing options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-indexing-options.png)Itâs not easy to figure out, not to mention that this option is turned off by default.

Thatâs confusing for beginners and makes the process of setting up PDF indexing with Relevanssi a little bit more complicated.

#### bbPress Integration

Do you have a forum on your site? If yes, then Relevanssi works with [bbPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-bbpress-forum-topics-replies-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include bbPress Forum Topics & Replies in WordPress Search"), the popular forum software from the WordPress creators.

![Relevanssi has the bbPress integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-has-the-bbPress-integration.png)With Relevanssi, you can make WordPress index your forum messages. This way, your visitors can find them using a search.

That makes it much easier to explore your forum and helps visitors find the information they need faster.

#### Search Tracking

Like SearchWP, Relevanssi allows you to track search queries of your visitors.

Sadly, this feature has several major drawbacks.

First, you may have a hard time figuring out how to turn it on because of Relevanssiâs cluttered interface.

![enable logging search queries of users](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-logging-search-queries-of-users.png)Secondly, the tab where you can see the collected search queries is located outside the Relevanssi interface.

Itâs confusing and can cause problems for newbies.

![Relevanssi user searches](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-user-searches.png)And finally, this plugin gives you a rather limited amount of information.

You can only see the list of search queries and the number of searches and hits.

![the list of common queries](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-list-of-common-queries.png)On the other hand, the SearchWP metrics extension allows you to start tracking visitorsâ searches immediately after installation.

It also gives you additional useful information such as total results viewed, number of searches per user, and more.

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)## Ease of Use

One of the most important things for every plugin is how easy it is to set up and use. Letâs take a look at the comparison of SearchWP and Relevanssi in this field.

### SearchWP

Customizing the way search works on your site is super easy with SearchWP.

This plugin comes with a beginner-friendly and clean user interface that you can access using your WordPress dashboard.

So, you can effortlessly set up SearchWP for your needs, even if youâre not a tech-savvy person. The best part is that it comes with a setup wizard that guide every step of the way after installation.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Besides that, SearchWP makes it super simple to customize the search engine on your site.

Here you can manage your default search engines or add new ones with SearchWP and customize them.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Configuring Search Engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-engine-setup-hr-1030x573.jpg)The process of search engine customization is intuitive and straightforward.

And if you come across any difficulties, you can check out the comprehensive [SearchWP knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/ "SearchWP Knowledge Base") and [blog section](https://searchwp.com/blog/ "SearchWP Blog") for step-by-step guides.

Plus, on the **Extensions** page, you can find useful SearchWP extensions that further expand its functionality.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)For instance, you can install the [Metrics extension](https://searchwp.com/search-metrics/ "Search Metrics") to track your visitorsâ searches or add a search box to your post with the Shortcodes extension.

The best part is that you can install extensions with a single click without leaving the WordPress dashboard.

SearchWP successfully combines powerful features with an easy-to-use user interface, making it suitable for both experts and newbies.

### Relevanssi

Unlike SearchWP, the Relevanssi dashboard looks overloaded, and it might be challenging to figure out how to use the plugin.

You can find a brief âGetting startedâ guide on the **Overview** tab of the plugin dashboard.

But to follow it, you need to understand how search works in WordPress, which isnât suitable for beginners.

While testing, we found that you wonât find a setup wizard to help you configure the plugin on your site.

![Relevanssi overview tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/relevanssi-overview-tab-1030x500.png)Even after you go through the initial plugin setup, the following process of its customization doesnât get any easier.

The dashboardâs number of tabs and checkboxes can be overwhelming even for experienced users.

![Relevanssi excerpts tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Relevanssi-excerpts-tab.png)Plus, Relevanssi doesnât have its UI or user interface, which may discourage you from exploring the pluginâs features.

These facts make Relevenssiâs dashboard more suitable for WordPress techies than beginners.

## Customer Support

Another important thing to consider when choosing a WordPress search plugin is its customer support service.

Having dependable customer support is crucial because theyâre dedicated to helping you whenever you need help using the plugin.

Letâs compare the customer support of SearchWP and Relevanssi plugins.

### SearchWP

SearchWP has an outstanding support team thatâs always ready to help you with any issues.

They use a ticket system, and you can create a new ticket without even leaving your WordPress dashboard.

Simply go to the **Tools** page and then switch to the âSupportâ tab.

![Support options inside SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/support-options-inside-searchwp-1030x541.png)You can also find help by going to your Account area on the SearchWP website.

Go ahead and switch to the âSupportâ tab, and youâll find a chat interface to ask questions.

![SearchWP support option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-support-option-1030x629.png)If youâre a more experienced WordPress user, you can also check out the [SearchWP documentation](https://searchwp.com/documentation/ "SearchWP Documentation") section.

There is also a dedicated [SearchWP blog page](https://searchwp.com/blog/ "SearchWP Blog Section") where youâll find many helpful tutorials and step-by-step guides.

### Relevanssi

The support options offered by Relevanssi are mainly through the support forum on WordPress.org.

You can visit the support page and start a new topic to get your queries resolved.

![Relevanssi support forum](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/relevanssi-support-forum-1030x504.png)Relevanssi doesnât have a ticket system. You can get support through the contact form on the plugin website or ask a question on the official forum.

As with SearchWP, you can check out the [Relevanssi knowledge base](https://www.relevanssi.com/category/knowledge-base/ "Relevanssi Knowledge Base") to try to solve your issue yourself.

## Pricing

Good value for money is one of the main factors when choosing a plugin. Letâs see the pricing packages that SearchWP and Relevanssi offer.

### SearchWP

SearchWP includes 3 pricing plans: Standard, Pro, and All Access Bundle.

![SearchWP pricing plans](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-pricing-plans-1-1030x683.png)Letâs take a closer look at them:

- **Standard**: $99 per year â includes a license for 1 site, all standard features, extensions, and support.
- **Pro**: $199 per year â includes a license for 3 sites, all standard features, extensions, and support. It also comes with eCommerce and advanced integrations, such as WooCommerce Integration, Metrics, Related Content, Redirects, and Custom Results Order.
- **All Access Bundle**: $399 per year â includes a license for an unlimited number of sites, all standard and pro features, extensions, and support.

SearchWP has a 14-day full refund policy, so you can get 100% of your money back if you believe itâs not a good fit. Youâre also free to change plans or cancel your account if you need to.

### Relevanssi

Relevanssi comes in 2 versions.

There is a free version that you can download from the official WordPress plugin repository.

It has fewer features and limited support â you can still use it if you want to provide fast search results but with little room for customization and adjustment.

When it comes to the premium version, it includes 2 pricing plans:

![Relevanssi pricing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/relevanssi-pricing-1030x493.png)- **Annual**: $119.61 per year â license for unlimited sites. Includes support and upgrades for a year.
- **Permanent**: $400.71 for a lifetime license for unlimited sites. Includes lifetime upgrades and support.

Relevanssi offers a 30-day money-back guarantee, so you can get your money back within 30 days of purchase, no questions asked.

## Our Verdict

Once we compare the features, ease of use, customer support, and pricing of SearchWP and Relevanssi, itâs time to choose the winner.

Our top pick for the best WordPress search plugin is [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). This plugin offers more value for its price.

In addition to all standard features, a premium SearchWP license also gives you access to its integrations and extensions, such as Metrics, Redirects, Custom Results Order, and many others.

Plus, it has an intuitive interface and responsive customer support. Due to that, you can boost your on-site search with just a couple of clicks, even if youâre a WordPress newbie.

To do the same with Relevanssi, you would have to be an advanced WordPress user. Not to mention that it wouldâve taken much more time and needed more technical knowledge to use Relevanssi.

Over 50,000 website owners are already using SearchWP to deliver the most relevant search results to their visitors.

We hope this article helped you pick between SearchWP vs Relevanssi. You may also want to see our guides on [how to make WordPress search media files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files") and [how to add a modal search form in WordPress without editing code](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-modal-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Modal Search Form in WordPress (No Coding)").

### [How to Filter by Multiple Taxonomies in WordPress (Step by Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-by-multiple-taxonomies-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** March 10, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Managing a WordPress site with hundreds of posts or products can feel overwhelming for your visitors. They land on your page and see a massive list of content organized by categories, tags, and other filtering options.

But hereâs the problem. Most sites only let users filter by one thing at a time.

Itâs like walking into a bookstore where you can browse by genre or by author, but not both together. Your WordPress visitors face this same frustration every day.

They want to narrow down results using multiple criteria simultaneously, but the default WordPress search doesnât allow this. Thatâs where filtering by multiple taxonomies comes in.

In this article, weâll show you exactly how to filter by multiple taxonomies in WordPress.

## Why Filter by Multiple Taxonomies Matters

Before we jump into the tutorial, letâs talk about why multiple taxonomy filtering is so important for your site.

**1. Improved User Experience**

Weâve seen countless sites where visitors give up because they canât narrow down results effectively.

Imagine running a recipe blog where someone wants vegetarian dinner recipes. With single filters, they either see all vegetarian recipes (including breakfasts and desserts) or all dinner recipes (including meat dishes).

Neither option gives them what they actually want.

Multiple taxonomy filters solve this by letting users combine criteria. They can select âvegetarianâ AND âdinnerâ to see exactly what they need.

**2. Better Content Discovery**

When visitors can filter by multiple taxonomies at once, they discover content they might have otherwise missed.

A user searching your [WooCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") might want products that are both âon saleâ AND in the âelectronicsâ category.

Without combined filtering, theyâd never easily find those specific items. This means more of your content actually gets seen and used.

**3. Reduced Bounce Rates**

Nothing drives visitors away faster than forcing them to dig through irrelevant results. Weâve watched analytics on sites before and after [adding multiple filters](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Set Up Filters in WordPress Without Coding"), and the difference is dramatic.

Users stay on pages longer because they quickly find what theyâre looking for instead of clicking away in frustration.

**4. Professional Appearance**

Sites with advanced filtering capabilities simply look more professional and trustworthy.

When visitors see that they can refine searches using multiple criteria, it signals that youâve thought about their needs.

It shows youâre running a well-organized site that respects their time.

Now, letâs look at how you can create filters in WordPress.

## Setting Up Multiple Taxonomy Filters in WordPress

The easiest way to add multiple taxonomy filters to your WordPress site is by using [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)It is the [best WordPress filtering plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-filter-plugins/ "Best WordPress Filter Plugins (Expert Review)") that makes creating advanced filters simple, even if youâve never done this before.

Created by the team behind SearchWP, WPFilters gives you the power to add professional filtering without writing a single line of code.

The plugin works seamlessly with all WordPress taxonomies, including categories, tags, and any custom taxonomies youâve added to your site.

You can also filter blog posts, WooCommerce products, custom post types, and more.

Hereâs what you can do with WPFilters:

- **Stack Multiple Filters**: Add as many taxonomy filters as you need, and they all work together automatically.
- **Choose Filter Styles**: Pick from checkboxes, dropdowns, radio buttons, [range sliders](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-range-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Range Filters in WordPress (Easy Way)"), or search fields for each taxonomy.
- **Custom Taxonomies Support**: Works with any taxonomy, whether itâs built into WordPress or created by a plugin.
- **Responsive Design**: All filters look great and function perfectly on mobile devices.
- **Shareable URLs**: Each filtered combination creates a unique URL visitors can bookmark or share.

With that, letâs see how you can set up multiple taxonomy filters on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a WPFilters account.

Once youâve created an account, simply head to your account area and go to the âDownloadsâ tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)From here, youâll need to click the **Download WPFilters** button and save the plugin files on your computer.

We also recommend copying the License Key while youâre on this page, as youâll need it during setup.

Next, you can upload the WPFilters plugin files to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

After activating the plugin, you will see the WPFilters setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)### Step 2: Create Your First Taxonomy Filter

Now that WPFilters is installed, you can start building your first taxonomy filter.

To get started, head to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-filter-1030x529.png)On the next screen, youâll need to give your filter a clear name at the top. For example, if youâre creating a category filter, you might call it âFilter by Category.â

This name is just for your reference, so pick something that helps you stay organized when you add more filters later.

Next, scroll down to the Filter Fields section and select the filter field you want to use.

For this tutorial, weâll use the âCheckboxâ option because it lets visitors select multiple options at once.

![Choose a filter field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-filter-field-1030x600.png)Now, scroll down to the Data Source section and click the dropdown menu. Hereâs where you tell WPFilters which [taxonomy](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce") to use for this filter.

For example, if you want to filter by categories, select âCategoriesâ from the list.

![Choose a data source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-data-source-1030x623.png)After selecting your data source, you can customize how the filter looks by scrolling to the Container section.

Here, you can enable the âShow Titleâ option so visitors know what theyâre filtering by. You can also make the filter collapsible to save space on your page.

![Edit filter container settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-brand-filter-container-settings-1030x494.png)Next, scroll down to the Items section where you can fine-tune how individual filter options appear.

We recommend enabling âItem Countâ so users can see how many posts match each option.

![Edit items settings for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-items-settings-for-filters-1030x456.png)When youâre satisfied with your settings, scroll to the top and click the **Save** button.

### Step 3: Create Additional Taxonomy Filters

Now that youâve created your first filter, itâs time to add more taxonomy filters that will work together with it.

The great thing about WPFilters is that multiple filters automatically work in combination.

When a visitor selects an option from one filter and then selects from another, the results narrow down to match both criteria.

For example, you can [create a tags filter](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-blog-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Filter Blog Posts by Tags in WordPress (4 Simple Steps)"), so users can go through multiple tags and use them in combination with the categories filter.

![Select source for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-source-for-filters-1030x582.png)To learn more, please see our guides on [how to filter WooCommerce products by tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-tags/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Tags (Step-by-Step)").

### Step 4: Display Multiple Filters on Your Site

Now that youâve created multiple taxonomy filters, itâs time to add them to your WordPress site so visitors can actually use them.

The best part about WPFilters is that you can place all your filters anywhere on your site using simple embed options.

Or you can use the Gutenberg block or [shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search") to display the filters on a page or in the sidebar.

For this tutorial, weâll add the filters to the sidebar where theyâre always visible and easy to access. This is a great way to keep filters available to users as they browse through your content.

To start, head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard.

Note: The steps might vary slightly depending on the WordPress theme youâre using. Weâre using the Astra theme for this tutorial.

Once youâre on the Widgets page, scroll down to your sidebar section (in our case, shop sidebar) and click the â+â button to add a new block.

![Add WPFilters element to shop sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-to-shop-sidebar-1-1030x617.png)Next, you can search for the âWPFilters Elementâ widget block and add it to your sidebar.

From the settings panel on the right, you will need to click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and choose your first taxonomy filter.

![Choose your taxonomy filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-taxonomy-filters-1030x574.png)Now, you will need to repeat these steps and click the â+â button again to add another WPFilters Element block right below the first one.

This time, select your second taxonomy filter from the dropdown menu in the settings panel.

You can continue adding as many WPFilters Element blocks as you have taxonomy filters. Stack them in your sidebar, and theyâll all work together automatically.

When a visitor selects an option from one filter and then selects from another, WPFilters combines both criteria to show only matching results.

When youâre done adding all your filters, click the **Update** button at the top to save your changes.

### Step 5: Test Your Multiple Taxonomy Filters

After adding the filters to your sidebar, itâs time to test them and make sure theyâre working correctly together.

Go ahead and visit your website where you added the filters. You should see all your taxonomy filters stacked in the sidebar.

![View multiple taxonomy filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-multiple-taxonomy-filters-1030x538.png)Now, letâs test how the filters work together by selecting options from multiple filters.

For example, start by [selecting a category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce") from your first filter. Youâll see the content on your page automatically update to show only posts from that category.

Next, select an option from your second filter, like a specific tag. The results will narrow down even further to show only content that matches both the category AND the tag you selected.

![Test multiple taxonomy filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-multiple-taxonomy-filters-1030x526.png)As you can see, both filters are working together perfectly. The content updates in real-time without requiring a page refresh, and only posts that match all your selected criteria appear.

You can continue selecting options from additional filters if you added more than two. Each selection further refines the results, giving your visitors precise control over what they see.

To reset the filters and see all content again, simply deselect the options or refresh the page. Or you can [add a reset filter button](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-reset-filter-button-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Reset Filter Button in WordPress (No Coding)") to your website.

Thatâs it! Youâve successfully set up multiple taxonomy filters on your WordPress site. Your visitors can now combine different criteria to find exactly what theyâre looking for without any coding required.

## FAQs About Filtering by Multiple Taxonomies

Here are some common questions we get about setting up multiple taxonomy filters in WordPress.

**1. Can I add more than two taxonomy filters?**

Absolutely. You can add as many taxonomy filters as you need on your site. Weâve seen sites with five or six different filters all working together perfectly.

Just keep in mind that too many filters can overwhelm visitors, so add only the ones that truly help people find content faster.

**2. Do the filters work with custom taxonomies?**

Yes, they do. WPFilters works with any taxonomy in WordPress, whether itâs built-in like categories and tags or custom taxonomies created by plugins like Advanced Custom Fields or Custom Post Type UI.

Simply select your custom taxonomy from the Data Source dropdown when creating a filter element.

**3. Will this slow down my site?**

Not at all. WPFilters is built by the same team behind SearchWP, so itâs optimized for performance. The filters use efficient queries that donât bog down your database.

Weâve tested it on sites with thousands of posts, and the filtering remains fast and responsive.

**4. Can I use different filter types for each taxonomy?**

Yes, you can mix and match filter types. For example, you might use checkboxes for categories, a dropdown for tags, and a search field for authors.

This flexibility lets you design the filtering experience that works best for your specific content and audience.

Adding multiple taxonomy filters transforms how visitors navigate your WordPress site. Instead of forcing them to scroll through endless pages or settle for broad search results, you give them precise control over what they see.

Weâve shown you how WPFilters makes this process simple, even if youâve never set up filtering before. By stacking multiple filters in your sidebar, you create a professional browsing experience that keeps visitors engaged and helps them find exactly what they need.

The best part is that all your filters work together automatically. No complex configuration or coding required.

We hope this article helped you learn how to filter by multiple taxonomies in WordPress. You may also want to see our guides on[ how to filter WooCommerce products by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-category/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Category (Step-by-Step)") and [how to optimize the WordPress search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-optimize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How To Optimize The WordPress Search Results Page").

Ready to give your visitors powerful filtering options? You can [get started with WPFilters here](https://searchwp.com/buy-wpfilters/ "Get started with WPFilters Today").

### [How To Find The Most Searched Product On Your Website](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/)

**Published:** March 5, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Do you want to learn how to find the most searched product on your website?

Imagine the journey of an online fashion retailer who checked their websiteâs search info and found lots of people looking for vintage-style dresses.

It was a clear signal from their audience. So, the retailer quickly increased their vintage dress inventory, showcased these finds on their homepage, and launched targeted campaigns.

The result? The sales went up, customers were happier, and they became the top spot for vintage fashion.

You can do the same for your website. By learning what your visitors are searching for, you can better decide what to sell, plan your marketing, and improve the overall shopping experience.

In this step-by-step tutorial, we will show you how to find the most searched product on your website.

## Why Track Product Searches on Your Site?

Before we get to the tutorial, letâs talk about how tracking product searches can transform your business.

**1. Stop Guessing What Customers Want**

Running an online store without search data is like flying blind. You make decisions based on hunches instead of facts.

Weâve seen store owners spend thousands on inventory they think will sell, only to watch it sit untouched. Meanwhile, customers search for products that arenât even in stock.

When you track searches, you see exactly what people type into your search bar. This raw data shows their genuine intent before they give up and leave your site.

**2. Improve Your Product Inventory**

Search data tells you which products deserve more attention. Maybe customers keep searching for âblue running shoes,â but you only stock them in black.

Thatâs a clear signal to adjust your inventory. Without this data, youâd never know why potential customers leave your site.

**3. Create Better Marketing Campaigns**

Your most searched products should be your most promoted products. Itâs that simple.

If âorganic dog foodâ gets searched fifty times a week, that topic deserves a featured spot on your homepage. You can also create targeted email campaigns around these popular items.

Search data removes the mystery from content creation. Youâll know exactly which blog posts to write and which products to highlight in your newsletters.

**4. Boost Sales with Data-Driven Decisions**

Every search represents a potential sale. When you track these searches, you can optimize your entire store around what actually sells.

Youâll spot trends before your competitors do. Maybe searches for âwaterproof jacketsâ spike every October, giving you time to stock up before the rush.

This information helps you price products correctly, create better product descriptions, and even improve your site navigation. All of these changes lead directly to more sales.

Now, letâs see how you can find the most searched products on your site.

## Easiest Way To Find The Most Searched Product On Your Website

Link these engines to [specific forms](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Advanced Search Forms in WordPress (Easily)"), making it easier for users to find exactly what they need where they expect to find it.
- **Product Tag Searches**: Simplify the shopping journey for your customers by enabling searches based on [product tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search by Product Tags in WooCommerce (Fastest Way)"). - **Search Results Control**: Take control of the order in which search results appear. This allows you to prioritize certain products or pages in search results, helping you guide customer focus and enhance page views.
- **Keyword Stemming**: SearchWP employs [keyword stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/keyword-stemming/ "Keyword Stemming"), which means it looks for terms similar to the ones you entered. For example, if you search for ârun,â youâll also see results for ârunningâ and ârunner.â
- **Exclude specific products:** Hide out-of-stock or discontinued items from search results to avoid disappointing customers.

Now, youâll need to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site and install the plugin. Check out the [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/) tutorial if you need help with that.

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)Products topping this list are the most valuable products that your audience is really interested in.

## Turning Insights Into Action

Knowing what your customers are searching for is like reading their minds. Itâs not just about satisfying curiosity; itâs about leveraging data to make informed decisions that drive sales, enhance user experience, and streamline your inventory management.

Here are a few proven strategies to help you turn insights into actions.

#### 1. Spotlight On Popular Products

#### 2. Inventory Insights

#### 3. Tailored Marketing Campaigns

Use the exact search terms from SearchWP as keywords in your advertising campaigns, social media posts, and email marketing efforts to increase engagement and click-through rates.

### 4. Enhanced User Experience

#### 5. New Product Development

### [How To Optimize The WordPress Search Results Page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-optimize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/)

**Published:** March 3, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

The search results page on your WordPress site determines whether visitors find what they need or leave frustrated.

When someone types a query into your search bar, they have a specific goal in mind. They want answers, products, or information that matches their needs. The search results page is where you either deliver on that expectation or fall short.

Most WordPress sites use the default search results template that comes with their theme. These templates are bare-bones at best. They show plain text links with no images, limited, and zero visual appeal.

In this article, weâll show you how to optimize the WordPress search results page using SearchWP.

## Why Optimize The WordPress Search Results Page For Your Website?

Just as a fisherman prefers a well-stocked pond where he can catch fish quickly over the vast, overwhelming ocean, website visitors appreciate a well-optimized search page.

It efficiently guides them to what they are looking for rather than sifting through an ocean of irrelevant content.

The default WordPress search has its limitations. It is clunky and often returns irrelevant results or no results at all. It is also slow and impacts your websiteâs speed, especially during peak usage.

Plus, the default WordPress engine canât handle custom post types, [eCommerce products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), document content, or taxonomies.

Therefore, optimizing the WordPress search results page on your website is essential.

If it does not present relevant information in an organized manner, your visitors may leave your site disappointed, reducing engagement and satisfaction. As a result, hurting your business in the process.

Letâs look at how you can easily optimize your search results page.

## How to Configure the WordPress Search Results Page

To overcome the limitations of the default WordPress search, you need to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"), the best WordPress search plugin.

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It comes with Search Results Templates that let you create beautiful, customized results pages without touching any code.

And over 50,000 WordPress site owners use SearchWP to deliver better search experiences to their visitors.

Here are some other things you can do with SearchWP:

- **Customize search sources:** Choose exactly which content types appear in search results, from posts and pages to custom post types and [WooCommerce products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)").
- M**ake everything searchable:** Include custom fields, [PDF ](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)")content, taxonomies, and other data that WordPress normally ignores.
- **Track visitor searches:** See [what people are searching for](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") on your site so you can identify content gaps and improve your offerings.
- **Enable live search:** Show instant results as visitors type, making it faster to find content.
- **Voice Search:** Enable the voice search option and allow users to speak their search queries.

We also recommend copying your license key from this page, as youâll need it during the setup process.

Now, youâll need to upload the SearchWP plugin to your WordPress site. If you need help with this step, check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activating the plugin, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen with a setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions to complete the initial setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Once the plugin is active and ready, you can go ahead and customize the search engine to include different sources in the search process.

SearchWP indexes your website in the background and replaces the default WordPress search with its powerful Engine.

To start, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. Here, you can edit the default engine or add a new one.

The plugin also lets you include multiple sources along with Posts, Pages, and Media. For instance, you can add more sources like Products, [Documents](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-documents-from-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How to Exclude Documents from Search Results in WordPress"), and Listings.

Besides that, you can also include [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), tags, and categories for each source as attributes and adjust the weights

![SearchWP engine index status](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-in-SearchWP.jpg)To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 2: Creating a Search Form For Your WordPress Website

The search results page is dependent on the search form, as it only appears after someone performs a search.

![Saving search form created with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save_search_form-1030x293.jpg)### Step 3: Customize Your Search Results Page

Do note that the final appearance may vary depending on the WordPress theme youâre using.

![Pre-designed theme layouts for search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/theme_layouts_predesigned-1030x916.jpg)SearchWP also gives you more options to customize the results page.

For more details, you can follow our guide on [how to customize the WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize the WordPress Search Results Page Easily (For Beginners)").

## Optimize The WordPress Search Results Page

You need to optimize the WordPress search results page to enhance the user experience and increase engagement on your website. Letâs explore several proven strategies to help you achieve this, all of which can easily be done using the advanced capabilities of SearchWP.

### Implement Live Search For Instant Results

One of the key features that makes Google search easy to use is its ability to perform live searches.

If you can implement something similar that shows results in real-time, it will significantly enhance the user experience on your WordPress site.

By activating Ajax search, the search results will update dynamically as the user types their query, providing instant feedback without reloading the page.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: How the live search looks finally](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Live-search-final-preview.gif)This not only makes the search process faster but also more interactive and engaging, encouraging visitors to explore your site further.

Follow this step-by-step guide to learn [how to add live autocomplete search in WordPress with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites").

### Take Control of the Order of Search Results

Customizing the order of search results allows you to prioritize more relevant or valuable content for your audience.

By strategically ordering search results, you can nudge visitors to your desired content, promotional materials, or important pages, enhancing the discoverability of key content on your WordPress site.

![Configure Custom Results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)Hereâs a detailed guide on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress using SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress").

### Exclude Irrelevant Content

Removing irrelevant content and showing only the relevant results are crucial for maintaining the search quality of your WordPress site.

By filtering out content that does not contribute to the userâs search intent, you can streamline the search process, making it easier for users to find the information they need.

Learn how to enhance the accuracy and usefulness of search results by [excluding certain pages or content types from site search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") using SearchWP.

### Show Related Content

Displaying related content alongside search results can significantly improve the user experience, offering users additional resources that are relevant to their interests.

This not only increases engagement but also provides an opportunity to showcase a wider range of content, potentially leading to increased page views and longer site visits.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: See Related Content - Final Result](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-6-1-1030x629.jpg) Learn more by following our guide on [how to show related content in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-show-related-content-in-wordpress/ "How To Show Related Content In WordPress").

While the default WordPress search results page comes with the bare minimum, itâs nowhere close to providing an optimum overall search experience for your users.

Your visitors deserve a proper search results page that helps them find what they are looking for instantly and seamlessly.

Develop a search results page that not only maintains visitor interest but also amplifies engagement, lifts your websiteâs key performance metrics, and, in turn, improves your business.

We hope this article helped you learn how to optimize the WordPress search results page. You may also want to see our guide on [how to filter brands on your WooCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-brands-on-your-woocommerce-store/ "How to Filter Brands on Your WooCommerce Store") and [how to customize your WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search").

[Get SearchWP today](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP") to optimize the WordPress search results page of your website completely.

These clean URLs can be added to your sitemap, helping search engines discover and rank your filtered product pages.

**Pricing**: Free WordPress filtering plugin.

## FAQs about WordPress Filter Plugins

**1. Do I need a filter plugin if I only have a few products?**

If you only have 10-20 products, you probably donât need a filter plugin yet. Customers can easily browse through a small catalog without feeling overwhelmed.

However, once you reach 50+ products or add multiple categories and variations, filters become helpful. They let customers narrow down options quickly instead of scrolling through pages of products they donât want.

**2. Will filter plugins slow down my website?**

Filter plugins can affect your site speed, but it depends on which one you choose and how you set it up. We tested all the plugins in this guide and found that lightweight options like HUSKY and [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters") had minimal impact on page load times.

AJAX filtering actually improves the user experience by updating results without reloading the entire page. Just make sure to use a good caching plugin and choose a filter plugin thatâs coded efficiently.

**3. Can I use filter plugins with any WordPress theme?**

Most filter plugins work with any WordPress theme, but compatibility varies. Plugins like WPFilters and [YITH WooCommerce Ajax Product Filter](https://searchwp.com/recommends/yith-woocommerce-ajax-product-filter/ "YITH WooCommerce Ajax Product Filter") work with most themes right out of the box.

Themify Product Filter works best with Themify themes, and you might need custom CSS to fix styling issues on other themes. Before committing to a plugin, check if itâs been tested with your theme or install the free version first to test compatibility.

**4. Whatâs the difference between AJAX filtering and regular filtering?**

Regular filtering reloads the entire page every time someone selects a filter option. This creates a clunky experience with loading screens between each filter selection.

AJAX filtering updates only the product results without reloading the whole page. Customers see results instantly, making the shopping experience smoother and faster. All the plugins we recommend in this guide support AJAX filtering.

**5. Do filter plugins work with WooCommerce variable products?**

Yes, most quality filter plugins handle WooCommerce variable products. They can filter by product attributes like size, color, and material that youâve set up in [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working").

Plugins like WPFilters and YITH WooCommerce Ajax Product Filter automatically detect your product variations and create filter options from them. You donât need to manually configure each variation.

### Choosing the Right WordPress Filter Plugin for Your Store

Adding filters to your WordPress site transforms how customers find products. Instead of endlessly scrolling through pages, they can narrow down exactly what they need in seconds.

After testing all these plugins, we found that [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters") offers the best balance of features and ease of use for most store owners.

The no-code block editor integration makes setup simple, and the ability to filter products, posts, and other content gives you flexibility as your site grows.

If youâre just starting out with a small budget, the free versions of YITH WooCommerce Ajax Product Filter or Filter Everything provide solid filtering without upfront costs.

You can always upgrade later when your store grows, and you need advanced features.

We hope this article helped you pick the best WordPress filter plugin for your site. ### [Introducing SearchWP Voice Search Feature for Better Experience & Accessibility](https://searchwp.com/introducing-searchwp-voice-search-feature/)

**Published:** February 24, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

We just shipped SearchWP 4.5.8, and this one comes with a feature weâve been genuinely excited to get into your hands: **Voice Search**.

As of today, your visitors can speak their search queries instead of typing them.

One tap on a mic button, a few spoken words, and theyâre looking at results. No keyboard, no typos, no friction.

Itâs a small change to the search experience that makes a surprisingly big difference, especially on mobile.

Hereâs everything thatâs new.

## Why Voice Search, Why Now?

Think about how you use your own phone. You ask it for directions. You dictate text messages. You ask questions out loud without thinking twice.

Your site visitors do the same thing, and that habit doesnât switch off when they land on your website.

They arrive expecting a fast, intuitive experience, and when the [search bar](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") only accepts typed input, thereâs an invisible gap between how they naturally want to interact and what your site actually offers.

Voice Search closes that gap.

But it goes beyond convenience and enhances accessibility for your WordPress website.

For people living with motor disabilities, arthritis, dyslexia, or other conditions that make typing difficult or painful, a voice-enabled search bar just takes the overall user experience to the next level.

Youâre making your site more welcoming to everyone. Thatâs the kind of upgrade that benefits your visitors quietly but meaningfully, and reflects well on the care youâve put into your site.

And the best part is that SearchWP makes it easy to get there without any complicated setup.

## Whatâs New with the Voice Search Feature

Hereâs exactly what your visitors will experience when you enable this feature:

- **A mic button appears next to the search field.** Clean, simple, and unobtrusive, it sits right alongside the search input, ready when your visitor needs it.
- **They tap, speak, and their words appear.** The spoken query is transcribed in real time and written directly into the search field. They can see it happening as they speak, which feels remarkably satisfying.
- **The search can trigger automatically.** If you enable the automatic form submission option, SearchWP will fire the search as soon as the final transcript comes in. This means the visitor goes from speaking to seeing results without any extra steps.

## Get Started with SearchWP Voice Search

Youâll find the Voice Search settings right inside [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). The best part is that itâs super simple to enable the option. No complicated setup required.

Simply head to the **Search Form** page in SearchWP from your WordPress dashboard. From here, scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section.

Now, click the toggle to enable the **Voice Search** option.

![Enable voice search option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-voice-search-option-1030x492.png)Youâll also find the âAutomatic Form Submissionâ right under Voice Search. This option controls whether the search fires automatically after speaking or waits for the visitor to confirm.

If you enable it, then the search form is automatically submitted after the speech recognition is complete.

Next, you can visit the search form on your website, and youâll notice the microphone icon in the search bar. Users can simply click the icon and speak the search query.

![View the voice search icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-the-voice-search-icon-1030x393.png)The voice search feature is now out!

Do note that this new feature works with browsers that support Web Speech API, which includes Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Safari.

So what are you waiting for?[ Update to the latest SearchWP version](https://searchwp.com/buy/ " Update to the latest SearchWP version"). Once youâre on 4.5.8, Voice Search will be waiting for you in the form settings inside SearchWP.

As always, we really appreciate your support in making SearchWP the best search plugin for WordPresss. If you have questions or need support, [our team is here to help](https://searchwp.com/account/support/ "Reach out to our team for support").

**Categories:** Announcements

**Tags:** SearchWP, SearchWP 4.5.8, speak your queries, speech recognition, voice search, Voice Search Feature, Web Speech API

### [How To Make WordPress Search Media Files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/)

**Published:** February 19, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâve ever uploaded important PDFs, product images, or downloadable guides to your WordPress site, you know how frustrating it is when visitors canât find them.

Weâve watched people use our search bar looking for a specific resource guide or product photo, only to get zero results. The file exists on our site, but WordPress acts like it doesnât.

This happens because the default WordPress search completely ignores your media library. It doesnât look at image filenames, PDF content, or any document youâve uploaded.

In this article, weâll show you how to make WordPress search media files.

## Why Do You Need To Make WordPress Search Media Files?

Enhancing your WordPress site to search all types of media files, such as images, videos, audio clips, and documents, transforms how visitors interact with your content.

Being able to search media files can directly impact sales. Imagine you sell electronic gadgets online.

A customer comes to your site looking for a âdigital camera.â If they canât search through images or manuals, they might miss the perfect match, especially if itâs only detailed in file attributes and not well-described on the page.

By searching across media types, you can make sure that your customers can find the right product on their first try with minimal effort.

Therefore, letâs see how to make the most common media file types searchable in WordPress:

## How To Make WordPress Search Media Files

The simplest way to make WordPress search media files is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

It is the most powerful search plugin for WordPress and will significantly enhance your websiteâs search results.

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP makes all your media files fully searchable. It indexes their titles and metadata, including alt text, captions, and descriptions.

The plugin can also search through document content and EXIF data in your images, significantly enhancing your siteâs search functionality.

Using SearchWP, you can make nearly everything on your site searchable. It also offers multiple search engines, customizable search rules, access to search statistics, and a ton of other features.

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners worldwide use this tool to outperform competitors and improve visitor search experiences.

Furthermore, here are some critical features of SearchWP that make it invaluable for online businesses:

- WooCommerce Integration: SearchWP seamlessly integrates with WooCommerce, improving search capabilities for [product media files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)") and beyond.
- PDF and Office Documents Indexing: You can make the content of [PDFs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), Office documents, and rich text documents searchable by parsing their content.
- Multiple Search Engines: You can create specialized search engines for different types of content, including a dedicated engine for media files.
- Search Behavior Analysis: [Tracks user search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site") trends on your site, providing valuable insights into customer preferences and behaviors.
- Prioritize Search Results: You can easily adjust the order of search results to [highlight specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress") or items, enhancing visibility with just a few clicks.

Now, letâs get started with setting up SearchWP so that you can make WordPress search media files.

### Step 1: Setting Up SearchWP On Your Site

First, you will need to choose a [SearchWP package](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today") that fits your needs.

After purchasing, head to the âDownloadsâ section on your Account page.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

We also recommend copying your license key from the bottom left corner of this page. Youâll need it in just a moment to activate the plugin.

Now, youâll need to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this step, check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After you activate SearchWP, youâll see the welcome screen with the setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions to complete the setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Set Up Search Engine to Make Media Files Searchable

SearchWP automatically replaces the default WordPress search on your site once installed. Youâll need to configure the search engine to enable searching for media files.

You can either modify the existing default Engine or create a new Supplementary Engine dedicated to searching media files.

For the purpose of this tutorial, letâs set up a new Engine. Simply visit **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** and click the âAdd Newâ button in the Engines tab to get started.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-2.jpg)In the popup window that appears next, select the **Media** option and give your engine a descriptive name.

As a result, this engine will only show media files in the search results. If you want to add more content types, please check other sources youâd like to have.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-media-engine.jpg)Once youâve done that, click âDoneâ to save your settings.

Now, you can click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button located on the Media tab so that you can identify what to index.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-to-engine.jpg)Next, you will see a popup window where you can select different attribute sources.

For example, you can make document content, PDF metadata, title, description, and other attributes searchable.

There is also an option to include custom fields and taxonomies in the search process.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-sources.jpg)When youâre finished, go ahead and click the **Done** button.

You can also adjust the attribute relevance for search results using the slider. The greater the relevance, the higher the content will rank on the search results page.

![Adjust media weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adjust-media-weights-1030x492.png)Next, you will need to scroll to the top of the page.

Finally, click **Save** to store the new settings.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine.jpg)Now, you might need to rebuild the index in order for the changes to take effect.

To do this, click **Rebuild Index** to initiate the process manually.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/rebuild-index.jpg)Based on your websiteâs current configuration, you might see a popup during this process. When SearchWP indexes an image or document file, it gathers all indexable data and saves it in its database.

If you prefer to replace the existing index data with new data, check the âRemove stored Document Content, PDF Metadata, and image EXIF when rebuilding indexâ option and then click **Continue Index Rebuild**.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Configure Engine Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Reset-document-content-warning-with-checkbox.jpg)Congratulations! You have successfully made WordPress search media files on your website!

### Step 3: Create a Search Form to Search Media Files

Now, you can use this Engine with any search form you create using SearchWP. To do this, navigate to SearchWP > Search Forms, then click âAdd New.â

![How to make WordPress search media files: Create Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step1-1.jpg)Next, you can start by entering a name of the search form

SearchWP also offers multiple Layout themes to choose from. Simply select one and customize it according to your needs.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Create Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-step-2.jpg)After that, you should be able to customize the search form as necessary.

Most importantly, youâll need to assign an **Engine** to this form. To do this, locate the Engine section and select the newly created âMedia Engineâ from the dropdown menu.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Create Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-step-3.jpg)Once youâve made other modifications according to your needs, you can click **Save** to store your settings.

Now, visitors will see media files such as PDFs, documents, videos, audio, and images in the search results made using this search form.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Create Search Form Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-step-4.jpg)SearchWP provides multiple ways to embed this search form into your website.

Go ahead and click the **Embed** button next to Save, and youâll see different options.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Publish Search Form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)You can enter the search form using a Gutenberg block, shortcode, or PHP code.

Pick the embed method that suits you best. For more details, please see this guide on [how to create an advanced search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/ "How To Create Advanced Search Forms In WordPress (Easily)").

Next, you can simply visit your website and test the search form.

![Search for image on your site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-image-on-your-site-1030x439.png)For example, on our demo site, we have image saved as âCoffee.â

Searching for the media file name showed that it appears in the search results.

![View image search result](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-image-search-result-1030x511.png)When we click on image, it opened the correct file.

This shows that the media file search is working properly.

## FAQs About Making Media Files Searchable

Here are some common questions we get about making media files searchable in WordPress.

**1. Will SearchWP search inside PDF files?**

Yes, SearchWP can read and index the actual text content inside your PDF files. This means visitors can find PDFs based on the words written inside them, not just the filename. Just make sure that your files are uploaded to the Media Library.

**2. Can I make only certain file types searchable?**

Absolutely. SearchWP lets you choose exactly which media attributes to include in your search. You can make only images searchable, or only PDFs, or any combination of file types you want.

**3. Will this slow down my website?**

No, SearchWP creates its own search index in the background. This means searching happens quickly without putting extra load on your server or slowing down your site for visitors.

**4. Do I need coding skills to set this up?**

Not at all. Weâve walked you through the entire process in this tutorial, and it only requires clicking a few buttons in your WordPress dashboard. No code editing needed.

We hope this tutorial helepd you learn how to make WordPress search media files. You may also want to see our guide on [how to customize the WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize the WordPress Search Results Page Easily (For Beginners)") and [how to highlight search terms in WordPress search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-highlight-search-terms-in-wordpress/).
