### [How to Filter WooCommerce by Product Type (5 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-by-product-type/)

**Published:** February 17, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When you add products to your WooCommerce store, youâre probably not thinking about how theyâll all look mixed together on one page.

You might have simple items, products with multiple sizes or color options, bundle offers, and even downloadable files all mixed together.

Weâve seen how confusing this becomes for shoppers. They land on your shop page and see every product type jumbled together without any way to sort through them.

The solution is adding a filter that lets shoppers sort products by type. It gives them control over what they see and helps them find the right kind of product in seconds.

In this article, weâll show you how to filter WooCommerce by product type.

## Why Filter WooCommerce Products by Type?

Before we walk through the setup process, letâs talk about why product type filtering matters for your store.

Think about a customer who only wants to buy gift cards or [downloadable products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-easy-digital-downloads-products-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How to Make Easy Digital Downloads Products Searchable in WordPress"). Without filters, theyâre forced to scroll past physical items they canât use.

Weâve noticed that mixing product types creates confusion at checkout, too. Customers donât always understand why some products have shipping costs while others donât, or why certain items have size options and others are fixed.

Product type filters solve these problems by letting shoppers narrow down to exactly what theyâre looking for. Someone searching for variable products can hide all the simple products with one click.

This makes the shopping experience smoother and reduces frustration. When customers can easily find the type of product they need, theyâre more likely to complete their purchase.

Now, letâs look at how you can easily filter by product type on your online store.

## Filter WooCommerce Products by Product Type

The easiest way to add product type filters to your WooCommerce store is by using [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)WPFilters is the best WordPress filtering plugin that makes sorting products simple for your customers. It works seamlessly with [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") right out of the box.

Created by the team behind SearchWP, this plugin gives you powerful filtering options without requiring any coding knowledge. You can set up professional Amazon-style filters in minutes.

The plugin lets you create filters for product types, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-blog-posts-by-category-in-wordpress/ "How to Filter Blog Posts by Category in WordPress (Easy Way)"), tags, attributes, and custom fields. Your customers get the control they need to find products faster.

Here are some features WPFilters offers:

- **Multiple Filter Types:** Choose from checkboxes, radio buttons, dropdowns, and more to match your storeâs design.
- **Shareable Filter URLs:** Every filtered search creates a unique link that customers can bookmark or share with friends.
- **Easy Placement:** Add filters anywhere on your site using Gutenberg blocks or simple shortcodes.
- **Full WooCommerce Support:** Built specifically to work with WooCommerce product data, including all product types.
- **Mobile Responsive:** Filters look great and work perfectly on phones and tablets.

With that, letâs see how you can set up product type filtering on your store.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a WPFilters account.

Once youâve created your account, head to your account area and click on the Downloads tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)From here, click the **Download WPFilters** button and save the plugin files to your computer. We also recommend copying your license key now since youâll need it during setup.

Next, you need to upload the WPFilters plugin files to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this step, please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activating the plugin, the WPFilters setup wizard will appear on your screen. Go ahead and click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button and follow the onscreen instructions to complete the setup.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)### Step 2: Create a Product Type Filter Element

Now you need to create the filter that your customers will use to sort products by type.

To start, head to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from your WordPress dashboard and click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new element in WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-element-in-wpfilters-1030x659.png)On the next screen, youâll start setting up your filter. First, enter a name for your filter at the top, like âProduct Type Filterâ or something similar that helps you stay organized.

Next, you need to choose what type of filter you want to create. WPFilters offers multiple checkboxes, radio buttons, dropdowns, and search bars.

For product type filtering, we recommend using the Checkbox option. This lets customers select multiple product types at once if they want to see both simple and variable products together.

![Enter product type filter name and filter field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-product-type-filter-name-and-filter-field-1030x612.png)Youâll see a live preview of your filter in the panel on the right side of the screen. This updates once you save your changes, so you can see exactly how it will look.

Now scroll down to the **Data Source** section. This is where you tell WPFilters what information to use for the filter.

Simply click the dropdown menu and look for the **Product Type** option. Go ahead and select it as your data source.

![Select product type data source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-type-data-source-1030x600.png)WPFilters will automatically detect all the product types youâre using in your store. These typically include simple products, variable products, grouped products, and external products.

### Step 3: Customize Your Product Type Filter

After selecting your data source, you can customize how the filter looks and behaves for your customers.

Simply scroll down to the **Container** section to adjust the display settings. Here you can control how the filter appears on your store.

![Edit brand filter container settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-brand-filter-container-settings-1030x494.png)For instance, enable the âShow Titleâ option if you want customers to see a label like âProduct Typeâ above the filter. This helps them understand what theyâre sorting by.

You can also turn on the âCollapsibleâ option to let users hide the filter when they donât need it. This keeps your sidebar tidy and gives customers control over the interface.

The âHorizontalâ option arranges the filter choices side-by-side instead of in a vertical list.

Next, look at the **Items** section below the Container settings. This controls how individual filter options appear to your customers.

![Edit items settings for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-items-settings-for-filters-1030x456.png)Enable âItem Countâ to show how many products exist for each type. For example, it might display âSimple Products (24)â or âVariable Products (18),â so customers know what to expect.

When youâre satisfied with how everything looks, scroll to the top of the page and click the **Save** button to store your changes.

![Save product type filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-product-type-filter-1030x497.png)Youâll see a preview of your completed filter in the right panel, showing exactly how it will appear to customers.

### Step 4: Add the Filter to Your Store

Now that your filter is ready, you need to place it on your WooCommerce store where customers can actually use it.

WPFilters makes this easy by offering multiple ways to embed your filter. You can use a Gutenberg block, a shortcode, or PHP code if youâre comfortable with development.

To get started, click the âEmbedâ button at the top of the Elements page.

![Embed search filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-filters--1030x492.png)A popup window will appear showing you the different embed options. You can add the filter to an existing page or create a new one.

For this tutorial, weâll show you how to add the product type filter to your WooCommerce shop page sidebar. This is where most customers expect to find filtering options.

Simply head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard. Do note that these steps might vary slightly depending on which WordPress theme youâre using for your store.

From here, scroll down to find your WooCommerce sidebar or Shop sidebar section. Then, you can click the + button to add the **WPFilters Element** widget block.

![Add WPFilters element to shop sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-to-shop-sidebar-1030x644.png)Once youâve added the WPFilters Element block, you need to tell it which filter to display. Look at the âSettingsâ panel on the right side of your screen.

And then click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and choose the product type filter you created in the previous steps.

![Select product type element](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-type-element-1030x545.png)When youâre done, click the **Update** button at the top to save your changes. Your filter is now live on your store.

### Step 5: Test Your Product Type Filter

After adding the filter to your store, the final step is to test it and make sure everything works correctly.

Visit your WooCommerce shop page to see the product type filter in action. You should see it displayed in your sidebar with all the product types from your store.

![View product type filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-product-type-filter-1-1030x682.png)The filter should show checkboxes for each product type you have, like simple product, variable product, and grouped product.

Now letâs test if the filtering actually works. Go ahead and click on one of the product types in the filter.

For example, if you select âVariable Productâ from the checkbox list, your shop page should refresh and show only products that have multiple variations, like [size or color options](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)").

![Test product type filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-product-type-filters-1030x629.png)All your simple products, grouped products, and other types should disappear from view. Only the variable products remain visible.

Try selecting multiple product types at once by checking more than one box. The shop page should now display products from both types you selected.

This gives your customers the flexibility to compare different product types or narrow down to exactly what they need.

## FAQs About WooCommerce Product Type Filters

**1. Can I add multiple filters to my WooCommerce store?**

Yes, you can create as many filters as you need. For instance, you can stack multiple filters in the sidebar so customers can sort by product type, category, price, and attributes all at once. Each filter works independently, giving shoppers precise control over what they see.

**2. Will this work with my current WooCommerce theme?**

WPFilters works with almost every WooCommerce theme. Weâve tested it on popular themes like Astra, Storefront, and OceanWP without any issues. The filters integrate seamlessly with your existing sidebar and shop page layout.

**3. Do customers need to reload the page to see filtered results?**

By default, the page will refresh when customers select a filter option. However, WPFilters supports Ajax filtering, which updates results instantly without reloading the entire page. This creates a smoother shopping experience for your customers.

Adding product type filters to your WooCommerce store helps customers find exactly what theyâre looking for without scrolling through irrelevant products.

Weâve shown you how to set up these filters using WPFilters in just a few simple steps. Your customers can now sort through simple products, variable products, grouped products, and more with a single click.

This small change makes a big difference in how shoppers experience your store. They spend less time searching and more time buying, which leads to better sales for your business.

We hope this article helped you learn how to filter WooCommerce by product type. You may also want to see our guides on [how to filter WooCommerce products by tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-tags/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Tags (Step-by-Step)") and [how to add a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

### [How to Add a Search Box to a WordPress Page (For Newbies)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-a-wordpress-page/)

**Published:** February 12, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Do you want to know how to add a search box to a WordPress page?

Itâll encourage your visitors to perform a search, boost your content discoverability, and increase the number of pageviews you get.

From this guide, youâll learn how to add a search box to your pages in a few clicks, even if youâre a complete newbie to WordPress.

Letâs begin!

## Benefits of Adding a Search Box to a WordPress Page

Adding a search bar to your pages will help you:

- **Get more pageviews**: When visitors see a search bar in a blog post or page, there is a great chance theyâll use it, especially if you motivate them to do that.
- **Boost your content discoverability**: Give your content with low view counts another chance to be discovered.
- **Deliver more relevant search results**: Help your visitors faster find what theyâre looking for by limiting your search boxes to specific content types or categories.
- **Increase the average session duration**: The more of your content visitors explore, the longer they stay on your site.
- **Rank higher in search engines**: A high average session duration is a strong signal to Google that your site is worth getting better positions in search results.

With that, letâs see how you can add a search box to a page in WordPress.

The easiest way to add a custom search bar to your WordPress pages is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

- **[Highlight search terms in search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-highlight-search-terms-in-wordpress/ "How To Highlight Search Terms In WordPress (Without Coding)")**: It makes it easier for your visitors to recognize if theyâve found what theyâre looking for in your search results.
- [**Enable fuzzy search on your site**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/): Due to that, your visitors will find the right content, even if they make a typo in a search query.

### Step 1: Install SearchWP

To get started, youâll need to [get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

Once done, simply head over to your SearchWP account and go to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you can press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, copy the license key in the bottom left corner.

The next step is to install and activate the SearchWP plugin on your WordPress website.

First, youâll need to upload the downloaded ZIP file to your site. You can check out the [how to install a WordPress plugin tutorial](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") if you need help with that.

After that, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

After installing SearchWP, navigate to your WordPress dashboard, point your cursor over the **SearchWP** button at the top bar and click **Activate License**.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, youâre ready to customize your search engine.

### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engine

Next, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard to customize the search engine.

You can consider search engines as a set of rules that they follow when performing a search. By customizing these rules, you can change how search works on your site.

For example, you can include custom fields, [exclude specific pages from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"), enable search by post tags, and many more.

To get started, click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup window will open where you can select the sources for your search engine.

As you can see, there are 4 search sources: **Posts**, **Pages**, **Media**, and **Users**.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Letâs say you want this search engine to search only among posts. This way, we can clean up search results on our site and help our visitors find the right content faster.

Then, uncheck all search sources except **Posts**.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)When youâre finished customizing your search engine settings, press **Done**.

Next, you can also give our visitors the ability to search for posts by tags and custom fields.

To get started, simply head to the **Posts** section and click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, a new window will popup, where you can specify what custom fields and taxonomies you want to consider when performing a search.

For example, letâs say you have a real estate website. You can include a custom field like number of bedrooms in the search process.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)Next, you can also allow search by post tags and categories.

First, click on the **Taxonomies** field and then select âTags (post\_tag)â or âCategories (category)â from the dropdown menu.

![Select post tags](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-post-tags--1030x597.png)Next, find the **Tags** taxonomy and click on it.

Now visitors of our site can search for posts by their tags and custom fields.

When youâre finished managing the attributes, press **Done** to save the changes.

The final step is to save your search engine. To do that, click the **Save Engines** button in the upper right corner.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)### Step 3: Create a Search Form

Now that youâve customized the search form, the next step is to create a new search form.

First, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and then click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-search-form-1030x533.png)On the next screen, you can change the name of the form at the top.

After that, SearchWP offers different theme layouts to choose from.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)If you scroll down, then youâll see more options to customize the form.

Plus, you get to select which engine, results page, and more for your form.

![Select search engine for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-engine-for-search-form-1030x586.png)To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### Step 4: Add a Search Bar to Your WordPress Page

Now, SearchWP also offers an option to automatically embed the search form on any page.

You can simply click the Embed button in the Search Form page.

![Embed the search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embeding-search-form-1030x728.jpg)Next, a new window will open with different embed options, which includes Gutenberg Block, Shortcode, or PHP code.

For this tutorial, weâll select the âGutenberg Blockâ and add it to a new page.

![Embed new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-new-search-form-1030x550.png)Alternatively, you can also add a SearchWP block to any page and display a search box.

To get started, navigate to **Pages Â» Add New** in your WordPress dashboard.

![add a new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-page.png)After you get to the page editor, click the **+** button to add a new block.

Simply navigate to the **SearchWP Form** block and add it your page.

![Add SearchWP form block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-form-block-1030x488.png)After that, you will need to select your search form.

Simply click the dropdown menu in the SearchWP Form block and choose the form.

![Select your search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-search-form-1030x461.png)When youâre done, go ahead and click the **Publish** button.

Once the page is live, you can check out your new search page.

Here is how this page looks on our test site:

![how to add a search box to a WordPress page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/our-search-form.png)Letâs also test our search box to make sure it works correctly.

We have many posts on our site related to WordPress in various categories, but only one of them is attached to the **Tutorials** category.

Letâs see what results weâll get by searching for **WordPress**.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-11.png)As you can see, we only find our single post thatâs placed within the **Tutorials** category.

It means that our search box works the way itâs supposed to.

![we found the post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-post-1.png)## Add Search Box to WordPress Page Builders

The best part about using SearchWP is that you get to add the search form to some of the most popular WordPress website and landing page builders.

Letâs look at how you can easily add a search box to each of them.

### 1. Oxygen Builder

Oxygen is a robust visual site builder that gives you granular control over your siteâs design.

### [How to Filter Brands on Your WooCommerce Store (2026)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-brands-on-your-woocommerce-store/)

**Published:** February 11, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Running a WooCommerce store with multiple brands can quickly become overwhelming for your customers. They land on your shop page and see hundreds of products from dozens of different brands all mixed together.

Weâve watched customers scroll endlessly through product pages, trying to find items from their favorite brand. Most of them give up after a minute or two and leave the site without buying anything.

The problem gets worse as your inventory grows. Adding more brands should help your business, but without proper filtering, it just creates more clutter for shoppers to wade through.

In this article, weâll show you how to filter brands on your WooCommerce store using WPFilters.

## Why Brand Filters Matter for Your WooCommerce Store

Before we jump into the tutorial, letâs talk about why brand filtering can transform your [WooCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

Think of your store like a department store without any signs or sections. Customers who want Nike shoes have to walk past every single brand in the building just to find what theyâre looking for.

Brand filters solve this problem by letting shoppers instantly narrow down products to only the brands they trust. Someone shopping for Apple accessories doesnât want to scroll past dozens of generic brands to find what they need.

Weâve noticed that stores with brand filters keep visitors browsing longer. When customers can easily find their favorite brands, theyâre more likely to explore multiple products and make a purchase.

Brand loyalty is real, and your customers have preferences. Some shoppers only buy organic skincare brands, while others stick to budget-friendly options from specific manufacturers.

Without filters, youâre forcing brand-conscious customers to work too hard. Theyâll often leave your site and buy from a competitor who makes brand shopping easier.

Adding brand filters also sets your store apart from competitors who havenât implemented this feature. It shows customers that you understand their shopping preferences and care about their experience.

That said, letâs see how you can easily add brand filters to your site.

## Filter Brands on Your WooCommerce Store

The easiest way to add brand filters to your WooCommerce store is by using [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)WPFilters is the best WordPress filtering plugin that makes adding brand filters simple, even if youâve never set up anything like this before.

Itâs built by the same team behind [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"), which means youâre getting a tool from experts who understand how filtering and search work together.

Using WPFilters, you can create professional brand filters without touching a single line of code. The plugin handles all the technical work while you just click through simple settings.

Here are some features you get with WPFilters:

- **Multiple Filter Types**: Choose from checkboxes, dropdowns, radio buttons, [slider range](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-range-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Range Filters in WordPress (Easy Way)"), or search bars for your brand filter.
- **WooCommerce Integration**: Works seamlessly with WooCommerce product brands and attributes.
- **Shareable Filter URLs**: Every filtered search creates a unique link that customers can bookmark or share.
- **Place Filters Anywhere**: Add brand filters to your shop page, sidebar, or any custom location using Gutenberg blocks.
- **Customization Options**: Control how your filters look with titles, item counts, and collapsible sections.

Now, letâs walk through the steps to add brand filtering to your WooCommerce store.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a WPFilters account.

Once youâve created an account, simply head to your account area and go to the âDownloadsâ tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)From here, youâll need to click the **Download WPFilters** button and save the plugin files on your computer.

We also recommend copying the License Key from this page, as youâll need it to activate the plugin.

Next, you can upload the WPFilters plugin files to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activating the plugin, you will see the WPFilters setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)Once youâve completed the setup wizard, youâre ready to create your first brand filter.

### Step 2: Create a Brand Filter Element

Next, youâll need to create a new filter element that your customers will use to sort products by brand.

To start, head to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from the WordPress dashboard and simply click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new element in WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-element-in-wpfilters-1030x659.png)On the next page, you can start setting up your brand filter.

First, enter a name for your filter at the top, like âFilter by Brandâ or âProduct Brands.â This name helps you stay organized if you create multiple filters later.

After that, youâll need to select the type of filter you want to create. WPFilters offers multiple options, such as a checkbox, a radio list, a dropdown, a search bar, and more.

For this tutorial, weâll use the âCheckboxâ option because it lets customers select multiple brands at once.

![Select checkbox filter element](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-checkbox-filter-element-1030x603.png)Youâll also see a live preview of your brand filter in the panel on the right. This allows you to see exactly how it will look to your customers.

### Step 3: Connect the Filter to Brand Data

Now you need to tell the filter which data to use for sorting your WooCommerce products.

Simply scroll down to the âData Sourceâ section and click the dropdown menu.

Here, youâll see different options for product data. Since you want to filter by brands, go ahead and select the **Brands (product\_brand)** option from the list.

![Select brands as data source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-brands-as-data-source-1030x541.png)WPFilters will automatically detect all the brands youâve added to your products. This means the filter will display every brand that exists in your storeâs inventory.

The filter will also update automatically when you add new brands to your products in the future.

### Step 4: Customize Your Brand Filter

After connecting the brand data, you can customize how the filter looks and behaves on your store.

Simply scroll down to the âContainerâ section to adjust the display settings.

![Edit brand filter container settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-brand-filter-container-settings-1030x494.png)Here, you can enable the âShow Titleâ option so customers see a clear label like âFilter by Brandâ above the filter.

You can also turn on the âCollapsibleâ option to let visitors hide the filter when they donât need it, which saves space on mobile devices.

If you want the brand names to appear side-by-side instead of in a vertical list, enable the âHorizontalâ option.

Next, scroll down to the âItemsâ section to control how individual brands appear in the filter.

![Edit items settings for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-items-settings-for-filters-1030x456.png)If you want, you can enable âItem Countâ so customers can see how many products are available for each brand. This helps set expectations before they click.

You can also consider turning off âShow Emptyâ to hide brands that donât have any products in stock. This prevents customers from clicking on a brand only to find zero results.

When youâre satisfied with all the settings, scroll to the top of the page and click the **Save** button.

![Save brand filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-brand-filters-1030x453.png)### Step 5: Add the Brand Filter to Your Store

Now that your brand filter is ready, the next step is to display it on your [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce") store, where customers can actually use it.

WPFilters makes this process simple by offering multiple ways to embed your filter.

To get started, simply click the âEmbedâ button at the top of the Elements page.

![Embed brand filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-brand-filters-1030x609.png)Next, youâll see a popup window with different embedding options.

You can choose between the Gutenberg block, a [shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"), or PHP code if youâre a developer.

![Embed search filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-filters--1030x492.png)However, the best method to add your brand filter is in the sidebar of your store.

Adding the filter to your sidebar keeps it visible while customers browse different pages of your store.

To do this, head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard.

**Note:** The steps might vary slightly depending on the WordPress theme youâre using for your WooCommerce store.

Next, scroll down to your WooCommerce sidebar section and click the â+â button to add a new block. Search for and add the **WPFilters Element** widget block.

![Add WPFilters element to shop sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-to-shop-sidebar-1-1030x617.png)From the settings panel on the right, click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and choose the brand filter you created earlier.

When youâre done, click the **Update** button to save your changes.

![Choose brands filter from settings panel](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-brands-filter-from-settings-panel-1030x530.png)### Step 6: Test Your Brand Filter

After adding the brand filter to your WooCommerce store, the next step is to test it and make sure everything works correctly.

Simply visit your shop page to see the brand filter in action.

![View brands filter in sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-brands-filter-in-sidebar-1030x649.png)You should see your brand filter displayed either on the shop page or in the sidebar, depending on where you placed it in the previous step.

Now, try selecting different brands from the filter. The products on the page should update automatically to show only items from the brands you selected.

For example, if we select âCeraVeâ on our demo store, then only products from this specific brand will be shown, while hiding everything else.

![Test brands filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-brands-filters-1030x651.png)If everything is working correctly, your customers can now easily filter products by their favorite brands, making it much easier for them to find exactly what theyâre looking for.

## FAQs About WooCommerce Brand Filters

Here are some common questions we get about setting up brand filters in WooCommerce.

**1. Do I need coding knowledge to set up brand filters in WooCommerce?**

Not at all. WPFilters handles all the technical work for you, so you can create brand filters just by clicking through the settings. We designed this tutorial specifically for store owners who donât have coding experience.

**2. Will this work with my current theme?**

Yes, WPFilters works with almost every WooCommerce theme. Weâve tested it with popular themes it integrates smoothly with their layouts.

**3. Can I combine brand filters with other filters, like price or category?**

Absolutely. You can create multiple filter elements in WPFilters and stack them together. For example, you could let customers filter by brand, price range, and product category all at the same time. This gives shoppers very precise control over what they see in your store.

Brand filters transform the shopping experience in your WooCommerce store by helping customers find their favorite manufacturers instantly.

We hope this article helped you learn how to filter brands on your WooCommerce store. You may also want to see our guides on [how to filter WooCommerce products by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-category/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Category (Step-by-Step)") and [how to filter WooCommerce products by attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-attributes/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Attributes (Simple Way)").

### [How to Include bbPress Forum Topics & Replies in WordPress Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-bbpress-forum-topics-replies-in-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** February 6, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Building a thriving online community takes real effort. You spend time moderating discussions, encouraging participation, and cultivating valuable conversations in your bbPress forums.

But thereâs a hidden problem that quietly undermines all that hard work.

When visitors search your site, they donât see any of those forum discussions. Years of helpful answers, detailed troubleshooting threads, and expert advice remain invisible to your search function.

Weâve watched site owners struggle with this exact issue since the default WordPress search has many limitations.

In this article, weâll show you how to include bbPress forum topics and replies in WordPress search.

## Why Doesnât bbPress Forum Content Appear in WordPress Search?

The [default WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)") function has a major blind spot when it comes to forum content.

When someone searches your site, WordPress looks through standard posts and pages by default.

It skips right over your forum discussions, even when they contain the exact information your visitor needs.

This creates a frustrating experience for everyone involved. Your community members have already answered common questions dozens of times in forum threads.

But new visitors keep asking the same things because they canât find those existing discussions.

The problem gets worse as your forum grows. More valuable content gets buried deeper, making your community less helpful to newcomers than it should be.

Fixing this issue will help your visitors find the information they need faster. It will reduce duplicate forum posts and make your entire community more valuable to everyone who uses it.

That said, letâs look at a quick and easy way of making your forum topics and replies searchable in WordPress.

## Make bbPress Forum Topics and Replies Searchable

The easiest way to make your bbPress forum content searchable is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners use SearchWP to improve their site search and help visitors find the content they need.

Beyond making forum content searchable, SearchWP offers powerful features that enhance your entire search experience:

- **Fuzzy Search:** Let visitors find forum posts even when they misspell search terms or use slightly different keywords.
- **PDF Indexing:** Make downloadable files and PDF documents from your forums searchable alongside topics and replies.
- **Seamless Integrations:** SearchWP works seamlessly with popular plugins like [WooCommerce ](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search")and page builders.

With SearchWP, you can transform your forum from a hidden archive into a searchable [knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-the-knowledge-base-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress") that serves your community better.

Now, letâs walk through the steps to make your bbPress content fully searchable.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a new account.

Once youâve created your account, log in and navigate to the **Downloads** tab in your dashboard.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)On this page, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button to save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer. If you need help with this step, check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activating the plugin, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen with the setup wizard. Click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the simple onscreen instructions to complete the initial configuration.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Once you finish the setup wizard, youâre ready to install the bbPress integration.

### Step 2: Install the bbPress Integration Extension

The [bbPress Integration extension](https://searchwp.com/features/bbpress-integration/ "bbPress Integration extension") allows SearchWP to index forum content and include it in search results.

To install this extension, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard.

From here, you can scroll down until you find the bbPress Integration extension in the list and then click the âInstallâ button.

![bbPress integration extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/bbpress-integration-extension-1030x570.png)The extension activates automatically after installation.

Now youâre ready to configure SearchWP to include your forums, topics, and replies in search results.

### Step 3: Customize Your Search Engine

Next, you can customize the search engine in SearchWP and include content from bbPress in the search results.

This can include the forums created on your site, the forum topics, forum replies, topic tags, and more.

First, you will need to head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard.

Youâll see your default search engine with its current sources listed. Go ahead and click the **Sources & Settings** button to modify what SearchWP includes when performing searches.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)A popup window will appear showing all available sources on your site. By default, it will already include posts, pages, and media as sources.

Next, you can select Forums, Topics, and Replies as sources for your engine.

![Select forms and replies as source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-forms-and-replies-as-source-1030x518.png)When youâre finished, click the **Done** button to save this change.

Now SearchWP will search through different forums, forum topics, and replies when visitors use your site search. This gives them access to the complete forum conversation, not just the original questions.

### Step 4: Customize Forum Search Attributes

Adding forums, topics, and replies as sources tells SearchWP where to look. But you also need to specify which attributes within those sources should be searchable.

These can include any custom fields or taxonomies, like tags or [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), in the search process.

For instance, you can scroll down on the âAlgorithmâ page to view the Forum until you see the âTopicsâ section.

Simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button to configure which parts of forum topics SearchWP should index.

![Edit topics attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-topics-attributes-1030x446.png)A popup window will open showing available attributes. By default, SearchWP includes basic attributes like title, content, slug, excerpt, and author.

You can also add [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") and taxonomies, such as âTopic Tags (topic-tags)â, to improve the overall search experience.

![Add topic tags attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-topic-tags-attribute-1030x473.png)Click **Done** when youâre satisfied with the topic attributes.

Now, you can scroll down to the Forums and Replies section and click the Add/Remove Attributes button to edit their attributes.

Simply configure their attributes the same way. The content attribute is especially important here since it contains all the actual reply text where answers and discussions happen.

The final step is to save all your changes. You can do that by scrolling to the top of the page and clicking the **Save** button in the upper-right corner.

![Save search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-product-search-engine-1030x481.png)### Step 5: Test Your Forum Search

Now that youâve configured SearchWP to include forum content, itâs time to make sure everything works correctly.

Visit your siteâs front end and navigate to the search bar. Think of a specific topic or reply you know exists in your forums.

For this test, choose something distinctive that appears in your forum discussions but not in your regular posts or pages. This way, you can clearly see that SearchWP is finding forum content.

Type your search term into the search bar and press Enter or click the search button.

![Search form on demo site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-on-demo-site-1030x394.png)You should now see forum topics and replies appear in your search results alongside your regular content. The results will show the topic title or reply content that matches your search query.

For example, on our test site about a coffee cafe, we searched for âmanagement,â which appears in a forum discussion. The search results now include both the original forum and its topics.

![View forum search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-forum-search-results-1030x495.png)If we click any of the results, it will take us to the forum where the discussion is happening.

This shows that the search is working correctly and the forums created using bbPress are now searchable in WordPress.

![See forum as results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/see-forum-as-results-1030x557.png)## FAQs about Including bbPress Search

Here are some common questions we get about making bbPress forums searchable in WordPress.

**1. Will this slow down my site?**

No, SearchWP is designed to be efficient and wonât impact your site speed. It builds a search index in the background, so searches happen quickly without putting extra load on your database during each query.

**2. Can I search by forum author?**

Yes, you can make forum authors searchable. When you configure the attributes for Topics and Replies, youâll see an Author option available. Simply include it in your searchable attributes, and visitors can find posts by specific community members.

**3. Does this work with other forum plugins?**

SearchWP is specifically designed to work with bbPress through its dedicated integration extension. You can check the [SearchWP Features page](https://searchwp.com/features/ "SearchWP Features") to see all available integrations for different forum solutions.

**4. What happens to new forum posts?**

SearchWP automatically indexes new forum topics and replies as soon as theyâre published. You donât need to manually rebuild the index every time someone posts in your forums. The plugin monitors your site for new content and updates the search index in real-time.

Making your bbPress forum content searchable transforms your community from a collection of isolated discussions into a valuable knowledge base.

Visitors can now find answers that community members already provided months or years ago. This reduces duplicate questions, helps newcomers get up to speed faster, and makes your entire forum more useful to everyone.

We hope this article helped you learn how to include bbPress forum topics and replies in WordPress search. You may also want to see our guides on [how to search posts by tags in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)") and [how to track searches on your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site").

Ready to make your forum content searchable and build a stronger community? You can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP here").

**Categories:** Tutorials

**Tags:** bbPress Integration Extension, forum topics, Forum Topics &amp; Replies in WordPress Search, forums, make replies searchable, searchable documents, SearchWP, WordPress search, wordpress search settings

### [How to Create Advanced Search Forms in WordPress (Easily)](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** February 4, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Want to create advanced search forms in WordPress without writing code?

Even if you have what your visitors are looking for on your website, you are losing business if they cannot find it, which is why you need a good search form.

It will help you reduce frustration and increase engagement with your content, providing an improved user experience.

An ideal search form is quite challenging to create with the default WordPress search, as itâs time-consuming and requires expert coding skills.

In this article, we will show you how to create advanced search forms in WordPress.

## Why Use Advanced Search Forms on Your Site?

Before we get to the tutorial, letâs talk about why you might need more than just a standard search bar.

We have seen that when visitors canât find what they need quickly, they usually leave the website.

An advanced search form helps prevent this by acting like a smart assistant that filters out the noise.

It lets your users narrow down their hunt to specific categories, [tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)"), or even custom post types right from the start.

This means someone looking for a âvideo tutorialâ wonât get stuck reading through âtext articles.â

We have found that this specificity keeps visitors happy and helps them engage more with your content.

Now, letâs look at an easy way of adding advanced search forms to your WordPress website.

## The Best Way to Create Advanced Search Forms

The easiest way to build these specific search forms is with a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") because it enhances the search experience on your site and replaces the weaknesses youâll find in the default search.

It allows us to create multiple unique search engines that can be tuned to look for exactly what we want.

For example, you can use it to create one search bar that looks only for products and another that looks only for support articles. It handles all the heavy lifting in the background to make sure your visitors get the right results instantly.

Here are a few other things we love about SearchWP:

- **Search PDF content:** It can read the text inside your [PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), so users can find documents easily.
- **Exclude specific pages:** You can use this to [hide specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or areas, like the checkout pages or admin content to keep the results clean.
- **View search statistics:** It tracks exactly [what people look for](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site"), so you can see what content is popular on your site.
- **Fuzzy search:** This feature helps users find the right result even if they make a spelling mistake in their query.

With that, letâs look at how you can set up SearchWP to create an advanced search form.

## Install And Activate SearchWP

To get started, you will need to [grab your copy of the SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today") plugin. You can do this by visiting the website and setting up a new account.

Once done, head over to the Downloads tab on your SearchWP account and click **Download SearchWP**.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)We would also recommend copying your license key from the same screen; it will be required after a few steps.

Now, you can install and activate SearchWP like any other plugin. If you are not sure how to do that, you can check out this beginnerâs guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activation, you will see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button on the welcome screen to finish the initial setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)## Configure Search Engine for WordPress

First things first: you need a search engine for your search form to work.

The built-in WordPress search engine is a basic one and does not include custom post types, eCommerce products, document content, or taxonomies, to name a few.

To set up the search engine, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** in your WordPress dashboard. Here, you have the option to edit the default engine or create new ones.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Besides that, you can select which sources to include by clicking the **Sources & Settings** button.

By default, SearchWP will include Posts, Pages, and Media as sources. However, if you have an eCommerce store, then you can add âProductsâ as a source.

Similarly, if youâre running a digital downloads store using [Easy Digital Downloads](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-easy-digital-downloads-products-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How to Make Easy Digital Downloads Products Searchable in WordPress"), then make âDownloadsâ searchable on your website.

![Include downloads in the search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-downloads-in-the-search-engine-1030x501.png)SearchWP also lets you add additional information in the search process. For example, you can include custom fields, categories, and tags.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

## Create Advanced Search Forms In WordPress Without Code

Using SearchWP, you can create a search form in WordPress in 3 easy steps.

Now, letâs see this in action step-by-step.

### Step 1: Add New Form

First, youâll need to navigate to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms**.

From here, you can then click on the **Add New** button.

![adding a new form on SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add_new_form.jpg)Youâll get a new form with a system-generated name. Letâs give it a meaningful name, like Product Page Search, so it can be recognizable down the road.

You can do that by clicking the pencil icon on the top left.

![naming the search formÂ ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/naming_search_form.jpg)### Step 2: Configure The Form

The effectiveness of a search form depends largely on its design and functionality. So, letâs pick a Layout Theme from the predesigned template in SearchWP.

You can see the Theme Preview right beneath the layouts. Go ahead and pick the one that serves you the best.

![create advanced search forms in WordPress by choosing a layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/different_search_form_layouts-edited.png)You can also control the layout and features by tweaking the Custom Styling settings. Letâs go over the settings one by one.

**Category Search:** Enabling this will show a category filter dropdown in the search form. It will show all the available categories. However, you can limit the categories by using the *Select Category* field below.

**Quick Search:** It promotes custom words right below the search form; with this, you can incentivize visitors to use these search terms if turned on.

**Advanced Search:** It gives your visitors more control over their search capabilities and lets them narrow their search results.

Any or all of these settings can be turned on independently.

![customizing the search form style ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-the-search-formstyle.png)Now, we can use different engines for different search forms. If you want to show different results on various forms, you can also benefit from using a supplemental engine.

Letâs stick with **Default,** as we configured that in the previous step. You can also let the form know which search results page to use by selecting one from the dropdown menu.

![selecting the right engine and choosing a search result page.](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/selecting-engine-and-search-result-page.png)As we have already discussed, SearchWP uses its engine to perform search, and what is to be indexed can be defined within the search engine by selecting sources. When a search form uses an engine, it limits the results to only that particular engine.

You can put even more restrictions on the form by using the following two settings:

**Type of search:** The search engine used by this form uses multiple sources. Selecting them here limits the search to one or more sources. If you leave this field blank, the engine will use all the available sources.

**Select Category:** You can put even more restrictions and limit the search only to a particular category. For a category to show up here, it must have at least one item assigned to it. Similar to *Advanced Search***,** your visitors can control search results by selecting their chosen category if Category Search is enabled. Please note the categories you put in here are the ones that will be displayed in the dropdown.

![limiting the search results to only the selected sources and categoriesÂ ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/limiting_search_results.png)Now, itâs time to customize the search form. Put the text you want to be shown as a placeholder inside the search form by using Field Label, and if you want the search form to have a button, turn on the Search Button.

![adding a placeholder and/or a button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding_placholder_button.png)**Quick Search Items:** Enables you to nudge the visitors in the direction you want. It lets you put specific keywords under the search form so the users can select what you want them to look for! You can add these keywords by selecting them from the dropdown. The suggestions you see here come from Metrics if you have it enabled; if not, they are based on Statistics.

**Advanced Search Filters:** You can give visitors more control and help them find exactly what they are looking for by letting them use multiple filters. SearchWP currently has support for Authors, Post Types, and Tags.

![leveling up the searching experience.Â ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/leveling_up_to_advance_search_filters.png)### Step 3: Style The Form According to Your Taste

SearchWP provides you with 4 different styles for the search form. You can pick one you like from the list, and you can also customize the color and font size of the form.

![customizing the search form styleÂ ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/different_form_styles_in_SearchWP-1.jpg)You may also like to modify the search button according to your taste. There are 3 button styles, so you have the ability to name the button and customize the background color, font color, and font size.

Please note that the modification will only work when the Search Button is turned on.

![modifying the form button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modifying_button_style.png)When you have the form you want perfectly configured, you need to store all the changes.

Simply click the **Save** button in the top right corner.

![saving the search form after configuration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save_search_form.jpg)### Step 4: Use the Form According to Your Need

Now that your search form is configured, you can put it anywhere on your site. To do that, click on **Embed** right next to the Save button.

![getting to the embedding optionsÂ ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/getting_to_publish_settings.jpg)There are a few options to embed your search form with just a few clicks. SearchWP offers 3 options: Gutenberg block, shortcode, and PHP Code.

Select how you want to embed the form and follow the instructions.

For instance, if you want to put the search form using a shortcode, select âShortcodeâ from the list, and youâll be presented with the shortcode associated with the form.

![publishing the search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)Go ahead and copy and paste it to your desired location. You can then paste the shortcode by opening the desired page.

Besides that, you can also add your search forms to any page or post using the **SearchWP Form** block.

First, you will need to edit the page and open the WordPress content editor. From here, click the + sign and add the âSearchWP Formâ block.

![Add SearchWP forms block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-forms-block-1030x514.png)Once thatâs done, you can then select your search form from the dropdown menu in the block.

After adding the form, you can update or publish your landing page or blog post.

![Choose the search form from the block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-search-form-from-the-block-1030x494.png)Voila! The setup is complete, and your search form is ready for action!

The best part about using SearchWP is that it also works with popular page builders like [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-elementor-page/ "How to Add a Search Box to Elementor Page (Beginnerâs Guide)"), Divi, [Oxygen](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-using-oxygen-visual-site-builder/ "How to Add a Search Box Using Oxygen Visual Site Builder"), and more.

## Wrapping Up

Using the same technique, you can configure as many custom search forms as you like with only a few clicks.

If you want different search forms to show different results, you can do that by leveraging the supplemental engine.

SearchWP removes the barrier to coding and enables you to add an advanced search with custom settings. It also comes with countless other features that will improve the search experience for your visitors significantly.

We hope this article helped you learn how to create an advanced search form in WordPress effortlessly without writing code. You may also want to see our guides on [how to create custom field filters in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-custom-field-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Custom Field Filters in WordPress (Step by Step)") and [how to include ACF fields into search results on your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)").

Ready to create advanced search forms in WordPress? [Get the SearchWP plan](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP Today") that fits your needs to take control of search.
