### [How to Customize the WordPress Search Results Page Easily (For Beginners)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/)

**Published:** January 29, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

The search results page on your website acts like a curator in a gallery, presenting the finest pieces most engagingly.

If the curator falters, even the most exquisite art fails to impress.

Similarly, if your search results page is clunky, the best of your products or content might go unnoticed. Itâs not just about finding the right items; itâs about displaying them well.

In this article, weâll show you how to customize the WordPress search results page using SearchWP. So, it can be the expert curator your website deserves, leading to increased engagement and growth.

## Why Do You Need A Good Search Results Page?

Having a well-designed and efficient search results page is crucial for a website or any online business, and the absence of one can have significant negative impacts. Here are some reasons why a good search results page matters.

- **User Experience**: A poor search results page can create a frustrating user experience. If customers cannot find what they are looking for quickly and easily, they are more likely to leave the website and seek alternatives. This not only reduces the chance of immediate sales but can also damage the long-term reputation of the business.

- **Conversion Rates:** The primary goal of a website or an online business is to convert casual visitors into paying customers. A search results page that is confusing, slow, or irrelevant can significantly lower conversion rates. Visitors who find what they are looking for have a positive experience and are more likely to make a purchase and return in the future.

- **Customer Insights:** A good search results page provides valuable data about customer preferences and behavior. This data can be used to improve inventory management, marketing strategies, and overall user experience. Without [effective search analytics](https://searchwp.com/features/metrics/ "SearchWP Metrics "), a business may miss out on these insights, hindering its ability to adapt and grow.

- **Competitive Advantage:** In a crowded online marketplace, having a superior search experience can be a significant competitive advantage. It demonstrates attention to customer needs and technological proficiency, setting the business apart from competitors with less effective search features.

- **Brand Loyalty and Trust:** A good search results page helps build brand loyalty and trust. When customers know they can easily find what they need, they are more likely to return and recommend the site to others.

## Customizing Your WordPress Search Results Page

Your WordPress websiteâs default search results page has many restrictions.

For instance, it provides limited customization options and cannot showcase non-post content types such as authors, categories, or tags.

This is where you need a plugin that lets you take control of your websiteâs search results page, like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that provides the most advanced search result customization for your website.

Over 50,000 website owners use it to improve their search results and keep users engaged.

SearchWP allows you to bypass the limitations of your theme and build a search page that actually helps your visitors.

The best part is that you can create multiple search results templates and connect to different search forms.

Besides that, here are some key SearchWP features has to offer:

- **Fuzzy search:** This feature lets your visitors find what they need even if they make a typo in the search bar.
- **PDF indexing:** You can make the content inside your [PDF files searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), which is great for document-heavy sites.
- **Visitor insights:** We use this to see exactly what people are searching for and find gaps in our content strategy.
- **Exclude specific content:** It allows you to hide specific pages or outdated posts from showing up in the results.

With that, letâs look at the steps to customize the WordPress search results page.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, you need to [grab your copy of the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP Here") by visiting the website and signing up for an account.

After picking your plan, go to the âDownloadsâ tab on your SearchWP account.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Go ahead and click the **Download SearchWP** button to save the ZIP file to your computer.

Remember to copy your license key from this page, as youâll be needing it in the setup process.

Next, install SearchWP like you would with any WordPress plugin. If you need help with this part, you can check out a beginnerâs guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After you activate the plugin, you will see a welcome screen with a setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the simple onscreen instructions to set everything up.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Setting Up Your Search Engine With SearchWP

Next, you can customize the search engine in SearchWP to provide a better search experience.

The default WordPress search is pretty basic and canât handle custom post types, [eCommerce products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), document content, or taxonomies.

To improve search process, simply go to your WordPress Dashboard and head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm**.

![click the Save button to start the engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/start-the-search-engine.jpg)Here, you can edit the sources for your search engine and include products, documents, jobs, and more.

Besides that, SearchWP also lets you include additional information stored in custom fields, categories, and tags as well.

You can simply edit the attributes for each of your sources and add them to the search process.

To customize your search engine, please see our detailed guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 3: Customize The WordPress Search Results Page

The groundwork is done. Now you can start customizing the search results page of your WordPress website.

To do that, navigate to the WordPress dashboard and go to **SearchWP Â» Templates**. From here, you can add a new results template or edit the default template.

![Add new results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-results-template-1030x512.png)SearchWP understands that your search results page is unique, and only you know which style would work best for your visitors.

Thatâs why there are 6 predesigned theme layouts for you to pick and choose. You can see the preview right underneath.

Do note that the final appearance may vary depending on the WordPress theme youâre using.

![pre-designed theme layouts for search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/theme_layouts_predesigned.jpg)After picking a style, you can scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section.

Here you can change the look and feel of the search results page to match the rest of your website. For that, you have the following options.

- **Layout Style:** Switch between a âGridâ or âListâ view for your search results. Additionally, you can decide the number of results to display in each row.

- **Basic Styling:** Customize the appearance of various elements, such as titles and images.

- **Description**: You can show or hide the description of the content/products from search results.

- **Images:** Define the size of the images on your search results page. Choose from small, medium, large, or none.

- **Title Style:** Adjust the font color and size for the titles in the search results.

- **Price Style:** If you have WooCommerce or [Easy Digital Downloads](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-easy-digital-downloads-products-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How to Make Easy Digital Downloads Products Searchable in WordPress") products on your website, you can change the color and font size of their prices.

- **Button:** If you choose to display a button (like âRead Moreâ), this setting allows you to toggle it on or off and customize the button label, background color, font color, and font size.

- **Results per Page:** Determine how many search results you want to show per page, which helps manage the length of your search results pages.

- **Pagination Style:** Customize the pagination navigation style at the bottom of the search results page. You can choose âNo Box,â âBoxed,â or âCircularâ styles to match your siteâs design.

![customizing options for search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/styling_options_for_search_resutls_page.png)When you are done customizing the WordPress search results page, click **Save** at the top to complete the process.

### Step 4: Make The Search Results Page Visible

The Search Results Page in SearchWP works hand in hand with Search Forms.

To create a search form, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** and click **Add New** or edit an existing search form.

![Add a new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-search-form-1030x533.png)Next, you can edit the search form by choosing a template, renaming it, and more.

For more details, please see our guide on [how to create advanced search forms in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/ "How To Create Advanced Search Forms In WordPress (Easily)").

![Choose your template for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-template-for-search-form-1030x459.png)During search form creation, scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section.

Here, youâll see a field titled **Results Page**. Please select SearchWP from the dropdown menu. There is also an option to change the target page and choose the template (if you have created more than 1).

When youâre done, simply save your settings.

### Step 5: Embed the Results Page on Your Site (Optional)

Now, before we proceed, dot note that this is an optional step.

Search results templates can be used without the need to embed them in a page.

In the form settings, if the Results Page is âSearchWPâ and the Target Page is âSearchWP Defaultâ, then the template will be used instead of the default WordPress template. No need to embed the template.

However, if you want to manually embed the templates, then you can click the **Embed** button on the Templates page in SearchWP to get started.

![Embed results page button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-results-page-button-1030x543.png)SearchWP will offer 3 options to embed your search results template, and they include a Gutenberg block, shortcode, and PHP code.

You can either choose an existing page or a new page to display the results template.

![Choose search results embed option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-results-embed-option-1030x482.png)For instance, selecting the Gutenberg block and a new page will take you to the content editor.

Here, your SearchWP Results block is added to your page.

**Note:** The results are displayed according to the engine and template assigned in the Search Form settings. Under the block settings in the panel on the right, you have the option to choose a different template and the engine to display the results.

This is not typically required and should be used only if necessary to override the setting set by the search form.

![Add results template to new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-results-template-to-new-page-1030x509.png)When youâre done, simply click the Publish or Update button in the content editor.

Now, perform a search in the form you just created and linked with the Search Results page on the front end of your website. Youâll see your tailor-made search results page appear.

Hereâs how the results template appeared on our demo eCommerce store:

![Final result after learning how to customize the wordPress search results page easily](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/example_search_results_page.jpg)## FAQs about Customizing WordPress Search Results

We know that changing core WordPress pages can bring up a few questions. Here are the answers to some of the most common questions we get asked about this topic.

**1. Can I customize the search results page without a plugin?**

Yes, you can, but it requires editing your themeâs `search.php` file with PHP code. We have found that this method is risky for beginners because one wrong line of code can break your site. Using a plugin like SearchWP is much safer and faster for most users.

**2. Will this work with my specific WordPress theme?**

SearchWP is designed to work with almost every WordPress theme out there. Since we are creating a brand new page and using a shortcode to display results, it usually plays nicely with your existing design. Plus, SearchWP supports popular website builders like [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-elementor-search-form-not-working/ "How to Fix Elementor Search Form Not Working (5 Easy Ways)"), Oxygen, [Beaver Builder](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-form-using-beaver-builder-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Form Using Beaver Builder in WordPress"), and [Divi](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)").

**3. Can I style the search results to match my brand colors?**

Absolutely. SearchWP templates come with the option to change the main color, edit titles, buttons, and more. This makes it super easy to match your brand colors without the need to use custom CSS for styling.

Your search results page is not merely about enhancing aesthetics; itâs about creating a seamless, intuitive experience for your users.

We hope this article helped you learn how to customize the WordPress search results page easily. You may also want to see our guide on [how to filter WooCommerce products by attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-attributes/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Attributes (Simple Way)") and [how to make comments searchable in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-comments-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress").

[Get SearchWP today](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!") to join the ranks of sites that prioritize excellence in every user interaction!

### [How to Improve Job Search Experience in WP Job Manager](https://searchwp.com/how-to-improve-job-search-experience-in-wp-job-manager/)

**Published:** January 27, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Running a job board is one of the most rewarding projects you might have ever managed on WordPress.

We remember launching our first career portal using the WP Job Manager plugin and feeling excited about connecting people with their dream jobs.

But we quickly realized that having great job listings doesnât matter if candidates canât actually find them. The default search in the plugin is very basic and has a lot of limitations.

This is where integrating SearchWP with WP Job Manager enhanced the overall search experience.

In this guide, we will show you how to improve the job search experience in WP Job Manager and help visitors find the right opportunities without getting frustrated.

## Why Improve the Default WP Job Manager Search?

Before we get to the tutorial, letâs talk about why the standard search in WP Job Manager might be hurting your job board.

The WP Job Manager plugin is fantastic for creating job listings and managing applications. It lets you list open positions and organize them by category.

However, the [native search](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") isnât designed to look through specific job data, like âRemoteâ tags, job type, salary ranges, or details in applications and resumes.

For example, a candidate might search for âMarketing Manager New York,â but if those words arenât in the main title, it might say no results found or show irrelevant results.

This creates a lot of friction for your users. If they canât find a job they qualify for, they will assume your board doesnât have it and leave.

We have found that fixing this search logic is the single best way to keep candidates on your site longer.

With that in mind, letâs look at how you can use SearchWP to make sure every job listing gets found.

## Enhance Search Experience in WP Job Manager

The easiest way to fix the search limitations on your WP Job Manager job board is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that is beginner-friendly to use. It enhances the default WordPress search with a much more powerful engine.

One of its standout features is that it works perfectly with WP Job Manager right out of the box.

Over 50,000 website owners use SearchWP to help their visitors find exactly what they need.

Using this plugin, you can make sure that job attributes, like location, salary, or job type, are actually searchable.

This means if someone searches for âChicago,â they will actually find jobs located in Chicago, even if the city name isnât in the job title.

Here are some other features that make us great for job boards:

- **Fuzzy Search:** Job seekers often make typos when typing fast. SearchWP can find matches even if the spelling is slightly off.
- **Search Tracking:** You can see exactly [what people are typing](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site") into your search bar. This helps you understand what kind of jobs your audience really wants.
- **Exclude Content:** You can keep your search results clean by removing outdated pages or unrelated blog posts from the job search results.
- **Custom Results Order:** If you have featured job listings, you can make sure they appear at the very top of the list by [changing the results order](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)").

With that, letâs see how you can set up SearchWP to improve your job board.

**Note:** If you want to learn how to use WP Job Manager, then please see this guide on [how to create a job board in WordPress.](https://www.wpbeginner.com/plugins/how-to-easily-create-a-job-board-in-wordpress-no-html-required/ "How to Easily Create a Job Board in WordPress (NO HTML Required)")

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

The first thing we need to do is get the SearchWP plugin up and running. You will need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for an account.

Once you have your account ready, go to the **Downloads** tab in your dashboard.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Go ahead and click the **Download SearchWP** button to save the ZIP file to your computer.

We also suggest copying your license key from this page right now, as you will need it while setting up the plugin.

Next, you need to upload and install the plugin on your WordPress site. If you need help with this, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After you activate the plugin, you will see a welcome screen with a setup wizard. You can simply click the **Start Onboarding Wizard** button to get everything initialized.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Install the WP Job Manager Integration Extension

Now that SearchWP is active, the next step is to integrate it with [WP Job Manager](https://searchwp.com/features/wp-job-manager-integration/ "SearchWPâs WP Job Manager Integration").

WP Job Manager comes with a feature-rich search, but if youâd like to enhance it further with keyword implementation, you can integrate SearchWP with it directly.

To start, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard.

![Install WP job manager integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-wp-job-manager-intergration-1030x599.png)Once you are there, scroll down until you see the **WP Job Manager Integration** extension.

Go ahead and click the **Install** button to add it to your site.

### Step 3: Customize Your Job Search Engine

Now you need to adjust the search engine settings in SearchWP and include jobs as a source.

The best part about using SearchWP is that you can create as many search engines as you want or customize the default engine.

This gives you complete control over what to include in the search process, such as [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") and taxonomies.

To do this, first navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** in your dashboard.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)From here, you will see the default search engine setup. Go ahead and click the **Sources & Settings** button.

A popup window will appear showing different sources for your engine. By default, it will include Posts, Pages, and Media.

Make sure you check the box next to **Jobs** as the source.

![Choose jobs as a source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-jobs-as-a-source-1030x494.png)When you are done selecting sources, simply click the **Done** button.

Next, youâll need to scroll down to the Jobs section and include additional information stored in custom fields, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), and tags in the search process.

To do that, click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add remove jobs attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-remove-jobs-attribute-1030x426.png)A new window will pop up where you can select specific data to include.

Here, you can add custom fields such as job location, company name, job expiration date, company website, and other information in the search process.

![Include job custom fields and taxonomies](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-job-custom-fields-and-taxonomies-1030x470.png)Similarly, you can also include taxonomies like job types to improve the search experience in WP Job Manager.

When you are finished, click the **Done** button.

Finally, donât forget to press the **Save** button at the top of the page to apply these changes to your search engine.

![Save your engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-engine-1030x437.png)### Step 4: Test Your New Job Search in WP Job Manager

Now that you have set everything up, it is time to see if it works.

We always recommend testing the search bar immediately to make sure the new attributes are being picked up.

To test your search settings, head over to your website and open your job board.

From here, try searching for something specific, like a job location or a company name.

![Search country in job board](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-country-in-job-board-1030x484.png)For example, we typed in âLondonâ to see if it pulls up jobs listed in that city.

As you can see, the search results now include jobs based on the location, even if âLondonâ isnât in the job title.

![Job board search preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/job-board-search-preview-1030x446.png)This means your candidates can now find opportunities much faster.

## FAQs about WP Job Manager Search

You can also check out some of the common questions users have about improving search on their job boards.

**1. Can you allow candidates to search by salary range?**

Yes, you can. If your job listings have a salary field, SearchWP can index it. You just need to add the salary custom field (often labeled as `_job_salary`) to the attributes list. This helps users find jobs that meet their financial expectations.

**2. Do I need to change my WordPress theme for this to work?**

No, you donât. SearchWP integrates automatically with your existing themeâs search template. We have tested it with many popular job board themes, and it works seamlessly without breaking your siteâs design.

**3. Can I search for jobs using partial words?**

Absolutely. If you enable the â[Partial Matches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites")â setting in SearchWP, candidates can find the right job even if they make a small typo, like searching for âenginierâ instead of âengineer.â

Creating a smooth job search experience is the best way to help your visitors find their next career move.

We hope this article helped you learn how to improve the job search experience in WP Job Manager. You may also want to see our guide on [how to search by custom fields in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") and [how to restrict search to specific post types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)").

If you are ready to upgrade your job board search, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP here").

### [How to Add a Reset Filter Button in WordPress (No Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-reset-filter-button-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** January 23, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâve visited online stores where you clicked a bunch of options to narrow down products, it feels great until you realize you selected the wrong size or color and canât figure out how to undo it.

The search results page gets stuck showing nothing, and there is no easy way to clear the selection. This usually forces users to hit the back button or refresh the page, which is very annoying.

The best fix is showing exactly which filters are active and giving users a simple button to reset everything.

In this guide, we will show you how to add a reset filter button in WordPress.

## Why Your Visitors Get Stuck Without a Reset Button?

Imagine walking down a grocery aisle and putting items in your cart, but then realizing you canât take them out.

That is exactly what it feels like for a user when they select a filter like âRedâ or âSmallâ and canât uncheck it easily.

We have noticed that when users select multiple options on a website, they often forget what they clicked a few moments ago.

They end up staring at a âNo results foundâ message and feel frustrated because they donât know how to fix it.

This confusion kills sales and makes people leave the sites we manage. By showing active filters, you are basically giving your visitors a map so they know exactly where they are.

Adding a reset button is like hitting a start over switch. It gives control back to the user so they can browse your content freely without the fear of getting stuck in a dead end.

That said, letâs look at a quick and easy way of adding a reset button to WordPress.

## Showing a Filter Reset Button in WordPress

The easiest way to let users control their search experience is by using a plugin like [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)WPFilters is the best filtering plugin for WordPress that makes managing search results effortless.

We love using it because it allows you to add helpful navigation tools without writing any code. Usually, you would need to hire a developer to show active filters or add a clear button.

But WPFilters handles these complex tasks automatically so you can focus on your content. It is built by the same team behind [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"), so we trust it to deliver accurate results every time.

Here are some things you can do with WPFilters:

- **Show Active Filters:** Display a list of selected criteria so users know exactly what they are viewing.
- **One-Click Reset:** Add a simple button that lets visitors clear all filters instantly.
- **Various Filter Types:** Choose from checkboxes, dropdowns, [range sliders](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-range-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Range Filters in WordPress (Easy Way)"), and radio buttons to suit your design.
- **Easy Integration:** Place your filters anywhere on your site using the block editor.

With that, letâs see how to set up active filters and a reset button on your website.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, you will need to visit the SearchWP website to get the WPFilters plugin.

When youâre done, simply go to your SearchWP dashboard and click the **Downloads** tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)Once you get to the Downloads tab, press the Download WPFilters button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

You should also copy your license key at the bottom left, as you will need it later in the setup process.

Next, you will need to install and activate WPFilters on your WordPress website. If you are not sure how to do that, then please see this beginnerâs guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, the welcome screen will appear along with the setup wizard. Simply click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)### Step 2: Create a New Reset Filter Element

To begin, you need to create a new element that will hold all your different filtering options.

Simply navigate to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from your WordPress dashboard and click the **Add New** button.

![Add a new filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-filter-1030x529.png)Next, you can type in a title for your filter set in the field provided at the top.

From here, youâll need to choose the specific type of filter you want to display. Go ahead and choose the **Reset** option under the Filter Type section.

![Select reset button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-reset-button-1030x574.png)After that, you can scroll down to the Container section.

Here, youâll see options to show the title of the filter and make it collapsible.

![Reset button container settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/reset-button-container-settings-1030x336.png)When youâre done, go ahead and scroll to the top.

From here, simply click the **Save** button to store your changes.

![Save the reset button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-the-reset-button-1030x535.png)### Step 4: Embed the Reset Button in WordPress

Now that youâve created the reset button, the last step is to add it to your website where youâve placed other filters.

The best part about using WPFilters is that you can easily embed the reset button anywhere, be it a specific page or the widget area.

If you want to add the filter reset button to a specific page, simply click the **Embed** button in the Element editor.

![Embed the reset button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-the-reset-button-1030x535.png)Next, a new popup window will open with different options for adding the filter element.

For instance, you can choose from a Gutenberg block, [shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"), and PHP code. After that, you can place the filter on a new page or an existing page.

![Embed search filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-filters--1030x492.png)Besides that, you can also embed the filter manually to a widget area of your site, like the sidebar.

For this tutorial, weâll add the reset button to the sidebar of our demo [online store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress") where there are other filters as well.

To do that, simply head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard. An important thing to remember is this step might differ depending on the WordPress theme youâre using.

Next, scroll down to the sidebar section and click the â+â sign to add the **WPFilters Element** widget block.

![Add WPFilters element widget block to WooCommerce sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-widget-block-to-woocommerce-sidebar-1030x562.png)Once thatâs done, youâll need to select the reset filter you created from the settings panel on the right.

Simply click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and pick your filter.

![Pick reset button filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/pick-reset-button-filter-1030x461.png)After that, you can click the **Update** button to save your changes.

The next step is to visit your website and test the reset button filter.

For example, hereâs how it works on our demo site:

![Reset filter button preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/reset-filter-button-preview.gif)As you can see, hitting the reset filters button removes all the selected filters. This is great way to quickly bring everything back to default with just a single click.

## FAQs about Reset Filters in WordPress

Here are some common questions our users ask about managing filters on their WordPress sites.

**1. Can I customize the look of the reset button?**

Yes, you can change how the button looks to match your brand. WPFilters is designed to work well with your active WordPress theme automatically. If you want specific colors or fonts, you can also use custom CSS.

**2. Does this work with WooCommerce products?**

Yes, these features work perfectly with WooCommerce. We have used WPFilters on many online stores to help shoppers navigate product categories and tags.

It ensures that customers can easily clear their size or color selections without reloading the page.

**3. Do I need to write code to add these features?**

No, you do not need to write any code. As we showed in this guide, WPFilters lets you add active filters and reset buttons using a simple block.

This saves you the cost and time of hiring a developer to build these functions for you.

We hope this article helped you learn how to show active filters and add a reset button in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add a search bar to your navigation menu](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") and [how to exclude specific pages from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress").

### [How to Create Custom Field Filters in WordPress (Step by Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-custom-field-filters-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** January 20, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Custom fields are a powerful way to display additional information on a WordPress site.

For instance, you can use them to store details like product SKU, color, recipe ingredients, or real estate pricing.

However, showing this data to visitors in a way they can interact with is often a challenge.

This is where creating a filter for your custom fields helps users to narrow down results and find exactly what they need. Fortunately, there is a simple way to let users sort content based on this custom data.

In this article, we will show you how to create custom field filters in WordPress.

## Why Are Custom Field Filters Important for Your Site?

We know from experience that [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") are great for storing extra data. But that data is useless if visitors cannot use it to sort content.

Imagine trying to buy a shirt online but having to click on every single product just to see if it comes in your size. That is exactly what happens when you donât have filters connected to your custom fields.

Letâs look at a real estate website as an example. You might use custom fields to store the number of bedrooms or the total square footage of a house.

Without a filter for those fields, a family looking for a three-bedroom home has to scroll through every single studio apartment listing.

The same applies to a food blog. We often see custom fields used for specific details like calorie counts or preparation time.

If a visitor wants a quick 30-minute meal, they shouldnât have to read through long slow-cooker recipes to find one.

Visitors want to find things quickly. If they cannot filter by [price](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-range-filters-in-wordpress/ "How to Create Range Filters in WordPress (Easy Way)"), location, or specific ingredients, they will likely leave your site.

Adding these specific filters bridges the gap between the additional data and the user experience. It turns a cluttered list into an organized catalog.

Now, letâs see how you can easily set up a custom fields filter in WordPress.

## Create Custom Field Filters in WordPress

The easiest method to add custom field filters is by using a plugin like [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)WPFilters is the most capable filtering plugin that is beginner-friendly to use and allows you to add Amazon-style filters to your WordPress site.

It lets you build custom filter sets based on [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-blog-posts-by-category-in-wordpress/ "How to Filter Blog Posts by Category in WordPress (Easy Way)"), tags, or custom data and place them anywhere on your site.

This allows your visitors to drill down through your content to find exactly what they need in seconds.

WPFilters is built by the same team behind [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). This gives us confidence that it will work perfectly with our search results.

With WPFilters, you can turn a simple text field into a checkbox, dropdown, range sliders, radio button, and more filters. This puts the power of your custom data right in the hands of your visitors.

Now, letâs see how you can use it to create custom field filters on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, you will need to head over to the SearchWP website to get your copy of WPFilters.

Once you sign up, log in to your account area and click on the **Downloads** tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)Here, click the **Download WPFilters** button to save the file to your computer. We also recommend copying your license key now so you have it ready for later.

Next, you need to upload and install the plugin on your site. If you are new to this, please refer to this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, the setup wizard will appear. Go ahead and click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button to complete the initial setup.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)### Step 2: Create a New Filter Element

To get started, simply navigate to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from your WordPress dashboard.

Then, click the **Add New** button to start building your filter.

![Add a new element in WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-element-in-wpfilters-1030x659.png)From here, you should give your filter a clear name at the top. This helps you keep track of things if you plan to make multiple filters later.

Next, look at the **Field Type** section. You can choose how you want the filter to look, such as checkboxes, a dropdown menu, a slider, or radio buttons.

![Create the type of filter you want](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-the-type-of-filter-you-want-1030x511.png)For example, a slider will work best if you want visitors to filter by price, a checkbox is great for sorting items using multiple options, or a dropdown for selecting a brand.

You can also allow users to filter by specific keywords using the search filter field.

### Step 3: Connect the Filter to a Custom Field

Now, you need to tell the filter where to get its information. To do that, simply scroll down to the **Data Source** section.

Then, click the dropdown menu and scroll to the **Custom Field** section. From here, you can choose which custom field youâd like to use to create a filter.

![Choose custom fields to filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-custom-fields-to-filter-1030x613.png)For example, you can pick price, color, brands, weight, length, and other product attributes.

WPFilters also works seamlessly with custom field plugins like [Advanced Custom Fields (ACF)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)"). You can easily create a filter for any of ACF on your site.

### Step 4: Customize the Filter Settings

After connecting the data source, you need to make the filter look good for visitors by customizing it.

You can start by scrolling down to the **Container** section to change how the filter box behaves.

For instance, enabling the âShow Titleâ option allows users to know what they are filtering, like âPrice Rangeâ or âIngredientsâ.

Or you can turn on the âCollapsibleâ option to let users hide the filter to save space. We also like using the âHorizontalâ option to place the choices side-by-side instead of in a long vertical list.

![Edit container settings for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-container-settings-for-filters-1030x428.png)Next, look at the **Items** section below it to control the specific choices in the list.

Here, you can enable âItem Countâ to show exactly how many results exist for each option, which helps set user expectations. We usually suggest keeping Show Empty turned off so your visitors donât waste time clicking on options that lead to zero results.

![Edit items settings for filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-items-settings-for-filters-1030x456.png)Once you are happy with how everything looks, simply scroll to the top and click the **Save** button at the top right.

You will also see a preview of the custom fields filter in the panel on the left.

![Save your custom fields filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-custom-fields-filter-1030x524.png)### Step 5: Display Custom Fields Filter on Your Site

The best thing about WPFilters is that you can put your new filter anywhere on your website.

Letâs look at how you can add the filter to a page and the sidebar of your site.

**1. Adding Custom Fields Filter to a Page**

The plugin offers a built-in embed option when creating the filter. Simply click the âEmbedâ button in the top right corner.

![Embed custom fields filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-custom-fields-filter-1030x524.png)Next, a popup window will open where you can choose from different embed options.

You can choose between the Gutenberg block, a [shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"), or PHP code if you are a developer.

![Embed search filters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-filters--1030x492.png)There is also an option to add the filter to an existing page or create a new one.

For example, letâs say you want to use the âGutenberg Blockâ and add it to a new page.

![Enter name for new filters page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-name-for-new-filters-page-1030x504.png)Next, you will need to enter the name of the new page and then click the âLetâs Goâ button.

Your custom fields filter will then be preloaded onto the new page, and you can view it in the WordPress content editor.

![Preview of custom fields filter in editor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/preview-of-custom-fields-filter-in-editor-1030x469.png)Next, you can click the âPublishâ button in the top right corner.

On the other hand, you can also place the WPFilters block manually on any page or post.

To start, simply open the page or post where you want the filter to appear, click the plus (+) button to add a new block, and search for the **WPFilters Element**.

![Add WPFilters block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-block-1030x536.png)Next, you will need to select the filter you just created from the Settings panel.

Simply click the dropdown menu under the âSelect Elementâ option and pick your filter.

![Select element filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-element-filter-1030x398.png)When youâre done, click the âSaveâ button.

**2. Adding Custom Fields Filter to the Sidebar**

You can also add the filter to the sidebar of your website. This is a great way of keeping filters visible to users so they can quickly sort and find the right product or content.

To start, you can head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard. Do note that this step might differ depending on the WordPress theme youâre using.

Next, scroll down to the sidebar section and click the â+â sign to add the **WPFilters Element** widget block.

![Add WPFilters element widget block to WooCommerce sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-widget-block-to-woocommerce-sidebar-1030x562.png)From here, you can select the custom fields filter you created from the settings panel on the right.

Simply click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and pick your filter.

![Select range filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-range-filter-1-1030x568.png)When youâre done, go ahead and click the **Update** button.

Now, visit the page to see the custom fields filter in action.

For example, hereâs how different custom field filters look on our demo online store.

![View custom fields filter in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-custom-fields-filter-in-woocommerce-1030x552.png)## **FAQs about Custom Field Filters**

**1. Can I use filters for Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) data?**

Yes, you definitely can. We use ACF on many of our sites, and WPFilters works perfectly with it.

**2. Can I add more than one custom field filter to a page?**

Absolutely. You can create separate filter elements for different fields, like one for âColorâ and another for âSize.â To make them more visible, you can stack them in your sidebar so users can narrow down results step-by-step.

**3. Do I need to know coding to set this up?**

Not at all. The main benefit of using WPFilters is that it handles the technical work for you. It helps you set up advanced filters just by clicking through the menus, without touching a single PHP file.

Custom field filters change how visitors interact with your data.

We hope this tutorial helped you learn how to create them in WordPress without any coding. You may also want to see our guides on [how to add live search to your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free") and [how to search by category in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

### [How to Create Range Filters in WordPress (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-range-filters-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** January 15, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Running a website with lots of data, like an online store or a real estate directory, often leads to cluttered search results.

We have noticed that visitors struggle to find specific items that fit within their budget or timeframe when there are too many options.

Without a way to narrow down these results, potential customers often get frustrated and leave the site. It is like trying to find a specific book in a library that isnât organized by genre or author.

Adding range filters solves this problem by allowing users to slide between a minimum and maximum value, such as price or dates. However, we found that most default WordPress setups do not offer this feature, and adding it usually requires complex coding.

In this article, we will show you how to create range filters in WordPress.

## Why Offer Range Filters to Your Visitors?

Before we get to the tutorial, letâs talk about how range filters improve the user experience on your site.

We have seen that visitors who cannot filter by price or date often abandon their search quickly.

Imagine walking into a shoe store and having to look at every single pair, regardless of your size or budget. That is exactly how your visitors feel when they have to scroll through irrelevant products on your website.

Range filters act like a helpful shop assistant that immediately shows items fitting specific criteria.

We found that this small addition keeps visitors engaged for much longer because they see exactly what they can afford or need.

Whether it is filtering a [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search") price range or selecting dates for a booking, this feature saves time.

It clears the clutter and helps your customers make decisions faster, which often leads to more sales for you.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily add a range filter to your WordPress website.

## Adding a Range Filter in WordPress

The easiest way to add range filters to your WordPress site is by using a plugin like [WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/ "WPFilters").

[![WPFilters - Best WordPress Filtering Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-1030x620.png)](https://searchwp.com/product/wpfilters/)WPFilters is the best filtering plugin for WordPress that makes it simple to sort content. You can use it to give visitors precise control over what they see without writing any code.

It works perfectly with popular [eCommerce plugins](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads") like WooCommerce and Easy Digital Downloads.

Using the plugin, you can create all types of filters for tags, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-blog-posts-by-category-in-wordpress/ "How to Filter Blog Posts by Category in WordPress (Easy Way)"), custom fields, and other data types. The best part is that WPFilters offers multiple data fields for adding filters to your site.

Which means that you can easily set up price sliders, date ranges, and numerical filters that look great on any device.

Here are some things you can do with WPFilters:

- **Filter by price:** Let customers set a minimum and maximum budget for their shopping.
- **Filter by custom fields:** Use numerical data from your posts to create specific range sliders.
- **Improve search results:** Help users find exactly what they need by narrowing options significantly.
- **Customize the look**: Display the filter title, make it collapsible, and arrange it horizontally.

With that, letâs see how you can use WPFilters to create a range filter on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate WPFilters

First, you will need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a WPFilters account.

Once you have done that, simply head to your SearchWP account area and go to the âDownloadsâ tab.

![Download WPFilters plugin zip files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-wpfilters-plugin-zip-files-1030x618.png)Next, you can click the **Download WPFilters** button and save the plugin files on your computer.

While you are on the downloads page, we also recommend copying the license key, as you will need it to set up the plugin.

Now, the next step is to upload the WPFilters plugin files to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activating the plugin, you will see the WPFilters setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âLetâs Get Startedâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![WPFilters setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpfilters-setup-wizard-1030x587.png)### Step 2: Add a Range Filter Element

Now that the plugin is active, it is time to build your first range filter for WordPress.

To start, head to **WPFilters Â» Elements** from the WordPress dashboard and simply click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new element in WPFilters](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-element-in-wpfilters-1030x659.png)After that, you can start by entering a name for your filter at the top. You can use a specific name that helps you stay organized when there are multiple filters on a site.

Next, go ahead and select the **Slider** option under the Filter Fields section. This specific element creates the sliding bar that your visitors will use to select their minimum and maximum values.

![Select slider filter field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-slider-filter-field-1030x587.png)Once you have selected the filter type, scroll down to the **Data Source** section. Here, you can choose exactly what data determines the range values.

For example, if you are running a store, then you can select Product Price or total in-stock items as a source.

![Choose range data source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-range-data-source-1030x562.png)After selecting your data, you can customize how the slider looks to match your site design.

Simply scroll down to the âContainerâ section to adjust the display settings. For instance, you can show a label title to the filter.

![Edit container settings for range filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-container-settings-for-range-filter-1030x639.png)When you are satisfied with the appearance, scroll to the top of the page.

Then, click the Save button to store your changes.

![Save range filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-range-filter-1030x625.png)### Step 3: Add the Range Filter to Your Site

The best part about using WPFilters is that you can embed your filters anywhere on your site with ease.

To get started, simply click the âEmbedâ button on the Element page.

![Click embed button in element](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-embed-button-in-element-1030x625.png)Next, you will see a popup window with different options.

You can choose between the Gutenberg block, a shortcode, or PHP code if you are a developer.

![Embed search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-results-page-1030x494.png)Besides that, you can also manually add the WPFilters Gutenberg block to the sidebar and other areas on your website.

For this tutorial, we will add the range filter to the sidebar of your demo WooCommerce site.

To do that, simply head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from your WordPress dashboard. Do note that this step might differ depending on the WordPress theme youâre using.

Next, scroll down to the sidebar section and click the â+â sign to add the **WPFilters Element** widget block.

![Add WPFilters element widget block to WooCommerce sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wpfilters-element-widget-block-to-woocommerce-sidebar-1030x562.png)Now, youâll need to select the range filter you created from the settings panel on the right.

Simply click the âSelect Elementâ dropdown menu and pick your filter.

![Select range filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-range-filter-1-1030x568.png)When youâre done, go ahead and click the **Update** button at the top.

From here, you can visit your website to see the range filter in action.

![Range filter preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/range-filter-preview-1030x549.png)By sliding the slider, your visitors can filter products based on prices.

## FAQs about Creating Range Filters in WordPress

Here are some common questions we get about setting up range filters in WordPress.

**1. What types of data can I filter with a range slider?**

You can filter almost any numerical data. We often use range sliders for product prices, booking dates, or measurements like weight and size on directory sites.

**2. Does this work with WooCommerce products?**

Yes, it integrates perfectly with WooCommerce. We found that adding a price range slider helps customers find affordable products much faster, which can increase conversion rates.

**3. Can I add multiple range filters to one page?**

Absolutely. You can stack filters to let users sort by price and user rating simultaneously. This creates a very precise search experience for your visitors.

We hope this article helped you learn how to create range filters in WordPress. You may also want to see our guides on [how to filter WooCommerce products by tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-tags/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Tags (Step-by-Step)") and [how to filter WooCommerce products by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-filter-woocommerce-products-by-category/ "How to Filter WooCommerce Products by Category (Step-by-Step)").

### [9 Best WordPress Analytics Plugins [Compared]](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-analytics-plugins/)

**Published:** January 13, 2026
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

We still remember staring at our WordPress dashboard many years ago, completely confused about why our website traffic had suddenly dropped.

We had no idea which blog posts were actually being read, where our visitors were coming from, or what they were doing once they landed on our site.

Without proper analytics, you are basically flying blind. Imagine spending hours writing content but having no clue if anyone was reading it or if they were leaving after a few seconds.

Thatâs where choosing the right analytics plugin is essential for understanding our audience and growing our website. After extensive testing, we uncovered that MonsterInsights, SearchWP, and AIOSEO are the best options to choose from.

In this article, weâve handpicked 9 popular analytics plugins for WordPress and compared their features to help you choose the right option.

Hereâs a quick overview of our top picks:

\#**Analytics Plugins****Best For****Starting Price**1.MonsterInsightsBeginners & non-technical users$99.50/year2.SearchWPInternal search tracking$99/year3.All in One SEO (AIOSEO)SEO-focused analytics$49.50/year4.ExactMetricsSmall business owners$99.50/year5.AnalytifyVisual data presentation$99/year6.GA Google AnalyticsFree basic trackingFree7.10WebAnalyticsBusiness growth$85/year8.Enhanced Ecommerce Google AnalyticsWooCommerce stores$139/year9.WP StatisticsCookie-free trackingFree + $119/year## Why Should You Use an Analytics Plugin?

Before we get to the plugin overviews, letâs talk about why you should use an analytics plugin on your WordPress site.

The analytics plugin allows you to automatically collect valuable data about your visitors, which you can further use to improve your marketing strategy.

Namely, using such a kind of plugin, you can:

- Get insights into your audience demographics, geography, and interests
- Figure out how people find your site and what you can do to increase your traffic
- Learn what type of content on your site is most popular
- See how many clicks your links get and what their click-through rate is
- Increase your conversion rate by breaking down your customersâ journey into steps

In addition, analytics plugins usually allow you to link your site to Google Analytics without editing your theme code.

Itâs handy for beginners and helps you start collecting data straight away.

Now, letâs see how we tested each plugin and bring you the best options.

## How We Test and Review Analytics Plugins

When testing WordPress analytics plugins, we focus on what really matters for small business owners and WordPress users. We need to know if a plugin actually helps you understand your visitors without making things complicated.

We install each analytics plugin on real WordPress websites and run them for several weeks. This gives us time to see how accurate the data is, how the plugin affects site speed, and whether the reports actually make sense.

Our testing process looks at ease of setup because nobody wants to spend hours configuring an analytics plugin.

We also check how quickly we can connect to Google Analytics, whether the dashboard is easy to read, and if the reports provide us actionable information we can actually use.

With that, letâs move on to the 9 most popular WordPress analytics plugins.

### [1. MonsterInsights â Best WordPress Analytics Plugin for Beginners](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ "MonsterInsights")

[![MonsterInsights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/monsterinsights-site-1030x577.png)](https://www.monsterinsights.com/)[MonsterInsights](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ "MonsterInsights") is one of the most powerful WordPress analytics plugins on the market. Weâve been using MonsterInsights on several of our websites for years now.

It takes all that complex Google Analytics data and presents it right inside your WordPress dashboard in a way that actually makes sense. You donât need to be a data analyst to understand where your traffic comes from or which posts are performing best.

Plus, it allows you to [connect your site to Google Analytics](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/how-to-install-google-analytics-in-wordpress/ "How to Install Google Analytics in WordPress for Beginners") and properly configure it in a few easy steps without editing your theme code.

MonsterInsights lets you to track almost everything on your site and see the complete picture of your business performance.

For example, you can get detailed reports on your traffic, the conversion rate of your landing pages, the click-through rate of your links, and more.

The best part is that you donât even have to leave your site to view these reports, as MonsterInsights displays them in your WordPress dashboard.

![MonsterInsights dashboard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/MonsterInsights-dashboard.png)Itâs convenient for eCommerce site owners since they can immediately see comprehensive data on their sales.

Namely, they can track their revenue, conversion rate, number of transactions, top conversion sources, and other vital metrics.

More than 3 million website owners already use MonsterInsights to collect data about their online performance and grow their business.

**Features**:

- **Beginner-friendly**. You can effectively use MonsterInsights to grow your business regardless of your WordPress knowledge and digital marketing skills.
- **Easy GA integration**. Connect your site to Google Analytics in just a few clicks without making any changes to your WordPress theme code.
- **Real-time analytics**. Get instant reports on what visitors are doing on your site, where they came from, and moreâall within your WordPress dashboard.
- **eCommerce reports**. See the full picture of your customersâ journey from entering your site to making a purchase with enhanced eCommerce tracking.
- **Affiliate links tracking**. Increase your affiliate income by gathering information about how many clicks your affiliate links get, how clickable they are, and more.
- **Forms tracking**. Automatically track sign-up, contact, lead, and any other forms on your site to increase your conversion rate.
- **EU Compliance**. Helps you comply with privacy laws like GDPR, CCPA, and more.

**Our Verdict:** MonsterInsights is our top pick for most WordPress users, especially if youâre new to analytics or donât want to deal with Google Analytics directly. The plugin makes understanding your website data simple and actionable.

**Pricing**: The basic version is free. The Pro version, which comes with extended reports, enhanced tracking, and a set of growth tools, starts from $99.50/year for 1 site.

### [2. SearchWP â Best Plugin for Internal Search Analytics](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")

[![SearchWP - Best WordPress Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the best [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that helps you deliver more relevant search results to your visitors.

Most website owners focus on traffic from Google but completely ignore what visitors search for once theyâre on the site. This is a huge missed opportunity because internal search data tells you exactly what your audience wants to find.

Even though itâs not a classic analytics plugin and is designed to boost your site search, it comes with a powerful Metrics extension.

Using this extension, you can track your visitorsâ searches, see which pages get a higher click-through rate in search results, and more.

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)By analyzing this data, you can get new content ideas, find out whatâs missing from your site, and see whether it has duplicate content.

In addition, using SearchWP, you can make the search on your site way smarter, allowing you to increase your pageviews and boost your sales.

**Features**:

- [**Search tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"). Track your visitorsâ search queries to better understand their needs, find out what content is most popular on your site, and more.
- **Dashboard widget**. See all important metrics when you log in to your site, as the Metrics extension comes with an informative dashboard widget.
- **Customize data**. Choose which search engines and metrics you want to see, control the date range, search terms, and other data that will be displayed.
- **Automatic theme integration**. SearchWP automatically integrates with every search form on your site, so you can start tracking searches right away.

**Our Verdict:** SearchWP is perfect if you run a product or content heavy website where visitors use search frequently. The analytics data helps you understand what your audience wants and shows you exactly what content you should create next.

**Pricing**: Starting from $99/year for 1 site.

### [3. All in One SEO (AIOSEO) â Best Plugin for SEO-Focused Tracking](https://aioseo.com/ "All in One SEO (AIOSEO)")

[![All in One SEO (AIOSEO)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/aioseo-website-1030x586.png)](https://aioseo.com/)[All in One SEO (AIOSEO)](https://aioseo.com/ "All in One SEO (AIOSEO)") is the #1 WordPress SEO plugin on the market, with over 3 million active users.

The main purpose of this plugin is to help you optimize your site for better ranking in search engines, but it also has a couple of powerful analytics features.

Weâve been using All in One SEO (AIOSEO) on multiple websites, and its analytics features give us exactly what we need to improve our search rankings. The plugin combines traditional analytics with SEO-specific data, showing us not just how many visitors we get but how our content performs in search results.

During testing, we could see which keywords were bringing in traffic, how our posts ranked for specific search terms, and which pages needed SEO improvements. This connection between analytics and SEO strategy proved incredibly valuable.

## Which is the Best Google Analytics Plugin for WordPress?

After weâve taken a look at the 9 most popular WordPress analytics plugins, itâs time to pick a winner.

Given the number of features, ease of use, and value for money, we believe that [MonsterInsights](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ "MonsterInsights") is the best WordPress analytics plugin.

It makes it easy to integrate your site with Google Analytics and properly set it up regardless of your technical skills.

Plus, the built-in Google Analytics dashboard allows you to take a look at advanced metrics of your site without leaving your site.

MonsterInsights is perfect for staying on top of your key SEO metrics. Using it, you can see how users engage with your site, which links they click, how you can improve your content, and more.

It also comes with advanced eCommerce tracking, allowing you to see your conversion rate, average order value, best-selling products, and other useful metrics in real-time.

By analyzing this data, you can reduce cart abandonment on your site and boost your sales. MonsterInsight also has an impressive base of addons that further expand its features.

For example, the EU Compliance addon allows you to automatically comply with data protection laws, which is handy and saves time.

## FAQs About Best Analytics Plugins for WordPress

**1. What is a WordPress analytics plugin?**

A WordPress analytics plugin helps you track and understand your website visitors. It shows you how many people visit your site, which pages they view, where they come from, and what actions they take. Most analytics plugins either connect to Google Analytics or provide their own tracking system directly in WordPress.

**2. Do I need Google Analytics if I use an analytics plugin?**

It depends on the plugin you choose. Some plugins, like MonsterInsights and ExactMetrics, require a Google Analytics account because they display Google Analytics data in WordPress. Other plugins like WP Statistics work independently and donât need Google Analytics at all. We recommend starting with Google Analytics because itâs free and provides comprehensive data.

**3. Can I use multiple analytics plugins at the same time?**

Technically yes, but we donât recommend it. Running multiple analytics plugins can slow down your site and may cause tracking conflicts that make your data inaccurate. Choose one main analytics solution and stick with it. If you need specific features from different plugins, look for an all-in-one solution that offers everything you need.

**4. How do I know if my analytics plugin is tracking correctly?**

After installing an analytics plugin, visit your website in a different browser or incognito mode. Then check your analytics dashboard to see if the visit was recorded. Most plugins show real-time visitor data, so you should see yourself appear within a few minutes. You can also use browser extensions or Google Tag Assistant to verify the tracking code is firing properly.

We hope this article helped you pick the best WordPress analytics plugin. You may also want to see our list of [best WordPress page builders for newbies](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/ "6 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)") and [the best affiliate marketing plugins for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-affiliate-marketing-plugins-for-wordpress/ "5 Best Affiliate Marketing Plugins for WordPress (Compared)").
