Next, you can also edit the attributes for each source. These are additional details youâd like to include in the search process, like title, content, slug, excerpt, and author.

To get started, simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the search source section.

![Change downloads attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-downloads-attributes-1030x423.png)From here, you can add taxonomies such as categories and tags for your source.

Similarly, if youâre using custom fields, then you can also include them in the search process.

![Add download categories and custom fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-download-categories-and-custom-fields-1030x550.png)Once youâre finished, click the Done button.

Lastly, you will need to scroll to the top and **Save** your engine.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)For more information, please see our detailed guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 3: Create a Custom Search Form in SearchWP

Now that youâve customized the search engine, you can also create a custom search form in SearchWP.

To create a search form, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can create or edit a form, customize its layout and features, and select your newly created engine.

![Add a new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-search-form-1030x533.png)From here, you can select theme for your search form.

SearchWP offers multiple pre-built templates to choose from and offers lots of customization options.

![How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress: Create Custom Search Form: Choosing Layout Theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/naming-search-form-1030x644.jpg)To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### Step 4: Add Search Box Using Beaver Builder

Now, the next step is to add your SearchWP form to a landing page using Beaver Builder.

Simply go to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard and edit a page or add a new one.

Once youâre in the WordPress content editor, simply click the **Convert to Beaver Builder** button.

![Click convert to Beaver Builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-convert-to-beaver-builder-1030x498.png)This will launch the drag and drop page builder by Beaver Builder.

Go ahead and click the + button in the top right corner to add modules.

![Click the plus button in Beaver Builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-plus-button-in-Beaver-Builder-1030x490.png)Next, you can scroll down to the SearchWP section.

From here, simply drag the **Search Form** module and place it onto your page.

![Add search form module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-form-module-1030x719.png)After that, you will need to select your search form.

Go ahead and click the dropdown menu and select the form you created in the previous step.

![Choose your search form in Beaver Builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-search-form-in-Beaver-Builder-1-1030x534.png)When youâre done, simply click the **Save** button.

Now simply click the âDoneâ button in the top right corner and then hit **Publish**.

![Publish Beaver Builder landing page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish-Beaver-Builder-landing-page-1030x550.png)Thatâs it! Youâve successfully created a search form and added it to a landing page using Beaver Builder.

You can now visit your site to see it in action.

![View beaver builder search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-beaver-builder-search-form-1030x616.png)If you want, you can also test the search form and see if it is working according to your requirements.

For example, is it finding the right products or download files, showing content from specified categories, and more.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a search box using Beaver Builder in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add a search box in WPBakery page builder](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-in-wpbakery-page-builder/ "How to Add a Search Box in WPBakery Page Builder") and [how to enable search by WooCommerce product tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search by Product Tags in WooCommerce (Fastest Way)").

### [How to Limit Search Results to WooCommerce Products Only (Easy)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-results-to-woocommerce-products-only/)

**Published:** October 16, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâre running a WooCommerce store but also have other content on the site, then it can be hard for your visitors to find the right products.

But what if you could limit search results to WooCommerce products only?

Itâll help you to clean up your search results from redundant pages, allow your visitors to find the products theyâre looking for faster, and increase your sales.

In this article, weâll show you how to limit search on your site to WooCommerce products in a few minutes, with no coding needed.

## Why Limit Search Results to Products on Your Site?

Before we get to the tutorial, letâs talk about how limiting WooCommerce search to products can benefit your business.

### 1. Get More Sales

First, it helps your customers to find the right products faster, which directly affects the number of your sales.

By default, WordPress performs searches among titles, content, and [excerpts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-excerpts-on-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Display Excerpts on the WordPress Search Results Page") of all posts and pages published on your site.

Not surprisingly, your site visitors see a lot of redundant search results when searching for a product.

Thatâs especially true if your online store has a blog where you publish company news, featured deals, product manuals, and other relevant content.

### 2. Build Customer Loyalty

Secondly, having an accurate search on your site is crucial to building a loyal customer base.

Visitors whoâve had a positive search experience on your site are more likely to return when they need to purchase something else.

Thatâs why itâs essential to make it as easy as possible to find the right product in your store if you want to succeed online in the long run.

After youâve learned why it makes sense to limit your [WooCommerce store search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") to products, letâs talk about how you can do that on your site.

### 3. Improve Product Visibility

Finally, limiting search on your site to only products allows you to boost product visibility across your site and also better optimize it for ranking in search engines.

If your visitors can easily find on your site what theyâre looking for and have a positive user experience, itâs a positive signal for Google to rank your site higher.

With that, letâs see how you can limit search on your site to WooCommerce products with no coding needed.

## How to Limit Search Results to WooCommerce Products Only

The easiest way to limit your siteâs search to WooCommerce products is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the best search plugin for WordPress that has seamless integration with WooCommerce.

Using it, you can enhance search in your store to make it easier for your customers to find the product theyâre looking for.

For example, SearchWP allows you to [make product SKUs searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-search-by-sku-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Search by SKU Not Working (Step by Step)"), display search results in live mode, enable fuzzy search, and many more.

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners already use SearchWP to give their visitors more relevant search results and grow their business.

Here are some other amazing SearchWP features:

- **[Manage the order of search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)")**: Customize the order of search results in your WooCommerce store to promote the best-selling products.
- **[Exclude specific products from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce")**. Clean up search results on your site by excluding from search redundant or outdated products.
- [**Track searches of your customers**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Look at what visitors are searching for on your site to find out which products are most popular and come up with new product ideas.
- [**Add a search form to your shop page**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/ "How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page"): Enhance your shop page by placing a search form on it to get additional pageviews and sales.

With that, letâs see how you can use SearchWP to limit search results on your site to WooCommerce products only.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP on Your Site

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and [sign up for a new account](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today").

When youâre done, simply go to your SearchWP dashboard and click the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once you get to the Downloads tab, press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

Weâll also recommend that you copy your license key in the bottom left corner when youâve finished downloading, as youâll need it later in the setup process.

Next, youâll need to install and activate SearchWP on your WordPress website. Not sure how to do that? Check out this beginnerâs guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, the welcome screen will appear along with the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After setting up SearchWP, youâre ready to move to the next step and install the WooCommerce Integration extension.

### Step 2: Install the WooCommerce Integration Extension

This extension ensures seamless SearchWP integration with WooCommerce, allowing you to fully customize the way search works in your store.

To install it, simply navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard.

![Installing the WooCommerce Integration extension for SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension-1030x464.jpg)Next, you will need to find the **WooCommerce Integration** extension and click Install under its section.

After that, youâre ready to start customizing your search engine to make it search only among WooCommerce products.

### Step 3: Limit Your Search to WooCommerce Product Only

To get started, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard.

 click the **SearchWP** button on the left side of your WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can customize the search engine by selecting which sources to include, or which additional information like custom fields and tags to make searchable.

For example, you can enable search by WooCommerce product tags, [hide specific WooCommerce categories from search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-category-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Hide Category from Search in WooCommerce"), and many more.

To limit search on your site to WooCommerce products, youâll need to add them as a search source and then remove all other search sources.

To get started, simply click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, you will see a popup window that will have all the default sources, such as Posts, Pages, and Media.

Go ahead and check the box next to the **Products** search source to make it active, and uncheck all other sources.

![Only select product as source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/only-select-product-as-source-1030x500.png)When finished, click **Done** to save your changes.

You can go a step further and also select product attributes in the search process. For example, you can make product details like SKUs, color, size, categories, tags, and more searchable using SearchWP.

To start, simply scroll down to the Products section and click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)A new popup window will now open where you can select product attributes. By default, the title, content, slug, excerpt, and author attributes will be selected.

You can simply click the âCustom Fieldsâ dropdown menu to add additional product details and the âTaxonomiesâ dropdown menu to add tags and categories to the search process.

![How to Add WooCommerce Product Search Widget: customize attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-product-attribute-1030x567.jpg)When youâre finished, go ahead and click the **Done** button.

The last step is to save your search engine. You can do that by pressing the **Save** button in the upper right corner.

![Save product search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-product-search-engine-1030x481.png)Thatâs it. Now search on your site is limited to WooCommerce products.

Letâs test it to make sure that everything works as it should.

### Step 4: Test Your New Product Search

To do that, go to your site and type some search query into the search field.

For example, letâs say we sell tea on our test site and have several blog posts related to various tea topics.

Letâs type âgreen teaâ into the search and see what results we get.

![make a search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-a-search.png)As you can see, we only have products among our search results.

It means that we have successfully limited search on our site to WooCommerce products, and now nothing will distract our customers from shopping.

![how to limit search results to WooCommerce products only](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-limit-search-results-to-WooCommerce-products-only.png)## Bonus: Create a Custom Search Widget for WooCommerce

Now that youâve customized the WooCommerce search to only look for products, you can also set up custom widgets for your store.

SearchWP offers prebuilt search form templates that you can link to your custom search engine.

This way, you get full control over the appearance of the form and also power it with the search engine you just customized in SearchWP.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)For more details, please see our guide on [how to add custom WooCommerce search widgets](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to limit search results on your site to WooCommerce products only. You may also want to see our guides on [how to make a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") and [how to search by WooCommerce product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

Ready to improve your WooCommerce search? You can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP Today").

### [How to Create a Search Form in Breakdance (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-form-in-breakdance/)

This is where using SearchWP can transform the search experience completely. Plus, it easily integrates with Breakdance, making it very easy to add custom search forms.

In this guide, weâll show you how we set up powerful search forms in Breakdance using SearchWP.

## What is Breakdance?

Breakdance is a popular visual page builder plugin for WordPress. It lets you create custom websites without writing any code, using a drag-and-drop interface that feels intuitive and responsive.

What sets Breakdance apart from other page builders is its focus on clean code output and performance.

The plugin comes with a comprehensive set of design elements, from basic text and image blocks to advanced features like dynamic content, animations, and form builders.

We particularly appreciate how it handles responsive design automatically, ensuring our sites look great on all devices.

Breakdance also integrates well with popular WordPress plugins and themes. This includes SearchWP, which allows you to enhance the search process on your site.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily include a search form in Breakdance builder.

## Adding a Search Form in WordPress Using Breakdance

Setting up a powerful search form in Breakdance is easier than you might think. And the best way to go about it is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

It is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") thatâs trusted by over 50,000 business owners to enhance their website search.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)With SearchWP, you can overcome the limitations of the default search in WordPress.

For instance, you get to include important details in custom fields, categories, tags, [product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), and more in the search process.

Besides Breakdance, SearchWP also supports [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)"), [Divi](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-divi-search-not-working/ "How to Fix Divi Search Not Working in WordPress (4 Solutions)"), [WPBakery](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-in-wpbakery-page-builder/ "How to Add a Search Box in WPBakery Page Builder"), Oxygen, and other website builders.

- **Provide live search suggestions:** Show visitors instant results as they type, allowing them to find the right products more efficiently and improving their experience.
- **Customize the order of your results:** Control the ranking of search results to feature specific items and align them with your business goals.
- **Hide specific content from search:** Prevent certain pages or products from appearing in your search results, ensuring a clean and focused customer journey.

Now, you can follow these steps to easily customize your website search, create a search form, and display it using Breakdance.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, you will need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a new account.

Now, the next step is to install SearchWP on your site. To do that, youâll need to upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

If you need help, then please follow this [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step guide](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Edit and Customize the Search Engine

With SearchWP, you can improve your site search by customizing the search engine.

A search engine contains a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing a search. You can edit it to include additional information.

For example, there are options to make documents like [PDFs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), product details like color, size, [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), categories like tutorials, and other details in the search process.

First, youâll need to go **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a small window will open where you can choose sources for your engine.

By default, SearchWP will include Posts, Pages, and Media as sources. But if you have an [eCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads (2025)"), then you can include Products or Downloads as a source.

![Include downloads in the search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-downloads-in-the-search-engine-1030x501.png)When youâre done, simply click the **Done** button.

Another benefit of using SearchWP is that you can further customize the search by editing source attributes.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, you can add any additional information stored in custom fields in the search process. For example, if you have a food blog, then you can add custom fields like ingredients.

Similarly, there is also an option to make [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce") and tags searchable. All you have to do is click the Taxonomies dropdown menu.

Do note that SearchWP will include title, content, slug, excerpt, and [author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (Simple Steps)") as attributes by default.

![Post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/post-attributes-2-1030x506.png)Go ahead and click the **Done** button when youâre finished.

Now, you can scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)For more details about customizing the search engine, you may also want to see our guide on [how to change the default WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 3: Create a Search Form in SearchWP

Now that youâve customized the search engine, you can also create a custom search form in SearchWP.

First, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard and then create a new form or edit an existing one.

![Select a search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-a-search-form-1030x518.png)On the next screen, you can select a theme for your form.

SearchWP offers multiple search form templates that you can use for your site.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)With SearchWP, you can link each form to a specific search engine.

This allows you to create unlimited search forms and display them in different areas of your site.

![Select new engine for form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-new-engine-for-form-1030x484.png)To learn more, please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### Step 4: Adding a Search Form in Breakdance

The next step is to add the search form you just created to a page using the Breakdance [page builder](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/ "6 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)").

Simply head to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard. From here, you can edit an existing page or add a new one.

![add a new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-page-1-1030x484.png)Next, youâll see the content editor in WordPress.

From here, you can click the **Edit in Breakdance** button.

![Click edit with Breakdance](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-edit-with-breakdance-1030x497.png)On the next screen, you will see the Breakdance editor.

To add a new element, simply click the **+** sign on the page. After that, you can select the **SearchWP Form** element from the menu on the left under the âSiteâ group.

![Add SearchWP form element in breakdance](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-form-element-in-breakdance-1030x657.png)Once the SearchWP Form element is added, you can select your search from.

Go ahead and click the dropdown menu under SearchWP Form settings in the panel on the left.

![Select your form in Breakdance](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-form-in-breakdance-1030x524.png)When youâre done, simply click the **Save** button at the top.

After that, you can click close button (X icon) and then click the **Exit to Frontend** option.

![Save form and exit to frontend](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-form-and-exit-to-frontend-1030x491.png)This will take you to the page you just created with Breakdance to display the search form.

Hereâs a preview of how the search form looks like on our demo site.

We hope this article helped you learn how to create a search form in Breakdance. ### [How to Search by Product Tags in WooCommerce (Fastest Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** October 9, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâre running a WooCommerce store, then youâll probably have tags to organize your products.

But did you know that the default search on your site completely ignores them when performing a search?

By making tags searchable, you give your customers an extra way to search for products in your store and help them find what theyâre looking for faster.

In this guide, weâll show you the fastest way to enable search by WooCommerce product tags on your site with no coding needed.

## Why Add Search by Product Tags

Allowing your visitors to search by product tags is helpful for several reasons.

First, tags are a great source of additional information about your products.

They are ideal for placing such data as product material, type, style, size, weight, and many other valuable attributes.

So, by making this data searchable, you can help your visitors discover your store and increase your sales.

Secondly, searching by tags is simply convenient.

For example, you can create a product collection and add a unique tag for it so that customers can find products from that collection faster.

With that, letâs see how you can make WooCommerce product tags searchable on your website.

## How to Enable Search by WooCommerce Product Tags on Your Site

The easiest way to make WooCommerce product tags searchable on your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the best WordPress search plugin used by over 50,000 website owners worldwide.

Thanks to seamless integration with WooCommerce, you can use the full power of SearchWP to customize your store search.

For instance, you can [make product attributes searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), limit search to specific product categories, [track searches of your customers](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), and many more.

And this is just a short list of SearchWP features. Here are some other things you can do with this plugin:

- [**Enable search by product SKUs**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"): Let your customers search for products by SKU to help them find what theyâre looking for faster.
- **[Exclude specific products from search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce")**: Clean up your search results by hiding redundant products to make it easier for visitors to find the right products.
- [**Restrict product search to titles only**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/ "How To Restrict WooCommerce Product Search To Title Only"): Make WordPress only consider product titles when performing searches to reduce the amount of clutter in search results.
- **[Add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)")**: Create an additional search engine, customize it to your needs, and link it to the search widget on your site.

Now, letâs see how you can use it to enable search by WooCommerce product tags.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP on Your Site

To begin, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and [grab a copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP ").

After that, you can log in to your SearchWP account and click the **Downloads** tab in the dashboard.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, youâll need to press the **Download SearchWP** button to download the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, we recommend to also copy your SearchWP license key in the lower left corner.

Now, the next step is to install and activate the SearchWP plugin on your WordPress website. If you need help, then please check out this detailed guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once the plugin is active, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After activating the plugin on your site, the next step is to install the WooCommerce extension in SearchWP.

### Step 2: Install WooCommerce Integration Extension

By installing this extension, you ensure seamless integration of SearchWP with WooCommerce.

To get started, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard.

From here, you can find the **WooCommerce Integration** extension and click **Install**.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)After youâve installed and activated the extension, youâre ready to start customizing your search engines to enable search by WooCommerce product tags.

### Step 3: Add Products as a Search Source

To get started, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard.

This is where you can manage your search engines created with this plugin. As you can see, there is already the **Default** search engine, which SearchWP automatically creates after installation.

You can simply customize this engine or add new ones as well. For instance, you can limit your search to WooCommerce products only, put specific products at the top of your search results, and more.

To let your customers search for WooCommerce products by tags, the first step is to add products as a search source.

First, youâll need to click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a new window will open where you can manage sources for your engine. For instance, the default sources will include Posts, Pages, and Media.

Go ahead and check the checkbox next to the **Products** search source to make it active.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, click **Done** to save your changes.

### Step 4: Make Product Tags Searchable

The next step is to add the **Product tags** taxonomy as a product attribute that SearchWP pays attention to when performing a search.

To get started, simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the **Product** search source section.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)After that, youâll see a new window popup, where you can manage post attributes, like title, content, slug, excerpt, and author.

From here, you can click on the **Taxonomies** field and choose the **Product tags** option from the dropdown menu.

![Select product tags](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-tags--1030x625.png)When youâre finished managing your taxonomies, click **Done** to save the changes.

The last step is to save your search engine. To do that, press the **Save** button.

![Save product search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-product-search-engine-1030x481.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully enabled search by product tags on your site.

Letâs test your new search to make sure it works as itâs supposed to.

### Step 5: Test Your New Search by Product Tag

To get started, go to your website and search for any product you have in your store by its tag.

For example, on our test site, we have a âGreen hoodieâ product with the âWomenswearâ tag.

Letâs see if we can find this product by the tag only.

![click Find Search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Search.png)As you can see, we successfully found our product, even though there is no word âwomenswearâ in its title or description.

It means that WordPress now considers product tags, and visitors can use them to find the product they want.

![we found our product by the tag](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-product-by-the-tag.png)## Bonus: Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working

When your WooCommerce product search suddenly stops working, it can feel like a nightmare, especially when you have thousands of items in your store.

If customers canât find what theyâre looking for, theyâll likely leave, which is the last thing any online store owner wants. The tricky part is that there isnât just one single cause for a broken search.

The problem could be anything from a conflict with another plugin, a server memory limit issue, or even a corrupted database after a large product import using a CSV file.

![Nothing found results preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/nothoing-found.jpg)The good news is that most of these issues are fixable. You can find all the details in our guide on [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working").

We hope this article helped you learn how to enable search by WooCommerce product tags. You may also want to see our guide on [how to make a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") and [how to hide category from search in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-category-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

If youâre ready to empower your WooCommerce search, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today").

### [6 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/)

**Published:** October 7, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Are you looking for the best WordPress page builder plugin for newbies?

Page builders allow you to create beautiful and modern pages for your site within minutes, no coding needed.

And to improve your WordPress search, you can use a page builder to add a powerful search widget to your website.

Since there are tons of plugins available, we picked the best page builders for WordPress and compared their features in this post to help you get started.

Letâs dive into it.

**Page Builders****Best For****Key Feature**1.[SeedProd](https://www.seedprod.com/ "SeedProd")Most Powerful Website and Page BuilderDrag-and-drop builder with smart sections & AI-powered2.[Thrive Architect](https://thrivethemes.com/architect/ "Thrive Architect")Complete Marketing Suite and Page BuilderConversion-focused page building elements3.[Beaver Builder](https://searchwp.com/recommends/beaver-builder/ "Beaver Builder")Best for Learning Page BuildingClean interface with helpful tutorials4.[Divi](https://searchwp.com/recommends/elegant-themes-divi/ "Divi")All-in-One Page Builder SolutionComplete theme and builder combo5.[Elementor](https://searchwp.com/recommends/elementor-2/ "Elementor")Best Free Page Builder OptionPowerful free version with customization options6.[Visual Composer](https://searchwp.com/recommends/visual-composer/ "Visual Composer")Comprehensive Customization OptionsHuge template library & content elements## What to Look for in a WordPress Page Builder

First, letâs figure out what you should pay attention to when choosing a page builder plugin for WordPress.

A great page builder should be:

- **Easy to use**. Using the beginner-friendly plugin, you can [start creating your pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)") immediately after installation.
- **Optimized for search engines**. The proper HTML markup of your pages is crucial if you want to attract traffic from search engines.
- **Fast and lightweight**. Site loading speed plays a huge role in the user experience of your visitors and the number of leads you get.
- **Mobile-ready**. Responsive pages are the key to success since about 70% of the worldâs traffic comes from mobile devices.
- **Compatible with your theme**. This way, you can be sure that your new pages will match your current design.
- **Packed with templates**. Ready-made page templates allow you to quickly customize them for your needs and get your site up and running.

## How We Test and Review

Weâve spent countless hours testing page builders with one specific goal in mind: finding which ones actually work for people whoâve never built a website before. Our testing process goes far beyond just clicking through features.

We approach each page builder like a complete beginner would. This means starting with fresh WordPress installations, timing how long it takes to create a basic page, and noting every moment of confusion or frustration.

We also pay close attention to onboarding experiences, documentation quality, and how quickly we can get help when stuck.

Our evaluation focuses on four key areas that matter most to newbies. First, we test the initial setup process and measure how many steps it takes to create your first page. Second, we evaluate the interface complexity and whether common tasks feel intuitive.

After that, we examine the template library and starter content quality since beginners often need inspiration to get started.

Finally, we stress-test the learning resources each company provides. We read through their tutorials, test their customer support response times, and check if their help materials actually make sense to non-technical users.

### [How to Add a Search Box in WPBakery Page Builder](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-in-wpbakery-page-builder/)

**Published:** October 3, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When we started building websites with WPBakery Page Builder, we quickly discovered a frustrating problem. The default WordPress search feature was letting our visitors down.

People would search for content we knew existed on our sites, but theyâd get irrelevant results or no results at all.

We watched potential customers leave our sites because they couldnât find what they were looking for. The default WordPress search misses important details that make results truly helpful.

In this guide, weâll show you how to add a search box in WPBakery Page Builder and customize the search experience.

## What is WPBakery Page Builder?

WPBakery Page Builder is one of the [most popular drag-and-drop page builders](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/ "7 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)") for WordPress. It comes with dozens of prebuilt content elements, from basic text and images to complex pricing tables and testimonials.

Plus, you get to choose from a template library and start customizing your page in an instant.

 WPBakery also offers an AI website building assistant that helps you create stunning pages using prompts.

Now, why should you add a search box in WPBakery?

WordPressâs built-in search works like a basic keyword matcher that only scratches the surface. When visitors search your site, they often get confused by results that donât match what theyâre actually looking for.

Poor search results create a domino effect that hurts your business. Frustrated visitors spend less time on your site and are more likely to leave without converting.

When people canât find what they need quickly, they assume you donât have it and go somewhere else.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily add a search box in WordPress using WPBakery page builder.

## Adding a Search Box in WPBakery Page Builder

Before integrating a search form using WPBakery Page Builder, you can first enhance your websiteâs search functionality with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

SearchWP is the [leading search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), trusted by over 50,000 website owners.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)With SearchWP, you can create a smarter, more effective search experience without any coding skills required.

It allows you to decide exactly whatâs searchable, such as easily excluding specific pages from results or ensuring your [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") are included.

This results in an improved user experience, making it easier for your visitors to find what theyâre looking for.

Besides that, SearchWP offers customizable SearchWP Forms and SearchWP Results blocks, which you can effortlessly add within the WPBakery builder to display your search forms and custom results templates.

In addition to WPBakery Page Builder, SearchWP supports other popular site builders like [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)"), Divi, Beaver Builder, [Bricks](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-search-forms-in-wordpress-using-bricks/ "How to Add Search Forms in WordPress Using Bricks"), Breakdance, and Oxygen.

Here are more key features offered by SearchWP:

- **Search Analytics:** Understand user intent by tracking and analyzing what visitors search for on your site.
- **Live AJAX Search:** Provide an interactive experience by displaying search results instantly as users type.
- **Advanced Customization:** Make custom fields searchable, [exclude specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or categories, and fine-tune relevance weights for attributes.
- **Manual Result Reordering:** Gain complete control by manually adjusting the order of search results to feature specific content.
- **Multiple Search Engines:** Create and manage different search configurations, each assignable to various search forms on your site.

Now, letâs see how you can quickly set up SearchWP and use it to customize search on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website to get your copy of the plugin.

Once thatâs done, simply log in to your SearchWP account, navigate to the âDownloadsâ tab, and click the **Download SearchWP** button.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to save the plugin ZIP files on your computer.

From here, you can install SearchWP on your site, upload the pluginâs ZIP file and activate it. If you need assistance, please follow this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the setup wizard to get started.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Customize or Add a New Search Engine

SearchWP also lets you edit the default engine on your site and add new ones, defining rules for searches.

This way, you can customize which sources to include in the search process, which content to exclude, and more.

To start, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard. From here, simply click the âSources & Settingsâ button, and choose your preferred search sources.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)By default, Posts, Pages, and Media sources will be already included, but you can add more depending on your needs.

For example, if you want to hide specific content, then you can simply uncheck that particular source.

![Choose sources for your default engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-sources-for-your-default-engine-1030x441.png)SearchWP also lets you edit the attributes for each source. Attributes are types of content within each Source, such as title, content, custom field, [category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), tag, and more.

Simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under any source.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)On the next screen, you can choose which attributes to include.

For example, you can add custom fields like real estate listings or ingredients, post categories like tutorials, product details like [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), and more.

![Post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/post-attributes-2-1030x506.png)To learn more, please see our detailed guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 3: Create a Search Form in SearchWP

Next, SearchWP also lets you create custom search forms that you can easily connect with the search engine.

To create a search form that you can use with WPBakery Page Builder, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard.

You can create or edit a form, customize its layout and features, and select your newly created engine.

![Add a new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-search-form-1030x533.png)On the next screen, you can start by editing the name of the form at the top.

After that, SearchWP lets you choose a layout for your form.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)Besides that, you also get more options to edit the form.

For example, you can change the form style, button style, colors, select an engine and results page, and more.

![Select new engine for form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-new-engine-for-form-1030x484.png)For more details, please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

## Step 4: Add Search Box Using WPBakery Page Builder

Now, you can add the search form to a page using WPBakery Page Builder.

Simply go to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard and edit a page or add a new one.

When youâre in the content editor, click the **WPBakery Page Builder** button.

![Edit with WPBakery](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-with-wpbakery-1030x380.png)Next, you will be taken to the WPBakery Page Builder, where a popup message will appear.

You can simply click the âGot Itâ button to continue.

![WPBakery welcome screen popup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpbakery-welcome-screen-popup-1030x583.png)After that, you can start editing the page using WPBakery.

By default, the page builder will open the Classic Mode. However, you can switch to Frontend Editor or [Gutenberg Editor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)") as well.

For this tutorial, weâll use the Frontend Editor and see how the search form will appear on the website in real time.

![Choose mode to edit the page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-mode-to-edit-the-page-1030x682.png)Next, you will need to select a layout for your page.

You can choose between the default WordPress theme layout or a blank page layout by WPBakery.

![Choose default layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-default-layout--1030x581.png)For instance, we will use the âBlank Page Layout by WPBakeryâ option.

Next, you can click the â+ Add Elementâ button.

![Add a new element button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-element-button-1030x455.png)After that, you will see a list of elements to add. First, you need to switch to the âSearchWPâ tab at the top.

From here, simply add the **SearchWP Form** element.

![Add the SearchWP form element](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-the-searchwp-form-element-1030x521.png)Next, WPBakery will ask you to select your SearchWP search form.

Go ahead and click the dropdown menu to pick a form and then click the âSave Changesâ button.

![Select your form from dropdown](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-form-from-dropdown-1030x489.png)Once thatâs done, go ahead and click the **Publish** button at the top.

Next, you can close the page builder and visit the page to see the search box in action.

![View WPBakery search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-wpbakery-search-form-1030x366.png)Now, try searching for categories, custom fields, tags, or any other information that you made searchable while customizing the search engine for your respective form.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a search box in WPBakery page builder. You may also want to see our guide on [how to use SearchWP with Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)") and the [best WordPress backup plugins](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-backup-plugin/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)").

If youâre ready to create a smart search page on your site, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today").

    - [How to Choose the Right Search Results Page Template](#how-to-choose-the-right-search-results-page-template)
        - [Step 1: Check Out What Template You Chose](#aioseo-step-1-check-out-what-template-you-chose)
        - [Step 2: Create the Search Results Page Using the Right Template](#aioseo-step-2-create-the-search-results-page-using-the-right-template)
        - [Step 3: Choose One of the Ready-Made Product Archive Pages](#aioseo-step-3-choose-one-of-the-ready-made-product-archive-pages)
        - [Step 4: Publish Your Template and Add a Condition](#aioseo-step-4-publish-your-template-and-add-a-condition)
    - [How to Customize Your Search Results Page](#how-to-customize-your-search-results-page)
        - [Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP](#aioseo-step-1-install-and-activate-searchwp)
        - [Step 2: Customize Your Elementor Search Results Page](#aioseo-step-2-customize-your-elementor-search-results-page)

Letâs begin!

## Reasons Why Elementor Search Results Page Not Working

Letâs look at the 2 most common situations when you might have problems with your Elementor search results page.

### 1. Picking the Wrong Page Template

The most popular reason why your Elementor search results page isnât working is that you chose the wrong page template.

The thing is, there are 2 different Elementor search page templates. The first is for regular blog posts and pages, and the second is for WooCommerce products.

So if youâre a WooCommerce store owner, and you donât see search results on your Elementor search page, the chances are that youâve chosen the wrong template.

![check the type of your template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-the-type-of-your-template.png)### 2. The Default WordPress Search is Weak

Another popular reason your Elementor search page doesnât show any results is that WordPress hasnât found any relevant matches.

In this case, you may think that the search on your site is broken, especially if youâre a beginner who just created your first page with Elementor.

But the actual reason is that the default WordPress search engine is far from perfect.

For example, it doesnât consider post custom fields, tags, categories, and other valuable data when performing a search.

It also doesnât correct mistakes in search queries, so if you make a typo, you wonât get any results.

Thankfully, this problem is easy to fix. Weâll show you how to customize your search results page to make it smarter further in this article.

## Fix Elementor Search Results Page Not Working

After youâve learned the most popular reasons that can lead to an Elementor search results page not working, letâs look at how you can fix them.

### How to Choose the Right Search Results Page Template

The first step is to check if you chose the right template when you created the Elementor search results page.

#### Step 1: Check Out What Template You Chose

To get started, navigate to the **Templates** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![click templates](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-templates.png)Then, find the search results page you created and check out its **Type**.

If itâs set to the **Search Results** type, youâll see only regular blog posts and pages among your siteâs search results.

![search results type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-results-type.png)If you want WooCommerce products to appear on your Elementor search results page, youâll need to create a page with another type.

Letâs see how you can do that in the next step below.

#### Step 2: Create the Search Results Page Using the Right Template

To get started, navigate to **Templates Â» Add New** in your WordPress dashboard.

![add a new template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-template.png)In the next step, you can choose the type of page template you want to create.

For example, to create a search page for WooCommerce products, click on the dropdown menu and choose the **Products Archive** type.

![Select product archive template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-archieve-template-1030x574.png)Then, youâll also need to select the Post Type from the dropdown menu.

After that, simply enter the name of your template and press the **Create Template** button.

![Enter name of template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-name-of-template-1030x625.png)#### Step 3: Choose One of the Ready-Made Product Archive Pages

Next, Elementor will offer you a selection of ready-made product archive pages to choose from.

Simply hover your mouse over the template that suits you best and insert it.

![Select blocks from library](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-blocks-from-library-1030x615.png)#### Step 4: Publish Your Template and Add a Condition

After choosing a template, you can customize your WooCommerce product search results page just like any other page you customize with Elementor.

When youâre happy with how it looks, click **Publish** in the top right corner of your page.

![Publish Elementor product results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish-elementor-product-results-page-1030x575.png)In the next step, you can add a condition to your template. A condition specifies where your template is used on your site.

To get started, you can click the **Add Condition** button.

![Add conditions to publish template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-conditions-to-publish-template-1030x645.png)Then select the **Search Results** option from the dropdown menu.

When youâre finished, simply click **Save & Close** in the bottom right corner to save the condition.

![Choose search results condition](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-results-condition-1-1030x596.png)Thatâs it. Youâll now see the search results page you created every time you search for WooCommerce products on your site.

### How to Customize Your Search Results Page

The second reason your Elementor search results page is not working is that the default WordPress search is weak.

The easiest way to make your search results page smarter is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)This is the[ best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") trusted by over 50,000 site owners worldwide.

After installing it, you get complete control over how search works on your site and can customize it to meet your needs.

For instance, SearchWP allows you to [add multiple search forms to your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)"), change the order of search results, and much more.

This way, you can help your visitors find what theyâre looking for on your site faster and improve their user experience.

Here is a quick overview of how else you can use SearchWP to grow your business:

- [**Show search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Display search results on your site in real-time without writing a single line of code.
- [**Exclude specific pages from search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"): Hide any page on your site from appearing on search results in a few clicks.
- [**Create custom search fields**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): Add custom search fields to your site to deliver more relevant results to your visitors.
- [**Track searches of your visitors**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Collect data on what visitors are looking for on your site and use it to get new content ideas.

Now that you know how SearchWP can help you improve your search results page, letâs see how to install and configure this plugin.

#### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

The first step, youâll need to grab[ your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

After that, you can navigate to your SearchWP account dashboard and click **Downloads**.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once you get to the Downloads page, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the plugin to your computer.

When you finish downloading, you should also copy the SearchWP license key on the same page. Youâll need it further to activate your copy of the plugin.

The next step is to install SearchWP. To do that, youâll need to upload the downloaded file to your site. If you need help with that, you can check out this [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") detailed step-by-step tutorial.

Upon activation, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now that SearchWP is up and running on your site, you can customize the WordPress search.

To learn more, please see our guide on [11 ways to improve WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/ways-to-improve-wordpress-search/ "11 Ways to Improve WordPress Search (Expert Tips)").

#### Step 2: Customize Your Elementor Search Results Page

Using SearchWP and Elementor, you can completely customize the search results page on your site.

SearchWP offers pre-built templates that you can use for your results page.

![Choose a template and change name](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-template-and-change-name-1030x439.png)It also offers lots of customization options, which give you more control over how search results appear on your site.

For more details, please see our guide on [how to create a search page in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

The best part is that you can easily add the search results template in Elementor using the **SearchWP Results** widget.

![Add search results block in Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-results-block-in-elementor-1030x597.png)For more details, check out our guide on [how to customize your Elementor search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)") step-by-step tutorial.

By following the steps in this guide, youâll be able to easily tackle any possible problems with Elementor search results pages and give the best user experience to your visitors.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix the Elementor search results page that isnât working on your site. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") and [how to find out why your Elementor search form not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-elementor-search-form-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working").

If youâre ready to empower search on your site and grow your business, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today").
