### [How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/)

**Published:** September 25, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Building your first WordPress site can be tricky. With thousands of themes to choose from, it can be hard to find the right fit for your brand.

This is where creating a custom theme that suits all your requirements comes in handy.

However, the tricky part for beginners is that it usually requires coding experience and deep knowledge of WordPress.

But with the right WordPress theme builder, you can simply use a drag and drop builder to build your own theme.

In this article, weâll show how to create a custom theme in a few simple steps with no coding needed.

## What is a WordPress Theme?

To begin with, letâs clarify what exactly WordPress theme is and what it consists of.

A WordPress theme is a set of files placed in a common folder in the WordPress Themes directory on your hosting.

This set includes page templates written in HTML, PHP, and JavaScript languages, CSS style sheets, and graphics.

When it comes to creating a custom theme from scratch, there are 2 typical ways:

- **Do it yourself**: It requires extensive experience in web development and a lot of free time.
- **Hire a developer or web agency**: The average price ranges from a couple of hundred to a couple of thousand dollars.

For obvious reasons, both of these methods are hardly acceptable to beginners.

Thankfully, there is a third way to create a custom theme without breaking the bank or being a coding guru.

This is possible through the use of WordPress website builder like SeedProd. Letâs take a look at it in detail and find out why itâs so popular among WordPress users.

## Why Use SeedProd to Create a Custom WordPress Theme?

[SeedProd](https://www.seedprod.com/ "SeedProd") is the #1 AI-powered website builder plugin for WordPress thatâs beginner friendly to use.

[![SeedProd](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/seedprod-site-1030x530.png)](https://www.seedprod.com/)This is a real game-changer, as it allows you to create top-notch custom WordPress themes in minutes, no coding required.

The secret ingredient is the handy and powerful SeedProd visual editor. Using it, you can easily drag and drop the necessary site elements, blocks, or even whole sections to your page.

Here is a short preview of how it works:

![add a button to your page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-button-to-your-page.gif)SeedProd automatically optimizes pages and themes for fast loading. It allows you to ensure the best user experience for your visitors.

They are also responsive and mobile-ready by default, so you donât have to worry about how your site looks on different devices.

Along with that, SeedProd comes with [powerful features](https://www.seedprod.com/features/ "SeedProd Features") like:

- **Hundreds of ready-made page templates**: Donât feel inspired to create a theme from scratch? You can use one of 200+ pre-built templates and easily customize them to meet your needs.
- **WooCommerce integration**: Selling products online? SeedProd is fully compatible with WooCommerce, so you can create modern coming soon and checkout pages, product grids, and more.
- **E-mail subscribers management dashboard**: Do you have an email newsletter? SeedProd allows you to collect emails and track the status of your subscribers on a handy dashboard.
- **SEO-optimized code**: Worried about code clarity? The code of themes and pages you create with SeedProd is SEO-friendly and meets all search engine requirements.

Plus, SeedProd has an awesome and responsive support team.

Even though this plugin is super easy to use, if you come up with any questions, theyâre always ready to come to the rescue.

The feedback from excited users leaves no chance to doubt that this rating is well deserved.

Here is what people are saying about their experience with SeedProd:

![SeedProd reviews](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/seedprod-reviews-1030x595.png)Now that you know why using SeedProd is the best way to create a custom theme for beginners, letâs see how you can do that.

## How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme

This detailed tutorial has 6 easy steps. Follow them to create a beautiful custom WordPress theme without touching a single line of code.

Here is a table of contents to help you navigate:

- [Step 1: Install and Activate the SeedProd Plugin](#1)
- [Step 2: Select a Pre-built WordPress Theme](#2)
- [Step 3: Customize Your WordPress Theme Parts](#3)
- [Step 4: Customize Your Themeâs Global CSS (No Code)](#4)
- [Step 5: Connect to Your Email Marketing Service](#5)
- [Step 6: Publish Your Custom WordPress Theme](#6)
- [Bonus: Create Custom Search Forms and Results Page](#aioseo-bonus-create-custom-search-forms-and-results-page)

### Step 1: Install and Activate the SeedProd Plugin

To get started, you can [grab your copy of SeedProd here](https://www.seedprod.com/pricing/ "Get SeedProd Today").

Next, you can log into your SeedProd account and go over to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SeedProd](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-seedprod-1030x549.png)Then, click the **Download SeedProd Pro** button to start downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

When youâve finished downloading, copy your license key on the same tab. Youâll need it further to activate your copy of SeedProd.

The next step is to upload the downloaded ZIP file to your site and install the plugin. If you need help, you can follow along with this tutorial on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After youâve installed the plugin, you will see the SeedProd welcome screen. Here, you can go to your WordPress dashboard and click on the **SeedProd** button on the left panel.

Then, paste your license key into the license field and press the **Verify key** button.

![verify your key](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/verify-your-key.png)Once you activate your copy of SeedProd, youâre ready to start creating your custom WordPress theme.

### Step 2: Select a Pre-built WordPress Theme

To get started, navigate to **SeedProd Â» Theme Builder** in your dashboard.

![go over to the theme builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-theme-builder.png)On this page, you can manage your custom themes and create new ones.

From here, you have 2 ways to get started.

![manage your themes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/global-CSS.png)The first one is to create each part of your custom theme from scratch.

Every WordPress theme consists of the following parts:

- Header
- Footer
- Single Post
- Single Page
- Archive (Blog Page)
- Home Page
- Sidebar

These parts make up your theme, and SeedProd allows you to create and customize them individually.

To start creating a new part of your theme, press the **Add New Theme Template** button.

![press Add New Theme Template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Add-New-Theme-Template.png)Here, you can choose the type of your theme part, give it a name, and then start building it from scratch.

![choose a type of your template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-type-of-your-template.png)But, if you feel like itâs too overwhelming for your first try, there is a second way to create your custom WordPress theme.

SeedProd comes with a bunch of professional-looking premade theme templates. You can select them and customize them for your needs.

In this tutorial, weâll focus on this way as itâs easier for beginners and allows us to show you more SeedProd features.

To start customizing one of the ready-made theme templates, press the **Theme Template Kits** button.

![Click theme template kits](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-theme-template-kits--1030x521.png)Here you will find a variety of themes for different purposes.

For example, there are themes for personal blogs, portfolios, business or eCommerce sites, and more.

![how to create a custom WordPress theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-create-a-custom-WordPress-theme.png)To choose a theme you like, hover your cursor over it and click the checkmark icon.

For example, weâll choose one of the starter templates.

![click to choose a template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-to-choose-a-template.png)Once youâve selected a template, SeedProd automatically creates the parts that make it up.

As you can see, our theme not only includes page part templates, such as the header, footer, and sidebar.

![View theme template pages](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-theme-template-pages-1030x603.png)There are also templates for entire pages, like a single page, post, archives, and homepage.

If you donât need a specific template on your site, you can unpublish it by switching the toggle next to it.

![Unpublish a theme template page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/unpublish-a-theme-template-page-1030x497.png)To change the name of a template or edit conditions when it should appear on your site, click the **Edit Conditions** link.

![Edit conditions option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-conditions-option-1030x571.png)Itâll bring up a modal window where you can name a template the way you want and change its type, priority, and conditions.

When youâre done, simply click the **Save** button.

![edit the page conditions](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-the-page-conditions-1.png)Now that you know how to manage parts of your theme, youâre ready to start customizing it.

### Step 3: Customize Your WordPress Theme Parts

First, letâs customize the header as itâs the first thing that visitors see after coming to your site

**Header**

To get started, scroll the **Theme Builder** page down, find the **Header** template and click on it.

![click Header](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Header.png)**Note:** The header and footer are common to the entire site. It means that every change you make to them will be visible on every page of your site.

As you can see, our header consists of two parts. There is the left part with the site logo and the right part with the navigational menu.

![edit the header](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-the-header.png)SeedProd has a visual drag-and-drop editor. You can interact with any element on the page and add new ones by simply dragging them on the page.

Letâs try to change the logo. To do so, simply click on it, and a sidebar will appear where you can edit the element settings.

![edit your logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-your-logo.png)Each type of element in the SeedProd editor has its own set of settings and customization options.

For example, our logo has the **Image** type. So it has all the settings you would normally expect from an image, such as its source file, alt text, size, and more.

You can also switch between the **Content**, **Templates**, and **Advanced** tab on the left sidebar to discover even more customization options.

![you can switch between tables](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/you-can-switch-between-tables.png)Now, letâs say you want to replace the default logo with your own.

To do that, click on the current logo image, and then you can select the image you want to use instead.

![click on the image](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-the-image.png)Here is how our new logo looks on our test site:

![click on your logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-your-logo.png)Now, letâs change the header background color.

To do so, point a cursor over your header until it has a blue outline, and click on it.

![hover a mouse over your header](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/hover-a-mouse-over-your-header.png)After that, youâll see the header customization options on the left sidebar. It has the **Row** element type.

![editing row](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/editing-row.png)On the **Content** tab, you can change your header background color, replace it with an image and choose the header width.

To change the background color, click the color palette icon and select the color you want.

![choose a color](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-color.png)Then, on the **Advanced** tab, you can add a shadow or border to your header, change its spacing settings, and more.

![go to the Advanced tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-Advanced-tab.png)As the finishing touch, letâs make the navigation menu font a bit bigger.

To do so, point a cursor over the menu and click on it.

![click on menu](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-menu.png)Now, in the **Font Size** field on the left sidebar, enter the desired font size.

Simply move the slider or enter a number to change the font size.

![change the font size](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-the-font-size.png)When youâve finished customizing the header, press the **Save** button in the top-right corner.

Then, press the exit icon to leave the visual editor and go back to the theme templates page.

![press Save](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Save.png)Next, letâs customize the **Homepage**.

**Homepage**

To do so, find the **Homepage** template among the other theme templates and click on it.

![click Homepage](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Homepage.png)The starter template has a homepage with features you can find on a typical business site, such as:

- Business description section
- List of clients with their logos
- Business features
- Testimonials
- FAQ section
- and moreâ¦

As with the header, you can interact with every element on the page and change its appearance.

For example, you can see how to easily change the title, description and text of the button in the hero section with just a few clicks:

![how to create a custom WordPress theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-create-a-custom-WordPress-theme-1.gif)Now, letâs try to add something new to our page.

If no element on the page is currently selected, on the left sidebar, you can see the **Blocks** tab.

![the Blocks tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-Blocks-tab.png)There are plenty of ready-made blocks that you can add to your page with a few clicks, no coding needed.

For example, you can find such blocks as the [post author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (Simple Steps)"), price table, search form, and many others.

To add a specific block to your page, drag and drop it at the place you want.

For example, letâs add a **Countdown** block to the Features section:

![add a countdown to your page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-countdown-to-your-page.gif)Plus, if you go over to the **Sections** tab, youâll find tons of premade sections that you can also add to your page, such as:

- Headers
- Footers
- Call to action
- Features
- Testimonials
- and many moreâ¦

![go over to the Sections tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-Sections-tab.png)To add a new section to the page, hover your mouse over the desired section and click the plus icon.

For example, letâs add a new **Call to Action** section to our page.

![add a call to action](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-call-to-action.png)By default, the new section will appear at the very end of the page.

To change the section position, point a cursor over its top border, choose the **Move Section** option and drag it the same way as a block.

![move a section](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-a-section.png)When youâre finished customizing your homepage, you can see how it looks on mobile devices.

To do that, click the mobile preview icon at the bottom left corner.

![switch to the mobile version](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/switch-to-the-mobile-version.png)If there are some blocks or sections that donât look right, you can move them around.

This way, you can ensure that your mobile version offers the best user experience.

![our site mobile version](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/our-site-mobile-version.png)When youâre satisfied with your homepage, donât forget to press the **Save** button to save all the changes youâve made.

**Blog Index, Archives, and Search pages**

Letâs now look at a template thatâs called **Blog Index, Archives, Search**.

![click Blog Index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Blog-Index.png)As you can assume from its name, this template is responsible for displaying the blog posts page, archives, and search results.

This is handy since you can change the appearance of 3 pages at once.

![the archive page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-archive-page.png)As youâve already learned how to customize pages, and add new blocks and sections to them, letâs focus on setting up specific elements.

For example, if you click on the **Posts** element, you can change the postsâ order, exclude posts from specific categories, tags or authors, and more.

![change the query type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-the-query-type.png)Plus, youâre free to fully customize the layout of the post list.

Namely, you can change the number of columns and posts per page, change the visibility of the authorâs name, publish date, time, and comment count.

Itâs rare that a website builder allows you to control such things, no coding needed, which is another reason for SeedProdâs popularity.

**Single Post**

When youâre finished customizing your blog page, letâs also edit the **Single Post** template.

![click Single Post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Single-Post.png)A typical blog post has the following parts:

- Featured image
- Post title
- Post info
- Post content
- Author box
- Comments section

![singe post template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/singe-post-template.png)The best thing is, with SeedProd, you can fully customize each of these parts.

For example, if you click on the **Post info** element, you can add new useful data to it.

![customize the layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customize-the-layout.png)It can be a time of publication or modification, additional info about the post author, or something else you want to specify.

For example, letâs say we want to display the number of comments in our post info section. To get started, press the **Add Item** button.

![press Add Item](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Add-Item.png)Then, click on the **Type** drop-down menu and select the **Comments** option.

After that, youâll see that the number of comments now displays inside the post info element.

![click on the Post Info element](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-the-Post-Info-element.png)This way, you can easily customize any element on the page.

**Sidebar**

Letâs also change the appearance of our themeâs sidebar. Head over to the theme builder dashboard and click on the **Sidebar** template.

![click sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-sidebar.png)[Sidebar](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-wordpress-search-widget/ "How to Customize the Search Widget in WordPress (Easy Way)") is very popular among WordPress site owners.

Itâs a great place to put relevant website elements such as a list of [recent posts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-show-related-content-in-wordpress/ "How To Show Related Content In WordPress"), subscription form, and many others.

![edit your sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-your-sidebar.png)SeedProd also allows you to customize your sidebar totally in the same way as other templates.

For example, you can add an email newsletter signup form to your sidebar with a few clicks.

To do that, simply drag and drop the **Optin Form** element from the **Blocks** tab to your sidebar:

![add an optin form to your sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-an-optin-form-to-your-sidebar.gif)Then, if you click on it, you can change the number of fields, their type and more.

![customize the opt in form fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customize-the-opt-in-form-fields.png)Now that you know how to work within the SeedProd visual editor, youâre ready to customize your themeâs global CSS.

### Step 4: Customize Your Themeâs Global CSS (No Code)

First, letâs clarify what CSS is and how you can change your siteâs global appearance by editing this file.

CSS stands for cascading style sheets. Itâs a file that contains code that determines your siteâs global styling, typography, and colors.

When you change the appearance of an element in the CSS file, those changes apply to that element across your entire site.

Every WordPress theme has its own CSS file. Typically, to make changes to it, you need to have some background in web development.

But, SeedProd allows you to customize your siteâs global CSS in a simple and beginner-friendly editor.

To get started, click on the **Global CSS** template in your theme templates dashboard.

![open Global CSS](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/open-Global-CSS.png)This will take you to the global CSS settings visual editor.

On the left side, youâll find tabs where you can change your siteâs color, fonts, background, and more.

And on the right side, you can see the results of the changes youâve made in real-time.

![global CSS settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/global-CSS-settings.png)For example, letâs change the font of the headings.

To do that, you can click on the **Fonts** tab.

![go over to the Fonts tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-Fonts-tab.png)To change the font of all headings from H1 to H6, select a new font from the dropdown menu under the **Header Font** title.

There is also an option to change the color of the font.

![change the header font](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-the-header-font.png)If you want to change the font of a particular heading, you can switch the **Show H1 â H6 Settings** toggle.

![click Show H1-H6 settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Show-H1-H6-settings.png)Then, click the **Edit** button next to the **Typography** option of a chosen heading and select the font you want to have.

![edit the font family](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-the-font-family.png)For example, weâll change the H1 heading font to **Comic Sans**. As you can see, the font changed instantly.

![changhe the heading font family](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/changhe-the-heading-font-family.png)When youâre done adjusting the CSS settings, click the **Save** button to save the changes youâve made.

![save your changes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-changes.png)By following this way, you can customize a lot of things related to the appearance of your custom WordPress theme.

Now that you know how to edit the global CSS settings, youâre ready to move on and connect your email marketing service account.

### Step 5: Connect to Your Email Marketing Service

SeedProd has seamless integration with the most popular email marketing services. This list includes but isnât limited to Mailchimp, Constant Contact, and Drip.

This is great news if you already have an active base of email subscribers or want to start growing your first email list.

To connect your email marketing account, first navigate to the **Theme Builder** tab and click on any template.

![open a single post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/open-a-single-post.png)Then, go over to the **Connect** tab at the top panel.

Once there, point a cursor over a service youâre using, press the **Connect** button and follow the on-screen instructions.

![connect an email service](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/connect-an-email-service.png)When youâre done connecting your email service, press the **Save** button at the top right to save the changes.

Now, SeedProd will collect emails of visitors who filled out your optin form and add them to your email base.

![press the Save button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-the-Save-button.png)### Step 6: Publish Your Custom WordPress Theme

The final step is to publish your custom theme and make it live on your website.

To do that, first, go to **SeedProd Â» Theme Builder** from your WordPress dashboard.

![navigate to the Theme Builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-Theme-Builder-1.png)Then, switch the **Enable SeedProd Theme** toggle at the top right.

Thatâs it. Your custom WordPress theme is live, and you can see it on your site.

![publish your theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish-your-theme.png)To do that, simply visit your website and see the new custom theme.

For example, here is our customized homepage on our demo site:

![our homepage](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/our-homepage.png)And here is the single blog post template we also customized with just a few clicks using SeedProd:

![our single post template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-changed-the-heading-font-family.png)## Bonus: Create Custom Search Forms and Results Page

Now, would you like to also enhance the search experience on your new custom themed WordPress site?

The default WordPress search lacks a lot of features and often fails to deliver relevant results, especially if you have lots of articles or products on your site.

This is where [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") can help you out. It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and enhances the default search by allowing you to include custom fields, tags, categories, product details, media files, documents, and more in the search process.

With SearchWP, you can [set up custom search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") and link them to specific search engines. It also offers prebuilt layouts and lots of customization options.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)Thatâs not all! SearchWP also comes with results template for WordPress. You can customize how the search results will appear on your site and select from prebuilt templates.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to create a professional-looking search page in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to create a custom WordPress theme in 6 easy steps, no coding needed. You may also want to see our guide on [best WordPress search plugins to boost your site search](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/) and [how to create a search page in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

If also want to improve the WordPress search, then you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").

This is where a search plugin like SearchWP comes in handy. It enhances your website search and helps visitors find what theyâre looking for with ease.

- [How to Help Your Visitors Find the Right Content Faster](#3)

Letâs dive into it!

## What is SearchWP and Why Use It

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the best WordPress search plugin on the market, with over 50,000 active installs.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It comes with many useful features that you can use to make search on your site way smarter.

The thing is that one of WordPressâs weaknesses has always been its search functionality. It ignores a lot of valuable data when performing searches and doesnât give you any way to customize how the search works.

This results in your visitors often not finding what theyâre looking for and leaving your site unsatisfied.

Hereâs where SearchWP comes to the rescue. Using this plugin, you can make searchable almost anything on your site.

This list includes custom post types, custom fields, post tags, categories, and other data you want to consider.

SearchWP also allows you to completely customize how search works on your site. For example, you can exclude any pages from search results, limit search to specific content, and many more.

This way, you can make it easier for your visitor to find the right content and improve their user experience.

Here are some other SearchWP features thatâll help you to boost your search:

- **[Multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)")**: Would you like to create a custom search field? With SearchWP, you can create supplemental search engines with unique settings and link them to individual search fields.
- [**WooCommerce integration**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)"): Are you selling online using WooCommerce? SearchWP has seamless integration with this plugin to make your search better and boost your sales right away.
- [**Search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): SearchWP collects data on any search activity on your site so you can get new content ideas and better understand your audience.
- [**Fuzzy search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"): Want to enrich your search results? With SearchWPâs fuzzy search feature, you can make WordPress search for partial matches when there are no exact ones.

## How to Install SearchWP on Your WordPress Site

Now that you know why SearchWP is the best search plugin, letâs see how you can install it on your website.

To begin, youâll need to [get your copy of the Search](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

After that, you can log into your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** section.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once there, click the **Download SearchWP** button to download the SearchWP pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

We also recommend copying the license key in the bottom left corner of the same page. Youâll need this key to activate your pluginâs copy later.

The next step is to install SearchWP. Simply upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site to do that. Need a refresher on how to do that? You can follow the [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step tutorial](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once the plugin is active, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and the setup wizard. You can click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, youâre ready to start boosting search on your site.

## 1. How to Add a Custom Search Widget to Elementor

Using SearchWP and Elementor, you can add a custom search widget to your pages.

It will help your visitors to discover more of your content and allow you to get more pageviews.

To get started, navigate to the **Pages** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![click Pages](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Pages.png)Next, you will see all the pages on your site.

Go ahead and hover your cursor over the page to which you want to add the search form and click **Edit with Elementor**.

![click Edit with Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Edit-with-Elementor-1.png)Once you get to the Elementor editor, you can add a search form to your page.

Simply head to the SearchWP section from the panel on the left and look for the **SearchWP Form** widget.

![Add SearchWP form block in Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-form-block-in-elementor-1-1030x679.png)Then drag this widget to the place on your page where you want to add the search form.

After that, you can select a search form from the dropdown menu.

![Select the search form to add](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-the-search-form-to-add-1-1030x647.png)When youâve finished, save your Elementor page.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to add a search box to Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-elementor-page/ "How to Add a Search Box to Elementor Page (Beginnerâs Guide)").

## 2. How to Help Your Visitors Find the Right Content Faster

SearchWP has seamless integration with Elementor and automatically connects to every existing search field on your site.

So, after you add a search widget to your page, youâre ready to customize search on your site.

**Customize Your Search Engine**

To begin, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard. Here you can find the search engine created with SearchWP.

At the moment, youâll see that there is a single search engine called **Default**. SearchWP automatically creates it after the plugin installation.

By customizing the **Default** search engine settings, you can modify how searches work on your site. For example, you can edit the set of search sources, manage the list of applicable attributes, and much more.

In addition, SearchWP allows you to add supplemental search engines and link them to specific search forms. This way, you can create custom search fields with unique settings and place them anywhere on your site.

Letâs say we want to help our visitors find what theyâre looking for faster by using any search field on our site. Weâll need to set up the **Default** search engine to do that.

To begin, click the **Sources & Settings** button at the top right corner of the **Default** search engine section.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)After that, youâll see a pop-up where you can add or remove search sources.

By default, the **Posts**, **Pages**, and **Media** sources will be selected.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)However, you can choose which sources to choose and limit the search on your site

When youâre finished managing your search sources, press **Done** to save the changes.

Another way to make search on your site smarter is to enable search by post custom fields, tags, and categories.

To get started, simply click on the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under **Posts** source.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Here you can manage post attributes that WordPress considers when performing searches.

Post attributes are additional data that you can add to make it easier for your visitors to find the more relevant content. For example, it can be tags, custom fields, and more.

![Post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/post-attributes-2-1030x506.png)Letâs say youâre using the [Advanced Custom Fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") plugin and added custom fields.

To make them searchable, you can simply click the Custom Fields dropdown menu and select the fields you want to make searchable.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)You can find more details by following our guide on [how to add custom fields search in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

When youâre finished managing searchable custom fields and taxonomies, press **Done** to save all your changes.

SearchWP also allows you to control the [relevance weight](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "Relevance Weights") of attributes by moving the slider next to them to the left or right. The further right the slider, the more importance or relevance that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

![Move slider for custom fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-slider-for-custom-fields-1030x595.png)Once youâre happy with how you customized your search engine, simply scroll to the top of the page.

From here, you can press the **Save** button at the top right.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Now, the post search on your site is a lot smarter.

But, you can make it start delivering even more relevant search results to your visitors.

**Exclude Specific Pages From Search Results**

You can clean up search results on your site to get rid of redundant pages and any other clutter.

Simply hide any pages from appearing in search results on your site by using the [SearchWP Exclude UI](https://searchwp.com/features/exclude-ui/ "SearchWP Exclude UI") extension.

The extension adds an option inside the WordPress content editor. You can click the Exclude from SearchWP checkbox to hide any content.

![Check exclude from search checkbox](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-specific-post-1030x984.jpg)For more information, check out our step-by-step guide on [how to exclude specific pages from search results on your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress").

**Prioritize Specific Pages in Search Results**

Another way to give your visitors more relevant search results with SearchWP is to use the [Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/features/custom-results-order/ "Custom Results Order") extension.

This extension allows you to change the order of search results on your site.

You can then manually put the most relevant posts or pages at the top of search results for specific keywords.

![Configure Custom Results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)For more details, please follow our step-by-step guide on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress").

## 3. How to Display Live Search Results on Your Elementor Site

Live search results are another SearchWP feature that you can use with Elementor to boost search on your site.

By default, WordPress shows search results by opening a new separate page. Itâs inconvenient and may confuse your visitors.

With SearchWP live search feature, your visitors will start getting search results instantly without leaving the current page.

![live search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/live-search.gif)You can learn more by following our guide on [how to add live search to WordPress for free](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free").

## 4. How to Customize Your Elementor Search Results Page

Using Elementor with SearchWP, you can create a custom search results page thatâll help you to get more pageviews.

First, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Templates** from your WordPress dashboard and create a search results template.

Now, you can add a new template or edit an existing one.

![Add multiple results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-multiple-results-template-1030x445.png)Next, SearchWP will offer different themes for your results page.

Go ahead and select one. Youâll also get to see a preview of the theme under the layouts, which will help you pick the right theme for your results page.

![Choose a template and change name](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-template-and-change-name-1030x439.png)Once thatâs done, simply scroll down to the Custom Styling section.

Here, you can further edit the style of your results page.

![Change theme custom styling](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-theme-custom-styling-1030x640.png)To learn more, please see our guide on [how to create a search page in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

Once the results page template is ready, you can add it using Elementor theme builder.

Next, you can head to the page where you added the SearchWP search form and then click the **Edit with Elementor** button.

![Edit with Elementor button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-with-elementor-button-1030x484.png)Once youâre in the Elementor page editor, scroll down to the SearchWP section from the panel on the left.

From here, simply drag and drop the **SearchWP Results** widget.

![Add search results block in Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-results-block-in-elementor-1030x597.png)After adding the widget, you will also see options in the left panel to select a template and search engine.

However, we recommend to keep the default settings, as this way the search results will appear based on your search form settings.

![Choose your search results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-search-results-template-1030x532.png)When youâre done, simply update or publish your Elementor page. Thatâs it. You successfully customized your search results page.

We hope this article helped you learn how to use SearchWP with Elementor to boost your site search. You may also want to see our guides on [how to include shortcode results in WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search") and [how to make WordPress search in post content](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-in-post-content/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search").

Ready to boost Elementor search on your site? Get started with the most powerful [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today") today.

### [How to Search by Co-Authors Plus Writers in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-co-authors-plus-writers-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** September 18, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Multi-author WordPress sites that use Co-Authors Plus plugin face a common search problem that frustrates both site owners and readers.

When visitors search for content by specific guest authors or contributors, they often get zero results, even when those writers have published multiple posts.

The issue comes from how the default WordPress search works. While Co-Authors Plus works perfectly for assigning credit to multiple writers per post, WordPressâs default search function completely ignores these co-authors.

SearchWP solves this problem by improving the search process with support for Co-Authors Plus.

In this article, we will show you how to search by Co-Authors Plus Writers in WordPress.

## Why Search by Co-Authors Plus Writers in WordPress

WordPressâs default search has a major blind spot when it comes to Co-Authors Plus.

It only searches through post titles, content, and [excerpts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-excerpts-on-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Display Excerpts on the WordPress Search Results Page"), completely skipping over the additional authors youâve carefully assigned to each piece of content.

This creates real issues for your readers. For example, when someone searches for âJohn Smithâ on your site, they wonât find any of the five articles John co-authored, even though his name appears prominently in the byline of each post.

Your editorial team faces similar frustrations. Finding all content by a specific freelance writer or guest contributor becomes a manual process of scrolling through archives or [filtering by categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

The user experience suffers too. Readers who discover a great article by a guest author have no easy way to find more of that personâs work on your site.

Theyâre likely to leave instead of exploring further, which hurts your engagement and time-on-site metrics.

That said, letâs look at how you can make Co-Author Plus writers searchable in WordPress.

## Set Up Search by Co-Authors Plus Writers in WordPress

The easiest way of making Co-Authors Plus writers searchable is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the most powerful [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that improves your siteâs search capabilities far beyond WordPressâs default limitations.

While WordPress only searches post titles and content, SearchWP can make authors, custom fields, tags, categories, [product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), and virtually any data on your site searchable.

The plugin comes with built-in support for Co-Authors Plus through its extension. This means you wonât need to write custom code or configure complex settings to make co-authors searchable.

SearchWP offers several other features that make it perfect for multi-author sites. You can create custom search forms with unique settings, [change search result ordering](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)"), track popular searches, and limit searches to specific post types or categories.

To get started, just follow these steps.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and create a new account.

After creating your account, log in and click on the **Downloads** tab. From here, click the âDownload SearchWPâ button and save the ZIP file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)You should also copy your license key from the same page, as youâll need it to activate your plugin in the next step.

Next, youâll need to upload the SearchWP plugin files to your WordPress site. If you need help with this, then please see this [guide on installing a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After the plugin installation is complete, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Install Co-Author Plus Extension

Next, youâll need to install the [Co-Author Plus extension](https://searchwp.com/features/co-authors-plus-integration/ "Co-Author Plus extension") in SearchWP.

The extension allows SearchWP to easily integrate with Automatticâs Co-Authors Plus plugin and include author details in searches.

First, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard. From here, navigate to the Co-Author Plus Integration and click the **Install** button.

![Install Co-Author Plus extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-co-author-plus-extension-1030x466.png)The extension will then automatically install and activate.

Now, youâre ready to edit the search engine and include multiple authors in the search process.

### Step 3: Edit Search Engine to Include Co-Authors Plus Writers

SearchWP lets you set up different search engines or edit the default engine on your WordPress site.

You can think of search engines as different sets of rules for what gets searched, which attributes to include, and more.

Simply navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard. This is where youâll customize [how your search engine works](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") and what content it includes.

Youâll see your Default search engine listed here, which SearchWP created during the initial setup.

First, you can edit the sources that the engine uses by clicking the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup window will appear showing the different sources your search engine currently includes. By default, this includes Posts, Pages, and [Media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files").

Here, you can narrow down your search and exclude sources that you donât to include. For instance, if you want users to only find blog posts, then you can simply select the Posts source and uncheck other sources.

Weâll keep the default sources for this tutorial.

![Choose sources for your default engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-sources-for-your-default-engine-1030x441.png)When youâre finished, simply click the **Done** button.

Next, you will need to add Co-Author Plus writers as custom fields for your sources. SearchWP stores the Co-Author Plus data as [custom fields,](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") so you can specify which details to include in the search process.

To start, simply head to a source like Posts and click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)This opens a new window where you can select which attributes to include. By default, SearchWP will include title, content, slug, excerpt, and [author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (Simple Steps)") as attributes.

To start, you can click the âCustom Fieldsâ dropdown menu and select fields you want to include in the search process.

For example, if you added author details in the Co-Author Plus plugin like a display name, first name, last name, and description, then you can make them searchable.

![Select Co Author plus custom fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-co-author-plus-custom-fields-1030x584.png)After selecting the attributes, you can click the **Done** button.

Next, you can select the [relevance weights](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "Engine Configuration - Relevance Weights") for each of these attributes by moving the slider. The further to the right the slider is set for an attribute, the more influence that attribute has on the position in the search results.

![Change co author attributes weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-co-author-attributes-weights-1030x549.png)Now, you can scroll to the top of the page.

After that, go ahead and click the âSaveâ and âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)### Step 4: Test Co-Author Plus Search 

Now that youâve set up the search engine to include Co-Author Plus writers, the next step is to test if everything is working correctly.

To start, you can visit your site and head to the search form.

From here, simply search for the author youâve created using Co-Author Plus.

![Search for Co Author writers](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-co-author-writers-1030x541.png)For example, we added an author using Co-Author Plus called âJohn Smith.â

Upon searching for the author, all the posts written by the author will appear.

![View Co Authors in search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-co-authors-in-search-results-1030x561.png)As you can see, the search is working perfectly. It was able to find content written by a Co-Author Plus writer.

### FAQs About Making Co-Author Plus Writers Searchable in WordPress

Here are some frequently asked questions about including writers in Co-Author Plus in the search process.

**1. Does SearchWP work with guest authors who donât have WordPress accounts?**

Yes, Co-Authors Plus allows you to create âguest authorâ profiles for people who donât have WordPress user accounts. SearchWP will index and search these guest author names just like regular user accounts.

**2. Can I search for partial author names?**

Yes, SearchWP supports partial name matching. Searching for âJohnâ will find authors named âJohn Smith,â âJohnny Doe,â and similar variations.

**3. How do I know if the co-author search is working correctly?**

The easiest way is to search for a co-authorâs name on your site and verify that their content appears in the results. You can also check the SearchWP statistics to see search queries and results.

We hope this article helped you learn how to search by Co-Authors Plus Writers in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add search forms in WordPress using the Bricks visual builder](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-search-forms-in-wordpress-using-bricks/ "How to Add Search Forms in WordPress Using Bricks") and comparison between [SearchWP vs Better Search](https://searchwp.com/searchwp-vs-better-search-which-wordpress-search-tool-is-better/ "How to Add Search Forms in WordPress Using Bricks").

Are you ready to become one of them and boost search on your site with the best WordPress search plugin? You can go and [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP Today").

**Tags:** add authors in WordPress search, advanced custom fields search &amp; filter, author search wordpress, authors, Co-Author Plus writers, Co-Authors Plus, custom fields, SearchWP, wordpress custom fields search, WordPress search, wordpress search settings

### [How to Add Search Forms in WordPress Using Bricks](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-search-forms-in-wordpress-using-bricks/)

**Published:** September 9, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Adding a search form to your WordPress site with Bricks Visual Builder is surprisingly simple.

However, the real magic happens when you combine Bricks with SearchWP. While Bricks handles the visual design of your search form, SearchWP powers up the search functionality to find content that WordPress normally misses.

SearchWP searches through custom fields, categories, tags, and even file attachments. This means your visitors get accurate results and have a positive search experience.

In this article, weâll show you how to add search forms in WordPress using the Bricks visual builder.

## Why Use Bricks Visual Builder for Your Search Forms?

Bricks is a visual page builder that lets you [create custom WordPress themes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)") and landing pages without writing any code.

Unlike other page builders, Bricks generates clean, lightweight code that doesnât slow down your website.

The plugin gives you complete control over every element on your pages. For example, you can add search forms to your siteâs theme that match your brand perfectly.

When you pair Bricks with SearchWP, you get the best of both worlds. Bricks handles the visual design and layout of your search forms, while SearchWP powers the intelligent search functionality behind the scenes.

SearchWP searches through custom fields, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce"), tags, and even file attachments that regular WordPress search ignores. Your visitors get accurate, relevant results every time they search.

This combination creates search experiences where visitors can find exactly what theyâre looking for quickly, which keeps them engaged with your content longer.

That said, letâs look at the steps you can follow to add a search form to your site using Bricks visual builder.

## Adding a Search Form in WordPress Using Bricks

Before adding a search form in Bricks visual builder, you can first use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") to enhance the search feature on your site.

SearchWP is the best search plugin for WordPress and is trusted by over 50,000 website owners.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP gives you total control to build a smarter, more effective search for your visitors, no coding required.

You get to decide exactly whatâs searchable. For instance, you can easily hide a [specific page from results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or make sure your custom fields are included.

The result is a much better experience for your users, who can find what theyâre looking for faster.

What makes SearchWP stand out is its SearchWP Forms and SearchWP Results blocks. You can simply add them inside the site builder and display your search forms and custom results templates.

Besides Bricks, SearchWP supports other popular site builders, such as Elementor, [Divi](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)"), Beaver Builder, WPBakery, Breakdance, and Oxygen.

Here are more key features offered by SearchWP:

- **Advanced Customization:** Make custom fields searchable, exclude specific pages or categories, and fine-tune result weighting.
- **Search Analytics:** Understand user intent by tracking and analyzing what visitors search for on your site.
- **Live AJAX Search:** Provide an interactive experience by [displaying search results instantly](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-ajax-search-to-your-wordpress-without-a-plugin/ "How to Add Live Ajax Search to Your WordPress Without a Plugin") as users type.
- **Manual Result Reordering:** Gain complete control by manually adjusting the order of search results to feature specific content.
- **Multiple Search Engines:** Create and manage several unique search configurations, each assignable to different search forms on your site.

Now, letâs see how you can quickly set up SearchWP and use it to customize search on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!").

Once done, simply log into your SearchWP account and go to the **Downloads** page.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg "Download SearchWP plugin from account area - SearchWP")Next, you can click **Download SearchWP** and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, we recommend also copying your SearchWP license key on the same page.

Now, the next step is to install SearchWP on your site. To do that, youâll need to upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

If you need help, then please follow this [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step guide](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png "Onboarding wizard SearchWP - SearchWP")Once SearchWP is up and running, you can use it to create custom search engines and search forms.

### Step 2: Customize or Add a New Search Engine

With SearchWP, you can edit the default engine on your site and also add new ones.

A search engine contains a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing a search.

By customizing the engine, you can select which sources to include, set up rules to exclude certain content, make custom fields, categories, [tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)"), and more searchable.

To begin, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)From here, simply select search sources you want to consider when performing searches on the pop-up window.

By default, SearchWP will include Posts, Pages, and Media sources. However, you can include more source.

For example, letâs say you have an [eCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), then you can also include Products as a source.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, click the âDoneâ button.

Besides that, SearchWP also lets you edit the source attributes. This allows you to include custom fields and taxonomies in the search process.

You can simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)Next, you can click the âCustom Fieldsâ dropdown menu to add additional information, like [SKU ](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)")or any other product detail.

Similarly, if you want to make categories and tags searchable, then simply click the âTaxonomiesâ dropdown menu to add them.

![Add SKU custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-6-1030x375.jpg)To learn more, you may want to see our guide on [how to change the default WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

### Step 3: Create a Search Form in SearchWP

The next step is to create a search form in SearchWP that you can place on your site using Bricks.

To start, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard. Now, you can create a new form or edit an existing one.

![Select a search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-a-search-form-1030x518.png)After that, you will see options to edit the search form. To start, you can change the form name at the top.

Next, you can choose a layout theme and customize it according to your needs.

SearchWP offers 5 prebuilt themes to choose from and also shows a preview for each style.

![Select search form layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-layout-1030x544.png)Next, you can scroll down to view more editing options.

Here, SearchWP lets you customize your selected theme layout, select the engine you just created, edit form and button style, and more.

![Select new engine for form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-new-engine-for-form-1030x484.png)To learn more, please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### Step 4: Add Search Forms Using Bricks

Next, you can add the search form to a landing page using Bricks.

To get started, navigate to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard. From here, you can edit an existing page or add a new one.

![Navigate to All Pages](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-All-Pages-1030x507.png)Next, you will see the WordPress content editor.

Go ahead and click the **Edit with Bricks** button at the top.

![Click edit with bricks](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-edit-with-bricks-1-1030x432.png)On the next screen, you will see the Bricks visual editor.

Here, you can drag and drop different Elements from the panel on the left onto the landing page.

Simply navigate to SearchWP from the left-panel and add the **Search Form** element.

![Add search form block in Bricks](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-form-block-in-bricks-1030x558.png)After adding the Search Form element, go ahead and select it.

Youâll now see options to select a search form from the panel on the left. Simply click the dropdown menu and select the search form you created earlier.

![Select your search form for bricks](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-search-form-for-bricks-1030x561.png)Once thatâs done, you can publish or update the landing page.

Go ahead and click the power icon at the top to publish your page.

![Publish bricks page with search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish-bricks-page-with-search-form-1030x434.png)### Step 5: Preview Your Search Form

Next, you can visit your visit and head to the newly created landing page with Bricks.

From here, simply enter a search term in the search form to test if itâs working properly.

![Bricks search form preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-your-search-form-1030x357.png)For example, you can try searching the categories and tags you configured when settings up the search engine.

Or if you have a [WooCommerce store](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working"), then you can search for product details and other attributes.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add search forms in WordPress using Bricks. You may also want to see our guide on [how to create a professional-looking search page on your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)") and [how to add a search box to Elementor pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-elementor-page/ "How to Add a Search Box to Elementor Page (Beginnerâs Guide)").
