### [6 Best WordPress Backup Plugin for 2025 (Expert Review)](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-backup-plugin/)

**Published:** September 3, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When youâre running a website, you need to prepare for all types of situations.

For instance, your website could disappear tomorrow due to server crashes, hacking attempts, accidental deletions, or plugin conflicts.

Any of these could wipe out months or years of hard work in seconds. So whatâs the solution?

Use a backup plugin that provides a reliable backup to restore your site in case something goes wrong. Our testing showed that Duplicator, UpdraftPlus, and Jetpack VaultPress are the top options.

In this article, weâll show you the best WordPress backup plugins. Hereâs a quick overview of the plugins we tested.

**\#****WordPress Backup Plugins****Best For****Pricing**1.[Duplicator](https://duplicator.com/ "Duplicator")Site migration & cloningPaid 2.[UpdraftPlus](https://wordpress.org/plugins/updraftplus/ "UpdraftPlus")Free automated backupsFree3.[Jetpack VaultPress Backup](https://searchwp.com/recommends/jetpack-vaultpress-backup/ "Jetpack VaultPress Backup")Automatic daily backupsPaid4.[Solid Backups NextGen](https://searchwp.com/recommends/solid-backups/ "Solid Backups")All-in-one solutionPaid5.[BlogVault](https://searchwp.com/recommends/blogvault/ "BlogVault")Large websitesPaid 6.[WPvivid](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/ "WPvivid")Free with premium featuresFree## How We Test WordPress Backup Plugins

When we test backup plugins, we create real WordPress sites with actual content, then put each plugin through situations you might face.

This includes testing full site restores, partial file recovery, and database-only restores to see how each plugin handles different emergency scenarios.

We also test backup speed with sites of various sizes. For example, some plugins work great on small blogs but struggle with large [eCommerce sites](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads (2025)") that have thousands of products and images.

Besides that, the most important test isnât how well a plugin creates backups, itâs how reliably it restores them. For example, if youâre [improving WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/ways-to-improve-wordpress-search/ "11 Ways to Improve WordPress Search (Expert Tips)"), then itâs a good idea to have backups and easily restore them in case something goes wrong.

Thatâs why we restored test sites dozens of times using each plugin, checking for missing files, broken links, and database errors. Some plugins create perfect backups but fail during the restore process, leaving you worse off than before.

The best backup plugins should also get your site back online quickly with minimal technical knowledge required. So we test restores to different hosting environments to ensure the backups work even when moving between hosting providers.

That said, letâs dive into the details for each backup plugin.

## [1. Duplicator â Best for Site Migration and Cloning](https://duplicator.com/ "Duplicator")

[![Duplicator](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/duplicator-1030x486.png)](https://duplicator.com/)[Duplicator](https://duplicator.com/ "Duplicator") is the best WordPress plugin for backups we have tested so far. When you need to move a WordPress site or create an exact copy for testing, most backup plugins fall short.

Thatâs not the case with Duplicator, as it creates a backup and also help migrate sites between hosting providers or create staging environments.

What sets Duplicator apart is how it packages your entire site (files, content, database, and all) into a single installer package that works anywhere.

Itâs like having a complete copy of your website that you can deploy on any hosting provider.

The plugin creates two files: an archive containing all your website files and an installer script that rebuilds your site automatically. We found this approach much more reliable than traditional backup methods when moving between different hosting environments.

The installer handles all the technical details like updating database URLs and file paths.

Weâve used Duplicator to migrate over 50 websites in the past two years, including moving WPBeginnerâs development sites between different servers.

Another interesting thing we found was Duplicatorâs scheduling feature that lets you create regular backup packages automatically. Besides that, the Pro version also includes cloud storage integration and larger site support.

**Pros:**

- Excellent for site migrations between hosting providers
- Creates complete, portable site packages
- Handles complex sites with custom configurations
- Free version works well for basic migrations
- Professional migration service available

**Cons:**

- Free version offers limited features for larger websites

**Why Select Duplicator:** After testing, Duplicator is the go-to choice when you need daily backups, migrate WordPress sites, or create exact copies for development.

**Pricing:** Duplicator offers premium plans starting from $49.99 per year. There is also a [Lite version](https://wordpress.org/plugins/duplicator/ "Duplicator Lite") that you can use for free.

## [2. UpdraftPlus â Best Free Backup Solution](https://wordpress.org/plugins/updraftplus/ "UpdraftPlus")

[![UpdraftPlus](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/updraftplus-1030x330.png)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/updraftplus/)Finding a backup plugin thatâs both powerful and free seems impossible, but [UpdraftPlus](https://wordpress.org/plugins/updraftplus/ "UpdraftPlus") delivers exactly that. We recommend UpdraftPlus to beginners because it offers automated backups without charging a monthly fee.

The free version handles everything most small websites need, including scheduled backups and one-click restores.

When we installed UpdraftPlus on our testing site, we were pleased to see that it automatically creates backups to our chosen schedule and stores them in the Google Drive account. When we restored some accidentally deleted posts, the process took less than five minutes from start to finish.

That said, the plugin separates your backups into logical components, such as database, plugins, themes, uploads, and core files. This means you can restore just your database if you need to recover posts, or only your uploads folder if you accidentally deleted images.

UpdraftPlus also works with different cloud storage services, including Dropbox, Google Drive, and Amazon S3. The free version limits you to basic cloud storage options, but thatâs perfect for most sites.

**Pros:**

- Completely free for basic automated backups
- Works with popular cloud storage services
- Advanced restore options for specific components
- User-friendly interface perfect for beginners
- Large community and excellent support

**Cons:**

- Advanced features require premium upgrade
- Can be slow with very large sites
- Limited backup destinations in free version

**Why Select UpdraftPlus:** UpdraftPlus offers the best balance of features and affordability weâve found in a backup plugin. The free version handles routine backup needs perfectly, making it ideal for small business websites and personal blogs.

**Pricing:** UpdraftPlus is a free WordPress plugin. It also offers [premium plans](https://searchwp.com/recommends/updraftplus/ "UpdraftPlus Pro") from $70 per year.

## [3. Jetpack VaultPress Backup â Best for Automatic Backups](https://searchwp.com/recommends/jetpack-vaultpress-backup/ "Jetpack VaultPress Backup")

[![Jetpack VaultPress backup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/jetpack-vaultpress-backup-1030x516.png)](https://searchwp.com/recommends/jetpack-vaultpress-backup/)When you want backups that happen automatically without any setup or maintenance, [Jetpackâs VaultPress Backup](https://searchwp.com/recommends/jetpack-vaultpress-backup/ "Jetpack's VaultPress Backup") is hard to beat.

Once installed, it creates daily backups automatically and stores them securely on Jetpackâs servers without requiring any configuration.

This feature impressed us the most because it doesnât impact your siteâs performance, as the backup process doesnât take place on your hosting service.

The restore process is exceptionally smooth through Jetpackâs dashboard. We were able to restore entire sites and individual posts with ease.

VaultPress also integrates seamlessly with other Jetpack features, creating a unified management experience. The security scanning and uptime monitoring complement the backup service well, giving you a complete site protection package.

However, this integration means youâre committed to the Jetpack ecosystem.

**Pros:**

- Completely automated with zero maintenance required
- Real-time backup capabilities for active sites
- Excellent restore interface with granular control
- Integrated security scanning and monitoring
- Reliable cloud storage on Jetpackâs servers

**Cons:**

- More expensive than standalone backup plugins
- Requires Jetpack installation and account
- Limited customization options

**My Verdict:** VaultPress Backup excels when you want a premium, fully automated backup solution. Itâs particularly valuable for busy sites where manual backup management isnât practical.

**Pricing:** Jetpack VaultPress Backup prices start from $4.95 per month. This is also a [free version](https://wordpress.org/plugins/jetpack-backup/ "Jetpack VaultPress Backup Free Version") of the plugin you can try.

## [4. Solid Backups â Cloud Based Backup Solution](https://searchwp.com/recommends/solid-backups/ "Solid Backups")

[![Solid backups nextgen](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/solid-backups-nextgen-1030x648.png)](https://searchwp.com/recommends/solid-backups/)[Solid Backups](https://searchwp.com/recommends/solid-backups/ "Solid Backups") by SolidWP (formerly known as BackupBuddy) is the next backup plugin for WordPress on our list. What sets it apart is how it combines automated backups, easy restores, and site migration tools into one comprehensive package.

The standout feature for us was the Solid Backup Stash, their cloud storage service that comes included with every plan. It is a secure, remote storage destination for your Solid Backups.

Unlike other plugins that require you to connect your own storage accounts, Stash provides cloud-based backup storage right out of the box.

We also tested the restoration process extensively, and the plugin handles both complete site restores and granular file recovery exceptionally well.

**Pros:**

- Comprehensive solution combining backups, migration, and security
- Included cloud storage eliminates setup complexity
- Strong restore and migration capabilities
- Malware scanning adds extra security layer
- Good support and documentation

**Cons:**

- Higher price point than specialized backup plugins
- Interface can feel overwhelming for simple backup needs
- No free version

**My Verdict:** Solid Backups works best when you want a cloud based plugin to handle all your backup and migration needs.

**Pricing:** Solid Backups NextGen prices start from $8.25 per month for 1 website.

## [5. BlogVault â Best for Large Websites](https://searchwp.com/recommends/blogvault/ "BlogVault")

[![BlogVault](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/blogvault-1030x583.png)](https://searchwp.com/recommends/blogvault/)[BlogVault](https://searchwp.com/recommends/blogvault/ "BlogVault") specializes in managing backups for large WordPress sites and is a SaaS based backup solution for WordPress.

The pluginâs incremental backup system only captures changes since the last backup, dramatically reducing the time and server resources required.

We tested the plugin on a large eCommerce site, and BlogVault completed daily backups in under 10 minutes. On the other hand, a few other plugins would take hours to complete the backup process.

Another thing we really liked was the staging environment feature thatâs invaluable for testing updates on large sites. You can create a complete copy of the production site for testing, then push approved changes back to the live site with confidence.

**Pros:**

- Excellent performance with large, complex sites
- Incremental backups reduce server load and backup time
- Integrated staging environment for safe testing
- Real-time monitoring and detailed reporting
- Handles high-traffic sites without performance impact

**Cons:**

- Higher pricing than simpler backup solutions
- High learning curve for advanced features

**My Verdict:** We recommend BlogVault for anyone looking for a SaaS based solution. It justifies its premium pricing for large websites where backup speed and reliability are critical.

**Pricing:** BlogVault prices start from $149 per year for 1 site with the Plus plan. The price goes up if you add more sites or require more features.

## [6. WPvivid â Best Free Alternative ](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/ "WPvivid")

[![WPvivid](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpvivid-1030x337.png)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/)[WPvivid](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/ "WPvivid") surprised us by offering premium-level features in its free version that many paid plugins charge for. We recommend the plugin to users who need advanced features but canât justify premium plugin costs.

The free version includes automated scheduling, multiple cloud storage destinations, and advanced backup options.

For instance, you can easily set up automated backups to Google Drive, Dropbox, Amazon S3, Microsoft Onedrive, DigitalOcean Space, and FTP services in the free version.

The backup process is also reliable, and the restore functionality works smoothly through the WordPress admin interface.

**Pros:**

- Substantial features available in free version
- Staging site functionality without premium cost
- Reliable migration and restore capabilities
- Multiple cloud storage options included
- Regular updates and active development

**Cons:**

- Some advanced features still require premium upgrade
- Documentation could be more comprehensive

**My Verdict:** WPvivid offers remarkable value by including premium features in its free version. Itâs an excellent choice for users who need advanced backup capabilities but want to minimize costs without sacrificing functionality.

**Pricing:** WPvivid is a free WordPress backup plugin. It also offers a Pro version with prices starting from $49 per year.

## Which WordPress Backup Plugin Should You Choose?

Protecting your WordPress site doesnât have to be complicated or expensive. The right backup plugin gives you peace of mind while running automatically in the background.

Based on our extensive testing, our top recommendation is [Duplicator](https://duplicator.com/ "Duplicator"). It provides reliable site migration capabilities and solid backup functionality that covers the essential protection most websites need.

The pluginâs ability to create complete site packages makes it invaluable for both routine backups and emergency site moves.

Duplicator also offers additional cloud storage options and automated scheduling make it worth the investment for growing businesses. The professional migration service can save hours of technical work when moving complex sites between hosting providers.

## FAQs about Backup Plugins for WordPress

Here are some questions about WordPress backup plugins.

#### 1. How often should I backup my WordPress site?

We recommend daily backups for active websites that get regular updates or new content. For static sites that rarely change, weekly backups are usually sufficient. However, you should always create a manual backup before making major changes like updating themes or plugins, regardless of your regular schedule.

**2. Whatâs the difference between full and incremental backups?**

Full backups create a complete copy of your entire website every time, while incremental backups only save files that have changed since the last backup. We prefer incremental backups for large sites because theyâre faster and use less storage space, but full backups are simpler to manage for smaller websites.

**3. Can backup plugins slow down my website?**

Well-designed backup plugins run in the background and shouldnât affect your siteâs loading speed for visitors. However, backup creation can temporarily use server resources. We recommend scheduling backups during low-traffic periods, typically late at night or early morning.

We hope this article helped you pick the best WordPress backup plugins. You may also want to see our pick for the [best WordPress search plugins](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and [how to create a professional-looking search page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

If youâre ready to create a smart search page on your site, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP").

### [How to Fix Divi Search Not Working in WordPress (4 Solutions)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-divi-search-not-working/)

**Published:** August 28, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Divi is among the most popular website and page builders for WordPress. It lets you create search pages and add search forms anywhere on your site.

However, itâs possible that the Divi search might not work due to different reasons.

Fixing the search function is an essential task since visitors who canât find what theyâre looking for on your site are likely to leave it immediately.

In this guide, weâll cover the most popular reasons why Divi search on your site may not work and how to fix the problem.

## 4 Common Reasons for Divi Search Not Working

To get started, letâs look at the 4 most popular reasons why Divi search might not work on your site.

### Weak WordPress Search

The first reason is WordPress itself.

Even though itâs a powerful and convenient content management system, it has a rather weak search engine.

No wonder when you do a search and get no results, you might think itâs broken.

For example, the default WordPress search engine doesnât:

- Search for custom post types
- Consider post categories, custom fields, and tags
- Have proper integration with eCommerce plugins
- Index content of documents and PDF files
- Allow you to customize your search
- And many moreâ¦

### Theme Incompatibility

Another reason why your Divi search may not work is the incompatibility of Divi with your current WordPress theme.

Not every theme is compatible with page builders by default.

So if itâs your case, the Divi search module may simply not work.

### Plugin Conflict

You may also have problems with Divi search in case of a plugin conflict.

There are a lot of plugins for WordPress, and not all of them are compatible with each other.

Thatâs especially true for unpopular plugins whose developers have stopped updating them.

If you use one of them on your site, it can cause problems not only with Divi but with any other plugins.

### Outdated Version of Divi or WordPress

Another possible reason for Divi search not working is outdated versions of Divi or WordPress.

Itâs crucial to update WordPress and the plugins you use on your site in time due to security and compatibility reasons.

If youâre using an older version, then itâs possible that the search feature might not work properly.

## How to Fix Divi Search Not Working

Now that youâve learned the most popular reasons Divi search may not work on your site, letâs look at how to deal with them.

### Create an Advanced Divi Search

The easiest way to improve search on your WordPress site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the best WordPress search plugin on the market, with over 50,000 active installs.

It helps you customize the search on your site to meet your needs with no need of coding or deep WordPress knowledge.

With SearchWP, you can [allow visitors to search for posts by tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-tags-not-searchable/ "How to Fix WordPress Tags Not Searchable (Easy Steps)") and custom fields, limit the search to specific post content, [change the order of search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Fix WordPress Tags Not Searchable (Easy Steps)"), and more.

Here are some other amazing features of this plugin:

- **[Fully compatible with Divi](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)")**: SearchWP automatically integrates with the Divi search module, allowing you to start improving your search right away.
- [**AJAX live search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Show search results on your site in real-time to help your visitors find the content theyâre looking for faster.
- [**Fuzzy search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"): Help your visitors find the right content, even if they made a typo in a search query, to increase the number of pageviews you get.
- **[WooCommerce integration](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)")**: Make product attributes searchable on your site to give your customers a new way to search for products and boost your sales.

With that being said, letâs start customizing the search on your site to make it smarter with SearchWP.

#### Step 1: Install and Activate the SearchWP Plugin

The first step is to download SearchWP and install it on your site.

To get started, you can go and [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) by visiting the website and signing up for an account.

Next, log in to your SearchWP dashboard and navigate the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once there, simply click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, you should also copy your SearchWP license key in the lower left corner.

Now youâre ready to install the plugin on your website. Need a refresher on how to do that? You can refer to this [detailed guide on installing a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once you install SearchWP on your site, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, youâre ready to move on to the next step and customize your search to make it smarter.

#### Step 2: Customize Your Divi Search to Make it Smarter

By setting up a search engine in SearchWP, you can control how search works on your site.

For example, you can manage which attributes it should consider, which categories to exclude, include custom fields, and more.

SearchWP allows you to create multiple search engines and connect them to different search forms. You can then display these forms on individual Divi pages on your site.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and click **Sources & Settings** under the Default search engine section.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a new window will open with different sources to consider in the search process. The default sources include are Posts, Pages, and Media.

If you want to exclude any sources, then simply uncheck the box next to the sources.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Letâs say you want to limit the search on our site to posts since thatâs the main type of content we publish.

You will need to uncheck all search sources except the **Posts** to do that.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)When youâre finished managing your search sources, press **Done** to save the changes.

Now, letâs make WordPress consider post custom fields, tags, and categories when performing a search. Itâll help our visitors to find the right content.

To get started, head to the Posts search source and click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, a new window will pop up where you can manage post attributes.

To include additional information in the search process, simply click the **Custom Fields** field and choose from the dropdown menu.

For example, if you have a food blog, then you can include custom fields like ingredients, calorie count, cuisine, and more.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)Once youâve done that, you can also click the **Taxonomies** field and choose the Categories and Tags taxonomies.

Making these taxonomies searchable allows your visitors to search for posts by their tags and categories.

![Choose post taxonomies to consider](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-post-taxonomies-to-consider-1-1030x599.png)After youâve finished managing the attributes, click **Done**.

When youâre happy with how you customized your Divi search engine, click **Save** to store all the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Your Divi search is now much smarter, and youâll always find what youâre looking for.

Letâs make sure of that by creating a new separate Divi search page and testing our new search engine.

#### Step 3: Create a Divi Search Page

To get started, youâll need to navigate to **Pages Â» Add New** in your WordPress dashboard.

![go to Pages Add New](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Pages-Add-New.png)On the next screen, you will see the content editor.

Go ahead and click the **Use Divi Builder** button.

![Click edit with Divi builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-edit-with-divi-builder-1030x517.png)On the following screen, Divi will present you with 3 options.

You can select the **Build From Scratch** option to get started.

![choose the Build from scratch option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-Build-from-scratch-option.png)Then, choose the row layout that suits you best.

 For example, weâll select the first one for this tutorial.

![choose a row layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-row-layout.png)Once done, youâll now need to add a module.

Simply search for the **SearchWP Form** module and click on it to add it to the page.

![Add SearchWP forms module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-forms-module-1030x551.png)Next, youâll need to select a search form. Simply click the Search Form dropdown menu and select one.

If you want to learn more, then please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

![Select your search form for divi](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-search-form-for-divi-1030x627.png)Once thatâs done, click the green check mark icon to save your changes.

When youâre happy with how your search page looks, click the **three-dot** icon at the bottom of the page.

![click three dot icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-three-dot-icon.png)This will open options to Save Draft or Publish the page.

Go ahead and click **Publish** to make your page live.

![click Publish](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Publish-3.png)#### Step 4: Test Your New Advanced Search in Divi

The next step is to test your new search engine. To do that, open the Divi search page you published and search for some search term.

For example, we have several posts on our site with the âTutorialâ custom field. Letâs try to find them only by this field to try out our new search.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-12.png)As you can see, we found our posts, even though they donât contain the word âTutorialâ in the title, excerpts, or content.

It means that our Divi search became smarter, as now it considers post custom fields when performing a search.

![we found the posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-posts.png)This will make it easier for visitors to find the content they want and reduce the number of cases of missing search results.

It should also fix the issue of Divi search not working.

### Fix Theme Incompatibility

The first step is to find out if the incompatibility of your theme actually causes your Divi search problems.

The fastest and easiest way to do that is to switch to one of the default WordPress themes.

To get started, navigate to **Appearance Â» Themes** from your WordPress dashboard.

![go to Themes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Themes.png)Then, find one of the default themes. For example, these will include **Twenty Twenty-Five**.

From here, you can click **Activate** under its section.

![Change to default theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-to-default-theme-1030x556.png)After youâve switched to a default WordPress theme, check out how the search works.

If it works fine now, then the problem was in the incompatibility of your theme with Divi.

The best solution in this case is to use a theme that is fully compatible with Divi. Itâll help avoid errors not only with search but also with other elements of your site.

But, if search still doesnât work even after youâve changed themes, the root of the problem lies somewhere else.

For example, it can also happen as a result of a plugin conflict.

### Fix Plugin Conflicts

To check if the plugin conflict causes problems with your Divi search, youâll need to deactivate all plugins except for the Divi Builder.

To get started, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![go to the Plugins tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-Plugins-tab.png)Once there, tick the leftmost checkbox to select all the plugins installed on your site at once.

After that, make sure you uncheck the **Divi Builder** plugin.

![uncheck the Divi builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/uncheck-the-Divi-builder.png)Next, click on the **Build actions** drop-down menu at the top.

From here, choose the **Deactivate** option, and click **Apply**.

![deactivate all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deactivate-all-plugins-3.png)After youâve deactivated all the plugins on your site except Divi Builder, check to see if your search now works.

If so, a plugin conflict was the reason Divi search wasnât working on your site.

The best solution in this case is to gradually activate plugins one by one until you find the one thatâs causing problems with search.

If your Divi search still doesnât work, check out the following solution.

### Update Your Divi Plugin and WordPress Versions

First, letâs check out if your WordPress is up to date.

To do that, navigate to your dashboard and see if there is a message saying that a new version of WordPress is available.

If so, click the **Please update now** link to update to the latest version of WordPress.

![click Update Now](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Update-Now-1.png)The next step is to check your version of Divi.

To get started, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![navigate to the Plugins tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-Plugins-tab-2.png)Then, find the **Divi Builder** plugin and check if it has the âThere is a new version of Divi Builder availableâ message under its section.

If the message is present, simply click the **Update now** link to update your Divi version.

![update your version of Divi](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/update-your-version-of-Divi.png)After you make sure your site has the latest available version of WordPress and Divi, go and check your search.

If it still doesnât work even after youâve tried everything weâve talked about in this guide, then it is a good idea to reach out to Divi customer service.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix Divi search not working on your site. You may also want to see [how to create a Divi search results page template tutorial](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)") and [best WordPress page builders for newbies](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/ "7 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)").

Ready to empower your Divi search? You can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP today").

### [SearchWP vs Better Search: Which WordPress Search Tool is Better?](https://searchwp.com/searchwp-vs-better-search-which-wordpress-search-tool-is-better/)

**Published:** August 26, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When it comes to choosing a search plugin for your WordPress site, youâll find multiple options in the market.

Among them will be SearchWP and Better Search. Both of these plugins help improve the default search and provide a better user experience for your visitors.

However, there are a lot of differences between the two in terms of features, ease of use, pricing, and more.

In this article, weâll compare the features, ease of use, customer support, and pricing of these 2 plugins.

Based on this comparison, you can choose the best option for your WordPress site that fits your needs. Hereâs a quick overview of the two plugins:

**[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")****[Better Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/better-search/ "Better Search")****Overview**Comprehensive, beginner-friendly, premiumFree, simpler, fewer features compared to SearchWP**Features**Seamless theme integration
Enrich search results
Multiple search engines support
On-site search activity tracking
PDFs and documents indexing
Show promoted ads
Change search results order
Add live searchSeamless integration with WordPress theme
Search everything (excerpts, categories, tags)
Inbuilt cache
Search tracking (limited)**Ease of Use**User-friendly interface, setup wizard, highly customizableStandard WordPress UI, less intuitive**Customer Support**Highly responsive support team 
AI assistant
Ticketing system
Extensive documentationWordPress support forum
Email support in paid plan
Basic documentation**Pricing**Premium plans starting from $99/yearFree + premium plans starting at $49/year## How We Tested SearchWP vs Better Search

To give you an honest comparison, we tested both SearchWP and Better Search on identical WordPress test sites over several weeks.

We wanted to see how they performed in real-world conditions, not just read about their features.

We evaluated each plugin across five key areas that matter most to WordPress site owners:

- **Overview** â We looked at the overall functionality and what each plugin actually delivers compared to their promises.
- **Features** â We tested different features offered by the plugin, how they help improve the default search, and how well each plugin worked with popular WordPress themes and other plugins.
- **Ease of Use** â Another factor we checked is how easy it is to set up and use each plugin from scratch.
- **Customer Support** â While testing, we reached out to customer support and how helpful their answers were for solving actual problems.
- **Pricing** â We compared pricing structures to see which plugin offers better value for different types of websites and budgets.

That said, letâs look at the comparison.

## SearchWP vs Better Search: Overview

Before we get into a detailed comparison of these 2 WordPress search plugins, letâs look at their brief overview.

### SearchWP

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the most comprehensive solution on the market that has over 50,000 active installs.

Itâs a premium plugin that comes with tons of helpful features you can use to completely customize how search works on your site.

For example, you can limit your search to certain types of content, [make PDFs and documents searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), include custom fields and taxonomies in search, and more.

In addition to being a powerful plugin, SearchWP is also beginner-friendly. It has a modern and user-friendly interface that you can easily navigate through.

Plus, you can set it up to meet your needs in a few minutes, even if youâre not tech-savvy.

SearchWP also has an outstanding support team thatâs always ready to help you with any question you have related to the plugin.

### Better Search

[![Better Search free plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/better-search-free-plugin-1030x335.png)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/better-search/)[Better Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/better-search/ "Better Search") is another WordPress plugin that helps you deliver more relevant search results to your visitors.

Itâs free to use and doesnât have a premium version. Trusted by over 6,000 website owners, this plugin has an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars.

Like SearchWP, Better Search allows you to include multiple post types in the search process, make additional data searchable, include or exclude posts, and more.

While testing, we found that it offers fewer features compared to SearchWP but still gets the job done. This plugin is a good choice if you want simplicity and making your site search smarter.

## SearchWP vs Better Search: Features

After getting a quick look at both plugins, letâs see what features they have.

### SearchWP â Features

SearchWP comes with a whole range of features that makes it one of the most powerful tools on the market.

#### Seamless Integration

To begin with, SearchWP has automatic theme integration. After installing it, the plugin integrates with the default search form on your site and also lets you create your own custom search forms.

It saves time and allows you to instantly improve your site search without writing a single line of code.

The best part is that it doesnât matter which WordPress theme you use on your site. SearchWP is fully compatible with any of them, including [custom themes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)").

It also works with the most popular WordPress page builders, such as [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)"), Divi, Oxygen, Beaver Builder and more. This shows that you can be 100% sure that SearchWP will work on your site without any issues.

Other than that, you can also integrate SearchWP with popular eCommerce plugins like WooCommerce, custom fields plugins like Advanced Custom Fields (ACF), form plugins like [WPForms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-wordpress-form-submissions-in-search-results/ "How to Include WordPress Form Submission in Search Results"), knowledge base plugins, and more.

#### Enrich Your Search Results

One of the standout features of SearchWP we found while testing was that you can make a lot of valuable data searchable.

For instance, you can [make custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), categories, shortcodes, tags, product details, and more searchable.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)It allows your visitors to find what theyâre looking for faster, improving their user experience and encouraging them to explore your site further and view more of your content.

To enrich your search results more, you can enable [SearchWPâs partial matches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites") and closest match options.

![Enable partial matches and closest match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-matches-and-closest-match-1030x573.png)This feature makes WordPress look for partial matches if there are no exact ones when enabled.

It also lets your visitors find the right content, even if they made a typo in a search query.

![we found the post despite the typo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-post-despite-the-typo.png)#### Multiple Search Engines Support

SearchWP has a multiple search engine support feature, which makes it more powerful than the Better Search plugin.

![Search engine in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-engine-in-searchWP-1030x521.png)With this feature, you can add supplemental search engines and link them to individual search fields.

As a result, you can create custom search fields with a unique set of settings and place them across your site.

![View new search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-new-search-engine-1030x528.png)For example, you can limit a search field to specific categories or [post types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)"). It allows you to narrow your search and deliver more relevant search results.

#### On-site Search Activity Tracking

Do you want to know what visitors are searching for on your site? Thatâs valuable information that you can use in a lot of different ways.

For example, you can develop new content ideas, find out whatâs missing from your site, collect unique keywords, and more.

While comparing SearchWP with Better Search, we uncovered that it offers a Metrics extension. You can [see your visitorsâ search queries](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") without leaving your dashboard.

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)Plus, you can see which pages are the most popular for a particular search query.

This information allows you to look at your content from a different angle, so itâs easier for you to learn from search insights to grow your business even more.

#### PDFs and Documents Indexing

The default WordPress search completely ignores uploaded PDF files and office documents. Itâs inconvenient and leaves your visitors with no other options but manually look for them.

With SearchWP, you can fix this WordPress issue and [make PDFs and documents searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") in a few clicks.

![Making PDF and documents file searchable in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-document-source-2-1030x550.jpg)Including documents and PDFs in search results helps your visitors find them much faster. It allows you to ensure a better user experience for your audience.

#### Show Promoted Ads on Search Results Page

Another feature that sets apart SearchWP from Better Search is its ability to [show promoted ads](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-promoted-ads-on-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Display Promoted Ads on WordPress Search Results Page") on the search results page.

When visitors use your internal search, theyâre actively looking for specific information or products. This makes them highly engaged and ready to take action.

With SearchWP, you can promote affiliate products, highlight your services, or feature sponsored content that genuinely helps visitors while boosting your revenue.

![Click the toggle to enable promoted ads](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-toggle-to-enable-promoted-ads-1030x512.png)#### Change Search Results Order

Would you like to control the order of search results for particular search terms in WordPress?

SearchWP lets you [change the search results order](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)") and lets you promote a specific page or product on your site.

The SearchWP Custom Results Order extension allows you to affect the order of the search results and customize it to fit your needs.

To start, you can enter a search term to change the results order.

![Enter search query for custom results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-search-query-for-custom-results-order-1030x464.png)Next, you will see a list of results for that particular keyword.

From here, you can simply select which post to display at the top by clicking the âPromote to Topâ button.

![promote specific posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/promote-specific-posts-1030x475.png)#### Add Live Search

While testing and comparing SearchWP with Better Search, we found that SearchWP offers a live search feature.

You can use the free [SearchWP Live Ajax Search plugin ](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/ "SearchWP Live Ajax Search")and allow users to view search results as they type in the search form.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: How the live search looks finally](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Live-search-final-preview.gif)This allows users to see results instantly as they type, significantly speeding up the search process.

Plus, live search is particularly beneficial for users who may not remember the exact terms or correct spellings of what they are looking for without the full query.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to add live search to WordPress for free](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free").

### Better Search â Features

Once you learn the main features of SearchWP, letâs see what Better Search has to offer.

#### Seamless Integration with WordPress Theme

We found that Better Search also has seamless integration with your [WordPress theme](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)").

Once you activate the plugin, it automatically replaces all existing default search fields on your site with smarter ones.

This way, you can start delivering more relevant search results to your visitors right after installation.

However, one downside we found while comparing Better Search with SearchWP was that it doesnât support popular page builders.

This means that you donât get the flexibility and customization options in Better Search as youâd get in SearchWP.

Besides that, we also didnât find any integrations with other WordPress plugins and tools.

#### Search Everything

With Better Search, you can make it consider excerpts, categories, tags, and other attributes of posts and pages.

![Better Search advanced features](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Better-Search-advanced-features.png)Itâs helpful as it allows your visitors to get more relevant search results.

But, in comparison with SearchWP, this plugin has a couple of significant drawbacks.

First, Better Search doesnât allow you to specify what particular taxonomies you want to make searchable. For instance, you cannot select which categories or [tags to include in the search process](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-wordpress-posts-by-tags-only/ "How to Search WordPress Posts by Tags Only (Newbie-Friendly)").

You can only enable search by each of them in general, which gives you less control over the search results.

![enable searching taxonomies](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-searching-taxonomies.png)Secondly, using Better Search, you cannot make custom fields searchable.

Thatâs a huge disadvantage since many WordPress plugins tend to store valuable information inside them.

For example, WooCommerce uses custom fields to store data about [product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"). This means that you canât make this data searchable using Better Search.

![WooCommerce product attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WooCommerce-prodcut-attrubtes.png)#### Inbuilt Cache

During testing, we uncovered that Better Search comes with the inbuilt cache feature to reduce the number of calls to your database.

Caching means storing data in a cache so that visitors can access it faster.

Once you enable the caching feature, the plugin will start caching search results for an hour. It ensures the stable performance of your site without slowing it down.

![enable cache](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-cache.png)In addition, Better Search has integration with popular WordPress caching plugins. This list includes WP-Super-Cache and W3 Total Cache.

#### Search Tracking

Like SearchWP, Better Search allows you to collect data on the search activity of your visitors.

![Better Search popular searches](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Better-Search-popular-searches.png)You can even display a list of popular search queries on your site as a heatmap using a widget.

Sadly, Better Search only shows a list of collected search queries and nothing else.

On the other hand, using the [SearchWP metrics extension](https://searchwp.com/features/metrics/ "SearchWP metrics extension"), you get a complete picture of what your visitors are searching for on your site.

For example, beyond the list of searches, you can also see the number of clicks per search, searches per user, and more.

## SearchWP vs Better Search: Ease of Use

Before choosing a search plugin, you should ask: how quickly you can install a plugin, how easy it is to use, and does it directly affect your siteâs performance.

Ease of use is one of the most important factors you should consider when choosing a plugin.

With that, letâs compare the ease of use of SearchWP and Better Search.

### SearchWP â Ease of Use

Setting up and configuring SearchWP is user-friendly and easy for beginners.

When you first install and activate the plugin, youâll see a setup wizard. By simply following the onscreen instructions, youâll be able to start using SearchWP in no time.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)SearchWP comes with a clean and modern user interface. You can easily navigate it, even if youâre launching your first WordPress site.

For instance, you can easily customize your search engine and add new ones.

![Add new engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-engine-1030x485.png)You can choose which sources to select and configure the search engine according to your requirements.

Besides that, SearchWP also lets you [exclude from search results specific categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)") by adding advanced search rules.

![Enter categories to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-categories-to-exclude-1030x510.png)Another feature we really liked while testing SearchWP was that you can add stopwords and synonyms to help it better understand your content.

A stopword is a word that WordPress ignores when performing a search to improve the relevance of search results.

![Enter stopwords for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-stopwords-for-search-results-1030x590.png)There are a bunch of popular stopwords that SearchWP adds by default, so you donât have to worry about adding them yourself.

SearchWP also lets you [set up a synonym rule](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-synonym-rules-for-wordpress-search/ "How to Set Up Synonym Rules for WordPress Search") and help users find relevant content using words with the same meaning.

![Add new synonyms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-synonyms-1030x421.png)SearchWP also has a [blog with plenty of helpful how-to guides](https://searchwp.com/blog/ "SearchWP Blog Section"). You can check if you need any help in your search engine configuration.

Plus, there is an extensive [knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/ "SearchWP Knowledgebase") for more experienced users. Itâs excellent for those who want to dive even deeper into customizing search on their site.

### Better Search â Ease of Use

Better Search is more suited for users that want basic controls for changing how search works on their site.

That said, it has a couple of major drawbacks regarding its ease of use.

First, it has the standard WordPress plugin user interface, which looks outdated and may scare off some users.

![Better Search settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Better-Search-settings.png)Secondly, if youâre a beginner, then you might not know which setting to enable. Many settings can be complicated for non-technical users to understand.

For example, you can only guess what âBooleanâ and âmySQL fulltext searchâ modes are, as there is no explanation.

![Better Search features](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Better-Search-features.png)## Better Search vs SearchWP: Customer Support

Customer support is one of the core things to consider when choosing a WordPress plugin.

The quality of support directly affects your user experience, thatâs why you shouldnât underestimate its importance.

With that, letâs compare what support options SearchWP and Better Search have to offer.

### SearchWP â Customer Support

SearchWP has a responsive support team thatâs always ready to come to the rescue.

You can start with the AI assistant inside your account dashboard in SearchWP. Simply ask a question and get quick and accurate answers.

![SearchWP support option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-support-option-1030x629.png)Besides that, you can also ask for help using the ticket system that you can find in your WordPress dashboard.

Itâs convenient and doesnât require you to mess around with sending emails.

![Fill out support ticket](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/fill-out-support-ticket-1030x668.png)Also, if you prefer to do everything yourself, you can check out the helpful, clear, and usable [SearchWP documentation](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/ "SearchWP Knowledgebase").

### Better Search â Customer Support

When it comes to Better Search, youâll notice that there is no dedicated customer support, like a live chat or email ticket, for the free version.

However, you can use the support forum on WordPress plugin page and leave your query.

![Better Search support forum](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/better-search-support-forum-1030x562.png)On the other hand, if you upgrade to a paid plan, then you can unlock direct email support.

Besides that, Better Search also offers a [knowledge base](https://webberzone.com/support/knowledgebase/ "Better Search Knowledgebase") that has basic documentation.

From our comparison, SearchWP offers a more extensive and detailed knowledge base and support options.

## SearchWP vs Better Search: Pricing

The price of a plugin plays a crucial role when selecting a solution for your business. Youâd want value for your money, so itâs important to choose the right plugin.

Letâs compare the pricing options that SearchWP and Better Search offer.

### SearchWP â Pricing

SearchWP is a premium WordPress plugin that has [3 pricing plans](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "SearchWP pricing plans") varying in price and features.

[![SearchWP pricing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-pricing-1030x585.png)](https://searchwp.com/buy/)Letâs examine them more closely:

- **Standard plan** â $99/year. License for 1 site, comes with all standard features, extensions, and support.
- **Pro plan** â $199/year. License for 3 sites, comes with everything in the Standard plan along with advanced features, integrations, and priority support.
- **Agency plan** â $399/year. License for an unlimited number of sites and comes with everything in the Pro plan. Plus, you get Client Management feature and premium support.

If you decide to change the plan or cancel your account, you can do that at any time.

SearchWP also offers a 14-day money-back guarantee if you feel that the plugin isnât the right fit.

### Better Search â Pricing 

The base Better Search plugin is free to use, and is excellent for new website owners who want to save some money.

However, it also offers 3 premium plans, starting from $49 per year with the Pro plan. The price goes up to $299/year with its Agency plan.

![Better Search pricing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/better-search-pricing-1030x553.png)When compared to SearchWP, the premium plans offered by Better Search donât offer a comprehensive set of features.

That said, Better Search also offers a 14-day refund policy. So you can cancel the subscription if you feel the plugin is not the right fit for your business.

## Better Search vs SearchWP: Our Verdict

Now that weâve compared SearchWP with Better Search, letâs choose the best among these plugins.

Given the number of features, ease of use, and support quality, the winner is SearchWP.

Using this plugin, you can easily customize search on your site to suit your needs without being a tech-savvy person. The number of features SearchWP offers sets it apart from other search plugins.

As the cherry on the cake comes a professional support team thatâs always ready to help you with any requests.

Even though Better Search has fewer features than SearchWP and isnât so easy to use, itâs also a great plugin for new website owners who are looking for a free solution.

But if youâre looking for the ultimate solution, then we highly recommend SearchWP.

We hope this article helped you learn which WordPress search tool is better: SearchWP or Better Search. You may also want to see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") and [how to fix WordPress tags not searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-tags-not-searchable/ "How to Fix WordPress Tags Not Searchable (Easy Steps)").

Are you ready to become one of them and boost search on your site with the best WordPress search plugin? You can go and [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today").

**Tags:** SearchWP, SearchWP or Better Search, SearchWP vs Better Search, what is better SearchWP or Better Search, what is the best search tool SearchWP or Better Search, what to choose SearchWP or Better Search, which plugin is the best SearchWP or Better Search, WordPress search, wordpress search settings

### [How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** August 21, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Looking for a way to create a search page in WordPress?

By adding a custom search page to your site, you can help your visitors find what theyâre looking for faster.

Itâs also a great way to encourage them to use search again in the future and discover more of your content.

In this guide, weâll show you 2 ways to create a professional-looking search page on your site.

You can simply click the links below to jump ahead to any section:

- [Method 1: Create a Search Page With SearchWP](#easiest-way-to-create-a-smarter-search-page)
- [Method 2: Create a Search Page With SeedProd](#create-a-search-page-with-SeedProd)

Letâs begin!

## Why Create a Search Page in WordPress?

Creating a search page on your WordPress site has many advantages:

- **Get more pageviews:** You can promote specific posts, pages, or categories on your search page to engage your visitors and get more pageviews.
- **Promote ads to boost revenue**: A custom search page also allows you to showcase affiliate links, blog posts, products, and more to increase conversions.
- **Customize your search form**: You can add a customized search form to your search page to narrow your search and deliver more relevant results to your visitors.
- **Improve the user experience of your visitors**: By customizing your search page and search form, you help your visitors find the right content faster.
- **Make visitors spend more time on your site**: Satisfied users who found what they were looking for on your site are more likely to continue exploring your content.
- **Improve your siteâs ranking in search engines**. High average session duration and low bounce rate are strong signals to Google that your siteâs pages deserve better positions.

Are you ready to add a search page to your site? Letâs see how you can do that below.

## Method 1: Create a Search Page With SearchWP

The best way to create a search page is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It offers pre-built templates and lots of customization options.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the most powerful WordPress search plugin trusted by over 50,000 website owners.

Using SearchWP, you can completely customize how search works on your site and create a smart search page â no coding needed.

For example, you can [exclude specific pages from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"), make custom fields searchable, and more.

As a result, you can give your visitors more relevant search results and improve their user experience.

Thatâs not all. Hereâs a quick overview of other SearchWP features:

- [**Search tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Want to know your audienceâs interest? SearchWP collects data on what visitors are searching for on your site, allowing you to understand their needs better.
- [**Live search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): With SearchWP, you can enable displaying real-time search results in a few clicks.
- [**Control over search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)"): This plugin lets you manually change the order of search results the way you want.
- **[Multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)")**: Want to add custom search forms to your site? SearchWP allows you to create multiple search engines, customize them, and link to any search forms across your site.

Now that you know how SearchWP helps you create a smart search page, letâs look at how to use it.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, you can visit the SearchWP website and [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today").

Once done, simply log into your SearchWP account and go to the **Downloads** page.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)From here, you can click **Download SearchWP** and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, also copy your SearchWP license key on the same page.

The next step is to install SearchWP on your site. To do that, youâll need to upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

Need a refresher on how to do that? You can follow this [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step guide](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now that SearchWP is up and running, you can use it to create custom search engines and search forms.

To learn more, you may want to see our guide on [how to change the default WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

Now, letâs add a new search

### Step 2: Create a Search Results Page

SearchWP offers pre-built templates for creating a search results page. Plus, you can set up unlimited search results templates and connect them to specific search forms.

To get started, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Templates** in your WordPress dashboard. Here, you will see the default template that will appear on your site when a user performs a search.

Now, you can edit the default template or simply click the âAdd Newâ button. For this tutorial, weâll add a new search results page.

![Edit templates page in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-templates-page-in-searchwp-1030x494.png)On the next screen, you can edit the name of the search results template at the top.

After that, you can select a layout theme for your search results page. SearchWP offers 6 different layouts to choose from.

![Choose a template and change name](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-template-and-change-name-1030x439.png)Under the layout, youâll also see a theme preview.

This helps you select the right theme for your site, as it shows how the results will look like. Do note that the final preview might differ depending on your WordPress theme.

![View theme preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Choose-layout-theme-1030x963.jpg)After that, you can scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section. Here, you will find more options to customize your results page.

For example, SearchWP offers options to change the layout style, enable description, change image size (if youâve select a theme that shows featured images), and more.

![Change theme custom styling](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-theme-custom-styling-1030x640.png)If you scroll down further, you will find more styling options for your search results page.

For instance, you can control the results per page. By default, SearchWP will show 10 results but you can change that.

Other than that, you also get to edit the pagination settings.

![Change results per page and pagination settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-results-per-page-and-pagination-settings-1030x544.png)When youâre done customizing the results page, simply scroll to the top and click the **Save** button.

This will help save all your settings.

![Save results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-results-template-1030x500.png)SearchWP also makes it very easy to embed the results template anywhere on your site.

To start, simply click the **Embed** button at the top next to Save. This will open a new popup window with multiple embed options.

![Embed search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-results-page-1030x494.png)You can use a Gutenberg block, shortcode, and PHP code to embed the template. Plus, youâll also need to select whether youâd like to embed the template on an existing page or create a new page.

For this tutorial, weâll select the âGutenberg Blockâ option and âCreate New Pageâ for our search results template.

Next, youâll need to enter a name for your new page and click **Letâs Go**.

![Enter a name for new results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-a-name-for-new-results-page-1030x513.png)On the next screen, youâll see the SearchWP Results block in the WordPress content editor.

You can simply click the **Publish** button at the top.

![View search results block preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-results-block-preview-1030x430.png)### Step 3: Edit Search Form Settings

Next, youâll need to change the search form settings in SearchWP and ensure that the form points to the newly created results page.

To start, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard. Now, you can create a new form or edit an existing one.

For this tutorial, weâll edit an existing form on our demo site.

![Select a search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-a-search-form-1030x518.png)After that, you will see options to edit the search form. For instance, you can choose a theme and customize it using other options.

For more details, please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

Next, you can scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section. From here, youâll need to change the âResults Pageâ to **SearchWP** and âTarget Pageâ to the search results template you created earlier.

![Point target page to results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/point-target-page-to-results-template-1030x661.png)Besides that, you also get options change the Engine and also choose a Template.

When youâre done, simply scroll to the top and click the **Save** button.

![Save search form settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-search-form-settings-1030x520.png)### Step 4: Test Your New Search Page

Now, letâs test our new search page, enhanced with SearchWP.

For instance, we have multiple posts on WooCommerce. Letâs try to search for it and see how it looks on the new search results page.

![View search form on demo site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-form-on-demo-site-1030x513.png)As you can see, we successfully found the posts using the search term.

Plus, the new search results template also works perfectly.

![Search results template testing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-results-template-testing-1030x522.png)## Method 2: Create a Search Page With SeedProd

If you want to create a fancier search page and want more customization options, then you can use [SeedProd](https://www.seedprod.com/ "SeedProd").

It is the best WordPress website builder plugin, that offers an AI-power website builder, prebuilt templates, and lots of customization options.

[![SeedProd ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/seedprod-site-1030x530.png)](https://www.seedprod.com/)SeedProd comes with an easy-to-use and powerful drag-and-drop editor that allows you to build different page types â without writing a single line of code.

It is fully compatible with popular WordPress themes so that you can easily integrate your new pages into your current design.

All pages you create with this plugin are optimized for fast loading, SEO-ready, and fully responsive.

The best part? SeedProd comes with a lot of ready-made templates so you can start building your page right off the bat and grow your business faster. No need to be an expert designer!

Here you can adjust the width of your search form by moving the **Width** slider left or right.

To do that, move the **Width** slider all the way right.

![set the max width](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/set-the-max-width.png)### Step 6: Add Recent Posts to Your Search Page

Now letâs also add recent posts to our search page. By doing this, you can promote the posts youâd like and get more traffic to your site.

Running a WordPress website teaches most site owners something important about missed opportunities.

This makes them highly engaged and ready to take action.

Without promoted ads, youâre essentially sending potential customers away empty-handed.

They might find your regular content, but they wonât discover the products, services, or affiliate offers that could solve their problems and generate income for your site.

Besides that, the search results page is also an overlooked place by site owners to show important messages and notices.

You can inform visitors about an upcoming sale or maintenance while theyâre searching on your WordPress website.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily show promoted ads on your search results page.

Next, you can upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site. If you donât know how to do that, you can see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activation, youâll see the SearchWPâs onboarding wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the instructions to complete the setup.

### Step 2: Enable Promoted Ads in SearchWP

Next, you can edit the search results template in SearchWP to display promoted search ads.

Hereâs how the settings will look like:

![Edit template settings for form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-template-settings-for-form-1030x625.png)Next, you can scroll to the top and click Save.

This will store all your changes.

![Save search form settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-search-form-settings-1030x520.png)### Step 4: Test Your Promoted Search Ads
