### [How to Fix Elementor Search Form Not Working (5 Easy Ways)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-elementor-search-form-not-working/)

**Published:** August 19, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Do you want to know how to fix Elementor search form not working? There are several reasons that can cause this problem.

Fixing your Elementor search form is very important because visitors who donât find what theyâre looking for on your site will leave it unsatisfied.

In this article, weâll show you how to find out why your Elementor search form isnât working and how to deal with that.

Letâs begin!

## What Causes Elementor Search Form to Not Work

Letâs take a look at the 5 most common reasons that can cause problems with the Elementor search form.

### 1. The Default WordPress Search is Weak

WordPress is a great and powerful blogging platform, but its search engine leaves much to be desired.

Thatâs why after youâve performed a search and found nothing, you might even think that it doesnât work.

It happens because WordPress:

- **Sorts search results by publication date, not by relevance**: Due to that, your recent posts will always appear higher in search results than more relevant ones.
- **Ignores a lot of valuable data when performing a search**: For example, it doesnât consider post custom fields, categories, tags, and more.
- **Doesnât look for partial matches**: If youâve misspelled a search query, you wonât get any search results and may think that search is broken.

### 2. Theme Compatibility Issue

Another reason you might have problems with search is that your WordPress theme isnât compatible with Elementor.

If itâs your case, there is no guarantee that all Elementor widgets will work as expected.

Since the search form is also an Elementor widget, and search plugins like SearchWP also add their own widgets to Elementor as well, then you might have compatibility problems.

### 3. Plugin Conflict

There are thousands of WordPress plugins, and Elementor might not be compatible with each of them.

So when you use Elementor along with incompatible plugins, that can cause problems with its functionality.

In most cases, it applies to unpopular and abandoned plugins, but there may be exceptions.

### 4. Hosting Limitations

There is also a rare but possible case when your hosting limitations cause problems with search.

For example, it requires the latest versions of PHP, MySQL, and a high volume of WP Memory Limit in WordPress.

So if your hosting doesnât meet these requirements, there is a great chance youâll encounter some problems using Elementor.

### 5. Outdated Elementor or WordPress Version

You may also have problems with the Elementor search form if you use an outdated version of Elementor or WordPress.

Itâs important to keep plugins and WordPress up to date to ensure their proper work and compatibility.

## How to Fix Elementor Search Form Not Working

After youâve learned what can cause issues with the Elementor search form, letâs look at how to deal with them in order.

### 1. How to Boost Your WordPress Search

The easiest way to fix the weaknesses of the default WordPress search engine and boost your search is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the most powerful and easiest to use WordPress search plugin on the market.

With SearchWP, you can make include custom fields, tags, categories, and many other post attributes in the search process.

Due to that, you can help your visitors find the right content faster and improve their user experience.

Plus, SearchWP comes with seamless Elementor integration, so you can start using it right after installation.

But these features are not the only reason why more than 50,000 website owners already chose SearchWP. You can also use this plugin to:

- [**Track your visitorsâ searches**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): You can use SearchWP to see what visitors are searching for on your site and find out which search results are most popular.
- [**Display search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): The plugin lets you display search results in live mode to help your visitors find the right content faster.
- [**Create custom search forms**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): SearchWP allows you to create custom search forms with an individual set of settings in just a few clicks, no coding needed.
- [**Exclude specific pages from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"): With SearchWP, you can clean up search results on your site by excluding redundant pages.

Now that you know why SearchWP is the best WordPress search plugin, letâs see how you can use it to boost search on your site.

#### Step 1: Get SearchWP and Install The Plugin

To get started, youâll need to go to the SearchWP website and [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

Once youâve signed up for a new account, you can sign in to your account and go to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)After you get there, click **Download SearchWP** to download the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

You should also copy your SearchWP license key on the same tab when you finish downloading, as youâll need it later on.

The next step is to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site and install the plugin. Need help with that? Check out the [step-by-step tutorial on how to install the WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After you activate SearchWP, youâll see the onboarding wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the instructions to complete the setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, you can move on to the next step and start customizing your search engine.

#### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engine to Make It Smarter

With SearchWP, you can create custom search engines for your site. Think of a search engine as a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing searches.

It also automatically connects to existing search form on your site after installation. So by customizing the **Default** search engine settings, you can control how search works on your site.

Another benefit of using SearchWP is that you can make additional post attributes searchable, limit your search to specific categories, and more.

With that, letâs customize your search engine to make it easier for your visitors to find what theyâre looking for.

First, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, you can choose the sources for your search engine. By default, youâll see that posts, pages, and media will be selected.

For instance, letâs limit the search on our site to posts only, to clean up the search results from redundant pages.

To do that, uncheck all sources except **Posts**.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)When youâre finished managing search sources, press **Done** to save the changes.

Now, letâs also make additional post attributes such as custom fields, tags, and categories searchable on your site. Itâll help you deliver more relevant search results to your visitors.

To get started, navigate to the Posts search source and click **Add/Remove** attributes.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, a new window will popup where you can manage the post attributes. By default, SearchWP will include title, content, slug, excerpt, and author as attributes.

Besides that, you can also include custom post fields in the search process since they often contain valuable information.

To get started, simply click on the **Custom Fields** field and select an option from the dropdown menu.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)For example, if youâre using Advanced Custom Fields, then you can select an option from the dropdown menu.

If you have a food blog, then you can add custom fields like ingredients or cuisine type in the search process. Or if you have a real estate listing, then you can make the number of bedrooms custom fields searchable.

You can also make add common post taxonomies, such as tags and categories, to the search process.

Simply click the **Taxonomies** field and select the Tags and Categories from the dropdown menu.

![Choose taxonomies for search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-taxonomies-for-search-engine-1030x591.png)When youâre finished managing which post attributes, press **Done** to save the changes.

The final step is to save all the changes weâve made to the search engine. Simply scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button.

![Save your engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-engine-1030x437.png)SearchWP also offers advanced options like partial matches. This allows the plugin to show relevant results even if a user makes a spelling mistake or a typo when entering the search term.

Simply go to **SearchWP Â» Settings** and scroll down to the âPartial Matchesâ under General Settings. From here, you can click the toggle to enable it.

![Enable partial searches](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-searches-1030x516.png)If you want to learn more, then please see our guide on [how to enable fuzzy search on your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites").

Thatâs it. Youâve just made search on your Elementor site way smarter than it used to be.

Now, your visitors wonât think your Elementor search form is broken if it doesnât show any results.

You can also create a custom search form and include it to your Elementor page using SearchWP blocks.

![Add search results block in Elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-results-block-in-elementor-1030x597.png)For more details, please see our guide on [how to add a search box to Elementor pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-elementor-page/ "How to Add a Search Box to Elementor Page (Beginnerâs Guide)").

#### Step 3: Test Your New Improved Search

Lastly, you can test your custom search engine and see if itâs working properly.

To get started, simply visit your site and then go to your Elementor page which contains a search form, and type a search term into it.

For example, we have a lot of posts on our test site related to WordPress. Letâs see what happens if we make a typo in a search query.

![click find results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-find-results-6.png)As you can see, we found our posts even though thereâs a typo in the search query.

Our website also highlighted the search terms in our search results. Due to that, we can be sure that our search engine understands that we were searching for âWordPressâ.

![we found out posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-out-posts.png)In this step-by-step guide, you learned how to make Elementor search on your site smarter so no one ever thinks itâs broken.

Now, letâs see how to deal with other issues that may be causing problems with your Elementor search form.

### 2. How to Fix Theme Compatibility Issue

The first step is to check the compatibility of your theme with Elementor.

If you use a premium theme, the easiest way to do that is to see its official site or documentation.

On the other hand, if you use a free theme, then you can switch your current theme with one that you know is fully compatible with Elementor.

For example, Astra is a theme that works seamlessly with Elementor.

To change the theme, navigate to **Appearance Â» Themes** in your WordPress dashboard.

![WordPress theme customizer](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wordpress-theme-customizer-1030x523.png)Here, you will see different themes for your WordPress website.

Go ahead and press the **Add Theme** button at the top.

![Add a new theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-theme.png)From here, you can use the search bar to find the **Astra** theme

Once youâve located it, and click **Install** and **Activate** under its section.

![install your theme](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-your-theme.png)If the search form works after you change the theme, then the problem is that your theme is not compatible with Elementor.

The best solution in this case is to use a different theme.

### 3. How to Fix Plugin Conflicts

There is an easy way to check if a plugin conflict causes the problem with your Elementor search form.

To do that, youâll need to deactivate all the plugins you use except Elementor. If the problem disappears after that, youâve successfully identified its source.

To get started, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![click Plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Plugins.png)This will take you to the Installed Plugins page in the WordPress dashboard.

Now, youâll need to tick the topmost checkbox to select all plugins at once.

![check all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-all-plugins.png)When youâre selecting all the plugins, make sure to leave out Elementor.

Simply uncheck the Elementor and Elementor Pro plugins.

![uncheck Elementor and Elementor Pro](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/uncheck-Elementor-and-Elementor-Pro.png)After that, click on the **Bulk actions** dropdown menu at the top.

From here, choose the **Deactivate** option and click **Apply** to deactivate all plugins except Elementor and Elementor Pro.

![choose Deactivate and press Apply](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-Deactivate-and-press-Apply.png)When youâve finished, go to your site and check if your Elementor search form is now working.

If plugins deactivation helped, the plugin conflict was the exact reason that caused problems with search on your site.

Now, you can gradually activate the plugins one by one to find out which one is incompatible with Elementor.

When you find it, you should stop using it to avoid any problems.

### 4. How to Deal With Hosting Limitations

To ensure that problems with search on your site are not caused by hosting limitations, you can check the [official Elementor requirements](https://elementor.com/help/requirements/).

But in most cases, you can neither see the full specifications of your hosting nor change them yourself.

So the best thing you can do in this situation is to contact your hosting customer support team.

Theyâll help you find out if your current hosting plan meets Elementorâs requirements and suggest changing it if thatâs not the case.

### 5. How to Update Elementor and WordPress

If your Elementor or WordPress versions are not up to date, this may cause performance and compatibility issues.

First, letâs check if you have the latest version of WordPress.

To do that, navigate to the **Updates** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![click Updates](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Updates.png)If you see the âYou have the latest version of WordPressâ message, then it means youâre using the latest WordPress version.

However, you have an older version, then consider updating to the latest and see if it fixes the error.

![Update wordpress version](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/update-wordpress-version--1030x451.png)After youâve ensured that you have the latest version of WordPress, letâs also check the version of Elementor.

To get started, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![navigate to the Plugins tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-Plugins-tab-1.png)From here, scroll down to the Elementor plugin.

Then, click the **update now** link if you see the notification that there is a new version for Elementor available.

![update your plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/update-your-plugin.png)After youâve updated your version of WordPress and Elementor, check if the problem with search form has disappeared.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix the Elementor search form not working. You may also want to see our guide on [how to exclude category from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)") and [how to customize your WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page (5 Ways)").

Ready to boost your WordPress search and help your visitors find the right content faster? Get started [with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!").

### [How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** August 14, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Do you want to know how to create product search in WooCommerce?

Itâll help your visitors find the products theyâre looking for faster, improve their user experience, and increase your profits.

In this guide, weâll show you how you can create a smart product search on your site in a few simple steps, with no coding needed.

Letâs begin!

## Why Create Product Search in WooCommerce?

The default WordPress search engine sees no difference between WooCommerce products and regular posts and pages.

It makes it harder for your customers to find the products they came for since they get a lot of redundant search results.

Plus, WordPress completely ignores WooCommerce product attributes when performing a search.

Due to that, your customers canât search for products by color options, size, SKU, and more.

Thankfully, you can easily tackle both of these problems by **creating a product search**. Itâll help you:

- **Clean up your search results**. Limit search on your site to WooCommerce products so that nothing distracts your customers from shopping.
- **Enable search by product attributes**. Make product attributes searchable on your site to make it easier for your customers to find the products they want.
- **Boost your sales**. The easier it is for visitors to find the right product in your store, the more sales youâll make.
- **Build a loyal customer base**. Happy customers whoâve had a positive user experience browsing your store are more likely to come again.
- **Improve your siteâs SEO**. When visitors spend a lot of time on your site, thatâs a good signal to Google that itâs worth ranking higher in search results.

After youâve learned why WooCommerce product search is essential, letâs see how you can add it to your site.

## How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search

The easiest way to make a WooCommerce product search on your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)This is the most powerful and easy-to-use WordPress search plugin trusted by over 50,000 website owners worldwide.

SearchWP has seamless integration with WooCommerce, allowing you to fully [customize WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search") to meet your needs.

For example, you can enable search by product SKUs, make product attributes searchable, [add search by WooCommerce categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce"), and many more.

Here are some other SearchWP features that will boost your search and help grow your business:

- [**Exclude specific products from search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce"): Hide redundant products from search results to clean them up and help your customers find what theyâre looking for faster.
- **[Track searches of your customers](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site")**: Find out what searches customers perform on your site to come up with new product ideas and meet their needs.
- [**Customize your search results page**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page (5 Ways)"): Change the appearance of your search results page to promote your products, collect emails, or anything else.
- **[Add multiple search forms to your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)")**: Place additional search forms on your site to encourage customers to use them and discover more of your products.

With that, letâs see how you can use SearchWP to enhance WooCommerce product search on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, you can go to visit the website and [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP today").

Once done, youâll need to log in to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, you should also copy the license key in the bottom left corner on the same page.

Now the next step is to upload the ZIP file you downloaded to your site and install SearchWP. If you need help with that, then you can check out this detailed step-by-step tutorial on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once you install and activate SearchWP, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After installing and activating SearchWP on your site, the next step is to install the WooCommerce integration extension.

### Step 2: Install the SearchWP WooCommerce Integration Extension

The extension ensures seamless integration of SearchWP and WooCommerce plugins, allowing you to make product searches much smarter.

Plus, if youâre using the WooCommerce search form, then the extension helps display results using the SearchWP engine.

To install it, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard. Then, find the WooCommerce Integration extension and click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)After installing and activating the extension, youâre ready to start setting up your search engine.

### Step 3: Set Up Your Custom Search Engine

A search engine includes a set of rules and conditions that SearchWP relies on when performing a search.

With SearchWP, you can completely customize them to meet your needs and change the way search works on your site.

For example, you can manage search sources, [exclude specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or categories from search, adjust attribute relevance weights, and much more.

The best part is that SearchWP allows you to add multiple search engines and link them to individual search forms throughout your site. This way, you can [create fully customized search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") with a unique set of rules and conditions.

Right now, there is only the **Default** search engine, which SearchWP automatically creates after installation. You can add new engines as well, but for this tutorial, weâll customize the default engine.

#### Limit Search to WooCommerce Products

First, youâll need to visit the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard.

From here, you can click the **Sources & Settings** button and see which sources the native search engine will include while searching.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Youâll notice that the **Default** search engine will have 3 search sources: **Posts**, **Pages**, and **Media**.

To create a smart product search, you can limit the search to WooCommerce products as a source.

Letâs uncheck all other search sources except **Products**, so that customers can only see our products among search results.

![Only select product as source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/only-select-product-as-source-1030x500.png)When youâre finished customizing your search sources, press **Done** to save the changes.

#### Make New Product Attributes Searchable

You can also completely remove attributes from a search source or add new ones.

To get started, scroll down to the Products source section and press the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)By default, SearchWP will use the title, content, slug, excerpt, and author as attributes for a source.

If you want to remove any default attributes, simply uncheck the boxes.

To add new attributes, you can click on the **Custom Fields** or **Taxonomies** field, depending on what you want to add. Then, start typing the name of an attribute you want to make searchable until the dropdown menu of available attributes pops up.

For example, you can add **\_sku** to make product SKUs searchable on your site.

![Add SKU custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-6-1030x375.jpg)There is also a way to quickly make other product attributes searchable, such as color, price, size, and so on.

#### Let Your Customers Search by Product Tags

You can also allow our customers to search by product tags.

First, you can click on the **Taxonomies** field.

![Select product tags](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-tags--1030x625.png)Then, find the **Product tags** option and click on it to add tags as an attribute.

When youâve finished managing your search engine attributes, click **Done** to save the changes youâve made.

#### Manage WooCommerce Categories You Want to Appear in Search

Along with tags, SearchWP also allows you to add product categories to the search process.

This allows users to find entire categories instead of looking for a single product.

You can do this by adding the **Product categories** under the Taxonomies section when managing the attributes.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)When youâre finished, click the âDoneâ button.

Besides that, you can also exclude some WooCommerce product categories from search on our site.

To get started, press the **Edit Rules** button.

![Edit product rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-product-rules-1030x443.png)Next, a new popup window will .

Initially, you wonât see any rules. To get started, simply click **Add Rule**.

![Add new rule for products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-rule-for-products-1030x383.png)On the next screen, you can set different conditions to exclude product categories.

First, youâll need to click on the **Only show entries if** menu and change it to **Exclude entries if**.

Once youâve done that, you can select **Taxonomy** and then select âProduct categories (product\_cat)â from the dropdown menu.

![Exclude products rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-products-rules-1-1030x504.png)Next, you can enter the name of the category you want to exclude from the search in the rightmost field.

For example, letâs say you want to exclude the âUncategorizedâ category from the search.

When youâre satisfied with excluded categories, press **Done**.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how you can exclude any product from search in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce").

#### Save Your Customized Search Engine

The final step is to save all changes you made to your search engine.

To do that, simply press the **Save** button in the upper right corner.

![Save your engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-engine-1030x437.png)Thatâs it. Now you have a smart WooCommerce product search on your site.

Letâs test it out and see how it works.

### Step 4: Test Your New Product Search

To do that, youâll need to leave your WordPress dashboard and go over to your site.

There are several posts on our test site about how to choose a nice beanie for each season.

![we search for beanie](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-search-for-beanie.png)Letâs try to search for âbeanieâ and see what results we get.

As you can see, there are **only products** among search results even though we have a few articles on the same topic.

![how to create product search in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-create-product-search-in-WooCommerce-.png)It means that the rule we added to our search engine works, and WordPress performs searches only among products.

Letâs also check if we can find a product by the tag. We have a product on our test site called **Warm Jacket**.

It doesnât have any description, hasnât been added to any category, but has the **Winter Clothes** tag.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-10.png)Letâs see if we can find this product by its tag alone.

As you can see, we successfully found the product.

![we found our product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-product.png)It means that WordPress now considers product tags when performing a search.

## Bonus: Create a Search Page in WooCommerce

Using SearchWP, you can add a search form to a separate page to give your visitors a more convenient way to search for products.

A separate search page helps you narrow your search results, promote your products, collect emails, and more.

The best part is that SearchWP now supports some of the most popular site builders, like Elementor, Divi, Beaver Builder, and Oxygen.

Here is an example of how it looks on our test site:

![search page with products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-page-with-products.png)For more information, check out our step-by-step guide on [how to create a search page in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

## FAQs About Product Search in WooCommerce

Here are some common questions users have about creating a product search for WooCommerce.

**1. Why should I create a product search in WooCommerce?**

Creating a product search in WooCommerce enhances user experience by allowing visitors to efficiently find the products they seek. It limits the search to WooCommerce products and enables searches by product attributes like color, size, and SKU, improving site navigation and potentially boosting sales.

**2. Can I add a search form to a specific page on my WooCommerce site?**

Yes, SearchWP allows you to add a search form to a separate page by using dedicated blocks. Plus, it also supports popular site builders like Elementor or Divi, allowing to your create custom search pages.

**3. What plugin can I use to create a smart WooCommerce product search?**

SearchWP is a widely recommended plugin for enhancing WooCommerceâs search capabilities. It offers functionalities like searching by custom fields, product categories, tags, and more, and is trusted by over 50,000 sites for its seamless integration with WooCommerce.

## Whatâs Nextâ¦

Creating a smart WooCommerce search helps your visitors find the right products, improve their user experience and boost your sales.

We hope this article helped you learn how to create a product search in WooCommerce in a few simple steps.

You may also want to see our guides on [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/) and [how to add a WooCommerce search form to your shop page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/).

If youâre ready to enhance search on your site and grow your business, you can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

### [How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** August 7, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Would you like to show certain blog posts or products at the top of the search results for certain keywords in WordPress?

The default search doesnât allow you to control the search results order. As a result, when someone searches for your main services, they might have to scroll past old blog posts and random pages to find what they need.

By changing the results order, you can deliver more relevant search results to your visitors and improve their user experience.

In this step-by-step guide, weâll show you the easiest way to change the order of search results on your site.

## Why Change the Search Results Order in WordPress?

Customizing the order of search results on your site can be useful for several reasons.

First, by default, WordPress sorts search results not by relevance but by publication date.

As a result, the newest posts rank higher than the older ones. It makes no sense, since a fresher publication date doesnât mean higher post relevance.

By manually changing the order of the search results, you can tackle this issue and deliver the most relevant matches to your visitors.

Secondly, reordering search results come in handy when you want to promote a specific page or product on your site.

You can put it at the top of your search results so that it gets more views. Thatâs a quick and free way to improve visibility of your content and increase your profits.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily change the search results order in WordPress.

## Change the Search Results Order on Your Site

The fastest and easiest way to change the order of search results with no coding is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)This is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that comes with tons of useful features to improve your website search.

Customizing the order of search results is just one of them. You can also use SearchWP to [track your visitorsâ searches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"), add search by categories, and many more.

Even though itâs a powerful tool, itâs also beginner-friendly. With SearchWP, you can get full control over how search works on your site regardless of your WordPress experience.

More than 50,000 site owners around the globe already use it to help their visitors find the right content faster.

Here are some other SearchWP features thatâll help you boost your search:

- **Automatic theme integration**: Like when everything works out of the box? SearchWP seamlessly integrates with existing search forms on your site, whatever theme you use.
- [**Searchable custom fields**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): Want your search to display more relevant content? Make WordPress consider the content of custom fields when performing searches with SearchWP.
- [**PDFs and documents indexing**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"): Uploading PDF files or office documents to your site? With SearchWP, you can include them in search results, making it easier for your visitors to find them.
- **[Multiple search engines support](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)")**: Looking for a way to create a custom search form? Using SearchWP, you can add a supplemental search engine, customize it, and link to a specific search form on your site.

With that, letâs take a look at the step-by-step process of customizing the search results order with SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP 

First, youâll need to [get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP todat") by visiting the website and creating a new account.

Next, you can sign in to your SearchWP account and go to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once there, simply click **Download SearchWP** and save the plugin files on your computer.

When youâre done, you should also copy the pluginâs license key on the same tab.

Next, youâll need to upload the SearchWP pluginâs ZIP file to your site. You can follow this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") if you need help.

Once the plugin is active, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now that SearchWP is up and running on your site, the next step is to install the Custom Results Order extension.

### Step 2: Install the Custom Results Order Extension

The SearchWP Custom Results Order extension allows you to affect the order of the search results and customize it to fit your needs.

To get started, youâll need to head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from your WordPress dashboard.

From here, you can navigate to the **Custom Results Order** extension and then click the âInstallâ button.

![Install custom results order extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-custom-results-order-extension-1030x522.png)The extension will then automatically install and activate on your site.

Now, youâre ready to start customizing the order of search results.

### Step 3: Change the Order of Search Results on Your Site

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and then switch to the âCustom Results Orderâ tab.

![Custom results order tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/custom-results-order-tab-1030x415.png)Here you can specify search queries for which you want to have a custom search result order.

To add a new search query, enter it in the **Enter search query** field and click **Add Search Query**.

For example, letâs say we want to adjust the order of search results for the âwordpressâ search query for the default engine on our site.

Hereâs how the search query settings would look:

![Enter search query for custom results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-search-query-for-custom-results-order-1030x464.png)After youâve added a search query, SearchWP will offer you to set your own order of search results for this query.

You can simply click the âPromote to Topâ button for the posts that youâd like to appear at the top of the search results.

![how to change the search results order in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-WordPress.png)Letâs say we want the âTOP-3 WordPress eCommerce pluginsâ post to appear on the top of search results.

To do that, weâll press the **Promote to Top** button next to it.

![promote a post to the top](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/promote-a-post-to-the-top.png)Thatâs it. The post you chose now appears at the top of search results.

If you want to undo the results order, then simply click the âRemove Promotionâ button.

![View promoted post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-promoted-post-1030x358.png)You can also change the position of several posts in the search results.

To do that, simply click the **Promote to Top** button next to each of them in the order you want them to appear in the search results.

For instance, letâs move the âTOP-3 WordPress eCommerce pluginsâ post to second place and put the âWhat is the Best CMSâ post first.

This is what the final result looks like on our test site:

![we customized the search results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-customized-the-search-results-order.png)Now, letâs also make sure that we can see these changes on the site itself.

### Step 4: Check Out Your Customized Search Results

To do that, simply visit your website and head to the search form.

From here, enter in the search box the search query for which you changed the search results order.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-5.png)As you can see, the order of search results we got is exactly the same as the one we customized with SearchWP.

This shows that the custom results order feature is working perfectly.

![we sucessfully changed the order of search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-sucessfully-changed-the-order-of-search-results.png)## FAQs about Changing Search Results Order

Here are some common question users have about altering the search results order in WordPress.

**1. How do you sort search results in WordPress?**

By default, WordPress sorts search results by publication date, displaying the most recent posts first. To sort search results differently, you can use a plugin like SearchWP. This plugin allows you to sort search results based on criteria such as relevance, custom post types, or specific keywords.

**2. Why should I change the search results order in WordPress?**

Changing the search results order can help:

- **Improve relevance:** WordPress, by default, sorts results by publication date, which may not reflect content relevance.
- **Highlight important content:** Promote specific posts, pages, or products to improve visibility and user experience.

**3. Can I reverse the changes if needed?**

Yes, you can undo changes by clicking the âRemove Promotionâ button next to the post you want to demote in the search results.

## Whatâs Nextâ¦

By promoting certain topics to the top, you make it easier for your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for.

We hope this article helped you learn how to change the search results order in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to search posts by tags in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)") and [how to exclude category from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)").

If youâre ready to start customizing the order of search results on your site, you can [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!").

**Tags:** change the order of search results in WordPress, change the search results order in WordPress, how to change the order of search results in WordPress, how to change the order of search results in your WordPress site, how to change the search results order in WordPress, SearchWP, woocommerce search form, WordPress search, wordpress search settings

### [How to Add a Search Box to Elementor Page (Beginner's Guide)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-elementor-page/)

**Published:** August 5, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Adding a search box to your Elementor page allows you to encourage visitors to explore your site.

This way, you can effortlessly boost your content discoverability and get more pageviews.

However, the default search functionality is pretty basic in WordPress and lacks advanced features.

As a result, it would often miss relevant pages and return results that didnât make sense. Visitors would search for topics, but theyâd get zero results or completely unrelated posts.

In this article, weâll show you how to add a search box to Elementor pages using 2 very simple methods.

- [How to Add a Default Search Box Without a Plugin](#1)
- [How to Add a Smart Search Box With the SearchWP Plugin](#2)

Letâs dive into it!

## Why You Should Consider Using a Search Plugin

You can easily add a search box to an Elementor page without any plugins. Weâll show you in detail how to do that later in this article.

But thereâs a reason why you should consider adding it using a search plugin.

The thing is, the default WordPress search engine is far from perfect. It searches only for titles, content, and excerpts of posts and pages.

Thatâs a huge disadvantage as there is a **lot of information that WordPress ignores** when performing a search.

This list includes custom fields, post tags and categories, shortcodes, and many other useful data.

By making these searchable, you can help your visitors find what theyâre looking for much faster and improve their user experience.

The easiest way to do that is to use a WordPress search plugin. Using it, you can also customize a bunch of things related to search on your site.

For example, with a search plugin, you can easily:

- Create custom search fields
- Change the order of search results
- Collect data on search activity on your site
- Limit search to specific posts, pages, or categories
- And more!

The best part? Using a plugin **takes only a few clicks to boost your site search** â no coding needed.

In addition, if you use a plugin, you can always contact its support team and get help from WordPress experts.

This way, you can **save money** on hiring a web developer if you face any issues adding a search box.

With that said, letâs move on to adding a default search box to your Elementor page without a plugin.

## How to Add a Default Search Box Without a Plugin

To get started, navigate to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard.

![navigate to All Pages](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-All-Pages.png)From here, you can select the page where youâd like to add the search box.

Next, you will need to click the âEdit with Elementâ button when youâre in the WordPress content editor.

![Edit with Elementor button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-with-elementor-button-1030x484.png)Once you are in the Elementor page editor, you will see multiple blocks in the panel on the left.

The best part is that you can simply drag and drop these elements onto your page.

![search for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-search-form.png)Simply type âsearch formâ in the **Search Widget** field on the left panel.

After that, youâll see the **Search Form** widget.

![find the search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/find-the-search-form.png)From here, you can drag and drop it to the spot on the page where you want the search box to appear.

You can see how we do that below:

![how to add a search box to Elementor page (beginner's guide)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-add-a-search-box-to-Elementor-page-beginners-guide.gif)After you add a search form, go ahead and click the **Publish** button. This will save all your changes.

Thatâs it. Youâve successfully added a search form to your Elementor page.

![click search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-search.png)Now, letâs see how to add a smart search field to your Elementor page using a plugin.

## How to Add a Smart Search Box With the SearchWP Plugin

If you want to add a smart custom search box to your Elementor page, then the easiest way to do that is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)This is the most powerful and intuitive WordPress search plugin on the market.

Using it, you can make search on your site better and help your visitors to find what theyâre looking for.

For example, with SearchWP, you can exclude specific pages from search results, [make custom fields searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), and more.

There are over 50,000 WordPress site owners who already use it to give their visitors the most relevant search results.

Beyond adding a smart search box to your pages, you can also use SearchWP to:

- [**Make shortcodes searchable**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"): Do you use shortcodes to display content on your site? With SearchWP, you can make shortcodes searchable so that your visitors can find that content.
- [**Add live autocomplete search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Want to display live search results on your site? Using SearchWP, you can turn on this feature in a couple of clicks.
- [**Track searches of your visitors**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): SearchWP collects data about search activity on your site so that you can see what your visitors are searching for.
- [**Enable search by tags**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)"): Add tags to your posts? With SearchWP, you can make them searchable so that your visitors can find the right content faster.

Now that you know how SearchWP can improve your on-site search, letâs see how you can add a smart custom search box to your Elementor page using it.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To begin, go and [grab your copy of the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today") by visiting the website.

Once youâve created a new account, the next step is to log into your SearchWP account. From here, go over to the **Downloads** tab in your dashboard.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the plugin and save the plugin files on your computer.

Once the download is complete, copy your SearchWP license key on the same page.

The next step is to upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site and install SearchWP.

If you need a refresher on how to do that, then you can follow this [step-by-step guide on how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After you activate SearchWP, youâll see the onboarding wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the instructions to complete the setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, you can move on to adding a new search engine and customizing it to meet your needs.

### Step 2: Add a New Search Engine and Customize It

A search engine contains a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing a search.

The best part is that you can create multiple search engines and link them to specific search forms.

SearchWP also allows you to create custom search boxes with individual sets of rules and settings.

Currently, you will see a single search engine named **Default**, which controls the native WordPress search forms. By customizing it, you can manage how search works across your site in general.

For example, you can make searchable a lot of useful data, exclude specific categories from search results, and more.

Since we want to make smarter every search box on our site, weâll edit the **Default** search engine.

To begin, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)You can choose which search sources you want WordPress to consider when performing searches on the pop-up window.

By adding and removing search sources from this list, you can control where WordPress looks for matches.

For example, letâs say we want to limit search on our test site to only posts. To do that, we uncheck all the search sources except **Posts**.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)When youâve finished managing your search sources, press **Done**.

Next, you can edit the attributes for each source that you want to consider while searching. Attributes are specific parts of the post, such as title, content, slug, and excerpt.

For example, letâs help your visitors find the posts they want faster.

To do that, simply head to the âPostsâ source section and click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, you can manage current attributes on the pop-up window and add new ones.

For instance, youâll see the default attributes like title, content, slug, excerpt, and author. Besides that, SearchWP also lets you add custom fields and taxonomies.

![Post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/post-attributes-2-1030x506.png)To let your visitors find the posts theyâre looking for faster, weâll add new attributes to this list.

The easiest way to make your post search smarter is to make SearchWP consider custom post fields.

To make data searchable in a specific custom field, simply click on the **Custom Fields** field and select which fields to consider.

For example, weâll make searchable the **\_tutorial** custom field that we use on our test site:

![Add custom field and meta key for posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-field-and-meta-key-for-posts-1030x489.png)Other than that, you can select the taxonomies such as categories and tags that you want SearchWP to consider when performing searches.

Taxonomy in WordPress is a method of organizing content. When it comes to posts, it might be a category, tag, format, or something else.

To do that, simply click on the **Taxonomies** field dropdown menu.

![Choose post taxonomies to consider](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-post-taxonomies-to-consider-1-1030x599.png)When youâre finished managing searchable post attributes, click **Done** to save the changes youâve made.

In addition, you can manage the [relevance weight](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "Relevance Weight") of applicable attributes by moving the slider next to them to the left or right. The further right the slider, the more importance or relevance that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

![Adjust relevance weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adjust-relevance-weights-1-1030x512.png)When youâre satisfied with the way youâve customized your search engine, youâll need to save the changes youâve made.

You can do that by pressing the **Save** button in the upper right corner.

![Save your engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-engine-1030x437.png)Now, the final step is to add your new smart search box to the Elementor page.

### Step 3: Add a Smart Search Box to an Elementor Page

All the changes youâve made to the **Default** search engine automatically apply to every search form on your site.

**Pro Tip:** SearchWP also lets you create custom search forms and link them to different engines. It offers pre-built templates and lots of customization options. To learn more, please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

Now, letâs add the search box to a page using Elementor.

To get started, navigate to **Pages Â» All Pages** in your WordPress dashboard.

![Navigate to All Pages](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-All-Pages-1030x507.png)From here, you can select the page where youâd like to add the search box.

Next, you will need to click the âEdit with Elementâ button when youâre in the WordPress content editor.

![Edit with Elementor button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-with-elementor-button-1030x484.png)After that, you will see the landing page editor in Elementor.

From here, you can add the **SearchWP Form block** from the panel on the left. Simply head to âSearchWPâ section and drag and drop the element anywhere on your page.

![Add SearchWP form block in elementor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-form-block-in-elementor-1-1030x679.png)After adding the SearchWP Form block, you also select which search form to show on the page.

To do that, simply click the dropdown menu under the Form Settings and choose your form.

![Select the search form to add](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-the-search-form-to-add-1-1030x647.png)After adding the search form, you can go ahead and **Publish** your Elementor page.

Next, you can visit your Elementor page and try out your new search engine.

![View Elementor search page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-elementor-search-page-1030x461.png)For example, on our test site, we have a post called How to Fix WooCommerce Search by SKU Not Working (Step by Step).

Letâs check if we can find this post using the search form.

![Test new Elementor search box](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-new-elementor-search-box-1030x483.png)As you can see, we successfully found the post since our site when performing searches.

It allows visitors to find the right content faster and makes their user experience more positive.

We hope this article helped you learned how to add a search box to your Elementor page. You may also want to see our guides on [how to fix custom post type search not working in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-custom-post-type-search-not-working-in-wordpress/ "How to Fix Custom Post Type Search Not Working in WordPress") and [how to search posts by tags in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)").

### [How to Add a Modal Search Form in WordPress (No Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-modal-search-form-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** February 12, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Ever feel like the search bar on your site is taking up a lot of space and your navigation looks cluttered? But what if it could be a little more engaging, a little more modern?

Over the years, weâve changed our website design multiple times and experimented with making the search form more attractive. And based on our experience, using a modal search form can transform the search experience on your site.

These nifty little pop-up search boxes can seriously enhance your websiteâs user experience, and theyâre not as complicated to set up as you might think.

In this article, we will show you how to add a modal search form in WordPress without editing code.

### What is a Modal Search Form & Why Use It? 

A modal search form is a popup window that overlays your current page. When a user clicks a search icon on your WordPress site, a window pops up directly over the page, containing only the search input field and submit button. Think of it like a spotlight, bringing focus to that specific content.

Itâs different from a traditional search bar that might be located in your header or sidebar, which is always visible. [Modal search forms](https://searchwp.com/features/modal-form/ "SearchWP Modal Search Form") are hidden by default and appear only when a user actively wants to search.

For instance, on [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"), we use a modal search form to provide a distraction free search experience to our users. Hereâs how it looks on our website:

![SearchWP modal form preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-modal-form-preview-2.gif)A modal search form keeps your page layout clean and uncluttered. No more bulky search bars taking up valuable space. Plus, the userâs attention is immediately drawn to the search function when it appears. Itâs hard to miss, which is great for improving engagement.

Besides, a modal search form offers design flexibility. Whether your site has a minimalist design or a complex layout, it can fit in seamlessly.

Now, letâs look at how to add a modal search form to your WordPress site.

### Adding a Modal Search Form in WordPress

The easiest way to display a modal search form is by using the [SearchWP Modal Search Form](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-modal-search-form/ "SearchWP Modal Search Form") plugin.

The best part is that the [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") is completely free to use and builds upon the style of your active WordPress theme. You donât have to touch a single line of code to edit its appearance or style.

To get started, you will need to install and activate the SearchWP Modal Search Form plugin. If you need help, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Modal Form** page from the WordPress dashboard. From here, you will see that the âEnable Modal Formâ option will be active.

![Enable modal form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-modal-form-1030x605.png)Next, you can scroll down and select which CSS file to load for your site. For instance, SearchWP Modal Search Form will let you choose between positioning and visual styling, positioning styling, or no styling.

We recommend using the default âPositioning and visual stylingâ setting. This way, you will get more control over where the search form will appear and how it will look.

![Edit modal form settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-modal-form-settings-1030x579.png)Besides that, there are also options to enable the full screen mode and disable scroll.

The **Full Screen Mode** will cover the entire screen when a user opens the form, providing a distraction free experience.

You can also enable the **Disable Scroll** option. This further improves the search experience by reducing the background motion when a user accidentally scrolls.

When youâre done, simply click the âSave Settingsâ button.

![Save your changes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-changes-1-1030x367.png)### Displaying Modal Search Form on Your Website

Next, youâll need to display the modal search form anywhere on your website. SearchWP lets you add the form to your siteâs menu, use a Gutenberg block, or shortcode.

Letâs look at each method for showing the modal search form in WordPress.

**1. Adding a Modal Search Form to WordPress Menu**

Depending on your WordPress theme, you can add the modal search form to any menu on your site.

Simply head to **Appearance Â» Menus** from the WordPress dashboard. SearchWP will now add a new group called âSearchWP Modal Search Formsâ under the menu items.

![Add SearchWP modal form to menu](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-modal-form-to-menu-1030x592.png)Go ahead and select the modal form and click the âAdd to menuâ button.

When youâre done, donât forget to click the âSave Menuâ button.

After that, you can visit your site to see it in action.

![View modal form in menu](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-modal-form-in-menu-1030x344.png)**2. Display Modal Search Form in Blog Posts & Pages**

You can also add the modal search form to any blog post or landing page using the Gutenberg block or shortcode. This is perfect if you want to show a search option within the content on your site.

First, youâll need to visit the page or post where you want to show the search form. Once you are in the content editor, simply click the â+â button and add the âModal Formâ block.

![Add modal form block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-modal-form-block-1030x520.png)After that, you can change the Modal Form block settings from the panel on the right.

For instance, there are options to change the text, choose a template, and select whether the form will be a link or a button.

![Change modal form block settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-modal-form-block-settings-1030x610.png)When youâre done, simply click the Save or Publish button.

From here, you can visit the page or post to see the modal form within your content.

![View modal form within content](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-modal-form-within-content-1030x554.png)If you prefer to use [shortcodes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"), then you can enter the following shortcode to display the modal form anywhere on your site.

```

[searchwp_modal_search_form]
```

You can also apply customizations that control various attributes of the modal itself. For example, the following shortcode will show a button with âOpen Searchâ text:

```

[searchwp_modal_search_form engine="my_searchwp_engine" template="My Custom Template" text="Open Search" type="button"]
```

You can edit each attribute in the shortcode to customize the modal search form. For instance, simply replace the type with a link if you donât want to show a button and edit the text.

**3. Show the Modal Search Form in the Widgets Area**

You can also add a modal search form to the widgets area of your WordPress theme, such as the sidebar or footer.

To start, simply head to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from the WordPress admin panel. After that, you can click the â+â button and add the Modal Form widget block.

![Add modal form widget block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-modal-form-widget-block-1030x498.png)Next, you will see different options to edit the modal form settings in the panel on the right.

There are options to change the text, choose a template, and change it from a link to button.

![Change modal form widget block settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-modal-form-widget-block-settings-1030x548.png)When youâre finished, go ahead and click the âUpdateâ button.

From here, you can visit your site and see the modal form in the widgets area. For instance, we added it to the sidebar of our demo site.

![View search modal in sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-modal-in-sidebar-1030x553.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Now that you know how to easily add a modal search form to your site, you can go a step further and improve the overall search experience for your visitors.

The default WordPress search has many limitations and doesnât include information like categories, tags, custom fields, product details, and more in the search process. However, you can change this by using SearchWP and [replace the default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

The plugin will help you create unlimited custom search engines for your website, set up custom search forms, and include taxonomies, custom fields, media files, and other important information when searching for content on your site.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)The best part, you can use SearchWP Modal Search Form plugin with SearchWP and use the whole potential of your website search.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a modal search form in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how you can create a Divi search results page template](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)") and [how to set up WordPress search settings for best results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-wordpress-search-settings-for-best-results/ "How to Set Up WordPress Search Settings for Best Results").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!") today!
