### [How to Improve Product Search in WooCommerce (7 Easy Ways)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-improve-product-search-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** July 31, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Looking for a way to improve product search in WooCommerce?

A robust search is one of the key success factors for any online store, as it ensures that customers can easily find what theyâre looking for.

In this guide, weâve picked the top ways to improve your product search in WooCommerce with no coding needed.

Letâs dive into it!

## Why Improve Product Search in WooCommerce

WooCommerce is one of the best and most powerful [eCommerce solutions](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads") for WordPress.

![the WooCommerce interface](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-WooCommerce-interface.png)But when it comes to product search, everything isnât so rosy.

The thing is, since WooCommerce is just a plugin, it uses the default WordPress search engine, which has many weaknesses and might not show relevant results.

For example, it doesnât consider product attributes, custom fields, taxonomies in the search process, doesnât give you a way to control your search results, doesnât collect any search data, and more.

Thatâs why if you want to succeed online and grow your business, improving WooCommerce search should be one of your main goals.

Especially for you, weâve selected the most effective ways to do that, regardless of your technical skills and WordPress experience.

Letâs take a detailed look at them.

## How to Improve Product Search in WooCommerce

The easiest way to make your WooCommerce product search smarter is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is a powerful and beginner-friendly WordPress search plugin that comes with lots of features.

Itâs also fully compatible with WooCommerce, allowing you to improve your search immediately after installation.

For example, you can [limit search to WooCommerce products only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-results-to-woocommerce-products-only/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), prioritize specific products in search results, [make product attributes searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), and more.

But this is just a short list of features for which more than 50,000 site owners appreciate this plugin.

You can also use SearchWP to:

- **[Get detailed reports on your visitorsâ search activity](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site")**: See what your customers are searching for on your site and get valuable insights into their search behavior.
- [**Display search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Give your visitors immediate search results to help them find the right products faster and improve their user experience.
- **[Customize the order of search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)")**: Change the order of search results on your site in the way you want to meet your business needs.
- [**Hide specific products from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)"): Exclude redundant products or pages from your search results so that nothing will distract your customers from shopping.

With that, letâs figure out how you can use SearchWP to make your WooCommerce search better.

To get started, youâll need to [get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

Once done, you can go to your SearchWP account dashboard and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to press the **Download SearchWP** button to save the plugin ZIP archive to your computer.

After downloading, we recommend copying the plugin license key in the bottom left corner of the same page.

Now, youâll need to upload the downloaded file to your site and install the plugin. If you need help, then check out this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") if you need help with that.

Upon activation, you will see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Next, you will need to install the SearchWPâs WooCommerce extension. This extension ensures seamless integration and allows you to customize your WooCommerce search easily.

To install it, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** in your WordPress dashboard. From here, go to âWooCommerce Integrationâ and click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)Once the extension is active, youâre ready to start customizing your WooCommerce search.

A search engine is a collection of settings and rules WordPress considers when performing searches across your site.

You can edit them to make your WooCommerce search smarter and help your customers find the right products faster.

For instance, you can limit your search to WooCommerce products only, enable search by product attributes, [exclude specific products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce"), and more.

The best part is that SearchWP allows you to add multiple search engines and customize them the way you want.

Letâs take a look at 5 of the most effective examples of how you can do that to boost your sales.

### 1. Restrict Your Search to WooCommerce Products

First, letâs see how you can limit your search to WooCommerce products to clean up your search results.

To get started, youâll need to navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see a new window open with sources for the default engine that includes posts, pages, and media.

To limit search to only WooCommerce products, go ahead and uncheck the boxes next to all search sources except **Products**.

![Only select product as source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/only-select-product-as-source-1030x500.png)When youâre finished, press **Done** to save the changes.

Now, your site visitors can only search for WooCommerce products.

### 2. Enable Search by Product Attributes

Letâs also see how you can make product attributes that WooCommerce stores as custom fields searchable.

To get started, press the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the **Products** source section.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)Next, a new window will open where you can select which product attributes to include in the search process.

By default, SearchWP will consider title, content, slug, excerpt, and author. However, you can go a step further and include custom fields as well.

For instance, letâs click on the **Custom Fields** field and choose from the dropdown options.

![Select SKU custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-7-1030x375.jpg)You can also enter the names of specific custom fields if you want, like size, color, model, or SKU.

When youâre finished managing product attributes, press **Done** to save your changes.

Now, your customers can find product by their attributes, like color, size, price, or whatever you specified.

### 3. Enable Search by Product Category

With SearchWP, you can also give your customers the ability to [search by product category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

Itâs handy when theyâre not looking for a specific product but a category.

You can include product categories by editing the product attribute settings in SearchWP. Simply click the dropdown menu under **Taxonomies** and add âProduct categoriesâ from the list.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)For more details, check out this tutorial on [how to make WooCommerce product categories searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

### 4. Enable Search by Product Tags

You can also enable search by product tags and include them as product attributes in SearchWP.

It opens up new ways to search for products and allows your customers to find what theyâre looking for faster.

![Select product tags](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-tags--1030x625.png)For more information, check out the [how to enable search by WooCommerce product tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search by Product Tags in WooCommerce (Fastest Way)") tutorial.

### 5. Make Product SKUs Searchable

Another way to help your visitors find the right products is to enable search by product SKUs.

SKU is a unique product identifier that you can specify by yourself. Making it searchable allows your customers to find a product immediately if they know its SKU.

![Include sku custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-sku-custom-field-1030x513.png)To learn more, please see this step-by-step guide on [how to make WooCommerce product SKUs searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)").

### 6. Exclude Products from WooCommerce Search

There might be situations where hiding products from search results on your site would be useful.

For example, if you have special deals for a certain group of visitors, such as your newsletter subscribers. If anyone can find these deals using a search, then it doesnât make much sense.

Or you have products that are out of stock or no longer offered (discontinued), then hiding them would provide users with a better user experience.

With SearchWP, itâs very easy to hide certain products. It offers an Exclude UI extension that lets you simply click a button and hide the item inside the WooCommerce product editor.

![Enable exclude from SearchWP option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-exclude-from-searchwp-option-1030x549.png)Besides that, you can also set up conditional rules to exclude products by categories or tags.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how you can exclude any product from search results in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce").

### 7. Prioritize Specific Products in Search Results

Would you like to customize the search results order and show certain products higher up in the results?

With SearchWP, itâs very easy to control the order of product search results in WooCommerce.

You can quickly select search terms and prioritize specific products or content by clicking the âPromote to Topâ button.

![Configure Custom Results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)You can learn more by following our guide on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress").

## Whatâs Nextâ¦

The default WooCommerce search isnât super powerful or customizable. However, with SearchWP, you can transform it and make it even smarter.

By following the tips in this guide, you can supercharge your WooCommerce search, help users find the right products, and boost sales.

We hope this article helped you learn how you can improve your WooCommerce product search in a few simple ways. You may also want to see our guides on [how to add Ajax search to WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-ajax-search-to-woocommerce/ "How to Add Ajax Search to WooCommerce (Step by Step)") and [how to make your WordPress site consider product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)").

Ready to empower your WooCommerce search? You can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/ "Get started with SearchWP today!").

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After SearchWP is up and running, you can use it to customize the [default search engine](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)") or create new ones.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)After that, you can select which sources to include in the search process.

SearchWP will use posts, pages, and [media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files") by default. However, if you have an eCommerce store, then you can include products or downloads as well.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)When youâre finished, click the **Done** button.

Next, you can also edit the attributes for each source that SearchWP should consider while searching.

However, you can also include any additional information thatâs stored in custom fields or consider categories and tags in the search process.

Next, you can scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button.

This will open a small window where you can choose different methods to display your search form.

![Choose how to embed results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-how-to-embed-results-page-1030x490.png)Simply select the âShortcodeâ as the Embed Results Template option.

From here, you can click the Copy button next to the shortcode.

![Copy the shortcode for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-the-shortcode-for-search-form-1030x525.png)Now, the next step is to add a search box to one of your posts using the shortcode.

### Step 3: Add a Search Box to Your Post With a Shortcode

To get started, navigate to the **Posts** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![go to Posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Posts.png)On the next screen, youâll see all the posts that you have on your website.

Simply click on the post where you want to add a search box.

After that, youâll need to click the **Plus (+)** button next to it and then add a âShortcodeâ block.

You can simply click the **Plus (+)** sign and add the âSearchWP Formâ block.

![Add SearchWP forms block to add a search box](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-searchwp-forms-block-1030x514.png)Next, you will see the SearchWP Form block added to your post.

From here, simply click the dropdown menu and select your search form.

![Choose the search form from the block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-search-form-from-the-block-1030x494.png)After you choose the right search form, you can publish or update your blog post.

Thatâs it. Youâve added a search bar to your post, so your visitors can discover more of your content.

### Step 4: Check Out Your Post With a Search Box

To go to your post, click **Preview**, and then select **Preview in new tab** option from the drop-down menu.

![Preview your post with search box](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/preview-your-post-with-search-box-1030x484.png)Hereâs how the search bar looks within a post on our test site.

To test it, letâs try to search for âWooCommerceâ.

![Test your search box](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-your-searchbox-1030x546.png)As you can see, search results for the âWooCommerceâ search term appear right inside our post.

Itâs helpful because it allows your visitors to search your website without getting distracted from reading your posts.

**Pro Tip:** You can use SearchWP Live Search extension to show search results in the dropdown as users type in the search term. To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to add live autocomplete search to WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites").

![Preview of search box](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/preview-of-search-box-1030x580.png)### FAQs About Adding a Search Box in WordPress Posts

Here are some common questions about showing a search in WordPress posts using shortcodes.

**1. Can I use SearchWP shortcodes without coding knowledge?**

Yes, SearchWP shortcodes work without any coding experience. You simply copy and paste the code snippet in a Custom HTML block into your post content where you want the search box to appear. The plugin handles all the technical aspects automatically.

**2. Will adding search boxes to my posts slow down my website?**

SearchWP is designed to be lightweight and wonât significantly impact your siteâs speed. The plugin uses efficient indexing methods and only loads search functionality when needed.

**3. Does SearchWP work with all WordPress themes?**

Yes, SearchWP is compatible with virtually all WordPress themes. The shortcode generates standard HTML that works across different theme designs. If you encounter any styling issues, they can usually be resolved with minor CSS adjustments.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a search box to a WordPress post with a shortcode. You may also want to see our guide on [how to exclude category from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)") and [how to limit search to only posts in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-only-posts-in-wordpress/ "How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)").

### [Introducing SearchWP Site Builder Compatibility and Promoted Search AdsÂ ](https://searchwp.com/introducing-searchwp-site-builder-compatibility-and-promoted-search-ads/)

**Published:** July 24, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

For the past few weeks, weâve been busy working on some exciting new features for SearchWP.

Today, weâre thrilled to introduce new site builder compatibility and promoted search ads features.

You can now integrate SearchWP with some of the most popular site builders and display search forms and results page using blocks.

Thatâs not all! The latest version also lets you display ads and promotions on the search results page without editing code.

Letâs look at each of these features in detail.

## Powerful Site Builder Compatibility

For years, users who worked with page builders had to hunt down shortcodes and paste them into their builderâs interface to display search forms.

But what if there is a better way to do this?

Now, SearchWP offers native blocks for the [most popular site builders](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-page-builders-for-newbies/ "7 Best WordPress Page Builders for Newbies (Compared)"), not just Gutenberg.

You can simply add the SearchWP Forms and SearchWP Results blocks inside the site builder and display your search forms and custom results templates.

Go ahead and click the â+â button or simply drag and drop the SearchWP Results block to the page.

However, by default the search results will appear here based on your search form settings pointing to this page.

We recommend using the default settings and keep it to âSet by Search Formâ, as this will lead to the best outcome.

![Choose your search results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-search-results-template-1030x532.png)## Display Promoted Search Ads on the Results Page

### [How to Make a Smart BetterDocs Search in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-betterdocs-search-in-wordpress/)

This is where SearchWP can help you out. It enhances BetterDocs search and offers more advanced features.

By making a smart BetterDocs search, you can create a better search experience that keeps people on your site instead of sending frustrated emails to your inbox.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily include BetterDocs content in the search process on your site.

SearchWP also lets you create a unified search experience where visitors can find answers from your documentation, [blog posts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-wordpress-search-form-for-custom-post-types/ "How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types"), and pages all in one place. Besides making BetterDocs search more powerful, SearchWP also lets you:

- **[Display Live Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free")**: Add instant search suggestions that appear as visitors type, making it even easier to find documentation quickly.
- **[Track Documentation Searches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site")**: See exactly what help topics people are searching for, so you can identify content gaps in your knowledge base.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP Plugin

If you need help with this, then check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

### Step 2: Install BetterDocs Integration Extension

You will first need to install the [BetterDocs extension](https://searchwp.com/features/betterdocs-integration/ "BetterDocs extension") in SearchWP. To start, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard.

### Step 3: Edit Default Engine to Include BetterDocs Documents

Next, you can edit search engines in SearchWP. Think of a search engine as a set of rules that tells the plugin what to search and how to show results.

The best part is that you can [create multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)") or customize the default engine on your site using SearchWP.

Here, you can see that there is only the **Default** search engine, which you can use to manage how search works on your entire site.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)A new window will now open where you can select the Sources for your new engine. Youâll see that it will already include Posts, Pages, and Media.

From here, make sure the **Docs** source is checked as a source.

![Include docs at source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-docs-at-source-1030x438.png)Next, you can click the **Done** button.

After that, simply scroll down to the Docs section and click **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Edit attributes for docs](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-attributes-for-docs-1030x421.png)Here, you can select different attributes to include in the search process. By default, SearchWP will include title, content, slug, excerpt, and author.

However, you can also include custom fields or any [categories and tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-wordpress-posts-by-tags-only/ "How to Search WordPress Posts by Tags Only (Newbie-Friendly)"). Simply click the Custom Fields or Taxonomies dropdown menu to add them.

Next, you can click the **Save** button at the top to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)### Step 4: Set Up Synonym Rules

Once youâve configured the search engine in SearchWP, you can also [set up a synonym rule](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-synonym-rules-for-wordpress-search/ "How to Set Up Synonym Rules for WordPress Search").

This is where SearchWP comes in handy. It offers a powerful synonym rule feature that lets you add search terms and their synonyms.

Next, go ahead and enter the âSearch Termsâ and their respective Synonyms.

![Add new synonyms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-synonyms-1030x421.png)Do note that SearchWP will only work with single words, such as âpasswordâ or âsupportâ, and not with sentences or phrases. So when setting up a rule, just make sure to use specific search terms.

Once youâve entered the search and synonym, simply click the **Save Synonyms** button.

### Step 5: Test BetterDocs Search on Your Site

Now, you can simply visit your site and head to the documentation area created using BetterDocs.

![Visit documentation area in wordpress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/visit-documentation-area-in-wordpress-1030x574.png)From here, you can click the search bar and enter a keyword or phrase to search for a document.

For instance, we have a category called âPasswordâ on our demo site, and we added an article on âHow to Reset Your Passwordâ to this category.

![BetterDocs live search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/betterdocs-live-search-1030x333.png)When we searched for the category in BetterDocs search, the correct article appeared.

This shows that SearchWP integration is working smoothly with BetterDocs.

## FAQs about Smart BetterDocs Search

Here are some common questions users have about creating a smart BetterDocs search in WordPress:

**1. What are the limitations of the default BetterDocs search feature?**

The default BetterDocs search is limited because it does not include advanced features such as keyword stemming, setting up synonym rules, or incorporating custom fields and taxonomies.

**2. Why is a smart BetterDocs search important for my site?**

A smart BetterDocs search improves the search experience on your site by helping visitors find relevant documentation more easily. This reduces frustration, decreases the likelihood of visitors contacting support unnecessarily, and keeps them engaged on your site.

**3. How does SearchWP help manage search results?**

The root of this issue lies in how WordPress handles search queries. The default search completely ignores tags and categories.

You canât search for posts by tags because WordPress doesnât consider them when performing a search.

By default, it searches only by title, content, and excerpts of posts and pages.

Thatâs inconvenient and limits the number of ways your visitors can search for content.

Visitors who havenât found what they came for on your site are likely to leave it straight away.

After youâve learned why itâs important to make tags searchable on your WordPress site, letâs see how to do that.

### Step 2: Make WordPress Search by Tags

With SearchWP, you can add new search engines and manage the existing ones.

By customizing an engine, you can control the way SearchWP performs searches across your entire site. For example, you can make it index post tags, limit search to a specific type of content, and many more.

![we found our post by a tag](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-post-by-a-tag.png)### FAQs about Tags Not Searchable in WordPress

Here are some common queries users have about how to fix WordPress search to include tags.

**1. Why arenât WordPress tags searchable by default?**

WordPressâs default search function only searches through post titles and post content. It doesnât include tags, categories, or custom fields in search queries. Thatâs when you search by tags, your content wonât appear in the search results.

**2. Can I make categories searchable too?**

Yes, SearchWP allows you to make categories, custom taxonomies, and even custom fields searchable. You can configure which content types to include when setting up your search engine and adding categories for any source in the plugin settings.

**3. Do I need to re-index my content after installing SearchWP?**

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") automatically builds an index of your content when you first activate it. If you have a large website, this initial indexing process might take some time, but it runs in the background without affecting your siteâs performance.

**4. Can visitors search for specific tags directly?**

With SearchWP configured properly, visitors can search for tag names and find all posts associated with those tags. The search results will show relevant content based on the tags youâve made searchable.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

By making tags searchable, you make it easier for your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix WordPress tags not searchable. You may also want to see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") and [how to add a WordPress search bar with a shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-wordpress-search-bar-with-shortcode/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### [How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (Simple Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/)

**Published:** July 15, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you run a multi-author WordPress site, then youâll quickly discover a frustrating problem.

Visitors would search for their favorite writers by name, only to find empty results or irrelevant content. The built-in WordPress search completely ignores authors.

But adding authors to WordPress search results can help your visitors find more content from their favorite authors and allow you to get more pageviews.

In this step-by-step guide, weâll show you how to include authors in search results on your site, no coding needed.

### Why Add Authors in WordPress Search Results

By default, WordPress ignores any data about post authors when performing a search. It makes finding posts from specific authors a real hassle for your site visitors.

The only option they have in this case is to check the authorsâ archive. Thatâs not convenient, not to mention that not every site has such an archive.

By making authorsâ data searchable, you can resolve this issue and help your visitors find the right content faster.

It also boosts your content discoverability and allows your authors to increase their online presence.

Now that you know why adding authors to search results can be beneficial, letâs see how you can do that.

It not only allows you to make WordPress index authorsâ data, but also custom fields, tags, categories, and other helpful information.

Beyond that, you can use this plugin to completely customize how search works on your site.

For example, you can [customize your search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page (5 Ways)"), exclude a specific post category from the search, and more.

But thatâs not all the features this plugin comes with. You can also use SearchWP to:

- [**Create custom search fields**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): Do you want to have a search field with a unique set of settings? With SearchWP, you can create a search form, customize it to meet your needs, and place it anywhere on your site.
- [**Change the order of search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)"): SearchWP allows you to change your search results order with just a few clicks, no coding needed.
- [**Track the most popular searches**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site"): Do you want to know what users are looking for most often on your site? SearchWP gathers data on all search activity on your site, allowing you to understand your audienceâs needs better.
- **[Limit search to specific post types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)")**: Using SearchWP, you can limit search on your site to specific post types, categories, or anything else.

There are over 50,000 website owners who already use SearchWP to deliver the most relevant search results to their visitors.

Are you ready to become one of them and add authors in search results on your WordPress site?

If so, follow the step-by-step guide below.

### Step 1: Install and Activate the SearchWP Plugin

First, youâll need to visit the website and [create a new SearchWP account](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today").

Once thatâs done, the next step is to install the plugin on your site.

To get started, you can log in to your SearchWP account and click on the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)To download the plugin, go ahead and click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the ZIP file to your computer.

You should also copy your license key on the same page. Youâll need it on one of the following steps to activate your plugin copy.

The next step is to upload the downloaded ZIP file to your site and install SearchWP. You can follow this [guide on installing a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") if you need help.

After the plugin installation is complete, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, youâre ready to move on to the next step and customize the search engine to include authors.

### Step 2: Customize Search Engine to Include Authors

To get started, youâll need to navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can customize the search engine for your website or create new ones.

SearchWP allows you to add multiple search engines and link them to separate search forms. This way, you can place fully customized search forms with a unique set of settings anywhere on your site.

But since we want to add authors to search across the whole site, we will customize the **Default** search engine.

First, you will need to press the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup window will open with different sources used by the search engine.

By default, it will include posts, pages, and media, but you can add more sources. For example, if youâre running a WooCommerce store, then you can also include products as a source.

![Choose sources for your engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-selection-1030x475.jpg)When youâre finished, simply click the **Done** button.

Next, will notice that SearchWP will include âAuthorâ as an attribute for all the sources, along with title, content, slug, and excerpt.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)However, if you want to customize the source attributes, then simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under any source.

This will open a new window where you can see all these attributes. Besides that, you can also add custom fields and taxonomies in the search process.

![Post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/post-attributes-2-1030x506.png)Go ahead and click âDoneâ when youâre finished.

SearchWP also lets you change the [relevance weight](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "Relevance Weight") of attributes by moving the slider next to them left or right.

The further to the right the slider is set for an attribute, the more influence that attribute has on the position in the search results.

![Adjust author slider](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adjust-author-slider-1030x486.png)Next, you can scroll to the top of the page.

From here, simply click the âSaveâ button to store all your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Now, your visitors can search for posts written by specific authors.

Letâs make sure and try out your new search.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search by Authors

To do that, simply visit your website and head to the search bar or search form.

Then enter the authorâs name whose posts you want to find into the search bar on your site.

For example, we have an author named **Henry Turner** on our test site. Letâs try to find posts written by him.

![how to add authors in WordPress search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-add-authors-in-WordPress-search-results.png)As you can see, we successfully found the posts written by Henry Turner.

It means that now search by author works on our site.

![we found posts written by the first author](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-posts-written-by-the-first-author.png)### FAQs About Adding Authors to WordPress Search

Here are some queries users ask when it comes to including authors in WordPress Search.

**1. Can I add authors to search results without using a plugin?**

While itâs technically possible to modify WordPress search through custom code, we donât recommend this approach for most users. SearchWP provides a much safer and more reliable solution that wonât break when you update your theme or WordPress core.

**2. Will adding authors to search slow down my website?**

SearchWP is optimized for performance and shouldnât noticeably impact your site speed. The plugin creates its own search index that runs efficiently alongside WordPress. However, if you have thousands of authors, you may want to monitor your siteâs performance after installation.

**3. Can I create a separate search form just for authors?**

Absolutely. SearchWP lets you create multiple search engines with different configurations. You can set up one search engine specifically for finding authors and another for regular content, then use different search forms on your site.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Allowing users to search by authors helps them find posts from specific writers faster and increase the number of pageviews you get.

We hope this article helped you learned how to add authors in WordPress search results. You may also want to see our guides on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/) and [how to add fuzzy search to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/).

Are you ready to include authors in search on your site and start delivering more relevant search results to your visitors? You can [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

### [How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/)

If you add tags to your blog posts or products, then they can boost the search experience on your website.

By including tags in the search process, you can allow your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for much faster.

As a result, youâll notice more engagement as people will spend more time on your site.

In this article, weâll show you how to make posts on your site searchable by tags without writing a single line of code.

### Why Make WordPress Consider Post Tags When Performing a Search?

By default, WordPress searches only by post title, description, content, and excerpts.

But there are many other attributes you can add to your posts to make it easier to find them using search. Post tags are one of these attributes.

For example, you can mark specific posts with the **Tutorial** tag to let your visitors find these posts by the âtutorialâ search term. Itâll allow them to find the right content much faster.

![add the Tutorial tag](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-the-Tutorial-tag.png)And this is not the only benefit of making post tags searchable on your site.

- **Increase the average session duration**: Users who find the right content stay on your site longer, which is one of the strongest signals for Google to rank your site higher.
- **Boost your content discoverability**: A positive search experience encourages your visitors to explore more of your content.

Now that youâve learned why itâs useful to enable search by post tags on your WordPress site, letâs see how you can do that.

### Step 2: Make Changes to Your Search Engine

You can think of a search engine as a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing a search.

SearchWP allows you to add multiple search engines and link them to individual search forms.

First, there will be only an automatically created search engine called **Default**.

Itâs linked to every existing search form on your site. By customizing its rules and settings, you can change how SearchWP performs a search across your entire site.

For example, you can add or remove search sources, change attribute relevance weights, and more.

To start, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard. The default engine will already have Post selected as a source.

However, you can confirm this by clicking the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup window will open where you can set the sources for the Default engine.

Just ensure that the âPostsâ option is selected as a source. Once finished, you can click the **Done** button.

A new popup window will now open where you can include custom fields and taxonomies to the search process.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search by Tags

To get started, simply visit your site and head to the search form or search bar.

For the purposes of this tutorial, weâve added a âHow to Install a WordPress Pluginâ post that has a **Tutorial** tag.

Letâs see if we can find it only by the tag.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-3.png)Even though there is no word âtutorialâ in the title, description, or content of the post, it still found the correct post.

![we found a post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-a-post.png)To make sure it wasnât a coincidence, letâs do another experiment.

![we found our posts](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-posts.png)### FAQs about Searching Posts by Tags

**1. What are tags, and how do they improve my websiteâs search experience?**

Tags are keywords or phrases that categorize and describe your content. Adding tags to your blog posts or products helps visitors find content more easily, enhancing the user experience by making searches quicker and more relevant.

### [How to Limit WordPress Search to Post Titles Only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-wordpress-search-to-post-titles-only/)

**Published:** July 1, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

In this article, weâll show you how to limit WordPress search to post titles only without writing a single line of code.

### Why Limit WordPress Search to Post Titles Only

![limit search to titles only](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/limit-search-to-titles-only.png)Here are the ones that stand out the most:

- **Lower bounce rate**. The easier it is for visitors to find what theyâre looking for on your site, the lower your bounce rate will be.
- **Better ranking in search engines**. Since bounce rate is one of the main ranking factors in Google, keeping it low allows your site to rank higher in search results.
- **Build a loyal audience**. Satisfied visitors who could easily find the desired content on your site are more likely to return again.
- **Content discoverability boost**. When visitors see that search on your site is convenient, it encourages them to explore your site.

### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engine

To get started, youâll need to navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard

Here youâll find your search engines created with SearchWP. Right now, there is only one search engine called **Default**. This search engine is connected to every single search form that exists on your site.

It means that by editing its settings, you can customize how search works across your entire site.

For example, you can add or remove search sources, [exclude specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or categories from search, and much more.

Go ahead and uncheck the Pages and Media sources so that only the **Posts** search source remains active.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)When youâre finished, simply click **Done**.

The next step is to customize the list of post attributes that WordPress takes into account when performing a search.

Youâll notice that it considers 5 post attributes: title, content, slug, excerpt, and author.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)To make WordPress only consider post titles, you need to remove all attributes except title. Simply click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button to get started.

Then, go ahead and uncheck all other attributes, other than âTitle.â

![Manage post attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/manage-post-attributes-4-1030x501.png)When youâre finished, press **Done**.

The final step is to press the **Save** button at the top right to apply the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)After youâve limited search on your site to post titles only, letâs check out how it works.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search

To do that, simply visit your website and head to the search bar or search form.

For the purposes of this tutorial, we added a post with the **Greek salad** title and **Onion soup** description to our test site.

If we can find this post by title but canât find it by description, it means that search on our site is now limited to post titles only. First, letâs search for âonion soupâ.

![search for description](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-description.png)As you can see, we didnât find any matches for the âonion soupâ search term.

It means that our WordPress site no longer considers a post description when performing a search.

![we haven't found a product by description](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-havent-found-a-prodcut-by-description.png)Now, letâs search for âgreek saladâ to make sure that post exists.

This will ensure that the search is able to find the content by the title.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-2.png)As you can see, we found the post.

It means that our WordPress site now only considers post titles when performing searches.

![we found a product by title](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-a-product-by-title.png)## FAQs About Limiting WordPress Search to Post Titles

Here are some common questions that users have about restricting search on their site to post titles.

**1. Why would I want to limit WordPress search to post titles only?**

Limiting WordPress search to post titles can enhance search relevance, especially if you have a large amount of content. This ensures that search results are more focused and directly relevant to the search query, as they match the titles specifically.

**2. Is there a plugin that can help me limit searches to post titles in WordPress?**

Yes, you can use plugins like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") to customize and limit searches to specific areas, such as post titles.

**3. Can I implement this search limitation without a plugin?**

Yes, itâs possible to achieve this by modifying your themeâs functions.php file or creating a custom search query. However, using a plugin can be more user-friendly and easier to maintain.

**4. How does limiting search to titles impact SEO?**

This change only affects how searches function internally on your site and does not directly impact your siteâs SEO. However, it may improve user experience by providing more relevant search results, potentially leading to better user engagement.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Limiting WordPress search to only titles helps you to give your visitors more relevant search results and reduce your siteâs bounce rate. It also boosts engagement and makes the search experience better.

We hope this article helped you learn how to limit WordPress search to post titles only. You may also want to see our guide on [how to make WordPress search in post content](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-in-post-content/ "How to Make WordPress Search in Post Content") and [how to customize your WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page (5 Ways)").

If youâre ready to limit search on your WordPress site to post titles only, you can[ grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today!").

### [How to Fix WooCommerce Search by SKU Not Working (Step by Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-search-by-sku-not-working/)

**Published:** June 26, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâre running a WooCommerce store, then allowing users to search by SKUs is a great way to help customers find the right products.

However, it might not work properly at times due to different reasons.

Solving this problem is important to ensure a positive user experience for your customers and increase your sales.

In this article, weâll show you how to find out why search by product SKUs doesnât work on your site and how to fix this issue.

Since this tutorial contains a lot of helpful tips, weâve organized it into several sections for you. Feel free to click on a specific section to skip ahead:

- [Fix WooCommerce Search by SKUs Doesnât Work on Site](#1)
- [Fix WooCommerce Search by SKUs Doesnât Work in The Admin Panel](#2)

### Why WooCommerce Search by SKU Does Not Work?

Letâs look at 2 of the most common cases why search by SKU of WooCommerce products might not work on your site.

#### WordPress Doesnât Search by WooCommerce Products SKUs on Site

To begin, SKU stands for stock keeping unit. This is a unique product ID youâre free to specify the way you want. By assigning it to a product, you can help your customers to find it faster.

The main reason you canât find a product by SKU is that [WooCommerce stores](https://searchwp.com/best-woocommerce-search-plugins/ "7 Best WooCommerce Search Plugins (Free + Paid)") this data in custom fields.

Custom fields are fields where you can add any extra information to your posts, pages, or products.

Apart from SKUs, WooCommerce also stores in custom fields product price, weight, color, and many other attributes.

This is valuable data, adding which can make it much easier for your customers to find the products theyâre looking for.

But, there is a problem. WordPress doesnât consider custom fields when performing a search by default.

Thankfully, you can handle this issue with a couple of clicks.

#### Search by WooCommerce Products SKUs Doesnât Work in the Admin Panel

WooCommerce supports searching for products by their SKUs in the admin panel by default.

So if you canât find your products, it means that your plugin doesnât work correctly.

A list of possible reasons why this might happen includes:

- **Plugin conflict**: Some of the plugins you use on your site may not be compatible with each other.
- **Import a large number of products via CSV**: If you uploaded tons of products at once through a CSV file import, it could have corrupted the WooCommerce database.
- **Hosting memory limit**: Some web hosting services have hidden memory limitations that may cause WooCommerce not to work properly.

With that, letâs see how you can quickly fix these issues.

### Fix WooCommerce Search by SKUs Doesnât Work on Site

The easiest way to enable search by product SKUs on your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the most powerful and easy-to-use WordPress search plugin on the market.

Using this plugin, you can make any additional info about your products searchable â no technical knowledge needed.

But this is only a small part of SearchWP features. In fact, with this plugin, you can [create custom search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), prioritize specific products in search results, and much more.

Over 50,000 eCommerce websites are already using SearchWP to give their customers more relevant search results.

Here are a few more amazing things that you can do with this plugin:

- [**Display search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Want to show search results to visitors in live mode? With SearchWP, you can add live search with autocomplete feature to your site in a few clicks.
- [**Track searches on your site**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Wondering what products your customers were looking for on your site? SearchWP collects data on search activity in your store so that you can better understand your audienceâs needs.
- [**Add a search bar with a shortcode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-wordpress-search-bar-with-shortcode/ "How to Add a WordPress Search Bar with Shortcode (Step-by-Step)"): Looking for a way to add a search bar to your site? With SearchWP, you can display a search bar using a shortcode at almost any place on your site.
- [**Enable search by partial matches**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"): Want your visitors to find the right product, even if they enter its name with a typo? Enable SearchWP fuzzy search feature to make it easier to find products in your store.

With that, letâs see how you can use SearchWP to enable searching by WooCommerce product SKUs on your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, you can [get your own copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") by visiting the site and signing up for a new account.

The next step is to sign in to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once youâre there, simply press the **Download SearchWP** button to begin downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After you finish downloading, you should also copy the plugin license key at the bottom left on the same tab.

The next step is to upload the SearchWP pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

Not sure how to do that? You can follow this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once SearchWP is installed and activated, you will see the setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Install the WooCommerce Integration Extension

Next, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard and navigate to the [WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/features/woocommerce-integration/ "WooCommerce Integration Extension") extension.

This extension ensures seamless integration of SearchWP and WooCommerce plugins.

From here, simply press the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)After that, you can move on to the next step and start customizing your siteâs search engine.

### Step 3: Make Custom Fields Searchable on Your Site

To get started, youâll need to visit the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard.

By default, there is only one search engine called **Default**. Any changes you make to it apply to all existing search boxes on your site.

With SearchWP, youâre free to fully customize a search engine to meet your needs. For example, you can specify what search sources WordPress should consider, which pages exclude from search, and more.

SearchWP also allows you to create multiple search engines and link them to individual search forms.

In this tutorial, weâll show you how to make WooCommerce product SKUs searchable using any search box on your site.

The first step is to add WooCommerce products to the Default search engineâs list of search sources. To get started, press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a small window will open with sources for the default engine. These include posts, pages, and media.

From here, you can tick the box next to the **Products** source and click âDoneâ to save the changes.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)Once youâve added WooCommerce products as a search source, the next step is to make WordPress consider SKUs.

To get started, click on the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the **Products** section.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)A small window will now open where you can select custom fields and taxonomies to include in the search process.

Go ahead and select **\_sku** from the dropdown menu under Custom Fields.

![Include sku custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-sku-custom-field-1030x513.png)Once youâve finished, press **Done** to save the changes.

The last step is to click the **Save Engines** button in the upper right corner to save all the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Now letâs try out how search by SKU works on your site.

### Step 4: Test Your New Search

To do that, simply visit your WooCommerce store and head to the search bar or form.

For the purposes of this tutorial, weâve added to our test site a product called **Mountain bike**, which has âMB7534â as the SKU.

Letâs see if we can find this product by SKU alone.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-1.png)As you can see, we found the product.

It means that search by WooCommerce product SKUs now works on our test site.

![we found a WooCommerce product by its SKU](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-a-WooCommerce-product-by-its-SKU.png)### Fix WooCommerce Search by SKUs Doesnât Work in The Admin Panel

Now letâs see what you can do if search by WooCommerce product SKUs doesnât work in the admin panel.

#### Fix Plugin Compatibility Issue

The most common reason for that is plugin incompatibility.

To find out if itâs your case, you first need to disable all the plugins installed on your site.

To get started, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![navigate to the Plugins tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-Plugins-tab.png)Then, tick a checkbox next to every installed plugin except for WooCommerce, SearchWP, and its extensions.

When youâre finished, select the **Deactivate** option from the drop-down menu and press **Apply**.

![deactivate all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deactivate-all-plugins-2.png)Try to search by a product SKU after these actions. If it works correctly now, then the problem was in the plugin incompatibility.

You can start activating plugins one by one to find out which one is causing problems with search.

If product search in the WooCommerce admin panel still doesnât work, you can also try the following solutions below.

#### Fix WooCommerce Products Database Corruption

Recently uploaded a large number of products using the CSV file import?

In this case, the problem with search in the admin panel may be related to corruption of the WooCommerce database.

You can update your product database to fix this problem.

To get started, navigate to **WooCommerce Â» Status** from your WordPress dashboard.

![go to the Tools tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-Tools-tab.png)Then, switch to the âToolsâ tab.

From here, you can scroll the page down to the **Product lookup tables** section and click **Regenerate**.

![press Regenerate](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Regenerate.png)After that, scroll down to the Update database section.

From here, you can press the **Update Database** button at the appropriate section.

![click Update database](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Update-database.png)When youâre done, wait for a couple of minutes to let WooCommerce finish updating the database.

Then check if the search in the admin panel has started working properly.

If updating the database didnât help, try the following method below.

![go to the All Products tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-All-Products-tab.png)First, navigate to **Products Â» All Products** in your WordPress dashboard.

From here, you can highlight the products visible on the page by checking the topmost checkbox.

![check all products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-all-products.png)Then, click on the **Bulk actions** drop-down menu at the top.

Next, you can choose the **Edit** option, and press **Apply**.

![click Apply](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Apply.png)After that, click the **Update** button to update all your products at once.

Itâll make WooCommerce reindex all products and help solve problems with their display in search in the admin panel.

![press Update](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Update-2.png)#### How to Deal with Hosting Memory Limitations

Another possible reason that can cause problems with search in the admin panel is hosting memory limitations.

Typically, you donât have access to the hosting configuration options and canât increase the memory limit yourself.

So the only way to solve the problem is to contact your hosting support team.

Theyâll help you to figure out if itâs your case and increase the memory limit if itâs necessary.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

By fixing SKU searchin WooCommerce, you allow your customers to find the products theyâre looking for faster and increase your sales.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix Woocommerce search by SKU not working. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") and [how to search by WooCommerce product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)").

### [How to Make MS Office Files Searchable in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-ms-office-files-searchable-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** June 19, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâve been managing WordPress websites for years, then you know that the default WordPress search wouldnât search MS documents on your site accurately.

Perhaps you uploaded Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, and PowerPoint presentations. But default search completely ignored what was inside those files.

However, with SearchWP, you can make your Office files and all that hidden content within the files searchable without editing code.

In this article, weâll show you how to make MS Office files searchable in WordPress.

### Why You Need to Make MS Office Files Searchable?

Your WordPress site probably has more content than you realize. Every time you upload a Word document, Excel spreadsheet, or PowerPoint presentation, youâre adding valuable information that your visitors canât actually find.

For example, when someone searches your site for âquarterly reportâ or âtraining materials,â [default WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") only looks at page titles and basic file names. It completely ignores the 50 pages of detailed analysis inside that âquarterly-report.docxâ file you uploaded.

This creates a frustrating experience for your visitors. They know the information exists somewhere on your site, but they canât locate it through search.

As a result, many people will give up and leave rather than dig through multiple pages trying to find what they need.

From a business perspective, this is like having a treasure chest thatâs locked away. All that expert knowledge, research data, and detailed documentation becomes essentially invisible to the people who need it most.

The problem gets worse as your site grows, and you have hundreds of Office documents uploaded over the years, containing thousands of pages of valuable content.

Making these files searchable transforms your website into a true [knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-the-knowledge-base-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress"). Suddenly, every piece of content becomes discoverable, and your visitors can find exactly what theyâre looking for with a simple search.

That said, letâs look at how you can include MS Office files in WordPress search.

### Make MS Office Files Searchable in WordPress

The best way to include MS Office files in the search process on your WordPress site is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that gives you complete control over how search works on your site.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)What makes SearchWP different is that it doesnât just index file names. It actually reads the content inside your Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, and PowerPoint presentations.

This means visitors can search for specific phrases or data that appear anywhere within these files.

Besides that, SearchWP also lets you:

- **[Display Live Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free")**: Want to instantly display search results on your site? With SearchWP, you can add live search results effortlessly with just a few clicks.
- **[Make WordPress Search PDF Files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)")**: Do you upload PDFs to your site? With SearchWP, you can make the content within them searchable, enriching your siteâs search capabilities.
- **[Exclude Pages from Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress")**: Want to exclude specific pages from search results on your site? SearchWP allows you to do so quickly and easily with just a few clicks.
- **[Track Searches on Your Site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site")**: Want to uncover what your visitors are searching for on your site? SearchWP gathers data about search activity, giving you valuable insights into your usersâ queries.

Now, letâs look at all the steps you need to follow to make MS Office documents searchable using SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, you need to get your [copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") by visiting their website and creating a new account.

After purchasing, you can navigate to your SearchWP account and go to the âDownloadsâ tab. From here, simply click the **Download SearchWP** button to get the pluginâs ZIP file on your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)You should also copy your SearchWP license key from the same tab, as youâll need this to activate the plugin shortly.

Once the download finishes, youâll need to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your WordPress site and install the plugin. If you need help with this, then check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After activation, youâll see SearchWPâs onboarding wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the instructions to complete the setup.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Thatâs it for the basic installation. Now, youâre ready to configure SearchWP specifically for Office file searching.

### Step 2: Customize Search Engine to Include MS Office Files

Now that SearchWP is installed, you need to configure it to actually search inside your Office files. By default, WordPress completely ignores attachment content, so this step is crucial for making your documents searchable.

Think of a search engine in SearchWP as a set of rules that tells the plugin what to search and how to rank results. You can [create multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)") or customize the default engine on your site.

For this tutorial, we will edit the default engine and include MS Office files.

To start, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)A new window will open showing different content sources SearchWP can index.

Youâll see options for Posts, Pages, and [Media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files"), this is where the magic happens for Office files.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Make sure the **Media** source is checked. This tells SearchWP to include your uploaded MS Office files in search results, not just your regular posts and pages.

After that, you can click âDoneâ to save these changes.

The next step is configuring which attributes SearchWP should consider when searching through your attachments.

To edit this, simply click **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the âMediaâ section.

![Add remove attributes for media](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-remove-attributes-for-media-1030x453.png)Next, youâll see several options already selected, like title, description, slug, caption, and author.

Just make sure that the **Document Content** attribute is checked. This allows SearchWP to read inside your Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, and PowerPoint presentations rather than just searching file names.

![Select attributes for media source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-attributes-for-media-source-1030x550.png)Thatâs not all, it also lets SearchWP look inside all media files, including PDF documents, image metadata, and more. This further improves your website search and helps users find what theyâre looking for.

When youâre finished, go ahead and click âDoneâ button.

After that, simply scroll to the top and click **Save** to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Your search engine is now configured to include MS Office file content.

### Step 3: Test Your MS Office File Search

Now comes the exciting part, testing whether SearchWP is actually reading inside your Office files.

First, make sure you have some Office files uploaded to your WordPress media library. If you donât have any test files, upload a Word document or Excel spreadsheet that contains specific text you can search for later.

Then, you can visit your websiteâs search form or search bar and try searching for your uploaded Office documents.

For instance, we uploaded an âEvent Planner and Timelineâ Excel spreadsheet on our demo site.

![Search for MS Office document](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-ms-office-document-1030x444.png)When we search for the document or enter search terms that are within the Excel file, the search showed correct results.

This means that your MS Office files are now searchable in WordPress.

### Bonus: Exclude Documents from Search Results

While making important documents searchable can improve user experience on your site, there are situations where you donât want visitors to find certain files.

For instance, you might want to hide outdated files, internal administrative documents, or irrelevant file types, such as zip archives or design mockups, from the search results.

Similarly, sensitive internal documents, policies, or exclusive content shouldnât be discoverable through a general site search.

This is where you can use SearchWP to easily exclude documents from search results. It lets you set up advanced rules to hide documents based on file type, filename, publish date, or ID.

![Enter name of file to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-name-of-file-to-exclude-1030x481.png)To learn more, please see our step-by-step guide on [how to exclude documents from search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-documents-from-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How to Exclude Documents from Search Results in WordPress").

We hope this article helped you learn how to make MS Office file searchable in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to display excerpts on the WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-excerpts-on-the-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Display Excerpts on the WordPress Search Results Page") and [how to limit WordPress search to post content](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-in-post-content/ "How to Display Excerpts on the WordPress Search Results Page").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") today!
