### [How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** June 17, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Have you ever noticed customers finding products they shouldnât see in your WooCommerce store search results?

Maybe youâre running a special promotion thatâs only meant for certain customers, or you have seasonal items that shouldnât appear during off-peak times.

Perhaps youâve got products that are temporarily out of stock, or items that are only available through direct links for VIP customers.

Weâve seen this frustration countless times with store owners who want more control over their search functionality. The default WooCommerce search can be pretty basic, showing everything to everyone, and thatâs not always what you want for your business strategy.

In this article, weâll show you how you can exclude any product from search results on your site with a few clicks.

### Why Exclude Products from Search in WooCommerce

There are a couple of common situations when hiding products from search results on your site might be useful.

For instance, letâs say you have special deals for a certain group of visitors, such as your newsletter subscribers. If anyone can find these deals using a search, then it doesnât make much sense.

By hiding such products from search results, you can control their visibility and ensure that they are only available to the right users.

Another example is when some of your products are no longer in business or are out of stock, but you donât want to remove them from your store.

If your customers come across such products in a search and decide to buy them, theyâll face disappointment.

Since frustrated customers are harmful to your business, itâs better to avoid such situations.

That said, letâs see how you can do that on your site.

### Exclude a WooCommerce Product from Search Results 

The easiest way to exclude specific products from your site search results is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the most powerful [WooCommerce search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-woocommerce-search-plugins/ "7 Best WooCommerce Search Plugins (Free + Paid)") that allows you to get full control over how search works in your store.

With SearchWP, you can create customized search forms, display search results in live mode, add or remove search sources, and more.

Plus, itâs fully compatible with WooCommerce and offers a dedicated extension. It means that you can start using the plugin right after installation without any extra steps.

Besides that, more than 50,000 eCommerce site owners already use SearchWP to give their visitors more relevant search results. It allows them to increase their revenue and build a loyal base of customers.

In addition to excluding products from search, you can also use SearchWP to:

- **[Enable search by product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)")**: Would you like to let your customers search by product attributes like color, price, and so on? Using this plugin, you can enable this feature in just a few clicks.
- [**Prioritize products in search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress"): Want to put a specific product at the top of search results by a particular keyword? SearchWP allows you to do that with no coding needed.
- [**Limit search to certain product categories**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce"): Prefer to limit search in your store to some specific categories? Just add the appropriate rule to your search engine in SearchWP settings to make it come true.
- [**Track searches of your customers**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Are you interested in what visitors were searching for in your online store? SearchWP automatically gathers data about all search activity on your site.

Now, letâs take a look at how you can exclude WooCommerce products from search using SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, you will need to [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

Once done, simply sign in to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)From here, you can press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After completing the download, you should also copy the license key at the bottom left, as youâll need it further to activate the plugin.

The next step is to upload the downloaded SearchWP ZIP file to your site.

Not sure how to do that? Follow this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step tutorial](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After youâve configured SearchWP, the next step is to install a couple of extensions.

### Step 2: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Integration Extension

The first extension youâll need to install is the [WooCommerce Integration extension](https://searchwp.com/features/woocommerce-integration/ "Advanced WooCommerce Product Search Plugin").

It ensures seamless integration of SearchWP and WooCommerce, so you can fully customize how search works in your store.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard.

Then head to WooCommerce Integration and click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)### Step 3: Install and Activate the Exclude UI Extension

The next step is to install the [Exclude UI](https://searchwp.com/features/exclude-ui/ "Exclude Content from WordPress Search Plugin") SearchWP extension. It allows you to exclude any post, page, or product from search results from the content editor.

You will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard and navigate to the âExclude UIâ extension.

![Install Exclude UI extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-exclude-ui-extension-1030x560.jpg)From here, simply click the **Install** button and the extension will automatically activate.

Once youâve installed the extensions, youâre now ready to start excluding products from search results.

### Step 4: Exclude Products from Search Results in WooCommerce

Now, there are 2 ways to hide products from WooCommerce search results.

First, you can use the Exclude UI option and manually exclude products from search. Second, you can set up rules in SearchWP and specify which products to remove from the search process.

Letâs take a look at each of these methods.

#### Method 1: Manually Mark Products You Want to Exclude from Search

The first way is to manually mark products that you want to exclude from search.

To get started, youâll need to visit the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can edit the default WordPress search or add a new engine for your site. Plus, you can control which sources and attributes to include in the search process.

Next, you can click the **Sources & Settings** button and see which sources the native search engine will include while searching.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a new window will open with the default sources, which includes posts, pages, and media.

From here, youâll need to ensure that **Products** is also selected as a source.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)Once youâre finished, click the âDoneâ button.

Next, you will need to navigate to the **Products** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![go over to the Products tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-Prodcuts-tab.png)Here, you can find all the products on your WooCommerce store.

After that, go ahead and click on a [product you want to hide](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)") from appearing in search results.

![click on a product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-a-product.png)Next, you will see the product editor in WooCommerce.

Go ahead and tick the **Exclude from SearchWP** checkbox under the Publish metabox.

![Enable exclude from SearchWP option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-exclude-from-searchwp-option-1030x549.png)Once done, press the **Update** button to save the changes.

Thatâs it. This product will no longer appear in the search results on your site.

Now, letâs take a look at the second way of excluding products with SearchWP where you can automatically exclude products by attributes, tags, or anything else.

#### Method 2: Automatically Exclude Products by Specific Taxonomy

To get started, you will need to go to the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard and customize the search engine.

You can think of a search engine as a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing a search. By adding or removing these rules, you can fully customize [how search works](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") on your site.

For example, you can specify where it should search, what attributes to consider, and many more.

To exclude products by a certain attribute, [tag](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-wordpress-posts-by-tags-only/ "How to Search WordPress Posts by Tags Only (Newbie-Friendly)"), category, or anything else, youâll need to add an appropriate rule.

But before that, we should add WooCommerce products as a search source to our search engine. So, go ahead and press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)A new window will open, where youâll see the sources used by the search engine.

After that, you need to tick the box next to the **Products** source and press **Done** when youâre finished.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)Now your WordPress site will consider WooCommerce products while performing a search.

The next step is to add a rule specifying which products you want to hide from displaying in search results.

To do that, you can scroll down to the **Products** section and press the **Edit Rules** button.

![Edit product rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-product-rules-1030x443.png)A new window will now open where youâll need to add new rules. At first, there will be no rules.

You can go ahead and click **Add Rule** to add the first rule.

![Add new rule for products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-rule-for-products-1030x383.png)On the next screen, you can set the rule to exclude WooCommerce products.

First, you will need to choose the **Exclude entries if:** option from the top-left dropdown menu.

![Choose exclude from option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-exclude-from-option-1030x516.png)After that, click on the **Taxonomy** field and choose a particular taxonomy by which you want to exclude products.

Taxonomy is a common attribute of a group of products. It might be a product type, category, tag, or something else.

![Select product tags to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-tags-to-exclude-1030x571.png)For example, letâs say that we want to automatically exclude from search all products with the **Special deal** tag.

To do that, you can select the **Product tags** taxonomy from the dropdown menu and then enter **Special deal** into the field next to it.

Here is how it looks on our test site:

![Enter product tags to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-product-tags-to-exclude-1030x503.png)You can also exclude products based on categories, color, size, [shipping class](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)"), and more by setting up different rules.

When you finish editing the rules, click **Done** to save them.

After that, you can press the **Save** button at the top right to save all the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Now all products with a **Special deal** tag will no longer appear in search results on your site.

The final step is to try out your new search.

### Step 5: Test Your New Search

To do that, simply visit your website and head to the search form on your WooCommerce store.

For the purposes of this tutorial, we uploaded the **Daily moisture lotion** product to our store and added a **Special deal** tag to it.

Letâs try to search for it and see if it shows up in the search results or not.

![press Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Find-Results-2.png)As you can see, there are no results for the **Daily moisture lotion**.

It means that the rule we added to our search engine to exclude products with the **Special deal** tag from the search works correctly.

![no results found](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/no-results-found-1.png)### FAQs About Excluding Products from WooCommerce Search

Here are some common questions people have about excluding products from search in WooCommerce.

**1. How do I hide specific products from WooCommerce search results?**

You can hide specific products from search results using a plugin like SearchWP. After installing the plugin and the Exclude UI extension, you can simply check the âExclude from SearchWPâ box in the product editor to remove it from search.

**2. Can I automatically exclude products based on tags or categories?**

Yes, with SearchWP you can set up rules to automatically exclude products from search based on taxonomies like product tags, categories, or even custom attributes. This is ideal for seasonal items, special deals, or limited-access products.

**3. Will excluded products still appear in my store or category pages?**

Yes, excluding products from search results only affects the internal search function. The products will still be visible on other parts of your store (like category pages or direct URLs) unless you take additional steps to hide them elsewhere.

**4. Can I reverse the exclusion and make products searchable again?**

Yes, you can easily make excluded products searchable again. Simply uncheck the âExclude from SearchWPâ option in the product editor or remove the exclusion rule from your SearchWP algorithm settings.

We hope this article helped you learn how to exclude product from search in WooCommerce. You may also want to see our guides on [how to make a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") and [how to search by SKU in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

If youâre ready to start easily exclude WooCommerce products from search on your site, you can [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").

### [How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/)

**Published:** June 12, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Did you know that the default WordPress search doesnât allow your customers to search product attributes?

WordPressâs built-in search treats your WooCommerce store like a blog, looking only at post titles and content. So, if you have product details like color, size, brand, material, and more, then WordPress will ignore these details.

But with the right plugin, like SearchWP, you can include product details and enhance the search process of your eCommerce store.

In this article, weâll show you how to make your WordPress site consider product attributes when performing a search.

## Why Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes

To begin with, letâs clarify what product attributes are. Product attributes are any additional information you specify about your product.

It might be its color, size, weight, price, or anything else. Making this info searchable is crucial, as it helps customers to find products theyâre looking for faster.

For example, letâs say youâre the owner of an online clothing store. Typically, the names of products in a clothing store contain only its type and brand, such as Adidas Full-Zip hoodie.

But what if a customerâs looking for a hoodie in a particular color?

You couldâve specified the color in the product attributes, but WordPress doesnât consider this data when performing a search.

![specify the product's color](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/specify-the-products-color.png)So, your customers have no choice but to manually browse your store to find the desired product.

Itâs tedious and boring, so itâs much easier for them to leave your site and make a purchase somewhere else.

Thatâs why itâs so important to make the product attributes in your [WooCommerce store searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") if you want to succeed online.

Additionally, customers who have had a positive user experience on your site are more likely to return once again.

Building a loyal customer base is essential, as it allows you to maintain a stable flow of orders.

Now that we know why to make WordPress consider product attributes while searching, letâs see how you can do that.

### How to Make WooCommerce Product Attributes Searchable

The easiest way to make WooCommerce product attributes searchable on your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP.](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the [best WooCommerce search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-woocommerce-search-plugins/ "7 Best WooCommerce Search Plugins (Free + Paid)") that brings tons of useful features. Plus, it has seamless WooCommerce integration.

With SearchWP, you can hide or prioritize specific products in search results, [add a search form to your shop page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/ "How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page"), and much more.

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners already use it to give their visitors more relevant search results.

In addition to making WordPress consider product attributes, you can also use SearchWP to:

- [**Track searches on your site:**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") Would you like to find out what customers were searching for in your store? SearchWP collects data about search activity on your site so that you can get useful information on your customersâ needs.
- [**Enable fuzzy search feature**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"): Want to improve your customersâ search experience even further? Turn on the partial match feature in SearchWP settings so that your visitors can find the right product even if they make a typo in its name.
- [**Make PDF files searchable**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"): Do you upload any PDFs to your site, such as user manuals or product installation guides? With SearchWP, you can make the text in them searchable so that your visitors can find them more easily.
- [**Restrict search to products only**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/ "How To Restrict WooCommerce Product Search To Title Only"): Want to exclude unnecessary pages such as âAbout usâ and so on from search results on your site? With SearchWP, you can do that in a few clicks so that nothing distracts your customers from shopping.

Now letâs take a look at how you can add a search by WooCommerce product attributes to your site using SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install and Activate the SearchWP Plugin

The first step is to [grab your copy of the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

Next, you can log into your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once there, click **Download SearchWP** to begin downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After downloading, you can also copy your license key in the same tab. Youâll need to activate the plugin further.

The next step is to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site. If you need help with this, then check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, you will see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After youâve set up SearchWP, the next step is to install the WooCommerce Integration extension.

### Step 2: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Integration Extension

This extension ensures the seamless integration of SearchWP with WooCommerce and helps customers to find different products on your site with ease.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard. Then head to WooCommerce Integration and click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)Now, youâre ready to start customizing your search engine to make it consider WooCommerce product attributes.

### Step 3: Customize Your Search Engine

SearchWP allows you to create an unlimited number of search engines and link them to specific search forms. With this feature, you can add separate, fully customized search forms to any place on your site.

Besides that, you can also edit the default search engines settings to customize how the search works across your entire WooCommerce store. For example, you can add new search sources, [exclude certain content](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)") from search results, and more.

To get started, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard.

Here youâll find the list of search engines created by SearchWP. By default, there is only the **Default** search engine, which is associated with every existing search form on your site.

For this tutorial, we want to allow visitors to search for [WooCommerce products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)") by the attributes using any search form on our site. Thatâs why weâll customize the **Default** search engine.

The first step is to add WooCommerce products as a search source. To do so, simply press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a new window will open where youâll see different sources that the default engine will use.

Go ahead and tick the checkbox next to the **Products** search source to make it active.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, press **Done** to save the changes.

The next step is to make the product attributes searchable.

To get started, scroll down to the **Products** section and press the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)Next, you will see a new window popup. Here, you can add attributes stored as custom fields or taxonomies (categories and tags).

First, click on the âCustom Fieldsâ field and choose the **Any Meta Key** option.

![Choose product any meta option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-product-any-meta-option-1030x502.png)This will automatically make all the custom fields on your site searchable so that you donât have to enter the name of each of them manually.

Since WooCommerce stores product attributes exactly as [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)"), by adding this shortcut, youâll make them searchable too.

Besides that, you can also include product categories, tags, type, shipping classes, and more in the search process.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)When youâre finished, go ahead and press **Done**.

Then scroll back to the top and click **Save** to apply all the changes youâve made to the Default search engine.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Now, your customers can search for products by their attributes.

The final step is to test your new search engine.

### Step 4: Test Your New Search by Product Attributes

To get started, simply visit your site and try entering a product attribute in the search form.

For the purposes of this tutorial, weâve added the **Menâs Casual Pullover Hoodie** product, which has the **black** color as an attribute. Letâs see if we can find this product by searching for **black**.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results.png)As you can see, we found the product, even though it doesnât have the word **black** in its title or description.

It means that our test site now considers WooCommerce product attributes when performing a search.

![we found the product by its attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-product-by-its-attributes.png)### Bonus: Search in Category in WooCommerce

Offering customers the ability to search for products exclusively within a specific category on your WooCommerce store can significantly refine their shopping experience.

When a user is already browsing a particular category, a site-wide search can introduce irrelevant results, causing frustration and potentially leading them away from their intended purchase.

By providing a category-specific search option, you help them quickly narrow down their choices and find exactly what theyâre looking for within that focused area of your inventory.

With SearchWP, you can create custom rules and limit the search to specific WooCommerce categories.

![Select your category](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-category-1030x500.png)The best part is that SearchWP lets you create unlimited search engines, which can be linked to different search forms. This allows you to create separate forms for your online store.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to search in category in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

We hope this article helped you learn how to search by WooCommerce product attributes. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") and [how to make a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)").

### [How to Exclude Documents from Search Results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-documents-from-search-results-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** June 10, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Managing document search results in WordPress can be tricky, especially when you have sensitive files or internal documents that shouldnât appear in public searches.

After managing multiple WordPress sites with thousands of documents, we learned that having control over what appears in search results is crucial for maintaining site security and user experience.

For instance, if every single PDF, document, and media file started showing up in search results, then this isnât ideal. Especially when some documents are meant for specific team members or contained information that wasnât ready for public viewing.

In this article, weâll show you how to exclude documents from search results in WordPress.

### Why Exclude Documents from WordPress Search Results

Tailoring your WordPress siteâs search by excluding certain documents can significantly enhance user experience and relevance.

Many website owners may want to remove items like outdated files, internal administrative documents, or irrelevant file types, such as zip archives or design mockups, from the search results.

This declutters the search experience, guiding visitors more efficiently towards key content like [blog posts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)"), product pages, or publicly available PDFs, rather than irrelevant documentation.

Besides, excluding documents is often crucial for maintaining privacy and security. Sensitive internal materials, draft policies, or member-only content, even if accessible via direct link, shouldnât necessarily be discoverable through a general site search.

Similarly, if you have dedicated document libraries or specialized search portals (like a [knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-the-knowledge-base-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress")), excluding those documents from the main site search can prevent duplicate work and direct users appropriately.

Ultimately, selectively excluding documents isnât about hiding information, but about curating a more effective and user-friendly search.

That said, letâs look at how you can prevent certain documents from appearing in WordPress search.

### Excluding Documents from Search Results in WordPress

The easiest way to include documents and filter which files to show in the search results is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)The default WordPress search is able to find [PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") and other documents in your media library. However, its search capabilities are quite limited and wonât show relevant results.

The [native search](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") also doesnât give you the option to customize which documents should appear in the search results.

This is where SearchWP helps you out. It is a powerful WordPress search plugin designed to dramatically improve your websiteâs native search capabilities.

Unlike the often limited default WordPress search, SearchWP allows you to make more of your siteâs content searchable, including PDF content, custom fields, [product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), categories, tags, and more.

It gives you control over the search algorithm, enabling you to define relevance and customize what users find, ultimately leading to a better user experience.

With that, letâs look at the steps for excluding certain documents from the search process.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to [visit the SearchWP website](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Sign Up for a SearchWP Account") and create a new account.

Next, you can go to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

Once there, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the plugin ZIP file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, youâll need to log into your WordPress admin dashboard to upload and set up the plugin.

If you need some help doing this, then check out this detailed guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, the welcome screen will appear along with the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Once SearchWP is ready to use, you can now customize the search engine to exclude documents.

### Step 2: Customize Engine to Exclude Documents from Search Results

To start, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can customize the default WordPress search or create new search engines for your site. Think of setting up a search engine like adding a mini Google search algorithm to your site. It allows you to control how SearchWP will look for search results.

First, you can click the âSources & Settingsâ button at the top and see which sources the default engine is using.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a small window will open, where you can edit the sources for the native search engine.

Just make sure that **Media** is selected as a source.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)When youâre finished, go ahead and click the **Done** button.

After that, you can scroll down to the Media source section and then add a rule to exclude specific documents.

To start, simply click the **Edit Rule** option.

![Edit media rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-media-rules-1030x456.png)Next, a new window will open where you can define rules for the search engine.

At first, there wonât be any rules. So, go ahead and click the **Add Rule** button.

![Add rule for media](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-rule-for-media-1030x378.png)On the next screen, you will need to click the dropdown menu at the top.

From here, simply choose the âExclude entries ifâ option.

![Select exclude from option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-exclude-from-option-1030x505.png)Next, SearchWP gives multiple options to exclude content from the search process.

For instance, you can exclude documents based on file type, file name, publish date, or ID.

![Choose how to exclude documents from search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-how-to-exclude-documents-1030x477.png)For this tutorial, weâll exclude documents based on âFilenameâ.

After that, weâll enter the title of the document.

![Enter name of file to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-name-of-file-to-exclude-1030x481.png)You can use the same option or use another one to exclude documents from WordPress search. For example, if you donât want anyone to find PDFs on your website, then you can simply choose the âFile Typeâ option and select the PDFs option.

When youâve added the rule, simple click the **Done** button.

Now, you can simply scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button to store changes to the search engine.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)### Step 3: Test Your New Search Engine for Excluded Documents

Now that youâve set up the search engine to search specific documents, the next step is to test it on your site.

To do this, simply visit your site and enter the name of the document in the [search bar](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") that you donât want users to find.

For example, if we search for the âInternal Team Trainingâ document on our demo site, the result doesnât pick up anything.

![No search result for document](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/no-search-result-for-document-1030x475.png)This shows that the search is working correctly, and it filters the document you donât want people to find on your site.

### Bonus: Use Boolean Search to Exclude Content

Did you know that you can use search operators or Boolean search to filter search results in WordPress?

Boolean search is a powerful technique that can significantly refine search results on your WordPress site. It goes beyond simple keyword matching by using operators to create more precise search queries.

While common operators include âANDâ to find results containing all specified terms and âORâ to find results containing either term, the âNOTâ operator is particularly useful for filtering out irrelevant content and [enhancing the search experience](https://searchwp.com/ways-to-improve-wordpress-search/ "11 Ways to Improve WordPress Search (Expert Tips)").

SearchWP offers a Boolean Search extension that users can use to exclude keywords from search results using the **NOT** or **â** operators. For example, they can search for âcats -dogsâ and the search would return results including âcatsâ but not âdogsâ.

![Search your site using operators](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-your-site-using-operators-1030x340.png)To learn more, please see our detailed guide on [how to use boolean search to exclude content from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-boolean-search-to-exclude-content-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Use Boolean Search to Exclude Content from WordPress Search").

We hope this article helped you learn how to exclude documents from search results in WordPress. You may also want to see our guides on [how you can exclude any product from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-product-from-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Exclude Product from Search in WooCommerce") and [how to exclude pages or posts from site search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) today!

### [How to Hide Category from Search in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-category-from-search-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** June 5, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Managing search results in your WooCommerce store can be tricky, especially when product categories keep showing up where they shouldnât.

Product categories are great for organizing your store, but they can clutter up search results and confuse your customers.

By hiding certain categories, you can avoid cluttering search results, making it easier to navigate your online store.

In this article, weâll show you the easiest way to exclude specific WooCommerce categories from search results on your site.

### Why Hide Categories from Search in WooCommerce

There are lots of reasons why you might want to hide some WooCommerce product categories from the search results on your site.

By hiding categories from search in WooCommerce, you can:

- **Make it easier to browse your store** â The easier it is for your customers to find a product theyâre looking for, the more profit youâll make.
- **Manage products more effectively** â Itâs convenient to have a specific category where you can store products that you donât want to appear in search results.
- **Restrict access to limited offers** â If you have a category with limited offers for certain customers, itâs better to exclude it from public search results.

Once weâve figured that out, letâs take a look at how you can hide specific WooCommerce categories from being displayed.

### Hide a WooCommerce Category from Search on Your Site

The easiest and fastest way to hide a WooCommerce category from search results on your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), and it gives you complete control over how search works on your WordPress site.

With it, you can select which categories you want to appear in search, prioritize specific search results, create custom search forms, and much more.

Over 50,000 eCommerce WordPress site owners already use it to give their customers more relevant search results and boost their sales.

SearchWP also improves your site search by helping you:

- **Enable search by product attributes**: Want your customers to search for products by their features, such as color, size, price, and others? With SearchWP, you can make WordPress consider product attributes when searching with just a few clicks.
- [**Add a search form to your shop page**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/ "How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page"): Want to effortlessly increase your profit and make it easier for customers to browse your store? One of the best ways to do that is to add a search form to your shop page with SearchWP.
- [**Track on-site search activity**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Wondering what your customers search for on your site? SearchWP gathers all the search data and gives you useful insights into your customersâ needs.
- [**Show search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"): Want to show search results in your store in Amazon style? With SearchWP, you can add live autocomplete search to your store, making it faster for your customers to find what theyâre looking for.

Now letâs take a look at how to exclude particular categories from your siteâs search results using SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, youâll need to visit the website and [signup for a SearchWP account](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

After that, you can go to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Then, go ahead and press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

Once the download begins, copy the SearchWP license key on the same tab. Youâll need it later to activate the plugin.

After youâve finished downloading, the next step is to upload the pluginâs ZIP file to your site. If you need a refresher on how to do that, please check this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Next, you will see the SearchWP setup wizard. Simply click the **Start Onboarding Wizard** button and follow the on-screen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)The next step is to install the WooCommerce extension on your site.

### Step 2: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Extension

This extension ensures seamless integration of WooCommerce and SearchWP, allowing you to get full control over search in your store.

To start, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard. From here, simply go to WooCommerce Integration and click the âInstallâ button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)Once youâve installed and activated the WooCommerce Integration extension, the next step is to customize your search engine.

### Step 3: Customize Your Search Engine

By setting up a search engine, you can specify the way WordPress should carry out searches on your site.

For instance, you can add or remove search sources, limit search to certain types of content, adjust the weight of relevance attributes and much more.

To get started, navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard.

You will see that there will be only one search engine called **Default**. By editing its settings, you can customize how the search works across your site.

To hide a specific WooCommerce category from search, you first need to add WooCommerce products as a search source. To do so, press the **Sources & Settings** button.

Youâll need to ensure that the checkbox next to the **Products** source is selected.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

After that, there will appear a new **Products** search source. It means that WordPress now considers WooCommerce products when searching.

The next step is to add a rule that will exclude certain WooCommerce categories from search results. To do so, press the **Edit rules** button within the Products source section.

![Product category rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/product-category-rules-1030x413.png)Here you can find the rules that apply to the **Products** search source.

![Edit rules of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-of-products-1-1030x383.png)First, youâll see that there will be no rules by default, so letâs add the first one.

To do that, you can click the **Add Rule** button.

Once done, youâll need to choose **Taxonomy** and then select the **Product categories** you want to exclude from the drop-down menu.

![WooCommerce category to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-woocommerce-category-to-exclude-1030x443.png)After that, enter the name of a WooCommerce category youâd like to exclude from the search to the very right field.

Click **Done** to save your changes when youâre finished adding categories you want to exclude.

Next, you can go to the top of the page and press the **Save** button to apply all the edits you made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it! Youâve successfully excluded a WooCommerce category from search results on your eCommerce website.

The final step is to test your new search.

### Step 4: Test Your New Search

To do that, simply visit your WooCommerce store.

For the purposes of this tutorial, we excluded the WooCommerce **Hats** category from the search results on our test site.

To check if the exclusion rules have been applied correctly, weâll search for the **Green hat** product, which is placed in the **Hats** category.

![search for a product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-a-product.png)As you can see, no results were found.

It means that weâve successfully excluded the **Hats** category from the search results.

![no results were found](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/no-results-were-found.png)We hope this article helped you learn how to hide a category from search in WooCommerce. You may also want to see our guide on [how to make a smart WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)") and [how to customize your WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search").

### [How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-smart-woocommerce-product-search/)

**Published:** June 3, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

For WooCommerce store owners looking to improve product discovery, reduce bounce rates, and ultimately boost sales, having a powerful search function is critical.

A smart search is essential if you want your customers to easily find what theyâre looking for, especially if you have thousands of products.

Thatâs because many online shoppers will quickly abandon your online store if they cannot find their desired products. But with the right plugin, you can boost the search experience on your WooCommerce store.

In this article, weâll show you the best way to make a smart WooCommerce product search without writing any code.

### What is a âSmartâ Product Search in WooCommerce?

Itâs hard to call the default WordPress search engine a smart one, as it has a lot of weak points.

To begin with, it doesnât reliably or extensively search for WooCommerce product attributes.

The thing is that WooCommerce stores products and their attributes as posts and [custom post fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), respectively.

But the native [WordPress search engine](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/ "How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)") is not good at including additional information stored in custom fields while performing a search.

So making them searchable is essential if you want to provide your customers with the most relevant search results.

Besides that, the default search also lacks many basic features when it comes to search. For example, you canât change the search results order, keyword stemming, [using synonyms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-synonym-rules-for-wordpress-search/ "How to Set Up Synonym Rules for WordPress Search"), show live searches, and more.

As a WooCommerce store owner, your income directly depends on your visitorsâ ability to find the products theyâre looking for. Thatâs why itâs essential to ensure that this process is as straightforward as possible.

Now, letâs take a look at how you can add a smart product search to your WooCommerce site.

### How to Make a Smart WooCommerce Product Search

Using this plugin, you can boost product discoverability and increase the number of sales.

Here are other things you can do with SearchWP:

- [**On-site search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Would you like to look at what your customers are searching for on your site? SearchWP tracks your visitorsâ searches and allows you to view them directly in your WordPress dashboard.
- [**Exclude pages from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"): Are there any unwanted pages in the current search results on your site? SearchWP allows you to hide them in just a few clicks.
- [**Prioritize specific search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress"): Launching a new product and want as many people as possible to notice it? Put it at the top of your storeâs search results with SearchWP.
- [**Enable fuzzy search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"): Do you want customers to find the right product even if they make a typo? Simply turn on fuzzy search in the SearchWP settings.

Now, letâs take a look at how to add a smart WooCommerce search to your online store using SearchWP.

The first step is to [grab your copy of the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!") by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

Once done, you can go to the **Downloads** tab in your SearchWP account dashboard.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)After you get there, simply press the **Download SearchWP** button to save the plugin ZIP file to your computer.

Then, copy the license key at the same tab, as youâll need it soon to activate SearchWP.

Once the download is finished, the next step is to upload the SearchWP plugin ZIP file to your site. If you need help, then please see this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and the setup wizard. You can click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After youâve installed and activated SearchWP, itâs time to install the WooCommerce Integration extension.

This extension ensures seamless integration of SearchWP and WooCommerce, allowing you to easily customize how search works in your store.

First, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard. From here, simply navigate to âWooCommerce Integrationâ and click the **Install** button.

![Installing the WooCommerce Integration extension for SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension-1030x464.jpg)Once youâve installed the WooCommerce extension, youâre ready to start making smart WooCommerce product search on your site.

### How to Add a Custom WooCommerce Search Widget

Would you like to add a custom WooCommerce search widget to your site? Itâll help your visitors find the products theyâre looking for faster.

For example, you can limit this widget to search only for [specific product categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)"). Or make it consider specific product tags, like refurbished or bestseller.

Using SearchWP, you can easily create and add a custom WooCommerce search widget to your store.

![press search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-search.png)The first step is to customize the default search engine and include WooCommerce products as a source.

To get started, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard. Then, press the **Source & Settings** button to select new sources.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)After that, youâll see the sources that the default engine uses. These include posts, pages, and media.

Make sure to select âProductsâ as a source and then click the **Done** button.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Now, you can scroll to the top and click the Save button to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)After that, you can add a WooCommerce widget to your siteâs widget area.

To start, simply go to **Appearance Â» Widgets** from the admin panel and click the + sign. From here, simply add the âProduct Searchâ widget block.

![Select product search widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-search-widget-1030x653.png)For more details, follow our step-by-step guide on [how to add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)").

### How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page

Do you want to add a WooCommerce search form to your shop page?

Thatâs a great way to boost your sales and allow visitors to navigate your store faster.

### How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes

Would you like to make your WordPress site search by WooCommerce product attributes?

Product attributes include features such as product size, color, price, and other details you would like to add.

With SearchWP, you can easily include categories, tags, custom fields, and other attributes in the search process.

Simply scroll down to the âProductsâ source and click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button.

You can then add custom fields like product color or model and taxonomies ([tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search by Product Tags in WooCommerce (Fastest Way)"), categories, shipping classes) to include in the search process.

![How to Add WooCommerce Product Search Widget: customize attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-product-attribute-1030x567.jpg)When you finish, donât forget to click the **Save** button at the top right once again to save your changes.

Thatâs it. Now, your WordPress site supports search by WooCommerce product attributes.

To learn more, you may want to see our guide on [how to search eCommerce product details in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress").

### How to Search in WooCommerce Category

Would you like to let your customers search by a specific category? Thatâs super convenient and allows them to find products much faster.

Using SearchWP, you can easily limit specific search forms to search by specific categories or create a category select search form.

This way, you can provide your customers with more relevant search results and boost your sales.

![Select your category](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-category-1030x500.png)For more details, see our detailed tutorial on [how to search in WooCommerce category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

### How to Add Fuzzy Search to Your Website

Do you want to make it even easier for your customers to find a product theyâre looking for?

You can do that by enabling fuzzy search in SearchWP.

This feature allows you to make WordPress search for partial matches. It ensures that your customers can find a product theyâre looking for even if they make a typo in its name.

For example, if while searching for marshmallows they misspell it as âmarshmellowsâ, they still will find this sweet product.

Using SearchWP, you can enable fuzzy search just by selecting a single checkbox.

![Enable partial matches and closest match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-matches-and-closest-match-1030x573.png)For more details, see our step-by-step tutorial on [how to add fuzzy search to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites").

### How to Prioritize Specific Products in Search Results

Would you like to bring some of your products to the top of the search results on your site?

This is a great way to promote new products and increase the number of sales.

With SearchWP, you can prioritize specific products in the search results for certain search terms within minutes using the Custom Results Order extension.

![Configure Custom Results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)For more details, check out our detailed guide on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress").

### How to Exclude Specific Product from Search Results

### [How to Display Excerpts on the WordPress Search Results Page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-excerpts-on-the-wordpress-search-results-page/)

**Published:** May 29, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Every website owner knows the frustration of a basic WordPress search that has limited capabilities and lack of control when it comes to showing search results.

To enhance the search experience, adding excerpts to WordPress search results transforms how users interact with your siteâs search function.

Instead of seeing just titles and a few lines from the introduction, visitors get preview text that helps them decide if a result matches what theyâre looking for.

In this article, weâll show you how to display excerpts on the WordPress search results page.

### Why Add Excerpts to WordPress Search Results?

Depending on your website theme, the default WordPress search shows the first few lines of your content in the search results. This means that even if someone searches for a term that appears in the middle of your post, theyâll only see the beginning text that might not include key information theyâre looking for.

Search results with excerpts give users a preview of the content before they click. This saves them time and helps them make better decisions about which results to explore.

Think of search excerpts like movie trailers. Just as a good trailer shows the most relevant scenes to attract viewers, a good search excerpt should show the most relevant parts of your content that match what people are searching for.

Adding excerpts to search results also helps prevent the frustrating experience of clicking back and forth between results. Users can scan the excerpts to find exactly what they need without loading multiple pages.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily show excerpts on the search results page on your site.

### Adding Excerpts to WordPress Search Results Page

One of the ways to display excerpts below search results is by customizing your WordPress website theme.

However, this method can be tricky for users, as it may involve hiring a developer.

An easier way to show excerpts for each search result is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that is beginner-friendly to use and helps transform the default WordPress search.

The plugin also lets you connect the results page with custom search forms, so you get complete control on how search works on your site.

Another benefit of using SearchWP is that you can include custom fields, categories, tags, [product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), documents, PDFs, and other additional information in the search process.

With that, letâs look at how you can display excerpts in search results.

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a new account.

After that, you should also copy your license key, as youâll need it further to activate the plugin.

When you finish downloading, the next step is to upload the SearchWP pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now that SearchWP is set up on your site, the next step is to create a results page template.

### Step 2: Customize the Results Page Template 

To start, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Templates** from the WordPress dashboard.

Here, you can edit the [default results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)") that will appear on your website or create a new one by clicking the âAdd Newâ button.

![Edit templates page in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-templates-page-in-searchwp-1030x494.png)For this tutorial, weâll go ahead and add a new results page template.

Next, you can start by editing the name at the top and then choosing a layout theme for your results page.

![Choose a search results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-search-results-template-1030x506.png)SearchWP offers 6 pre-built themes, each with different layouts. You can pick a minimal theme with title and excerpt or one that shows the featured image.

The best part is that you also get to see a preview of the page, so you can easily select the layout that you like.

![View preview of results page template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-preview-of-results-page-template-1030x633.png)Next, you can scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section and further customize your search results page.

When you change the layout style, ensure that the Description option is enabled to show excerpts. Besides that, you can also edit the title style, change the button style, and more.

![Change custom styling options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-custom-styling-options-1030x646.png)When youâre done customizing the theme, scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button to store your changes.

From here, youâll need to click the **Embed** button.

![Save and embed your results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-embed-your-results-page-1030x506.png)After that, you should see a popup window open. Here, you will need to choose how to embed the results template (Gutenberg block, shortcode, or PHP code) and where youâd like to embed it.

For instance, weâll select the âGutenberg Blockâ option and embed it by creating a new page.

![Change template and results page options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-template-and-results-page-options-2-1030x597.png)When youâre done, simply click the Save button at the top.

From here, you can go ahead and embed the form on your website.

![Save and embed category form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-embed-category-form-1030x489.png)If you want to learn more, then please see our guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

### Step 4: Test and See if Excerpts Appear on Search Results Page

Once youâve added the new search form to your site, the next thing to do is test if it is working correctly.

To do that, simply visit your website and search for a blog post that has an excerpt.

**Note:** If youâre not sure where to add an excerpt in the content, then you may want to see this guide on [how to easily add post excerpts to your WordPress theme](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wp-themes/how-to-display-post-excerpts-in-wordpress-themes/ "How to Display Post Excerpts in WordPress Themes").

For example, when we search for a blog post that had an excerpt using the search form on our test site, the results page showed the title of the post along with the excerpt under it.

![Search for posts with excerpt](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-posts-with-excerpt-1030x418.png)That it! Youâve now successfully added excerpts on the search results page.

We hope this article helped you learn how to display excerpts on the WordPress search results page. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix custom post type search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-custom-post-type-search-not-working-in-wordpress/ "How to Fix Custom Post Type Search Not Working in WordPress") and [how to customize your WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Fix Custom Post Type Search Not Working in WordPress").

### [How to Exclude Category from WordPress Search (3 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** May 27, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Categories can help you organize content on your site, but there are situations when you donât want users to find specific categories.

For instance, you might have sensitive content or want users to only find products from a certain category.

Sadly, there is no way to hide categories by default because WordPress search has many limitations.

In this article, weâll show you how to exclude any category from search results on your site in a few clicks.

### Why Exclude Category from WordPress Search

There are a couple of possible reasons why you might want to hide some categories or pages from search on your site.

To begin with, it helps your visitors to find what theyâre looking for faster.

Letâs assume you have the news section on your blog. News blends in with regular blog posts in search results, making it harder to find the right content.

Letâs say youâre an[ eCommerce WordPress site owner](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads "). Youâd prefer to exclude non-product categories from the search so that nothing distracts your customers from shopping.

Another reason why you might want to hide certain categories from showing up in search results is sensitive content.

Imagine that you have a category with content accessible only to a certain group of visitors. If anyone can find it using a search, it barely makes any sense.

Now that we know why it might be beneficial to hide specific categories from search results, letâs look at how you can do that.

### How to Exclude a Category from WordPress Search Results

The easiest way to exclude a category from search results on your WordPress site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Trusted by over 50,000 WordPress site owners, SearchWP is a powerful and easy-to-use plugin allows you to fully customize how search works on your site.

Specifically, you can use it to exclude or prioritize specific search results, make searchable custom post types, track searches, and many more.

SearchWP also improves your site search by helping you:

- [**Limit search to only posts**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-only-posts-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Only Posts in WordPress"): Would you like to limit the search on your site to only posts? Using SearchWP, you can do that within minutes, no coding needed.
- [**Add a search bar to your site**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)"). Looking for a way to add a search bar to your site without coding? There are 4 different ways to do that in a few easy steps with SearchWP.
- [**Create custom search forms**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"). Want to create a fully customized search form that meets your needs? SearchWP allows you to create multiple customized search engines and link them to separate search forms.
- [**Enable fuzzy search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"). Do you want WordPress to search for partial matches when there are no full ones? With SearchWP, you can enable this feature just by ticking a checkbox.

Now, letâs take a look at how to exclude a category from search on your WordPress site using the SearchWP plugin.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

The first step is to [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today"). Simply visit the website and create a new account.

After that, youâll need to open your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Then, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After completing the download, you can also copy the plugin license key in the bottom left corner on the same tab. Youâll need it further to activate your copy of SearchWP.

The next step is to upload the downloaded SearchWP ZIP file to your site. For more details, please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") step-by-step.

Upon activation, you will see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Once youâve activated SearchWP, youâre ready to start setting up your search engine.

### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engine

To get started, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard.

Here you can manage your search engines created with SearchWP.

By customizing search engine settings, you can specify how WordPress should perform searches.

For example, you can add or remove search sources, adjust attribute, set conditions for showing entries, and much more.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Currently, there is only one search engine called **Default**. WordPress uses it to perform searches across the entire site. It means that any changes you make in its settings will be automatically applied to every search form on your site.

SearchWP allows you to create multiple search engines with a different set of rules and link them to specific search forms.

You can also edit the sources of the search engine by clicking the âSource & Settingsâ button.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)When youâre finished, simply click the **Done** button.

Next, you can hide categories from WordPress search by adding rules.

For example, letâs say you want to exclude categories from posts. Simply navigate to âPostsâ and then click the **Edit Rules** button.

![Edit rules for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-for-search-results-1030x512.png)There are no rules by default, so youâll need to add the first one.

Go ahead and click **Add Rule**.

![Add a new rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-rule--1030x403.png)Then, choose the **Exclude entries if** option from the top-left drop-down menu.

![Select exclude from entries](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-exclude-from-entries-1030x489.png)Make sure you have the **Categories** taxonomy selected, and enter the names of the categories you want to exclude in the very right field.

For example, weâll exclude the **News** post category on our test site.

![Enter categories to exclude](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-categories-to-exclude-1030x510.png)When youâre finished adding categories, press **Done** to apply the rule.

Then, scroll to the top and click the **Save** button at the top right to save the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully excluded a category from appearing in search results on your site.

The final step is to try out your new search engine.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search

To do that, simply visit your website and access the search bar.

To test how search works on our test site, weâve added the **New features announcement** post and added it to the **News** category.

Letâs try to search for it.

![press Find results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Find-results-1.png)As you can see, there is nothing found.

It means that posts from the **News** category no longer appear in search results.

![no results found](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/no-results-found.png)We hope this article helped you learn how to exclude category from WordPress search. You may also want to see our guide on [how to track searches on your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") and [how to add a search form to your WooCommerce shop page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/ "How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page").
