### [How to Fix Custom Post Type Search Not Working in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-custom-post-type-search-not-working-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** May 22, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Custom post types help you organize different types of content on your WordPress site, like products, testimonials, or team members.

But thereâs one frustrating issue: custom post types donât show up in WordPress search results by default.

So you might wonder, how to fix custom post type search not working in WordPress?

It can be challenging, as there are a lot of possible reasons why this might be happening.

In this article, weâll show you the most popular reasons why your custom post type may not appear in search results and how to fix them.

### Why Custom Post Type Search Does Not Work? 

Custom post types are popular among WordPress site owners.

Adding them allows you to go beyond standard posts and pages and turn WordPress into a full-fledged content management system.

Specifically, youâre free to name a custom post whatever you want and add [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") containing any additional information to it.

It allows you to publish recipes, reviews, tutorials, and other types of content.

Here is an example of a custom post type:

![an example of a custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/an-example-of-a-custom-post-type.png)But if your visitors canât search by custom post types, it hardly makes sense to publish them, as they wonât get any views.

Below, youâll find the 3 most common examples of why search by custom post types may not work on your site.

#### 1. WordPress Doesnât Show Custom Post Type in Search Results

By default, WordPress will include custom post types in the search results, as long as youâve not made them hidden using a plugin.

However, the default search has many limitations and it often doesnât show relevant results.

So if you try to search for content thatâs a custom post type, then chances are that you might get accurate results.

To tackle this issue and make your custom post types searchable, you can use a search plugin and improve the default search process.

#### 2. You Hid Your Custom Post Type from Search

The easiest way to create a custom post type is to use a WordPress plugin.

But many plugins have a feature that lets you choose whether you want the custom post type to be visible in search or not.

So, if you changed this parameter, then it may be the reason your custom post type doesnât appear in search results.

#### 3. Plugin Compatibility Issue

Added a custom post type to your site using a plugin, but it doesnât appear in search results?

The possible reason may be a plugin compatibility issue.

When two or more plugins conflict with one another, it can cause problems which would lead to custom post type not appearing in search results.

### How to Fix Custom Post Type Search Not Working in WordPress

Now that you know why search by custom post types might not work on your WordPress site, letâs look at possible ways to solve this problem.

#### 1. Make WordPress Search by Custom Post Types

The easiest way to make WordPress search by custom post types is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)It is the best [search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and is a comprehensive solution that allows you to get complete control over how search works across your site. Beyond making custom post types searchable, it has tons of useful features.

For example, using SearchWP, you can display search results in live mode, add a search bar with a shortcode, and much more.

SearchWP boosts your on-site search by helping you:

- [**Prioritize specific search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress"): Would you like to put a specific page at the top of search results on your site? SearchWP allows you to manually affect the search results order, so you can easily swap them around.
- [**Exclude specific pages from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"): Are there some pages youâd prefer to hide from search? Using SearchWP, you can do that with a few clicks, no coding needed.
- [**Track searches of your visitors**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Wondering what search terms visitors enter on your site? SearchWP collects data about search activity on your site so you can understand your audienceâs interests better.
- [**Create custom search forms**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): Do you want to create a customized search form? With SearchWP, you can fully customize a search form to meet your needs and then add it to any place on your site.

Now, letâs look at how to make WordPress consider custom post types when searching with SearchWP.

**Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP**

To get started, youâll need to [grab a copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today") by visiting the website and creating a new account.

Once done, simply navigate to your SearchWP account and go over to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Then, press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

After that, you can also copy your license key at the bottom left on the same tab. Youâll need it further to activate the plugin.

When you finish downloading, the next step is to upload the SearchWP pluginâs ZIP file to your site.

If you need help with this, then check out the [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") step-by-step guide.

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the steps in the wizard.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After you set up SearchWP, the next step is to add your custom post type as a search source.

**Step 2: Add Your Custom Post Type as a Search Source**

To do that, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress admin panel.

By default, there is only one search engine called Default. You can customize the [default engine](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)") and change how the search works throughout your site.

For example, you can change what types of content WordPress considers when searching, exclude specific categories or pages, and more.

As you can see, currently, this search engine performs searches by 3 sources: posts, pages, and media.

To make WordPress search by a custom post type, we need to add it as a search source as well. To do that, you can press the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)After that, youâll see the window where you can choose search sources you want WordPress to take into account while searching.

Simply find your custom post type among these sources and tick the box next to it to add it as a search source.

For example, on our test site, we added a custom post type called **Tutorials**.

![Select custom post type source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-custom-post-type-source-1030x492.png)When youâre finished, go ahead and click the **Done** button.

Next, you can press the **Save** button at the top right to store the changes youâve made.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully added a custom post type to your site search. The next step is to try out your customized search engine.

**Step 3: Test Your New Search**

To do that, simply visit your website and search for the custom post type.

For example, on our test site, weâve added the âHow to install a WordPress pluginâ post as the **Tutorial** custom post type.

Letâs search for it and see if it appears in search results.

![press Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Find-Results.png)The search form should show the custom post type.

As you can see, it found the post.

![we found the custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-custom-post-type.png)This means that the search engine now considers custom post types and searches by them.

### How to Reveal Your Custom Post Type in Search Results

Added your custom post type as a search source following the guide above, but it still doesnât appear in search?

If youâve created a custom post type using a plugin, then there is a good chance you accidentally changed its visibility settings within the plugin.

For example, weâll show you how to check it on the [Custom Post Type UI](https://wordpress.org/plugins/custom-post-type-ui/ "Custom Post Type UI") plugin.

First, youâll need to navigate to **CPT UI Â» Add/Edit Post Types** in the left panel of your WordPress dashboard.

![press Add Edit Post Types](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Add-Edit-Post-Types.png)Here, you will see options to create a new custom post type.

Once there, you can switch to the **Edit Post Types** tab.

![go over to the Edit Post Types tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-Edit-Post-Types-tab.png)After that, you will need to choose a custom post you want to edit from the dropdown menu at the top left.

In this example, weâll select the **Tutorials** custom post type.

![choose your custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-your-custom-post-type.png)Once youâve selected the desired custom post type, scroll down to the Settings section and navigate to the **Exclude From Search** option.

![make sure you didn't exclude your post from search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-sure-you-didnt-exclude-your-post-from-search.png)Make sure that itâs set to **False**.

If not, then simply switch it from **True** to **False** to make the selected custom post type display in search on your site.

### How to Fix the Plugin Compatibility Issue

Have you tried all the guides above, but your custom post type is still not showing up in the search results? Another possible reason for that is a plugin conflict.

The easiest way to check if itâs the case is to deactivate each plugin in turn until you find the one thatâs causing the problem.

To get started, go over to the **Plugins** tab in the left panel of your WordPress dashboard.

![go to Plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Plugins.png)Then, tick the checkbox next to each installed plugin except SearchWP and the plugin you used to add a custom post type.

Once thatâs done, you can choose the **Deactivate** option from the **Bulk actions** drop-down menu and press **Apply**.

![deactivate all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deactivate-all-plugins-1.png)After deactivating the plugins, check if the issue with displaying custom post types in search disappeared.

If so, you can start activating the plugins gradually one by one. Itâll allow you to find out which one is causing the problem and avoid using it in the future.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix custom post type search not working in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix WordPress search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") and [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working").

If youâre ready to make custom post types searchable on your site and ensure a better search experience for your visitors, then you can [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").

### [How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page (5 Ways)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/)

**Published:** May 20, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Imagine you having full control of how search results will behave and appear on your WordPress site.

You can show certain articles at the top for specific search terms, display live search, and much more.

Sadly, the default WordPress search doesnât allow you to do so. But there is a way to gain complete control over your search results page without writing a single line of code.

In this article, weâll show you how to easily customize your WordPress search results page in just a few clicks.

### Why Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page

Customizing your siteâs search results page allows you to improve the user experience of your visitors and show them more relevant search results.

Itâs crucial if you want to drive traffic from search engines and ensure the success of your site in the long run.

There are a few ways how you can customize your WordPress search results page:

- **Show Search Results in Live Mode**: WordPress shows search results on a new separate page, which isnât convenient for most visitors. You can display them in live mode so that your visitors can instantly find what theyâre looking for.
- **Exclude Pages from Search Results**: Are there some pages you donât want to appear in search results? You can exclude them to avoid cluttering search results and make it easier to find the right matches.
- **Prioritize Pages in Search Results**: Do you want to promote specific pages in search results? You can give them a higher priority to make finding them easier for your audience.
- **Include Shortcode Results in WordPress Search**: WordPress search ignores shortcodes, making it impossible to find the content they display. You can make shortcodes searchable to solve this issue and enrich your search results.
- **Enable Fuzzy Search Feature**: Would you like to help your visitors find the results they want, even if they make a typo? Enable fuzzy search to make WordPress find partial matches.

Now, letâs take a look at how you can do all of the above even without being an experienced WordPress user.

### How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page

The easiest way to customize your WordPress search results page is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is a flexible and easy-to-use plugin designed to improve WordPress search on your website.

After the installation, youâll get full control over how search works on your site and can easily customize it.

Specifically, you can exclude and prioritize pages from search results, display matches in live mode, and many more.

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners worldwide already use SearchWP to provide more relevant search results to their audience.

Here are other things you can do with SearchWP:

- **Make custom data types searchable**: The native WordPress search indexes only the title and content of posts and pages. With SearchWP, you can make searchable custom fields, taxonomies, database tables, and many more.
- [**Track s**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/)**[e](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site")**[**arches**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/): SearchWP automatically gathers all data about your site visitorsâ search activity. You can use this information to better understand the needs of your audience and cover them.
- **Use multiple search engines**: With SearchWP, you can simultaneously use a few search engines with different settings. It allows you to create search forms limited to specific categories, showing your visitors more relevant results.
- [**Add search by PDFs and documents**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"): Using SearchWP, you can make WordPress search by the content within PDF files and office documents.

Now, letâs take a look at how to install SearchWP and use it to customize your search results page.

The first step is to [grab your copy of the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP") by visiting the website and creating a new account.

Once done, simply go to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab. Then, press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)You can also copy the license key, as youâll need it soon to activate your copy of the plugin.

When the download is finished, the next step is to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your WordPress site.

Need a refresher on how to do that? Kindly follow the instructions described in [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") step-by-step tutorial.

Once you install and activate SearchWP, youâll see the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Thatâs it. Youâve configured the plugin and are now ready to start customizing your search results page using SearchWP.

### 1. Show Search Results in Live Mode

If you want to make your WordPress site show search results in live mode, then the first step is to install the [SearchWP Live Ajax Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/ "SearchWP Live Ajax Search") plugin.

Anyone can add live search to their WordPress website using the Live Ajax Search plugin with only a few clicks, no coding knowledge is required.

After installing this plugin, all the existing search forms on your site will automatically start displaying search results in live mode.

You donât need to perform any extra steps. Every search box on your site now shows live search results.

Here is how it looks on our test WordPress site:

![category choose search field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/category-choose-search-field.gif)To learn more, please see our detailed guide on [how to add live search to WordPress for free](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free").

### 2. Exclude Specific Pages from Search Results

If you want to exclude specific pages from your site search results, then you can use the [SearchWP Exclude UI](https://searchwp.com/features/exclude-ui/ "Exclude UI Extension") extension.

This extension adds an exclude checkbox to the post and page editor, allowing you to hide them from search results with a single click.

You can simply install the extension from the WordPress dashboard by heading to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** and navigate to Exclude UI.

![Install Exclude UI extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-exclude-ui-extension-1030x560.jpg)After you install and activate the extension, the next step is to go to your WordPress dashboard and navigate to the post or page you want to hide from the search results.

Once youâre in the WordPress content editor (block editor), simply check the **Exclude from search** checkbox in the right panel to hide the post or page.

![check the exclude from search checkbox](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-the-exclude-from-search-checkbox.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully excluded a post from your WordPress site search results.

For more details, see our step-by-step guide on [how to exclude pages from site search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress").

### 3. Prioritize Specific Pages in Search Results

You might also want to prioritize specific search results to improve their visibility or boost conversions.

For instance, if you operate an online store, then you can increase sales by making your best-selling product appear first in the search results.

Unfortunately, the default WordPress search doesnât allow you to control the order of search results.

However, you can easily control the order of search results by using the [Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/features/custom-results-order/ "Custom Results Order Extension") extension in SearchWP.

This extension allows you to choose which posts or pages you want to show at the top of search results for a specific search term.

![Configure Custom Results order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)For more details, see our guide on [how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress").

### 4. Include Shortcode Results in WordPress Search

Would you like to enrich search results with shortcodes content? Itâll let your visitors search for galleries, tables, and anything else displayed by shortcodes.

Here is an example of a gallery that you added using shortcodes, and you want to make it searchable:

![search by galleries](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-by-galleries.png)With SearchWP, you can make WordPress include shortcodes with just a few clicks.

Simply enable the **Parse Shortcodes** options in SearchWP settings, and youâre all set.

![Enable parse shortcode](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-shortcode-searchable-932x1030.jpg)To learn more, please see our tutorial on [how to include shortcode results in WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search").

### 5. Enable Fuzzy Search Feature

Would you like to add a fuzzy search feature to your site? When enabled in SearchWP, it makes WordPress find partial matches when search terms yield no results.

This feature is super helpful and allows your visitors to find what theyâre looking for, even if theyâve made a typo.

For example, if they misspell WordPress as âWordtPress,â then theyâll still find posts on the right topic.

![find the right results even if you make a typo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/find-the-right-results-even-if-you-make-a-typo.png)Using SearchWP, you can enable fuzzy search just by ticking a checkbox, no coding needed.

For more details, follow the [how to add fuzzy search to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites") tutorial.

Thatâs it! By customizing your search results page, you can ensure a better user experience and boost engagement.

We hope this article helped you learn how to customize your WordPress search results page. You may also want to see our guides on [how to add a search bar in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress ") and [how to fix WordPress search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress ").

If youâre ready to gain full control over how search works on your WordPress site, then you can [get started with SearchWP today.](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today")

### [How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** February 25, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Having a search bar on your WordPress website is a helpful feature that allows visitors to find the content theyâre looking for in a few seconds.

Unfortunately, not every WordPress theme has a search bar by default. And it might be challenging to figure out how to add it if youâre a beginner.

But there are simple ways of showing a search bar on your site. You can simply enable an option in your siteâs theme settings, use a widget, or add it using the theme editor.

In this article, weâll show you how to add a search bar to your WordPress website without any code.

### Why Add a Search Bar to Your Website?

A search bar not only allows your website visitors to find the content faster. It also brings plenty of hidden advantages that significantly boost your websiteâs performance.

Here are just a few examples of why you need a search bar on your website:

- **Boost Conversion Rate:** The easier it is for people to find the product they want on your site, the higher the chances are that theyâll buy from you.
- **Increase Content Discoverability:** Creating quality content takes hard work and a lot of time. The search bar allows you to maximize your efforts by helping visitors discover your content with ease.
- **Improve User Experience:** If visitors had a great time exploring your website, it makes them return again and again. A loyal audience is valuable, as they want to consume as much of your content as possible.
- **Skyrocket SEO:** Rest assured, all of the above wonât escape the attention of Google. By increasing engagement on your site, you encourage users to spend more time on your site. This sends a positive signal to Google and helps boost your keyword rankings.

Now that we know the benefits of having a search bar, letâs see how to change the default WordPress search and different ways to add a search bar on your website. You can click the links below to jump ahead to any section:

- [Change the Default WordPress Search Settings](#aioseo-change-the-default-wordpress-search-settings)
- [Method 1: Add a Search Bar as a Widget](#aioseo-method-1-add-a-search-bar-as-a-widget)
- [Method 2: Add a Search Bar Using WordPress Theme Customizer](#aioseo-method-2-add-a-search-bar-using-wordpress-theme-customizer)
- [Method 3: Add a Search Bar Using Full Site Editor](#aioseo-method-3-add-a-search-bar-using-full-site-editor)
- [Bonus: Add a Modal Search Form to WordPress Menu](#aioseo-bonus-add-a-modal-search-form-to-wordpress-menu)

### Change the Default WordPress Search Settings

The native WordPress search is limited and not great for sites that have a lot of content or products.

For instance, it doesnât include important information like tags, categories, custom fields, product details, documentation like PDF files, and more while searching for content. This leads to poor user experience, as your visitors might not be able to find what they are looking for.

Besides that, you cannot exclude or prioritize specific pages, as well as customize the order of search results in general. And you also donât get any search statistics, which can be a great source for generating new content ideas and understanding your audience.

The best way to improve the search experience in WordPress is by replacing it with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and helps you create custom search engines, include custom fields, taxonomies, and other essential details in the search process.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Plus, you can also create custom search forms using the pre-made layouts in SearchWP. There are different options for customizations, so you can easily edit the templates to match your brand. The plugin also lets you [make a search bar that searches your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/ "How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site").

For more details, please see our guide on [how to change the default WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

Next, letâs look at different methods for adding a search bar in WordPress.

### Method 1: Add a Search Bar as a Widget

One of the most popular way of adding the search bar is to add it as a widget.

To do that, you only need to drag and drop the appropriate widget to the area where you want it to appear. However, youâll need to ensure that your WordPress theme supports a widget area.

The very first step is to open your WordPress dashboard and navigate to the **Widgets** tab under **Appearance**.

![Go to widgets tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-widgets-tab.jpg)Next, you need to decide where youâd like to show the search bar in the widget area.

Simply click the **Plus** button at the top left and find the âSearchâ widget.

![Find the search widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/find-the-search-widget.jpg)Then drag it to the area you want it to appear on your website.

Here is how it should look like:

![Drag and drop the search widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/drag-and-drop-the-search-widget.jpg)Donât forget to press the **Update** button at the top right.

This will save all your changes, and your search bar will be live on your website.

![press the update button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-the-update-button.jpg)Now we need to make sure that the search bar actually starts displaying in the footer widget area where we added it.

As you can see, the search bar works perfectly on our demo site. To provide a more rich experience, weâre also using the [Live Ajax Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-ajax-search-to-your-wordpress-without-a-plugin/ "How to Add Live Ajax Search to Your WordPress Without a Plugin") feature in SearchWP, so users can quickly get relevant search results.

![The search bar works](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-search-bar-works.jpg)### Method 2: Add a Search Bar Using WordPress Theme Customizer

Another way of adding a search bar in WordPress is by enabling it in your siteâs theme.

Some WordPress themes come with a built-in search bar, but itâs not active by default. You will need to open the Theme Customizer and head to the navigation settings to enable it.

**Note:** The steps may vary depending on the WordPress theme youâre using. For this tutorial, we used the GeneratePress theme.

To get started, you can head to **Appearance Â» Customize** from the WordPress dashboard.

![Go to customize tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-customize-tab.jpg)On the next screen, you should see the WordPress Theme Customizer.

From here, youâll need to go to **Layout Â» Primary Navigation** from the menu on the left.

![Go to primary navigation](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-primary-navigation-1.jpg)Next, you will see different navigation options for your theme.

Go ahead and click the dropdown menu under Navigation Search and select the **Enable** option.

![Enable the navigation search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-the-navigation-search.jpg)When youâre done, donât forget to press the **Publish** button at the top once done.

After that, letâs go ahead and visit our website and check if everything works fine. There should be a magnifying glass icon in the top right corner.

![Magnifying glass icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/magnifying-glass-icon.jpg)### Method 3: Add a Search Bar Using Full Site Editor

If youâre using a block based [WordPress theme](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)"), then you can add a search bar using the Full Site Editor (FSE).

To start, youâll need to head to **Appearance Â» Themes** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âCustomizeâ button.

![WordPress theme customizer](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wordpress-theme-customizer-1030x523.png)On the next screen, you will see the Full Site Editor.

From here, head to **Navigation** from the menu on the left.

![Head to navigation menu](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/head-to-navigation-menu-1030x476.png)After that, youâll need to click the 3-dots icon to open more options.

Then you can click the âEditâ button.

![Edit the navigation menu](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-the-navigation-menu-1030x585.png)After that, you can click the â+â button to add a new block.

Simply navigate to the âSearchâ block and add it to your navigation.

![Add search widget to FSE](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-widget-to-FSE-1030x510.png)When youâre done, simply save your changes.

After that, you can visit your website to see the search bar in action.

![View search in FSE](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-in-FSE-1030x565.png)### Bonus: Add a Modal Search Form to WordPress Menu

Is the search bar taking up a lot of space on your site, but you still want to offer a search functionality to visitors?

The perfect solution is to add a modal search form. It transforms the search experience and declutters your site. When a user clicks on the search option or an icon on your website, a small popup box opens with the search form.

The easiest way to add a modal search form to the WordPress navigation menu is by using the [SearchWP Modal Search Form](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-modal-search-form/ "SearchWP Modal Search Form") plugin. It lets you can add a lightweight modal search form next to your menu items in just a few clicks.

![SearchWP modal form preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-modal-form-preview-2.gif)A modal search form in the WordPress menu also provides a modern look. It provides a distraction free search experience and fits seamlessly into your siteâs theme.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to add a modal search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-modal-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Modal Search Form in WordPress (No Coding)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a search bar in WordPress using different ways. You may also want to see our guide on [how to customize the search widget in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-wordpress-search-widget/ "How to Customize the Search Widget in WordPress (Easy Way)") and [how to fix WordPress search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How To Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Issues Solved)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!") today!

### [7 Best WooCommerce Search Plugins (Free + Paid)](https://searchwp.com/best-woocommerce-search-plugins/)

**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Landing on a website and not being able to find what youâre looking for is one of the quickest ways to hit the back button. For an online store, thatâs not just frustrating for the customer, itâs lost revenue for you.

While WooCommerce provides a basic search feature out of the box, itâs often limited. It might only search product titles, ignore important product details, and lack the speed and intelligence modern shoppers expect.

The result? Customers abandon their search, bounce from your site, and take their wallets elsewhere.

To help you out, we tested multiple search plugins for WooCommerce. The result showed that SearchWP, Advanced Woo Search, and YITH WooCommerce Ajax Search were among the top plugins.

In this post, weâll share the best WooCommerce search plugins and break down their strengths and weaknesses to help you find the perfect solution for your online store.

If youâre in a hurry, then hereâs a quick look at some of the best WooCommerce search plugins you can get your hands on.

**\#****Search Plugins****Recommended For****Pricing**1.[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")Precise control, detailed analytics, full-site searchPaid2.[Advanced Woo Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/advanced-woo-search/ "Advanced Woo Search")Beginners, budget-friendly, instant improvementFree3.[YITH WooCommerce Ajax Search](https://searchwp.com/recommends/yith-woocommerce-ajax-search/ "YITH WooCommerce Ajax Search")Visually appealing live search, YITH usersPaid4.[FiboSearch](https://wordpress.org/plugins/ajax-search-for-woocommerce/ "FiboSearch")Fast, smart product search with live suggestionsFree5.[Ivory Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/add-search-to-menu/ "Ivory Search")Multiple custom search forms, basic needsFree6.[WooCommerce Product Search](https://searchwp.com/recommends/woocommerce-product-search/ "WooCommerce Product Search")Official WooCommerce tool, built-in analyticsPaid7.[SearchWP Live Ajax Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/ "SearchWP Live Ajax Search")SearchWP users needing live searchFree### How We Test and Review WooCommerce Search Plugins

Investing in a better search for your WooCommerce store helps boost conversions and sales. It also drastically improves the user experience on your eCommerce store.

Customers can quickly and easily find exactly what they need, leading to a more positive shopping journey. A search plugin helps find relevant search results, which means customers are more likely to find products they want to buy, turning searches into sales.

That said, choosing the best WooCommerce search plugin can be tricky. To give the best recommendations, we tested and reviewed multiple plugins based on:

- **Speed & Performance:** We assessed how quickly search results appeared, particularly testing the responsiveness of AJAX live search as users type. A fast search keeps users engaged.
- **Relevance Accuracy:** We tested how well each plugin returned the most relevant products for various types of queries, including exact terms, partial words, and related topics.
- **Searchable Content:** We checked how deeply the plugin searches. Does it only look at titles, or can it also find results based on descriptions, short descriptions, SKUs, categories, tags, product attributes (like color or size), or custom fields?
- **Ease of Use & Configuration:** When testing, we also checked how straightforward it is to install the plugin and configure its settings to get optimal results.
- **Customization Options:** What control do you have over the appearance of the search forms, do you get prebuilt templates, and can you customize the search results page?
- **Additional Features:** We noted any extra capabilities, such as search analytics, control over search results order, or support for searching non-product content (like blog posts).

Now, letâs look at some of the best search plugins for WooCommerce you can use today.

### [1. SearchWP ](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)If you need complete control over exactly how your search works on your WooCommerce store, then [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the best solution out there.

Itâs not just product search, itâs a full-site search solution for WordPress that integrates deeply with WooCommerce, allowing you to create advanced search rules to find specific products.

Unlike many plugins that offer limited controls, SearchWP lets you create multiple search engines, each with different rules and priorities.

During testing, we also found that you can filter or exclude certain content from the searching process. For example, you can limit searches to a particular product category.

![Exclude products rules](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-products-rules-1030x508.png)It also offers powerful search analytics, providing data on what customers are searching for, giving you actionable insights to improve both your search relevance and your product catalog.

Besides that, SearchWP also lets you make PDFs, documents, comments, custom fields, and more searchable in WordPress. It also easily integrates with other eCommerce platforms like Easy Digital Downloads and BigCommerce.

- Offers a WooCommerce extension for smooth integration

### [3. YITH WooCommerce Ajax Search](https://searchwp.com/recommends/yith-woocommerce-ajax-search/ "YITH WooCommerce Ajax Search")

### [How Does WordPress Search Work? (Beginners Guide)](https://searchwp.com/how-does-wordpress-search-work/)

**Published:** May 8, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When someone lands on your WordPress website, theyâre usually looking for something specific. Maybe itâs a particular product, a piece of information, or contact details.

But ever wonder what goes on behind the scene to help users find content theyâre looking for?

WordPress comes with its own built-in search feature right out of the box. But how does this standard WordPress search actually work? And is it doing the best job it could be for your visitors?

In this article, weâll break down exactly how does WordPress search work, point out its main limitations, and show you how you can easily make it much better for your website.

### How Does the Default WordPress Search Work?

WordPress comes with a built-in search system that people can use to find content or products on your site.

Users can use this feature by typing something in the [search bar](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") on your WordPress site. The location of the search bar depends on your website theme, as it could be in the sidebar or navigation menu.

That said, what happens behind the scenes when they hit the Enter button?

![View search in FSE](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-in-FSE-1030x565.png)At its core, the default WordPress search is quite straightforward. It works by looking through your websiteâs database, which is where all your websiteâs content (like your pages, posts, and settings) is stored.

Specifically, the WordPress search usually looks in just a few key places for the words or phrases someone types in:

- **The Title of Your Pages and Posts:** This is the main heading of your articles or pages.
- **The Main Content of Your Pages and Posts:** This is the body text you write.
- **The Excerpt:** A short summary you might write for blog posts.

Itâs like quickly scanning through a filing cabinet, but only looking at the label on the file folder and content inside. If the[ keyword](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site") is found in any of these spots, WordPress considers that piece of content a potential match and includes it in the search results.

The search results are based on the published date (newest first) by default, not by how relevant the content is to the query.

For instance, suppose youâve recently published a blog post called âWhy I Love Gardeningâ, and you also have an older post titled âGardening Tips for Beginners.â

If someone searches for the term âGardening,â the older post is likely to appear first in search results because the keyword is part of the title.

That said, letâs look at what the default WordPress search doesnât do well and the restrictions it offers.

### Limitations of Default WordPress Search

While the basic WordPress search is a good starting point for small websites, it has significant limitations.

Especially, if your website has thousands of blog posts, products, videos, and other types of content.

Hereâs where it often falls short:

- **Itâs Not Very Smart About Relevance:** The default search doesnât really understand which results are most important. If a word appears in the title of one page and just once deep inside the content of another, WordPress might actually show the less relevant one first. Itâs not good at figuring out the intent behind the search.
- **It Only Looks in Limited Places:** The default search completely ignores essential details that might be stored somewhere else. For instance, if you use custom fields to store specific information (like product numbers, ingredients, or listing details), the search wonât see it. Similarly, the default search completely ignores the actual text within [PDF guides](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)"), Word documents, or PowerPoint presentation files.
- **Can Get Slow on Larger Sites:** As you add more and more content, asking the default search to scan through everything each time someone searches can become slow. This means visitors wait longer and get irrelevant results.
- **You Have Little Control:** Want results with the keyword in the title to appear before results where itâs just in the content? The default search gives you almost no way to control the [order or importance of results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)").
- **Ignores Categories and Tags:** These are great ways to organize content, but the default search skips over tags and categories when searching for posts, pages, and products.
- **Skips Comments in the Search Process:** If valuable discussion or information is in your comments section, then WordPress search wonât find it.
- **Specific Data Types:** Running an online store with WooCommerce? Default search might miss product attributes, [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), or even product descriptions.

These shortcomings of the default search directly affect your websiteâs visitors and, ultimately, your business.

If someone canât quickly find the product, service description, or blog post theyâre looking for, then theyâre likely to get annoyed and go to a competitorâs site.

Imagine a customer searching for a specific product feature thatâs only mentioned in a custom field or a product PDF. If the search doesnât find it, then you could lose a sale.

So, whatâs the solution? Do you need to hire a developer or get super technical? Letâs find out.

### How to Improve Search on Your WordPress Site

The easiest way to remove the limitations of the default WordPress search and improve it is by using a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that offers powerful features and help provide a better search experience on your site.

The best part is that you donât need any technical skills, edit code, or hire a developer to get SearchWP to work. Itâs beginner-friendly to use and makes site search smart, fast, and genuinely helpful for your visitors.

SearchWP tackles all the limitations of the default WordPress search and adds a lot more capability.

For instance, it offers advanced indexing. Instead of just scanning the database on the fly for each search, SearchWP builds its own detailed index of your siteâs content. This index is like a comprehensive catalog that knows exactly where every word appears across your site, making searches much faster and more accurate.

Plus, you get more control over the search. SearchWP lets you decide how results should be ranked. You can easily give more importance to keywords found in titles compared to those in the content, or prioritize content from a specific category or [custom field](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"). You decide whatâs most relevant for your site.

Besides that, SearchWP goes way beyond titles and content. It can search all your posts, pages, products, and custom fields.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)With SearchWP, you can also include taxonomies (categories, tags, and [product attributes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress")) in the search process. This helps users find more accurate search results.

Another advantage of using the plugin is that it makes different documents searchable in WordPress. For instance, if you uploaded files like PDFs, Docs, XLSX, PPTX, and more, then SearchWP will search contents within these documents to find the best result.

![Showing only PDF files in the search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/showing-pdf-only-results.jpg)You can also set up rules for your search engine and filter search results. For example, you can exclude posts from a specific category, hide content thatâs published before a certain date, only show a specific type of content (PDFs, videos, spreadsheets), and more.

With SearchWP, you can [set up synonym rules](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-synonym-rules-for-wordpress-search/ "How to Set Up Synonym Rules for WordPress Search") and tell that âsofaâ and âcouchâ are the same thing. Simply add lists of synonyms, and SearchWP will ensure searches for one term find content using the other.

![Enter search terms and synonyms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-search-terms-and-synonyms-1030x490.png)SearchWP doesnât just improve the search, it also tells you what people are searching for. This valuable information shows you what content visitors are seeking (and potentially not finding), helping you identify gaps in your content or ways to improve your search configuration.

To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to see search metrics in WordPress.](https://searchwp.com/how-to-see-search-metrics-in-wordpress/ "How To See Search Metrics In WordPress (Easily)")

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)SearchWP also offers powerful extensions and integrations. It works seamlessly with eCommerce platforms like WooCommerce and Easy Digital Downloads, custom field plugins like [Advanced Custom Fields (ACF)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)"), and more.

That said, if youâre looking for more tips to enhance the search experience on your site, then check out our guide on [11 ways to improve WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/ways-to-improve-wordpress-search/ "11 Ways to Improve WordPress Search (Expert Tips)").

We hope this article helped you learn how does WordPress search work and how to improve it. You may also want to see our guide on [how to customize your WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search") and [how to use boolean search to exclude content from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-boolean-search-to-exclude-content-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Use Boolean Search to Exclude Content from WordPress Search").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!") today!

### [How to Add a WooCommerce Search Form to Your Shop Page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/)

**Published:** May 13, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

You might have seen search forms on many eCommerce sites, but they donât always provide relevant results.

Thatâs because the default search function in WordPress is quite limited and ignores important information while searching.

Adding a powerful search form to your shop page will help your site visitors navigate your store faster and get the results theyâre looking for. You can easily get more sales and boost customer satisfaction this way.

In this article, weâll show you how to add a search form to your WooCommerce shop page.

### Why Add a Search Form to Your Shop Page?

Site search is a common feature online shoppers use to navigate eCommerce websites. At least 30% of your store visitors are using the search box if your site already offers one. Adding a search form to your shop page will help them find your products even faster.

These days, site search is an important part of every successful eCommerce strategy. Itâs a major source of revenue, as shoppers who use search are more likely to buy from you than those who donât.

Site search is often more difficult in larger stores. Site visitors tend to spend more time looking for the products they need. You can make things easier by adding search to specific locations of your store. This will help you increase your conversion rates.

You can [add a search form](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)") to different locations of your site like the sidebar and the header. But adding one to your shop page (especially at the top) makes it easier to find.

![Search form at the top of WooCommerce shop page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Search-form-at-the-top-of-WooCommerce-shop-page.png)On most mobile devices, your users will need to scroll for a while to find the sidebar. A search form in the header or menu can also go unnoticed in some cases. If you want to make your search form more accessible, you should consider adding it to your shop page.

Adding a search form to your shop page isnât enough â you also have to make sure your search form is fast and functional.

WooCommerce offers a search feature by default. But if you have a medium or large site, youâll need a better solution. The default [WooCommerce search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working") is slow and doesnât always give relevant results. This means most of your site users wonât be able to find what theyâre looking for.

Want to add a powerful and fast search form to your WooCommerce shop page, even if youâre not tech-savvy?

Below, weâll show you how to easily do this without writing a single line of code.

### How to Add a Search Form to Your WooCommerce Shop Page

Do you want to add a fully featured WooCommerce search form to your shop page?

You can use a WordPress plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). This method lets you offer your users a lot of functionalities without the need to write any code.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)It is a leading [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"). It has all the search features your WooCommerce site users need. Unlike the default WooCommerce search, they can use it to find any product or post.

SearchWP indexes all your product attributes like variations, categories, and tags. This means your users can now find a product by simply typing any of its attributes in the search bar.

Other awesome features you get include:

- **Exclude attributes in results.** If you choose, you can exclude certain product attributes so SearchWP doesnât show results for them.
- **Search insights and statistics.** With SearchWP, you can now log searches. This way, youâll learn what your users are searching for. You can then use the statistics gathered to serve them better.
- **Search advanced custom fields.** [Custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") are good for storing more information about your site content. Your search engine will no longer ignore them when visitors use the search feature.

Once you install and activate the plugin, it automatically integrates with the native WooCommerce search. So, you can easily add a search bar to any location on your site with the block editor.

Weâll show you how to do this now.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

The first step to adding a search form to your WooCommerce shop page is to [grab a copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") by visiting the website and creating a new account.

Next, you can go to your SearchWP account and navigate to the **Downloads** tab. Once there, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the plugin ZIP file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)After that, you need to log into your WordPress admin dashboard to upload and set up the plugin.

If you need some help doing this, then check out this detailed guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, the welcome screen will appear along with the setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Congrats! Your SearchWP plugin is now ready for use.

Now, letâs look at how to use it to power your WooCommerce search.

### Step 2: Install and Activate the SearchWP WooCommerce Extension

The SearchWP WooCommerce extension helps customers find products on your store.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard. Then head to WooCommerce Integration and click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)The next thing you need to do is set up your first SearchWP search engine.

### Step 3: Set Up a New SearchWP Search Engine

SearchWP gives you a lot of control over how your users look for content on your site. You can select from a list of sources and adjust the relevance of each attribute.

To make sure your store visitors can [find products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search") on your shop page, you need to add products as a source in the search engine.

To do this, you will need to go to the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard.

Here, you get the option to edit the default search engine or create a new one. For this tutorial, we will edit the default WordPress engine.

Go ahead and click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see different sources that the default engine will use.

Make sure to tick the **Products** box from the list of sources available.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

After selecting products as a source, scroll down to the newly added Products tab and click **Add/Remove Attributes**.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)Here, you can choose to index any product attribute like the title, content, slug, excerpt, and [author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (5 Steps)").

You can further improve your WooCommerce search with custom fields and taxonomies. This will let your users find products with attributes like colors, tags, variations, categories, and shipping classes.

![How to Add WooCommerce Product Search Widget: customize attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-product-attribute-1030x567.jpg)Once youâre done selecting product attributes, click **Done**.

Next, you can scroll to the top and click **Save**. SearchWP will now apply the new settings.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Next, letâs add the search form to your shop page.

### Step 4: Add a Search Form to Your Shop Page

Using the block editor, you can add a search form to any page in your WordPress site. Weâll add one to your shop page now.

First you need to find your shop page by going to **Pages Â» All Pages**.

![Go to all pages in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Go-to-all-pages-in-WordPress.png)Next, you can navigate to the **Shop** page.

From here, hover over it with your mouse and click **Edit**.

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Edit-WordPress-shop-page-e1630919434789.png)Once you are in the WordPress content editor, you can add a new Search block to show a [WooCommerce search form](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)").

Simply click the **+** icon at the top and select the **Product Search** block.

![Select product search block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-search-block-1030x591.png)Next, youâll see a preview of the search form in the content editor.

From here, you can simply update or publish your product page.

![See product search preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/see-product-search-preview-1030x422.png)Youâve successfully added a WooCommerce search form to the store page.

Now, when your users visit your shop page, they can easily search for the products they want.

![WooCommerce product search form on shop page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WooCommerce-product-search-form-on-shop-page.png)We hope this article helped you learn how to add a search form to your WooCommerce search page. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)") and [how to customize the WooCommerce search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-woocommerce-search-results-page/ "How To Customize The WooCommerce Search Results Page").

If youâre ready to add a search form to your shop page, then [start by getting your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get Started with SearchWP today!").

### [7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/)

**Published:** January 22, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Having a good search on your site is important if you want to ensure a positive user experience for your visitors. Itâs also a great way to increase sales and grow your business, as itâs easier to find products with a powerful search.

However, the default WordPress search has limitations and doesnât include a lot of essential content on your site. For instance, it wonât include custom fields, tags, categories, product details, and documents when looking for content.

Fortunately, there are many WordPress search plugins, and SearchWP is one of the best options in the market. Our focus is on creating a better and more personalized search experience.

While SearchWP is powerful and full of features, we also tested several other options to help you find the right solution.

In this article, weâll show you the best WordPress search plugins that you can use to deliver more relevant search results to your visitors.

### Why Use a WordPress Search Plugin?

WordPress is a great and most popular blogging platform. But, its search functionality leaves much to be desired.

Namely, the native WordPress search engine doesnât search:

- **Custom fields and taxonomies**. Are you an online store owner? Most [WordPress eCommerce plugins](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads") store product attributes in custom fields and taxonomies. As WordPress doesnât index data inside them, customers wonât be able to find a product by its attributes.
- **Text within office documents and PDFs**. Do you upload any useful documents or [PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") to your site? By default, your visitors canât search for content within them, making it much harder to find a document theyâre looking for.
- **Custom database tables**. Do you use any popular WordPress plugins? Most of them store tons of valuable data within custom database tables. All of this data isnât searchable by default, making it challenging to find the right content in some cases.
- **Shortcodes**. Are you an active shortcodes user? They actually contain a lot of search-worthy information. For example, by making them searchable, you can let your visitors find specific galleries and tables.

In addition, the default WordPress search has some unpleasant features that limit your capabilities.

For instance, it doesnât allow you to impact search results in any way. You cannot exclude or prioritize specific pages, as well as customize the order of search results in general.

Similarly, it doesnât collect any search statistics. Your site visitorsâ search activity data is a great source of new content ideas. Plus, it displays search results by opening a new separate page, which is not convenient as most users expect to see live results on a site when they search.

Thankfully, there are lots of WordPress search plugins that can help you tackle these problems.

### How We Test and Review WordPress Search Plugins

Our goal is to give you a clear understanding of each pluginâs strengths and weaknesses so you can make an informed decision for your own site. Thatâs why we personally test each WordPress search plugin on real websites to see how they perform under different conditions.

Hereâs a peek into our testing and review process:

- **Feature Exploration:** We dive deep into each pluginâs features and examine its search capabilities, customization options, and any extra functionalities. For exmaple, we test features like [live search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free"), keyword stemming, and the ability to include/exclude specific content types.
- **Indexing Capabilities:** When testing search plugins, we check how effectively and efficiently each plugin indexes content. We look at how it handles different types of content including posts, pages, custom post types, [comments](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-comments-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress"), and even media files.
- **Search Accuracy and Relevance:** This is the core of our testing as we perform various searches on our test site using different keywords and phrases. We then analyze the relevance of the results, how well the plugin prioritizes content, and whether it delivers a satisfying search experience for the user.
- **Customization Options:** We also explore the pluginâs customization options and look for the ability to fine-tune search results, adjust styling to match our theme, and configure advanced settings to optimize search performance.
- **Pricing:** Finally, we consider the pluginâs pricing in relation to its features and performance. We determine whether the plugin offers good value for its price and compare it with other search plugins.

That said, letâs take a look at the best WordPress search plugins you choose for your website.

### [1. SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is a truly comprehensive solution that deservedly takes first place among WordPress search plugins.

We recommend SearchWP because it is the most powerful and comprehensive plugin thatâs capable of covering all your needs. You can add a lightening fast search to your site that improves the overall search experience.

One of the best things about using the plugin is that you can create custom search engines for your website. You get complete control over which sources to include, like pages, posts, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), tags, comments, and more.

![Choose search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-sources--1030x441.png)Besides that, another feature we really think is useful for users is [custom search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

You get pre-built templates for search forms, which includes a basic layout or more complex designs. The best part, these layouts can be embedded easily on your site.

![Choosing a search form template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-1-create-search-form-1030x614.jpg)SearchWP also has tons of useful [extensions](https://searchwp.com/features/ "SearchWP Extensions"). You can install them to display search results in live mode, add a search bar with a shortcode, prioritize or exclude search results, and many more.

If youâre ready to provide your site visitors with the best possible search experience, you can [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").

**Features:**

- **Make literally anything on your site searchable** â WordPress doesnât display your content in search results? With SearchWP, you can make searchable custom post types, taxonomies, documents, PDF files, and many more.
- **Add a custom search bar** â SearchWP supports the simultaneous use of multiple search engines with a different set of rules. It allows you to create custom search fields linked to certain types of content.
- **On-site search activity tracking** â SearchWP gathers the on-site [search activity of your visitors](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") and provides you with it right in the WordPress dashboard. Itâs an excellent opportunity to better understand your audience and give them the content or products they want.
- **Seamless integration with WooCommerce** â WordPress doesnât show WooCommerce products in search results by default. With SearchWP, you can make [WordPress search by product name](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)"), description, attributes, reviews, and more.
- **Everything works out of the box** â SearchWP automatically integrates into any existing search bar on your site after installation. Itâs easy to use and configure for your needs, even if youâve just launched your first WordPress site.

**Why We Recommend SearchWP:** When it comes to improving your siteâs search experience, nothing comes close to SearchWP. It offers powerful features and extensions that help you create custom search engines for your site. Plus, you can easily integrate with it other plugins like WooCommerce and WPForms to extend your siteâs functionality.

**Pricing:** Starting at $99 per year.

### [2. FiboSearch](https://wordpress.org/plugins/ajax-search-for-woocommerce/ "FiboSearch")

[![FiboSearch](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/FiboSearch.jpg)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/ajax-search-for-woocommerce/)[FiboSearch](https://wordpress.org/plugins/ajax-search-for-woocommerce/ "FiboSearch") is a must-have plugin for every WooCommerce store owner. We really liked that you can add to your site an advanced [AJAX search bar](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-ajax-search-to-woocommerce/ "How to Add Ajax Search to WooCommerce (Step by Step)") with live search suggestions to your eCommerce store.

Each suggestion contains a product image, price, and description, allowing customers to instantly determine if the product suits them or not.

That said, the main disadvantage we experienced while using the plugin is that itâs only useful if youâre the WooCommerce store owner.

So if itâs not the case, we recommend using a more versatile WordPress search plugin, like SearchWP, for all your WordPress search needs.

**Features:**

- **Ultra-fast search engine** â FiboSearch instantly shows search matches even if you have a whole bunch of products in your shop.
- **Advanced search results** â Just like SearchWP, this plugin also considers product attributes, tags, brands, and [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"). It helps to provide more accurate search results, thereby increasing sales.
- **The fuzzy search feature** â Fuzzy search ensures that customers can find a product even if they make a typo in its name. This feature is similar to the one SearchWP has.
- **Google Analytics integration** â With Google Analytics, you can find out what keywords your site is ranking for and what search terms users have entered on it. Itâs a great opportunity to discover the needs of your audience and provide them with new arrivals.

**Why We Recommend FiboSearch:** After testing the plugin, we recommend this plugin to all WooCommerce users. It works seamlessly with the [eCommerce platform](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads") and helps improve the overall search experience for your customers.

**Pricing:** The base plugin with limited features is free. The Pro version starts from $49 per year.

### [3. Ivory Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/add-search-to-menu/ "Ivory Search")

[![IvorySearch](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/IvorySearch.jpg)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/add-search-to-menu/)[Ivory Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/add-search-to-menu/ "Ivory Search") is the next search plugin for WordPress on our list. We tested the plugin and were pleased to see that it allows you to create an [unlimited number of search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)") and individually customize each of them.

This in turn opens up a lot of possibilities. For example, you can create a form limited to a specific category to provide your visitors with more accurate search results.

Or, if you have an online store, you might want to add separate search forms for regular pages and products to avoid cluttering search results.

That said, Ivory Search is a great plugin that has some useful features but thatâs barely half of what SearchWP has to offer.

If you want to have full control over your WordPress site search, need advanced features, and integrate with other tools, then itâs better to use SearchWP. You may want to see our comparison between [SearchWP vs Ivory Search](https://searchwp.com/ivory-search-vs-searchwp/ "Ivory Search vs SearchWP: Search Plugins Compared [2022]") for more details.

- **Exclude specific pages from search results** â As with SearchWP, you can use this plugin to exclude any pages from search results. It really comes in handy when you have a lot of technical pages that donât make sense to display in search.
- **Track searches of your visitors** â Just like SearchWP, the Ivory Search plugin allows you to track on-site search activity. Itâs a great opportunity to get to know your audience better and provide them with the content they want.
- **Multilingual search support** â Does your audience speak languages other than English? Due to multilingual search support, they can find content in any language.

**Why We Recommend Ivory Search:** If youâre looking for a simple search plugin that helps improve the overall user experience of your site, then we recommend using Ivory Search. It packs enough features to help you customize the search engine for WordPress.

**Pricing:** Ivory Searchâs base plugin with limited features is free to use. However, the Pro version starts at $19.99 per year.

### [4. SearchWP Ajax Live Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/ "SearchWP Ajax Live Search")

[![SearchWP live ajax search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-live-ajax-search-1-1030x336.png)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/)Would you like to display search results on your site in live mode?[ SearchWP Ajax Live Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/ "SearchWP Live Ajax Search") allows you to enable such a feature in a few clicks.

If you want to learn more about this plugin, feel free to check out the[ how to add live autocomplete search to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites") guide.
