### [How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/)

**Published:** April 15, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you have a WooCommerce store and have thousands of products, then youâd want your website search to work without any trouble,

But what if it doesnât?

Itâs an absolute nightmare for any eCommerce website owner. The main problem is that there are several possible reasons that can cause the product search to not work.

In this article, weâll talk about the most popular issues with search in WooCommerce and how to solve them.

Since this is a comprehensive article, here is a table of contents to help you navigate:

- [What Causes WooCommerce Product Search to Not Work?](#aioseo-3-common-examples-of-woocommerce-product-search-not-working)
- [Fix WooCommerce Doesn't Show Products in the Admin Panel](#admin)
- [Fix WordPress Doesnât Display WooCommerce Products](#product)
- [Fix WordPress Doesnât Search by WooCommerce Product Attributes](#attribute)

### What Causes WooCommerce Product Search to Not Work?

[WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/recommends/woocommmerce/ "WooCommerce") is one of the best and most powerful [eCommerce WordPress plugins](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "5 Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads"). But, as with any other plugin, you may encounter errors when using it from time to time.

Itâs especially frustrating when the errors are related to the product search. If the search on your website is broken, customers canât find what they came for!

Unfortunately, there are plenty of reasons that can cause troubles with WooCommerce search.

Letâs look at the most common issues that can cause the WooCommerce product search to malfunction.

#### 1. WooCommerce Product Search in Admin Isnât Working

There is a fairly widespread problem when WooCoomerce shows products in the search results on a site but doesnât display them in the admin panel.

Although this issue doesnât affect customers, it causes considerable inconvenience to the site owner.

Can you imagine how it feels to manually search for the product you want to customize out of a thousand others?

To solve this problem, you first need to figure out what caused it. Here is the list of the most likely reasons:

- **Plugin conflict**: Plugin incompatibility can sometimes lead to problems with [WooCommerce search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)").
- **Mass product import via CSV**: When you upload thousands of products to your site at once, it can corrupt the product database.
- **Memory limit**: If your hosting has strict memory limitations, it may cause problems with the WooCommerce search.

#### 2. WooCommerce Products Donât Display in Site Search Results

![nothing found](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/nothoing-found.jpg)If products donât show up in the search results on the site itself, then thatâs a much bigger concern.

Customers are very likely to leave your store as soon as they use a search on your site and figure that itâs broken. To prevent that, itâs important to quickly understand what caused the problem and fix it.

The main reason why WooCommerce products donât show up in on-site search results is because the default WordPress search is quite limited.

It will search for products, but might not show the right results to users. Plus, the default search doesnât take into account additional product information while searching, which can cause items to not show up in the search results.

One way to solve this problem is by using a WordPress search plugin like SearchWP. You can customize the default search or create a custom engine that only searches WooCommerce products.

If youâve already done that, but the problem still persists, then it may be caused by the following reasons:

- **Customized product visibility**: If a particular product doesnât show up in the search results, thereâs a good chance you changed its default visibility.
- **Cached search results page**: If youâve added products and havenât cleared the cache, then itâs possible that an outdated version of your site is being displayed.
- **Plugin compatibility issues**: As in the case of a broken search in the admin panel, products may not appear in on-site search results due to plugin compatibility issues.

#### 3. WordPress Doesnât Search by WooCommerce Product Attributes

The default WordPress search isnât that powerful when it comes to searching WooCommerce products. This also means that it wonât show any product attributes in the search results because WooCommerce stores them as custom fields.

To make them searchable, youâll first need to get WordPress to consider the custom fields they contain.

That said, letâs look at some simple ways you can fix the WooCommerce product search on your site.

### Fix WooCommerce Doesnât Show Products in the Admin Panel

Here are different ways to get WooCommerce product search to work in the admin panel in WordPress.

#### Plugin Compatibility Issues

Installed any new plugins shortly before WooCommerce stopped displaying products in search results?

Disabling every plugin apart from WooCommerce will help to figure out if itâs the reason.

To do so, navigate to the **Plugins** tab in your WordPress dashboard.

![go to the plugins tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-plugins-tab.jpg)Next, you can check the box at the top left to select all installed plugins at once.

This will help save time as you can perform bulk actions without having to deactivate each plugin individually.

![check out all the plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-out-all-the-plugins.jpg)After that, ensure you have unchecked the WooCommerce plugin.

From here, simply select **Deactivate** from the **Bulk actions** drop-down menu, and press **Apply**.

![deactivate all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deactivate-all-plugins.jpg)Thatâs it. You deactivated all the plugins youâve installed except for WooCommerce.

Did the problem with the search disappear after these actions? Then gradually activate the plugins one by one until you find which one is causing the problem.

If WooCommerce still doesnât display products in search results in the admin panel, then you can also try the following solutions below.

#### Mass Product Import via CSV

Have you recently imported a large number of products into WooCommerce? Then issues with search in the admin panel may be caused by database corruption.

![navigate to the WooCommerce tools](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-woocommerce-tools.jpg)To regenerate the database, navigate to **WooCommerce Â» Status Â» Tools** in your WordPress dashboard.

Once there, press the âRegenerateâ button in the Product lookup tables section.

![regenerate tables](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/regenerate-tables.jpg)After that, youâll need to scroll the page to the very bottom.

From here, simply press the **Update Database** button.

![press update database](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-update-database.jpg)Note that these operations may take some time to complete, depending on the number of products in your store.

If that doesnât solve the problem, then try the following way.

![navigate to the products tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-products.jpg)First, navigate to **Products Â» All Products** in your WordPress dashboard.

From here, tick the topmost checkbox to highlight all products.

![check all products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-all-products.jpg)Then, select the **Edit** option from the **Bulk actions** drop-down menu.

Once thatâs done, simply click **Apply**.

![press quick edit](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-quick-edit.jpg)This will open the quick edit options for your products.

Simply click **Update** to update all your products at once.

![click update](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-update.jpg)These actions make WooCommerce include all products and begin displaying them in the search results in the admin panel as itâs supposed to be.

#### Server Memory Limit

Just launched your first WordPress site? Or moved your site to new hosting?

That could be a possible reason for issues with the WooCommerce search, especially if you have a lot of products.

The thing is that the server that hosts your site may not have enough power to handle a large number of WooCommerce products at once.

Or, the server may have software restrictions that limit the amount of available memory.

The best possible solution is to reach out to your hosting providerâs customer support. Theyâll help you figure out if the issue with WooCommerce search is related to a server and solve it if itâs the case.

### Fix WordPress Doesnât Display WooCommerce Products

The best way to include WooCommerce products in your WordPress site search results is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Beyond making WooCommerce products searchable, this plugin has many other valuable features.

Over 50,000 site owners use this best WordPress search plugin because of its features, such as:

- **Automatic theme integration**. You donât need to be a tech-savvy user or edit any code to start using SearchWP. After installing, the plugin automatically integrates with all existing search forms on your site.
- [**Search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"). Do you wonder what products your visitors search for on your site? SearchWP allows you to start tracking searches and get valuable insights into your audienceâs interest.
- [**Search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites"). By default, WordPress shows search results by opening a new separate page. With SearchWP, you can display search results in live mode, providing your customers with a better user experience.

Now, letâs take a look at how to make WordPress display WooCommerce products in search results using SearchWP.

**Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP**

The first step is to [grab your copy of SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!").

Once done, simply go over to the **Downloads** tab. Then, you can press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin files to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Besides that, you should also copy your license key, as you will need it further to activate the plugin.

Once the download is completed, upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your WordPress site. If you need a help on how to do that, then feel free to check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step tutorial](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and the setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully activated SearchWP on your site.

**Step 2: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Integration Extension**

The next step is to install the WooCommerce Integration extension.

Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** and navigate to WooCommerce Integration. From here, click the **Install** button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)Once done, youâre ready to proceed to customize the default WordPress search engine.

**Step 3: Add WooCommerce Products as a Search Source**

To get started, you can go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. Here youâll find the list of search engines you currently have.

By customizing the default search engine, you can edit how the search works across the entire site. And if you want to add additional search engines and link them to specific search boxes, SearchWP allows you to do so, too.

The default search engine has only 3 sources: posts, pages, and media. It means that currently, WordPress does search exclusively among these 3.

To make WooCommerce products searchable, you need to add them as an engine source. To get started, first, click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see the default sources for the engine, which includes posts, pages, and media.

Simply check the box next to the **Products** source, and then click **Done** at the bottom right.

![Select products as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-products-as-sources-1030x497.png)After youâve finished, press the **Save** button at the top right to save the changes.

Thatâs it. Now WooCommerce products appear in search results on your website.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)**Step 4: Test Your New Search**

Letâs make sure that you can now find products through search.

To do so, simply visit your site and enter the name of any product you have in your store into the search box and click **Search**.

As you can see, WooCommerce products now appear in search results on our test site.

![we found the product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-product2.jpg)#### Customized Product Visibility

Did you add or edit a product, but it didnât appear in the search results? Thereâs a good chance that youâve accidentally changed its default visibility settings.

To figure that out, navigate to **Products** in your WordPress dashboard and click on the product whose visibility youâd like to change.

![open the product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/open-the-product.jpg)Then, make sure that the productâs catalog visibility option is set to **Shop and search results**.

You can find the option under the Publish metabox.

![check the product catalog visibility](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/check-the-product-catalog-visibility.jpg)If itâs not the case, youâve just found the reason why your product doesnât appear in the search results.

To fix this, simply press **Edit** next to the **Catalog visibility** option. Then, change this parameter to **Shop and search results** or **Search results only**, depending on your needs.

![change the product search visibility](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-the-product-search-visibility.jpg)Once done, you can press **OK**.

Then donât forget to save the changes by clicking the **Update** button.

![press update to update the product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-update-to-update-the-product.jpg)#### Cached Search Results Page

Do you use any caching plugins on your WordPress website? Your search results page may have simply been cached.

This problem can be solved in a couple of clicks by clearing your siteâs cache. The exact steps depend on which plugin youâre using.

For this tutorial, weâll show you how to clear the cache with one of the most popular WordPress caching plugins called [WP Super Cache](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-super-cache/ "WP Super Cache").

First, navigate to **Settings Â» WP Super Cache** from your WordPress dashboard.

![go to the wp super cache tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-wp-super-cache-tab.jpg)After that, youâll need to scroll down to the âDelete Cached Pagesâ section.

Then, press the **Delete Cache** button to display updated versions of pages on your site.

![Delete cache button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/delete-cache-button-1030x437.png)#### Plugin Compatibility Issues

A plugin conflict can cause problems with displaying products in search results not only in the admin panel but also on the site itself.

To resolve this issue, you can simply deactivate all the plugins on your site (except WooCommerce).

![deactivate all plugins](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deactivate-all-plugins.jpg)Once thatâs done, you can enable each plugin one at a time and check which one is causing the issue.

### Fix WordPress Doesnât Search by WooCommerce Product Attributes

The default WordPress search doesnât include product attributes in the search process.

To fix this issue, the easiest solution is to use a search plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

Its WooCommerce extension lets you make products searchable in WordPress and include different attributes in the search process. For example, you can use product categories, tags, shipping classes, SKUs, product type, and other product details.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)To learn more, you can see our guide on [how to search eCommerce product details in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress").

Using the tips in this post, you can easily solve almost any problem with search in your online store. Properly working search is essential to getting more sales and growing your business.

We hope this article helped you learn how to fix WooCommerce product search not working. You may also want to see our guide on [how to search in category in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce") and [how to add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)").

### [How to Add a WordPress Search Bar with Shortcode (Step-by-Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-wordpress-search-bar-with-shortcode/)

**Published:** April 10, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

One of the most common questions we get from our readers is if itâs possible to add a search bar to specific pages or posts using shortcodes.

Typically, a search bar on a WordPress site is in the site navigation menu or in the sidebar.

But using shortcodes lets you place a search bar right inside a post or page. And with SearchWP, you can take the search experience to the next level and help users find what theyâre looking for quicker.

In this article, weâll show you how you can add a search bar using a shortcode to your WordPress site.

### Why Add A Search Bar with Shortcode in WordPress?

Usually, there are not so many places where you can add a search bar.

Most of the WordPress themes allow you to put a search bar as a widget in one of the widget areas. You can also display your search bar next to the navigation menu.

But what if you want to create a page that contains a search bar? Or put a search bar inside the article, so your readers donât have to interrupt the reading if they want to search for something?

![a search bar within the post's body](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-find-results-2.jpg)Thatâs where shortcodes come in handy. Using shortcodes, youâre free to add a search bar to any place within a post or page.

This is helpful if your theme doesnât support adding a search box, and you donât want to edit the code to add it.

Now, letâs look at how you can add a search bar to your site using a shortcode.

### Adding a WordPress Search Bar with Shortcode

The easiest way to add a WordPress search bar with a shortcode is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Search WP is the most powerful and comprehensive [WordPress search plugin.](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") More than 50,000 website owners already use it to provide their visitors with more accurate search results.

This plugin not only allows you to add a search bar with a shortcode, it also helps you gain full control over the WordPress search engine.

For example, using SearchWP, you can customize the search results order, make documents, media files, product details, PDFs, [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), and a lot more searchable.

This plugin automatically integrates with every search bar you have on your site. So you can enhance your site search immediately after the installation, no coding needed.

Here are just some other useful things you can do with SearchWP:

- **Custom Fields Indexing**: Lots of WordPress plugins use custom fields to store your data. But, the native WordPress search engine doesnât consider them while searching. SearchWP allows you to [make custom fields searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") on your site.
- **Search Tracking**: Ever wondered [what searches visitors make](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") on your site? SearchWP gives you insights about search activity on your site in a few clicks, so you can get new content ideas and meet your audienceâs needs.
- **Seamless WooCommerce Integration**: By default, WordPress search ignores [WooCommerce product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), so itâs harder for your customers to find a product theyâre looking for. Using SearchWP, you can make any WooCommerce custom fields searchable, including product attributes, tags, and reviews.

Letâs see how you can use SearchWP to add a search bar with a shortcode in minutes.

#### Step 1: Install and Activate the SearchWP plugin

The first step is to visit the [SearchWP website](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today") and sign up for a new account.

Once done, youâll need to go to your SearchWP account and switch to the **Downloads** tab. From here, simply press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin ZIP file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)When the download is complete, copy your license key, as youâll need it later to activate the plugin.

After youâve downloaded SearchWP and copied the license key, upload the plugin ZIP file to your WordPress site and install SearchWP.

If you require any help on how to do that, then please check out this [step-by-step guide on how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once SearchWP is installed and activated, you will see the setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)#### Step 2: Install and Activate the Shortcodes Extension

Next, you need to install the [Shortcodes extension](https://searchwp.com/features/shortcodes/ "SearchWP Shortcodes Extension") in SearchWP. This extension allows you to add a search bar with a shortcode to any place in a post or page.

To start, you can head to the **SearchWP Â» Extensions** page and navigate to the âShortcodeâ extension. From here, simply click the âInstallâ button and the extension automatically activate on your site.

![Install shortcodes extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-shortcodes-extension-1030x500.png)#### Step 3: Create a Page for the New Search Bar

Now, you need to create a new page, on which youâll place the search box.

To do that, simply navigate to **Pages** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âAdd New Pageâ button.

![Add a new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-page-2-1030x465.png)Once youâre in the WordPress content editor, go ahead and add a title to your page.

After that, you can click the **Plus** button, search for the **Custom HTML** block, and then click on it to add it to the page.

![choose the custom html block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-custom-html-block.jpg)Then, copy the following code and paste it into the **Custom HTML** block:

```

[searchwp_search_form engine="default" var="searchvar" button_text="Find Results"]

 [searchwp_search_results engine="default" var="searchvar" posts_per_page=4]
  [searchwp_search_result_link direct="true"]
  [searchwp_search_result_excerpt]
 [/searchwp_search_results]

 [searchwp_search_results_none]
 No results found, please search again.
 [/searchwp_search_results_none]

 [searchwp_search_results_pagination direction="prev" link_text="Previous" var="searchvar" engine="default"]
 [searchwp_search_results_pagination direction="next" link_text="Next" var="searchvar" engine="default"]

```

The above code displays the search box on the page where you added the Custom HTML box.

It also displays the âNo search results foundâ message if there are no search results. Plus, it can also show pagination links if there are more than 4 search results.

Once youâve added the code, simply click the **Publish** button at the top right to publish your page.

![publish the page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish-the-page-1.jpg)Now, letâs go to our new page and test the search bar we just added.

#### Step 4: Test Your New Search Bar

To go over to the newly created page, click the **View Page** button.

![view the page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-the-page.jpg)As you can see, the search bar that you added with the shortcode is now successfully displayed on the page.

You can try searching for different blog posts and pages to test the search bar.

![the search box appears](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-search-box-appears.jpg)### Bonus: Change Default WordPress Search Settings

With SearchWP, you can change the default WordPress search settings and improve the overall search experience for your visitors.

The default search has many limitations and isnât ideal for sites that have a lot of content or products. For instance, it doesnât include custom fields, product details, categories, tags, and other additional information when performing a search.

This is where you can use SearchWP and change the search settings. With the plugin, you can include [comments](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-comments-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress"), reviews, taxonomies, custom fields, and more in the search results.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)SearchWP also lets you create unlimited custom search engines, which you can link to multiple search forms on your site.

To learn more, please see our guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to add a WordPress search bar with shortcode. You may also want to see our guides on [how to add a search bar to your WordPress website](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") and [how to make a search bar that searches your WordPress Site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/ "How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP today!") today!

### [How to Include WordPress Form Submission in Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-wordpress-form-submissions-in-search-results/)

**Published:** April 8, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you collect feedback, reviews, and other information through forms, then you can make this data searchable on your WordPress site.

Unfortunately, WordPressâs default search function doesnât include form data. This means that valuable information is essentially hidden from users.

But with SearchWP, you can easily include form submissions in the search process. Whether youâre managing surveys, reviews, or any other type of form, SearchWP will help make this content discoverable.

In this article, weâll show you how to include WordPress form submission in search results.

 **Note:** Before we proceed with the tutorial, please note that showing form entries as search results requires editing code. You need to have an extensive grasp of coding knowledge or need to hire a developer for this to work. If you require any assistance at any point, then please reach out to our support team. Weâd be happy to help! 

### Why Include Form Submissions in WordPress Search?

Making form submissions searchable in WordPress can significantly enhance the functionality and user experience of your website.

Users often submit information through forms and may need to access or recall what they submitted later. By including form submissions in search, you allow users to quickly find their own submissions without needing to contact site administrators.

For example, if a user submitted a support request or an application, they can easily search and find their submission to check its status or review the details.

Form submissions also contain valuable content that might not be readily available elsewhere on your site. This could include user-generated content from surveys, feedback forms, or [comment forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-comments-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress").

It also helps avoid duplication, as users can search for a particular form submission instead of creating a new one.

That said, letâs take a look at how you can easily include form submissions in WordPress search.

### Make Form Submissions Searchable in WordPress

The easiest way to search different forms in WordPress is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and offers powerful extensions for some of the most popular contact form plugins.

Currently, SearchWP supports WPForms and Gravity Forms. It makes it easy to add forms from these plugins as a search engine source, so users can easily search and discover form content.

Besides that, SearchWP is designed to overcome the limitations of default search and index everything that matters on your website. This includes [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), categories, tags, product details, media files, and more.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)From here, you can install and activate the SearchWP plugin on your site.

If you need help with this, then please see this guide on [how to install the WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After youâve activated the plugin, youâll see the SearchWP onboarding wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Next, you will need to install an extension to make form submissions searchable in WordPress. For this tutorial, weâll use the [WPForms extension](https://searchwp.com/features/source-wpforms/ "SearchWP WPForms Extension") and make our event submission form entries searchable.

You can simply head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress admin panel and navigate to **Source â WPForms** extension. From here, go ahead and click the âInstallâ button.

![Install WPForms extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-wpforms-extension.png)You should see that the extension will now automatically install and activate, with the Status changing from Not Installed to **Active**.

### Customize Search Engine to Include Form Submissions

Now that youâve set up SearchWP on your site and added the WPForms extension, the next step is to customize the search engine settings to make form submissions searchable.

First, youâll need to head to the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard and add a new engine. Simply click the âAdd Newâ button to continue.

![Add new engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-engine-1030x485.png)Next, a new window will open with your Supplemental engine. Here, you can select the sources for your new search engine.

By default, SearchWP will include [Posts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)"), Pages, Users, and Media along with all the forms you have created in WPForms as sources.

![Add new engine and select your forms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-engine-and-select-your-forms-1-1030x542.png)To limit the search to only WPForms, you can uncheck all other sources except the forms. If you need to make only a specific form searchable, then you can simply select that particular form.

For example, weâll only select the Event Submission Form as a source for this tutorial. This way, users can easily search for different events that are submitted through the form.

![Only select WPForms forms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/only-select-wpforms-forms-1-1030x534.png)You can also change the name of the new engine from Supplemental to anything you like under the âEngine Labelâ field. For example, weâll name our new engine as âWPForms Engine.â

When youâre finished, click the âDoneâ button.

Next, youâll see each form as a source in the engine. You can then select which form fields youâd want to use as attributes.

Attributes are fields you want to search for a given source. To edit them, simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button.

![Edit attributes for WPForms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-attributes-for-wpforms-1030x546.png)Next, a small window will open where you can select which attributes to use.

Simply uncheck the form fields you donât want the source to search.

![Select which attributes to use](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-which-attributes-to-use-1030x374.png)After choosing the attributes, simply click the âDoneâ button.

You can also move the slider to adjust the [relevance weights](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "SearchWP Engine Configuration"). The further to the right the slider is set for an attribute, the more influence that attribute has on the position in the search results.

![Move slider for WPForms engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-slider-for-wpforms-engine-1030x558.png)Next, you will need to scroll to the top and click the **Save** button to store your changes.

Youâve now successfully created a new search engine that search form submissions.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)### Displaying Form Entries as Search Results

### [How to Limit Search to Only Posts in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-only-posts-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** April 2, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâve been running a WordPress blog for a long time, then your siteâs search results can be cluttered with everything from media files to pages, making it hard for readers to find the blog posts they were looking for.

Sadly, WordPress doesnât come with a built-in option to filter site searches. Youâd have to edit code or hire a developer to customize the default search.

That said, there is an easier way. With SearchWP, you can make your siteâs search function exactly how you want it to.

In this article, weâll show you how to limit search to only posts in WordPress.

### Why Limit Search to Only Posts on Your Site?

By default, WordPress displays all published pages, posts, and custom post types in search results.

Thatâs why sometimes it might be challenging to find the right piece of content, especially on sites with a bunch of pages.

Have you ever used a search form on a site and come across pages like Sitemap, About Us, Checkout, and other similar page among the regular results? Then you know this feeling.

If youâre a blogger, then limiting search results to posts only will make it easier for your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for.

It ensures a better user experience and increases engagement. Besides, satisfied users who found the right content on your site are more likely to return again and become the loyal audience.

If you are an online store owner, then technical pages in search results can distract your customers from [searching for products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)").

Removing these pages can improve conversion and make your store look more professional.

That said, letâs see how you can limit the search on your WordPress site to only posts.

### Limiting Website Search to Only Posts in WordPress

The easiest way to limit search to only posts in WordPress is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Used by over 50,000 sites, SearchWP is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"). Using it, you can limit search on your site to only posts, pages, or custom posts types in a few clicks, without writing any code.

You also get pre-built form templates that are easily customizable. The best part, you can create unlimited search engines and link them to individual search forms to filter the results on your site.

Some of its other features also include:

- **Keyword Stemming:** Do you want to make your WordPress site recognize that search terms like âsearchâ and âsearchingâ are relevant to the same content? SearchWP allows you to enable search for matches related to the keywords you enter, just like in Google.
- **Custom Fields Indexing:** The native WordPress search engine doesnât consider any data stored within the custom fields. It makes it impossible to search on sites that actively use custom post types properly. SearchWP allows you to tackle this issue and [make custom fields searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") in a few clicks.
- **Search Activity Tracking:** Wonder what your visitors are searching for on your site? SearchWP gives you a full report on your [visitorsâ search behavior](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") so you can better know your audience and meet their content needs.
- **Seamless Integration:** SearchWP offers powerful extensions that allow you to integrate it with popular eCommerce platforms like WooCommerce, form plugins like WPForms, and custom fields plugins like Advanced Custom Fields (ACF).

Now, letâs proceed to the step-by-step tutorial on limiting search to only posts using the SearchWP plugin.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP in WordPress

The first step is to [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) by visiting the website and signing up for a new account.

After that, you can navigate to the **Downloads** tab in your SearchWP account. Once there, simply press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)After youâve downloaded the plugin, also copy your license key, as youâll need it soon to activate your license.

Then, go to your WordPress site dashboard and upload the SearchWP plugin ZIP file. Need help with that? Please check out this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step tutorial](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After installing and activating the plugin, youâll see the SearchWP setup wizard. You can click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully added SearchWP to your site. Now, you can fully use it to improve search on your site.

### Step 2: Customize the WordPress Search Engine

Next, you can start customizing your WordPress search engine and limit the search to posts only.

Think of a search engine as a set of rules that you can edit to control how search works on your site.

To get started, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. Here, you can customize your default search engine and add new ones.

From here, you can click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup will open where you can see the sources for the default search engine.

These include Posts, Pages, and Media.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Now, to limit search to only posts, letâs just remove any other sources from the default search engine source list. This way, your search engine will consider only posts when performing a search.

Simply uncheck the sources youâd like to remove. In our case, letâs uncheck Pages and Media, to leave **Posts** as the only active source.

![Select posts as only source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-posts-as-only-source-1030x438.png)After youâve finished, click **Done**.

Next, you can add or remove content attributes for the Posts source from your WordPress search engine.

Attributes are the fields you want to search for a given source, like title, content, excerpt, slug, custom fields, taxonomy terms, and more. This decides which content details to consider when your site is looking for results.

Go ahead and click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)A new window will now open with attributes like title, content, slug, and excerpt already selected.

Next, you can include any custom fields, categories, and tags as attributes as well. Simply click the respective dropdown menus and select additional attributes.

For example, if you have a food blog and have created an ACF custom field for ingredients, then you can include that in the search process.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Next, you can click the **Save** button in the top right corner to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Youâve now successfully limited search on your site to posts only.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search

Now, letâs test our customized search engine. For the purposes of this tutorial, weâve created a post and page, both entitled âWordPress.â

In the parentheses next to their titles, we indicated what type of content they belong to. It allows us to find out whether the search restriction works or not.

![the wordpress post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-wordpress-post.jpg)Now, letâs go over to our demo website and check what search results weâll get after searching for WordPress.

As you can see, there is no âWordPress (Page)â among the search results. Do note that weâre using the [SearchWP Live Search feature](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress"), which displays search results as you type in the search form.

![the search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-search-results.jpg)It means that weâve successfully excluded all types of content from search results, except for posts.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

You can now create multiple search engines and configure them to search for only pages or products.

The best part is that SearchWP lets you link each engine with a specific search form.

This way, you can set up custom searches for different parts of your website. For example, the search in the blog section will only look for posts, while the search in the online store section will only search for products.

![Select new engine for form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-new-engine-for-form-1030x484.png)To learn more, please see our guide on [how to add multiple search forms to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to easily limit search to only posts in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add a search bar in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)") and [how to fix WordPress search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!") today!

### [How to Search in Category in WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** March 27, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Helping customers find what theyâre actually looking for can skyrocket your sales and improve customer retention.

However, thatâs easier said than done if you have thousands of products and categories on your WooCommerce store.

So, whatâs the solution? Allow your visitors to search in categories.

By restricting the search to a specific category, you can help users find relevant products faster. As a result, youâll deliver a better shopping experience and boost conversions.

In this article, weâll show you how to search in category in WooCommerce.

### Why Search by Category in WooCommerce

By default, WooCommerce search results display products from all the existing categories. Thatâs not a problem if you have an online store with a few products.

But when it comes to a huge store with thousands of products and tons of categories, it could become a hassle to search.

Have you ever [searched for a specific product](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-woocommerce-product-search/ "How to Customize Your WooCommerce Product Search") and gotten a massive number of search results that didnât even contain what you were looking for? Then you know how frustrating this can be.

If your online store visitors couldnât find the product they came for, they most likely would leave immediately.

As a result, it can impact your sales and revenue. Plus, people wonât spend more time on your online store and engage with different products.

Thatâs why [searching by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)") in WooCommerce can significantly improve the search experience.

Letâs look at how you can include categories in the search process on your WooCommerce site.

### Including Categories in WooCommerce Search

The easiest way to create a search bar that searches in a specific WooCommerce category is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Itâs the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), trusted by over 50,000 websites around the world.

Apart from searching by WooCommerce categories, SearchWP allows you to make product details, tags, documents, [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), reviews and other information searchable.

It will make it much easier for your visitors to find the products they want, increasing sales and providing a better user experience.

SearchWP can also boost your WordPress search by:

- **PDF and Office Document Indexing**: By default, WordPress doesnât index any text within the documents you have on your site. SearchWP allows you to [include PDF](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") and office documents into search results in just a few clicks.
- **Keyword Stemming**: The native WordPress search engine doesnât understand that different keyword variations, such as âhatâ and âhats,â essentially refer to the same product. Using SearchWP, you can enable [keyword stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/keyword-stemming/ "Keyword Stemming") and solve this issue.
- **Search Tracking**: Have you ever been curious about what visitors are searching for on your website? SearchWP allows you to [collect all the on-site search activity](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") data. Using this valuable information, you can better understand your customersâ needs.

Now letâs look at how to add a search bar bound to a specific WooCommerce category using SearchWP.

The first step is to [grab your copy of SearchWP ](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP") and sign up for a new account.

After that, you can go to your SearchWP account and open the âDownloadsâ tab. Once there, press the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin on your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)The next step is to upload the SearchWP plugin ZIP file to your WordPress site. If you require help with this, then please see this step-by-step tutorial on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once youâve installed SearchWP, youâll see the SearchWP setup wizard. Go ahead and click the **Start Onboarding Wizard** button and follow the onscreen instructions to configure the plugin.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)The next step is to install the [WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/features/woocommerce-integration/ "WooCommerce SearchWP Extension") in SearchWP.

You can head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard and navigate to WooCommerce Integration.

Once you get there, simply click the **Install** button and the extension will automatically install and activate.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)The next step is to edit the default engine to include your WooCommerce products or create a new one.

You can think of a search engine as a set of rules that SearchWP follows when performing searches. By editing them, you can completely customize the way search works on your site.

For example, you can [exclude specific posts](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/ "How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)") or pages from search results, add any custom post types as search sources, and more.

First, youâll need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard.

As we want to create a custom search form that searches only WooCommerce products, weâll add a new search engine for it. Go ahead and click the **Add New** button.

![Add new engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-engine-1030x485.png)After that, a new window will open with the **Supplemental** search engine.

As you can see, it has 5 search sources: posts, pages, media, products, and users.

![View new search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-new-search-engine-1030x528.png)The number of sources may also depend on the number of plugins installed on your site.

Since we want this search engine to search solely among [WooCommerce products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), weâll uncheck all other sources except **Products**.

![Select product as sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-as-sources-1030x514.png)Besides that, you can also change the name of your new search engine. SearchWP will automatically call it Supplemental, but you can edit that under the **Engine Label** field.

When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

![Add and remove attributes of products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-and-remove-attributes-of-products-1030x427.png)Next, you can scroll down to the âProductsâ source in the new Supplemental engine. From here, click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button to include WooCommerce product categories in the search process.

A new window will now open where you can select different attributes.

These are fields you want to search for a given source. By default, SearchWP will use [title](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-wordpress-search-to-post-titles-only/ "How to Limit WordPress Search to Post Titles Only"), content, slug, excerpt, and author as attributes. But you can also include custom fields and taxonomies.

Since we want to include WooCommerce product categories in the search process, go ahead and click the **Taxonomies** dropdown menu and select the âProduct categories (product\_cat)â option.

![Select product category as attribute](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-product-category-as-attribute-1030x482.png)After that, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Now, do you want to limit your search results to a specific WooCommerce product category?

You can specify which product category you want the new search engine to search by setting up rules. Go ahead and click the **Edit Rules** button.

![Edit product attributes rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-product-attributes-rule-1030x426.png)Next, a new window will open.

Then, you can click the **Add Rule** option.

![Add a new rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-rule--1030x403.png)After that, select the **Product categories** taxonomy from the dropdown list.

From here, you can choose a specific category you want the search engine to stick to.

![Select your category](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-category-1030x500.png)In our case, weâll pick the Shoes category. But you can select more categories on your WooCommerce store.

When youâre finished, press the **Done** button.

Next, youâll need to store your changes by scrolling to the top and clicking the **Save** button.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully created a new search engine that searches only a specific WooCommerce product category.

### Create a Custom Search Form for WooCommerce Category

Next, you can create a [custom search form](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") for your WooCommerce store.

Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âAdd Newâ button.

![Add a new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-search-form-1030x533.png)After that, you can change the name of your search form at the top by clicking the pencil icon.

SearchWP also comes with pre-built search form templates. You can simply select one and customize it.

![Select search form template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-form-template-1030x489.png)After that, youâll need to scroll down to the âCustom Stylingâ section.

From here, click the **Engine** dropdown menu and select the âSupplementalâ search engine that you created earlier.

![Select search engine for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-engine-for-search-form-1030x586.png)If you scroll down, then youâll see more options to customize your form. For instance, you can specify the type of search, select category, change field label, and enable the search button.

After youâre done editing your form, simply click the âSaveâ button at the top.

You can then click the âEmbedâ button and add the search form anywhere on your WooCommerce store.

![Save and embed category form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-embed-category-form-1030x489.png)SearchWP offers 3 ways to add the search form in WordPress. You can select the Gutenberg block, [shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search"), or PHP code.

Next, you will also need to choose whether youâd like to display the form on a new page or an existing page.

![Embed new search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-new-search-form-1030x550.png)For this tutorial, weâll select the âCreate New Pageâ option.

On the next screen, you can enter a name for the new page and click **Letâs Go**.

![Enter name for new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-name-for-new-page-1030x520.png)After that, you should see the search form in the WordPress content editor.

Go ahead and **Publish** your page.

![Preview and publish search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/preview-and-publish-search-form-1030x393.png)Next, you can visit your website to see the search form in action.

From here, simply test the new search form and see if it looks for products in the specified category.

For instance, weâll search for âshoesâ on our demo WooCommerce store, since we limited the search engine to only look for WooCommerce products in the Shoe category.

As you can see, the results show the correct product.

![View WooCommerce category search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-woocommerce-category-search-results-1030x540.png)On the other hand, you can also test by looking up other products that are not part of the specified category.

For example, if we search for another product on our WooCommerce store, the search didnât display any results since itâs limited to a particular category.

![Test WooCommerce category search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/test-woocommerce-category-search-results-1030x475.png)We hope this article helped you learn how to search in category in WooCommerce. You may also want to see our guides on [how to hide WooCommerce product from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)") and [how to fix WooCommerce product search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-woocommerce-product-search-not-working/ "How to Fix WooCommerce Product Search Not Working").

Meta Box is popular for creating custom fields, post types, and taxonomies, where you can store extra information for your posts and pages. They are super useful for organizing your website content in exactly the way you need.

However, there is a catch: WordPressâs default search ignores all that extra information youâve carefully put into Meta Box fields. If someone searches for a specific detail youâve added in Meta Box, they wonât find it with the default search.

Users are much more likely to find exactly what theyâre looking for when the search looks everywhere, including those [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") youâve set up with Meta Box.

This better search experience makes your visitors happier. Instead of getting frustrated and leaving your site, they can find the information they need and stick around longer.

For sites that rely heavily on custom post types and lots of extra data managed by Meta Box, this is especially important.

Imagine someone searching for a âblue shirtâ on your [online store](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-for-digital-downloads/ "Best WordPress eCommerce Plugins for Digital Downloads"). If youâve used Meta Box to create a âcolorâ custom field for your products, and your search includes those fields, then visitors will actually find what they are looking for.

### Change Search Settings to Include Meta Box Fields

### [How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/)

**Published:** March 20, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Over the years, weâve learned that the default WordPress search barely scratches the surface of what users need. When visitors couldnât find specific details in custom fields, it would leave them frustrated and they would eventually abandon our website.

Custom fields are a useful WordPress feature for adding extra information to your site content. If your site users can search your custom fields, theyâll get to see more relevant results. This can help you boost sales and customer satisfaction on your site.

Sadly, WordPress doesnât include custom fields in the search process by default. But you can easily include them by using a plugin like SearchWP.

In this article, weâll show you how to add custom fields search in WordPress the easy way and without writing a single line of code.

### What Are WordPress Custom Fields?

A custom field is a content attribute that WordPress uses to store information about a particular post, page, or any content type.

Each time you add content to your site, WordPress automatically saves it in two different areas.

The first area is the body of the content and the second area is the extra information related to the main content. Typically, this is where you save information like the title, description, tags, and categories. This area is also called the metadata.

WordPress offers specific metadata fields by default. But you can add even more information to your content by adding custom fields to WordPress.

To add new custom fields to your site, you can use dedicated plugins like [Advanced Custom Fields](https://searchwp.com/advanced-custom-fields-acf/ "Search Advanced Custom Fields Data in WordPress"). This way, you get to separate your content details into more areas than WordPress offers by default.

For example, you may have a WordPress movie review website. By default, WordPress allows you to add metadata fields like titles, tags, and categories to your posts. But with custom fields, you can add more details like rating, genre, and producer.

![custom fields for WordPress movie review website](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/custom-fields-for-WordPress-movie-review-website.png)So, WordPress will include these information fields each time youâre adding a movie review to your site.

This makes it easy to maintain the same styling and formatting across all your movie review posts. [Custom fields](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wp-tutorials/wordpress-custom-fields-101-tips-tricks-and-hacks/#cfadvancedcustomfields "WordPress Custom Fields 101: Tips, Tricks, and Hacks") can also come in handy for many other purposes, like user account management and WooCommerce inventory management.

Now, letâs talk about why custom fields search is important.

### Why Add Custom Fields Search to WordPress?

WordPress custom fields help you to add more information to your site content. With WordPress custom fields search, your site can search these extra information fields.

This means your WordPress site users can now look for search results in more ways than before. This automatically improves your site search and makes it more flexible.

For example, you can have a movie review site with custom fields like âGenreâ and âProducerâ. With custom fields search, a site visitor can easily type a producerâs name in the search bar. Your site will then display all the movie reviews from that producer.

![WordPress custom field search results for producer](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WordPress-custom-field-search-results-for-producer.jpg)This makes it super easy for your readers to navigate your movie review site.

Similarly, you may have a WooCommerce store with custom fields like âBrandâ and âManufacturing Yearâ. Your site users can easily [use a custom search form to find products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)") from a specific brand or manufacturing year.

![WordPress Custom fields search results for car brand](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WordPress-Custom-fields-search-results-for-car-brand-1030x492.jpg)That said, letâs look at how you can easily make custom fields searchable in WordPress without editing code.

### How to Add WordPress Custom Fields Search

The easiest way to add custom fields to WordPress search is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)As the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), SearchWP allows your site visitors to search every custom field on your WordPress site. This can be super useful for finding the site content they need.

The tool integrates well with other plugins, like Advanced Custom Fields. So, even if you added custom fields with a third-party plugin, you can still search them in WordPress.

Here are other features SearchWP offers:

- **Search Everything on Your Site â** SearchWP is not limited to custom fields only. Unlike the default WordPress search, your site can include categories, tags, product details, media files, PDFs, [documents](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-woocommerce-product-documents-searchable/ "How to Make WooCommerce Product Documents Searchable"), SKUs, and more in the search process.
- **Seamless WooCommerce Integration â** The plugin allows you to combine the power of [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/ "How to Hide WooCommerce Product from Search Results (No Coding)") and custom fields. This helps keep your online store as organized as possible. Even if you have thousands of products in your store, your visitors should be able to find products fast.
- **Site Statistics and Insights â** SearchWP logs all your site [visitorsâ searches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"). This way, you can monitor what theyâre looking for on your site. You can even use these statistics to decide whether your site needs custom fields search or not.

Now, letâs use SearchWP to add custom fields search to your WordPress site in 3 easy steps.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To set your custom fields search, you can get started by visiting the SearchWP website and [signing up for a new account](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Sign Up for a SearchWP account").

Next, youâll need to sign in to your SearchWP account and download the plugin.

Simply navigate to the âDownloadsâ tab within the dashboard. Then, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin files on your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, itâs time to upload the plugin to your WordPress site. If you need help with this, then you can use this detailed step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now that youâve set up the plugin, letâs use it to set up custom fields search. You can do this by setting up your WordPress search engine.

### Step 2: Add Custom Fields as Attributes in Your Search Engine

SearchWP uses search engines to help you customize your site search behavior. You can decide how your site looks for search results by simply making changes to the search engine.

A WordPress search engine works like a small Google search algorithm. It determines where to look for matches during site search and which content attributes to check first.

The best part about using SearchWP is that you can edit the default WordPress search and also add your own custom engines. For this tutorial, weâll simply edit the default engine.

To get started, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and then click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)On the next screen, youâll see the default sources that the engine will use to search content on your site. These include posts, pages, and media files.

Simply select which sources the search engine should consider when finding content or products on your site.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Go ahead and click the âDoneâ button when youâre done.

Next, you can add or remove content attributes for each source from your WordPress search engine. This decides which content details to consider when your site is looking for results.

Attributes are the fields you want to search for a given source, like title, content, excerpt, slug, custom fields, taxonomy terms, and more.

To add custom fields as attributes for any source in your search engine, click **Add/Remove Attributes**.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, a popup window will appear, where you can select all the custom fields you want to add to your WordPress search.

SearchWP will show you all the available custom fields, no matter the plugin you used to add them. For example, on our demo site, it automatically detected the [Advanced Custom Fields (ACF)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") weâve set up.

![Add custom fields to search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-custom-fields-to-search-1030x542.png)You can also select taxonomies like categories and tags to include as a source attribute. When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

You can also rank each custom field value according to how relevant you want it to be by moving the slider. The further right the slider, the more importance or [relevance](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/ "Engine Configuration") that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

![Move slider for custom fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-slider-for-custom-fields-1030x595.png)The last step is to scroll back to the top and click the âSaveâ button to store your settings.

Once thatâs done, you may also need to click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)SearchWP will now start applying your new changes by building a new search index.

Now, letâs test if everything works fine on the userâs end.

### Step 3: Test Your New Custom Fields Search

To see how effective your WordPress custom fields search is, simply visit your website.

Next, type a custom field value in the search bar and click **Search**. For example, âSci-fiâ is one of the values we added to the âGenreâ custom field in this tutorial. Your site should then show you relevant search results like this.

![Wordpress custom fields search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Wordpress-custom-fields-search-results.jpg)Congratulations! Youâve successfully added custom fields search to your WordPress site.

We hope this article helped you learn how to add custom fields search in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to make WordPress search PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") and [ how to fix WordPress search not working](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How To Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Issues Solved)").

Are you ready to add custom fields search to your WordPress site? [Get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").
