### [How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/)

**Published:** March 18, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Imagine knowing exactly what your audience is looking for when they land on your page.

This is where tracking what visitors search for on your WordPress site comes in handy.

Tracking data removes guesswork and provides actionable information that can transform your website from good to great.

Why is this so important? Because your site search bar is a goldmine of information revealing user intent. Understanding these search queries allows you to fine-tune your content strategy, improve site navigation, and ultimately give your visitors exactly what they want.

In this article, weâll show how to track your WordPress site search activity.

## Why Track Searches on Your Site?

If you want to ensure the best user experience for your site visitors, then itâs crucial to examine their needs.

One of the best ways to do this is by start collecting data about searches users make on your site.

Analyzing the visitorsâ search behavior lets you know the way they search and what theyâre looking for.

Thatâs precious information that you can use in different cases:

- **Get New Content Ideas:** Gathered search data is a great source of fresh content ideas. Analyze it to get in touch with your audience and provide them with the content they want.
- **Figure Out Whatâs Missing From Your Site:** See a lot of search queries related to a certain topic but donât have content dedicated to it? Itâs a missed opportunity to get more traffic.
- **Restructure and Update Your Content:** Search data tracking allows you to take a look at your site content from a different angle and restructure it if needed.
- **Get to Know Your Audience Better:** There are not so many opportunities for website owners to get in touch with their audience. Tracking on-site searches is one of the best ways to better understand your audienceâs needs.

Now letâs see how you can start collecting data about search activity on your WordPress site. Weâll cover 2 methods in this guide, which includes using SearchWP and MonsterInsights. You can click the links below to jump ahead to your preferred method.

- [Method 1: Track Site Searches Using SearchWP](#aioseo-method-1-using-the-searchwp-plugin)
- [Method 2: Track WordPress Searches Using MonsterInsights](#aioseo-method-2-using-the-monsterinsights-plugin)

### Method 1: Track Site Searches Using SearchWP 

The easiest way to track searches on your WordPress site is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

As the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), it drastically expands the capabilities of the native WordPress search engine and helps users find what theyâre looking for.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Here are just some of the useful things you can do with SearchWP:

- **Searchable Custom Fields:** The default WordPress search engine doesnât consider data inside the custom fields, but with SearchWP, you can make custom fields searchable.
- **PDF and Document Indexing:** The native WordPress search engine completely ignores the text inside PDFs and other uploaded documents. With SearchWP, you can include documents into the search results in just a few clicks.
- **Live Search Results:** SearchWP comes with plenty of useful extensions, including Live Search. It eases your website navigation and ensures a better user experience for your visitors.
- **Automatic Theme Integration:** You donât have to take any additional steps after installing SearchWP, as it integrates with your current site search form and search results template.

You can start by visiting the SearchWP website and [signing up for a new account](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!").

When youâre done, simply go to the âDownloadsâ tab in your SearchWP account and click **Download SearchWP** to save the plugin to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to log into your WordPress admin area and upload SearchWP to your site.

Need help with that? Kindly check out this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After that, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Even though SearchWP gathers search data by default, using the [Metrics extension](https://searchwp.com/features/metrics/ "Search Analytics Plugin for WordPress") lets you get advanced information about your usersâ search behavior.

Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard and navigate to the âMetricsâ extension. From here, you can click the **Install** button.

![Install metrics addon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-metrics-addon-1030x491.png)Once the extension is active, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Metrics** from your WordPress dashboard.

On this tab, you can find all the data about searches made by your visitors.

There will be no data initially because you just installed the plugin. But starting today, all searches made on your site will appear here.

![the list of your site's searches](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-list-of-your-sites-searches.jpg)SearchWPâs Metrics extension collects comprehensive data for on-site search, and provides you with actionable advice about changes you can make to your content as time goes on.

Once you start collecting search data, you will see different statistics like total searches, clicks per search, and [popular searches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-the-most-popular-search-keywords-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track the Most Popular Search Keywords on Your WordPress Site").

The best part is that SearchWP also shows actionable insights, which can help you grow your business. For example, it will highlight queries with high conversion rate, so you can optimize them across your site.

![SearchWP Metrics preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim-1-1030x619.png)### Method 2: Track WordPress Searches Using MonsterInsights 

You can also track searches on your WordPress site in Google Analytics using [MonsterInsights](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ "MonsterInsights").

It is the [best analytics plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-analytics-plugins/ "Best WordPress Analytics Plugins [Compared]") and helps you set up tracking in Google Analytics without editing code.

[![MonsterInsights ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/monsterinsights-website-1030x600.png)](https://www.monsterinsights.com/)Normally, you can track searches by connecting Google Analytics to your site. But itâs tricky to do for beginners since it requires making changes to the theme code.

MonsteInsights allows you to connect your site with Google Analytics in just a couple of clicks.

Besides, you also get reports inside the WordPress dashboard, real-time analytics, eCommerce tracking, site speed report, and much more.

The best part is that you can get started with the [MonsterInsights Lite version](https://wordpress.org/plugins/google-analytics-for-wordpress/ "MonsterInsights â Google Analytics Dashboard for WordPress") for free. It will help you set up Google Analytics in just a few clicks.

First, you will need to go to **Plugins Â» Add new** from your WordPress dashboard and search for MonsterInsights.

![Add MonsterInsights plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-monsterinsights-plugin-1030x451.png)From here, you will need to install and activate the MonsterInsights plugin. If you need help with this, then please see the guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, youâll see the MonsterInsights setup wizard. Simply click the âLaunch the Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions to connect your site with Google Analytics.

![MonsterInsights setup wizard](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/monsterinsights-setup-wizard-1030x623.png)You may want to see this guide on [how to install Google Analytics in WordPress](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/how-to-install-google-analytics-in-wordpress/ "How to Install Google Analytics in WordPress for Beginners") for complete step-by-step instructions.

After successfully integrating Google Analytics with your WordPress site, the next step is to activate site search tracking.

Simply begin by logging into your Google Analytics account and selecting the relevant site for search tracking activation.

![Select your site in GA](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-site-in-GA-1030x562.png)After that, you will need to click **Admin** cog icon in the bottom left corner.

Next, you can click the âData streamsâ option under Data collection and modification.

![Go to data streams options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-data-streams-options-1030x484.png)Here, you will see all your data streams in Google Analytics.

Go ahead and select the active stream.

![Select your data stream site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-your-data-stream-site-1030x441.png)After that, youâll need to ensure that the âEnhanced measurementâ option is enabled. If it is not, then simply click the toggle to enable it.

From here, you can click the settings cog icon to configure Enhanced measurement options.

![Enable enhanced measurement](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-enhanced-measurement-1030x518.png)Next, you will need to check if **Site search** tracking is enabled or not.

You can simply click the toggle to enable it in Google Analytics.

![Enable site search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-site-search-1030x459.png)Google Analytics will now start tracking site searches on your website.

The next step is to view the report in Google Analytics. To start, open the **Explore tab** and create a **Blank** report.

![Create a blank report](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-a-blank-report-1030x607.png)After that, you will need to select variables for your report.

First, click the + sign in the **Dimensions** section.

![Select dimensions](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-dimensions-1030x628.png)Next, you will need to select **Search term** and **Event name** as the Dimensions.

You can use the search bar at the top to find these Dimensions.

![Select search term](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-term-1-1030x389.png)When youâre done, simply click the **Confirm** button.

After that, you will need to choose Metrics for your report. Simply click the + icon to select metrics for your report.

![Choose a metric](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-metric-1-1030x524.png)On the next screen, search for the **Event count** metric.

After that, youâll need to click the âConfirmâ button at the top.

![Select event count metric](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-event-count-metric-1030x396.png)Next, you will need to drag the âSearch termâ Dimension under the **Rows** box.

Once thatâs done, drag the âEvent countâ Metric down to the **Values** box.

![Drag dimension and metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/drag-dimension-and-metrics--1030x643.png)Lastly, youâll need to drag the âEvent nameâ Dimension to the **Filter** box.

After that, you can set the conditions to **exactly matches** and **view\_search\_results**.

![Drag event name to filter](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/drag-event-name-to-filter-1030x641.png)When thatâs done, go ahead and click the âApplyâ button.

Your custom report for site searches in Google Analytics is now ready. When your visitors search anything on your site, it will be recorded in this report.

For example, hereâs a report we created for our demo site. In the report, youâll see the search terms and how many times each one was searched.

![Site search report in GA4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/site-search-report-1030x500.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Thatâs it, weâve covered 2 simple methods for tracking searches in your WordPress site.

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the best search plugin for WordPress and helps you view top searches in the admin panel. Plus, it offers a powerful Metrics extension that offers additional insights.

On the other hand, if youâre using Google Analytics and want to see what people are searching in your site, then [MonsterInsights](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ "MonsterInsights") is the best solution.

We hope this article helped you learn how to track searches in WordPress. You may also want to see our guides on [how to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/ "How To Restrict WooCommerce Product Search To Title Only") and [how to add WordPress search by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)").

If youâre ready to start tracking search queries on your site and prefer the first method, then [grab your copy of SearchWP today](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!").

### [How to Make Easy Digital Downloads Products Searchable in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-easy-digital-downloads-products-searchable-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** March 13, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you have ever tried to find a specific product in an online store, but the websiteâs search just wasnât helpful, then it can be like looking for a needle in a haystack.

Thatâs exactly how your visitors will feel when they visit your Easy Digital Downloads (EDD) store and canât find what theyâre looking for.

If people canât search your store easily, then itâs like having a shop with all the lights turned off. They might get frustrated and leave without buying anything. But with the right plugin, you can easily make your digital products searchable.

In this article, we will show you how to make Easy Digital Downloads products searchable in WordPress.

### Why Default WordPress Search Doesnât Work for EDD?

The standard WordPress search is okay for basic eCommerce stores that have few products. It can find some things, but it misses a lot. Thatâs why it is far from perfect if you have an [Easy Digital Downloads](https://searchwp.com/features/easy-digital-downloads-edd-integration/ "Easy Digital Downloads Integration with SearchWP") store with lots of products.

One big problem is how the default search looks at product details. It may not pay attention to things like product categories made just for your digital items or special tags youâve added to describe them.

Beyond that, think about all the words you use in your [product descriptions](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/ "How To Find The Most Searched Product On Your Website"). You might describe file types, software versions, or other specific details that help customers find the right thing. The default search can sometimes miss words within these descriptions, making it harder for people to find exactly what they need.

This can be really annoying for your customers. They might type in exactly what theyâre looking for and get nothing useful. This bad search experience can lead to fewer sales and losing potential customers.

That said, letâs look at a simple way of helping visitors find digital products on your Easy Digital Downloads store.

### Make EDD Products Searchable in WordPress

The best way to include your Easy Digital Downloads product in the search process is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") thatâs trusted by over 50,000 website owners. SearchWP easily integrates with Easy Digital Downloads along with other popular eCommerce plugins like WooCommerce and BigCommerce.

Whatâs great about using SearchWP is that it lets you include [product details](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-ecommerce-product-details-in-wordpress/ "How to Search eCommerce Product Details in WordPress"), order history, custom fields, comments, SKUs, taxonomies, and much more in the search process.

First, you can visit the SearchWP website and sign up for a new account.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)After that, youâll need to do is download the SearchWP plugin.

To do that, head to your account area and switch to the âDownloadsâ tab. From here, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you can install and activate the SearchWP plugin on your site. For more details, please see this guide on [how to install the WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once the plugin is active, SearchWP will show a welcome screen and setup wizard in your WordPress dashboard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After that, you can head to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** from the WordPress dashboard.

Simply navigate to the âEDD Integrationâ and then click the âInstallâ button.

![Add EDD integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-edd-integration-1030x355.png)The extension will now automatically install and activate. You should see the Status change from Not Installed to Active.

The next step is to customize the search engine settings and include your digital products in the search process.

### Customize Search Engine Settings to Include Digital Products

With SearchWP, you can edit the default WordPress search or create an entirely new engine.

The best part is that you can set up multiple search engines and link them to different search forms. This way, you can help customers find exactly what they are looking for by narrowing down the search process.

For this tutorial, we will edit the default WordPress search settings. But if you want to set up additional engines, then please see our guide on [how to add multiple search forms in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)").

To edit the search settings, you will need to go to the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Youâll now see a new window with the default sources for the search engine, which includes posts, pages, and media by default.

To include your EDD products, make sure to select the **Downloads** option as a source and then click the âDoneâ button.

![Include downloads in the search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/include-downloads-in-the-search-engine-1030x501.png)After that, youâll need to scroll down to the âDownloadsâ section in the search engine settings and select the attributes you want to search.

Attributes are the fields you want to search for a given source, like title, content, excerpt, slug, custom fields, taxonomy terms and more.

To include more details, simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button.

![Change downloads attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-downloads-attributes-1030x423.png)Next, you will see a new popup open where you can manage the search attributes.

SearchWP will use [title](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-wordpress-search-to-post-titles-only/ "How to Limit WordPress Search to Post Titles Only"), content, slug, excerpt, and author as attributes by default. But you can also include custom fields, categories, and tags when searching for products.

For instance, if you have organized your digital products based on categories like e-books, music and videos, then you can include them under Taxonomies. Simply click the dropdown menu and select the âDownload Categoriesâ option.

Similarly, you can also include custom fields, such as file format and size, software version number, duration for videos and audio files, and more.

![Add download categories and custom fields](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-download-categories-and-custom-fields-1030x550.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

SearchWP will now include download details you selected as attributes during the search process.

You can also use the slider to adjust the relevance weight. The further right the slider, the higher in search results that particular Attribute will appear.

![Move slider to change download weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-slider-to-change-download-weights-1030x455.png)Next, you can scroll back to the top and click the âSaveâ button.

Once thatâs done, you will also need to click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)Thatâs it! You have now successfully made your digital products searchable on your Easy Digital Downloads store.

Next, you can visit your website and search for different products to see if the search is working properly.

For instance, we added an e-book about cooking to our demo EDD site. When searching for the digital product using the [live search features](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites") in SearchWP, it showed the e-book.

![Preview of EDD search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/preview-of-edd-search-1030x579.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

The best choice is a plugin that supports the largest number of digital product types.

With that in mind, letâs take a look at the best WordPress eCommerce plugins for digital downloads.

### [How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/)

**Published:** March 6, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Over the years, weâve noticed that a single search form for your website might not be enough, especially if you have many blog posts or products.

Imagine being able to offer your visitors specialized search options. For instance, you can create a form to search specifically within your blog posts, another for product listings if you run an online store, or a dedicated search just for your knowledge base.

By using multiple search forms on your site, you can narrow your search results to help your visitors find what theyâre looking for faster.

In this article, weâll show you how to add multiple search forms to your WordPress site in a few minutes, with no coding required.

### Why Use Multiple Search Forms on Your Site

There are several reasons why using multiple search forms on your site can be helpful.

First, if your site has more than one search form, then the chance that visitors will notice and use one of them increases.

![how to add multiple search forms to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-WordPress-site.png)It will improve your contentâs discoverability and encourage visitors to spend more time on your site.

The longer they stay, the more likely they will sign up for your newsletter, purchase a product, or click on an [affiliate link](https://searchwp.com/best-affiliate-marketing-plugins-for-wordpress/ "5 Best Affiliate Marketing Plugins for WordPress (Compared)").

Secondly, you can customize search forms to meet your needs and place them in different areas of your website.

For example, if youâre an online store owner, you can create a search form limited to a specific product category and put it into a showcase blog post.

![search form for products](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-for-products.png)This way, you can motivate your customers to search for your products and boost your sales.

On the other hand, if youâre a blogger, you can create and add separate search forms for pages and blog posts to your site.

Itâll help you clean up search results on your site and make it easier for your audience to find the right content for them.

Ready to add multiple search forms to your site? Letâs talk about how to do this below.

### How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your Site

The easiest way to add multiple search forms to your site is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It is the best WordPress plugin on the market that allows you to fully customize your siteâs search.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Over 50,000 site owners already use it to improve their search and deliver more relevant search results to their visitors.

Using this plugin, you can add and use multiple different search forms even if youâre not a tech-savvy person.

Plus, with SearchWP, you can make custom fields, tags, categories, and many other valuable posts attributes searchable on your site.

Now, letâs see how you can use it to add different search forms to your site.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, youâll need to [get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP today") by visiting the website and signing up for an account.

Next, youâll need to sign in to your SearchWP account and download the plugin.

To do that, simply navigate to the âDownloadsâ tab within the dashboard. Then, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin files on your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)After downloading, copy your plugin license key in the bottom left corner on the same tab.

The next step is to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site and install the plugin.

If you need help with that, you can check out the [how to install a WordPress plugin step-by-step guide](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Next, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Now, youâre ready to move on to the next step and create additional search engines.

### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engines

To get started, you can navigate to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. You can manage your search engines, customize them, and add new ones on this page.

From here, simply click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see a new window with the default sources for the search engine, which include posts, pages, and [media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files").

Go ahead and select which sources the search engine should consider when finding content or products on your site. For example, you can limit search to posts or pages.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Donât forget to click the âDoneâ button when youâre finished.

Next, you can add different attributes to each source in the search engine.

Attributes are the fields you want to search for any source, such as title, content, excerpt, slug, [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), taxonomy, and author.

Simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button to continue.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, you will see a new window open where you can manage the search attributes and add custom fields and taxonomies as attributes.

For example, letâs say you have an [ACF custom field](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") for âIngredientsâ on a food blog, which youâd like to include when searching blog posts.

Similarly, if you have multiple categories and tags on your site, then you can also make them searchable by selecting them from the âTaxonomiesâ dropdown menu.

![ACF field in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/acf-field-in-searchwp-1030x516.png)When youâve added attributes, click the âDoneâ button.

Besides that, you can also change the relevance weights assigned to each attribute for different post types by moving the slider. The further right the slider, the more weight that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

SearchWP will automatically assign weights for the default attributes like title and content. This will work for most websites in general, but you can always change the weights according to your requirements.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)When youâve finished adjusting the relevance weights, simply scroll to the top.

After that, click the **Save** button to store your changes.

![Save engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-engine-settings-1030x523.png)Youâve just changed the settings of the **Default** search engine, which is associated with every existing search form on your site.

Next, letâs see how to add more search engines to your WordPress site.

### Step 3: Create an Additional Search Engine

If you want to add multiple search forms with different sets of search settings, then youâll need to create additional search engines.

To do that, simply head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and click the **Add New** button.

![Add new engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-engine-1030x485.png)Next, youâll see the Sources window for the new engine. It will be called âSupplemental engineâ by default, but you can change the name under the Engine Label field.

After that, you can select which sources the new engine will use for the searching process. For example, weâll just select âCategoriesâ as a source for this search engine.

![Change new engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-new-engine-settings-1-1030x598.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

From here, you can adjust the weights of the attributes by moving the slider and also edit the attributes for your source. Simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button to continue.

![Edit attributes for new engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-attributes-for-new-engine-1030x469.png)A new popup window will now open.

Here you can choose attributes for your source and add any custom field to the search process. After selecting the attributes, simply click the âDoneâ button.

![Change attribute settings for categories](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-attribute-settings-for-categories-1030x418.png)Once the new engine is added, you will need to scroll to the top and click the âSaveâ button.

After that, youâll also need to click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)Now, you have 2 search engines which are configured separately. You can repeat this process for create as many engines as you want in SearchWP.

The best part is that it allows you to link the search engines to specific search forms on your site. This way, you can create individual search forms with a unique set of settings and place them anywhere on your site.

Letâs take a look at how to create a search form using SearchWP.

### How to Create Multiple Search Forms in WordPress

Using SearchWP, you can create various custom search forms to meet your needs.

First, youâll need to head to **SearchWP Â» Search Forms** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âAdd Newâ button.

![How to make WordPress search media files: Create Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step1-1-1030x376.jpg)Next, you can choose a theme for your search form and also edit the name of the search form at the top.

SearchWP shows a live preview of how the layout will look like, so you can select the best option.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step2-2-1030x574.jpg)After that, youâll need to scroll down to the Custom Styling section.

From here, click the **Engine** dropdown menu and select the search engine that youâd like to link with the search form.

![Select search engine for search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-search-engine-for-search-form-1030x586.png)If you scroll down, then you will see more options to customize the appearance of your search form.

When youâre done, simply click the âSaveâ button at the top.

![Save the search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step5-1030x574.jpg)For more information, check out our step-by-step tutorial on [how to create a WordPress search form for custom post types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-wordpress-search-form-for-custom-post-types/ "How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types").

Now, you can repeat this process to create multiple search forms for your website.

Next, youâll need to embed the search form on your site. Letâs look at some common places where you can show search forms.

**1. Add a Search Form to Your Navigational Menu**

The navigation menu is one of the best places to put a search form on your site.

Since this is the most visible part of your site, visitors are less likely to miss it.

By placing the form in the header, you can be sure that they wonât encounter any problems with searching for content on your site.

![View search in FSE](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/view-search-in-FSE-1030x565.png)For more details, check out our step-by-step guide on [how to add a search bar to the WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-search-bar-in-wordpress/#how-to-add-a-search-bar-to-wordpress-menu "How to Add a Search Bar in WordPress (3 Ways)").

**2. Add a Search Form to Your Sidebar**

The sidebar is another excellent place to put a search form on your site. Thatâs a specific area where you can display information other than your main content.

By placing a search form in your siteâs sidebar, you help visitors notice it and encourage them to perform a search.

![put a search form to the sidebar](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/put-a-search-form-to-the-sidebar.png)**3. Add a Search Form Inside Posts and Pages**

Adding a search bar to your posts or pages is a great way to help visitors explore your site.

For example, if youâre an online store owner, then you can add it to the showcase article.

This way, your customers can immediately search for products without being distracted from browsing the showcase.

If you are a blogger, then by adding a search form to your post, you can suggest your readers see more information related to the articleâs topic.

![put a search form inside the post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/put-a-search-form-inside-the-post.png)For more details, see our guide on [how to add a WordPress search bar with a shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-wordpress-search-bar-with-shortcode/ "How to Add a WordPress Search Bar with Shortcode (Step-by-Step)") in a few simple steps.

### Bonus: What Type of Custom Search Forms Can You Create? 

After youâve created search engines and multiple search forms, letâs take a look at different use cases for creating search forms in WordPress.

**1. Create a Custom Post Type Search Form**

Custom post types are super popular among WordPress users. You can use them to publish content beyond regular posts or pages.

For example, it might be reviews, recipes, special deals, real estate listings, or something else.

The best way to help your visitors find custom posts is to add a separate search form limited to them.

Hereâs an example of such a form on the WPBeginner site that allows you to search for coupons:

![custom search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/custom-search-form.png)**2. Create a Category Search Form**

Adding a category search form to your site will allow your visitors to choose by which category they want to search.

This way, they can get more relevant search results and find what theyâre looking for faster.

![search by category form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-by-category-form.gif)For more details, see our guide on [how to search by category in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce").

**3. Create a Custom Page and Post Search Form**

By default, WordPress performs a search among all the posts and pages that match the search query you have on your site.

But what if you donât want some of them to appear in the search results?

For example, you may have posts with confidential information, paid content, or pages that shouldnât appear in search results (like the checkout page, sitemap, contacts, and others).

The best way to solve this problem is to add a custom page and post search form.

This way, you can exclude specific content from search results so that it doesnât get in the way of your visitors.

For more details, check out our detailed tutorial on [how to exclude pages from site search results in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress").

We hope this article helped you learn about how to add multiple search forms to your WordPress site. You may also want to see our guide on [how to add authors in WordPress search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results") and [how to change the search results order in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today!") today!

**Categories:** Tutorials

**Tags:** add multiple search forms in WordPress, add multiple search forms to your WordPress site, how to add multiple search forms in WordPress, how to add multiple search forms to a WordPress site, how to add multiple search forms to your WordPress site

### [How to Set Up Synonym Rules for WordPress Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-synonym-rules-for-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** March 4, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Imagine your visitors are searching for something on your website. They type in a word, but the website uses a different word for the same thing, and users get no results.

It can be annoying, right?

This happens with WordPress search. The default search is okay, but it is not very smart about words that mean the same thing. For example, if someone searches for âautomobileâ on your site, but you use âcars,â then they might miss some of your great content.

Thatâs why using synonyms in WordPress search provides a great user experience. Users can find what they need, even if they use slightly different words. And the easiest way to do that is by using a plugin like SearchWP.

In this article, weâll show you how to set up synonym rules for WordPress search.

### Why Include Synonyms in WordPress Search?

Synonyms are simply words that mean the same or almost the same thing. For instance, âbigâ and âlargeâ are synonyms of each other, just like âfastâ and âquickâ can be used interchangeably.

When WordPress search understands synonyms, itâs like it understands different names for the same content on your website.

Why is this important for your WordPress site?

Well, people search using different words. If someone searches for âpicturesâ but you only use the word âimagesâ on your site, then they might not find what they are looking for.

But if your search knows that pictures and images are synonyms, then it will show them the right results. This is a good thing for several reasons.

When people find what they need more easily, it makes them happy and encourages them to stay on your site longer. They might even become long-term customers or regular readers. Plus, it also makes your website content easier to discover.

That said, letâs look at how you can allow WordPress search to include synonyms.

### Setting Up Synonyms Rule in WordPress Search

The default WordPress search doesnât understand synonyms and wonât know if two words mean the same thing. It is also not that great at finding content on your site because it doesnât include other key elements like categories, tags, custom fields, product details, documents, and more in the search process.

This is where [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") comes in handy. It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and you can easily include different synonyms for search terms.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)Plus, it lets you customize WordPress search by creating your own search engine and including different sources. You can also include custom fields, taxonomies, product details like SKUs, and much more to enhance the search experience on your site.

You can start by visiting the SearchWP website and signing up for an account.

After youâve subscribed to a pricing plan and created a new account, the next step is to download the plugin files.

You can find the plugin by logging into your SearchWP account area. Simply go over to the âDownloadsâ tab and click the âDownload SearchWPâ button.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to log into your WordPress admin area and upload SearchWP to your site.

If you need help, then you can follow this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

From here, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Youâve now fully set up the SearchWP plugin on your WordPress site. The next step is to customize your siteâs search engine and synonyms for your search terms.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and then switch to the âGlobal Rulesâ tab.

![Global rules SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/global-rules-searchwp-1-1030x474.png)You can then click the âAdd Newâ button under the Synonyms section.

From here, simply enter the Search Terms and their respective Synonyms.

![Add new synonyms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-synonyms-1030x421.png)When entering synonyms, think about the words people use to search for things on your site.

For example, if you sell clothes, then you might use words like shirt, tee, or T-shirt. Or if you write about cooking on a food blog, then you could use synonyms like ârecipeâ for cooking instructions or food preparation.

**Note:** The SearchWP synonym feature currently works with only single words, such as âshirtâ or âinvestigationâ. It doesnât work with multiple words or phrases right now. So when youâre setting up a synonym rule, please ensure that you only enter a specific term.

![Enter search terms and synonyms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-search-terms-and-synonyms-1030x490.png)### Change the Default WordPress Search Settings

The default WordPress search works fine for some websites, but itâs far from perfect. If your website has a ton of content or your online store has loads of products, then you might need to [change the default search settings](http://search settingshttps://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-wordpress-search-settings-for-best-results/ "How to Set Up WordPress Search Settings for Best Results").

With SearchWP, you can easily customize your WordPress search without editing code. The best part about using SearchWP is that it supports multiple search engines on 1 site. This way, you can create different search engines, allowing your users to search for things in different ways in different site locations.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. After that, you can click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see a new window with the default sources for the search engine, which include posts, pages, and [media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files").

You can simply select which sources SearchWP should consider when finding content or products on your site. For example, if you want to limit search to specific post types, then you can only choose âPostsâ as the source.

![Choose search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-sources--1030x441.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

After that, you can choose attributes for each source in the search engine. Attributes are the fields you want to search for a given source, such as title, content, excerpt, slug, [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), categories, tags, and author.

Simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button to continue.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)From here, you will see a new popup open where you can manage the search attributes.

You can now add custom fields and taxonomies as attributes. For example, letâs say you have an [ACF custom field](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") for ingredients for a food blog or number of bedrooms for a real-estate listing, which youâd like to include when searching blog posts.

Similarly, if you have different categories and tags on your site, then you can also make them searchable by selecting them from the âTaxonomiesâ dropdown menu.

![ACF field in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/acf-field-in-searchwp-1-1030x516.png)Go ahead and click the âDoneâ button when youâre finished.

Next, you can change the weights assigned to each attribute for different post types by moving the slider. The plugin will initially set weights for the main attributes like the title, content, slug, excerpt, and [author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (5 Steps)") to the best values for most websites. But you can move the slider to adjust the weights according to your requirements.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)When youâre done, you can scroll to the top and click the Save button.

After that, youâll also need to click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)SearchWP will automatically start building a new search index for your site.

If you want to learn more, then please see our guide on [how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)").

Next, you can test if your synonym rules are working. Simply use the search bar to search for something using one of your synonyms.

If your synonym rules are set up correctly, then you should see the same search results for all the words you set up earlier. For example, we entered âinvestigationâ as the synonym for the search term âkeywordâ and got the correct result on our demo site.

![Synonyms preview search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/synonyms-preview-search--1030x509.png)### Bonus: Add Partial Matches (Fuzzy Search) to WordPress

Want to provide an even better search experience to your visitors in WordPress?

With partial matches or fuzzy search, you can help users find the right content on your site even if they make a spelling mistake or typo when entering the search term.

Sadly, WordPress does not support fuzzy search by default. If your visitors type a word or a product name incorrectly, then they wonât get any results and will leave your site unsatisfied.

But with SearchWP, itâs very easy to set up partial matches. You can simply enable the âPartial Matchesâ option with 1-click in the SearchWP settings.

![Enable partial matches and closest match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-matches-and-closest-match-1030x573.png)For more details, please see our guide on [how to enable fuzzy search on your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites").

We hope this article helped you learn how to set up synonym rules for WordPress search. You may also want to see our guide on [how to customize your WordPress siteâs search widget ](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-wordpress-search-widget/ "How to Customize the Search Widget in WordPress (Easy Way)")and [how to add a modal search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-modal-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How to Add a Modal Search Form in WordPress (No Coding)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? [Get started with SearchWP today!](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today")

**Categories:** Tutorials

**Tags:** default wordpress search settings, partial matches, same words search, SearchWP, synonym rule, synonyms, wordpress fuzzy search, WordPress search, wordpress search settings

### [Ivory Search vs SearchWP: Search Plugins Compared (2025)](https://searchwp.com/ivory-search-vs-searchwp/)

**Published:** February 27, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Choosing the right search plugin for your WordPress site is an essential step in delivering a great user experience for your website visitors.

It helps replace the default WordPress search and help users find exactly what theyâre looking for.

However, selecting between SearchWP and Ivory Search can be tricky. These are among the best search plugins for WordPress and offer different features.

In this article, we made a detailed comparison of Ivory Search vs SearchWP and compared their features, ease of use, quality of support, and pricing plans to help you choose the best one.

Short on time? Hereâs a quick breakdown of SearchWP and Ivory Search, so you can quickly decide which is the best.

**ð¥** **[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP")**ð¥ **[Ivory Search](https://ivorysearch.com/ "Ivory Search")****Features**Create advanced search engines, eCommerce integration, search tracking & analytics, result control, live autocomplete, & moreMultiple search forms, media search, WooCommerce compatible, content exclusion, live AJAX search, and more**Ease of Use**Beginner-friendly, intuitive, easy to navigate and customizeSlightly overwhelming interface initially, can be less intuitive for beginners**Customer Support**Fast ticket system, comprehensive knowledge base & resourcesTiered support with email for premium users, forum for free users, and knowledge base**Pricing**Premium plans starting for $99/yearFree + Paid plans starting from $19.99/year**Best For**Complete search plugin with powerful features & customization capabilitiesSuitable for simpler needs or those on a budget### SearchWP vs Ivory Search: Overview

 First, letâs take a brief look at both SearchWP and Ivory Search to get a better understanding of what they are.

**SearchWP â Overview**

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is a powerful and easy-to-use plugin which lets you take complete control of your search.

It has an intuitive interface that allows you to quickly customize how search works on your site, even if youâre new to WordPress.

With Search WP, you can choose where WordPress should search for matches, [manage the order of search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/ "How to Change the Search Results Order in WordPress (No Coding)"), make tags, categories, and custom fields searchable, and many more.

Over 50,000 WordPress website owners already use SearchWP to enhance their search and deliver more relevant content to their visitors.

**Ivory Search â Overview**

[![Ivory Search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/ivory-search-1-1030x524.png)](https://ivorysearch.com/)[Ivory Search](https://ivorysearch.com/ "Ivory Search") is another WordPress search plugin that allows you to make your search smarter and improve your content visibility.

Itâs easy to use and has plenty of features to customize your search to meet your business needs.

For example, using Ivory Search, you can create unlimited search forms, exclude specific pages from search results, make WordPress search for media files, and more.

Ivory Search is trusted by over thousands of WordPress site owners and has a free version with limited functionality.

## SearchWP vs Ivory Search: Features

After weâve got acquainted with the plugins, letâs take a look at their features they have to offer and see which one is the best solution for your business.

**SearchWP â Features**

SearchWP is packed with powerful features which you can use to enhance the search experience on your website, deliver more relevant search results to your visitors, and grow your business.

**1. Advanced Search**

First, with SearchWP, you can make almost everything on your site searchable.

This list includes titles, content, slugs, tags, categories, custom fields, and other source attributes.

![Choose taxonomies for search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-taxonomies-for-search-engine-1030x591.png)While comparing the two plugins, we found that you can [make WordPress search PDF files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") and documents that you upload to your site.

Secondly, using SearchWP, you can control where and how WordPress should look for matches.

For example, you can limit search results to specific sources, such as posts and [exclude other sources](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") like pages from the search process.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude All Pages/Posts Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Exclude-all-pages-1030x440.jpg)Besides that, you can make WordPress consider different attributes like page titles and slugs when performing a search.

You can also edit the relevance weight of various search source attributes to control which ones WordPress should give the highest priority.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)Plus, you can add rules to your search engine to automatically exclude content from search results.

For example, there are options to exclude entries if they are from specific categories or tags. You can also show search results based on published date.

![Rule to exclude taxonomies](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/rule-to-exclude-taxonomies-1030x506.png)SearchWP also allows you to add multiple search engines that you can link to specific search forms on your site.

This way, you can create different custom search engines to meet your business needs and deliver the most relevant results to your visitors.

![Add new search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-search-engine-1030x593.png)**2. eCommerce Integration**

Another SearchWP feature that sets it apart from Ivory Search is the seamless eCommerce integration. It works with WooCommerce, Easy Digital Downloads, and BigCommerce.

Due to it, you can make search on your store smart and efficient in a few clicks.

For example, you can [make WooCommerce product attributes searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-woocommerce-product-attributes/ "How to Search By WooCommerce Product Attributes") to make it easier for your customers to find the right products.

![How to Add WooCommerce Product Search Widget: customize attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-product-attribute-1030x567.jpg)Or, you can limit search on your site to WooCommerce products only to clean up your search results from redundant pages.

Itâll help your customers to find what theyâre looking for faster and increase the number of your sales.

**3. Search Tracking**

What impressed us the most when comparing SearchWP was that it gives you detailed reports on your visitorsâ search behavior and lets you see [what theyâre looking for](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site") on your site.

![track searches of your visitors](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/track-searches-of-your-visitors.png)Using this valuable information, you can adjust your marketing and content strategy to grow your business and drive more traffic from search engines.

For example, you can see which search results are the most popular, how many searches visitors make on average, and many more.

**4. Control Over the Search Results Order**

With SearchWP, you can manually edit the order of search results on your site.

It allows you to prioritize specific posts, pages, or products to promote them and get more pageviews.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results n WordPress: Configure Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step4-1030x557.jpg)You can also exclude specific posts or pages from search results with a single click.

SearchWP offers an Exclude UI extension that gives you an option inside the WordPress block editor to exclude the page or post from search.

![exclude specific posts from search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-specific-posts-from-search.png)Thanks to these SearchWP features, you get better control over your search results and give your visitors more accurate matches.

**5. Live Autocomplete Search**

Another awesome SearchWP feature is a [live autocomplete](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites") search. With it, visitors can get instant search results and faster navigate your site.

![live search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/live-search-results-1.gif)It improves their user experience and encourages them to make more searches and view more of your content.

**6. Automatic Theme Integration**

SearchWP automatically integrates with your search results page and every search form existing on your site.

Itâs also fully compatible with the most popular page builders, such as [Elementor](https://searchwp.com/how-to-use-searchwp-with-elementor-to-boost-your-site-search/ "How to Use SearchWP with Elementor to Boost Your Site Search (4 Ways)"), [Divi](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)"), Beaver Builder and more.

Due to that, you can start customizing your search immediately after installing the plugin, regardless of what WordPress theme you use.

Thatâs very helpful for beginners and allows you to give your visitors a better search experience without writing a single line of code.

**Ivory Search â Features**

After getting familiar with SearchWP features, letâs look at Ivory Search features and compare them with SearchWP.

**1. Unlimited Search Forms**

One of the similarities we found between SearchWP and Ivory Search is that you can [create unlimited multiple search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-multiple-search-forms-to-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Add Multiple Search Forms to Your WordPress Site (No Coding)") and place them across your site using shortcodes.

![add multiple search forms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-multiple-search-forms.png)This is a useful feature that helps you to boost your content discoverability and encourages your visitors to search more.

For example, if you own a food blog, then you can add a customized search form that only searches for recipes.

Or, if youâre an eCommerce store owner, then you can quickly increase your sales by putting a customized search form in your showcase posts.

**2. Search Images, Media, Attachments & Files**

Another thing you can do with Ivory Search is to enable on your site search for various [media files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files"), post attachments, PDFs, and office documents.

![make media files searchable](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-media-files-searchable.png)WordPress doesnât consider media files by default when performing searches, so this is a good opportunity to enrich your search results.

**3. WooCommerce Compatibility**

Like SearchWP, Ivory Search also has a WooCommerce integration allowing you to ensure [better product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)") for your visitors.

![manage your WooCommerce products search settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/mange-your-WooCommerce-products-search-settings.png)Thanks to this feature, you can make product taxonomies such as tags and categories searchable, [enable search by SKU](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), and more.

Since a robust search is essential for every online store to succeed online, this feature is invaluable for WooCommerce site owners.

**4. Exclude Anything From Search**

Another feature offered by Ivory Search that youâll also find in SearchWP is that it gives you the ability to exclude specific posts, pages, or categories from search results on your site in a couple of clicks.

![choose searchable categories](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-searchable-categories.png)This is a super helpful feature, as it allows you to get rid of redundant pages in search results and help your visitors find the right content faster.

Unlike SearchWP, Ivory Search doesnât offer an option to set up rules for excluding post types and taxonomies when searching for content.

**5. Live AJAX Search**

Ivory Search also allows you to display search results on your site in real-time, just like SearchWP.

![manage your AJAX search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/manage-your-AJAX-search-results.png)In addition, you can [customize how the search results look](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page"). For example, you can manage the length of the description, add or remove images, author info, and more.

**Winner:** After going through the features offered by both the plugins, SearchWP is the clear winner. Not only does it offer all the features offered by Ivory Search but makes them super simple to use. Plus, you get to create your own custom search engines, multiple search forms, seamless integrations, and powerful extensions in SearchWP.

### Ivory Search vs SearchWP: Ease of Use

One of the key points to consider when choosing a plugin is how easy it is to set up and use.

Letâs compare how easy it is to use SearchWP and Ivory Search, and see which plugin is most beginner-friendly.

**SearchWP â Ease of Use**

SearchWP has a simple and modern-looking interface that you can easily navigate, even if youâre a WordPress beginner.

All plugin features are intuitively grouped, allowing you to customize how search engine on your site in just a few clicks.

On the **Engines** page, you can easily manage the settings of your search engines, edit the search sources and attributes, add rules, and more.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)SearchWP also lets you manage a list of your stopwords that WordPress will ignore when performing searches to give more relevant search results.

These help refine your search process and improve the accuracy of the search results.

![Enter stopwords for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-stopwords-for-search-results-1030x590.png)In the plugin settings, you can also enable and disable various advanced SearchWP features.

For example, there is an option for [partial matches or fuzzy search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites"), which allows your visitors to find the right content even if they make typos or misspellings in the search query.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: enable partial match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-search-1030x532.jpg)To sum up, SearchWP is super easy to use and allows you to quickly enhance your siteâs search without being technically savvy.

**Ivory Search â Ease of Use**

Ivory Search has a slightly overwhelming interface, which can be difficult for beginners to navigate at first.

![edit your search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-your-search-form.png)But after you spend some time studying it, youâll see that it has almost the same set of settings as SearchWP.

For example, in the **Search** tab, you can configure what types of posts you want WordPress to search among, manage searchable attributes, and more.

![make WordPress search for media files](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-WordPress-search-for-media-files.png)Similarly, you can choose specific posts, pages, or categories you want to [exclude from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") on your site.

In the **Design** tab, you can use a built-in search form customizer to change the appearance of your search form. While this feature is good for basic editing, it lacks the form customization options offered by SearchWP.

![customize your live search form](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customize-your-live-search-form.png)You can also enable live AJAX search on your site and manage how it works in the AJAX tab. For example, you can choose which page parts should be shown in search results, edit the length of the search result description, and more.

Finally, you can enable and disable some advanced features, like keyword stemming, fuzzy matching, search term highlighting, and more.

**Winner:** In terms of ease of use, SearchWP offers a more clean and beginner friendly interface compared to Ivory Search. From experienced WordPress users to new comers, everyone can easily find all the options in SearchWP and make changes to customize the search process.

### SearchWP vs Ivory Search: Customer Support

Another important point worth paying attention to is the pluginâs customer service.

The quality and speed of support responses directly affects how effectively you can use the plugin.

With that said, letâs see which plugin has the best support service, SearchWP or Ivory Search.

**SearchWP â Customer Support**

SearchWP has an outstanding support team that you can easily contact via the ticket system right from your WordPress dashboard.

The average response time is only a few hours, so you can count on getting help very quickly.

Plus, there are SearchWP [knowledge base](https://searchwp.com/documentation/ "SearchWP Documentation") and [blog](https://searchwp.com/blog/ "SearchWP Blog") full of comprehensive guides and tutorials to help you get started.

### Ivory Search vs Search: Pricing

The last but not the least thing to consider when choosing a WordPress search plugin is the price.

SearchWP comes in 3 pricing plans that differ in the number of features and included addons.

- **Pro plan** â License for 3 sites. Costs $199 per year and includes all the standard features plus an expanded set of extensions, including Metrics, Redirects, Custom Results Order, Related Content, eCommerce Integration, and more.
- **Agency plan** â License for an unlimited number of sites. Costs $399 per year and includes all the pro features plus along with Client Management.

All plans include free support through a ticketing system and updates during the license period.

Plus, if for any reason youâre not satisfied with SearchWP, you can get a refund within 14 days without any questions.

**Ivory Search â Pricing Plans**

Ivory Search also comes in 3 pricing plans, among which you can choose.

![Ivory Search pricing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/ivory-search-pricing-1030x562.png)Here is the breakdown of each pricing plan:

- **Pro plan** â License for 1, 5, or 25 sites. Starts from $19.99 per year and includes all features of the Starter plan plus a number of premium features, like managing search results order, adding authors to search results, and more.
- **Pro Plus plan** â License for 1, 5, or 25 sites. Starts from $49.99 per year and includes all features of the Starter plan plus a number of premium features, like managing search results order, adding authors to search results, and more.

Ivory Search also offers a refund within 30 days of purchase if you encounter an issue with the plugin that the support team cannot resolve.

**Winner:** If you just look at the prices, then Ivory Search has an edge over SearchWP as it also offers a free version. That said, the features offered by SearchWP are considerably more powerful and advanced than youâd get in Ivory Search.

### SearchWP vs Ivory Search: Our Verdict 

After comparing the features, ease of use, customer support, and pricing plans of both plugins, we think that [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the best and most versatile WordPress search plugin based on its easy user interface and the number of features.

With this plugin, you can get complete control over search on your site and customize it to meet your business needs regardless of your technical skills.

Namely, using SearchWP, you can:

- And moreâ¦
