### [How to Customize the Search Widget in WordPress (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-wordpress-search-widget/)

**Published:** February 18, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Letâs be honest, the default WordPress search widget isnât exactly a powerhouse and not that great at finding content.

If you want to help your customers find what they need faster, then you need to customize your siteâs search widget. This provides a better user experience and encourages your visitors to spend more time on your site.

Now, there are different ways to make changes to the search widget, but they involve tweaking code or hiring a developer. But you can easily modify the widget with the right WordPress plugin, like SearchWP. It gives you complete control over how search works on your site.

In this article, weâll show you how to customize your WordPress siteâs search widget using a plugin.

### Why Customize Your WordPress Search Widget?

Site search is an important feature people use to navigate websites. In WordPress, you can add a search widget to different locations of your site, like in the sidebar, navigation menu, or within the content. Your site visitors can then type into these search forms to find what they need quickly.

However, the default WordPress search widget is not very good at finding site content. It is slow and only searches certain content types like posts, pages, and media content. Your users canât find a match using other attributes like tags, [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), or custom fields.

![WordPress not giving relevant search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WordPress-not-giving-relevant-search-results.png)This means your site search may exclude relevant results sometimes. Once your site visitors canât find what theyâre looking for, theyâll have to check elsewhere.

Customizing your WordPress search widget will help you avoid losing potential site traffic and sales. Your site visitors can now search everything on your site. As a result, they will get a more accurate and faster search experience.

A custom WordPress search widget allows you to control how your site looks for search results. You can choose additional sources to include in the search process, and also rank attributes higher than the other when searching your site.

For example, you can rank a [PDF fileâs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") excerpt higher than the document content. So, when the user searches for a keyword, WordPress will prioritize matches in the excerpts over the document content.

If you have a WooCommerce store, then customizing your search widget allows shoppers to find accurate matches using product attributes like categories, tags, [SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/ "How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)"), product details, documents, and shipping classes.

That said, letâs see how to customize the WordPress search widget without writing any code.

### Customize WordPress Search Widget on Your Site

Are you a WordPress newbie? Do you want to fully customize your WordPress search widget without hiring a developer?

The easiest way to do this is to use a WordPress plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)As the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), it offers features that make it easy to customize your WordPress search widget. You can choose which sources to include in the search process and which content attributes to check first.

For example, you can use the plugin to[ add search by category to your WordPress site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"). So, site visitors can now find results faster by searching a specific section of your site.

Here are some other interesting features you get:

- **PDF and Office Document Indexing:** SearchWP lets your site users search the content of your PDF files and other text-based documents. This can come in handy if you own a bookstore or any site with lots of documents, making it easy for your visitors to find PDF files.
- **WooCommerce Integration:** The [SearchWP WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/features/woocommerce-integration/ "SearchWP WooCommerce Integration") addon can index product attributes like categories, tags, product type, color, size, product details, and shipping classes. This way, your site visitors can find the products they need even faster.
- **Custom Fields Support:** Custom fields help you store extra data about your site content. Unlike the default WordPress search, SearchWP can search custom fields.

Ready to create your first WordPress custom search widget? To get started, simply follow these steps:

Letâs show you how to do it!

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for an account.

After youâve subscribed to a pricing plan and created a new account, the next step is to download the plugin files.

Youâll find the plugin by logging into your SearchWP account area by going over to the âDownloadsâ tab and clicking the **Download SearchWP** button.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to log into your WordPress admin area and install SearchWP to your site.

If you need help, then you can follow this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Once youâve now fully set up the SearchWP plugin, the next step is to adjust your search engine settings.

### Step 2: Set Up Your Search Engine

SearchWP allows you to control how your site looks for content by customizing the search engine on your site. You can change the default WordPress search or create new search engines.

You can then choose from different content sources like posts, pages, products, and the [media library](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files"). Not only that, but you can also decide how relevant you want each source attribute like title and slug to be.

Another benefit of using SearchWP is that you can set up more than 1 search engine for a site. This means you can display different search bars in different locations on your site. For example, you can add a search engine that looks for products only on your shop page and add another search engine that searches your entire site.

First, you will need to head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, youâll see a pop-up with a list of sources and additional options. You can choose from sources like posts, products, comments, and media files. The available options may vary from site to site, based on the plugins on your site.

After selecting your settings, you can also select some additional options. For example, you can enable keyword stemming.

Keyword stemming helps your site to search for words similar to what the user is looking for. If itâs enabled, your site will also search for words like ârunningâ when the user types ârunâ. After choosing the options you want, simply click **Done**.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)Next, itâs time to decide which content attributes you want your site to consider when searching for content.

You can adjust these settings for each source youâve added to the search engine. Simply scroll to the source you want to edit and click **Add/Remove Attributes**.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Youâll now see a pop-up with a list of attributes for each source. By default, SearchWP will look for results in the title, content, slug, excerpt, and [author](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (5 Steps)").

You can remove any of these attributes by unchecking the box next to it. Or you can add other advanced attributes like custom fields and taxonomies. These attributes can be categories, tags, or even product variations like color and model.

![Add or remove attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-or-remove-attributes-1030x493.png)If you select these attributes, your site visitors can now find products easily by simply typing any attribute into the search bar. When youâre finished, click the **Done** button.

The next thing is to add other specific rules to your search engine settings. For example, you can decide to only search for pages published after a particular date.

To do this, simply click the **Edit Rules** button.

![Edit rules for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-for-search-results-1030x512.png)On the next page, you can adjust the ruleâs settings based on your needs.

Go ahead and click the **Add Rule** button.

![Add new search rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-search-rule-1030x385.png)After that, you can enter the conditions for your new rule.

Letâs say you want to select whether to show or exclude entries for posts published before a certain date, display results from specific categories, or enter post IDs to filter content.

![Create new rule for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-new-rule-for-search-results-1030x491.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Finally, itâs time to adjust how your search engine ranks each of the content attributes you added earlier.

For example, you can choose to rank a pageâs slug higher than the excerpt. When SearchWP is looking for search results, itâll first check the slug before checking the excerpt.

Each attribute has its own slider. You can adjust each of them to determine the attributeâs relevance.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)Youâve successfully chosen your content sources, added attributes, and adjusted their relevance.

You can now click **Save** to apply your new settings and then click the Rebuild Index button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)After customizing your WordPress search settings, the last step is to add a widget to your site.

### Step 3: Add a Search Widget to Your Site

You can add your new custom search widget to different locations of your WordPress site.

Some WordPress themes have a search widget in some locations by default. In this tutorial, weâll show you how to add one to your sidebar. Itâs easy for site visitors to find the widget in this location.

You can add a new widget to your site by going to **Appearance Â» Widgets** in your WordPress dashboard.

![Go to widgets tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-widgets-tab.jpg)Next, you can choose where to add the widget and click the **+** icon.

Weâll select the **Sidebar** option for this tutorial.

![Add WordPress sidebar widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Add-WordPress-sidebar-widget-1-e1630949949509.png)Next, you will need to a âSearchâ widget block to your sidebar.

Simply click the â+â button and choose the **Search** block.

![Add customized search widget to WordPress sidebar
](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-bar-to-WordPress-sidebar.png)You should now see the search block in your sidebar widget area.

Now, click **Update** in the top right corner to save all your changes.

Now, when visitors go to your pages or posts, theyâll see your new custom search widget. This widget will search your site based on your new custom settings.

![View WordPress search widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Customized-WordPress-search-widget-in-sidebar.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Do you want to create custom search forms for your website? With SearchWP, you can create multiple search forms for your website.

It offers different layouts to choose from and customize its appearance to match your website theme and brand.

![WordPress search widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/naming-search-form-1030x644.jpg)You can also define which engine your custom search form will use, as SearchWP lets you set up multiple Engines to find content.

To learn more, please see our guide step-by-step guide on [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

We hope this article helped you learn how to customize the search widget in WordPress. You may also want to see our guides on [how to add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/ "How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)") and[ how to add autocomplete search to your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? [Get started with SearchWP today](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get started with SearchWP today!")!

### [How to Perform a Multisite Search in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-perform-a-multisite-search-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** February 6, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Have you ever found yourself browsing through a bunch of websites and wished you could just perform a multisite search across all of them at once?

Itâs a common challenge, especially if youâre using WordPress Multisite network. The regular WordPress search only works for individual sites, which makes it a real pain for your website visitors to find that specific piece in your network.

However, with the right plugin like SearchWP, you can easily set up a search functionality across multiple sites in the network. Plus, you also get to create custom search engines and replace the default WordPress search for better user experience.

In this article, weâll show you how to perform multisite search in WordPress.

### What is WordPress Multisite?

A WordPress multisite is a fantastic feature that allows you to create and manage a network of websites from a single WordPress installation.

Think of it like having a central control panel where you can manage many websites, all sharing the same core WordPress files, plugins, and [themes](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)").

This can save you a lot of time and server resources. Plus, it lets you manage users across multiple sites from the admin network, which is a great way to scale a network.

For example, letâs say you run a university website with different departments or campuses. Or your business has several products, services, or branches. You can set up a WordPress multisite network for each department, product, or campus instead of creating separate sites.

If you want to learn more about multisite, then please see this guide on [how to install and setup WordPress multisite network](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wp-tutorials/how-to-install-and-setup-wordpress-multisite-network/ "How to Install and Setup WordPress Multisite Network").

### Why Set Up a Multisite Search in WordPress?

Imagine you have a network of 20+ sites. If someone wants to find something across the entire network, theyâd have to search each site individually. Frustrating, right?

Thatâs where a multisite search comes to the rescue.

- **Improved User Experience:** Visitors get instant access to information across your entire network. No more clicking through multiple sites. Itâs all in one place, making it fast and efficient.
- **Enhanced Content Discovery:** Users often stumble upon relevant content they never knew existed. A multisite search increases the chance that theyâll discover new content and engage with your network more.
- **Efficiency for Network Managers:** As a network manager, [finding your content](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-blogs-searchable/ "How To Make WordPress Blogs Searchable (For Beginners)"), pages, and articles across the network should be easy. A unified search allows you to find things quickly and easily, allowing for better site management.
- **Consistency and Branding:** A shared search will give a consistent branding experience to your users as they move across your network.

That said, letâs look at how you can easily add a multisite search to your network without editing code.

### Setting Up a WordPress Multisite Search 

The easiest way to add a multisite search feature to WordPress is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that indexes content, custom fields, and taxonomies across your entire network or individual sub-sites.

With SearchWP, you can also create a custom search engine, live search, exclude pages and posts, get search analytics, create custom search forms, and much more.

To get started, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for an account.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)After creating an account, the next thing youâll need to do is download the SearchWP plugin.

Simply head to your account area and switch to the **Downloads** tab. From here, you can click the âDownload SearchWPâ button and save the plugin file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Next, you will need to install and activate the plugin on the [multisite network](https://searchwp.com/features/wordpress-multisite-search-plugin/ "Smart WordPress Multisite Search").

To do that, simply open the Network Admin dashboard and then head to **Plugins Â» Add New Plugin**. From here, you can click the âUpload Pluginâ button at the top.

![Upload plugin in wordpress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/upload-plugin-in-wordpress-1030x576.png)After that, you will see an option to upload your new plugin.

Go ahead and click the âChoose Fileâ button and then select the plugin files on your computer.

![Click choose file option](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-choose-file-option-1030x351.png)Once thatâs done, you can click the âInstall Nowâ button.

On the next screen, you will need to click the âNetwork Activateâ button. This will activate the SearchWP plugin on your multisite network.

![Activate plugin for network](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/activate-plugin-for-network-1030x395.png)After that, you will need to activate the SearchWP license on all the sites in the multisite network.

To do that, simply go to the **SearchWP Â» Settings** from the WordPress dashboard and enter the license key.

You can find the license key in your SearchWP account area. Go ahead and click the âVerify Keyâ button to continue.

![Pasting License Key in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2-1030x722.jpg)Now, SearchWP will be active on your sub-sites in your WordPress multisite network.

### Enable Cross-Site Searching in Multisite Network

Next, you will need to enable multisite search by customizing the behavior of SearchWP.

By default, SearchWP will only search for posts, pages, and [media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files") on a specific sub-site instead of the multisite network. In order to make it search cross-sites, you will need to modify its behavior.

To do that, you can [create a custom plugin](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/creating-searchwp-customizations-plugin/ "Creating Your SearchWP Customizations Plugin") called SearchWP Customizations plugin and add hooks.

[Hooks](https://searchwp.com/documentation/hooks/) are functions that integrate with SearchWP and allow unique customizations without the need to edit the core plugin files. Plus, your new features will remain in place when you update SearchWP or your website theme.

First, youâll need to create your own plugin by simply open a Notepad or TextEdit file on your computer and enter the following code:

```

### [How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/)

**Published:** February 4, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you manage a blog website that has a lot of content or an online store with many products, then itâs very important to provide a great search experience to your visitors.

When users can find valuable information and products on your site with ease, they are likely to spend more time reading articles and purchasing items. As a result, youâll boost engagement and conversions.

Sadly, the default WordPress search is quite limited. It is slow and doesnât always give relevant results. But you can improve your siteâs search experience by changing the default search settings.

In this post, weâll show you how to change your WordPress siteâs default search settings. You can do this by following 3 simple steps.

### Why Change Default WordPress Search?

The default WordPress search may be enough for some websites, even though itâs limited.

But if you have a large site with lots of content or an eCommerce site with thousands of products, then you may need to customize your search settings.

For example, you may want to allow users to [search by category on your site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/ "How To Add Search By Category In WordPress (For Beginners)"), like this:

![How To Add Search by Category To WordPress: Embed Search Form ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/embed-search-form-step5-1030x436.jpg)Customizing the search feature in WordPress helps you:

- **Make Your Site Search Faster:** As your site content increases, the default search starts to slow down. This can negatively impact your user experience.
- **Control How Your Site Looks for Results:** You can control how your site looks for search results. For example, you may want to[ restrict your WooCommerce product search to title only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/ "How To Restrict WooCommerce Product Search To Title Only") or even add search by tags to your site.
- **Add Extra Features to Your Site Search:** You can boost the search experience by including custom fields, comments, and PDF document content.
- **Make Site Search More Efficient:** Changes to your default search can help you[ fix common WordPress search errors](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/ "How To Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Issues Solved)") like the search box not appearing and make the overall search more efficient.
- **Get Search Statistics:** The default search in WordPress doesnât provide any data on what people are searching for. But with the right plugin, you can get search statistics and get insights about your usersâ behavior.

You can change your default search settings by either adding code to your site or using a WordPress plugin. Using a plugin is very easy and will save you a lot of time.

That said, letâs show you how to change your default search settings in WordPress.

### How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings

The easiest and fastest way to change your default WordPress search settings is to use a WordPress plugin called [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). The plugin helps you to customize your WordPress search without writing any code.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)As the[ best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search"), SearchWP gives you a lot of control over your site search. With the tool, you can add so many search features to your site. It makes your site search faster and also gives more relevant search results.

Here are some of the most important features youâll get to enjoy:

- **Search Everything On Your Site:** Is your site not returning the search results you expect? SearchWP improves your site search. With the plugin, your site will now look for search results in [comments](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-comments-searchable-in-wordpress/ "How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress"), reviews, taxonomies, and even custom fields.
- **Advanced eCommerce Product Search:** The tool also integrates seamlessly with WooCommerce, Easy Digital Downloads, and BigCommerce. So, if you own an eCommerce store, then your site visitors can now find your products faster. SearchWP includes your products using attributes like tags, categories, SKUs, shipping classes, and other details in the search process.
- **Site Search Insights:** One way you can improve your business is by making data-backed decisions. SearchWP tracks your site searches and shows you what your users are looking for. You can use these insights to satisfy their needs better.

SearchWP still packs lots more features. The good thing is once you install the plugin, you can make a lot of changes to your search settings. And you donât need any prior experience to do this.

Letâs show you the 3 easy steps to follow to do this.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

You can start by visiting the [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) website and signing up for an account.

After youâve subscribed to a pricing plan and created a new account, the next step is to download the plugin files.

You can find the plugin by logging into your SearchWP account area. Simply go over to the âDownloadsâ tab and click the âDownload SearchWPâ button.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Now, itâs time to log into your WordPress admin area and upload SearchWP to your site.

Need help doing this? You can follow this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Next, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)Youâve now fully set up the SearchWP plugin, the next step is to customize your siteâs search engine.

### **Step 2: Set Up Your Search Engine**

Want to change your default search settings? You can set up a new search engine or edit the default engine in WordPress.

The cool thing with SearchWP is that it supports multiple search engines on 1 site. This way, your users can search for things in different ways in different site locations.

First, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard

After that, you can click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Youâll now see a new window with the default sources for the search engine, which include posts, pages, and [media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files").

You can simply select which sources SearchWP should consider when finding content or products on your site. For example, if you want to limit search to specific post types, then you can only choose âPostsâ as the source.

![Choose search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-sources--1030x441.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

After that, you can add different attributes to each source in the search engine. Attributes are the fields you want to search for a given source, such as title, content, excerpt, slug, [custom fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"), taxonomy, and author.

Simply click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button to continue.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, you will see a new popup open where you can manage the search attributes.

Here, you can add custom fields and taxonomies as attributes. For example, letâs say you have an [ACF custom field](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/ "How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)") for âReading Timeâ which youâd like to include when searching blog posts.

Similarly, if you have different categories and tags on your site, then you can also make them searchable by selecting them from the âTaxonomiesâ dropdown menu.

![Choose ACF custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-acf-custom-field-1-1030x507.png)Go ahead and click the âDoneâ button when youâre finished.

Next, you can change the weights assigned to each attribute for different post types by moving the slider.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)Finally, you can also add extra rules to your custom WordPress search.

For instance, letâs say you donât want users to find content thatâs old and outdated. You can simply set up a condition to show entries after a specific date.

To do this, simply click the Edit Rules button.

![Edit rules for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-for-search-results-1030x512.png)Next, a new popup window will open where you can edit the rules.

Go ahead and click the âAdd Ruleâ button.

![Add new search rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-search-rule-1030x385.png)After that, you can enter the conditions for your new rule.

For instance, youâll need to select whether to show or exclude entries for posts published before a certain date, display results from specific categories, or enter post IDs to filter content.

![Create new rule for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-new-rule-for-search-results-1-1030x491.png)Simply click the âDoneâ button after entering the rules. Your visitors will now see search results based on the conditional logic rules youâve set.

Next, you will need to click the **Save** button to store the changes to the default search engine, and then click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)SearchWP will automatically start building a new search index for your site.

Congratulations! Youâve successfully changed your default WordPress search settings.

There are some additional options offered by SearchWP to fine tune your search engine.

For instance, if you switch to the âGlobal Rulesâ tab in the Algorithm page, then you can add synonyms and stopwords.

Adding synonyms will help users find relevant content when they enter search terms related to topics on your site. Simply click the âAdd Newâ button and then enter the search terms and their synonyms. When youâre done, simply click the âSave Synonymsâ button.

![Enter synonyms for search terms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-synonyms-for-search-terms-1030x421.png)If you scroll down to the âStopwordsâ section, then you can add common words that are not helpful when searching and do not carry significant meaning in identifying the content. These include words like is, the, an, a, in, at, and if.

Now, SearchWP will already have a library of different stopwords which it will ignore when performing a search. However, you can add more stopwords to improve your search experience.

![Enter stopwords for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-stopwords-for-search-results-1030x590.png)When youâre done, simply click the âSave Stopwordsâ button.

The final step is to make sure your site search works as you want on the front-end.

### Step 3: Test Your New Settings

o test your new changes, you can visit your website and type a search term into the search bar and click **Search**.

From here, you can confirm if the search results reflect your new changes to the default WordPress search settings.

For example, WordPress may still be showing posts published before the specific date we set. In this case, go back to the search engine settings to make sure you saved all your settings properly.

![Confirm changes to default WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Confirm-changes-to-default-WordPress-search-settings.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

Now that you know how to easily change the default search settings in WordPress, you can go an extra step and enhance the search experience for your visitors.

Here are some helpful guides that will help you improve the WordPress search:

- [How To Add Filters To WordPress Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-filters-to-wordpress-search/ "How To Add Filters To WordPress Search")
- [How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress")
- [How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)")
- [How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/ "How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free")
- [How To Highlight Search Terms In WordPress (Without Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-highlight-search-terms-in-wordpress/ "How To Highlight Search Terms In WordPress (Without Coding)")

We hope this article helped you learn how to change your default WordPress search settings. You may also want to see our guide on [how to see search metrics in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-see-search-metrics-in-wordpress/ "How To See Search Metrics In WordPress (Easily)") and [how to make WooCommerce product documents searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-woocommerce-product-documents-searchable/ "How to Make WooCommerce Product Documents Searchable").

But with the right plugin, like SearchWP, you can enhance the search experience for your visitors and boost conversions and engagement on your online store.

A good search function also facilitates a seamless buying process. This improves conversion rates and increases revenue.

Besides that, a poor search experience is a common reason why users abandon a website. When searches fail, visitors are likely to leave. Adding product detail to search keeps visitors on your site for longer, reducing bounce rates.

Having a powerful search feature on your eCommerce store can also help with [product discoverability](https://searchwp.com/how-to-improve-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Improve Product Search in WooCommerce (5 Easy Ways)"), even if a customer doesnât have an exact product in mind. Users might not be aware of all the products that you offer, and a detailed search experience helps them uncover these products.

For example, letâs say you want to include product details on your [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/features/woocommerce-integration/ "WooCommerce Integration ") or [Easy Digital Downloads](https://searchwp.com/features/easy-digital-downloads-edd-integration/ "WooCommerce Integration ") store. Simply navigate to the respective extension or use the search bar at the top to look for the eCommerce extension and then click the âInstallâ button.

![Install WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration-1030x542.jpg)### Edit Search Engine Settings to Include Product Details

Now that youâve enabled the eCommerce extension in SearchWP, the next step is to configure the search engine settings.

You can simply go to the **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** page from your WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Manage product attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/manage-product-attributes--1030x518.png)After selecting product attributes, donât forget to click the âDoneâ button.

SearchWP will now include product details you selected as attributes during the search process. You can also use the slider to adjust the relevance weight. The further right the slider, the more weight that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

### [How to Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search (Easy Way)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-acf-fields-in-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** January 28, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If you use Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) on your site to create custom fields, then you should know that the default WordPress search wonât include them in the searching process. This can be a major roadblock when it comes to providing a smooth user experience.

By making data in custom fields searchable on your site, you give your visitors a new way to search for content and help them to get more relevant search results.

In this article, we will show you how to include ACF fields into search results on your WordPress site with no coding needed.

### Why Include ACF Fields in WordPress Search

[ACF, or Advanced Custom Fields](https://searchwp.com/advanced-custom-fields-acf/ "Advanced Custom Fields and SearchWP"), is one of the most popular WordPress plugins for managing custom fields on your site.

With it, you can easily create and set up custom fields for your posts, users, taxonomy terms, and media.

For example, you can create a [custom field](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)") to include calories and serving sizes on your food blog, show reading time for guides, display number of bedrooms for real estate listings, and more.

Making these fields searchable on your site will make it easier for visitors to find the desired content, since they usually contain a lot of valuable data.

Now, the [default WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-default-search-url-in-wordpress/ "How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress (Safely)") will not include the custom fields in the search process.

With that, letâs see how you can make ACF fields searchable on your site in a few clicks, even if youâre a complete WordPress beginner.

### How to Make ACF Fields Searchable on Your Site

The easiest way to include Advanced Custom Fields in search results on your site is to use the [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/).

It is the [best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") that allows you to drastically enhance search on your site and make ACF fields searchable.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)In addition to ACF, you can also use SearchWP to make any post custom fields, attributes, or taxonomies searchable.

And this is just one of the many SearchWP features that will help you grow your business.

For example, you can use this plugin to [track your visitorsâ searches](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"), limit search to specific post types and categories, change the order of search results, and much more.

Over 50,000 WordPress website owners use SearchWP to deliver their visitors the most relevant search results.

Here are some other amazing things you can do with this plugin:

- **Turn on fuzzy search**: Make WordPress search for partial matches in case there are no exact ones, to keep your visitors from leaving your site unsatisfied.
- **Exclude specific pages from search results**: Get rid of redundant pages in your search results to make it easier for visitors to find the right content.
- **Enable search for PDF files**: Make [PDFs and office document files searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)") on your site, so visitors can quickly find them using a search bar.
- **Add search by categories:** Allow your visitors to choose in which category they want to search for content to give the most relevant search results.

Now, letâs take a step-by-step look at how you can do it.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP on Your Site

The first thing you need to do is visit the [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com "SearchWP") website and sign up for a new account.

Once youâve created a new account, youâll need to download the plugin files from the account area.

Simply head to the âDownloadsâ tab and click the **Download SearchWP** button to save the plugin file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once the download is finished, copy your SearchWP license key on the same tab as well. Youâll need it when installing the plugin on your website.

The next step is to install and activate SearchWP on your site. You can follow the step-by-step instructions on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners") if you require assistance.

Upon activation, a SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard will appear in your WordPress dashboard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After you activate SearchWP on your site, youâre ready to enable search by ACF fields on your site.

### Step 2: Start Customizing Your Search Engine

To get started, you will need to go to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from your WordPress dashboard and click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, a popup window will open with the default sources.

These include posts, pages, and media.

![Choose search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-sources--1030x441.png)However, SearchWP allows you to add an unlimited number of search engines and link them to individual [search forms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)").

This way, you can create fully customized search forms and place them anywhere across your site.

Since you want to make custom ACF fields searchable, you will need to configure the Default search engine.

### Step 3: Make ACF Fields Searchable

To get started, select the type of content you want to make searchable by ACF fields among the **Default** search engine search sources.

It can be the Posts, Pages, or [Media](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-media-files/ "How To Make WordPress Search Media Files") sources.

For example, letâs make an ACF field searchable for posts. Simply head to the Posts section and then click the **Add/Remove Attributes** button.

![Add attribute for post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-attribute-for-post-1030x429.png)Next, you will see a new popup open where you can manage the **Posts** search attributes.

To include ACF fields in your site search, first click the **Custom Fields** field and then choose the ACF field you want to make searchable from the drop-down menu.

![Choose ACF custom field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-acf-custom-field-1-1030x507.png)When youâre finished managing the Posts search source attributes, press **Done** to save your changes.

The last step is to save the changes you made to the Default search engine. You can do that by pressing the **Save** button in the top right corner of your SearchWP dashboard.

Next, you will need to click the **Rebuild Index** button next. Itâll make WordPress to reindex all your posts and check if they have any custom fields.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)After youâve enabled ACF field search on your site, letâs test your new search.

### Step 4: Test Your New Search

To do that, go to your site and search for any content placed within the ACF field you made searchable on your site in the previous step.

For example, on our test site, we included a custom field called **Reading Time** in the search.

Letâs try to find a post named âThe 10 Best WordPress Analytics Pluginâ that takes 15 minutes to read.

As you can see, WordPress will look for the phrase â15 minutesâ in the custom field created with the ACF fields plugin and display relevant posts.

![we found a post by an ACF field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-a-post-by-an-ACF-field.png)### Whatâs Nextâ¦

We hope that this guide helped you learned how to include ACF fields in WordPress search. You may also want to see our guide on [how to search posts by tags in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-posts-by-tags-in-wordpress/ "How to Search Posts by Tags in WordPress (No Coding Needed)") and [how to add a WordPress search bar with a shortcode](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-a-wordpress-search-bar-with-shortcode/ "How to Add a WordPress Search Bar with Shortcode (Step-by-Step)").

Ready to make ACF fields searchable on your site? You can [get started with SearchWP here](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "Get SearchWP Today").

Itâll help your visitors faster find the right content and improve their user experience.

### [How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress (No Code)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-limit-search-to-specific-post-types-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** January 24, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

When it comes to limiting search to specific post types, you might run into a bit of trouble.

Thatâs because it usually requires extensive coding experience, since there is no such feature in WordPress by default.

However, with the right plugin, you can easily filter post types from your website search feature without touching a single line of code or hiring a developer.

In this article, weâll show you the easiest way to limit search on your site to specific post types with no coding needed.

### Why Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress

Limiting searches to certain types of posts on your site brings many advantages.

One of them is it allows you to deliver more relevant [search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/ "How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)") to your visitors, making their user experience better.

For example, if you run a cooking blog, you can limit the search on your site to only recipes to make it easier to browse your site.

![an example of a custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/an-example-of-a-custom-post-type-1.png)In addition, limiting search on your site to a certain post type will help you boost your content discoverability. If visitors quickly find what they were looking for on your site, then it encourages them to explore more of your content.

Similarly, it also increases engagement and encourages users to stay longer on your site. As a result, you might see an improvement in [WordPress SEO](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wordpress-seo/ "Ultimate WordPress SEO Guide for Beginners (Step by Step)").

Besides that, limiting search results can increase your conversion rate. If youâre an eCommerce site owner, then you can restrict each [search on your site to products](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/ "How to Create Product Search in WooCommerce (Step by Step)") only to make it easier to browse your store and increase the revenue.

Now that you know why to limit the search on your site to specific types of posts, letâs see how you can do that.

### How to Limit Search to Specific Post Types in WordPress

The easiest way to limit search to specific post types in WordPress is to use a plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP").

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)SearchWP is the[ best WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") on the market, with over 50,000 active installations.

Using it, you can limit search on your site to specific post types in just a few clicks, no coding needed.

But this is not the only thing you can do with this plugin. In fact, SearchWP allows you to get complete control over how search works on your site.

For example, you can[ prioritize specific search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress"), display search results in live mode, and more.

Here are some other SearchWP features that will help you to make search on your site better:

- [**Make custom fields searchable**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-custom-fields-search/ "How to Add Custom Fields Search in WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"): Use custom post types to publish your content? There is a lot of useful data within their custom fields, which you can make searchable with SearchWP.
- [](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)")**[**Add custom search forms**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)"):** Looking for a way to put a search form inside the post or page? Using SearchWP, you can add a customized search form using the Gutenberg block or shortcode and place it anywhere in your article.
- [**Exclude specific pages from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"): Want to hide some posts or pages from search results on your site? With SearchWP, you can do that with just a few clicks.
- [**Track searches on your site**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/ "How to Track Searches on Your WordPress Site"): Would you like to track your visitorsâ searches? SearchWP collects data on all search activity on your site so that you can learn more about your audience.

Now that you know why using SearchWP is the easiest way to limit search on your site to specific post types, letâs see how you can do that below.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP 

The first step is to head to the [SearchWP website](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") and signup for a new account.

Once youâve subscribed to a pricing plan and create a new account, the next step is to download the plugin files.

You can find the plugin by logging into your SearchWP account area and go over to the **Downloads** tab.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)Once there, you can press the **Download SearchWP** button to begin downloading the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

When youâre finished downloading, copy your license key on the same tab. Youâll need it to activate your SearchWP license a bit further.

The next step is to upload the downloaded file to your site and install the plugin.

Not sure how to do that? You can follow this step-by-step guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

After the plugin installation, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### Step 2: Customize Your Search Engine

Next, you will need to edit the search engine to limit posts on your website.

By default, there is already one search engine called **Default**. You can customize how search works across your site by editing its setting.

For example, you can [exclude from search specific pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress") or categories, adjust the post attributesâ relevance weight, and more.

Plus, SearchWP allows you to add an unlimited number of search engines and link them to separate search fields. This lets you create customized search forms with a unique set of settings and place them anywhere on your site.

Since we want to limit search to specific post types on our entire site, weâll customize the âDefaultâ search engine.

Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard and click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Here, you can select which search sources you want WordPress to consider.

By default, all post types are selected as active sources in SearchWP.

![Default search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/default-search-sources-1030x488.png)To limit search on your site to a specific post type, youâll need to uncheck all other sources except it.

For example, we have a custom post type on our test site called **tutorials**.

Letâs say that we want to limit search to it. To do that, weâll check this source and leave it as the only active one.

Here is how it looks on our test site:

![Select specific post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-specific-post-type-1030x488.png)When youâve finished setting up your search sources, simply click **Done**.

Thatâs it. Now, search on your site is limited to the selected post type.

The last step is to click the **Save** button to store the changes youâve made to the search engine, and then click the **Rebuild Index** button.

![Save and rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-and-rebuild-index-1030x587.png)Now letâs try your new search.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search Limited to Specific Post Types

To do that, simply visit your website and head to the [search form](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-custom-search-in-wordpress-without-a-plugin/ "How to Create Custom Search in WordPress Without a Plugin").

For the purposes of this guide, weâve added a tutorial post type called How to install a WordPress theme in the search bar.

![search for a custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-a-custom-post-type.png)As you can see, there are no results except our custom post type, to which weâve limited search on our site.

The search feature should not show any other post type on the website.

![we found our custom post type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-custom-post-type.png)To make sure that our search is truly limited to the tutorials, we also tried to search for other post types.

For example, we have a regular post on our site called âWhy WordPress is the most powerful CMSâ.

As you can see, we havenât found any results.

![there are no regular posts in the search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/there-are-no-regular-posts-in-the-search-results.png)It means that we successfully limited search on our site to a certain post type.

### Whatâs Nextâ¦

In this tutorial, you learned how to limit search to specific post types in WordPress. Itâll help you to give your visitors more relevant search results and improve their user experience.

We hope this article helped you learn how to limit search to specific post types in WordPress. You may also want to see our guide on [how to customize your WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page") and [how to create a custom search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/ "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page").

In this article, you learned how to limit search to specific post types in WordPress. Itâll help you to give your visitors more relevant search results and improve their user experience.

### [How to Make Divi Search WooCommerce Products (No Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-divi-search-woocommerce-products/)

**Published:** January 10, 2022
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

Want to know how to make Divi search WooCommerce products?

Itâs a problem that every WooCommerce store owner who uses Divi as their primary page builder needs to fix if they want to succeed online.

In this tutorial, weâll show you how to enable search by WooCommerce products on your Divi site in a few simple steps.

Letâs dive into it!

## Why Make Divi Search for WooCommerce Products

The default WordPress search engine is rather weak and isnât the best choice if youâre looking to give your customers the positive user experience.

For example, it doesnât:

- consider product attributes when performing a search
- allow your customers to search by product tags
- pay attention to the product category
- look for partial matches if there are no exact ones
- collect any data on your visitorsâ search activity
- and many moreâ¦

The same goes for Divi search modules, as Divi is a pagebuilder plugin that uses the same default WordPress search engine.

Thankfully, there is a way to make search on your WooCommerce site way smarter and help your visitors find the product theyâre looking for faster.

Letâs look at how you can do that in just a few clicks, with no need for coding or technical skills.

## How to Make Divi Search for WooCommerce Products

The easiest and fastest way to make Divi search for WooCommerce products is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/).

![SearchWP logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-twitter-poster-1.png)

SearchWP is a beginner-friendly and powerful plugin with many features to help you [improve your WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-improve-wordpress-search/).

It allows you not only to make WooCommerce products searchable but fully customize your search for your business needs.

For example, you can limit your search, [enable search by product tags](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-product-tags-in-woocommerce/), prioritize specific products in search results, and many more.

More than 30,000 WordPress website owners use SearchWP to deliver their visitors the most relevant search results.

Here are some other features of this plugin that make it so popular:

- **Search activity analytics**. Get detailed reports on your visitorsâ search behavior to see what search terms they enter, which search results are most popular, and more.
- [**Custom search forms**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/). Add multiple custom search engines and connect them to individual search forms across your site.
- **Control over search results**. Change the order of search results on your site the way you want to promote specific pages and get more pageviews.

With that, letâs talk about how you can use SearchWP to make Divi search for WooCommerce products.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

The first thing you need to do is to [grab your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

Once youâve got your copy of the plugin, go to your SearchWP account and head to the **Downloads** tab.

![go to the Downloads tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-Downloads-tab-28.png)

Then, press the **Download SearchWP** button and download the pluginâs ZIP file to your computer.

![press Download SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Download-SearchWP-23.png)

After downloading, copy the plugin license key on the same tab.

![copy your license key](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-your-license-key-34.png)

Next, youâll need to upload the SearchWP ZIP file to your site and install the plugin. Check out this detailed tutorial on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/) if you need help with that.

After you install the plugin, youâll need to activate your copy with your license key.

To do that, navigate your WordPress dashboard and click **Activate License** under the **SearchWP** button at the top panel.

![click Activate License](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Activate-License-23.png)

Next, paste your SearchWP license key into the **License** field and click **Activate**.

![press the Activate button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-the-Activate-button-2.png)

After you activate your license, the next step is to install the WooCommerce Integration extension.

### Step 2: Install WooCommerce Integration Extension

Using this extension, you can seamlessly integrate SearchWP with WooCommerce and get complete control over your product search.

To install it, go to **SearchWP Â» Extensions** in the left panel of your WordPress dashboard.

![go to SearchWP Extensions](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-SearchWP-Extensions-1.png)

Then, find the **WooCommerce Integration** extension and click **Install**.

![install the WooCommerce Integrations Extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-the-WooCommerce-Integrations-Extension.png)

After you install and activate the extension, youâre ready to start making Divi search WooCommerce products.

### Step 3: Customize Your Search Engine Search Sources

To do that, first, click the **SearchWP** button in the left panel of your dashboard.

![go to SearchWP settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-SearchWP-settings-1.png)

After that, youâll get to the **Engines** tab in the **SearchWP** settings, where you can manage and customize your search engines.

![your search engines](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/your-search-engines-8.png)

A search engine is a set of settings and rules that WordPress follows when performing searches on your site.

With SearchWP, you can fully customize it to meet your business needs, including making Divi search for WooCommerce products.

Plus, you can also [hide specific products from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-hide-woocommerce-product-from-search-results/), [enable search by product SKUs](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/), make WordPress search for particular matches, and many more.

To make WooCommerce products searchable on your Divi site, youâll need to make them an active search source.

To do that, click the **Sources & Settings** button.

![press Sources and Settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Sources-and-Settings-2.png)

Then, tick a checkbox next to the **Products** source to make it active and press **Done** to save your changes.

![make Products as an active search source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-Prodcuts-as-an-active-search-source.png)

Now your Divi site will search for WooCommerce products.

Letâs also make WooCommerce product attributes such as tags, categories, and custom fields searchable to increase their discoverability on your site.

To get started, press the **Add/Remove Attributes** button under the **Products** search source section.

![click Add Remove attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Add-Remove-attributes-11.png)

Then, click on the **Custom Fields** field.

![click on the Custom Fields field](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-the-Custom-Fields-field-5.png)

Next, choose the **Any Meta Key** option from the drop-down menu.

![add the Any Meta Key shortcut](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-the-Any-Meta-Key-shortcut-1.png)

This is a SearchWP shortcut that allows you to automatically make the content of any custom field you have on your site searchable.

The next step is to enable search by product tags and categories.

To do that, click on the **Taxonomies** field and choose two options: **Product Tags** and **Product Categories**.

![add product categories and tags](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-product-categories-and-tags.png)

When youâre finished managing searchable product attributes, press **Done** to save the changes youâve made to the **Products** search source.

![save your attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/save-your-attributes.png)

The final step is to save your customized search engine. You can do that by pressing the **Save Engines** button at the top right corner.

![press Save Engines](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Save-Engines-15.png)

Then, click the **Rebuild Index** button to make WordPress reindex your WooCommerce products and check what attributes they have.

![click Rebuild Index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Rebuild-Index-12.png)

Thatâs it. Your Divi WooCommerce search has become much smarter, and now your customers can find the right products more easily.

Letâs create a new Divi search page and add your enhanced search bar to it.

### Step 4: Add Search Widget to Divi Page

To get started, navigate to **Pages Â» Add New** in your WordPress dashboard.

![add a new page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-a-new-page-1.png)

![click Edit with Divi Editor](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Edit-with-Divi-Editor.png)

Next, click **Start Building** under the **Build From Scratch** section.

![press Start Building](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Start-Building-1.png)

![choose the first row layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-first-row-layout.png)

After that, you can choose the module you want to add to your page. Search for the **Search** module and click on it.

![add the search module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-the-search-module-1.png)

Then, press the **green checkmark** button to save your new module.

![click the green checkmark icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-green-checkmar-icon.png)

Once youâre happy with how your search page looks, click the **three dot** icon at the bottom of your Divi editor.

![click three dots icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-three-dots-icon.png)

Then, press the **Save** button at the lower right corner of the Divi editor to save your page.

![press Save](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Save-1.png)

After saving the page, the last step is to test your new Divi search.

### Step 5: Test Out Your New Divi Search

To get started, click the **Exit Visual Builder** button at the top of the Divi Editor to go to your page.

![click Exit Visual Builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Exit-Visual-Builder.png)

Next, search for some WooCommerce products that you have in your store. For example, weâll search for **Fancy Coat**.

![press Search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Search-1.png)

As you can see, we found the product using our new Divi search form, as it now searches for WooCommerce products.

![we found our product](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-product-1.png)

Letâs also try to find a product by category since our Divi search, enhanced with SearchWP, has become much smarter.

For example, weâll search for products placed in the **Hats** category.

![search for product by category](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-product-by-category.png)

You can see there are only products from the **Hats** category, which means that weâve successfully made WooCommerce product attributes searchable.

![we found products by category](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-products-by-category.png)

From this guide, you learned how to make Divi search WooCommerce products.

Itâll allow you to create beautiful pages and themes for your WooCommerce store using Divi without losing customers.
