### [How to Create a Divi Search Results Page Template (For Newbies)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-divi-search-results-page-template/)

**Published:** January 10, 2025
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

By creating a custom search results page, you can deliver more relevant search results to your visitors and encourage them to explore more of your content.

One of the easiest ways to do that is to use Divi. This powerful WordPress page builder plugin that allows you to create a custom search results page without being tech-savvy.

However, the default WordPress search has limited features. To improve the search performance, you can combine Divi with SearchWP.

In this article, weâll show you how you can create a Divi search results page template.

## Why Create a Divi Custom Search Results Page Template?

When visitors use the search bar on your site, they expect to see relevant results.

If they see a blank page after entering a search query, thereâs a good chance theyâll leave your site right away.

Thankfully, you can easily avoid such situations by customizing your [search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-search-page-in-wordpress/ "How to Create a Search Page in WordPress (2 Simple Ways)").

You can change the appearance of your search results page to make it user-friendly and more aligned with your websiteâs needs.

For instance, you can manage the number of results on the page, [specify information about the postsâ authors](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-authors-in-wordpress-search-results/ "How to Add Authors in WordPress Search Results (5 Steps)"), add a newsletter signup form, and much more.

![custom search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/custm-search-results-page.png)Beyond that, creating a custom search results page will also help you to:

- Boost your content discoverability
- Increase the number of views per visit
- Reduce your siteâs bounce rate

Now that you know why you need to create a custom search results page on your site, letâs talk about how you can do that next.

## How to Create a Search Results Page with Divi and SearchWP

The easiest way to create your own unique search results page is to use a WordPress page builder plugin like Divi.

And if you also want to make search on your site smarter, you can use Divi together with SearchWP.

### What is SearchWP and Why Use It?

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") is the most powerful and easy-to-use [WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") on the market.

It allows you to fully customize how search works on your site, even if youâre not a tech-savvy person.

For example, with SearchWP, you can [exclude any pages from search results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/ "How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress"), make post tags searchable, include SKUs for WooCommerce store, and many more.

This way, you can give your visitors more relevant search results and boost your content discoverability.

The best thing is that **SearchWP is fully compatible with Divi** and automatically integrates with your [WordPress theme](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-wordpress-theme/ "How to Create a Custom WordPress Theme (6 Easy Steps)").

After installing the plugin, you can immediately start customizing your search to make it better.

Letâs see how you can use it with Divi to create a search results page.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

To get started, youâll need to visit the [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP") website and signup for an account.

Simply click the âGet SearchWP Nowâ button to continue.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)After creating an account, the next thing to do is download the SearchWP plugin.

Simply head to your account area and switch to the Downloads tab. From here, you can click the **Download SearchWP** button and save the plugin file to your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)The next step is to install and activate the SearchWP plugin on your site. If you need help with that, you can check out the [how to install the WordPress plugin step-by-step guide](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Upon activation, you will see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Simply click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)After the plugin activation, SearchWP automatically integrates with any search field on your site.

This way, you can immediately start delivering more relevant search results to your visitors. You can also check out our guide on [how to customize your WordPress search results page with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-your-wordpress-search-results-page "How to Customize Your WordPress Search Results Page").

Now letâs move on and see how you can create a search results page using the Divi page builder.

### Step 2: Create Your Custom Search Results Page Template

To get started, navigate to **Divi Â» Theme Builder** in your WordPress dashboard.

![go to the theme builder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-the-theme-builder.png)Once you get to the theme builder page, you will see options to edit default website template or add a new one.

Go ahead and click the **Add New Template** option.

![click Add New Template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Add-New-Template.png)After that, you will see different template settings.

Simply tick the box next to the **Search Results** option at the bottom of the drop-down menu and click the âCreate Templateâ button.

![create a search results template](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-a-search-results-template.png)As you can see, our new Search Results page template has now appeared among other templates.

To start customizing it, go ahead and click **Add Custom Body**.

![click Add Custom Body](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Add-Custom-Body.png)Next, Divi will ask you to build a custom body or select from a library.

You can choose the **Build Custom Body** option.

![click Build Custom Body](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Build-Custom-Body.png)Itâll take you to the page where you can select whether you want to build a page from scratch or use one of the pre-made templates.

In this tutorial, weâll build a search results page from scratch, so click the **Start Building** button.

![press Start Building](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Start-Building.png)After that, you can select a row for your future page.

For example, weâll select the first one for this tutorial.

![insert a row](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/insert-a-row.png)Then, you can add the first module to our new page.

There will be several options to pick from, such as bar counters, blog, button, comments, and more.

![insert module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/insert-module.png)To display search results on this page, youâll need to add the Blog module.

To get started, you can click on the **Blog** module.

![choose the Blog module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-the-Blog-module.png)Then, toggle the **Posts for Current Page** switch to Yes.

It ensures that this module will only display search results.

![switch the toggle](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/switch-the-toggle.png)### Step 3: Customize the Search Results Layout

Now you can customize your search results page like any other Divi page.

For example, letâs change the search results layout to Grid.

![go over to the Design tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-over-to-the-Design-tab.png)To do that, go over to the **Design** tab in the Blog module settings

From here, you can click on the âLayoutâ item to expand its settings.

![click Layout](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Layout.png)After that, change the module layout from **Fullwidth** to **Grid**.

When youâre finished, click the button with the checkmark to save the changes you made to the Blog module.

![click the checkmark icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-checkmark-icon.png)Now, letâs also add a heading to our search results page.

To do that, click **Plus** at the bottom of the Blog module section to add a new module.

![click Plus](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Plus-1.png)This will show different modules that you can add to the search page.

Simply search for the **Text** module and click on it to add it to the page.

![click on the text module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-on-the-text-module.png)To change the text within the module, click on it and type whatever you want in the appeared setting window.

For example, weâll change it to âSearch resultsâ for this tutorial.

![edit your text](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-your-text.png)You also get options to change the format of the text.

To transform the text to the heading, click **Paragraph** and choose the **Heading 1** item from the drop-down menu.

![change the text type](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/change-the-text-type.gif)Letâs also align the heading to the center.

To do that, click on the **Center** button.

![align text to the center](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/align-text-to-the-center.png)Donât forget to save the changes when youâre finished editing the heading.

The last step is to change the headingâs position to be above the search results, not below them.

To do that, hover your cursor over the heading until the gray toolbar appears and drag it to the top of the page.

![move your module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-your-module.png)Letâs also add a search bar to our page so that visitors can search again if there are no results for their query.

To get started, click on the **Plus** button that appears when you hover your cursor over your heading.

![click Plus to add a new module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Plus-to-add-a-new-module.png)Then, look up the **Search** module in the search bar.

After that, you can click on it to add it to the page.

![add the Search module](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-the-Search-module.png)Next, you can add text to the search form by entering it in the **Input Placeholder** field.

For example, weâll enter âTry another searchâ text.

![we set the custom placeholder](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-set-the-custom-placeholder.png)When youâre happy with how your search form looks, click the green checkmark button to save the changes you made to it.

Hereâs the final result of what the Divi WP search results page looks like on our test site:

![that's how our search page looks](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/thats-how-our-search-page-looks.png)When youâre done customizing the search results page, youâll need to save it.

To get started, click the purple three-dot button at the bottom of the Divi page editor.

![click the three dots icon](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-three-dots-icon-1.png)You will now see more options appear in the bottom corner of the page.

Go ahead and press the **Save** button to store your new search results page changes.

![press Save to save your changes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-Save-to-save-your-changes.png)The next step is to close the page editor and go back to the **Theme Builder** page.

To do that, simply click the cross in the upper right corner.

![click the cross icon at the top right](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-the-cross-icon-at-the-top-right.png)Next, the page builder will ask you to store your new template.

Once there, you can click **Save Changes** in the top left corner.

![press the Save Changes button](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/press-the-Save-Changes-button.png)The final step is to check out how your search results page looks on your site.

### Step 4: Check Out Your New Search Results Page

The fastest way to do that is to press the **Visit site** button under your siteâs name in the top left corner of your WordPress dashboard.

![click Visit Site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Visit-Site-8.png)Once you get to your site, enter a search query in the search field to see the search results for it.

For example, weâll search for the âWooCommerceâ search term.

![click Search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Search-1.png)After entering what youâre looking for, simply click the âSearchâ button.

As you can see, the search page on our site looks like the way we customized it with Divi.

![the final results of our search page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-final-results-of-our-search-page.png)Thatâs it! Customizing the appearance of your search results page will help you encourage visitors to view more of your content and grow your business.

We hope this article helped you learn how to create a Divi search results page template. You may also want to see our guide on [how to fix WordPress tags not searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-tags-not-searchable/ "How to Fix WordPress Tags Not Searchable (Easy Steps)") and [how to exclude category from WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-category-from-wordpress-search/ "How to Fix WordPress Tags Not Searchable (Easy Steps)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "SearchWP Pricing") today!

### [How to Set Up WordPress Search Settings for Best Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-set-up-wordpress-search-settings-for-best-results/)

**Published:** December 23, 2024
**Author:** Aazim Akhtar

**Content:**

If youâre looking to provide an exceptional user experience, then WordPress search plays a vital role. A clunky search experience can leave users lost and abandoning your site in a flash.

The default WordPress search has very limited features and doesnât provide full control. However, with the right settings and plugin, you can improve your visitorsâ searching experience.

In this article, we will show you how to set up WordPress search settings for best results.

### Why Change WordPress Search Settings?

Think of your WordPress websiteâs search function as a friendly guide. It helps users navigate your content quickly and efficiently.

By fine-tuning the search settings, you can enhance the user experience. A seamless search experience keeps users engaged and encourages them to come back for more.

It also helps users find evergreen and valuable content on your site. Imagine writing a great blog post, but nobody can find it when they search for it on your site.

With the right search settings, you can make the process more efficient and provide visitors with relevant results.

That said, letâs look at how to configure WordPress search for best results.

### 1. Replace Default WordPress Search with SearchWP

While the default WordPress search might be good enough for small businesses and personal websites, it might not be the right fit for large websites that have lots of content or online products.

The default search has limited features and customization options.

For instance, the default search only covers post titles, content, and excerpts. It does not include additional information such as categories, tags, user comments, custom fields, documents, or details like SKUs and product attributes from WooCommerce products.

Plus, you canât customize the default search settings from the WordPress dashboard. Youâd have to edit code or hire a developer, which can be tricky and expensive for beginners.

This is where you can replace the default search with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/ "SearchWP"). It is the [best search plugin for WordPress](https://searchwp.com/best-wordpress-search-plugins-to-boost-your-site-search/ "7 Best WordPress Search Plugins to Boost Your Site Search") and helps provide more accurate search results to visitors.

You can set up custom search engines for your website and include additional information in the search process without touching a single line of code.

First, youâll need to visit the SearchWP website and sign up for an account. Simply click the âGet SearchWP Nowâ button to get started.

[![SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-6-1030x578.png)](https://searchwp.com/)After signing up for a new account, go ahead and download the SearchWP plugin

You can find the plugin in your SearchWP account area under the âDownloadsâ tab. Simply click the âDownload SearchWPâ button and save the file on your computer.

![Download SearchWP plugin from account area](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1-1030x603.jpg)From here, you can install and activate the plugin on your site. If you need help, then please see this guide on [how to install a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/ "How to Install a WordPress Plugin â Step by Step for Beginners").

Once the plugin is active, youâll see the SearchWP welcome screen and setup wizard. Go ahead and click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button and follow the onscreen instructions.

![Onboarding wizard SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/onboarding-wizard-searchwp-1030x591.png)### 2. Add New Sources and Attributes to WordPress Search 

Next, you can [change WordPress search settings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-wordpress-search-settings/ "How to Change the Default WordPress Search Settings (3 Steps)") by editing or creating a new search engine.

With SearchWP, you can run multiple search engines on your site and choose additional sources and attributes to find content on your site.

To start, head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress dashboard. Here, you can edit the existing search engine or add a new one. For this tutorial, we will simply change the default search engine.

![Edit or add new search engines in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-or-add-new-search-engines-in-wordpress-1-1030x545.png)Next, you can click the âSources & Settingsâ button at the top. This will allow you to include additional sources for your WordPress search engine.

For example, you can select [categories](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-in-category-in-woocommerce/ "How to Search in Category in WooCommerce"), comments, tags, users, eCommerce products, and other sources to be included in the search process.

![Choose search sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-search-sources--1030x441.png)After selecting different sources, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Next, you can change the weights assigned to each attribute for different post types by moving the slider.

![Move the slider to assign weights](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/move-the-slider-to-assign-weights-1030x512.png)SearchWP also lets you add or remove attributes.

If you click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button under the source, then youâll see a new popup window with options to manage attributes.

For instance, you can choose different Attribute Sources, select custom fields on your site, and add taxonomies to add to the search process.

![Add or remove attributes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-or-remove-attributes-1030x493.png)### 3. Add Rules to Get the Best WordPress Search Results

Next, you can also add multiple rules in SearchWP to create custom searches and help users find the best results.

Simply click the âEdit Ruleâ button for any source in the search algorithm.

![Edit rules for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-for-search-results-1030x512.png)Next, a new popup window will open where you can edit the rules.

Go ahead and click the âAdd Ruleâ button.

![Add new search rule](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-search-rule-1030x385.png)After that, you can enter the conditions for your new rule.

For instance, you can only show search results for posts and pages published before a certain date, display results from specific categories, or enter post IDs to filter content.

![Create new rule for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-new-rule-for-search-results-1030x491.png)When youâre finished, simply click the âDoneâ button.

Your visitors will now see search results based on the conditional logic rules youâve set.

### 4. Add Synonyms and Stopwords to Improve Search Accuracy 

Another way you can configure WordPress search settings is by adding synonyms for specific search terms and adding stopwords to enhance search accuracy.

This will help users find relevant content when they enter synonyms for related topics on your site.

In SearchWP, you can easily specify synonyms and stopwords. Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Algorithm** from the WordPress admin panel and then go to the âGlobal Rulesâ tab.

![Enter synonyms for search terms](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-synonyms-for-search-terms-1030x421.png)After that, you can click the âAdd Newâ button and then enter the search terms and their synonyms. When youâre done, simply click the âSave Synonymsâ button.

Next, you can scroll down to the Stopwords section and omit certain words to streamline the searching process.

Stopwords are common words (like is, the, an, a, in, at, and if) that are not helpful when searching and do not carry significant meaning in identifying the content.

By default, SearchWP will have a lot of stopwords added to the library and ignore them when searching. But you can also add more to improve search relevancy and performance.

![Enter stopwords for search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enter-stopwords-for-search-results-1030x590.png)### 5. Enable Partial and Fuzzy Searches in WordPress

You can further expand WordPress search and show accurate results by enabling partial and fuzzy searches.

Fuzzy search helps provide search results for terms that are phonetically similar. So, if a user enters an incorrect spelling or makes a typo when typing the search term, then theyâll still see the right content.

With SearchWP, you can easily enable partial and fuzzy searches. Simply head to **SearchWP Â» Settings** from the WordPress admin panel and go to the âGeneralâ tab. From here, scroll down to the General Settings section and click the toggle to enable Partial Matches.

![Enable partial searches](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-searches-1030x516.png)For more details, please see our guide on [how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/ "How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites").

### Bonus: Improve WordPress Search Experience for Visitors

Aside from changing WordPress search settings, you can also improve the overall search experience for your visitors by making small tweaks.

**1. Add Live Autocomplete Search**

Many popular search engines, like Google, offer an autocomplete feature that guesses what the user is searching for and provides relevant results as the user types.

You can add the same autocomplete feature to your WordPress site and boost the search experience. Users can instantly find the content theyâre looking for and also learn about other content on your site.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Live Search Working Proof](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/live-search-sample-5-1030x386.gif)With SearchWP, itâs very easy to add an autocomplete feature to your siteâs search. You can learn more by going through our guide on [how to add live autocomplete search to WordPress sites](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/ "How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites").

**2. Highlight Search Terms in WordPress**

Do you want to point users to the right content quickly and get more clicks?

A simple way to do that is to highlight search terms on the search page. Your visitors can quickly scan the page, find relevant content, and click on the link.

![we highlight search terms on our site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-highlight-search-terms-on-our-site.png)For more details, please see our step-by-step guide on [how to highlight search terms in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-highlight-search-terms-in-wordpress/ "How To Highlight Search Terms In WordPress (Without Coding)").

**3. Add Filters to WordPress Search**

If your website has a lot of content or products, then you can add filters to WordPress search and make it easier for users to find what theyâre looking for.

For example, you can create filters based on tags, authors, post types, product categories, and more. Your visitors can then use these filters to narrow down their search and quickly find relevant posts and pages.

![How To Add Filters To WordPress Search: Embed Filterable Search Form Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Search-form-in-action-1030x368.jpg)If you need help setting up filters, then see our guide on [how to add filters to WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-filters-to-wordpress-search/ "How To Add Filters To WordPress Search").

We hope this article helped you learn about how to set up WordPress search settings for the best results. You may also want to see our guide on [how to include shortcode results in WordPress search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/ "How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search") and [how to see search metrics in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-see-search-metrics-in-wordpress/ "How To See Search Metrics In WordPress (Easily)").

Ready to create a better and more personalized WordPress search experience? Get started with [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/ "SearchWP Pricing") today!

### [How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce (Easily)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-search-by-sku-in-woocommerce/)

**Published:** November 26, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you wondering how to search by SKU in WooCommerce?

If you run a WooCommerce store, you likely know the importance of keeping your product search fast, accurate, and user-friendly.

While the default WooCommerce search does a decent job, it doesnât allow customers to search by SKU (Stock Keeping Unit). This can be a problem, especially if you deal with a large inventory where products are identified by SKUs.

In this article, weâll show you how to customize the WordPress search to enable search by SKU for your WooCommerce store.

**Key Takeaways:**

- SKUs help customers quickly find specific products, especially in stores with a large inventory.
- WooCommerceâs default search doesnât support SKU search; a drawback for large B2B stores.
- SearchWP makes it easy for anyone to add SKU search functionality to your WooCommerce store.

## Why Enable Search By SKU In WooCommerce

The Stock Keeping Unit, popularly known as an SKU, is a unique product identifier that is the backbone of inventory management.

They help store owners, warehouse teams, and customers quickly identify and locate products. However, when SKUs arenât searchable, it creates friction for customers who prefer using these unique identifiers, especially in B2B stores or when dealing with repeat orders. For instance:

Imagine a customer who bought a specific pair of headphones last year. They still have the SKU from their receipt and want to reorder the same product.

If your store doesnât support SKU searches, customers will have to sift through categories or filters, which can lead to frustration and possibly abandoning their purchase.

Adding SKU search ensures:

- Faster product discovery for customers who know what theyâre looking for.
- Improved user experience by offering more search flexibility.
- Reduced support inquiries from customers needing help finding products.

So, letâs see how to configure search by SKU in WooCommerce for your online store.

- [**Exclude content from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** SearchWP lets you filter out irrelevant content, ensuring that customers quickly find what theyâre looking for by removing non-essential results from the search experience.

So letâs see step by step how to enable search by SKU in WordPress using SearchWP.

### Step 1: Install & Activate SearchWP

[Get the SearchWP package](https://searchwp.com/buy/) that fits your needs, as different packages come with different features. After purchasing, navigate to your [Accounts](https://searchwp.com/account/) page and [download](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) the plugin file.

Once activated, youâll see a welcome screen. To begin the onboarding process, click âStart Onboarding Wizard.â This will help you configure the basic settings.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Install SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)Alternatively, you can activate your copy of SearchWP directly by visiting SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Install SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)Paste the license key you copied earlier (in the previous step) into the designated field, then press âVerify Keyâ to complete the activation process.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Install SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)Your licensing information will appear below when the key is verified.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Install SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info.png)Thatâs it. You have successfully configured SearchWP. Now, we can proceed to the next step!

### Step 2: Install & Activate The WooCommerce Extension

In order to enable search by SKU in WooCommerce, we first need to make all WooCommerce products searchable. You can do that by installing and activating the WooCommerce extension of SearchWP.

Therefore, please navigate to SearchWP > Extensions and find WooCommerce Integration from the list. Then, click Install.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Install WooCommerce Integration Extension](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension.jpg)Wait a few moments, and youâll see it successfully installed. From now on, SearchWP have access to all WooCommerce products on your site.

Finally, to enable search by SKU in WooCommerce, we need to configure the search engine. Letâs do that next.

### Step 3: Configure The Search Engine To Enable Search By SKU In WordPress

Now, we need to customize the SearchWP engine to make SKUs searchable. To do that visit SearchWP > Algorithm from the left sidebar of your WordPress dashboard.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchWP-engine-customization-1.jpg)From here, click âSources & Settingsâ to configure the Default search engine.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-setttings.jpg)Youâll see a modal pop-up with a list of sources to choose from. We need to make this search engine index the content of all WooCommerce products. Therefore, select Products from the list. Then, click the âDoneâ button after choosing sources that interest you.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp2.jpg)Now, a new section titled âProductsâ will appear on the search engine block. The engine now indexes only the title, content, slug, and excerpt of products.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-4.jpg)To make it index SKUs, we need to add them as an attribute to the search source. Hence, click on the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button under the Products section.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-5.jpg)This will open up a modal. Type SKU into the âCustom Fieldsâ field and choose the â***\_sku***â option from the drop-down menu.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-6.jpg)When youâre finished, click Done.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-7.jpg)As you can see, our newly added ***\_sku*** attribute currently has the lowest relevance weight.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-8.jpg)It means that WordPress will give it the lowest priority when searching. Since we donât want that to be the case, letâs set its weight to the maximum.

To do so, move the slider next to the ***\_sku*** attribute all the way to the right.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 9](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-9.jpg)Once done, press the âSaveâ button at the top right to save the changes you made.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 10](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-10.jpg)Depending on your specific case, SearchWP may ask you to update indexing. In that case, click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: Configure SKU search in WooCommerce Step 11](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Configure-SKU-search-step-11.jpg)Thatâs it. Now, your site visitors can find products even when they search for them using the product SKU. Letâs test our new WordPress search to make sure everything works fine.

### Step 4: Test Your New Search

Itâs time to test whether everything works properly. To do that, visit your websiteâs front end and perform a search by SKU.

For the purposes of this tutorial, weâve added the âBlue Hoodieâ product that has the âBH7564â SKU.

Letâs see if WordPress can find this product solely by SKU.

To do so, weâll enter the âBH7564â query into the search field on our test site and press Search.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: See search by SKU in action step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-a-prodcut-sku.jpg)As you can see, weâve successfully found the product by its SKU only.

![How To Search By SKU In WooCommerce: See search by SKU in action step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-our-product.jpg)It means that WordPress now indexes the SKU attribute and considers it when performing a search.

## Next Up

### [How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-include-shortcode-results-in-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** November 22, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you wondering how to include shortcode results in WordPress search?

Shortcodes are quite popular among the WordPress community. They allow website owners to show dynamic content in any post, page, or sidebar with a few clicks.

The shortcodes often contain a lot of useful search-oriented content. Unfortunately, the default WordPress search engine doesnât consider it.

In this tutorial, weâll show you step-by-step instructions on how to include shortcode results in WordPress search.

**Key Takeaways**

- Making shortcode content searchable ensures your users find all relevant content, enhancing their experience and engagement.
- Anyone can make shortcode content searchable easily with only a few clicks with the SearchWP plugin.
- Having a good search engine on your site, therefore, improves content discoverability and conversion rate.

## What Are WordPress Shortcodes?

Shortcodes are snippets of code enclosed in square brackets that enable you to add dynamic elements to your sites. Typically, shortcodes consist of a few words and sometimes include additional parameters. For instance:

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Shortcode example](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/shortcode-example-2.jpg)Shortcodes can be placed in posts, pages, or anywhere within the WordPress editor. Their functionality is determined by the active theme or plugins installed on your website.

**Common Use Cases:**

- Displaying image galleries with gallery plugins.
- Adding contact forms with form builder plugins.
- Embedding tables or other interactive elements.

These small snippets provide significant flexibility without requiring advanced technical knowledge. Once placed, WordPress processes the shortcode and renders the appropriate content.

Hereâs an example of how a shortcode from a popular gallery plugin looks in the post editor:

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: How the WordPress shortcode looks like](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-the-wordpress-shortcode-looks-like-1.jpg)And here is what it renders on the website:

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: WordPress gallery displayed by a shortcode](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wordpress-gallery-displayed-by-a-shortcode-1.jpg)The best thing about shortcodes is that you donât even need to be a web developer or tech-savvy person to use them, but it lets you do things that a web developer normally does.

To use them, check the list of available shortcodes supported by your theme or installed plugins. You can usually find them either on the plugin or theme description page or on their official website.

## Why Add Shortcode Results To WordPress Search?

By default, WordPress only indexes the text within the shortcode brackets. Take the following shortcode, for instance:

```
[gallery id="12" columns="4"]
```

The default WordPress search would index this as plain text without any context or meaning. This can be a major limitation, especially if your shortcodes display rich content such as:

- **Galleries**: Indexing image file names, descriptions, and alt text can enhance image discoverability. This is particularly useful for photography portfolios and cooking blogs, where images play a critical role.
- **Page Builder Content**: Many popular page builders rely heavily on shortcodes to create complex layouts. Without indexing this content, visitors may struggle to find relevant pages.
- **Custom Content Elements**: Shortcodes used for testimonials, sliders, or product showcases often hold valuable information that users might search for.

Now you know why including shortcode results in WordPress search is essential. Letâs show you how to do that on your website.

## How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search

To make shortcode content searchable, youâll need a solution that extends WordPressâs default search capabilities. Hereâs where [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) comes in.

It automatically integrates with any WordPress theme and indexes all content instantly. Using SearchWP, you can include shortcode results in WordPress with only one click.

SearchWP is the best search plugin for WordPress that is trusted by over 60,000 businesses globally. It enables you to make almost anything on your website searchable, including shortcodes, documents, and custom fields.

Here are a few other features of SearchWP that make it one of the most powerful WordPress search plugins:

- [**Live Search Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/): Improve user experience with real-time search suggestions as users type. This feature accelerates the search process, making it easier for customers to find what theyâre looking for and boosting engagement and conversions.
- [**PDF & Document Content Search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/): Make PDFs and various document types, such as Office files, fully searchable. This feature is invaluable for WooCommerce stores that offer product manuals, guides, or specifications, allowing users to find needed information without having to download and open each file.
- [**Customizable Search Rankings**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/): Adjust search result rankings to highlight priority content, such as new products or special promotions. This feature ensures that the most relevant results align with your business goals.
- [**Customizable Search Results Page**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/): Personalize your search results page to better serve your visitorsâ needs. Enhance the layout, add filters, or display additional product details, making the search experience more user-friendly and informative.
- [**Search Insights and Analytics**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): Analyze search query patterns to understand visitor preferences. This insight helps tailor your content strategy, ensuring that your site aligns with user interests and increases overall engagement.

Letâs take a look at how you can include shortcode results in a WordPress search.

### Step 1: Install & Activate SearchWP

First, you need to [get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) from the official website. After purchasing, log in to your account and download the plugin Zip file from the [Downloads](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) section of your profile.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1.jpg)Copy your license key from the same page, as youâll need it later to activate the plugin.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step2.jpg)Using the plugin zip file, you can now [install & activate the SearchWP plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/) like any other WordPress plugin.

Once you activate the plugin, youâll see a welcome screen to start your onboarding process. Click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button. This will guide you through the initial setup.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)Alternatively, you can activate SearchWP directly by visiting SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)Paste your copied key in the âLicense Keyâ field and click âVerify Key.â

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)If everything worked smoothly, youâll see the verification confirmed and your package information displayed.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Install SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg)Congratulations! You have successfully installed SearchWP on your website. Now, youâre ready to include shortcode results in WordPress search.

### Step 2: Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search

SearchWP lets you include shortcode content in search results on your website with only one click. From your website backend, you just need to navigate to SearchWP > Settings.

On this page, scroll down to find âParse Shortcodes,â then turn it on.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Configure the settings for showing shortcode content in search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/make-shortcode-searchable.jpg)Thatâs it. Youâve successfully added shortcode results to the WordPress search! From now on, when a user performs a search on your website, they will find relevant content, even if itâs rendered from a shortcode.

### Step 3: Test Your New Search Results

To test the improved WordPress search results, you need to leave the dashboard and go to the frontend of your website. Then, type anything related to a shortcode you have used into the search bar.

For example, for this tutorial, weâve created a gallery with soup pictures. Letâs check if WordPress search can find this gallery.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Final search.](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wordpress-found-an-article-1.jpg)As you can see, everything works great. WordPress found an article that contains a picture of the shrimp soup.

![How To Include Shortcode Results In WordPress Search: Final output. ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/the-soup-gallery.jpg)This is a great result, given that the word âshrimpâ is not mentioned either in the title or in the articleâs content.

This works because WordPress now indexes the imagesâ alt text, even if theyâre placed inside a gallery output by a shortcode.

### [How To Highlight Search Terms In WordPress (Without Coding)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-highlight-search-terms-in-wordpress/)

**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to highlight search terms in WordPress search results?

This will make it easier for your visitors to find exactly what theyâre looking for on your site. Highlighting search terms in your siteâs search results can be a game changer.

By doing this, youâre guiding your visitors to their desired content faster, which can lead to more page views and longer session durations. Ultimately, this small but powerful tweak can boost user satisfaction and keep them coming back for more.

## Why Highlight Search Terms In WordPress Search Results

Highlighting search terms in search results on your site can be beneficial for several reasons.

Firstly, an average website visitor reads only [20% of the information](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/how-little-do-users-read/) on a page. Instead of studying it carefully, they prefer to quickly scan the page until their eye catches the information theyâre looking for.

The search results page is no exception. By highlighting search terms on it, you can help your visitors understand faster if theyâve found the right content.

![we highlight search terms on our site](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-highlight-search-terms-on-our-site.png)Secondly, once your visitors realize that your site has a powerful search, itâll encourage them to explore it further.

This way, beyond helping your visitors, you can also [boost the number of pageviews you get](https://optinmonster.com/how-to-increase-pageviews-on-your-website/) and increase your siteâs average session duration.

Since the average session duration is one of the major ranking factors in Google, you can also expect to improve your siteâs ranking.

Now that you have learned why you should highlight search terms in your siteâs search results, letâs see how you can do that.

## How To Highlight Search Terms In Your Site Search Results

The easiest way to highlight search terms in WordPress sites without coding is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/). Itâs the best WordPress search plugin on the market, with over 60,000 active installations.

### [How To See Search Metrics In WordPress (Easily)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-see-search-metrics-in-wordpress/)

**Content:**

Do you want to see search metrics in WordPress?

Understanding what your visitors are searching for on your website is invaluable. These insights reveal what users are looking for and how well your content meets their needs.

You can make data-driven decisions that increase engagement, improve content relevance, and maximize conversions by accessing the search metrics.

However, the default WordPress search doesnât have the ability to provide search metrics. Therefore, weâll show you how to see search metrics in WordPress. So that you can make informed decisions to grow your business.

**Key Takeaways:**

- WordPress doesnât provide search insights by default.
- SearchWP provides insightful search metrics to help you make informed decisions.
- Itâs very easy for anyone to set up SearchWP and see search metrics in WordPress.

## Why You Need Search Metrics For Your WordPress Site

Understanding search behavior is like gaining a behind-the-scenes look into your visitorsâ minds. Hereâs why tracking search metrics is beneficial for your business:

- **Understanding User Intent**: Search metrics help reveal user interests, preferred topics, and pain points. If users repeatedly search for âhow-to guides,â âtutorials,â or âbeginner content,â this points to a need for educational resources.
- **Identifying Content Gaps**: Search data exposes whatâs missing on your site. For example, if users frequently search for âevent calendarsâ and you donât offer one, this signals an opportunity to expand content or integrate a new feature.
- **Improving Site Navigation**: Common search terms can highlight areas where site navigation might need adjustment. If users consistently search for content already accessible from the menu bar, you may want to make these links more prominent.
- **Driving Conversions**: In eCommerce, tracking product-specific searches helps you optimize popular items, improve product visibility, and create promotions around highly sought-after products.
- **Enhancing User Engagement**: If you know what content visitors are most interested in, you can prioritize these topics, ensuring that your content resonates and keeps users on your site longer.

## How To See Search Metrics In WordPress

Now that you have a solid understanding of why search metrics are crucial for your online business. Letâs show you how to set up search metrics in WordPress. To do that, you need to install SearchWP on your website.

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) is the most powerful search engine for WordPress. Itâs trusted by over 60,000 businesses worldwide. With SearchWP, you gain full visibility into your siteâs search behavior, allowing you to track the search terms and patterns that drive user engagement.

Here are a few other notable features that SearchWP brings to your website:

This includes title, slug, excerpt, keywords, etc. You can set rules as well to control what to show and what not.

This feature is ideal for highlighting related products, documents, or blog posts, guiding users to explore your site further.
- [**Arrange Search Results By Priority**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** SearchWP enables you to adjust the order of search results so you can feature products or pages that align with your business objectives. This feature allows you to highlight popular products, new arrivals, or featured items, ensuring they appear first for relevant searches and capture usersâ attention right away.

If your WooCommerce store offers digital assets such as product manuals, specifications, or guides, customers can find what they need quickly within the search itselfâno downloads required.

This streamlined experience reduces search time, leading customers straight to the content or products they want, ultimately encouraging more clicks and engagement.

So make sure to choose responsibly.

![How to See Search Metrics in WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step2.jpg)You can install and activate SearchWP as [you would any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Once activated, follow the on-screen steps to complete the setup by selecting âStart Onboarding Wizard.â

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg)Congratulations! You have successfully installed SearchWP on your site.

## How To See Search Metrics In WordPress: Final Configuration 

Before you can start seeing search metrics in WordPress, youâll need to install the Metrics extension of SearchWP. To do that, navigate to SearchWP > Extensions.

![How to See Search Metrics in WordPress: Install SearchWP Metrics Extension: Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/setup-Metrics-extension-step1-2.jpg)On this page, youâll find all the extensions offered by SearchWP. Each extension is designed to serve a specific purpose. Look for Metrics in this list. Click âInstallâ when you find it.

![How to See Search Metrics in WordPress: Install SearchWP Metrics Extension: Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/setup-Metrics-extension-step1.jpg)Now, wait a few seconds, and itâll activate automatically.

![How to See Search Metrics in WordPress: Install SearchWP Metrics Extension: Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/setup-Metrics-extension-step2.jpg)Once the Metrics extension is installed, refresh (reload) your website by pressing CTRL + R on Windows or CMD + R on MacOS. You have successfully activated the Metrics extension. You can access it by visiting SearchWP > Metrics.

![How to See Search Metrics in WordPress: See search metrics in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/final-searchwp-metics.jpg)Hereâs a brief description of what you can expect from the SearchWP Metrics:

1. **Metrics Dashboard**: The Metrics landing page displays a list of search terms, the frequency of each term, timestamps, search trends, and search results from different engines for comparison.
2. **Filter By Date Range**: The date filter helps you analyze search trends over specific periods. By adjusting this range, you can identify seasonal interests, emerging trends, or changing patterns in user behavior.
3. **Identify Popular Search Terms**: The Popular Search card on the dashboard orders search terms by popularity, for each engine, giving you a quick insight into trending topics; click âView Moreâ to see the complete list. Make a note of any high-frequency terms, as these represent your most sought-after content or products.
4. **Address Zero Result Searches**: The zero result searches are also logged in SearchWP. These are queries for which your site has no relevant content. Itâs a great opportunity to investigate if you lack content on these topics or if users are misspelling. In the latter case, you can leverage the [Spell Correction](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/) feature.
5. **Get Actionable Insights:** The dashboard comes with an âInsightsâ section for each engine. Itâs where SearchWP gives you practical suggestions that you can implement to improve your siteâs search and overall user experience.

## Practical Tips On Growing Your Business Using Search Metrics

SearchWP Metrics provides valuable insights by revealing exactly what your audience wants. Using these insights, you can create a more dynamic and user-friendly website. Here are a few proven ways to use search metrics to craft an effective business strategy that drives growth:

- **Refine Site Navigation Based On Search Data:** If certain topics that are already available on your site, such as âpricing plans,â appear frequently in searches, it may indicate that users find these resources hard to locate. Assess the visibility of these links within your navigation menu and make them more accessible to create a smoother, user-friendly experience.
- **Highlight Products That Draw Attention:** For eCommerce stores, search metrics help pinpoint popular product searches. For instance, if you notice frequent searches for âeco-friendly mugs,â consider adding more options for that product or promoting it on your homepage to capture interested buyers. Adjusting product visibility based on search interest can significantly enhance conversions.
- **Discover SEO Opportunities With Popular Keywords:** Search metrics also reveal keywords users are interested in but may not be addressed in your content. For example, if âremote work tipsâ is a frequent search term, make sure this term appears in your content titles or descriptions. Adjusting your SEO strategy to include these keywords helps attract organic traffic from users seeking these specific topics.
- **Spot Seasonal & Trending Content Needs:** Search metrics provide insight into seasonal and trending topics, helping you prepare content in advance. For example, if winter searches for âholiday recipesâ peak each year, create and promote holiday recipe content ahead of the season. Likewise, if âAI toolsâ is increasingly popular, a guide on this emerging topic can position you as a resource for cutting-edge information.
- **Offer Related Content Recommendations**: Use search data to create custom recommendations for high-interest topics. For instance, if âhealthy meal prepâ is popular, link related articles on meal planning or nutrition within relevant posts using [SearchWPâs related content feature](https://searchwp.com/how-to-show-related-content-in-wordpress/) to keep users engaged.

## Next Up

You will gain a clear view of your audienceâs search behaviors with SearchWPâs search metrics. This is invaluable for tailoring your site to better meet their needs. By periodically identifying popular searches, addressing content gaps, and making strategic decisions, you can build a sustainable business that grows over time.

### [How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress (Safely)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-default-search-url-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** October 18, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to change the default search URL in WordPress?

When users perform a search, WordPress generates a URL that looks something like this: *`http://example.com/?s=search-term`*.

Many website owners prefer to change the format as it is hard to read.

You can improve the readability of your websiteâs search URL by changing the default slug to something cleaner, like `http://example.com/search/search-term`.

Therefore, in this article, weâll show you how to change the default search URL in WordPress safely.

**Key Takeaways:**

- The default WordPress search URL is difficult to read and not user friendly.
- Having a cleaner URL helps search engines better understand your site and rank your content appropriately.
- You can safely change the default search URL slug without editing core files using WPCode.

## Why Change The Default Search URL In WordPress?

By default, WordPress uses a user-friendly URL structure for most pages and posts. For example, pages may look like this:

- *`http://example.com/post-url/`*
- *`http://example.com/2018/03/post-url/`*
- *`http://example.com/category/post-url/`*

These URLs are clean, easy to understand, and provide useful information to both your site visitors and search engines.

However, when a user performs a search on your website, WordPress provides the result on a page with a complex URL format:

- *`http://example.com/?s=search-term.`*

As you can see, the *â?s=â* in the URL may confuse both users and search engines. Here are a few reasons why changing this default search URL slug can be beneficial:

1. **SEO Benefits:** According to some experts, search engines like Google prefer clean URLs because they provide better context for a page. For example, a URL like *`http://example.com/search/shoes`* is more SEO-friendly than *`http://example.com/?s=shoes`*. A clean search slug helps search engines index and rank your pages more effectively.
2. **Improved User Experience:** Clear, readable URLs are easier for users to remember and navigate. A URL like `http://example.com/search/best-laptops` is more professional and user-friendly than the default ?s=best-laptops.
3. **Brand Consistency:** Clean URLs keep your brand image polished. Untidy URLs can diminish the professionalism of your site, while a clean search slug reinforces your brandâs quality and trustworthiness.

For instance, imagine a customer is searching for âsmartphonesâ on an online store, if the search results page URL is *`http://example.com/search/smartphones`,* it becomes easy for them to remember and share the URL with friends and family.

## How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress

To change the default search URL in WordPress, we need to add custom code to your websiteâs codebase. There are different ways to do that.

You can add code to your website by using a separate plugin or FTP. While one is safer than the other, weâll show you both in this tutorial. However, please follow one of the following methods.

### Method 1: How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress Using WPCode (Recommended)

The easiest and safest way to add code to your site is by using custom code snippets.

Code snippets are short, reusable pieces of code that you can add to your website to customize its functionality or introduce new features, all without modifying your theme, plugins, or core WordPress files.

Now, the best way to add code snippets to your website is to use [WPCode](https://wordpress.org/plugins/insert-headers-and-footers/). Itâs the most popular code management plugin for WordPress. It inserts code into your website without modifying the actual codebase.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: WPCode Plugin in WordPress plugin repository](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WPCode-WP-Org-Repository-Thumbnail.jpg)Using WPCode, anyone, from beginners to experts, can easily add, edit, and manage code snippets to WordPress with its user-friendly interface. Not to mention, itâs completely free!

Here are a few key benefits of using WPCode:

- [**Curated Snippet Library**](https://library.wpcode.com/): WPCode provides a vast library of pre-approved snippets, carefully vetted by the WPCode team, so you can easily enhance your siteâs functionality without needing additional plugins.
- [**Custom Insertion Options**](https://wpcode.com/introducing-wpcode-218/#:~:text=2%20New%20Auto%20Insert%20Methods%20For%20Better%20Content%20Insertion): WPCode gives you full control over snippet placement. You can choose exactly where the code appears, whether after a certain number of words in a post or on a specific page.
- [**Advanced Conditional Logic**](https://wpcode.com/introducing-wpcode-218/): WPCodeâs smart conditional logic allows you to set precise rules for when and where snippets should be applied based on factors like user roles, post types, or taxonomy terms.
- [**Supports Multiple Code Types**](https://wpcode.com/docs/what-type-of-snippet-do-i-need/): Whether you need to add HTML, PHP, JavaScript, CSS, or even text snippets, WPCode has you covered. Snippets can be inserted via shortcodes or automatically.
- [**Built-in Error Handling**](https://wpcode.com/docs/php-error-handling-safe-mode/): If an issue arises, WPCodeâs safe mode lets you access your site for quick fixes. Error logs are available to help you diagnose and resolve any problems.

To add code snippets to your site thatâll change the default search URL in WordPress, please install [WPCode](https://wordpress.org/plugins/insert-headers-and-footers/). [You can set it up like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

If your installation is successful, youâll see a new menu item called Code Snippets appear in the left sidebar. Now, please navigate to Code Snippets > Add New.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code snippet step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-snippet-WPCode.jpg)On this page, youâll see various code snippets. Each of these can be used to add a particular feature to your website. Since weâll be adding custom code, please hover over the âAdd Your Custom Codeâ card and click on the âAdd Custom Snippetâ button.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code snippet step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-code-to-wpcode-step1.jpg)Now, give your snippet a proper name, and choose the Code Type as âPHP Snippetâ. Then, paste the following code. You can change the keyword from â/search/â to anything else you prefer.

```

function custom_search_url_rewrite() {
    if ( is_search() && ! empty( $_GET['s'] ) ) {
        wp_redirect( home_url( "/search/" ) . urlencode( get_query_var( 's' ) ) );
        exit();
    }
}
add_action( 'template_redirect', 'custom_search_url_rewrite' );
```

Since weâll only change the visitor-facing URL, letâs load this snippet to only frontend. To do that, from the Insertion section, choose âAuto Insertâ as the Insert Method and âFrontendâ as the Location.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code snippet step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-code-to-wpcode-step2.jpg)Weâre almost set. Please activate the snippet by turning on the following toggle and clicking âSave Snippetâ to put it into action.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code snippet step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-code-to-wpcode-step3-1030x696.jpg)Thatâs it. You have successfully added the code snippets to change the default search URL in WordPress.

### Method 2: How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress Using FTP

Another way to change the default search URL in WordPress is to edit your siteâs *.htaccess* file and add the code responsible for changing the URL.

However, this approach is more complicated and not advised for beginners. We recommend only advanced users who have worked with FTP before follow this route. This method gives you greater control and does not require additional plugins.

To add code to your *.htaccess* file via FTP, youâll need an FTP client like [FileZilla](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php) to connect to your websiteâs server. Alternatively, you can use other clients like [WinSCP](https://winscp.net/eng/index.php), [Cyberduck](https://cyberduck.io/), or [Transmit](https://panic.com/transmit/), depending on your preference.

Use the credentials provided by your hosting provider to access your server.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code using FTP step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/connet-filezilla-step1.jpg)Once connected to your server, locate and open the *public\_html* folder, where youâll find the *.htaccess* file.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code using FTP step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/connet-filezilla-step2.jpg)Now, download the *.htaccess* file to your computer. Before making any changes, itâs a good idea to keep a backup of the original file in case anything goes wrong; you can use it to undo mistakes.

Next, open the *.htaccess* file in a text editor such as Notepad++ or Sublime Text, and insert the following code at the bottom of the file:

```

# Change the default WordPress search results page URL

RewriteCond %{QUERY_STRING} \\?s=([^&]+) [NC]
RewriteRule ^$ /search/%1/? [NC,R,L]
```

Hereâs what it looks like:

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code using FTP step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/connet-filezilla-step3.jpg)After adding your code, save the file and upload it back to the public\_html folder via FTP, replacing the current *.htaccess* file. In FileZilla, you can do this by simply dragging and dropping the updated file into the window.

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Adding code using FTP step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/connet-filezilla-step4.jpg)Thatâs it! Youâve successfully modified your *.htaccess* file to change the default WordPress search URL.

## See The Changed URL In Action

Now that you have successfully added the code to change the default search URL for your WordPress site, itâs time to see if everythingâs working as expected. To do that, please visit the frontend of your site and perform a search. You should see the following:

![How To Change The Default Search URL In WordPress: Changed URL in action. ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/changed-url-in-action.png)### Whatâs Next?

Now that you have a cleaner search URL slug, itâs time to think about enhancing your search functionality even further. The default WordPress search is functional, but itâs fairly limited.

You can significantly improve your website search by using SearchWP. Itâs the most powerful WordPress search plugin that allows you to customize your siteâs search engine, helping users find the most relevant content faster. Some standout features of SearchWP include:

- [**Custom Search Algorithms**](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/): You can create tailored search rules to promote specific types of content (like posts, pages, or products) that matter most to your audience.
- [**Keyword Stemming**](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/keyword-stemming/): Improve search results by including results for related terms. For example, searching for ârunâ can show results for ârunningâ or ârunnerâ as well.
- [**Search by Category or Tag**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/): This feature allows users to narrow their search results by category or tag, making it easier for them to find what theyâre looking for.
- [**Boost Product Search for WooCommerce**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-product-search-in-woocommerce/): If you run an eCommerce site, SearchWP can dramatically improve your product search, helping customers find exactly what they need with fewer clicks.

## Conclusion

We hope you were able to change the default search URL in WordPress following this tutorial. This simple but powerful tweak improves your siteâs user-friendliness and significantly boosts SEO.

And, if you want to improve your siteâs search functionality, [get SearchWP for your website](https://searchwp.com/buy/) to significantly enhance user experience and boost your business!

**Categories:** General
