### [How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-product-search-to-the-header/)

**Published:** October 7, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to add WooCommerce product search to the header in WordPress?

If you run a WooCommerce store, making it easier for your customers to find products is key to improving conversions.

The search bar is one of the most important tools on any eCommerce site because it helps users quickly locate the products theyâre looking for.

But having your WooCommerce product search buried at the bottom of your page or lost in the sidebars can make this process harder than it needs to be.

Thatâs why it is a great idea to add WooCommerce product search to the header. By making it accessible at all times, youâre streamlining your customersâ shopping experience.

In this article, weâll show you how to add WooCommerce product search to the header in WordPress.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Why adding a product search to the header is important.
- How to improve WooCommerce product search to index all content.
- How to add a WooCommerce product search to the header in WordPress.

## Why Add Product Search To the Header Of Your WordPress Site?

Your websiteâs header is one of the first places visitors look for navigation tools. No matter which page the visitor is on, the header is always visible, so placing the search bar on the header makes it more accessible.

Imagine you run an online electronics store. When a potential customer comes to your homepage after searching for a specific gadget, they see a search bar right at the top of the page. They can type in the item they want and see relevant products right away.

This quick and easy process reduces the chances of the customer leaving for another website. Having a search bar in the header saves time and makes it more likely for the customer to buy, as they are taken directly to the product page they wanted.

Hereâs why adding a WooCommerce product search to the header is necessary:

- **Increased visibility:** Customers expect to see the search functionality always. By placing it on your website header, you can ensure itâs visible on every page of your site.
- **Better user experience:** If customers canât find what theyâre looking for easily, theyâre likely to leave your store. Adding a search bar in the header ensures users have quick access to product searches.
- **Improved conversions:** Search bars can reduce friction in the purchasing process. Customers who can search and find what they need without effort are more likely to convert.

## How To Improve The WooCommerce Product Search

Having powerful search functionality can significantly increase the chance of having a successful online business. The search bar acts like a salesperson for your online store, helping customers quickly find exactly what they need without having to browse through endless product pages.

While the default WooCommerce search feature does an adequate job, it doesnât always index the crucial product data that customers rely onâsuch as product tags, attributes, variations, or even customer reviews. Without these, users may struggle to locate specific items, which can result in missed sales opportunities.

This is where SearchWP comes in handy. Itâs the most advanced search plugin for WordPress, and it expands WooCommerceâs search functionality by allowing it to index important product data, such as categories, tags, descriptions, and attributes.

Here are other key features of SearchWP that make it integral for your online business:

- [**On-site search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): SearchWP enables you to track every search performed on your WooCommerce store, offering invaluable insights into customer behavior. By analyzing this data, you can better understand what products your visitors are looking for and identify opportunities to refine your product offerings or promotions.
- [**PDF and documents searchable**](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-pdf-office-text-documents/): SearchWP makes document files, such as PDFs and other Office documents, fully searchable. If your WooCommerce store includes items like manuals, guides, or product specifications, this ensures customers can easily find the information they need without having to download and open each document manually.
- [**Custom search results order**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/): SearchWP allows you to set a custom order for search results, prioritizing specific products or pages based on their relevance to your business goals. Whether you want to highlight best-selling items, featured content, or new arrivals, this flexibility ensures that visitors see your most important products first.
- [**Live search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/): SearchWPâs live search feature enhances the user experience by displaying search results in real-time as customers type. This instant feedback reduces the time users spend searching, helping them quickly find the products or content theyâre looking for, ultimately boosting engagement and sales.
- [**Custom search results page**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/): With SearchWP, you can fully customize your WooCommerce search results page to better suit your customersâ needs. Whether you want to improve the layout, add filters, or display additional product information, you can do so easily with SearchWP

So, letâs follow the following steps to configure SearchWP for your site.

### Step 1: Installing SearchWP

First, visit the [SearchWP website to grab your copy](https://searchwp.com/buy/) of the plugin. Then, log in to your account and download the plugin zip file from the [Downloads tab of your profile](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/).

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Installing SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step1.jpg)On the same page, copy your license key, which youâll need later.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Installing SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-install-step2.jpg)Now [install & activate the SearchWP plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/) like any other WordPress plugin.

In your WordPress dashboard, youâll see a new menu item called âSearchWP.â Click on it and then âAdd License Key.â

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Installing SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg  )Paste your copied key in the âLicense Keyâ field and click âVerify Key.â

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Installing SearchWP Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)If everything worked smoothly, youâll see the verification confirmed and your package information displayed.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Installing SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg )Congratulations, you have successfully installed SearchWP on your website.

### Step 2: Configuring WooCommerce Integration

Now that SearchWP has been installed and activated successfully, we need to install the [SearchWP WooCommerce extension](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/) to make sure all WooCommerce content can be indexed properly.

You can do that by visiting SearchWP > Extensions, finding the WooCommerce Integration in the list, and clicking âInstall.â

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Configuring WooCommerce Integration](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension.jpg)Youâll see it is successfully installed and activated within a few moments. Doing this will ensure all WooCommerce products are accessible to SearchWP.

Next, weâll show you how to add WooCommerce product search to the header, but before that, you need to configure the search engine.

### Step 3: Customize The Search Engine

Once installed, SearchWP automatically replaces the default WordPress search. Also, it gives you full control over how you want the search engine to find content on your site.

You can select different sources, adjust the relevance of each product or content attribute, and even have [multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

To customize your SearchWP engine, click SearchWP > Algorithm from the left sidebar of your WordPress dashboard.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Configuring SearchWP engine step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchWP-engine-customization-1.jpg)To set up a WooCommerce product search, click âSources & Settings.â

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Configuring SearchWP engine step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-setttings.jpg)A modal will pop up with a list of sources to choose from. Select âProductsâ from the list box. Click âDoneâ after choosing sources that interest you.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Configuring SearchWP engine step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp2.jpg)Then click âSaveâ so that SearchWP can start rebuilding your search index based on the new settings.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Configuring SearchWP engine step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-saving.jpg)Finally, itâs time to display your custom WooCommerce search widget on your website. Letâs talk about how you can do this in the next step below.

## How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header In WordPress

In order to add WooCommerce product search to the header on your site, visit your WordPress admin dashboard. From there, navigate to Appearance > Editor.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step1.jpg)Now, select âNavigationâ from the left side menu.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step2.jpg)Then, click on the three vertical dot icons next to Navigation and select âEditâ to enable customization.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step3.jpg)Youâll see that all menu items are displayed here on this page. To add a new item, click on the â+ Add blockâ icon.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step4.jpg)From this dropdown, click on â+ Add block.â

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step5.jpg)You should see the âProduct Searchâ block listed here. Click on it to select. If you donât see any search block, click on âBrowse allâ to find your desired block.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step6.jpg)This will add WooCommerce product search to the header of your WordPress site. You can customize the search form if needed. Then, click âSaveâ to apply the changes.

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step7.jpg)Finally, visit the frontend of your website to see the newly added WooCommerce product search in action!

![How To Add WooCommerce Product Search To The Header: Adding search bar to the header - final preview](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-wocommerce-search-header-step8.jpg)## Conclusion

We hope you are able to successfully add WooCommerce product search to the header of your WordPress site. Having a search that is always accessible improves both user experience and your conversion rate for your store. And having the advanced features of SearchWP, you can ensure that your customers always find the right products quickly and easily.

### [How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-wordpress-search-form-for-custom-post-types/)

**Published:** September 6, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you wondering how to create a WordPress search form for custom post types?

If your site contains custom post types other than the default post types: pages or posts, WordPress canât search them properly. Because, the default engine only indexes title and content, ignoring crucial information such as meta, taxonomies, etc.

Therefore, a separate search form for custom post types is necessary to properly index important data points. Otherwise, your visitors will not find what theyâre looking for.

So, in this step-by-step tutorial, weâll show you how to create a WordPress search form for custom post types.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Custom post types are what make WordPress so popular.
- WordPress does not index custom post types by default.
- SearchWP makes custom post types, fields, and taxonomies searchable.

## Why Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types?

By default, WordPress comes with two main content typesâposts and pages. However, youâre free to create your own [custom post types](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wp-tutorials/how-to-create-custom-post-types-in-wordpress/) and add any custom fields. This is why WordPress has gained immense popularity and now powers more than [40% of all websites](https://w3techs.com/technologies/details/cm-wordpress) on the internet.

Custom post types are very useful if youâre creating content that doesnât fit within the boundaries of standard posts or pages. They open up endless possibilities for organizing your content in ways that make the most sense for your websiteâs goals.

Simply put, custom post types allow you to turn WordPress from a simple blogging platform into a full-fledged content management system.

For example, if you have a recipe blog, you might want to create a custom post type called âRecipe.â You can even add custom fields like ingredients, cooking methods, and preparation time to make the content more specific and tailored to your audience.

Here is an example of a custom post type at [WPBeginner](https://www.wpbeginner.com/) in their Deals section, where they showcase featured deals to their visitors.

![How to Create a WordPress Search Form for Custom Post Types: Custom Post Type Example](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/deals-custom-posts-type-1.jpg)

Now, why should you create a search form that searches exclusively among certain custom post types? Because you can make it much easier for your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for.

For example, if you add a search form that searches only for the âRecipeâ custom post type. It ensures that visitors who type âchickenâ as a search query will find chicken recipes onlyânot an article, news, or just a page about chicken.

Also, the native WordPress search completely ignores custom fields. This means that even though your visitors can find a chicken recipe, they still canât search for specific meal ingredients.

Thatâs why creating a search form that searches custom post types, including custom fields, is necessary.

So, letâs show you how to create a WordPress search form for custom post types that also considers custom fields when searching.

## How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types

The most efficient way to build a custom search form for WordPress is by using SearchWP. Itâs the most powerful search plugin used by more than 60,000 websites to enhance the default WordPress search functionality.

SearchWP gives you the freedom to control how your siteâs search engine works. It allows you to include or exclude specific post types, taxonomies, and even custom fields in your search results.

Hereâs what you can do with SearchWP:

- [**On-site search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): SearchWP tracks all search queries and provides you with a comprehensive report on your siteâs visitorsâ search behavior.
- [**Seamless WooCommerce integration**](https://searchwp.com/search-ecommerce-product-details-sku/)**:** SearchWP allows you to not only make WooCommerce products searchable but also attributes, tags, reviews, and product media.
- [**Searchable PDFs and documents**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/)**:** SearchWP can make PDFs and other types of office documents searchable, ensuring users get more accurate and comprehensive search results.

Creating a search form for all custom post types is similar, so today, we will create a search form that searches exclusively among the custom post type: Products.

Even if you have other custom post types on your website, the process is exactly the same. So, letâs get started!

### Step 1: Install & Activate SearchWP

[Get your copy of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/) by choosing the package that fits your needs as different packages come with different features.

When the purchase is complete, log in to your [Accounts](https://searchwp.com/account/) page and download the plugin file from the [Downloads](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) tab.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-SearchWP-zip-1030x457.jpg)

Also, be sure to copy the license key from the same screen as youâll require it for activating the plugin later.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-SearchWP-license-1030x457.jpg)Once you have the plugin zip file, you can [install and activate SearchWP like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

When you activate the plugin, youâll be greeted with a welcome screen to start the onboarding process. Click the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button, this will guide you through the initial setup.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)

Alternatively, you can activate SearchWP directly by visiting SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

On this page, paste the license key (which you copied earlier in the previous step) into the appropriate field and press âVerify Keyâ to complete the activation process.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Your licensing information will appear below once the key is successfully verified.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Install & Activate SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info.png)

Thatâs it. SearchWP has been configured successfully. Now, we are ready to configure the custom post type search engine.

### Step 2: Set Up a New Search Engine for Your Custom Post Type

The next step is to add a new search engine to your site. Think of the search engine as a set of rules that determine how WordPress performs a search.

The good news is that once activated, SearchWP will start indexing the content on your site and replace the default WordPress search automatically.

The default configuration indexes only posts, Pages, and Media files. However, you can [modify the engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/) to customize the priority of specific posts or page attributes, add/remove search sources, exclude results, and more.

Using [SearchWPâs Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) we can create multiple search engines. Therefore, letâs leave the default settings as is and create a separate engine for custom post types.

So to create a dedicated search form only for the desired custom post type, visit SearchWP > Algorithm and click the âAdd Newâ button.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Configure Search Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-engine-1.jpg)

From here, we need to select the sources we want this search engine to consider when performing the search.

For the purpose of this tutorial, we will create a search form that searches exclusively among the custom post type: Products. So, letâs uncheck all the boxes except âProducts.â Name the Engine appropriately, then click âDoneâ.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Configure Search Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-engine-2-1.jpg)

**Please note:** Any post type that is set to be public will be available as a source, so you just need to select the custom post type of your choice from here. Thatâs it.

Whether your eCommerce store is built with WooCommerce or EDD, you can [make the products searchable](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/). And if you have any other custom post types on your website, you can make them searchable as well by following the steps below.

Now, click âSaveâ to apply the changes. Our Engine setup is complete!

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Configure Search Engine Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-search-engine-3-1.jpg)

### Step 4: Create a Custom Post Type Search Form

Next, we need to create a search form for the custom post type. SearchWP also makes this super easy! You just need to visit SearchWP > Search Forms and click âAdd New.â

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Creating-search-form1.jpg)

Now, pick a meaningful name for your search form, which will help you recognize it easily, and select a theme layout.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step2-2.jpg)

Then, configure the search form according to your needs. All fields are pretty self-explanatory; however, [if you need any help, follow this tutorial](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step3-2.jpg)

The most important step here is to configure which engine this search form will use. So, letâs pick âProducts,â as we have created this engine to search exclusively for our desired custom post type. Also, you can use the SearchWP search results page to show the search results, as [it comes with advanced features](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/).

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step4-2.jpg)

Finally, click âSaveâ to finish creating the Search Form.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step5.jpg)

There are multiple ways to add this search form to a page. Click the âEmbedâ button next to âSaveâ to see available methods.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Create Custom Post Type Search Form Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)

Letâs show you how to add the custom post type search form using the Gutenberg block. To do that, open the page where you want to place the search form in Edit mode. Then, click âAdd blockâ in the designated location.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Show Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/show-search-form-step1.jpg)

Next, find the âSearch Formâ block and select it. ![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Show Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/show-search-form-step2.jpg)

Now, pick the search form you created earlier from the dropdown.

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Show Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/show-search-form-step3.jpg)Finally, click âSave/Publishâ depending on your scenario.

### ![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: Show Search Form Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/show-search-form-step4.jpg)

### Step 5: Test Your Custom Post Type Search Form

Your new custom post type search form is now live! Letâs test it to make sure everything is working correctly. To do that, visit the page where you placed the search form and perform a search query.

If your search results are pulling only the custom post types you configured, then youâve successfully set up your custom search form!

![How To Create A WordPress Search Form For Custom Post Types: See Search Form in Action](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/live-search-form.jpg)

## Wrapping Up

The standard WordPress search ignores custom post types. However, you have learned how to create a WordPress search form for custom post types to address this issue. We hope youâll be able to apply this knowledge and give your visitors a smooth search experience.

**[Grab your copy of SearchWP now](https://searchwp.com/buy/)** if youâre ready to create your own search form for custom post types.

### [How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-disable-the-search-feature-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** September 24, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you need to disable the search feature in WordPress?

WordPress comes with a built-in search function that many site owners find useful. However, not every website needs a search feature.

For example, if your site is a portfolio, a small business landing page, or even a simple blog with a limited number of posts, the search bar can seem unnecessary.

Or, perhaps the search form provided by your theme might interfere with your siteâs design or functionality.

In some cases, removing the search option can declutter your website and improve the overall user experience.

So, letâs see how to easily disable the search feature in WordPress in this article.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Disabling the search feature can improve your websiteâs user experience, especially for sites with limited content.
- You can easily disable the search function using a plugin or by adding custom code, depending on your preference.
- Even when you disable the search on the front end, you can still search for posts and pages within your WordPress admin area.

## Why Should You Disable The Search Feature In WordPress?

By default, WordPress comes with a search feature that helps visitors find content on your website. However, not all websites need this feature.

For instance, if your website is a small business site with just a few pages or a single-page site, a search bar may be overkill. When users see a search bar, they might assume thereâs more content than what is visible, leading to confusion.

Here are a few more reasons why you might want to disable the search feature in WordPress:

- **Simplifying the User Experience:** On sites with minimal content, the search bar can be misleading. Removing it helps users focus on the content you want them to see.
- **Improving Aesthetics:** If youâre using a minimalistic design, the search form may not fit the layout, and removing it will keep your site clean and organized.
- **Optimizing for Performance:** The search functionality can sometimes slow down your site. Disabling it can marginally improve your siteâs load speed.

## How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress

There are mainly two simple ways to disable the search feature in WordPress. You can either remove the search using a plugin or write custom code to disable it.

### Method 1: Disable The Search Feature Using A Plugin

The easiest and most straightforward way to disable the search feature is to use a plugin. This method requires no coding knowledge and only takes a few clicks.

Weâll use the [Disable Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/disable-search/) plugin to achieve this task. You can install this plugin [like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Disable Search Plugin As Shown In WordPress Plugin Directory ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/disable-search-plugin.jpg)

Just log in to your WordPress dashboard and navigate to Plugins > Add New. Then, type âDisable Searchâ in the search bar.

When you find the plugin, click âInstall Nowâ and then âActivateâ to bring this plugin into action.

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Install Disable Search Plugin](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-disable-search.jpg)

Once the plugin is successfully activated, it automatically disables the search functionality on your entire website.

Thereâs no need to configure any settings. The plugin removes all search forms from your theme and deactivates the search widget.

If a user tries to perform a search directly via a search URL, they will see a 404 error page instead of search results.

### Method 2: Disable Search In WordPress With Custom Code

If youâre tech-savvy and want more control over your site, you can disable the search feature by adding custom code. However, editing WordPress theme files directly can sometimes break your site and is not recommended.

Therefore, weâll use [WPCode](https://wordpress.org/plugins/insert-headers-and-footers/) â the best code management plugin for WordPress. It allows you to add custom features to your website while distancing yourself from touching the codebase.

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: WPCode Plugin As Shown In WordPress Plugin Directory ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpcode-plugin.jpg)

Itâs also beginner-friendly and doesnât require you to learn any programming language to add advanced features to your website. You can generate custom code using AI and configure smart conditional logic to any code snippet.

So, letâs install [WPCode](https://wordpress.org/plugins/insert-headers-and-footers/) to disable the search functionality on your site. You can install the free version by visiting Plugins > Add New Plugin and searching for WPCode. Click âInstallâ once you find it. Then, Activate.

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Add New Snippet Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-wpcode-1.jpg)

Once WPCode is activated, youâll see a new menu item named âCode Snippetsâ appear in the left sidebar menu. Navigate to Code Snippets > Add Snippet.

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Add New Snippet Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-snippet.jpg)

Youâll see a page full of code snippets. Each of these code snippets represents a custom feature. You can easily add these features to your site by using these snippets. Even if you canât find a feature you want, you can simply [use the AI code generator](https://wpcode.com/introducing-wpcode-2114/) to create your custom snippet.

Now, to disable the search feature in WordPress, find the âDisable Searchâ snippet and click âUse Snippet.â

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Add New Snippet Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/find-disable-search-snippet.jpg)

Just activate this snippet by switching on the Activate button and click âUpdateâ, thatâs it!

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Add New Snippet Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/activate-disable-search-snippet.jpg)

This code snippet completely disables the search functionality and removes all search bars from the front end of your website. Now, if a user tries to perform a search directly via a search URL, they will receive a 404 error.

The following image shows what happens when you apply either one of these methods. All search bars are completely removed from the front end. Here is a before-and-after comparison.

![How To Disable The Search Feature In WordPress: Disabled Search Shown As Final Output](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-comparison.png)

### Does It Disable The Admin Search?

No. The admin search remains active. No matter which method you follow, both the âDisable Searchâ plugin and the custom code only remove the search feature from the front end. Youâll still be able to search through posts, pages, and media within your WordPress admin dashboard.

If youâd like to get the search back, simply disable the plugin if you followed method 1 or disable the code snippet if you followed method 2.

## Conclusion

If you disable the search feature in WordPress, it can make your site look cleaner and improve the overall user experience, especially on sites with limited content. Whether you choose the plugin method for a quick solution or add custom code for more flexibility, disabling search is a simple process.

In the future, if you need to add search functionality to your website, hereâs a guide for beginners on [how to create an advanced search in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/).

**Categories:** Tutorials

### [How To Display Clickable Keywords Below The Search Bar In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-display-clickable-keywords-below-the-search-bar-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** September 13, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are your users struggling to find helpful content quickly on your site?

Many visitors to your site may need help finding the content they are looking for, even with a search bar.

This can happen for one of two reasons: users may not always know what to search for or which terms will yield the best results.

This can lead to frustration, lost opportunities, and users leaving the site prematurely.

However, if you display clickable keywords below the search bar in WordPress, you can easily solve this problem.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Many users are unaware of what they want or need from your website.
- Displaying popular keywords right below the search bar will solve this problem.
- SearchWP lets you add this feature to your site without needing to write any line of code!

## Why Display Clickable Keywords Below Search Bar In Your Site

When you display clickable keywords below your WordPress search bar, you are nudging your users to explore key content without them needing to type in a query.

These pre-selected keyword prompts allow you to guide visitors toward specific products, services, or blog posts that align with your business goals.

It eliminates the guesswork for your visitors, making it easier for them to access valuable content. It also allows you to promote specific topics or products and boost your business.

For instance, if you run an online store, you could display keywords like âSummer Sale,â âNew Arrivals,â or âBest Sellersâ under the search bar. This draws attention to high-priority categories or promotions without the user having to search for them manually.

Similarly, for a blog, you might promote keywords such as âTop Tips,â âHow-To Guides,â or âBeginnerâs Guide,â directing visitors to cornerstone content thatâs proven to drive traffic and engagement.

Now that we have a good idea of when to display clickable keywords below the search bar in WordPress. Letâs see how to do it easily!

## How To Display Clickable Keywords Below Search Bar In WordPress

SearchWP is one of the most powerful search plugins available for WordPress. It allows you to easily display clickable keywords below the search bar in WordPress.

While the default WordPress search is functional, it covers only the basics. On the other hand, SearchWP allows you to control how your search algorithm works, making it possible to promote specific content, keywords, and post types.

Here are a few key features of SearchWP:

- [Customizable Search Algorithms](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/): With SearchWP, you can adjust how search results are ranked, giving you the power to boost the visibility of content with specific keywords.
- [Keyword Stemming](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/): SearchWP understands the relationship between words, so if a user searches for ârun,â theyâll also see results for ârunningâ and âran.â

### [How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** August 6, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to prioritize specific search results in WordPress?

Just like a grocery store puts new or featured products on display at the entrance to attract customers, you may want to prioritize content such as a new product page to ensure it gets the most visibility even in search results.

However, the default WordPress search engine doesnât support search results ordering. Therefore, weâll show you how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress in just a few clicks.

**Key Takeaways:**

- The default WordPress search doesnât let you customize search result order.
- Being able to prioritize specific search results can help grow your business.
- Anyone can do it without writing a single line of code using SearchWP.

## Why Prioritize Specific Posts Or Pages In Search?

By default, WordPress doesnât allow you to control the order of search results. However, it can be really helpful in certain situations.

For instance, if you operate an online store, you can increase sales by making your best-selling product appear first in the search results.

Or, if youâre launching a new product, positioning it at the top of the search results is an excellent strategy to maximize exposure and collect customer feedback.

Similarly, for bloggers with extensive content, adjusting search priorities can enhance the visibility of lesser-viewed articles, driving additional traffic.

Conversely, you might choose to feature your most popular articles prominently to align with the search preferences of your audience.

It guarantees a better user experience for your audience and allows you to reduce the [bounce rate](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/how-to-increase-pageviews-and-reduce-bounce-rate-in-wordpress/), which is one of the most contributing factors for search engine ranking.

Besides, satisfied visitors are more likely to return to your site once again, which means even more traffic for your business.

So, letâs see how you can prioritize specific search results in WordPress.

## How To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress

The best and easiest way to prioritize specific search results in WordPress is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/). It not only lets you prioritize search results but also allows you to add and remove content sources, collect on-site search activities, and exclude content when necessary.

Join the team of over 60,000 website owners who use SearchWP because of its rich features. Hereâs what you can do with SearchWP:

- [Display Live Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/): Instantly shows search outcomes as users type, enhancing user interaction by providing immediate feedback.
- [Track Search Behaviors](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): Monitors search activity on your site, offering insights into customer preferences and behaviors.
- [Expand Keyword Flexibility](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/): Utilizes keyword stemming to enhance search results by including related terms, thus broadening the search scope.
- [Integrate with WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/): Seamlessly connects with WooCommerce, enhancing the search functionality for product media files and more.
- [Index PDFs and Office Documents](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-pdf-office-text-documents/): Enables searchability of content within PDFs, Office documents, and both plain and rich text files, improving access to document information

Now, please follow the step-by-step guide on how to prioritize specific search results in WordPress.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

First you need to select a [SearchWP package](https://searchwp.com/buy/) that suits your needs as each offers distinct features.

Download the plugin by accessing the âDownloadsâ section on your [Account](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) page.

Copy your license key from the same page; this is essential for the activation process.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Activate SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-SearchWP-license-1030x457.jpg)

Install and activate SearchWP just [like any other standard WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/). Once activated, click âStart Onboarding Wizard**â** for a step-by-step guided setup once the plugin is active.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Activate SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)

Alternatively, you can activate SearchWP manually by going to SearchWP > Add License.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Activate SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Paste your previously copied license key into the âLicense Keyâ field and click âVerify Keyâ to finalize the activation.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Activate SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Your licensing information will be displayed below if the key verification is successful.

When SearchWP is installed, it starts to replace your websiteâs default search automatically.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Activate SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info.png)

### Step 2: Configure SearchWP To Prioritize Specific Search Results In WordPress

Now that SearchWP has been installed and activated successfully, we can move on to prioritizing specific search results. To do that, we need to install the [Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/extensions/custom-results-order/) extension.

You can do that by visiting SearchWP > Extensions, finding the Custom Results Order in the list, and clicking Install.

Youâll notice itâs installed and activated shortly after. Now weâre ready to customize the search results order. To do that, visit Search WP > Algorithm and navigate to the Custom Search Order tab.

You should now be presented with the search order configuration page.

Here, you can add the search queries youâd like to prioritize in search results. A query is a phrase or a keyword that your visitors type into the search box.

For example, we have a lot of WordPress-related posts on our website. Letâs set the highest priority for one of them so that it appears in the very first place for the query âWordPressâ.

Enter âWordPressâ to the query field, then click âAdd Search Queryâ and wait a few moments.

Click the âPromote to Topâ button next to the post or page youâd like to promote. For example, weâll prioritize the âHow To Create Advanced Search Forms In WordPress (Easily)â post.

If someday you decide to remove the post promotion, just click the âRemove Promotionâ button at the top right.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: Configure Custom Results Order: Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-custom-search-order-step5.jpg)

Congratulations! You have successfully prioritized specific search results in WordPress. Simply repeat this process to configure custom search orders for different posts or pages. Now, letâs see if prioritization works properly.

### Step 3: Verify If Custom Search Order Is Showing Properly

Go over to your website by clicking the Visit Site button under your siteâs name.

![How to Prioritize Specific Search Results in WordPress: See Custom Results Order In Action: Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/show-custom-search-order-step1.jpg)

### [How To Exclude Pages or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** August 15, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you wondering how to exclude pages or posts from site search results in WordPress?

By default, WordPress includes all published content in search results, which might not always be ideal. You may have technical pages like login, checkout, or even documentation posts that you donât want to see in the search results.

Therefore, in this guide, weâll show you how to exclude pages or posts from site search results in WordPress in just a few clicks. So, letâs get started!

- [Verify if the Excluded Content Is Showing Up](#verify-exclude-content)

## Why Exclude Pages Or Posts from WordPress Search Results?

Excluding certain pages or posts from WordPress search results can be beneficial for various reasons.

For example, technical pages like [sitemap](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/what-is-a-wordpress-sitemap-how-to-create-a-sitemap-in-wordpress/), category, or login pages might not serve your visitors when they are included in search results.

The same goes for online stores. WordPress doesnât see any difference between regular and technical pages such as checkout and the thank you page.

Another good example is membership websites. If youâre selling online courses or other digital products, you donât want paid content to appear in the search results.

Even for a regular website, you may want to hide certain pages or posts from search results to provide a more relevant search experience for your users.

## How To Exclude Pages or Posts from WordPress Website Search Results

- [Search Insights & Analytics](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): Track and analyze search patterns on your site to gain a deep understanding of what your visitors are searching for. This data helps you tailor your content strategy and boost user engagement by aligning with their preferences.
- [Customizable Search Rankings](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/): Easily modify the ranking of search results to highlight specific posts or products, ensuring key items receive the attention they deserve.
- [Seamless WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/): SearchWP integrates effortlessly with WooCommerce, enhancing the search functionality for product images, descriptions, and more.
- [Searchable PDFs and Documents](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/): If your site hosts PDFs or other office documents, SearchWP can make them searchable, ensuring users get more accurate and comprehensive search results.

## How To Exclude All Pages Or Posts From Site Search Results In WordPress

SearchWP replaces the default WordPress search the moment itâs activated. Now, you can exclude a particular type of content by removing its source from the SearchWP Search Engine. To do so, visit SearchWP > Algorithm.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude All Pages/Posts Step 1 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-in-SearchWP.jpg)

Now, when you perform a search, only results from the selected sources will appear, and all other content will be hidden from the search results page.

Once the Exclude UI is activated, we can proceed to hide a specific page, post, or product from site search results in WordPress. Therefore, to install this extension, visit SearchWP > Extensions. Then, find the âExclude UIâ extension from the list and click the âInstallâ button.

### How To Exclude A Specific Page From WordPress Search Results

### How To Exclude A Specific Post From WordPress Search Results

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Post Step 1 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/access-all-posts.jpg)

Then, create a post or find the post youâd like to hide from search results and click on it.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Post Step 2 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-post-to-exclude.jpg)

Once you open the post in Edit mode, click on the Post tab on the right sidebar. Now, check the âExclude from SearchWPâ checkbox and click the Publish/Update/Save button at the top right, depending on your scenario.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Post Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-specific-post.jpg)

Thatâs it. From now on, this post wonât show up in the search results of your WordPress website.

### How To Exclude Specific Products from WordPress Search Results

If your eCommerce website has been created using WooCommerce or Easy Digital Download, you can exclude specific products from the WordPress search results page. Letâs use WooCommerce for the purpose of this tutorial.

First, you need to activate the WooCommerce Integration extension. Visit SearchWP > Extensions and find WooCommerce Integration from the list. Then, click the âInstallâ button. Please wait a few moments for the extension to be activated.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Install WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-woocommerce-integration.jpg)

Now, to exclude specific products from WordPress search results, click the menu item âProductsâ from the left sidebar in your WordPress dashboard.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Product Step 1 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-products.jpg)

Then, create or find the product you want to hide from search results and click on it.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Product Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/choose-a-produts-you-want-to-exclude.jpg)

Once you open the post, check the âExclude from SearchWPâ box at the right. Finally, click the Update/Publish/Save button, depending on your case.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Exclude Specific Product Step 3 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/exclude-specific-product.jpg)

Thatâs it. From now on, this product wonât show up in the search results of your WordPress website.

## Verify If The Excluded Content Is Showing Up

Itâs time to test your new WordPress search to see if itâs excluding what you want it to exclude. Press the âVisit Siteâ button under your siteâs name.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Verify Excluded Search Results: Step 1 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/visit-you-site.jpg)

For the purposes of this tutorial, we created a post titled â How to add live search to a WordPress website.â Letâs see if the new search finds it.

![How to Exclude Pages from Site Search Results in WordPress: Verify Excluded Search Results: Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/final-output.jpg)

As you can see, no matches were found. This means that our changes have taken effect, and WordPress no longer includes pages or posts that match the search term in the search results.

The search form shown above has also been created using SearchWP; hereâs [how to create a custom search form](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/) with just a few clicks.

## Conclusion

In this article, you learned how to exclude pages, posts, and products from site search results in WordPress. Additionally, we believe youâll also benefit from learning [how to redirect search results to specific posts or pages](https://searchwp.com/redirect-search-results-in-wordpress/).

![How To Add Search by Category To WordPress: Create Search Form Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-from-satp7.jpg)

### [How To Show Related Content In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-show-related-content-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** August 30, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Have you ever wondered how to keep visitors engaged on your site? It would be easier if you could show related content in WordPress.

By displaying related content such as posts, pages, or products, you can guide your visitors to other areas they might find interesting.

Imagine someone reading one of your blog posts and loving it but leaving your site because thereâs nothing more to catch their attention.

Instead, presenting related content would keep them on your site longer and help you upsell your products or services without much effort.

Therefore, in this article, weâll guide you on how to show related content in WordPress easily. Everyone, from beginners to experts, can do it with only a few steps.

**Key takeaways:**

- Discover how related content can increase engagement and boost sales.
- Learn how to show related content in WordPress without writing any code.
- Determine which content to display as related content for a specific page/post.

## Why Show Related Content In WordPress

Showing related content is a great way to keep your audience interested. When visitors find something they like, theyâre more likely to explore other similar topics on your site. This keeps them on your site longer and makes them more likely to come back in the future.

For example, if you have a cooking blog and someone visits to learn how to bake a cake, you could suggest related recipes like frosting techniques or other cake varieties. The reader will likely click on these suggestions, staying engaged with your content.

Or, if you run an online store, showing related products can encourage more purchases. For instance, someone looking at a phone might also be interested in accessories like cases or screen protectors. Displaying these related items increases the chances of additional sales.

So, letâs see step by step how you can leverage this strategy to boost your sales.

## How To Show Related Content In WordPress

The fastest and easiest way to show related content in WordPress is by using SearchWP. Itâs the most powerful search plugin for WordPress that even comes with extra functionalities to improve your website, one of which is showing related content.

Over 60,000 website owners rely on SearchWP to improve their websites and increase sales. Apart from displaying relevant content in WordPress, hereâs what SearchWP enables you to do:

- [**Exclude Content From Search Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/): Improve search quality by filtering out irrelevant content or categories, allowing users to find exactly what theyâre looking for more efficiently.
- [**Prioritize Content in Search Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** You can adjust which content appears first in search results. This allows you to highlight specific pages or items, making it easy to draw attention to what matters most.
- [**Support Keyword Stemming**](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/): SearchWP automatically includes different forms of your search terms, such as ârunningâ and ârunnerâ, when searching for ârun.â You can customize this feature to suit your siteâs needs.
- [**Analyze Search Trends**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/)**:** Track and analyze what visitors are searching for on your site, gaining valuable insights into user behavior. This data can help you fine-tune your content strategy and improve overall user engagement.

Now, letâs dive into the step-by-step guide on how to display related content in WordPress.

### Step 1: Install & Activate SearchWP

First, choose the SearchWP package that best serves your needs, as each package provides different features.

After making the purchase, download the plugin file from the [Downloads](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) tab on your [Accounts](https://searchwp.com/account/) page.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-SearchWP-zip.jpg)

After downloading the zip file, remember to copy your license key from the same page; youâll need it to activate your SearchWP plugin later.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-SearchWP-license.jpg)

Once the download is complete, install and activate SearchWP [like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

After activation, a welcome wizard will appear. Click âStart Onboarding Wizardâ to follow the initial setup configuration.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard.jpg)

Alternatively, if you prefer to set up everything manually, go to SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Now, paste the copied license key into the License Key field and click âVerify Keyâ to activate SearchWP.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Once your license key is verified, your licensing details will appear, confirming your successful activation.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/successful-activation-with-license-information.jpg)

### Step 2: Configure Search Engine

Once activated, SearchWP immediately begins indexing your website content. To check the progress, navigate to SearchWP > Algorithm.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-in-SearchWP.jpg)

The default Engine indexes Posts, Pages, and Media files. However, you can choose which sources to index. To do that, click the âSource & Settingsâ button.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-setttings.jpg)

Check all the sources you want to index from the âSourcesâ list, and finally click âDoneâ when youâre satisfied.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/setup-Engine-step4.jpg)

The best thing about SearchWP is that it lets us [create multiple search engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) to use different search settings for various needs. Therefore, letâs create a search engine dedicated to showing related content. To do that, click âAdd Newâ next to Engines.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-2.jpg)

Now, choose which sources you want to use to show related content. Letâs make Posts and Pages appear as related content. If youâre running an online store with WooCommerce/EDD, you can show related products as well.

Select Posts and Pages, then name the engine âRelatedâ so that itâs easily recognizable. Finally, click âDoneâ when youâre satisfied.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step1-related-engine.jpg)

Please click âSaveâ to apply the changes we have made so far.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step1-save-engine.jpg)

SearchWP periodically indexes your website content. However, you can initiate the process manually by clicking the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step3-rebuild-engine.jpg)

Although this is not common, you might see the following popup during this process, depending on your websiteâs current configuration. When SearchWP indexes an image or document file, it gathers and saves all indexable data in its database.

If you prefer replacing the existing index data with the new one, check the option âRemove stored Document Content, PDFâ¦â Otherwise, leave it as is and click âContinue Index Rebuild.â Thatâs it! Youâre all set.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Configure SearchWP Engine Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step3-document-content.jpg)

### Step 3: Install & Activate Related Content Extension

Next, weâll need to install the Related Extension of SearchWP, which will show related content in WordPress. To do that, visit SearchWP > Settings > Related.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install Related Content Extension](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step2-install-realted-extention-step1.jpg)

Click on the âActivate Extensionâ button to take you to the Extensions page.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install Related Content Extension Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step2-install-realted-step2.jpg)

Now, find the âRelatedâ extension from the list and click the âInstallâ button. After a few moments the extension will be successfully installed.

SearchWP has many more extensions for specific tasks. You can browse and use the ones that you need.

### Step 4: Customize Related Content Block

Itâs time to configure how youâd like your website to show related content. To do that, visit SearchWP > Settings > Related, then choose the layout theme that blends with your website design.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Customize Related Content Block Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step4-customize-related-1.jpg)

Using the following settings, you can give the Related Content block a custom name. You can also define whether to show descriptions and how many results to display.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Install Related Content Extension Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step4-customize-related-2.jpg)

Most importantly, you need to define which Engine youâll use to show related content. Since we have already created a new engine called Related, letâs pick that from this dropdown.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Customize Related Content Block Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step4-customize-related-3.jpg)

Finally, click âSaveâ to apply the changes.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Customize Related Content Block Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step61-show-related-3.jpg)

### Step 5: Define Related Content Logic

Since every piece of content is unique, the content we would show as suggestions must be unique as well. However, setting them up one by one is a tedious process and will not address our goal.

Therefore, we need a way to set related content dynamically. SearchWP takes care of this problem brilliantly.

Open a post, page, or product in Edit mode and scroll to the bottom. Youâll see a new section titled âSearchWP Related Content.â From here, you can customize which content you want to display as related content for this particular post, page, or product.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Define Related Content Logic Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step5-define-logic-1.jpg)

Now, put the keyword using which SearchWP will find related content in the designated field. Once you do, youâll see related content appear below based on the Engine settings.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Define Related Content Logic Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step5-define-logic-2.jpg)

Since weâve selected the Related engine to show related content in Step 4, only this content will be displayed. Now, if you want to make a particular content to be shown as related content for this specific item, you can do so by clicking the âSettingsâ button.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Define Related Content Logic Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step5-define-logic-4.jpg)

Now, find the content youâre looking for using the designed search. When you find it, click on it to make it selected. Once you do, youâll see that this item will always be displayed first when showing related content.

### Step 6: Show Related Content In WordPress

You can show related content on your websiteâs front end automatically or manually. Letâs show you how to do both so you can go with the one that fits your needs.

#### Step 6.1 Show Related Content In WordPress Automatically: Use Auto Append

To automatically show related content in WordPress, you need to visit SearchWP > Settings > Related, then scroll down to find âAuto-append.â

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step61-show-related-1.jpg)

Now, define the type of content for which you want the Related Content block to appear automatically. You can also determine the position of the block. You can either display it at the bottom or after a few paragraphs. Letâs select âBottom.â

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step61-show-related-2.jpg)

Finally, click âSaveâ when youâre satisfied with your settings.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step61-show-related-3.jpg)

#### Step 6.2 Show Related Content In WordPress Manually: Use Gutenberg Block

SearchWP provides a Gutenberg block to show related content. You can use it like any other Gutenberg block. Just open the designed location in Edit mode and click the Add Block button.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Using Block Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-1.jpg)

Next, find the âRelatedâ block from the list and select it.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Using Block Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-2.jpg)

You should now see the Related block appear. As youâre on the backend, you will not see any content now. They will appear once you visit the frontend.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Using Block Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-3.png)

Just as in Step 5, you can use the âSearchWP Related Contentâ section at the bottom to determine which content should be shown as related content.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Using Block Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-4.jpg)

Thatâs it. When youâre satisfied with your configuration, press âSave/Publish,â depending on your scenario.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: Show Related Content On Frontend Using Block Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-5.jpg)

#### Step 6.3 See Related Content In Action

Now, when you visit your websiteâs front end, a âRelated Contentâ section will appear in the designated space.

![How To Show Related Content In WordPress: See Related Content - Final Result](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step62-show-related-6-1.jpg)

## Conclusion

You can easily show related content in WordPress using SearchWP. Whether youâre running a blog, an e-commerce store, or a news website, showing related content effectively keeps visitors engaged, increases sales, and reduces bounce rates.

### [How To Add Filters To WordPress Search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-filters-to-wordpress-search/)

**Published:** August 23, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you trying to add filters to WordPress search?

Search filters are incredibly useful, especially for websites with extensive content. This feature allows users to refine their search by categories like Media, Pages, Posts, Authors, and Tags, helping them find exactly what they need more efficiently.

In this article, weâll show you how to add filters to WordPress search, enabling you to improve user experience and make your website content more accessible.

- Understand how search filters can improve your websiteâs user experience.
- Learn how to add filters to WordPress search using SearchWP.
- Explore advanced ways to enhance your siteâs search functionality.

## Why Add Filters To Your WordPress Search?

If your website is content-rich, allowing users to filter their searches can significantly enhance their experience.

By adding search filters for Post Types (such as Media, Pages, and Posts), Authors, and Tags, you enable visitors to narrow their search and find exactly what theyâre looking for.

For instance, if you have a blog with various articles authored by different contributors, users can filter their searches by author to find all posts written by a specific person. Or, they might filter by tags to locate posts that fall under a particular topic.

Adding search filters to your website not only simplifies navigation for your visitors but also helps them discover additional content that might interest them, leading to greater engagement and higher conversion rates.

You can add search filters to various websites, including online stores, blogs, eLearning platforms, and membership sites. So, letâs show you how to add filters to WordPress search.

## How To Add Filters To WordPress Search (For Beginners)

The default WordPress search comes with basic functionality and doesnât allow users to filter search results based on specific criteria. This is where [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) comes in.

SearchWP is the most powerful WordPress search plugin, used by over 60,000 businesses worldwide to add advanced search features to their websites.

You can easily add filters to your WordPress search without needing any coding skills using SearchWP. It lets you create a filterable search form, allowing users to filter searches based on post types, authors, or tags.

Here are a few additional features of SearchWP:

- [**Keyword Stemming**](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/)**:** Configure SearchWP to include variations of search terms. For example, you can set searching for ârunâ to bring up results for ârunningâ and ârunner.â
- [**Search Behavior Analysis**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/)**:** Analyze search trends to track what visitors are searching for on your site. This data can offer valuable insights into user preferences, helping you refine your content strategy and boost engagement.
- [**Filter Out Irrelevant Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** Improve the accuracy of search results by excluding non-relevant content such as pages or posts. This ensures that users find exactly what they need quickly and efficiently.

Now, letâs walk you through the steps to add filters to WordPress search.

Once your license key is confirmed, youâll see your licensing details, indicating the successful activation of the plugin.

### Step 2: Customize Your WordPress Search Algorithm

SearchWP will start indexing your siteâs content automatically after activation. However, you can customize the search algorithm to better fit your needs. To do this, go to SearchWP > Algorithm.

Finally, customize the search form design according to your website theme. Youâll have different controls for changing the color and size of both the search form and the button styles.

### Step 4: Embed The Filterable Search Form In WordPress

There are several ways to add a filterable search form to your site. Click the âEmbedâ button next to the âSaveâ button to see all options.

Making comments searchable can significantly enrich the search experience on your site, helping users find relevant conversations and information directly tied to your content.

Letâs explore how to make comments searchable in WordPress so that no valuable interaction goes unnoticed.

**Key Takeaways:**

- WordPress search does not include comments, missing out on valuable user-generated content.
- Enabling comment search can significantly increase engagement and help visitors find important discussions.
- Using SearchWP, you can easily expand your search capabilities to include comments without writing any code.

## Why Do You Need To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress?

Incorporating comments into your WordPress search can transform how visitors engage with your site.

For instance, if you run a product review site, a visitor might be looking for specific user feedback on a product before making a purchase. By making comments searchable, you provide a more comprehensive search result, potentially speeding up the userâs decision-making process and improving satisfaction.

Additionally, incorporating comments into search results can boost your SEO by keeping your content dynamic and regularly updated, which search engines love.

So, letâs learn how to make comments searchable in WordPress without writing a single line of code.

The easiest and most effective way to include comments in your WordPress search is by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/)âthe best search plugin for WordPress. It perfectly bridges the gap in WordPressâs default search functionality.

Using SearchWP, you can make almost everything on your site searchable, including comments. Itâs packed with features like multiple search engines, customizable search rules, and in-depth search analyticsâeverything you need to enhance site interaction.

Currently, more than 60,000 WordPress site owners globally are using SearchWP to stay ahead of the competition and provide exceptional search experiences to their users.

Here are a few key features of SearchWP that will significantly improve your online business:

- [Search Behavior Analysis](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): Monitor and analyze search trends on your website to get deep insights into user preferences and behaviours.

- [Keyword Stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/): Expand search flexibility by finding similar keywords and providing search results even if users make spelling mistakes.

- [Live Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/): Improve user experience by instantly displaying search results such as posts, pages, and products as users type, offering quick and dynamic feedback.

- [Prioritize Search Results](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/): Adjust the ranking of search results easily to promote specific posts or products, improving the visibility of key items quickly.

- [Exclude Results](https://searchwp.com/extensions/related/): Configure your search engines to omit specific types of content from search results, including posts, pages, and other content deemed irrelevant for searches.

Now, letâs see how to make comments searchable in WordPress step by step.
