Start by getting the [SearchWP package](https://searchwp.com/buy/) that fits your needs. Then, log into your [Account](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) page to download the plugin zip file, itâs under the Downloads section.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-SearchWP-zip-1030x457.jpg)

After downloading the plugin, copy the license key as itâs needed later for activation.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-SearchWP-license-1030x457.jpg)

Proceed to install and activate SearchWP the way you would [any WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Once activated, you can follow the welcome wizard to complete the setup. Start the process by clicking the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ button.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)

You also have the ability to configure the setup manually. To do that, navigate to SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Then, paste the license key you copied earlier into the License Key field and click âVerify Keyâ to complete the activation.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Youâll see the licensing information once the activation is successful.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Setting Up SearchWP Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info-2048x592.png)

The stage is set to make comments searchable in WordPress. Now, we need to configure the search engine.

### Step 2: Set Up SearchWP Engine To Make WordPress Search Media Files

Once activated, SearchWP automatically replaces the default WordPress search. But in order to make comments searchable, we need to configure the search engine.

To do that, visit SearchWP > Algorithm. From here, youâll be able to customize the âDefaultâ engine or create as many search engines as needed for different use cases. For the purpose of this tutorial, letâs configure the Default search engine to enable support for comments. Hence, click the âSources & Settingsâ button.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step1.jpg)

Now, select âCommentsâ along with other sources you want to index from the popup window. Click âDoneâ to apply changes.

**Please note**: comments are associated with posts/pages/custom post type; therefore, make sure to select the associated sources so that they appear in the search results.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step2.jpg)

You should now see separate sections for each Source. Letâs find âCommentsâ from the list and click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button so that we can choose what elements of comments we want to use for searching.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step3.jpg)

Letâs now select which attributes of the comments weâll consider for the search. Choose from âComments,â âAuthor Name,â and/or âAuthor Emailâ to consider. After checking the desired sources, click âDoneâ to save your settings.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step4.jpg)

You can determine which attribute of comments will get the most priority by setting their relevance. The left end represents the minimum, and the right end represents the maximum weight.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step5.jpg)

**Please note:** comments are not stand-alone items, so they cannot be shown independently. Rather, when a comment is matched for a search term, the associated page, post, or product will be shown in the search results.

Finally, click âSaveâ to apply the changes made so far.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Configure Search Engine Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/config-engine-step6.jpg)

Congratulations! You have successfully made comments searchable in WordPress!

### Step 3: Create A Search Form To Perform Search

SearchWP lets you make almost all of your website content searchable and provides a simple, no-code search form builder. Using this builder, you can create a search form and place it anywhere on your website.

To create a new search form, navigate to SearchWP > Search Forms, then click âAdd New.â

Next, give your search form an appropriate name and select a Layout theme.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Create Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step2-1.jpg)

Now, adjust the search form according to your needs. Itâs crucial that you assign the appropriate engine to this form. Scroll down to find âEngineâ and select âDefault,â as weâve customized the default engine. However, if you created a new Engine, you must choose that one.

Additionally, SearchWP gives you the ability to take control of how search results appear. When you pick âSearchWPâ from the Results Page, you can customize the search results page completely. Finally, click âSaveâ to finish creating your first search form.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Create Search Form Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-search-form-step4-1.jpg)

You can insert this search form anywhere on your website. There are multiple ways to do that; click the âEmbedâ button next to âSave,â and youâll see the instructions.

![How To Make Comments Searchable In WordPress: Create Search Form Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)

You can follow one of these three methods that suits you best. For further help, please [follow this neatly crafted tutorial](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/) to help you create an advanced WordPress search form.

### [How To Make WordPress Search Image Files](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-image-files/)

**Published:** June 13, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to make WordPress search image files on your website?

The default WordPress search is primarily designed to index text content within posts and pages. It does not index the image metadata.

As a result, when a user searches using keywords within an imageâs alt text, caption, or description, the default search cannot show relevant results.

Therefore, if you run a website that relies heavily on images, itâs high time to make WordPress search image files, as it can significantly boost your growth.

**Key Takeaways:**

- The default WordPress search is designed to search text content, not image files.
- So, it cannot show relevant images in the search results. But it can be fixed easily.
- Using the no-code search plugin SearchWP, you can index all image attributes and their metadata.

## Why You Need To Make WordPress Search Image Files?

Whether youâre a photographer showcasing your portfolio, an eCommerce site showcasing your products, or a blogger enriching your posts with infographics, images are crucial to decorating websites.

Itâs not just aesthetics but plays a critical role in persuading visitors to take action. So, making images findable can enhance user experience and engagement.

If youâre relying on the default WordPress search for your website, it only shows images in search results if their titles match the search terms. This means most of your images remain hidden from visitors.

Not only is this costing you business, but you cannot even measure how many sales opportunities youâre missing out on.

So, letâs learn how to make WordPress search image files of your website.

## How To Make WordPress Search Image Files

The easiest way to enable advanced image file indexing and search on your WordPress site is by using the [SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/). Itâs the most powerful tool that extends beyond basic search functions, allowing for a deep search into the image metadata and other crucial attributes.

SearchWP ensures that your images are searchable by their titles and metadata, such as alt text, caption, or description. On top of that, you can also make the EXIF data searchable with SearchWP.

Additionally, SearchWP supports other file types and offers features such as:

- [PDF and Office Documents Indexing](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-pdf-office-text-documents/): Makes document content searchable by parsing PDFs, Office documents, and plain (and rich) text documents.
- [WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/): Automatically integrates with WooCommerce, enhancing search capabilities for product images and more.
- [Keyword Stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/): Expands search flexibility by finding similar keywords, improving the comprehensiveness of search results.
- [Multiple Search Engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/): Allows the creation of custom search engines tailored for different content types, such as one specifically for images.

Now, letâs get started with setting up SearchWP to make your image files searchable.

### Step 1: Setting Up SearchWP on Your Site

Get the [SearchWP package](https://searchwp.com/buy/) that suits your needs. Then download the plugin file from the âDownloadsâ tab of your [Account page](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/).

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Installing SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info-2048x592.png)

Now, we are ready to make WordPress search image files.

### Step 2: Set Up SearchWP Engine To Make WordPress Search Image Files

SearchWP has replaced the default WordPress search on your website once it was installed. Next, to make images on your website searchable, you just need to configure the search engine.

Now, you have the option to modify the default Engine or create a new Supplementary Engine specifically for searching image files. Letâs create a new Engine for this task. To do so, click on the âAdd Newâ button.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-2.jpg)

Next, in the popup window, ensure that the âMediaâ option is selected and give your engine a suitable name. Click âDoneâ to save these settings.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step2.jpg)

Now, click the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button on the Media tab to specify which sources to index.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step3.jpg)

Choose the sources you want to index from this popup, and then click âDoneâ to save your changes.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step4.jpg)

Lastly, weâll confine this search engine to only returning image files in the search result. You can totally skip this step. If you do, the default behavior is that the search will return all types of media files, such as images, videos, audio, documents, etc.

To do so, click on the âEdit Rulesâ button.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step5.jpg)

You can manipulate how and which results will be displayed using this Engine from this popup. Play around to come up with new ideas.

To keep the search results to images only, we need to create a new rule. So click on âAdd Rule.â from there, set the rule to Only show entries if: File Type: is In: Images. After that, click âDone.â

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step6.jpg)

Finally, click Save to store the new settings.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step7.jpg)

Now, you may be asked to rebuild the index so that the new settings can be applied. Click âRebuild Indexâ to start the process manually.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-step8.jpg)

Depending on the state of your website, the following popup may appear. When an image or document file is indexed in SearchWP, it extracts all indexable data and stores it in its database.

If you want to completely replace the previous index data with the new one, select the âRemove stored Docment Content â¦.â checkmark and click on âContinue Index Rebuild.â Youâre done!

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Configure SearchWP To Search Images Step 9](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Reset-document-content-warning-with-checkbox.jpg)

Congratulations! You have successfully made WordPress search image files on our website. You can repeat this process to make other media files such as audio, video, PDFs, and office documents searchable in WordPress.

Now, you can use this Engine with any search form created with SearchWP. You can do that by going to SearchWP > Search Forms > Add New.

Next, give your form a name, and then choose a Layout theme for the search form.

You can tweak the search form as needed. Most importantly, we need to assign an Engine to this form. To do that, find the Engine and select the newly created âImage Searchâ from the dropdown.

Once youâve made other modifications to your liking, you can click âSaveâ to store your settings.

Now, visitors can only see images in their search results whenever they use this form to search. There are multiple ways to embed this search form into your website. Click the âEmbedâ button next to âSave,â and youâll see the instructions.

![How To Make WordPress Search Image Files: Embed Search Form Options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)

Pick the embed method that suits you best. [This comprehensive tutorial provides a detailed guide on creating an advanced WordPress search form](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

### Wrapping Up

### [How To Redirect Search Results In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/redirect-search-results-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** June 7, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to redirect search results in WordPress to a specific page or a post? Using this technique, you can architect a brilliant funnel that converts.

When a visitor searches for an item or content unavailable on your website, you can do one of a few things.

You can straightforwardly say you do not currently have this product/content available, or, like an expert salesperson, you can [suggest a few alternatives](https://searchwp.com/extensions/related/). The drawback with this approach is that your visitors may or may not want the solution you suggest.

Hereâs how you can do one better: wherever they search for this particular item, you can redirect them to a specific page or post. That way, you can funnel your audience to strategic areas of your site, potentially increasing conversion rates and ensuring youâve tailored search for maximum impact!

In this tutorial, weâll show you how to redirect search results in WordPress to a specific page or post and how this can benefit your online business.

**Key Takeaways**

- One of the tactics to improve your websiteâs search experience is to redirect specific search queries.
- This is particularly helpful when customers look for something unavailable on your website.
- Instead of saying no, you could [suggest alternatives](https://searchwp.com/extensions/related/) to your audience or take them directly to specific pages.
- You can also employ this strategy to create a funnel and guide your visitors to strategic areas of your website.

## Why Redirect Search Results In WordPress And How It Can Benefit You!

When visitors perform searches on your website, they expect results that directly match their query. This basic expectation provides a foundation for free exploration. However, you can optimize this experience by strategically directing them to specific pages based on their search terms, enhancing user satisfaction and your siteâs conversion rates.

For example, suppose you run a technology blog, and a reader searches for âprivacy policies of social media platformsâ instead of navigating through a sea of related articles. In that case, they are immediately redirected to a comprehensive guide. This saves them time and establishes your blog as a primary resource for detailed information.

Similarly, consider you manage an e-commerce store. When a customer searches for âlimited edition sneakers,â they are redirected to a âComing Soonâ page specifically designed for this product. Here, they can sign up for notifications, preview the product, and learn about its features. This approach doesnât just maintain interest; it actively builds anticipation and prepares them for the productâs launch, improving engagement and customer retention.

By effectively redirecting search queries, you cater to your visitorsâ immediate needs and guide them toward more engaging and rewarding interactions. This proactive strategy ensures each search adds value, keeping your audience satisfied and eager to return.

Therefore, letâs learn how to redirect search terms to specific pages in WordPress.

## How To Redirect Search Results In WordPress To A Specific Page

The easiest way to redirect search results in WordPress is by using SearchWPâthe best search plugin for WordPress. It not only enables you to redirect searches but also enhances the native WordPress search capabilities.

The default search function in WordPress is limited. It primarily focuses on the titles, contents, and excerpts of posts and pages. This often overlooks critical content types, such as custom post types, which are essential for many websites.

This is where SearchWP shines. It allows your website to perform in-depth searches across various content types, set specific search guidelines, and track search behavior to understand what your visitors are looking for.

Here are some of the features of SearchWP that over 50,000 website owners use:

- [**Live Search Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/): Enhances the user experience by displaying search results as users type, providing immediate feedback.
- [**Search Behavior Analysis**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/): Allows you to track customer search patterns on their site, offering insights into consumer preferences and trends.
- [**PDF And Office Document Search**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/): Improves the searchability of text-based resources like books by indexing the content of document files in your media library.
- [**Multiple Search Engine**](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/): Supports the setup of multiple search engines across various pages and locations, each customized to meet the specific search needs of different user groups.

Using SearchWP, we can redirect search results in WordPress to specific pages or posts in 2 simple steps. Hereâs how.

### Step 1: Activating SearchWP On Your Website

Visit the official SearchWP website to [pick the package](https://searchwp.com/buy/) that fits your needs, as each comes with unique features.

Download the plugin from the âDownloadsâ section on your [Account page](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/).

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/download-SearchWP-zip-1030x457.jpg)

Remember to copy your license key from the same page; you will need it for activation.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-SearchWP-license-1030x457.jpg)

Install and activate SearchWP [like any standard WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Once activated, you can use the âStart Onboarding Wizardâ for guided setup.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-welcome-wizard-1244x1536.jpg)

Or, you can choose to configure the settings manually by navigating to SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Here, paste your previously copied license key into the designated field on your SearchWP Account Dashboard and click âVerify Keyâ to complete the activation.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Your licensing information will appear below if the verification is successful.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Activating SearchWP Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-info.png)

Now your websiteâs default search has now been automatically replaced with SearchWP, and we are ready to redirect search results in WordPress to specific pages.

### Step 2: Configure SearchWP To Redirect Search Results In WordPress

In order to redirect search results, navigate to the âRedirectsâ tab from SearchWP > Settings page.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Configuring Redirect with SearchWP Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/configure-redirect-extension-step1.jpg)

Youâll notice the [Redirects extension](https://searchwp.com/extensions/redirects/) has not been installed yet. To activate it, click on âActivate Extension.â This will take you to the Extensions page.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Configuring Redirect with SearchWP Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/configure-redirect-extension-step2.jpg)

Here, youâll see all the extensions available on SearchWP, each developed to cater to specific tasks. Letâs find âRedirectsâ from the list. Click âInstallâ when you find it. Once the installation is complete, itâll automatically activate.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Configuring Redirect with SearchWP Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/configure-redirect-extension-step3.jpg)

Now that the Redirects extension is active. Please navigate SearchWP > Settings > Redirects. Youâll see no query has been configured yet. Letâs do that by clicking on the âAdd Redirectâ button.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Configuring Redirect with SearchWP Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/configure-redirect-extension-step4.jpg)

SearchWP Redirects allows you to define the following fields:

- **Query:** allows you to define the search string.
- **Partial Match:** enables you to decide whether youâd allow typo or other [partial matches](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/).
- **Engines:** lets you choose the [applicable engine(s)](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) for the redirect.
- **Redirect:** here, youâll put the destination URL slug.

For instance, to redirect âRed Sneakersâ to a specific page. Write âRed Sneakersâ in the Query field. Tick the Partial Match checkbox as weâd enable typos. Leave the Engines field as is; this way, itâll be applicable to all engines. Finally, write the page slug in the Redirect field.

Add as many queries as you like. When youâre done, click âSave Redirects,â and weâre all set!

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Configuring Redirect with SearchWP Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/configure-redirect-extension-step5.jpg)

You can also redirect search queries to specific blog posts by following a similar process; just add the blog post slug in the Redirects field, and youâre done!

### Step 3: See The Redirected Search Results In Action

To check if everything is working correctly, visit the front end of your website. Write one of the queries in the search form and press Enter or click the search button. Letâs write down âred sneakerâ and hit Enter for our case.

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Final result Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/final-result-step1.jpg)

Youâll land on the redirected page instead of seeing the search results page. Notice that even though we removed the âsâ from sneakers, we got the desired output. This is because we enabled [partial search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/).

![How to redirect search results in WordPress: Final Result Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/final-result-step2.jpg)

If your website lacks a good search form, you can learn [how to create an advanced search form in this comprehensive tutorial](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

## Taking The Search Experience To The Next Level

Other than redirecting users to specific locations, you can also improve the search experience by refining the search results page. By controlling what your visitors see, you can significantly improve their interaction with your site. Below are several effective strategies to enhance your search results page:

[**Design an Effective Search Form**](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/)**:** Implement a well-designed search form to simplify the search process. Provide clear options and advanced filters that help users refine their searches and locate the necessary items or content.

[**Strategically Sort Search Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** Organize search results strategically to guide visitors to the most relevant content. Customize your search results page to highlight key products, services, or content, thus increasing visibility and boosting conversion chances.

[**Display Related Content**](https://searchwp.com/related-extension-v1-5-0/)**:** Boost user engagement by presenting related content or products alongside the search results. This method keeps users engaged by offering additional relevant items that complement their current interests, potentially extending their session duration.

[**Filter Out Irrelevant Results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/)**:** Elevate the quality of search results by excluding non-relevant content or categories. Configure SearchWP to filter out unnecessary items, streamlining the search process and enabling users to find what they are looking for quickly.

## Conclusion

Now that you understand how to redirect search results in WordPress to specific pages or posts, you can strategically funnel users to key areas of your website. We hope youâll use this advanced technique to boost conversions and expand your online business.

[**Get SearchWP today**](https://searchwp.com/buy/) to enhance your siteâs functionality and keep your visitors returning for more!

### [How To Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Issues Solved)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-fix-wordpress-search-not-working/)

**Published:** April 9, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you trying to figure out why is the WordPress search not working on your website?

Troubleshooting WordPress search issues can be quite challenging. This is because there are lots of reasons why your site search may not be working. But if you know where to look, you can fix these issues easily and quickly.

In this article, weâll examine some common examples of WordPress search not working. Weâll then show you how to fix these issues without the need for coding skills.

So, letâs dive in!

## 4 Common Examples Of WordPress Search Not Working

Even though WordPress is a powerful website-building platform, its search capabilities are limited.

By default, WordPress only searches specific content types like the titles of posts, pages, or media files. This means your users wonât be able to find matches in comments, reviews, or product descriptions.

Even worse, you may be experiencing issues with WordPress search not working at all. These issues can arise from different sources, making them difficult to fix.

Search functionality is an important part of every website. If your WordPress search not working properly, itâs almost impossible for users to find what theyâre looking for. This can make them leave your site faster, leading to poor sales or lower conversions.

Thereâs no one-size-fits-all solution for WordPress search issues. If you want to solve a problem with WordPress search not working, you need to find the root cause of the error.

Therefore, letâs talk about some of the most common WordPress search errors below.

### Issue: WordPress Search Box Doesnât Appear

This is a common search issue WordPress users experience on their sites. In this case, your site visitors canât search your website because they donât see any search bar.

There are a number of reasons why WordPress may not be displaying the search form on your site. Here are 2 possible causes:

- By default, your theme doesnât have a search bar. Some WordPress themes add a search form to your site header or sidebar by default. If you change to a new theme, you may find that your old search box doesnât appear anymore.

- Compatibility issues with your theme or plugins. Even when you manually add a search form to your site, you may still experience some issues. Changes made to your theme or plugins can easily affect the search function, causing the search form to stop working properly or even disappear completely.

Continue reading to find out [how to fix WordPress search box not appearing](#search-box-not-appearing) below.

### Issue: WordPress Search Doesnât Show Relevant Results

You may have noticed that your site isnât returning expected content when visitors search for keywords.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): No results search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/no-results-found.jpg)

Here is the most common reason why this may be happening. You see, the default WordPress search isnât powerful enough. It doesnât index everything on your website and isnât always accurate. This means your site users may not always find relevant results when they search your site.

This can be solved by using a WordPress search plugin like [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) to improve your WordPress search. Continue reading to find out [how to fix WordPress search not showing relevant results](#fix-no-relevant-search-results) below.

### Issue: WordPress Search Is Slow

If youâve been using WordPress for a while now, you may have noticed that your site search is slow. It can be even worse when you have a large website with hundreds of content such as posts, pages, and products.

The reason for this is simple: the type of database WordPress uses is not well-suited to performing search queries. Hence, your site will struggle to return results and become slow as you scale up your content.

The easiest way to fix this is to use a dedicated search engine solution such as SearchWP. Continue reading to find out [how to fix WordPress search slow issue](#slow-wordpress-search) below.

### Issue: WordPress Search Showing 404 Error â Page Not Found

Another example of a WordPress search not working is seeing a 404 error or âPage Not Foundâ page. 404 errors are a common WordPress issue. When WordPress returns this error, it simply means the system cannot find the page youâre requesting.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Page not found example](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/404-not-found.jpg)

When it comes to WordPress search, these are the two possible reasons youâre seeing the 404 error:

- Your site is experiencing a permalink redirect error. A permalink is the Uniform Resource Locator (URL) WordPress uses to find a specific post or page on your site. Every time you try to access your search results, WordPress redirects you to a new permalink. If something is wrong with your siteâs rewrite rules, you get a 404 error.

- Your theme is missing the search.php file or using the wrong template. The search.php file is the template for displaying your search results page. If WordPress canât find this file, itâll display the 404 error.

The good news is that you can resolve the issues listed above without writing any code. All you need to do is install a powerful WordPress plugin or change a few settings in your WordPress dashboard.

Letâs show you how to go about it.

## How To Fix WordPress Search Not Working

Most WordPress search issues are related to the limitations of the platformâs default search. You can easily fix these issues by using [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/). It is the most advanced WordPress search plugin available on the market. With SearchWP, you can fix the most common reasons that make WordPress search not work properly.

For example, SearchWP can speed up your site search so your users donât have to wait several seconds to see their search results. It can even [search through taxonomies](https://searchwp.com/introducing-taxonomy-source/) such as tags, making sure your visitors do not leave empty-handed. This can increase customer satisfaction, engagement, and sales.

Other features SearchWP offers include

- Ability to search everything on your site. SearchWP doesnât limit your search to posts, pages, and products only. Users can also find matches in categories, tags, and even custom fields.

- [Partial matching](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/): Site visitors can see related keywords when they search. For example, searching for ârunâ will show results for ârunningâ and ârunnerâ. [You can customize how you want this to work](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/).

- [Visitor insights and statistics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/). Find out what your site users are looking for on your site. You can use these insights to make informed decisions and improve your content or inventory.

Now, letâs show you how to leverage SearchWP to improve your WordPress site search.

### How To Install & Set Up SearchWP

To fix WordPress search not working, you need to add SearchWP to your site. First, pick the [SearchWP package that suits you best](https://searchwp.com/buy/). Then, [download the plugin zip file](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/). You can install SearchWP [like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Now, to activate the plugin, copy the [license key from your account page](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/) and paste it on the License Key field by going to SearchWP > Add License Key from your websiteâs backend. Then, click âVerify Keyâ to complete the verification process.[![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Configuring SearchWP](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-key-pasting-in-SearchWP.jpg)](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-key-pasting-in-SearchWP.jpg)

Congratulations! Youâve now installed and activated SearchWP. The plugin integrates with your native WordPress search automatically. This will automatically fix many of the WordPress search not working issues and your visitors can now enjoy a better search experience immediately.

Now, letâs talk about the specific cases in which WordPress search still does not work. Hereâs how you can fix common WordPress search errors mentioned above.

### Solved: How To Fix WordPress Search Bar Not Appearing

After installing SearchWP, it automatically replaces all your native WordPress search forms. It integrates well with other third-party themes and plugins. Which generally resolves most issues with the search bar not appearing.

You can also manually add a new search bar to your site very easily with SearchWP. Simply visit SearchWP > Search Forms and click âAdd Newâ. This will create a new search bar.[![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Creating search form step 1](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-create-search-form.jpg)](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-create-search-form.jpg)

[Customize the search bar according to your needs](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/) and click âSaveâ to apply changes.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Creating search form step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-3-create-search-form.jpg)

In order to display the search bar on your desired location, open the webpage in Edit mode. Then click the â+ Add blockâ icon and select Search Form.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Creating search form step 3](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-show-search-form.jpg)

Choose the search form you just created.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Creating search form step 4](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-2-show-search-form.jpg)

Click âUpdateâ to publish the changes. Now, the search bar should appear at your desired location.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Creating search form step 5](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-3-show-search-form.jpg)

If the search box is still not showing, a plugin you recently installed may also be the cause. You can deactivate or delete some plugins to see if the error continues. Follow this guide to learn [how to properly uninstall a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/how-to-properly-uninstall-a-wordpress-plugin/).

### Solved: How To Fix WordPress Search Not Showing Relevant Results

With SearchWP, your site checks all your site content for search results. This means your users will now see relevant results that match their search.

SearchWP also allows you to rank content based on attributes. Attributes such as title, excerpt, and product variations store important information about your content. When this content is utilized in search, your visitors will find information on your website more easily.

For example, using the [SearchWP Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/), you can choose to [rank a postâs excerpts higher than its title](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-results-sorted-by-relevance/). This means when users search, your site will look for matches in excerpts before checking titles.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Selecting attributes in the search engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/set-attribute-relevance.jpg)

You can also choose to [limit search results](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/limit-results-post-type/) to specific post types. Bonus: if you are running an online store, hereâs a tip for [restricting your product search to the product title only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/).

### Solved: How To Fix Slow WordPress Search

Installing SearchWP automatically speeds up your WordPress site search. It builds a new search index for itself and returns search results faster than the default WordPress search.

Other ways you can make WordPress search faster include:

- Add live search to your site: The attention span of the average visitor is decreasing every day. By implementing a search that instantly shows results as users type in the search bar, you can speed up the search experience. Hereâs a complete step-by-step guide on [how to add live search to your website](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/).

- Increase your hosting server resources. This may be necessary if you have a large site with lots of traffic. When your search form is getting a lot of action, it may take some time for the hosting server to handle these requests. You can buy more server RAM and bandwidth to improve this. For more details, check out this guide on [how to choose the best WordPress hosting](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wordpress-hosting/).

After following these tips, you can expect your site users to enjoy faster search.

### Solved: How To Fix WordPress Search Showing 404 Error

To troubleshoot this issue, you can start by checking if the problem is a permalink error. You can do this by updating your permalink settings. For more details, check this simple guide on [how to fix WordPress returning 404 errors](https://www.wpbeginner.com/wp-tutorials/how-to-fix-wordpress-posts-returning-404-error/).

If the WordPress search is still showing a 404 error, then the search form may be missing a search results page. To fix this, you can open up the search form by visiting SearchWP > Search Forms > open the search form that has the issue and select âSearchWPâ as the results page. Then click âSaveâ to apply changes.

![How to Fix WordPress Search Not Working (4 Main Issues Fixed): Selecting search results page](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/selecting-results-page-in-SearchWP.jpg)

**Bonus**: If you want to take control of the look and feel of the results page, hereâs a detailed tutorial on [how to customize the WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-customize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/) to fit your needs.

## Conclusion

In this article, youâve learned how to fix the most common WordPress search not working issues.

Troubleshooting your WordPress site search issues may appear challenging. But we hope following this tutorial you were able to fix your WordPress search not working issue and improve the search experience for your users.

### [How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress (3 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** May 15, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to create a custom search form in WordPress? It only takes 3 easy steps!

A search form lets visitors quickly find specific content, products, or information on your website without manually navigating through multiple pages.

This saves time and enables them to take the desired action faster, whether it is to make a purchase or read content, depending on the nature of your website.

**Key Takeaways**

- Custom search forms help you increase sales and grow your business.
- You can create search forms using SearchWP, the best no-code search solution.
- By leveraging search analytics, you can better understand and serve your audience.

Read along to learn how to use a no-code tool to create a custom search form in WordPress. You donât need any coding skills to do so!

## Why Having A Search Form Is Important

There are plenty of websites on the Internet without a search form because they are optional. But having a search form can greatly accelerate your growth.

Imagine a retail business in your neighborhood that sells various home goods. Everything from furniture to kitchen appliances is available in the store.

When you visit them, do you go there to shop for everything you need to decorate a new home? No.

You visit the store only when something in your house breaks or you need to buy a new item. So, youâre stepping into the shop with a specific goal in mind.

Itâs the same for almost all visitors to your website. People who visit your online shop or blog already know what they want. And your growth directly correlates to how easily they can complete this desired action.

Here are a few known benefits of having a search form on your website:

- **Increases Store Sales:** Integrating a search form can directly increase sales by enabling visitors to locate and purchase products quickly. By reducing friction, search forms hugely influence buying decisions.

- **Enhances User Experience:** A search form simplifies how visitors can find content, products, or information on a website. Removing the need to navigate pages individually enhances the overall user experience. This efficiency can lead to longer visits and more interactions on the site.

- **Boosts User Engagement:** Search forms help visitors quickly find the content theyâre interested in. This ease of access encourages users to engage more deeply, whether by reading articles, browsing products, or signing up for services, all of which improve engagement.

- **Increases Conversion Rates:** A straightforward search tool lets users find what they need, reducing decision-making time and quickly increasing accessibility. This can lead to a higher likelihood of purchase or other key actions, thus boosting conversion rates.

- **Helps Gather Insights:** Information from search queries provides valuable insights into user preferences and behaviors. These insights can inform content creation, product offerings, and strategic planning, ultimately influencing the websiteâs development and marketing strategies.

## Why You Need To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress

The native WordPress search engine has limited capabilities. By default, it searches only for posts and pagesâ titles, content, and excerpts, completely ignoring everything else.

So, if you use a custom post type on your website, WordPress cannot include it in the search results.

This is especially critical for online business owners. Most [eCommerce plugins](https://www.wpbeginner.com/plugins/best-wordpress-ecommerce-plugins-compared/) store products and their attributes as custom post types and custom fields.

Your theme can also limit the locations where the search form can be placed. On the other hand, you can display custom search forms anywhere on your website.

This can be handy in some particular situations. For example, you can create a page that only shows a specific type of content. A custom search form can make this happen.

So, letâs see how to create a custom search form in WordPress with only 3 easy steps.

## How to Create a Custom Search Form in WordPress

The easiest way to create a custom form in WordPress is by using the [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) plugin.

It is a complete search solution for WordPress that provides an incredibly powerful search engine for internal search and works as a no-code search form builder.

With this plugin, you can make almost anything on your site searchable. Youâll also be able to have multiple search engines, set custom search rules, view search statistics, and much more!

Over 50,000 WordPress site owners worldwide actively use it to stay ahead of their competitors and provide their audiences with the best search experience for their users.

Here are some other things you can do with SearchWP:

- [**On-site search activity tracking**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-track-searches-on-your-wordpress-site/)**:** Do you want to see what visitors are looking for? SearchWP lets you track all searches, enabling you to understand your audienceâs needs better.

- [**Prioritize search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-prioritize-specific-search-results-in-wordpress/): Want to bring a particular page or item to the top of your siteâs search results? With SearchWP, you can customize the order of search results with just a few clicks.

- [**Make documents and PDFs searchable**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/)**:** Do you have PDFs and office documents on your site? SearchWP can make them searchable and provide better search results.

Now, letâs proceed to the step-by-step guide on how to create a custom search form in WordPress using SearchWP, the no-code search form builder for WordPress.

### Step 1: Install & Activate The SearchWP Plugin

### Step 2: Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress

To create a custom search form, visit SearchWP > Search Forms from the backend Dashboard, then click the âAdd Newâ button.

Give your search form a recognizable nameâin this case, letâs call it âHomepage Search.â Then, choose a theme from the 5 pre-designed layout themes.

### Step 3: Display Your Custom Search Form

Now that you have created your first custom search form in WordPress, you can display it anywhere on your website. There are a few ways to add search forms to your website.

Click âEmbedâ right next to the Save button to get instructions.

For the purpose of this tutorial, letâs see how you can display your custom search form using the Gutenberg Block.

To do that, open the post or page where you want to show the search form in Edit mode. Then, find your desired location and click the â+ Add blockâ button.

![How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress: Display Custom Search Form: Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/display-search-form-step3.png)

Look for âSearch Formâ and select it.

![How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress: Display Custom Search Form: Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/display-search-form-step4.jpg)

Then, click âUpdateâ or âPublishâ depending on your case, to make the search form available to your visitors.

![How To Create A Custom Search Form In WordPress: Display Custom Search Form: Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-3-show-search-form.jpg)

### [How To Make WordPress Blogs Searchable (For Beginners)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-blogs-searchable/)

**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to make WordPress blogs searchable?

A well-functioning search bar can differentiate between a frustrated user and a satisfied one.

If you are a blogger, you are likely uncomfortable writing code, so creating an advanced search for your blog may seem troublesome.

Therefore, in this detailed tutorial, weâll show you how to make WordPress blogs searchable without writing a single line of code!

- You can provide your visitors with the best in class search experience using SearchWP.
- The advanced features of SearchWP will help you better understand and serve your audience.

Imagine a visitor landing on your WordPress blog, eager to find specific information.

They type a keyword into your search bar, but the results are irrelevant or nonexistent. Frustration builds, and the visitor quickly leaves, possibly never to return.

This scenario highlights a common issue with many WordPress blogs: a weak search functionality that can lead to lost content, high bounce rates, and, ultimately, a negative impact on your siteâs performance.

Powerful search engines such as Google have spoiled users with high expectations.

If your blogâs search functionality isnât up to par, youâre likely losing valuable traffic and potential conversions. A weak search bar not only frustrates users but also diminishes the perceived value of your content.

## Why Itâs Necessary To Make WordPress Blogs Searchable

Making your WordPress blog searchable is not just good practice; itâs crucial for the following reasons. Adding an advanced search solution to your blog:

**Enhances User Experience:** A robust search bar lets visitors quickly find what they need, encouraging longer stays and frequent returns while simplifying navigation and boosting user satisfaction.

**Reduces Bounce Rates:** Efficient search tools increase time spent on your blog and reduce bounce rates, signaling your blogâs value and user-friendliness to search engines.

**Improves Content Discoverability:** A strong search function ensures all content remains accessible, helping users easily navigate vast amounts of information and keeping your full library at their fingertips.

**Increases Conversion Rates:** Quick and reliable search helps users find exactly what theyâre looking for, boosting the likelihood of conversions from calls to action, product sales, signups, or subscriptions.

**Provides Behavioral Insights:** Search data offers valuable insights into user preferences and needs, allowing you to refine your content strategy and align more closely with your audienceâs interests.

### [How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-live-search-to-wordpress-for-free/)

**Published:** April 30, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to learn how to add live search to WordPress for free?

In this fast-paced world, everyone expects quick results. Retaining your customers would be quite challenging if you canât immediately serve them what they need.

Live search helps your customers find results even before they finish typing what they desire. It removes friction and reduces the gap between aimlessly wandering and taking action, such as purchasing or finding desired content.

Follow along to learn more about live search and how to add live search to WordPress for free.

**Key Takeaways:**

- What is a live search
- Why is having a live search important
- How can you add a live search to WordPress for free

## Live Search Explained

Live search, also known as AJAX search, displays search results as users type in the search form. This immediate feedback improves the user experience by making search interactions faster and more intuitive.

Consider when youâre quickly trying to find a specific email in your crowded inbox. You start typing a keyword or the senderâs name, and almost instantly, the exact emails youâre looking for begin to appear. This is a prime example of the live search in action.

### The Perks Of Having Live Search On Your Site

Adding a live search to your website isnât just about keeping up with technology trends. Here are a few ways how live search enables you to serve your customers better and helps you stay ahead of your competitors:

1. **Speedy Results:** Users see results instantly as they type, significantly speeding up the search process. This enables them to learn if you have what they are looking for. By respecting their time, you are valuing your customers.
2. **Reduced Bounce Rates:** Quick and relevant results keep users engaged and interested. This engagement reduces the chances that they will leave your site out of frustration, thereby decreasing your siteâs bounce rates.
3. **Increased Conversions:** Faster searches help users make decisions quicker. This speed can lead to higher conversion rates, as users are more likely to complete purchases or sign-ups without hesitation.
4. **Improved Accessibility:** Live search is particularly beneficial for users who may not remember the exact terms or correct spellings of what they are looking for without the full query. Making it easier and faster for them to find their desired items.
5. **Enhanced User Experience:** Live search transforms the search experience by providing an interactive and responsive journey. This modern approach makes your website feel more dynamic and user-friendly.

## How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free

Once you decide to add live search to your website, the rest is easy! All you need to do is use the free [SearchWP Live Ajax Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/) plugin. Itâs a powerful WordPress search plugin that makes the complex task of adding live search to WordPress very easy and beginner-friendly.

Anyone can add live search to their WordPress website using the Live Ajax Search plugin with only a few clicks; no coding knowledge is required. Letâs see how to add live search to WordPress for free, step by step.

### Step 1: Install The SearchWP Live Ajax Search Plugin

First, you need to install the SearchWP Live Ajax Search plugin. Log into your WordPress dashboard, then navigate to Plugins > Add New Plugin.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Install Live Ajax Search Step 1 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-live-ajax-search-step1.jpg)

Search for âSearchWP Live Ajax Searchâ in the plugin search bar. When you find it, click the âInstall Nowâ button.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Install Live Ajax Search Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-live-ajax-search-step2.jpg)

Once the SearchWP Live Ajax Search is installed, click Activate.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Install Live Ajax Search Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/install-live-ajax-search-step3.jpg)

### Step 2: Creating A Search Form

Itâs time to create your first live search form. After successfully installing and activating the SearchWP Live Ajax Search plugin, a new menu item named SearchWP will appear in the left sidebar. Visit SearchWP > Search Forms.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Create Live Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-live-search-form-step1.jpg)

Click âAdd Newâ to create your first live search form.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Create Live Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-live-search-form-step2.jpg)

Give your search form a proper name; in this case, letâs call it âHomepage Search,â then choose a theme for your search form among the pre-designed layout themes.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Create Live Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-live-search-form-step3.jpg)

Customize the search formâs style to match your siteâs aesthetics. You can tweak everything from colors to font sizes.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Create Live Search Form Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-live-search-form-step4.png)

Click Save to finish creating your live search form.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Create Live Search Form Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/create-live-search-form-step5.jpg)

### Step 3: Customizing Search Results Appearance

You can customize the search results appearance to ensure they appear how you want them to. To do that, navigate to SearchWP > Settings.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Modify Search Results Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modify-search-results-step1.jpg)

Scroll down to choose a theme from the 4 predefined layout themes for how the results should appear.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Modify Search Results Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modify-search-results-step2.png)

Adjust the styles as needed, including the result positioning and the number of results displayed, and customize the message when no results are found for the search term. The settings are pretty self-explanatory.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Modify Search Results Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modify-search-results-step3.png)

Click âSaveâ to apply the changes.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Modify Search Results Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modify-search-results-step4.jpg)

### Step 4: Displaying The Search Form

Now that you have created your first live search form and customized how you want the results to look. The next step is to display it on your website. There are a few ways to add search forms to your website.

Letâs see how to add live search form to WordPress using Gutenberg block. To do that, open the page where you want to show the search form in Edit mode. Then, find your desired location and click the â+ Add blockâ button.

Look for âSearch Formâ and select it.

Choose the form you created to show in this location from the âSelect a Formâ dropdown.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Display Search Form in Frontend Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/display-search-form-step5.jpg)

Update or Publish your page and make the live search form available for your visitors.

![How To Add Live Search To WordPress For Free: Display Search Form in Frontend Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/display-search-form-step6.jpg)

Hereâs the live search form in action!

### [How To Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets (Step By Step)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/)

**Author:** Saif Khan

Adding a search widget to your website helps visitors to find your products faster. By doing this, you get to boost conversion and improve user experience.

- Why is it necessary to add a product search to WooCommerce

## Why Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets?

Product search is an important part of the eCommerce shopping experience. If your store visitors canât find the products they need, they may look elsewhere.

The default WooCommerce product search feature is slow and lacks the power your store needs. It doesnât index product attributes, categories, tags, and reviews. This makes it hard to give relevant search results.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Default WooCommerce Search Example](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Default-WooCommerce-search-lacks-power-e1630567794487.png)

Want to improve your online storeâs search experience? Then, you need to add custom WooCommerce search widgets where applicable.

You can add a search box at certain locations on your site, like the sidebar. With this widget, your users can navigate your store better and complete purchases faster.

A custom product search widget has lots of other benefits. For example, you can control where you want the search engine to look for results. You can choose to index product attributes like variations, tags, and short descriptions.

This will help your users find all the products with a specific attribute. For example, they can easily search for items tagged ârefurbishedâ in an electronics store.

You can add a search widget to WooCommerce either by writing code manually or using a WordPress plugin. Some benefits of using a plugin include:

- You get to save time.
- No need to hire a developer.
- You donât require any technical expertise.

SearchWP improves your native WordPress search by indexing everything on your website. By doing this, your site visitors wonât miss out on any product, document, or page they need.

With this plugin, your search engine will no longer ignore important product details like variations, categories, and tags. This means your users get to find relevant WooCommerce products faster.

Besides WooCommerce search, SearchWP also offers other powerful features like

- [PDF and office document indexing](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-pdf-office-text-documents/). If you sell books or other text-based resources, your users will be able to find them more easily. SearchWP considers the content of document files in your media library when searching.

- [Visitor statistics and insights](https://searchwp.com/how-to-find-the-most-searched-product-on-your-website/). You get to see what your visitors are searching for. You can use this information to improve your services.

- [Multiple search engine support](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/). Set up different search fields in different site locations and pages. You can customize each field to meet unique user search needs in each location.

Once you install and activate SearchWP, itâll automatically add these features to your store.

You can use the plugin to add custom WooCommerce search widgets in 4 steps.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

Visit SearchWPâs official website and [pick the package that suits you best](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

Then, download a copy of the plugin from [your account](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/). You can install and activate it [like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

After activation, you need to insert the license key. You can do that by navigating to SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Paste the license key (which can be found in your [SearchWP Account Dashboard](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/)) in its respective field, then press Verify Key.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Install and Activate SearchWP step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Your verification is successful when you can see the licensing information.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Install and Activate SearchWP step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/successful-activation-with-license-information.jpg)

SearchWP is all set and ready for use!

### Step 2: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Extension

SearchWP comes with a WooCommerce extension that automatically indexes your WooCommerce products once activated.

Please visit SearchWP > Extensions and find WooCommerce Integration from the list. Then, click Install.

![How to Add WooCommerce Product Search Widget: Install and Activate the WooCommerce Extension](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension.jpg)

Youâll see it is successfully installed and activated within a few moments. With this, SearchWP will have access to your WooCommerce products.

Finally, weâll show you how to create custom WooCommerce Search widgets, but before that, you need to configure the search engine.

### Step 3: Set Up a SearchWP Custom Search Engine

A custom WooCommerce search widget allows your users to search for products in different ways. To support this, you need control over how your search engine looks for products.

SearchWP lets you select different sources. You can also adjust the relevance of each product or content attribute. For example, you can choose to index the product title, short description, or category.

You can even create multiple search engines and display them on different pages. This will offer your user a unique search experience on each page.

To customize your SearchWP engine, click SearchWP > Algorithm from the left sidebar of your WordPress dashboard.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Step 1 - Set Up Search Engine In SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchWP-engine-customization-1.jpg)

To set up a WooCommerce product search, click Sources & Settings.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: how to setup a WordPress search engine step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-setttings.jpg)

A modal will pop up with a list of sources to choose from. Select Products from the list box. Click Done after choosing sources that interest you.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: how to setup a WordPress search engine step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp2.jpg)

Then click Save so that SearchWP can start rebuilding your search index based on the new settings.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: how to setup a WordPress search engine step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-saving.jpg)

Finally, itâs time to display your custom WooCommerce search widget on your website. Letâs talk about how you can do this in the next step below.

### Step 4: Add WooCommerce Search To Your Website

Now, you need to create a search bar so that your customers can start searching for their desired products.

Whether you need to use a widget or a block to create the search bar depends on the theme you are using. A WooCommerce Search Widget is your best choice if you are using a classic theme. On the other hand, if you are using a modern FSE (full-site editing) theme, then utilizing a WooCommerce search block is the standard method.

You add a search bar anywhere on your website, regardless of the method you use. Hereâs how you can add WooCommerce search to your website for both cases.

#### Step 4.1 Add WooCommerce Search Widget For Classic Themes

You can add a custom WooCommerce search widget anywhere on your site if your theme supports it. Letâs see how you can add the widget in the sidebar. You can also put it anywhere on your site, such as the header and footer. It depends on your needs, but the process is similar.

To add the widget to the sidebar of your website, visit Appearance > Widgets.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Adding Search Widget Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-search-widget-step1-1.jpg)

On the widgets settings page, select the area where you want to add the widget and click the + icon. In this tutorial, weâll be adding the search widget to the sidebar area.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Adding Search Widget Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-search-widget-step2-1.jpg)

Type search into the field, and then select the search icon.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Adding Search Widget Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-search-widget-step3-1.jpg)

This will add a search form that you can customize according to your needs. Then, click Update in the upper right corner. Your sidebar now has a custom WooCommerce search widget.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Adding Search Widget Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-search-widget-step4-1.jpg)

Now visit your website front-end and see your WooCommerce search widget in action!

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Search box preview on frontend](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WooCommerce-custom-search-results.png)

#### Step 4.2 Add WooCommerce Search Block For Modern FSE Themes

Before displaying the search bar, youâll need to create a search form. The process is quick and easy with SearchWP and doesnât involve writing any code.

To create a search form, go to SearchWP > Search Forms and click âAdd New.â

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Creating Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-create-search-form.jpg)

Pick a name for your search form, select a theme layout, and configure it according to your needs. Please note that SearchWP enables you to [configure multiple Engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/), leveraging which you can serve different content in different locations on your website.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Creating Search Form Step 1-1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-1-create-search-form.jpg)

Choosing SearchWP as the Results page gives full control and optimization of the WordPress search results page. Hereâs a detailed tutorial on [how to optimize the WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-optimize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/).

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Creating Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-2-create-search-form.jpg)

Once you are done configuring the search form. Click âSaveâ. If you are interested in learning more, hereâs a detailed tutorial on [how to create an advanced search form in WordPress](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Creating Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-3-create-search-form.jpg)

Now, open the page where you want to display the search bar in Edit mode. Click the â+ Add blockâ icon, then find and select the Search Form from the list of blocks.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Showing Search Form Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-1-show-search-form.jpg)

Then, pick the search form you created a few moments ago.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Showing Search Form Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-2-show-search-form.jpg)

You should now see the search bar displayed on your desired location. Click âUpdateâ to publish the WooCommerce search bar.

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Showing Search Form Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/step-3-show-search-form.jpg)

Now visit your website front-end and see your WooCommerce search in action!

![How to Add Custom WooCommerce Search Widgets: Search Form Live on Frontend](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/woocommerce-search-bar-live.jpg)

## Wrapping Up

In this article, youâve learned both how to add custom WooCommerce search widgets in classic WordPress themes and how to add custom WooCommerce search forms in modern FSE (block) themes.

Now, your online store visitors can find relevant products more quickly and easily. SearchWP is packed with many amazing features to make it convenient for your customers to find what they are looking for.
