### [How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/)

**Published:** March 13, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to make a search bar that searches your WordPress Site?

A search bar is crucial for your website as it helps visitors find posts, pages, and products they need instantly. The easier it is to find what your customers want, the greater your chance of boosting revenue.

In this article, weâll show you how to make a search bar that searches your WordPress Site. Youâll also learn how to add it to specific locations on your site. Whatâs more, you donât need to write a single line of code to achieve this.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Why having a search bar is important
- How to create a search bar for your WordPress site
- How to add a search bar to specific locations on your website

## Why Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site?

Poor site navigation is one of the major reasons why people leave websites.

If you want to offer your site visitors the best user experience, you need to pay attention to your site search. Users need the search feature to find what theyâre looking for on your website.

This is even more important if you have a large website with lots of content. Adding a powerful search bar that searches your site will save your visitors a ton of time.

If your site is experiencing a high bounce rate, it may be because your site visitors need to search through many pages and posts just to find what theyâre looking for. Adding a search bar that searches everything on your site can help you [reduce your bounce rate](https://www.monsterinsights.com/how-to-reduce-bounce-rate/).

But adding just any search bar is not enough. You also want to make sure that your siteâs search shows your visitors all the content you have to offer.

Take the default WordPress search for an example. It lacks power and doesnât always give the user the results they need. Because it only scans titles, excerpts, and content bodies for keywords. Additionally, custom fields and content types created by plugins wonât be included in searches.

However, donât lose hope. Are you ready to make a search bar that searches your site? Weâll show you how you can easily do it with the best search engine available on the market. So your users can find just what they are looking for!

## How To Make a Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site

To make a search bar that searches your WordPress site content, you can start by getting [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/), the best WordPress search plugin. Itâs the go to tool for everyone for improving WordPress website search capabilities.

Even if youâre not tech-savvy, you can accomplish this with just a few clicks. No writing code!

SearchWP gives you full control over how you want your site to look for search terms, and your visitors get to enjoy instant, appropriate search results in seconds. Over 50,000 website owners already use the plugin to improve their business.

Here are some of the powerful features you get using SearchWP:

- Search your entire site. SearchWP indexes everything on your website. When your users type in the search bar, your site will look for results in product details, database table content, and even custom fields.
- Multiple search engines. You can use multiple search engines to create unique search experiences across your website pages. Configure each engine to meet your usersâ needs at different points of their journey on your site.
- Text document indexing. Do you have PDF and office documents on your website? Your customers can now find them by searching for keywords they contain.

SearchWP will help you transform your site search and give your users the best experience possible. You can even use the plugin to [add live autocomplete search to WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/).

Hereâs how to make a search bar that searches your WordPress site easily in 3 simple steps.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

SearchWP comes with different features in different packages; [pick the one that best suits your needs](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

Download your copy of the plugin from the [Downloads tab on your SearchWP account](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/). Then, install and activate it [like any other WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/).

Once activated, you can insert the license key by navigating to SearchWP > Add License Key.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Add SearchWP License Step 1](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Paste the license key in its respective field. Youâll find the key on your [SearchWP Account Dashboard](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/), then press Verify Key.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Add SearchWP License Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Youâll see your licensing information appear right below if the verification is successful.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Add SearchWP License Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/successful-activation-with-license-information.jpg)

SearchWP is all set and ready for use! Now, itâs time to configure your first search engine.

### Step 2: Set Up Your Search Engine

As soon as youâve activated SearchWP, it starts indexing your website content right away! To verify, go to SearchWP > Algorithm.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Algo-page-2.jpg)

If you see the Index Status at 100%, then SearchWP has completed scanning your website content. Otherwise, please wait a few minutes, and itâll complete the indexing. If you want to reindex your site anytime, click the âRebuild Indexâ button.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-in-SearchWP.jpg)

Now, you can either choose to edit the default search engine according to your needs or add a new one. [SearchWP allows you to add multiple search engines to your website](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/). This way, you can offer unique search experiences in different locations of your site.

To configure your search engine, click âSource & Settings,â and youâll see customization options.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-setttings.jpg)

Now, itâs time to select where you want SearchWP to look when searching for results. To do this, click âSource & Settings,â and youâll see customization options. Tip: SearchWP can index eCommerce products as well, learn [how to improve WooCommerce product search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-improve-product-search-in-woocommerce/).

You can choose to search within sources like posts, pages, or media files. Also, decide if you want support for [keyword stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/keyword-stemming/) (search for similar keywords). Plus, you can use SearchWP to search your WordPress admin backend, to do so, enable Admin Engine.

Click âDoneâ afterward once you finish making changes.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-source-selection.jpg)

By default, SearchWP will look at the post title, content, slug, excerpt, and author for matches. If you want, you can adjust these by clicking on the âAdd/Remove Attributesâ button of a respective source.

Here, you can configure which attributes you want to restrict search from this source to. You can choose from attributes like title, slug, content, or short description. You can also include custom fields and taxonomies like post tags, categories, etc. Click âDoneâ after choosing all your preferred attributes.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-attribute-selection.jpg)

Also, you can add specific rules to customize the search experience further. To do this, click âEdit Rules.â

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-edit-srule-step1.jpg)

Now click â+ Add Ruleâ to create a new rule.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-edit-srule-step2.jpg)

With rules, you can choose to exclude or search for only posts that are published after a certain date. Tip: [here are the supported date formats](https://www.php.net/manual/en/datetime.formats.php). Click âDoneâ to finish setting up the rule.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-edit-srule-step3.jpg)

Additionally, you can adjust how your siteâs search engine values and rank content when looking for results. For example, if you want to rank the post title higher than the content, then you can adjust the slider to give more weight to it. Click âSaveâ once youâre done configuring the search engine according to your needs.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Configure Search Engine Step 9](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Config-engine-saving.jpg)

SearchWP will then apply your new settings and build your search index again. As for the last step, weâll show learn how to display a search bar on any location of your site.

### Step 3: Display The Search Bar On Your Site

Before you can display the search bar on your site, you need first to create a search bar. The best part of using SerachWP is that you can create a search bar with only a few clicks without worrying about writing code!

To create a search bar, go to SearchWP > Search Forms and click âAdd New.â

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Creating-search-form1.jpg)

Name the form, pick a theme layout, and [configure it according to your needs](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/). You can pick the engine this search form will use, as SearchWP lets you [set up multiple Engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) to serve different content. You can also select the SearchWP as the Results page in order to take control and [optimize the WordPress search results page.](https://searchwp.com/how-to-optimize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/)

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/selecting-results-page-in-SearchWP.jpg)

Click âSaveâ to complete setting up the Search Form. For learning how to create an advanced search form in WordPress, [refer to this tutorial](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Creating-search-form-save.jpg)

Now we are ready to display the search bar on any location of your website. To do that, click âEmbedâ right next to the Save button.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/showing-search-form-embed.jpg)

Youâll see instructions on how to embed the search form with only a few clicks.

For instance, if you want to put the search form using a shortcode, select Shortcode from the list, and youâll be presented with the shortcode associated with the form. Copy and paste it to your desired location.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/publish_search_form.jpg)Letâs see how you can add a search bar to your WordPress site using a Gutenberg Block. Itâs pretty simple. Just open the page where you want to display the search bar in Edit mode. Then click the â+ Add blockâ icon.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/shwoing-search-form-guten-block1.jpg)

Look for âSearch Formâ and select it.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/shwoing-search-form-guten-block2.jpg)

Pick the search bar from the list that you just created for this location. Thatâs it!

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 8](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/shwoing-search-form-guten-block3.jpg)

Congratulations! Youâve just learned how to make a search bar that searches your WordPress site.

![How To Make A Search Bar That Searches Your WordPress Site: Showing Search Bar Step 9](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/shwoing-search-form-guten-block4.jpg)

## Wrapping Up

In this article, youâve learned how to make a search bar that searches your WordPress site. Now, when visitors roam around your website, theyâll see a search bar that can search everything on your website. ### [How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-fuzzy-search-to-wordpress/)

**Published:** March 22, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Would you like to know how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites?

It can make it easier for your visitors to find the content theyâre looking for, even if they make mistakes! Sounds unbelievable, isnât it?

In this guide, youâll learn how to enable fuzzy search on your site in a few easy steps without writing a single line of code.

Letâs dive in!

**Key Takeaways:**

- What is fuzzy search, and why itâs necessary.
- How to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites without coding.
- How to test if the fuzzy search is working properly.

## What Is Fuzzy Search & Why Enable It?

Fuzzy search is a search feature that you can use to expand search results on your site.

It can be best described with an example. When a fuzzy search is enabled, the search term âsockerâ (even though misspelled) will include results for âsoccerâ (which is the correct spelling).

In other words, fuzzy matching provides search results that are phonetically similar. It allows your visitors to find the right content even if they make typos or misspellings in the search query.

Unfortunately, WordPress does not support fuzzy search by default.

So, if your visitors type a word or a product name incorrectly, they wonât get any results and will leave your site unsatisfied.

This is where fuzzy search comes to the rescue.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: example of fuzzy search](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/how-does-fuzzy-search-work.png)

Fuzzy search allows you to keep visitors on your site and [increase the number of pageviews](https://optinmonster.com/how-to-increase-pageviews-on-your-website/) you get.

Now that youâve learned what fuzzy search is and what are the benefits of adding it to your site, letâs see how you can utilize it below.

## How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress Sites

The easiest way to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites without writing code is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) â the #1 WordPress search plugin on the market, trusted by over 50,000 site owners worldwide.

It allows you to get complete control over your site search and easily customize it to meet your needs.

For example, with SearchWP, you can [create custom search fields](https://searchwp.com/how-to-create-a-custom-search-form-in-wordpress/), limit search to specific categories, and much more.

The best thing is that this plugin is super easy to use, so you can easily set it up even if youâre a WordPress newbie.

Here are some other things SearchWP lets you do with just a couple of clicks:

- [**Show search results in live mode**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/). Install the SearchWP Live Search extension, and all the search fields on your site will automatically start displaying search results in real time to your visitors.
- [**Change the order of search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/). Donât like the current order of search results on your site? Using SearchWP, you can completely customize it the way you want.
- [**Exclude pages from search results**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-exclude-pages-from-site-search-results-in-wordpress/). Want to hide some pages from displaying in search results? With SearchWP, you can exclude both individual pages and groups of pages from the search to make it easier for your visitors to find what they came for.
- [**Make WordPress index PDF files**](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/). Upload PDFs or other documents to your site? Make these files searchable with SearchWP so that your visitors can quickly find them using a search bar.

Now that you know why SearchWP is the best WordPress search plugin, letâs see how you can add fuzzy search to WordPress sites using it.

### Step 1: Install & Activate SearchWP

The first step is to [get the SearchWP package that suits you best](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

After that, go to the [Downloads tab of your SearchWP account.](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/)

![navigate to the Downloads tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/navigate-to-the-Downloads-tab-4.png)

Once there, press the **Download SearchWP** button to start downloading the plugin to your computer.

![click Download SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Download-SearchWP-4.png)

When the download is complete, copy the plugin license key on the same page.

![copy your license key](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/copy-your-license-key-16.png)

The next step is to install SearchWP. You can follow [this guide on installing a WordPress plugin](https://www.wpbeginner.com/beginners-guide/step-by-step-guide-to-install-a-wordpress-plugin-for-beginners/) if you need help.

After installing the SearchWP plugin, youâll need to activate it with your license key.

To do that, go to your WordPress dashboard and navigate to **SearchWP** > **Add License Key**.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: Adding SearchWP license key](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Then, paste the license key in its respective field and click **Verify Key**.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: Pasting SearchWP license key](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

You should now see your license information as the confirmation of your successful activation.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: License confirmation](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/successful-activation-with-license-information.jpg)

SearchWP is all set! Youâre ready to enable the fuzzy search feature on your site.

### Step 2: How To Add Fuzzy Search To WordPress With SearchWP

You just need to do one simple step to add a fuzzy search to your WordPress site. Hereâs how to do it: navigate to **SearchWP** > **Settings**. Now, from the **General Settings** section, turn on **Partial Matches**.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: enable partial match](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/enable-partial-search.jpg)

This will enable SearchWP to show partially matched content if the exact search term cannot be found.

Which means if somebody searches for *ball* when no exact match is found, from now on, the search engine will include suffixes for ball, such as *balloon*, *ballet*, and *ballroom*. These are examples of partial matches.

Now, if the search term entered by your visitor does not return an exact match or partial match, in that case, SearchWP will look for content that sounds similar, taking misspellings into account. For instance, if someone searches for *socker,* SearchWP will show results for *soccer.*

[Fuzzy search works as the last resort](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/partial-matches-fuzzy-matches-spelling-corrections/) when neither exact match nor partial match yields any result. However, if you wish to enable fuzzy search along with partial search, [please visit this complete guide](https://searchwp.com/documentation/hooks/searchwp-query-partial_matches-fuzzy-force/).

### Step 3: Test Your New Search

To do that, youâll need to go over to your site from the WordPress dashboard.

The fastest way to do that is to click the **Visit Site** button under your siteâs name at the top left corner.

![how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites: visit website frontend](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/visit-website.jpg)

Once you get to your site, enter a search term containing a typo into a search box to check if a fuzzy search works.

For example, we have a blog article titled **How to Install a WordPress Theme** on our test site.

Letâs see if we can find it using the intentionally misspelled search term â âwortpress theameâ.

![click Find Results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/click-Find-Results-9.png)

As you can see, we found the post even though there were a considerable number of typos in our search query.

![we found the post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-the-post.png)

Or, here is another example. Letâs try to search for â âe mail makretingâ.

![search for email marketing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-for-email-marketing.png)

We also had no problem finding a post on email marketing because the fuzzy search is enabled on our test site.

![we found another post](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/we-found-another-post.png)

In this article, you learned how to add fuzzy search to WordPress sites.

Itâll help your visitors find the content theyâre looking for, even if they make spelling mistakes. Itâll provide a better user experience and increase the time spent on your website so that you can grow your business faster.

[Get SearchWP now](https://searchwp.com/buy/) if you are tired of typos that steal your visitors!

You might also be interested in our tutorial on [how to optimize your WordPress search results page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-optimize-the-wordpress-search-results-page/).

Looking for a way to add an advanced search bar to a post on your site? Check out the step-by-step guide on [how to add a search bar in WordPress without writing any code](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-a-search-bar-that-searches-your-wordpress-site/)!

### [How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files (In 2 Easy Steps)](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-wordpress-search-pdf-files/)

**Published:** January 22, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Are you trying to make WordPress search PDF files on your website?

WordPress allows you to upload PDF files to your site and store them in your media library. You can also make them available to your site visitors for download. By making WordPress search PDF files and their content, your users can find them more easily.

In this article, weâll show you how to make WordPress search PDF files.

Youâll be able to do this in 2 simple steps. Letâs get started!

## Why Search PDF Files in WordPress?

WordPress is the leading platform [for building a website](https://www.wpbeginner.com/guides/). Currently, [43.1% of all websites](https://w3techs.com/technologies/details/cm-wordpress) are powered by WordPress. It supports different document file formats, including PDFs, one of the most common formats for sharing documents online.

Are you an author, or do you own an online bookstore? WordPress offers all the features you need to showcase and sell your work.

Even if you own a blog, you can convert some of your long guides and tutorials into PDF files so your users can download and read them later.

You can offer PDF files for free download or [sell them as individual products](https://optinmonster.com/how-to-sell-digital-downloads-with-woocommerce/).

Whichever method you choose, you should make sure your site visitors can find these PDF files easily. To make this possible, your WordPress site must be able to index and search PDF files properly.

The native WordPress search can look for PDF files in your media library. But it will only consider the title and short description when searching. This means you wonât always get relevant results.

Thatâs why you need to use a WordPress plugin to offer a better search experience. The plugin should be able to index and search the PDF metadata and other important custom fields.

This way, your site visitors can find all the PDF files on your site faster.

So, letâs show you how to make WordPress search PDF files without any previous technical experience.

## How To Make WordPress Search PDF Files Using SearchWP

The easiest way to make WordPress index and search PDF files is to use a plugin. [SearchWP is the most powerful WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/) available in the market. It offers many advanced features that help improve your siteâs search capabilities.

SearchWP doesnât only index your PDF filesâ titles, content, and short descriptions. Your users will be able to search using the metadata and other important custom fields. You can also adjust how relevant you want SearchWP to consider each attribute when searching.

What about other Office and rich text document formats like DOC, DOCX, and TXT files? SearchWP allows you to index and search them all, too.

Beyond indexing PDF and Office documents, SearchWP can do much more. You get to enjoy other features like:

- **WooCommerce integration:** Do you want to sell PDFs or any other type of products on your site? SearchWP integrates automatically with WooCommerce. Once you set it up, it will take over your native WooCommerce search. You can also [add a custom WooCommerce search widget](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-custom-woocommerce-search-widget/) anywhere on your site.
- **Keyword Stemming:** When you turn on this feature, SearchWP will search for keywords similar to the one you typed. For example, youâll see results for keywords like ârunningâ and ârunnerâ when you search for ârunâ. You can also customize how this works 100%.
- **Multiple search engines:** SearchWP allows you to create more than one search engine on a site. A search engine controls how your site searches for results. For example, you can add a search bar that indexes PDF files to only 1 page on your site. Other search fields on other pages will then search for other content types.

SearchWP provides full-fledged search functionalities that improve your native WordPress search in just a few clicks.

Now, letâs see how to use SearchWP to make WordPress search PDF files. All you need to do is follow these 2 steps.

### Step 1: Install And Activate SearchWP

Are you ready to make WordPress search your PDF files? The first step is to [grab your copy of SearchWP here.](https://searchwp.com/buy/)

You can download the plugin zip file from the [Downloads tab of your account page](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/).

Your license key is also available here, right next to your package name. Copy this Key to activate SearchWP in your WordPress dashboard later.

Now, log into your WordPress dashboard to [install and activate the SearchWP plugin.](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/)

When youâre done, youâll see a new menu item called **SearchWP** appear in the left panel of your WordPress dashboard.

The next thing is to activate the license. Click on **SearchWP > Add License Key,** and youâll be taken to the **Settings** page.

![Adding License Key to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Find the **License** section in the **General** tab. Now paste the license key that you copied earlier in its field. Click **Verify Key.**

![Pasting License Key in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

If everything goes all right, the button will change to **Remove Key,** and youâll see your package information below.

![SearchWP Successfully Activated](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Congratulations! Youâve successfully activated the SearchWP plugin. Itâs now ready for use.

Now, weâll customize the search engine to search PDF files.

### Step 2: Set Up SearchWP Engine to Make WordPress Search PDF Files

After activating SearchWP, you need to set up a search engine that searches PDF files on your site. Setting up a search engine is like adding a mini Google search algorithm to your site. It allows you to control how SearchWP will look for search results.

You can choose from different content sources like posts, pages, or the media library. You also get to decide how relevant each attribute from these sources should be when searching.

To make WordPress search PDF files, you need to select the media library as a source for your site search. To do this, navigate to **SearchWP > Algorithm** page.

![Navigate to Algorithm Page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Algo-page-2.jpg)

Now, you can either edit the default search Engine or create a new one for searching PDF files. Click **Add New** to configure a new engine from scratch.

![Add New Engine in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-2.jpg)

Now, from the popup modal, make sure Media is checked and name your Engine properly. Click **Done** to store the settings.

![Selecting media as a source for the engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-media-source-2.jpg)

Now scroll to the **Media** tab and click **Add/Remove Attributes**. This is where you get to choose which elements of the media library SearchWP should index.

![Setting media attributes for the Engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-remove-attribute-2.jpg)

Make sure to check the **Document Content** and **PDF metadata** boxes, as we aim to make WordPress search PDF files and other types of documents. Then click **Done**.

![Making PDF and documents file searchable in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/select-document-source-2.jpg)

You can also add specific rules to the **Media** tab. Click **Edit Rules** to do this.

![Setting custom rules in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/edit-rules-of-pdf-2.jpg)

For example, you can decide to show results for PDF files only. To do so, click **Add Rule.** Youâll need to set the Engine to show entries only when the file type is in PDFs. Click on the blank field and choose **PDFs**. Then click **Done**.

![Showing only PDF files in the search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/editing-rules-2.jpg)

Finally, click **Save Engines** to save the new Engine settings.

![Saving engine to store the changes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/saving-engine-22.jpg)

SearchWP will then apply your new preferences by rebuilding your search index. However, depending on the state of your website, you may need to rebuild the index manually, in which case click **Rebuild Index**.

![Manually rebuild index](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/rebuild-index-2.jpg)

The following popup will appear soon after. What it means is when a PDF or document file is indexed in SearchWP, it extracts all indexable data, i.e., text, and stores it in its database. In other terms, a copy of the PDF or the document file is stored in the SearchWP database.

![Document content reset warning](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Reset-document-content-warning-2.jpg)

SearchWP is so advanced that it even lets you modify the extracted content from the original file. So you can take more control over the content. To do that, you need to open up the file from **Media.** Click on the PDF or document file of your choice. Itâll pop up a modal; click on **Edit more details**.

![Going to Document Content via Media](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/going-to-document-content-2-1.jpg)

Now, youâll see a section for SearchWP Document Content. This is where the extracted content is stored. If you wish, you can modify the data from here.

![Modifying PDF or document file content](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/modifiying-document-content-2-1.jpg)

Letâs come back to the **Reset Document Content** modal. If youâve modified any file and want to keep the changes, you must leave the checkbox as is. However, if you want to reset and scrape all the modifications youâve made to all the files, then you need to check âRemove stored Document Content, PDF Metadata, and image EXIF when rebuilding index.â This will erase all modifications and restore all files to their initial format. Therefore, we advise you to proceed cautiously. Finally, click **Continue Index Rebuild**.

![Reset document content finalizing](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Reset-document-content-warning-with-checkbox.jpg)

Congratulations! Your WordPress site can now index and search PDF files.

You can plug this Engine into any search form created with SearchWP. [We have created a detailed guide on how to easily add search forms in different locations and use different Engines](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

Now, when your site visitors search for a keyword or authorâs name, they can find all the relevant PDF results.

![make WordPress search PDF files successful result](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WordPress-search-PDF-files-result.png)

Youâve learned how to make WordPress search PDF files in this article. Now, visitors can find all the PDF files on your website easily.

Ready to make your WordPress site search PDF files? [Get started with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/)!

You can also check out our guides on [how to add WordPress search by category](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-search-by-category/) or [add a WooCommerce search form to your shop page](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-woocommerce-search-form-to-shop-page/).

### [How To Restrict WooCommerce Product Search To Title Only](https://searchwp.com/how-to-restrict-woocommerce-product-search-to-title-only/)

**Published:** March 5, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only?

Limiting product search to product titles helps your online store show precise results so that your visitors can find specific products faster.

In this post, weâll show you how to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only.

The best part? You can do this even if youâre new to WordPress. Letâs get started!

## Why Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only?

Itâs always important to offer your store visitors the best user experience possible. One of the ways you can do this is to make sure that they find the products theyâre looking for fast.

When shoppers search your store, they donât want to scroll through lots of results to find what they need. Many eCommerce stores lose potential sales because of this. If you want to avoid this, you need to improve how your site searches for products.

Restricting WooCommerce product search to title only improves your store search. It helps your store to target products better and offer more relevant results.

By default, WooCommerce checks titles, slugs, and short descriptions for product search results. However, some product descriptions may have keywords that do not match what the user is looking for.

When users type a keyword into the WooCommerce search form, your site will show products with that keyword in the description. This can make your search less efficient and affect the search experience negatively.

For example, a t-shirtâs description may include words like âWear with denim jean trousers.â When the shopper searches for âdenimâ or âjeans,â your site will show this t-shirt too.

![Default WooCommerce product search results](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Default-WooCommerce-product-search-results.png)

If you have a large store with similar products, then you donât want this. It means your users may have to scroll through lots of irrelevant results. You can restrict WooCommerce product search to title only to make your search more precise.

This way, your store visitors can enjoy a better user experience, and youâll get to [sell more through your site](https://www.monsterinsights.com/ecommerce-email-marketing-best-practices-to-grow-sales/).

You can easily restrict WooCommerce product search to title only by using a WordPress plugin.

Using a plugin has lots of benefits over adding code to your site manually. When you use a plugin:

- You donât need to hire a developer.
- You donât need to be a WordPress expert.
- Even if youâre technically sound, a plugin will save you time so you can focus on [growing your business](https://optinmonster.com/how-to-create-a-successful-content-marketing-strategy-in-8-simple-steps/).

Now, letâs talk about how to use a plugin to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only.

## How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only

The easiest way to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only is to use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/).

It is the best WordPress search plugin. You can use the plugin to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only. This way, you get to improve your eCommerce siteâs search experience without writing any code.

[SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) is easy to use, and you only need to change a few settings to refine your siteâs search completely. You can fully customize how your site looks for search results.

Other powerful SearchWP features include:

- **Multiple search engines:** You can set up different search engines on your site. Each engine can show different results and can be placed in different locations.
- **Search insights and statistics:** SearchWP saves information about what site visitors are looking for on your website. These statistics can help you understand your users better and improve your inventory.
- **Fully-featured search:** You can look for search results in product attributes like variations and categories. With SearchWP, you can search everything in your store â even custom fields.
- **Live Search Results:** Your customers can see instant results as they type, making it easier for them to find what theyâre looking for and potentially boosting your sales.

SearchWP integrates automatically with WordPress native search. So it takes minimal effort to set up, and you get to enjoy advanced search features without breaking a sweat.

Now, letâs look at how to use it to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only.

### Step 1: Install and Activate SearchWP

Visit the [SearchWP website and get your copy](https://searchwp.com/buy/) of the plugin.

[Download the plugin zip file from your account](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/). On the same page, copy your license key, which youâll need later.

Now [install & activate the SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/) like any other WordPress plugin. Once activated, youâll see a new menu item called âSearchWP in your WordPress dashboard.

Navigate to SearchWP > Add License Key to add your license key.

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Installing SearchWP Step 1](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Paste the âLicense Keyâ you copied earlier in its field and click âVerify Key.â

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Installing SearchWP Step 2](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Now you should see your package information appear if everything goes smoothly.

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Installing SearchWP Step 3](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Congratulations! SearchWP plugin is now active and ready to use on your website.

Next, itâs time to add the WooCommerce extension.

### Step 2: Install and Activate the SearchWP WooCommerce Extension

SearchWP provides a WooCommerce Integration extension that automatically improves your product search once activated. This extension will help us restrict WooCommerce product search to title only.

Please visit SearchWP > Extensions and find WooCommerce Integration from the list. Then, click Install.

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Installing WooCommerce Integration extension](https://cdn.searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/installing-woocommerce-extension.jpg)

Youâll see the successfully installed and activated message appear momentarily.

The SearchWP add-on will automatically integrate with all your WooCommerce search fields. Now, you can restrict product search to titles only.

Finally, weâll show you how to customize your search engine to consider only product titles for searching.

### Step 3: Customize Your SearchWP Search Engine

SearchWP uses search engines to control how your site looks for search results. It can decide where the site should look and which attributes to consider. You can also add other specific rules for even more control.

The first step to limiting product search to product titles is to add products as a source for your search index.

To do this, navigate to SearchWP > Algorithm. Youâll see the default engine. You can either choose to [create a new search engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) or edit the existing one. Letâs go with the default engine for the purpose of this tutorial. Click on Sources & Settings.

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Configuring Engine Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp1.jpg)

Now tick âProductsâ from the list to make sure that SearchWP considers WooCommerce products when looking for search results.

You can choose as many sources as youâd like based on your needs. If you want to limit your results to products only, simply uncheck all the other boxes. Once youâre done choosing your sources, click âDone.â

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Configuring Engine Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp2.jpg)

Next, expand the Products tab and click âAdd/Remove Attributes.â

By default, SearchWP will look at the product title, content, slug, and description for matches. Since weâre limiting product search to product title only, you can just uncheck all other attributes except âTitleâ then click âDone.â

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Configuring Engine Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp4.jpg)

Next, click âSave.â It will reindex the website and apply the updated settings.

![How to Restrict WooCommerce Product Search to Title Only: Configuring Engine Step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/customizing-engine-setp5.jpg)

Now, when your visitors search for products, your store will restrict all the results to product titles only.

![WooCommerce search results after restricting product search to product title only (1)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WooCommerce-search-results-after-restricting-product-search-to-product-title-only-1.png)

## Conclusion

In this article, youâve learned how to restrict WooCommerce product search to title only. We hope this newfound knowledge will help you improve your online shopâs search experience.

Your users can now target specific products and get more relevant results when using the search feature.

### [How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/)

**Published:** February 26, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to learn how to add live autocomplete search to WordPress sites?

Live autofill search helps your site visitors find posts, pages, and products faster. Yet, WordPress doesnât offer this feature by default.

In this article, youâll learn how to add live autocomplete search to WordPress sites so you can improve your website browsing experience.

**Key Takeaways:**

- What live autocomplete search is.
- Why live autocomplete search is important.
- How to add live autocomplete search to WordPress.

## What Is Live Autocomplete Search & Why Does It Matter?

These days, online users have high expectations for search. Search engines like Google offer advanced features, including live autocomplete search and predictive suggestions. These features give better search results and save time, enhancing overall user experience.

If you want to improve your siteâs search experience, you need to add live autocomplete search. It allows search engines to guess what a user is searching for and, in return, provides relevant suggestions as they type. With this feature, users donât need to manually complete their search or load a different page to see the results.

Adding this feature to your site has tons of benefits. For example, adding live autocomplete search to WordPress will help your website visitors find what theyâre looking for instantly.

The live autofill feature suggests results as users type in their queries. Your site will also show more relevant results based on its content.

![WordPress live autocomplete search in action](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/WordPress-live-autocomplete-search-in-action.gif)

Besides improving the search experience, autocomplete educates users about your site. Even when the suggestions donât match the search intent, the user knows your site contains the suggested product or content.

Since your users no longer need to type many keywords, they can avoid typos and reduce search time. This ensures a better user experience, and your visitors can spend more time on your site and buy more products.

The good news is that you can easily add live autocomplete search to WordPress. Letâs see how to do it step by step.

## How To Add Live Autocomplete Search (Easy Way)

You can add live autocomplete search to WordPress without writing any line of code. It is easy for anyone to do and takes only a few minutes to set up.

Weâll use [SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/) as it is the best WordPress search plugin available to date. It improves your site search by delivering the most relevant results. It helps reduce site abandonment, boosts content discoverability, and increases sales.

To add live autocomplete search to your website, you first need to get the free [SearchWP Live Ajax Search](https://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-live-ajax-search/) plugin. It automatically adds live search to your website.

And we suppose youâd want even more awesome features such as multiple search engines, PDF or document indexing, WooCommerce integration, and plenty more. In that case, we recommend you get the [premium version of SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/buy/).

Now, letâs set up live autocomplete search for your website in one simple step.

### Step 1 (& only): Install And Activate The SearchWP Live Ajax Search Plugin

To install SearchWP Live Ajax Search, go to Plugins > Add New in your WordPress dashboard.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-plugin-1-hr.jpg)

On the plugins page, type âSearchWPâ in the search bar. Youâll see a list of SearchWP extensions.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-plugin-2-1-hr.jpg)

Once you find the SearchWP Live Ajax Search plugin, click Install Now and then Activate.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-new-plugin-2-2-hr.jpg)

The Settings page will open automatically once you activate the SearchWP Live Ajax Search plugin. The features are self-explanatory. Please adjust the configuration according to your needs. If you need further assistance, please [read the documentation](https://searchwp.com/extensions/live-search/).

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search Step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/review-settings-searchwp-live-search-hr.jpg)

Click âSave Changesâ once you are done. Thatâs it! You have successfully added live autocomplete search to your WordPress site.

### See The Live Autocomplete Search In Action

After installing SearchWP Live Ajax Search, test the new autocomplete feature. You can do this by navigating to your siteâs front end.

When you type in any query on your existing search forms, youâll see the live autocomplete search in action.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Live Search Working Proof](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/live-search-sample-5.gif)

## How To Add An Autofill Search Form In WordPress

Now that your site has a live autocomplete search, itâs time to decide where you want to display the search forms.

You can add live autocomplete search to your siteâs sidebar, header, and footer areas.

You can also add a search form to any WordPress page or post.

Letâs see how to display the search bar in any of these locations.

### Displaying Autofill Search Form In WordPress Sidebar/Footer/Header

Most WordPress site owners like to display the search form in the sidebar. In this location, your visitors can quickly complete their search no matter what page theyâre on.

The footer and header may be ideal for pages like the homepage or shop page.

You can add a search bar to any of these locations using the widget function.

To access it, go to Appearance > Widgets.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search To Sidebar Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-sidebar-search-1-hr.jpg)

On this page, you can add a live search bar to any area you choose using the block-based widget editor.

First, click on the widget area you want and then the â+â icon. When the widgets list pops up, type âSearchâ in the search bar. Then click the SearchWP Live Search widget to add the autofill search field.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search To Sidebar Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-sidebar-search-2-hr.jpg)

Now, please customize the search bar according to your needs. After that, click Update in the upper right corner.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Adding SearchWP Ajax Live Search To Sidebar Step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/add-sidebar-search-3-hr.jpg)

Your search field is now ready for display. Please note that depending on your active WordPress theme, you may not be able to use some widget areas.

### Displaying Autofill Search Field On Any Page Of Your Website

You can also add a search field to any page of your website. The Block Editor makes it easy to add a search bar here.

To do this, open any page in Edite mode and click the â+â icon on the location where you want to add the search bar. Next, type âsearchâ and select the Search block.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Showing SearchWP Ajax Live Search On Pages Step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-1-hr.jpg)

Once you customize the block according to your choice, click Update.

![How To Add Live Autocomplete Search To WordPress Sites: Showing SearchWP Ajax Live Search On Pages Step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/search-form-2-hr.jpg)

You have successfully added the live autocomplete search to your WordPress site. You can also follow these same steps to add a live search bar to any post.

### How To Customize The Live Autocomplete Search Form

On one hand, the WordPress default search form offers limited features, which may frustrate your visitors. On the other hand, creating an ideal search form is challenging as itâs time-consuming and requires expert coding skills.

Even though you can walk around the marketplace, you cannot ask for help or get any assistance. Frustration sets in, and you leave empty-handed.

- Why making multilingual sites searchable is essential.
- How to make multilingual sites searchable in WordPress.

You can help visitors quickly find the content they want by making your multilingual website searchable. This improves their experience and keeps them on your website for longer.

Moreover, studies reveal that 76% of shoppers prefer to buy from websites in their native language. This highlights the importance of removing language barriers and creating a seamless experience for all visitors.

Therefore, letâs learn how to make multilingual sites searchable in WordPress.

### [How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress](https://searchwp.com/how-to-make-the-knowledge-base-searchable-in-wordpress/)

**Published:** February 15, 2024
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Do you want to make the knowledge base searchable in WordPress?

Knowledge Base (KB) is a treasure trove of valuable information on products, services, FAQs, and troubleshooting guides.

However, having a sea of content on your website doesnât ensure your visitors are finding what they need.

Because when searching through the vastness of content, itâs easier for your visitors to lose their way and give up.

This is why having a good search feature on your Knowledge Base is crucial for your business. Therefore, youâll learn how to make the knowledge base searchable on WordPress.

**Key Takeaways:**

- Why making the knowledge base searchable is important.
- How to make the knowledge base searchable in WordPress.
- How to restrict search results to only knowledge base content.

## Why You Need To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable

Picture your website as a supermarket but without labels! Now imagine walking into that supermarket. It is overflowing with aisles upon aisles of products.

You need milk, but every shelf is packed, and none of them have labels. The search feels like a wild goose chase. This is the struggle users face on websites with knowledge bases lacking robust search functionality.

Without an intuitive search, this abundance of valuable information becomes useless if information canât be readily found.

Hereâs why making the Knowledge Base searchable is important:

- **Reduced âWandering Aislesâ Frustration:** Instead of aimlessly navigating endless pages, users can pinpoint specific information through relevant keywords and powerful filtering. Imagine customers seamlessly locating their desired milk brand instead of scanning every dairy section.

- **Faster Checkouts (Resolved Issues):** Imagine frustrated customers leaving your âsupermarketâ empty-handed after failing to find their desired item. A searchable knowledge base empowers users to find solutions independently, reducing support tickets and speeding up problem resolution. Itâs like offering self-checkout lanes for frequently encountered issues.

- **Improved Customer Satisfaction:** No more wandering around lost and confused! With information readily available, users feel empowered and valued, leading to increased satisfaction and loyalty. Picture happy customers leaving your âsupermarketâ with their desired products (information) and a positive shopping experience.

- **Reduced Overhead Costs:** Less time spent answering repetitive questions translates to cost savings for your team. Imagine fewer staff members needed to serve at the âinformation deskâ as users efficiently locate answers themselves.

- **Competitive Edge:** Accessible knowledge is the differentiator for many customers. A searchable knowledge base positions you as a leader, attracting and retaining users who appreciate self-service and readily available information. Imagine your âsupermarketâ standing out with clear signage and helpful staff, winning over customers compared to competitorsâ unlabeled aisles.

## How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress

The easiest way to make the knowledge base searchable in WordPress is by using a plugin. [SearchWP is the most advanced WordPress search plugin](https://searchwp.com/) that offers robust functionalities to help improve your siteâs search capabilities.

Hereâs why we chose SearchWP to make the knowledge base searchable:

- **Live Search Results:** [SearchWPâs live search](https://searchwp.com/how-to-add-wordpress-live-autocomplete-search/) displays results as users type, eliminating frustrating page reloads and fostering an intuitive, engaging experience. This saves them time, reduces bounce rates, and encourages deeper exploration of your valuable information.

- **Keyword Stemming:** Knowledge bases often use specific terminology. [Keyword stemming](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/keyword-stemming/) ensures users find relevant information even if they use synonyms or related terms. For example, youâll see results for keywords like ârunningâ and ârunnerâ when you search for ârun,â ensuring users discover the answers they need, regardless of wording.

- **Related Content:** Guide users beyond their initial search intent. [SearchWP lets you add related articles](https://searchwp.com/related-extension-v1-5-0/) and resources, enabling users to explore relevant topics and uncover valuable information they werenât aware of. This increases engagement, knowledge acquisition, and overall site satisfaction.

- **Search Statistics and Insights:** Turn user search behavior into actionable insights. [SearchWPâs Metrics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/) reveal frequently searched terms, popular articles, and blind spots in your knowledge base. Leverage this data to refine content, address unmet needs, and optimize your knowledge base for even greater effectiveness.

- **Custom Results Order:** Ensure critical knowledge is readily accessible. With SearchWP, you can [prioritize specific articles or pages](https://searchwp.com/how-to-change-the-search-results-order-in-wordpress/) for relevant search queries, guiding users directly to the most important information first. This saves them time, reduces frustration, and ensures they find the solutions they need quickly.

Bonus: if you have created your knowledge base with BetterDocs, as it has a [SearchWP Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/betterdocs-integration/).

### Step 1: Getting SearchWP For Your Website

Head over to the [SearchWP website to grab your copy](https://searchwp.com/buy/) of the plugin.

Log in to your account and download the plugin zip file from the [Downloads tab of your profile](https://searchwp.com/account/downloads/). On the same page, copy your license key, which youâll need later.

Now [install & activate the SearchWP plugin](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/) like any other WordPress plugin.

In your WordPress dashboard, youâll see a new menu item called âSearchWP.â Click on it and then âAdd License Key.â

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Adding SearchWP license key step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-step-1-1.jpg)

Paste your copied key in the âLicense Keyâ field and click âVerify Key.â

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Adding SearchWP license key step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-before-pasting-key-2.jpg)

If everything worked smoothly, youâll see the verification confirmed and your package information displayed.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Adding SearchWP license key step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/license-add-after-pasting-key-2.jpg)

Congratulations, you have successfully installed SearchWP on your website.

### Step 2: Creating A Custom Engine For Your Knowledge Base

Now, you need to set up a search engine that will be used to search through the knowledge base content. To do that, navigate to SearchWP > Algorithm page.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a new engine step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/go-to-Algo-page-2.jpg)

Instead of using the default Engine, weâll [create a Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) that will only index knowledge base content, nothing else. Therefore, click âAdd New.â

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a new engine step 2 ](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Create-new-engine-2.jpg)

Now, from the popup modal, select all the sources that are available on your knowledge base. The sources are the type of content that are available on your knowledge base. Then, name your Engine properly. Click âDoneâ to store the settings.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a new engine step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/saving-sources-supplimental-engine.jpg)

*Please note: the Engine will limit its indexing to only the sources you select. Thereby restricting all other content on your website.*

The new Engine immediately went into action and started indexing your knowledge base content. Youâll see the progress as Index Status when it says 100%; then indexing is complete. Finish up this process by clicking âSaveâ to save the new Engine settings.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a new engine step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/saving-engine-22-1.jpg)

### Step 3: Adding Search Form To Your Desired Location

Now that you have a functional search engine that indexes all of your knowledge base content. Itâs time to put that Engine into action by adding a search form. To do that, navigate to SearchWP > Search Forms.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 1](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form1.jpg)

This page shows all the [search forms created with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/search-forms/). Please click on âAdd Newâ to create a new search form.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 2](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form2.jpg)

Name your search form appropriately and select a layout theme.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 3](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form3.jpg)

Make changes to its design according to your needs. However, most importantly, select the newly created Engine from the âEngineâ dropdown to be used for this form.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 4](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form4.jpg)

One other thing left to do: in the second step, while creating the Engine, we selected Docs Categories and Docs Tags. These are called taxonomies, and WordPressâs default search results page does not support that. Therefore, you need to select SearchWP from the Results Page dropdown if you want to make taxonomy searchable.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Selecting search results page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/selecting-search-results-page.jpg)

Save the form when youâre done making changes.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 5](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form5.jpg)

Now, there are several ways to showcase the search form on the front end. You can either use a Gutenberg Block or a Shortcode. Click on âEmbedâ to see the instructions.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 6](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form6.jpg)

If you want to show the form using a shortcode, then click on the âShortcodeâ button and copy the code. Now paste it into your desired location.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: Creating a search form step 7](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/creating-a-search-form7.jpg)

Voila! You have successfully made your WordPress knowledge base searchable.

![How To Make The Knowledge Base Searchable In WordPress: a sample knowledge base search created with SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/example-of-searchable-knowledge-base.jpg)

## Closing Thoughts

The knowledge base is a dynamic tool that fosters self-sufficiency, empowers your users, and strengthens your brand. We hope that from this tutorial, you have learned how to make a knowledge base searchable on WordPress sites.

By following these steps and leveraging the power of SearchWP, you can transform your WordPress knowledge base into a user-friendly, intuitive, and valuable resource for your website visitors.

[Get SearchWP today](https://searchwp.com/buy/) to get the most out of your knowledge base and elevate your website experience.

**Author:** Saif Khan

Are you struggling to retain visitor attention on your website?

Imagine you walked into a supermarket. Has it ever occurred to you that you went to buy one particular product but ended up buying a lot? Thatâs the power of upselling.

Itâs much easier to upsell to customers rather than attract them to the shop. This is where businesses can have the most return by spending the least. Captivating current readers with related content is easier for blog owners than bringing in visitors.

### [Introducing Taxonomy Source For Better Content Discovery](https://searchwp.com/introducing-taxonomy-source/)

**Published:** December 29, 2023
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

SearchWP makes your website search great with its ability to search all entries in categories and tags. Now, it gets even better with the addition of Taxonomy Source!

Imagine a vast library where books are organized not just by titles or authors but also by themes, genres, and subjects. While good categorization helps with a book search, the readers still need to return to the directory every time they want to check out a new book, theme, or subject.

But you can remove this friction from your website! Taxonomy Source, coupled with the power of Taxonomy Attributes, makes all books and categories appear together, enabling visitors to dive into both of them right from the search results page.

This brings a more intuitive and comprehensive way to explore and navigate content to your WordPress site.

## What Is A Taxonomy Source?

First things first: What is taxonomy in WordPress? Well, You can group posts in WordPress using something called taxonomies. Think of them as labels or folders. So you can find related items easily. Tags and categories are examples of the two most popular taxonomies.

Now, imagine you are the owner of an online bookstore. You have a category called âProductivityâ that has all the books related to productivity. Now, if you only use Taxonomy Attributes, when someone searches for âProductivity,â they will see all the books in that category, even if those books donât actually mention âproductivityâ in their titles or descriptions. You already have this extremely powerful feature in SearchWP.

But, if you use Taxonomy Source, when users search for âProductivity,â they will see the âProductivityâ category itself in the search results, like its name or description. Clicking on it will take them to the category page where all the books assigned in the âProductivityâ category are listed.

Now, hereâs the exciting part. Youâll get the best of both worlds if you use both Taxonomy Attributes and Taxonomy Sources. For example, considering the previous cases, youâll see the âProductivityâ category page in the search results alongside all the books assigned in that category. Itâs like getting a complete picture of the content related to âProductivityâ on your bookstore website.

Please note Taxonomy Source can only be used with [Supplemental Engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

## Why Does It Matter?

The Taxonomy Source feature takes the benefits of [Taxonomy Attributes](https://searchwp.com/wordpress-search-custom-fields-meta-taxonomy-terms/) to the next level. Just as the Taxonomy Attributes made it possible for productivity-related books to surface, the Taxonomy Source adds depth to those results. Itâs invaluable for sites with diverse and rich content, making every category, tag, or custom taxonomy an accessible and searchable entity.

Letâs look at another example. Imagine youâre running a bustling online store, selling a wide range of products from electronics to fashion. Customers visiting your site have varying preferences, from the latest gadgets to trendy clothing.

With Taxonomy Source, youâre no longer limited to generic search results. Customers can directly search for âsmartphones,â âdresses,â âlaptops,â or any other product category. This means they can easily explore all related products, quickly narrowing down their choices.

## How To Use Taxonomy Source?

Firstly, you need to create a supplemental engine. To do so, navigate to the backend of your WordPress Dashboard > SearchWP > Algorithm. Youâll land on the Engines tab. Now click on the âAdd Newâ button.

![How To Add Taxonomy Source, Step 1: Add New](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-taxonomy-source-step-1.jpg)

Notice a supplemental engine has been created instantly. Now click on âSource & Settingsâ.

![How To Add Taxonomy Source, Step 2 Go to Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-taxonomy-source-step-2.jpg)

All the taxonomy sources are listed under âSources.â Please check the ones that youâd like to index. For the purpose of the article, choose Categories and Tags, then click âDone.â

![How To Add Taxonomy Source, Step 3 Selecting Sources](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/adding-taxonomy-source-step-3.jpg)

Now, youâll see two sections titled Categories and Tags added in the supplemental engine. Pick the attributes you want to use for these Taxonomy Sources and adjust their relevance accordingly.

For example, choose the âNameâ attribute for both sources and give them the maximum relevance.

![Setting up attribute relevance for Taxonomy Source](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/setting-up-attribute-relevance.jpg)

This will make the names of all categories and tags searchable and show them as items on the search results page. Clicking those will take the visitors to their respective pages.

Click âSaveâ to complete the process.

![completing the taxonomy source setup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/finalizing-taxonomy-source-setup.jpg)

You can use this supplemental engine in any Search Form on your website. Please visit [this tutorial if you need help setting up a search form](https://searchwp.com/create-advanced-search-forms-in-wordpress/).

## Unlock The Power Of Taxonomy Source Today

**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

Similarly, a search results page presents the results returned by a search engine. Thatâs why a well-structured results page is crucial for your website.

Even if your search engine can find the right products or content, a poorly designed search results page might not display them properly. Making your visitors leave without discovering what theyâre looking for, even though you have exactly what they need.

To help you overcome this, we present to you the Search Results Page in SearchWP! So, you can break the barrier to engagement and take your website to the next level!

## Search Results Page In SearchWP â A New Experience!

The all-new search results page in SearchWP lets you provide better search results and take control of the look and feel of the page without writing a single line of code!

To customize the search results page easily, you have:

- 6 pre-designed themes
- 3 pagination styles

- And countless styling combinations

## Why Need Another Search Results Page In WordPress?

As such, by using the default search results page, you are preventing your visitors from finding crucial information. You can change that as well as improve the look and feel of your search results page according to your taste.

### No Code Building and Customization

### Better Shopping Experience

SearchWP lets you index [WooCommerce](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/) and [Easy Digital Downloads](https://searchwp.com/extensions/easy-digital-downloads-edd-integration/) items. Now, with the new search results page, you will be able to show product information such as price and customize their appearances.

### Future-Proof Implementation

## The Bottom Line: Your Growth Awaits

Incorporating the SearchWP Search Results Page isnât just about enhancing search functionality and making it look beautiful; itâs about elevating your entire website. It leads to better user engagement, increased conversions, and a noticeable boost in your websiteâs performance.

**Published:** November 30, 2023
**Author:** Saif Khan

**Content:**

The battle for attention on the internet is tough.

The average visitor spends 47 seconds per page, and the bounce rate for websites falls somewhere between 39 â 72%, [according to Contentsquare](https://explore.contentsquare.com/2023-benchmark-en/2023-benchmark-en).

People not being able to find what they are looking for is the leading cause of this abandonment.

Businesses that fail to hook their customers within these tight constraints are losing big on opportunities. We donât want that to happen to you.

We want your visitors to find what they need instantly and for it to be hyper-relevant to their requirements. Because when they do, theyâll buy more from you, and your business will grow! So you can increase your conversion rate and grow your business.

Thatâs why we are introducing our newest feature: **Search Forms!**

## Meet Search Forms: Increase Discoverability For Your Visitors

You know your business and customers better than anyone, and Search Forms puts you in control. You can provide your visitors with a tailored search experience that suits their unique needs.

Whether youâre running an e-commerce store, managing a knowledge base or content-heavy website, or curating a library of PDF documents, Search Forms empowers you to craft multiple search forms without writing a single line of code!

- 4 pre-designed form styles
- 3 pre-designed button styles

With these, you can customize your search form according to your taste â play with layout styles, font sizes, and colors. Also, add a placeholder or a button next to the search field to match your siteâs aesthetic.

![Different form styles introduced in SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/different_form_styles_in_SearchWP.jpg)

## How A Good Search Experience Helps Grow Your Business

You can totally rely on the default WordPress search form; thatâs okay. However, you should also consider its inability to index various custom content types on your website.

This can hurt your business as the users canât find what they need and leave without engaging with your content or making a purchase, depending on the nature of your site.

With Search Forms, you can craft multiple search forms for different areas of your website effortlessly using only a graphical user interface, with no coding involved!

By leveraging the [supplemental engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/), you can also limit the results returned by the Search Form to a particular section of your website.

This enables you to build a search form that solely focuses on products on your shop page, restricts the search to relevant articles on your blog page, and tailors searches relevant to PDF documents if you have any. The application is limitless!

All of the perks of having a good search form on your website make your visitors understand that you value and respect their time, which keeps them happy and coming back more often. As a result, your business grows exponentially.

## Summing Up

Providing features that are crucial for your business, such as Search Forms, is part of our ongoing endeavor to help small businesses such as yours grow & compete with the big guys. SearchWP lets you break free from WordPress search limitations by providing many useful features.

**Author:** Egor Yupatov

A good search is one of the major success factors in eCommerce and digital marketing. With it, you can increase the number of sales with the same amount of traffic.

Letâs dive into it!

## What to Look For in a WooCommerce Search Plugin

To get started, letâs talk about how to choose a good WooCommerce search plugin.

Here are several features you should pay attention to:

- **Full WooCommerce compatibility**. The most important thing to check when choosing a plugin is whether itâs fully compatible with WooCommerce to avoid potential problems.
- **Beginner-friendly**. A good plugin should have a straightforward setup and an intuitive interface so that you can start using it to improve your WooCommerce search right after installation.
- **Reports on search activity**. A plugin should collect data on the search activity of customers on your site so that you can analyze the needs of your audience and boost your sales.
- **Control over search results**. You should check if a plugin allows you to narrow your search, exclude pages from search results, prioritize WooCommerce products, and more.
- **Live search results**. Using a good WordPress search plugin, you should be able to add a live display of search results on your site without coding.
- **Search by category**. Enabling category-specific search is essential for every eCommerce site since it allows customers to find the right products faster.

Now that youâve learned what to look for when choosing a search plugin for your WooCommerce store letâs move on to our list.

## Our Top Picks of WooCommerce Search Plugins

Here are the best WooCommerce search plugins to help you enhance your siteâs search and boost your sales.
