### [WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/woocommerce-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

WooCommerce uses a post type archive template to facilitate search results. This extension allows SearchWP to work with this default behavior by WooCommerce. This extension also incorporates other features of WooCommerce search such as Layered Navigation.

If you are using the native WordPress search (e.g. your search results URL does not have `post_type=product` *anywhere*) this extension is **not necessary**. As a result, if you would like to disable SearchWPâs notification add the following to your themeâs `functions.php`:

```

// This is for SearchWP 4.
add_filter('searchwp\missing_integration_notices', '__return_false');

// This is for SearchWP 3.
add_filter( 'searchwp_missing_integration_notices', '__return_false' );
```

WooCommerce is one of the leading e-commerce platforms for WordPress. SearchWP makes searching your WooCommerce store much more powerful than native WordPress search. WooCommerce also has itâs own set of search tools and filtering options. WooCommerce Integration aims to bridge the gap between all of WooCommerceâs search tools and SearchWP.

*Simply download WooCommerce Integration and activate it alongside SearchWP. SearchWP extensions are separate plugins that in and of themselves are standalone WordPress plugins.*

## Product Variations

WooCommerce stores Product Variations in a different way than it does Products. There are a few things you can do to integrate Product Variation data alongside your Products depending on how you want results to appear.

If you would like to index Product Variation data alongside Products (allowing Product Variation data to cause the parent Product to appear in search results) you can follow the example outlined by this KB article: [Add WooCommerce Product Variations to Products](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/add-woocommerce-product-variations-to-products/)

Alternatively, you can force Product Variations to appear as their own post type by using this as a starting point: [view code snippet](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/7bb906ef556120e46e09513c2a5539ee)

---

### [Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/custom-results-order/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

Using the Custom Results Order extension you can apply your own, hand curated influence on search results sets.

SearchWPâs algorithm does a great job returning results based on relevance, but there are times when you may want certain entries to appear at the top of search results for specific search queries. Custom Results Order can do just that!

[![SearchWP Custom Results Order in action!](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-cro-1.1.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-cro-1.1.gif)Youâre able to add a search query and engine âtriggerâ which will then display the results for that search. Using the available controls you can promote an entry to the top spot. With that promotion in place that result will *always* be the top result, regardless of calculated weight.

You can promote multiple results for each search query if youâd like, and you can also re-promote an entry if things get out of order.

Thereâs no limit to the number of triggers you can add when using Custom Results Order, if there are cases where you want to be sure that a certain result is the top result, this extension will get the job done!

### A note about partial matches

There is support for partial matches in Custom Results Order, but it is not recommended. When partial matching is enabled, Custom Results Order will apply custom sorting if the search query trigger appears *anywhere* in the search query itself. This can have influence on search queries you would rather not have influenced, and there is no way for Custom Results Order to take that into consideration. Please disable exact matching with caution ?

## Why is it necessary?

*If SearchWP was originally built to return the most relevant results, why is this Extension necessary at all?*

SearchWP **does** return the most relevant results it can find, but those results are directly tied to SearchWPâs algorithm used to find those results in the first place. As with all algorithms, there are edge cases that go against the norm, and finding search results is no different.

You are the most knowledgeable of your site content, and you know what your visitors are looking for. Using SearchWPâs [Metrics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/) you can gain insight not only into what your visitors are searching for, but **also which results theyâre clicking**.

There will be times that visitors are clicking results that donât rank highest in the results set. You *could* manually edit the engine configuration to compensate, or stuff some keywords in your content, or even create a âhiddenâ Custom Field to stuff keywords to get the overall rank higher.

Those solutions can work, but Custom Results Order removes the burden of having to set up and manage some sort of undocumented system. It provides you with a single screen containing all of these customizations you have in place.

### Make your search even better

I hope Custom Results Order helps you improve your on-site search to an even higher degree. Itâs already in use on this site, making sure that customers can find the documentation theyâre looking for!

You can take advantage of Custom Results Order on the front end of your site too by [styling promoted entries](https://www.billerickson.net/manually-promote-featured-content-in-search-results/)!

---

### [Redirects](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/redirects/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

**Automatically redirect to a specific page when certain searches are performed!** While SearchWP will find the most relevant search results for you, there are times where you simply want to help your visitor move directly to a page of your choosing, saving them a click in the process.

## Setup

SearchWP Redirects is a standalone WordPress plugin that is installed and activated alongside SearchWP. Once activated, youâll see a new entry on the SearchWP > Settings.

Clicking through to the Redirects settings screen, youâre given full control over how Redirects should automatically guide your visitors depending on search query.

## Configuration

SearchWP Redirects allows you to define:

- The query string
- Whether partial matches should be accepted
- Applicable engine(s) for the redirect
- Destination URL

This allows you to define a comprehensive set of automatic redirections in response to various searches that can improve your visitor experience:

[![SearchWP Redirects settings screen](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-redirect.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-redirect.png)You can add as many Redirects as youâd like, theyâre put in place as soon as you save the settings!

### Troubleshooting

If after activating Redirects you see an error message that looks like the following:

`Warning: Cannot modify header information - headers already sent by (output started at /var/www/wp-content/plugins/searchwp-redirects/includes/SearchWP_Redirects.php:56) in /var/www/wp-includes/pluggable.php on line 1195`

That means that either your theme or an active plugin has sent output to the browser before Redirects could handle the request. If you are using a supplemental engine please try editing your supplemental engine template so that your call to `get_header()` occurs ***after*** your SearchWP logic. If that doesnât work, please open a support ticket.

---

### [Meta Box Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/meta-box-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/meta-box-integration-card.png)With **hundreds of thousands of active installations** [Meta Box](https://metabox.io/) is one of the premier methods of managing Custom Fields on your WordPress website.

Meta Box takes a code-focused approach to custom fields, and developers have latched on because of its unique approach to implementing meta boxes.

## Meta Box and SearchWP

With SearchWPâs Meta Box Integration, youâll be able to easily add your Meta Box fields to your search engines, theyâre organized by the Meta Boxes you set up:

[![Animated GIF adding Meta Box fields to a SearchWP engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-01.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-01.gif)Easily add Meta Box fields to SearchWP enginesFurther, Meta Box Integration also makes your [MB Custom Table](https://metabox.io/plugins/mb-custom-table/) content searchable as well!

[![Animated GIF adding MB Custom Table fields to a SearchWP engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-02.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-02.gif)Make MB Custom Table content searchableSearchWP groups your MB Custom Table content together and makes it possible to search the data stored in MB Custom Table fields instantly!

---

### [Polylang Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/polylang-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[Polylang](http://wordpress.org/plugins/polylang/) is a fantastic plugin that adds multilingual support to WordPress:

> Polylang allows you to create a bilingual or multilingual WordPress site. You write posts, pages and create categories and post tags as usual, and then define the language for each of them. The translation is optional. The plugin does not integrate automatic or professional translation.

After installing and activating this Extension, SearchWP will limit search results to the active language of your Polylang-powered content segmentation.

---

### [EDD Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/easy-digital-downloads-edd-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

Easy Digital Downloads is a fantastic plugin that makes selling digital products super easy. EDD comes with a number of features but one in particular requires a bridge for SearchWP. EDD has a `[``downloads]` Shortcode that can be used to output your Downloads, and some themes/plugins have implemented their own search feature. EDD Integration allows you to couple with that functionality and use SearchWP to provide the results.

### Available hooks

**`searchwp_edd_engine`** Set which SearchWP engine to use for the `[downloads]` Shortcode (default is `default`)

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_searchwp\_edd\_engine( $engine, $query, $atts ) {     return 'default'; // the name of the engine you want to use for the \[downloads\] Shortcode    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_edd\_engine', 'my\_searchwp\_edd\_engine', 10, 3 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/9a20381a1e8e56f0d1be262c2373bb79/raw/8dc2dc921cbda52b5e8c412f11445694c6531d56/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/9a20381a1e8e56f0d1be262c2373bb79#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

---

### [Co-Authors Plus Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/co-authors-plus-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[Co-Authors Plus](https://wordpress.org/plugins/co-authors-plus/) is a plugin built and maintained by Automattic that allows you to define multiple Authors for your content. This extension will index and make searchable all multiple Authors.

Once the extension has been activated, you **must** rebuild your index on the Advanced tab of the SearchWP settings screen, and then trigger a reindex.

While the index is rebuilding, youâll notice a number of new Custom Fields in the SearchWP settings dropdown for each post type, they all start with `coauthorplus_`

By default the extension will index:

- `user_nicename`
- `display_name`
- `nickname`
- `first_name`
- `last_name`
- `biography`

and there are separate entries for each of those, but the extension also bundles all of those fields into a `coauthorsplus_any` Custom Field if you want to match any of those.

Once the index has rebuilt you will be able to search by Author data as defined by Co-Authors Plus. Searching for configured Author data will return the post(s) written by that author, not the Author archive or a User profile.

---

### [PrivateContent Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/privatecontent-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[PrivateContent](http://codecanyon.net/item/privatecontent-multilevel-content-plugin/1467885) is a plugin that allows you to restrict content based on user groups. PrivateContent Integration allows for the indexing of this content and also ensures that search results are limited to the appropriate user group(s).

---

### [WP Document Revisions Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/wp-document-revisions-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

Integrate SearchWP with [WP Document Revisions](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-document-revisions/), a popular plugin used by many to help keep track of documents within their WordPress install. Using this extension SearchWP will properly parse and index the content from the latest revision of a Document so as to make it searchable.

---

### [DirectoryPress Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/directorypress-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[DirectoryPress](http://directorypress.net/) is a popular theme that facilitates creating directories of many kinds. It offers an advanced search with a number of filters specific to DirectoryPress. This extension integrates SearchWP into the text search in DirectoryPress.

More information on DirectoryPress:

> Our directory theme for WordPress has been hand crafted by business owners to include everything you need to setup search engine friendly directory websites for any niche market. Weather your starting church directory, hotel directory or a business directory for personal or business use, our directory theme can handle it! Powered by WordPress this directory theme can handle 50,000+ listings easily on a shared hosting account with lots of add-onâs available help you expand your website when you need it. Furthermore, our directory theme is fun, easy to use and quick to setup which means youâll have your website setup in no time!

---

### [BetterDocs Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/betterdocs-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/betterdocs-card.png)[BetterDocs](https://wordpress.org/plugins/betterdocs/) calls itself the âBest Documentation & Knowledge Base Pluginâ. Use this Extension to use SearchWP to power BetterDocâs live search implementation.

By default your **Default** Engine will be used for results, but you can customize the Engine used for BetterDocs searches with this hook:

 All hooks should be added to *your custom* [SearchWP Customizations Plugin](/documentation/knowledge-base/creating-searchwp-customizations-plugin/).

### [BigCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/bigcommerce-integration/)

**Published:** December 10, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![BigCommerce logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/bigcommerce-integration-card.png)[BigCommerce](https://wordpress.org/plugins/bigcommerce/) is a popular e-commerce platform that includes powerful features. SearchWPâs BigCommerce Integration Extension makes your online store even more powerful by directly integrating BigCommerce product attributes into your on-site search.

The primary benefit to using SearchWPâs BigCommerce Integration is the ability to individually integrate BigCommerce Product Custom Fields into your WordPress search, allowing you to customize your search engine and make it a powerful asset to your customers!

### [Shortcodes](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/shortcodes/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

This extension provides Shortcodes that generate both search forms and results pages for SearchWP search engines. Itâs useful if youâre not comfortable (or able) to edit theme files directly to implement your search form(s) and results loops.

After activating Shortcodes, the following will be available to you:

ShortcodeAttribute(s)`searchwp_search_form`

Outputs a search form for a specific search engine
`target` â The form action (Default is the existing permalink)
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`button_text` â Form button text (Default is `Search`)
`hide_button` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, hides the search form submit button
`placeholder` â Form input placeholder text (Default is `Search`)
`live_search` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, force enable/disable Live Ajax Search for this form, ignoring the value of the global âEnable Live Searchâ setting`searchwp_search_results`

Outputs the list of search results once a search has been submitted
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`posts_per_page` â How many posts per page to display

*Note that this is not a self-closing Shortcode, the markup for each search result should be nested inside, see the sample below for example*`searchwp_search_result_link`

Outputs a link to the current search result with the text of the resultâs title
`direct` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, when true link directly to the permalink, if itâs an attachment link directly to itâs URL, not the parent page`searchwp_search_result_excerpt`

Outputs the excerpt for the current search result
None`searchwp_search_results_none`

Output if no search results were found
None

*Note that this is not a self-closing Shortcode, the markup for each search result should be nested inside, see sample below for example*`searchwp_search_results_pagination`

Outputs pagination of search results
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`direction` â Either âprevâ or ânextâ
`link_text` â The text to use in the link
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)`searchwp_search_results_paginate_links`

Outputs paginated links for search results
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
In addition to the above, arguments from [`paginate_links`](https://developer.wordpress.org/reference/functions/paginate_links/) are also supported.`searchwp_search_result_image`

Outputs Featured Image for the current result
`size` â Image size (Default is `thumb`)`searchwp_search_result_permalink`

Outputs permalink for the current result with the text of the resultâs permalink
`direct` â Whether to link directly to Media files (Default is `'true'`)`searchwp_total_results`

Outputs a total number of results for the current search
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`text` â The text to use alongside the number of results. Supports `%total%` and `%query%` placeholders for precise formatting.`[searchwp_total_results text="Found %total% results for %query%"]` converts to âFound 0 results for example searchâ.Here is a sample of a comprehensive implementation of Shortcodes that searches a supplemental engine with a name of `my_test_engine` :

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <h3>Search Form</h3>    \[searchwp\_search\_form engine="my\_test\_engine" target="http://example.com/supplemental-search/" var="swpquery" button\_text="Find Results"\]         <div class="search-results-wrapper">     \[searchwp\_total\_results text="Found %total% results for %query%" engine="my\_test\_engine" var="swpquery"\]     \[searchwp\_search\_results engine="my\_test\_engine" var="swpquery" posts\_per\_page=10\]     <h2>\[searchwp\_search\_result\_link direct="true"\]</h2>     \[searchwp\_search\_result\_excerpt\]     \[/searchwp\_search\_results\]    </div>         <div class="no-search-results-found">     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_none\]     No results found, please search again.     \[/searchwp\_search\_results\_none\]    </div>         <div class="search-results-pagination">     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_pagination direction="prev" link\_text="Previous" var="swpquery" engine="my\_test\_engine"\]     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_pagination direction="next" link\_text="Next" var="swpquery" engine="my\_test\_engine"\]    </div>  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/eb1ff51275ae7ec8371b51338cdad87a/raw/1a4d4d5460f599ce77a673ceba67d51712016a50/gistfile1.html) [ gistfile1.html ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/eb1ff51275ae7ec8371b51338cdad87a#file-gistfile1-html) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

You can customize the attributes as per the table above.

### [Exclude UI](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/exclude-ui/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

SearchWP allows you to exclude content from search in a number of ways on the main SearchWP settings screen. Exclude UI adds this functionality to edit screens so you can easily exclude specific entries from search with the tick of a checkbox.

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-exclude-ui-gutenberg-3.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-exclude-ui-gutenberg-3.png)**Note:** When working in the block editor with a post type that *does not* support `custom-fields` the Exclude from Search checkbox will display in a meta box

---

### [Xpdf Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/xpdf-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

**Warning:** This Extension requires the use of `exec()` and also requires you to install Xpdf command line tools yourself.

SearchWP offers the unique feature of extracting plain text from PDF files uploaded to your WordPress website. Out of the box, SearchWP attempts to do this using only PHP, but due to the complexity and variation of the PDF format that sometimes results in content not being accurately extracted. Enter [Xpdf](http://www.xpdfreader.com/).

**Xpdf** has a set of command line tools that **must be installed on your server** in order for this Extension to work. Instructions are included here.

Using the Xpdf Integration Extension you can offload all the work PHP has to do in processing your PDF files to Xpdfâs command line tools, which are extremely fast and accurate when extracting content from your PDFs. After activating the Extension, you will need to follow the installation instructions. Once installed, SearchWP will offload the PDF content extraction process to Xpdf.

## Installing Xpdf command line tools

Using this extension you can utilize [Xpdf](http://www.xpdfreader.com/) to extract the content from your PDFs.

**IMPORTANT:** Xpdf command line tools are not provided in this Extension download. You *must* follow these instructions to download the command line tools and upload them to a **non-public** (outside your Web root) location.

You can download the Xpdf command line tools for both Windows and Linux at .

### Installation

Once you have downloaded the **command line tools** for your server type:

1. Extract `xpdf-tools-linux-4.03.tar.gz` (the version number may be different)
2. Upload the `pdftotext` binary (found in either the `bin32` or `bin64` directory after extracting, depending on your server architecture) to a **non-public** location, outside your Web root
3. Upload the `pdfinfo` binary (found in either the `bin32` or `bin64` directory after extracting, depending on your server architecture) to a **non-public** location, outside your Web root
4. Ensure that both `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo` have execute permissions for the PHP user on your server

The last step is to tell SearchWP Xpdf Integration where you installed `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo`. To do this:

Add the following to your [SearchWP Customizations](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/creating-searchwp-customizations-plugin/) plugin, **replacing** `/path/to/pdftotext` with the actual path **to the `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo` binaries (not the folder)** on your server.

```

// Tell SearchWP the location of the pdftotext binary.
add_filter( 'searchwp_xpdf_path', function() {
    return '/home/johndoe/pdftotext'; // Full absolute path to the binary NOT A FOLDER, NOT A URL.
} );

// Tell SearchWP the location of the pdfinfo binary.
add_filter( 'searchwp_pdfinfo_path', function() {
    return '/home/johndoe/pdfinfo'; // Full absolute path to the binary NOT A FOLDER, NOT A URL.
} );
```

Thatâs it!

## Adding PDF password support in Xpdf Integration

[Xpdf](http://www.foolabs.com/xpdf/) **does** support parsing of password protected (read: not *encrypted*) PDFs using the searchwp\_xpdf\_command filter. This filter allows you to directly manipulate the command being executed to fire Xpdf, and since Xpdf supports an option to include a password, you can go ahead and do that like so:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_searchwp\_xpdf\_command( $cmd, $filename ) {     return $cmd . ' -upw password';    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_xpdf\_command', 'my\_searchwp\_xpdf\_command', 10, 2 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/6b9c5918e0764417a6417e0baf1531c5/raw/82af1ad3b39a2d3aeed95e8017e8b20a4bf90add/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/6b9c5918e0764417a6417e0baf1531c5#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

## Manually Testing Xpdf Integration

After uploading and activating the Xpdf Integration Extension and defining your path to `pdftotext`, you can manually confirm that Xpdf text extraction is working as expected on specific PDFs uploaded to your Media library. Begin by going to the SearchWP Settings screen (Settings > SearchWP) and find the Xpdf Integration link in the Extensions on the SearchWP settings screen.

On the Xpdf Integration Testing screen, you can enter in the ID of the PDF youâd like to test:

![Screen Shot 2013-12-09 at 11.34.24 AM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2013-12-09-at-11.34.24-AM.png)*The ID can be found by navigating to your Media section and then clicking the Edit link of your PDF, the ID will be in the URL, followed by* `post=`

After submitting a valid ID you will be given a detailed log of the steps taken by the Xpdf Integration Extension as well as any failure points that may have occurred. Youâre also shown the exact content Xpdf extracted from the PDF:

![Screen Shot 2013-12-09 at 11.36.16 AM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2013-12-09-at-11.36.16-AM.png)If the log displays a point of failure, please include that in any support requests you submit.

## Xpdf Integration error codes

If Xpdf had any issue running, one of the error codes listed below will be indicated in the log.

Exit CodeDescription0Command executed successfully1Catch-all for any unspecified error2Permissions issue, check to ensure `www-data` (or your web server user) has permissions to execute `pdftotext`11Segmentation fault. Are you using the proper Xpdf binary for your server environment?126There is a permissions problem executing pdftotext from the web server user. Please check with your host to ensure proper permissions.127Your server was not able to execute pdftotext. Please check with your host to ensure the web server user can execute pdftotext.139There is a permissions problem executing pdftotext from the web server user. Please check with your host to ensure proper permissions.

---

### [Boolean Search](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/boolean-search/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

Boolean Search requires no configuration. It adds support for excluding results based on keyword. For instance, if the search query is âcats -dogs -birdsâ only entries with âcatsâ and without both âdogsâ and âbirdsâ will be included.

---

### [WP Job Manager Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/wp-job-manager-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[WP Job Manager](https://wpjobmanager.com/) is a popular, well written job board plugin with a thriving add-on library. WP Job Manager offers various search implementations for itself and add-ons. If you would like to enhance the keyword (*only*) aspect of those search fields, WP Job Manager Integration will do the trick.

Just by activating WP Job Manager Integration youâll automatically enable SearchWP to handle the keyword search field for both WP Job Manager core and the Resume add-on. All of the other filters will remain functional an untouched, this extension merely restricts the keyword-provided results.

### Filters

There are a number of filters to help with your WP Job Manager Integration implementation:

**`searchwp_wpjmi_hijack_job_listing_search`** Whether the extension will integrate with Job Listing searches. Default is **true**, to disable:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // disable SearchWP integration with WP Job Manager Job Listing searches    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_hijack\_job\_listing\_search', '\_\_return\_false' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793075/raw/23e7f24095eee2e5c93be4e4cccd41c9101d0fbf/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793075#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_job_engine`** Which search engine configuration to use for Job Listing searches. Default is `default`, but you can easily use a Supplemental Search Engine:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // use my\_search\_engine instead of the default search engine when performing    // Job Listing searches in WP Job Manager    function my\_searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine() {     return 'my\_search\_engine';    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine', 'my\_searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793116/raw/27443be11e635f6d996f67c9442b6d1739b9cd0e/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793116#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_job_post_type_override`** WP Job Manager appropriately limits Job Listing search results to the expected `post_type` but if you want to override that and force the search engine configuration to take precedence, you can do that:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // force SearchWP configuration to override WP Job Manager's when performing a Job Listing search    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_post\_type\_override', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793149/raw/f0d9663762211ac8ac92fd509f401d19e3efaad7/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793149#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_hijack_resume_search`** Whether the extension will integrate with Resume searches. Default is **true**, to disable:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php         // disable SearchWP integration with WP Job Manager Resume searches    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_hijack\_resume\_search', '\_\_return\_false' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793172/raw/d3a947c86b7b7d7ccd8195c1b8ee0e5543caed6a/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793172#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_resume_engine`** Which search engine configuration to use for Resume searches. Default is `default`, but you can easily use a Supplemental Search Engine:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

**`searchwp_wpjmi_resume_post_type_override`** WP Job Manager appropriately limits Resume search results to the expected `post_type` but if you want to override that and force the search engine configuration to take precedence, you can do that:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php         // force SearchWP configuration to override WP Job Manager's when performing a Job Listing search    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_resume\_post\_type\_override', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793202/raw/48c5224c9b15b1bb9d29ad3032a2edad522e450f/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793202#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_bubble_featured`** As of version `1.5.12` WP Job Manager Integration will bubble Featured listings to the top of results. This behavior can be prevented by adding the following to your themeâs `functions.php`:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Prevent SearchWP WP Job Manager Integration from bubbling Featured    // listings to the top of search results.    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_bubble\_featured', '\_\_return\_false' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/a1812a7f2ce86cec4ad426dc0b4ef605/raw/c238b6efa2a7719c85cf28cbc2ee37f6673a175a/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/a1812a7f2ce86cec4ad426dc0b4ef605#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

### [bbPress Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/bbpress-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

By default, bbPress registers itâs post types to be excluded from search. Use the bbPress Integration Extension to allow SearchWP to **include bbPress content in search results**.

Whatâs most useful about this Extension is the ability to have search weight normally found in Replies actually count toward the Topic by enabling post parent attribution when configuring Replies:

[![SearchWP bbPress Integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-bbpress.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-bbpress.png)When enabled, search results will **point to the parent Topic instead of the single Reply**, which is awesome.

---

### [Term Archive Priority](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/term-archive-priority/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

**The Term Archive Priority extension is deprecated**
Please use a [Taxonomy source](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/#source-settings) instead.

The Term Archive Priority Extension will force taxonomy term archive pages to bubble to the top of search results when a match is found. This allows you to search by taxonomy term and have applicable taxonomy archive pages show up within your search results!

**This Extension only applies to supplemental search engines.**

Out of the box, all supplemental search engines will apply this change when the plugin is active, but you can filter that down to specific supplemental search engines by doing something like the following:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_maybe\_include\_term\_archive( $include, $engine, $terms ) {     // only have term archives included for supplemental search engine with name 'supplemental'     return ( $engine == 'supplemental' ) ? true : false;    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_term\_archive\_enabled', 'my\_maybe\_include\_term\_archive', 10, 3 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/eeab34945f977de707eb/raw/12e90e03226fb21b169517b1f69295648a875e2b/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/eeab34945f977de707eb#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**NOTE:** by default, Term Archive Priority supports *exact* taxonomy term matches. If you would like to enable matching of any word within a taxonomy term, use the following filter:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_tax\_term\_or\_logic', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9925992/raw/306b91b227656d1ff4c3e87eeaf43fe031384eed/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9925992#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

Once activated, *you will need to customize your search results loop so as to account for term archive pages in the results*. This is a full theme template example outlining how to properly implement the Term Archive Priority Extension:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        /\* Template Name: SearchWP Term Archive Priority Sample \*/        global $post;        // Retrieve applicable query parameters.    $search\_query = isset( $\_GET\['searchwp'\] ) ? sanitize\_text\_field( $\_GET\['searchwp'\] ) : null;    $search\_page = isset( $\_GET\['swppg'\] ) ? absint( $\_GET\['swppg'\] ) : 1;        // Perform the search.    $search\_results = \[\];    $search\_pagination = '';    if ( ! empty( $search\_query ) && class\_exists( '\\\\SearchWP\\\\Query' ) ) {     $searchwp\_query = new \\SearchWP\\Query( $search\_query, \[     'engine' => 'supplemental', // The Engine name.     'fields' => 'all', // Load proper native objects of each result.     'page' => $search\_page,     \] );         $search\_results = $searchwp\_query->get\_results();         $search\_pagination = paginate\_links( array(     'format' => '?swppg=%#%',     'current' => $search\_page,     'total' => $searchwp\_query->max\_num\_pages,     ) );    }        get\_header(); ?>        <div id="primary" class="content-area">     <main id="main" class="site-main" role="main">         <header class="page-header">     <h1 class="page-title">     <?php if ( ! empty( $search\_query ) ) : ?>     <?php printf( \_\_( 'Search Results for: %s' ), esc\_html( $search\_query ) ); ?>     <?php else : ?>     SearchWP Supplemental Search     <?php endif; ?>     </h1>         <!-- BEGIN Supplemental Engine Search form -->     <form role="search" method="get" class="search-form"     action="<?php echo site\_url( 'search-results/' ); ?>">     <label>     <span class="screen-reader-text">     <?php echo \_x( 'Search for:', 'label' ) ?>     </span>     <input type="search" class="search-field"     name="searchwp"     placeholder="<?php echo esc\_attr\_x( 'Search...', 'placeholder' ) ?>"     value="<?php echo isset( $\_GET\['searchwp'\] ) ? esc\_attr( $\_GET\['searchwp'\] ) : '' ?>"     title="<?php echo esc\_attr\_x( 'Search for:', 'label' ) ?>" />     </label>     <input type="submit" class="search-submit"     value="<?php echo esc\_attr\_x( 'Search', 'submit button' ) ?>" />     </form>     <!-- END Supplemental Engine Search form -->     </header>         <?php if ( ! empty( $search\_query ) && ! empty( $search\_results ) ) : ?>     <?php foreach ( $search\_results as $search\_result ) : ?>     <article class="page hentry search-result">     <?php     switch( get\_class( $search\_result ) ) {     case 'WP\_Post':     $post = $search\_result;     ?>     <header class="entry-header"><h2 class="entry-title">     <a href="<?php echo get\_permalink(); ?>"><?php the\_title(); ?></a>     </h2></header>     <div class="entry-summary"><?php the\_excerpt(); ?></div>     <?php     wp\_reset\_postdata();     break;     case 'SearchWPTermResult':     ?>     <header class="entry-header"><h2 class="entry-title">     <a href="<?php echo $search\_result->link; ?>">     <?php echo $search\_result->taxonomy; ?>: <?php echo $search\_result->name; ?>     </a>     </h2></header>     <div class="entry-summary">     <p><?php echo $search\_result->description; ?></p>     </div>     <?php     break;     }     ?>     </article>     <?php endforeach; ?>         <?php if ( $searchwp\_query->max\_num\_pages > 1 ) : ?>     <div class="navigation pagination" role="navigation">     <h2 class="screen-reader-text">Results navigation</h2>     <div class="nav-links"><?php echo wp\_kses\_post( $search\_pagination ); ?></div>     </div>     <?php endif; ?>     <?php elseif ( ! empty( $search\_query ) ) : ?>     <p>No results found, please search again.</p>     <?php endif; ?>         </main> <!-- .site-main -->    </div> <!-- .content-area -->        <?php get\_footer(); ?>  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/c4edb1cb21f7621d7deb0bd2587ca6d0/raw/ff7bacbac799e15ca51329122147807cba1cab1c/template-searchwp-term-archive-priority-sample.php) [ template-searchwp-term-archive-priority-sample.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/c4edb1cb21f7621d7deb0bd2587ca6d0#file-template-searchwp-term-archive-priority-sample-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

### [Enable Media Replace Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/enable-media-replace-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[Enable Media Replace](https://wordpress.org/plugins/enable-media-replace/) is a popular plugin that allows you to replace existing files within your WordPress Media library. This process is really handy because it helps you to avoid having to update existing links used throughout your site that reference Media library items.

The Enable Media Replace Integration Extension for SearchWP augments this process by telling SearchWP to update file content when Enable Media Replace is used to update a file within your Media library.

---

### [Source - Gravity Forms](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/source-gravity-forms/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-card-gravity-forms.png)[Gravity Forms](https://www.gravityforms.com/) is a premier forms plugin for WordPress. It is immensely popular and even more capable. This Extension allows you to use Gravity Forms form entries as a custom Source for SearchWP, making it possible to search Gravity Forms Entries with SearchWP!

Table of Contents

- [Setup](#setup)
- [Results](#results)

## Adding Gravity Forms Entries to SearchWPâs Index

Once **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** has been installed and activated, your Gravity Forms will appear in the Sources list for your SearchWP Engine.

**Note:** This custom Source can be used only with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Letâs say you were building a feature request area on your website and using Gravity Forms to collect entries.

 [![Screenshot of Gravity Forms setup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-form-form.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-form-form.png) 

You could utilize SearchWP to allow customers to first search existing entries before submitting their own, so they could join in up-voting the existing submission instead of creating a duplicate.

The first step would be to create a new Supplemental Engine that has our Feature Request Form as the Source:

 [![Adding Gravity Form to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source.png) 

With the form chosen (note that you can combine entries from multiple Gravity Forms into a single Engine if youâd like!) you can pick and choose which field(s) youâd like to consider for searching:

 [![Adding Gravity Forms attributes to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source-attributes.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source-attributes.gif) 

SearchWP allows you to search the entire Gravity Forms Entry as a whole, optionally consider any Notes that have been added, or choose exactly which field(s) should be considered when searching, each with its own relevancy weight!

## Displaying Gravity Forms Entries as Results

The **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** Extension will make available Gravity Forms Entries as search results. This integrates as any Source would when working with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Unlike a native WordPress search that has [The Loop](https://codex.wordpress.org/The_Loop) which is an array of `WP_Post` objects that WordPressâ API interacts with, the results set for a Supplemental Engine can be made up of any SearchWP Source object.

Gravity Forms does not have a proper object for Form Entries, each Entry is an array of key value pairs. As a convenience, **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** will return results in a wrapper object:

`\SearchWP\Sources\GravityForms\Entry`

This will help as you iterate over your results array:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        $searchwp = new \\SearchWP\\Query( 'marketing', \[     'engine' => 'gravity',     'fields' => 'all',    \] );        foreach ( $searchwp->results as $result ) {     switch ( get\_class( $result ) ) {     case 'SearchWP\\Sources\\GravityForms\\Entry':          // NOTE: Gravity Forms Field values are keyed by the Field ID in the Form editor.          ?>     <div class="result">     Form ID: <?php echo esc\_html( $result->id ); ?><br>     Name: <?php echo esc\_html( $result->{'1'} ); ?><br>     Email: <?php echo esc\_html( $result->{'2'} ); ?><br>     Type: <?php echo esc\_html( $result->{'3'} ); ?><br>     Feature: <?php echo esc\_html( $result->{'4'} ); ?>     </div>     <?php     break;         default:     // Another Source was added to the SearchWP Engine.     print\_r( $result );     }    }  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/7c908aa998e63e2c72648a1a2102b0e7/raw/20a69f725a1620817167c14f35affc6693a2d095/page.php) [ page.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/7c908aa998e63e2c72648a1a2102b0e7#file-page-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**Note:** You can prevent SearchWP from wrapping results in the class with the following hook:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        add\_filter( 'searchwp\\source\\gravity\_forms\\entry\\raw', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/f0603d3f0dfdc50f3eaa5c60754bb54b/raw/f6f72dadba9c34559e1e99ef37064b9ce1e63724/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/f0603d3f0dfdc50f3eaa5c60754bb54b#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** allows you to make any Gravity Forms Entry searchable using SearchWP! You can make as many SearchWP Engines with as many Gravity Forms Sources as youâd like, each with their own configuration of Fields and/or Notes. Use SearchWPâs capable search algorithm to make your Gravity Forms Entries searchable!

---

### [HeroThemes Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/herothemes-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[![Screen Shot 2016-06-29 at 2.16.14 PM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2016-06-29-at-2.16.14-PM-300x103.png)](https://herothemes.com/ref/39/)[HeroThemes](https://herothemes.com/ref/39/) makes fantastic products to help set up an awesomely usable knowledge base full of content. If youâd like to integrate SearchWPâs results, all you need to do is activate and install this extension.

---

### [Flatsome Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/flatsome-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-card-flatsome-integration.png)[Flatsome](https://flatsome3.uxthemes.com/) is a very popular WooCommerce & Business theme that has a great looking live search feature. Using the Flatsome Integration Extension for SearchWP you can directly utilize SearchWP for searches performed using that feature of Flatsome.

---

### [MyListing Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/mylisting-integration/)

**Published:** December 11, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/banner-mylisting.png)](https://mylistingtheme.com/)[MyListing](https://mylistingtheme.com/) is a very popular theme designed to facilitate a custom directory website. If youâre using MyListing and SearchWP this Integration Extension will greatly improve the keyword search in MyListing!

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/listing-type-1030x603.jpg)](https://mylistingtheme.com/)

---

### [WooCommerce Product Table Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/woocommerce-product-table-integration/)

**Published:** December 13, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

[![Barn2 Plugins logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/extension-banner-woocommerce-product-tables.png)](https://barn2.co.uk/wordpress-plugins/woocommerce-product-table/)The latest version of WooCommerce Product Table has full SearchWP integration and this extension is no longer needed.

The SearchWP WooCommerce Product Table Integration provides integration between SearchWP and [WooCommerce Product Table](https://barn2.co.uk/wordpress-plugins/woocommerce-product-table/), the easy way to speed up shopping by listing your products in a quick order form. It upgrades the basic search box which comes with WooCommerce Product Table so that your customers can benefit from SearchWPâs advanced search features.

Simply install the integration extension along with SearchWP and WooCommerce Product Table, and enable the [lazy load](https://barn2.co.uk/kb/lazy-load/) option in WooCommerce Product Table. The search box above your product tables will be powered by SearchWP.

---

### [Source - WPForms](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/source-wpforms/)

**Published:** December 13, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

![WPForms logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpforms-searchwp-banner.png)[WPForms](https://wpforms.com/) is the leading drag-and-drop form builder plugin for WordPress. Itâs widely recognized for its user-friendly interface and powerful capabilities. This Extension enables you to utilize WPForms entries as a custom Source for SearchWP, making it possible to search WPForms entries with SearchWP!

Table of Contents

- [Setup](#setup)
- [Results](#results)

## Adding WPForms Entries to SearchWPâs Index

Once the **SearchWP Source â WPForms** extension has been installed and activated, your forms by WPForms will appear in the Sources list for your SearchWP Engine.

**Note:** This custom Source can be used only with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Letâs say you were building a Suggestion request area on your website and using WPForms Suggestion Form to collect entries.

 [![Screenshot of WPForms setup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpforms-form.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpforms-form.png) 

You could utilize SearchWP to allow customers to first search existing entries before submitting their own, so they could join in up-voting the existing submission instead of creating a duplicate.

The first step would be to create a new Supplemental Engine that has our Suggestion Form as the Source:

 ![Adding WPForm to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/wpforms-searchwp-source-1.png) 

Please note that you can add multiple forms by WPForms to the same engine if you want.

Once the form has been added to the SearchWP engine, all the searchable form fields are added as attributes to the engine source. You can click on **Add/Remove Attributes** to change the list of fields you want to search.

## Displaying WPForms Entries as Results

The **SearchWP Source â WPForms** Extension will make available WPForms Entries as search results. This integrates as any Source would when working with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Unlike a native WordPress search that has [The Loop](https://codex.wordpress.org/The_Loop) which is an array of `WP_Post` objects that WordPressâ API interacts with, the results set for a Supplemental Engine can be made up of any SearchWP Source object.

WPForms uses a stdClass for Form Entries. As a convenience, **SearchWP Source â WPForms** will return results in a wrapper object:

`\SearchWP\Sources\WPForms\Entry`

This will help as you iterate over your results array:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        $searchwp = new \\SearchWP\\Query( 'marketing', \[     'engine' => 'wpforms',     'fields' => 'all',    \] );        foreach ( $searchwp->results as $result ) {     switch ( get\_class( $result ) ) {     case 'SearchWP\\Sources\\WPForms\\Entry':          $form\_id = $search\_result->form\_id;     $entry\_id = $search\_result->entry\_id;     // NOTE: WPForms Field values are stored in the entry fields property as an array.     // Each field has the array key equal to the order they were added to the form editor.     $fields = $search\_result->fields;          ?>     <div class="result">     Form ID: <?php echo esc\_html( $form\_id ); ?><br>     Entry ID: <?php echo esc\_html( $entry\_id ); ?><br>     Name: <?php echo esc\_html( $fields\[0\]\['value'\] ); ?><br>     Email: <?php echo esc\_html( $fields\[1\]\['value'\] ); ?><br>     Subject: <?php echo esc\_html( $fields\[5\]\['value'\] ); ?><br>     Comment: <?php echo esc\_html( $fields\[2\]\['value'\] ); ?><br>     </div>     <?php     break;         default:     // Another Source was added to the SearchWP Engine.     print\_r( $result );     }    }  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/f298042908b9bd501cac1ad84815c254/raw/4cd4a39beeff144ec587f22832b8cba9bec478f4/page.php) [ page.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/f298042908b9bd501cac1ad84815c254#file-page-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**Note:** You can prevent SearchWP from wrapping results in the class and return instead a stdClass with the following hook:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        add\_filter( 'searchwp\\source\\wpforms\\entry\\raw', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/1be3c42ee881ea2e8d4d470ca5196ef6/raw/0c07e23fd4079a23d0a2158b8cdb9280f8f66643/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/1be3c42ee881ea2e8d4d470ca5196ef6#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**SearchWP Source â WPForms** allows you to make any WPForms Entry searchable using SearchWP! You can make as many SearchWP Engines with as many WPForms Sources as youâd like, each with their own configuration of Fields. Use SearchWPâs capable search algorithm to make your WPForms Entries searchable!

---

### [FacetWP](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/facetwp/)

**Published:** December 13, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

This is a **third party** extension

 [![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/facetwp-1-300x96.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/facetwp-1.png)[FacetWP](https://facetwp.com/?ref=1) adds Amazon-like filtering to any WordPress content listing. It allows users to quickly browse and drill-down through your siteâs content to find exactly what theyâre looking for.

[See the demo](https://facetwp.com/demo/cars/?ref=1)

### Integration

FacetWP includes a âSearchâ facet, which renders an input box. When configuring a search facet, a âSearch Engineâ setting lets you choose a SearchWP engine. When a SearchWP engine is selected, the results will be retrieved directly from the SearchWP API.

### Support

**FacetWP is a 3rd-party plugin.** Please [contact the FacetWP support team](https://facetwp.com/contact/) for more information or pre-sales questions.

---

### [Ajax Load More](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/ajax-load-more/)

**Published:** December 13, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

This is a **third party** extension

 [![Ajax Load More logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/ajax-load-more-banner@2x.png)](https://connekthq.com/plugins/ajax-load-more/)[Ajax Load More](https://connekthq.com/plugins/ajax-load-more/) is an extremely popular WordPress plugin that facilitates infinite scrolling behavior.

The Connekt team has built an integration for Ajax Load More and SearchWP, which allows your Ajax Load More implementations to work with SearchWP where applicable.

---

### [BasePress Knowledge Base Integration](https://searchwp.com/documentation/extensions/basepress-knowledge-base-integration/)

**Published:** December 13, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

This is a **third party** extension

 [![BasePress logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/basepress-card.png)](https://wordpress.org/plugins/basepress-searchwp-integration/)[BasePress Knowledge Base + SearchWP Integration](https://wordpress.org/plugins/basepress-searchwp-integration/) provides integration between SearchWP and [BasePress](https://codesavory.com/), a powerful (and great looking!) knowledge base.

From the documentation:

> BasePress was developed with a search feature of its own which filters the results according to the Knowledge Base visited, by language when used with WPML and by BasePress content restrictions. This add-on makes it possible to use SearchWP retaining the automatic filtering of the content. Also the add-on is necessary for SearchWP to power BasePress live Ajax results as well.

### Support

**BasePress Knowledge Base + SearchWP Integration is a 3rd-party plugin.** Please [contact the BasePress support team](https://codesavory.com/contact-us/) for more information or other questions.

---

### [Classes](https://searchwp.com/documentation/classes/)

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

# SearchWP Classes

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

### [Hooks](https://searchwp.com/documentation/hooks/)

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

---

### [FTC Disclosure](https://searchwp.com/ftc-disclosure/)

**Published:** October 8, 2024
**Author:** Pavlo

**Content:**

Weâre required by law to comply with the Federal Trade Commissionâs 16 CFR, Part 255: âGuides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising.â

Additionally, we believe that being transparent with our audience is the right thing to do. So weâve created this disclosure page to let you know that we do make money from this website.

If youâre using this website, then you should know:

- We get direct benefit from every post and page on this website. Whether that is monetary or simply the joy of helping others, we benefit from it.
- If you see an external link with a prefix /refer/, then itâs an affiliate link. That means that if you click on that link and purchase a product, weâll receive a referral commission at no-additional cost to you.
- We do write about other products and websites associated with this brand, so that means those projects get additional PR and exposure.

**Itâs important that we emphasize that the words on this website are not influenced by any sort of compensation. We only recommend products or services that we use personally and/or believe will add value to our readers.**

If you have any questions regarding the above, then please donât hesitate to contact us by using our [contact page](https://searchwp.com/contact/).

### [WP-CLI](https://searchwp.com/documentation/wp-cli/)

**Published:** January 15, 2021
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

# SearchWP & WP-CLI

Table of Contents

- [Commands](#commands)
- [Examples](#examples)

SearchWP has built-in support for a number of [WP-CLI](https://wp-cli.org/) commands.

## Commands

`wp searchwp index [--site=] [--rebuild]`Build (or rebuild) the SearchWP index`wp searchwp reindex ... [--source=]`Reindex entries`wp searchwp diagnostics  [--source=]`Diagnose information about SearchWPâs index## Examples

Trigger the indexer`wp searchwp index`Rebuild the index`wp searchwp index --rebuild`Reindex `WP_Post` 114`wp searchwp reindex 114`Reindex `WP_Post`s 88 and 314`wp searchwp reindex 88 314`Reindex all `WP_Post`s of Page post type`wp searchwp reindex $(wp post list --post_type=page --format=ids)`Reindex `WP_User` 2`wp searchwp reindex 2 --source=User`Retrieve tokens for `WP_Post` 19`wp searchwp diagnostics tokens 19`Retrieve index status for `WP_User` 7`wp searchwp diagnostics status 7 --source=User`

### [Support & License](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/support-license/)

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

# Support & License

Your SearchWP license entitles you to automatic updates and support. If your license is not active automatic updates are not possible, and requesting support will require the extra burden of validating your license key when opening a ticket.

Your license can be activated by entering its key on the [SearchWP Settings screen](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/settings/).

 [![Screenshot of SearchWP's Support tab](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-support-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-support-1.png) 

Once your license has been activated, automatic updates will be enabled and the ticket submission form will become available.

## License will not activate?

In order for the license to activate, your server must be able to communicate with the licensing server at `searchwp.com`. If that communication is not possible, activation of the license is not possible.

If your network configuration prevents communication with the licensing server, you will need to *manually* apply updates as they become available. A download link to the latest version of SearchWP is always available within your [Account](/account) and release notifications are published (and available via RSS) in the [News](/news/) section.

When requesting support you will need to email  **including your license key** and outlining the issue youâre having.

---

### [Engines](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/engines/)

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

# Engine Configuration

Table of Contents

- [Engine Settings](#engine-settings)
- [Source Settings](#source-settings)
    - [Attributes](#attributes)
    - [Rules](#rules)

The primary way to configure SearchWP is using the **Engines** tab of the SearchWP Algorithm screen:

 [![Screenshot of SearchWP's Engine settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engines-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engines-1.png) 

Specify exactly what to consider when performing searches

## Engine Settings

To edit the settings for an Engine, click the **Source & Settings** button for that engine. A dialog will be presented to you:

 [![Screenshot of SearchWP's Engine Settings dialog](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engine-settings-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engine-settings-1.png) 

Easily control which Sources to consider during searches

**Note:** The *Default* Engine is special in that it is the Engine that will be used for native WordPress searches.

As a result, the only enabled Sources for the Default Engine are Post Types (e.g. Posts, Pages, Custom Post Types). A [supplemental Engine](/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/) can include *any* Source.

Using this dialog you can pick and choose which Sources will be included in search results for this Engine. Further you can:

- Determine whether [keyword stems](#) should be considered
- Designate the Engine as the one to use for searches in the WordPress Admin

## Source Settings

Each SearchWP Engine has a collection of [Sources](/documentation/classes/searchwp-source/). A Source is an âindexableâ type of content on your site. An Engine will include results only from the Sources which have been added to it.

Each Source in turn has its own settings â Attributes and Rules.

**Attributes**Types of content within each Source, e.g. Title, Content, Custom Field, Category, Tag, etc.**Rules**Control whether entries are returned or not based on your defined criteria.Using Attributes and Rules you can customize your Engine to do exactly what you want!

### Attributes

Each Source within a SearchWP Engine has its own unique set of Attributes. You can pick and choose which Attributes are considered during searches for each Source within each Engine by clicking the **Add/Remove Attributes** button for that Source.

For example, when considering Posts, the following Attributes are made available:

 [![Screenshot of Source Attribute management](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-source-attributes-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-source-attributes-1.png) 

Pick and choose which Attributes to consider for each Source

Once youâve decided which Attributes youâd like to consider, each can be given its own relevance weight by adjusting the slider. By default there are three slots on the slider. The further right the slider, the more weight that particular Attribute will be given in the search algorithm.

 [![Screenshot highlighting weight adjustment](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engine-weights.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-engine-weights.png) 

Assign relevance to individual Attributes

**Note:** Adjusting weights can be done at any time *without* needing to rebuild the index. Application of these values is applied as searches are run. There *may* be a time where customizing the Attributes for a Source necessitates that the Index be rebuilt. SearchWP will notify you when that happens.

### Rules

As is the case with Attributes, Rules can be applied per Source per Engine, allowing you to define what should (or should not!) be returned as results. Some example restrictions include:

- Exclude entries in a certain Category
- Only show entries published within the past 6 months
- Limit Media to a file type
- etc.

Clicking the **Edit Rules** button allows you to define the circumstances under which entries should (or should not) be returned.

 [![Screenshot of SearchWP Engine Source Rules management](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-source-rules.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-algorithm-source-rules.png) 

Establish Rules for your Engines

There are some things to note when it comes to setting up your Rules:

- Rules are grouped using conditions either by `IN` logic or `NOT IN` logic.
- *Within Rules* each condition is evaluated alongside the next using `OR` logic, meaning *any* condition can be met.
- When multiple Rules are added, the set of rules is evaluated using `AND` logic, meaning *all* Rules must be satisfied in order to take effect.

Given this setup, you will need to be mindful of the way you are setting up your Rules and conditions. If something seems impossible it may be worth trying a new combination of `OR` and `AND` logic.

### Options

**Transfer weight**When this option is enabled on a source, the entries from this source that match the current search, would not be included in the results. The designated entry to receive the transferred weight would be included instead. For example if a Post ID is specified, if any Post matches the search, the Post with the specified ID will be included in the results in its place.

---

### [Search Metrics](https://searchwp.com/search-metrics/)

**Published:** July 19, 2018
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

An effective on-site search implementation provides unique insight into your visitors. By observing what visitors are searching for you can make educated decisions about areas of improvement when it comes to your content.

**Note:** SearchWP by default includes [Statistics](https://searchwp.com/gain-visitor-insights-search/) within the core plugin while **Metrics** (described here) is a Pro Extension which requires a Pro or Pro Plus license

[SearchWPâs Metrics extension](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/) (requires a Pro or Pro Plus license) collects comprehensive data for on-site search, and provides you with actionable advice about refinements you can make as time goes on.

[Watch Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WvMr0u8qzys)

[![Screenshot of SearchWP Metrics main interface](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim.png)SearchWP Metrics main interface

SearchWPâs Metrics extension provides actionable insights into search traffic on your site.

- What are visitors searching for?
- What are visitors finding when they search?
- How often are visitors searching?
- What searches are (incorrectly) yielding no results and frustrating visitors?
- Do visitors look beyond page 1 of our search results?

Metrics answers questions like these and at the same time provides actionable insights based on the data thatâs collected.

### How Metrics compares to SearchWPâs statistics

SearchWP itself includes [statistics](https://searchwp.com/gain-visitor-insights-search/) that give an overview of on-site search at various time frames. This data is valuable, and Metrics expands on that. With Metrics you can:

- Customize the date range
- Actionable insights based on data collected
- Export data to CSV
- Limit the data to any number of search queries
- Display multiple engines at once for direct comparison
- View additional computed data per engine (e.g. total results viewed)
- Track conversions (clicked search results) for all searches

With all of these tools at your disposal youâre able to get a more detailed picture of search on your site.

## Data within Metrics

On a surface level, Metrics will track what visitors are searching for, when they searched for it, and what results were clicked. With this data you can discover:

- Trends in search over time
- Total number of searches
- Total number of searches that had zero results
- How many results were viewed
- How many searches each user performed
- How many results (on average) are viewed per search
- The average click position of search results

With this data we can gain insight into how well search has been implemented on the site. For example:

**Searches over time** help us to determine whether visitors as a whole are searching more or less often. Is there a trend that aligns with outside influence such as marketing initiatives? Are we seeing more searches because our overall traffic volume is increasing? Did adding that new section to the site result in a drop in searches because visitors are naturally finding information?

**Searches with zero results** indicate where we can improve on the content of our site. Are these searches failing because of industry jargon weâve avoided? Are there zero results because of a common misspelling? [Term Synonyms](https://searchwp.com/extensions/term-synonyms/) can resolve visitor frustration here.

**Popular searches** show us which topics could use even more content, and we can gauge that popularity in context of other popular topics to ensure weâre prioritizing effectively.

*â¦ and more!*

## Gaining actual insight

Data is great, and Metrics has a lot to learn from. Some of the data requires a bit of digging around to become actionable, however. To make that easier, Metrics includes a number of Insights that present the data plainly:

[![Screenshot of Insights within Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-insights.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-insights.png)Insights give actionable advice based on collected data

Using Insights you can review the suggestions and implement changes as you see fit in the context of the site as a whole. Observing Insights over time will allow you to gauge whether or not your content is optimized for your visitors.

---

**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

There is no demo or trial version of SearchWP available. If you have any pre-sale questions regarding how SearchWP will work for you that are not answered in the documentation, please fill out this form and weâll get back to you quickly. Thank you!

### [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/)

**Published:** March 5, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

# Supplemental Engine

Table of Contents

- [Setup](#setup)
- [Form](#form)
- [Results](#results)

SearchWP allows for the setup of any number of [Engines](/documentation/setup/engines/), each with their own individual configuration. This segmentation facilitates multiple customized implementations of search throughout your site.

The only limitation of the Default Engine is that its Sources *must* be based on `WP_Post` objects. Thatâs because WordPress core *assumes* search results to fit within [The Loop](https://codex.wordpress.org/The_Loop) and The Loop is made up of `WP_Post`s. `WP_Post` objects are used for Posts, Pages, Media, and Custom Post Types. That covers a lot!

SearchWP also facilitates searching *custom* Sources, however. In order to fully take advantage of that you will need to setup and implement a Supplemental Engine.

## Setup
