### [WooCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-integration/)

**Published:** November 21, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** WooCommerce is one of the leading e-commerce platforms for WordPress. SearchWP makes searching your WooCommerce store much more powerful than native WordPress search.

**Content:**

**Please note** that this is a Pro-level Extension

WooCommerce uses a post type archive template to facilitate search results. This extension allows SearchWP to work with this default behavior by WooCommerce. This extension also incorporates other features of WooCommerce search such as Layered Navigation.

If you are using the native WordPress search (e.g. your search results URL does not have `post_type=product` *anywhere*) this extension is **not necessary**. As a result, if you would like to disable SearchWPâs notification add the following to your themeâs `functions.php`:

```
// This is for SearchWP 4.
add_filter('searchwp\missing_integration_notices', '__return_false');

// This is for SearchWP 3.
add_filter( 'searchwp_missing_integration_notices', '__return_false' );
```

WooCommerce is one of the leading e-commerce platforms for WordPress. SearchWP makes searching your WooCommerce store much more powerful than native WordPress search. WooCommerce also has itâs own set of search tools and filtering options. WooCommerce Integration aims to bridge the gap between all of WooCommerceâs search tools and SearchWP.

*Simply download WooCommerce Integration and activate it alongside SearchWP. SearchWP extensions are separate plugins that in and of themselves are standalone WordPress plugins.*

## Product Variations

WooCommerce stores Product Variations in a different way than it does Products. There are a few things you can do to integrate Product Variation data alongside your Products depending on how you want results to appear.

If you would like to index Product Variation data alongside Products (allowing Product Variation data to cause the parent Product to appear in search results) you can follow the example outlined by this KB article: [Add WooCommerce Product Variations to Products](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/add-woocommerce-product-variations-to-products/)

Alternatively, you can force Product Variations to appear as their own post type by using this as a starting point: [view code snippet](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/7bb906ef556120e46e09513c2a5539ee)

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Related](https://searchwp.com/extensions/related/)

**Published:** April 16, 2017
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Use SearchWP to power related content on your site! One click integration combined with an awesome template loader makes implementation easy!

**Content:**

**Use SearchWP to power related content on your site!** SearchWP Related was built to work with minimal overhead, while allowing for customization to any degree. Showing related content to visitors is a great way to keep attention on your site! If youâre already using another system for related content, this SearchWP extension will likely save you some overhead by utilizing the content index it already created.

## Setup & Integration

Like all SearchWP Extensions, Related is a standalone WordPress plugin that runs alongside SearchWP. Once installed, Related takes no action by default. You can view the settings screen (found within the SearchWP settings screen)

**Note:** When activating Related on a site with existing content, entry titles will be used as the default keyword list to find related content.

[![SearchWP Related Content Settings](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-related-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-related-1.png)

On this screen you can:

- Enable auto-appending of Related content per post type
- Exclude content (comma separated IDs) from Related content per post type
- Limit potential Related content (comma separated IDs) per post type

When auto-append is enabled for a post type, Related content will be automatically output when viewing a single entry of that post type.

### Customizing **output**: the template loader

As is the case with [Live Search](https://searchwp.com/extensions/live-search/), Related also uses a template loader to allow for **full customization** of Related results. There is a default template that looks something like this:

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2017-04-14-at-10.10.33-AM.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2017-04-14-at-10.10.33-AM.png)The default Related template will match your theme font and colors (to an extent)

In the Related plugin folder there is another folder named templates containing this default Related template (`related.php`) used to display Related content.

`~/wp-content/plugins/searchwp-related/templates/related.php`

The contents of this file are as follows: [`related.php`](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/5a39677b04bdb13e4021d266cd64911a)

***Do not edit this file directly.*** If you install an update to Related, any customizations you make to this file ***will be overwritten!***

Instead, you can utilize the template loader built into Related to add your own custom template within your theme.

To customize the output of Related content, create a new folder within your theme and paste a copy of the default Related template into that folder, keeping the same filename:

`~/wp-content/themes/my-theme/searchwp-related/related.php`

You can fully customize this template to meet your needs. Itâs based on The Loop, and you can do anything within the template you would in any other theme template.

#### Template Options

While the template used to show Related content is *mostly* a standard theme template, you can influence how Related behaves by editing the optional comment block at the top of the file:

`/*** SearchWP Engine: default* Maximum Results: 3*/```

You can customize the SearchWP engine used, and the maximum number of Related entries displayed by editing the applicable line at the top of the template.

You can also customize results templates **per post type** by appending a hyphen and the post type to the file name. For example, if you were to create a template with this filename:

`~/wp-content/themes/my-theme/searchwp-related/related-page.php`

It would be used *only* for Pages. All other post types would use the base template.

### Programmatically retrieve Related content

If you want to use the template loader but in a different spot than the auto-append checkbox uses, leave that checkbox unticked and edit your template file to include the following where you want Related output:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // This should be within your single.php or other applicable template that has the $post defined    $related = new SearchWP\_Related\\Template();    echo $related->get\_template();  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/ee12559d819601f88529ec03711019fe/raw/70705ab1a60a9524f7c2e30478f8ae1147b2a880/single.php) [ single.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/ee12559d819601f88529ec03711019fe#file-single-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

You can also skip the template loader entirely and instead retrieve Related content directly in your theme code. Hereâs how you can do that:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        /\*     \* Programmatically find Related content from SearchWP Related     \*/        // Instantiate SearchWP Related    $searchwp\_related = new SearchWP\_Related();        // Use the keywords as defined in the SearchWP Related meta box    $keywords = get\_post\_meta( get\_the\_ID(), $searchwp\_related->meta\_key, true );        $args = array(     's' => $keywords, // The stored keywords to use     'engine' => 'default', // the SearchWP engine to use     'posts\_per\_page' => 3, // how many entries to find    );        // Retrieve Related content for the current post    $related\_content = $searchwp\_related->get( $args );        // Returns an array of Post objects for you to loop through    print\_r( $related\_content );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/cb12ef2d72c0fc9be6c2de94c30bff12/raw/469029a1038ce5598e187db9587dc28bcd1a7142/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/cb12ef2d72c0fc9be6c2de94c30bff12#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

There are a number of arguments you can pass to customize how Related will find related content, hereâs a full breakdown of the possible arguments:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Use the saved keywords from the meta box    $keywords = get\_post\_meta( get\_the\_ID(), $searchwp\_related->meta\_key, true );        $defaults = array(     'engine' => 'default', // Engine to use     's' => $keywords, // Terms to search     'fields' => 'all', // Return post objects, use 'ids' to return IDs     'posts\_per\_page' => 3, // How many results to return     'log' => false, // Log the search?     'post\_\_in' => array(), // Limit results pool?     'post\_\_not\_in' => array() // Exclude posts from results?    );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/edc68e5d2084aed89c89ede635f1bc98/raw/2df1e041aebf44ee660bd1d42e88c631baa70e49/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/edc68e5d2084aed89c89ede635f1bc98#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

Itâs suggested that you set up some sort of caching (e.g. via [Transient](https://codex.wordpress.org/Transients_API)) to reduce resource usage.

### Customizing **input**: defining Related content

Related by default will build a keyword list from the title of your entries. Common words are removed so as to increase relevancy of Related results. On every entry edit screen, Related will output a meta box allowing you to customize what keyword(s) are used to determine Related content:

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-related.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-related.gif)Real time results samples when customizing keyword(s)

A set of three sample results are displayed, allowing you to customize the keyword(s) youâre using if youâd like. This is just a sample, the template loader controls how the results are displayed on the front end, and SearchWP determines which entries to display at runtime.

The Results samples will include all the available engines. If you want to limit the samples displayed to only some engines you can use this filter:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Define the engines to use for the metabox samples.    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_related\_meta\_box\_sample\_engines', function( $engines ) {     return \[ 'default', 'products' \];    } );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/cd479055b77bb3e2374de2f40c0f88d9/raw/faeaef526a1c0ca9891030c660dead557465e306/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/cd479055b77bb3e2374de2f40c0f88d9#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

**Extension Types:** Pro

### [EDD Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/easy-digital-downloads-edd-integration/)

**Published:** July 22, 2015
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Automatically integrate with EDD's Shortcodes and more

**Content:**

Easy Digital Downloads is a fantastic plugin that makes selling digital products super easy. EDD comes with a number of features but one in particular requires a bridge for SearchWP. EDD has a `[``downloads]` Shortcode that can be used to output your Downloads, and some themes/plugins have implemented their own search feature. EDD Integration allows you to couple with that functionality and use SearchWP to provide the results.

### Available hooks

**` searchwp_edd_engine `**
Set which SearchWP engine to use for the `[``downloads]` Shortcode (default is `default`)

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_searchwp\_edd\_engine( $engine, $query, $atts ) {     return 'default'; // the name of the engine you want to use for the \[downloads\] Shortcode    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_edd\_engine', 'my\_searchwp\_edd\_engine', 10, 3 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/9a20381a1e8e56f0d1be262c2373bb79/raw/8dc2dc921cbda52b5e8c412f11445694c6531d56/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/9a20381a1e8e56f0d1be262c2373bb79#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

**Extension Types:** Pro

### [Xpdf Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/xpdf-integration/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** While SearchWP does the best it can to extract content from your PDFs out of the box using PHP only, Xpdf does an even better job.

**Content:**

**Warning:** This Extension requires the use of `exec()` and also requires you to install Xpdf command line tools yourself.

SearchWP offers the unique feature of extracting plain text from PDF files uploaded to your WordPress website. Out of the box, SearchWP attempts to do this using only PHP, but due to the complexity and variation of the PDF format that sometimes results in content not being accurately extracted. Enter [Xpdf](http://www.xpdfreader.com/).

**Xpdf** has a set of command line tools that **must be installed on your server** in order for this Extension to work. Instructions are included here.

Using the Xpdf Integration Extension you can offload all the work PHP has to do in processing your PDF files to Xpdfâs command line tools, which are extremely fast and accurate when extracting content from your PDFs. After activating the Extension, you will need to follow the installation instructions. Once installed, SearchWP will offload the PDF content extraction process to Xpdf.

## Installing Xpdf command line tools

Using this extension you can utilize [Xpdf](http://www.xpdfreader.com/) to extract the content from your PDFs.

**IMPORTANT:** Xpdf command line tools are not provided in this Extension download. You *must* follow these instructions to download the command line tools and upload them to a **non-public** (outside your Web root) location.

You can download the Xpdf command line tools for both Windows and Linux at .

### Installation

Once you have downloaded the **command line tools** for your server type:

1. Extract `xpdf-tools-linux-4.03.tar.gz` (the version number may be different)
2. Upload the `pdftotext` binary (found in either the `bin32` or `bin64` directory after extracting, depending on your server architecture) to a **non-public** location, outside your Web root
3. Upload the `pdfinfo` binary (found in either the `bin32` or `bin64` directory after extracting, depending on your server architecture) to a **non-public** location, outside your Web root
4. Ensure that both `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo` have execute permissions for the PHP user on your server

The last step is to tell SearchWP Xpdf Integration where you installed `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo`. To do this:

Add the following to your [SearchWP Customizations](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/creating-searchwp-customizations-plugin/) plugin, **replacing** `/path/to/pdftotext` with the actual path **to the `pdftotext` and `pdfinfo` binaries (not the folder)** on your server.

```
// Tell SearchWP the location of the pdftotext binary.
add_filter( 'searchwp_xpdf_path', function() {
    return '/home/johndoe/pdftotext'; // Full absolute path to the binary NOT A FOLDER, NOT A URL.
} );

// Tell SearchWP the location of the pdfinfo binary.
add_filter( 'searchwp_pdfinfo_path', function() {
    return '/home/johndoe/pdfinfo'; // Full absolute path to the binary NOT A FOLDER, NOT A URL.
} );
```

Thatâs it!

## Adding PDF password support in Xpdf Integration

[Xpdf](http://www.foolabs.com/xpdf/) **does** support parsing of password protected (read: not *encrypted*) PDFs using the searchwp\_xpdf\_command filter. This filter allows you to directly manipulate the command being executed to fire Xpdf, and since Xpdf supports an option to include a password, you can go ahead and do that like so:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_searchwp\_xpdf\_command( $cmd, $filename ) {     return $cmd . ' -upw password';    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_xpdf\_command', 'my\_searchwp\_xpdf\_command', 10, 2 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/6b9c5918e0764417a6417e0baf1531c5/raw/82af1ad3b39a2d3aeed95e8017e8b20a4bf90add/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/6b9c5918e0764417a6417e0baf1531c5#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

## Manually Testing Xpdf Integration

After uploading and activating the Xpdf Integration Extension and defining your path to `pdftotext`, you can manually confirm that Xpdf text extraction is working as expected on specific PDFs uploaded to your Media library. Begin by going to the SearchWP Settings screen (Settings > SearchWP) and find the Xpdf Integration link in the Extensions on the SearchWP settings screen.

On the Xpdf Integration Testing screen, you can enter in the ID of the PDF youâd like to test:

![Screen Shot 2013-12-09 at 11.34.24 AM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2013-12-09-at-11.34.24-AM.png)

*The ID can be found by navigating to your Media section and then clicking the Edit link of your PDF, the ID will be in the URL, followed by* `post=`

After submitting a valid ID you will be given a detailed log of the steps taken by the Xpdf Integration Extension as well as any failure points that may have occurred. Youâre also shown the exact content Xpdf extracted from the PDF:

![Screen Shot 2013-12-09 at 11.36.16 AM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2013-12-09-at-11.36.16-AM.png)

If the log displays a point of failure, please include that in any support requests you submit.

## Xpdf Integration error codes

If Xpdf had any issue running, one of the error codes listed below will be indicated in the log.

Exit CodeDescription0Command executed successfully1Catch-all for any unspecified error2Permissions issue, check to ensure `www-data` (or your web server user) has permissions to execute `pdftotext`11Segmentation fault. Are you using the proper Xpdf binary for your server environment?126There is a permissions problem executing pdftotext from the web server user. Please check with your host to ensure proper permissions.127Your server was not able to execute pdftotext. Please check with your host to ensure the web server user can execute pdftotext.139There is a permissions problem executing pdftotext from the web server user. Please check with your host to ensure proper permissions.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Shortcodes](https://searchwp.com/extensions/shortcodes/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** If youâre not comfortable (or able) to edit template files to implement search forms and results, this extension provides Shortcodes that generate both search forms and results loops for SearchWP search engines

**Content:**

This extension provides Shortcodes that generate both search forms and results pages for SearchWP search engines. Itâs useful if youâre not comfortable (or able) to edit theme files directly to implement your search form(s) and results loops.

After activating Shortcodes, the following will be available to you:

ShortcodeAttribute(s)`searchwp_search_form`Outputs a search form for a specific search engine`target` â The form action (Default is the existing permalink)
`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`button_text` â Form button text (Default is `Search`)
`hide_button` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, hides the search form submit button
`placeholder` â Form input placeholder text (Default is `Search`)
`live_search` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, force enable/disable Live Ajax Search for this form, ignoring the value of the global âEnable Live Searchâ setting`searchwp_search_results`Outputs the list of search results once a search has been submitted`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`posts_per_page` â How many posts per page to display
*Note that this is not a self-closing Shortcode, the markup for each search result should be nested inside, see the sample below for example*`searchwp_search_result_link`Outputs a link to the current search result with the text of the resultâs title`direct` â Either âtrueâ or âfalseâ, when true link directly to the permalink, if itâs an attachment link directly to itâs URL, not the parent page`searchwp_search_result_excerpt`Outputs the excerpt for the current search resultNone`searchwp_search_results_none`
Output if no search results were foundNone *Note that this is not a self-closing Shortcode, the markup for each search result should be nested inside, see sample below for example*

`searchwp_search_results_pagination`Outputs pagination of search results`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`direction` â Either âprevâ or ânextâ
`link_text` â The text to use in the link
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)`searchwp_search_results_paginate_links`Outputs paginated links for search results`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)In addition to the above, arguments from [`paginate_links`](https://developer.wordpress.org/reference/functions/paginate_links/) are also supported.`searchwp_search_result_image`Outputs Featured Image for the current result`size` â Image size (Default is `thumb`)`searchwp_search_result_permalink`Outputs permalink for the current result with the text of the resultâs permalink`direct` â Whether to link directly to Media files (Default is `'true'`)`searchwp_total_results`Outputs a total number of results for the current search`engine` â Search engine name (Default is `default`)
`var` â The search query variable name (Default is `swpquery`)
`text` â The text to use alongside the number of results. Supports `%total%` and `%query%` placeholders for precise formatting.`[searchwp_total_results text="Found %total% results for %query%"]` converts to âFound 0 results for example searchâ.Here is a sample of a comprehensive implementation of Shortcodes that searches a supplemental engine with a name of `my_test_engine` :

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <h3>Search Form</h3>    \[searchwp\_search\_form engine="my\_test\_engine" target="http://example.com/supplemental-search/" var="swpquery" button\_text="Find Results"\]         <div class="search-results-wrapper">     \[searchwp\_total\_results text="Found %total% results for %query%" engine="my\_test\_engine" var="swpquery"\]     \[searchwp\_search\_results engine="my\_test\_engine" var="swpquery" posts\_per\_page=10\]     <h2>\[searchwp\_search\_result\_link direct="true"\]</h2>     \[searchwp\_search\_result\_excerpt\]     \[/searchwp\_search\_results\]    </div>         <div class="no-search-results-found">     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_none\]     No results found, please search again.     \[/searchwp\_search\_results\_none\]    </div>         <div class="search-results-pagination">     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_pagination direction="prev" link\_text="Previous" var="swpquery" engine="my\_test\_engine"\]     \[searchwp\_search\_results\_pagination direction="next" link\_text="Next" var="swpquery" engine="my\_test\_engine"\]    </div>  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/eb1ff51275ae7ec8371b51338cdad87a/raw/1a4d4d5460f599ce77a673ceba67d51712016a50/gistfile1.html) [ gistfile1.html ](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/eb1ff51275ae7ec8371b51338cdad87a#file-gistfile1-html) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

You can customize the attributes as per the table above.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Source - Gravity Forms](https://searchwp.com/extensions/source-gravity-forms/)

**Published:** July 27, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Quickly make Gravity Forms Entries searchable on your site using SearchWP!

**Content:**

Table of Contents

- [Setup](#setup)
- [Results](#results)

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-card-gravity-forms.png)

[Gravity Forms](https://www.gravityforms.com/) is a premier forms plugin for WordPress. It is immensely popular and even more capable. This Extension allows you to use Gravity Forms form entries as a custom Source for SearchWP, making it possible to search Gravity Forms Entries with SearchWP!

## Adding Gravity Forms Entries to SearchWPâs Index

Once **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** has been installed and activated, your Gravity Forms will appear in the Sources list for your SearchWP Engine.

**Note:** This custom Source can be used only with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Letâs say you were building a feature request area on your website and using Gravity Forms to collect entries.

 [![Screenshot of Gravity Forms setup](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-form-form.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-form-form.png) 

You could utilize SearchWP to allow customers to first search existing entries before submitting their own, so they could join in up-voting the existing submission instead of creating a duplicate.

The first step would be to create a new Supplemental Engine that has our Feature Request Form as the Source:

 [![Adding Gravity Form to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source.png) 

With the form chosen (note that you can combine entries from multiple Gravity Forms into a single Engine if youâd like!) you can pick and choose which field(s) youâd like to consider for searching:

 [![Adding Gravity Forms attributes to SearchWP](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source-attributes.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-gravity-source-attributes.gif) 

SearchWP allows you to search the entire Gravity Forms Entry as a whole, optionally consider any Notes that have been added, or choose exactly which field(s) should be considered when searching, each with its own relevancy weight!

## Displaying Gravity Forms Entries as Results

The **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** Extension will make available Gravity Forms Entries as search results. This integrates as any Source would when working with a [Supplemental Engine](https://searchwp.com/documentation/search/supplemental-engine/).

Unlike a native WordPress search that has [The Loop](https://codex.wordpress.org/The_Loop) which is an array of `WP_Post` objects that WordPressâ API interacts with, the results set for a Supplemental Engine can be made up of any SearchWP Source object.

Gravity Forms does not have a proper object for Form Entries, each Entry is an array of key value pairs. As a convenience, **SearchWP Source â Gravity Forms** will return results in a wrapper object:

`\SearchWP\Sources\GravityForms\Entry`

This will help as you iterate over your results array:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Modal Search Form](https://searchwp.com/extensions/modal-form/)

**Published:** June 26, 2019
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Use Modal Form to quickly and easily add a lightweight, accessible modal search form (that matches your active theme) to your site!

**Content:**

Modal Search Form **does not require SearchWP** but will use it if available

Use SearchWP Modal Search Form to easily integrate an accessible, lightweight modal search form into your WordPress website! Designed to integrate any theme and any SearchWP configuration, Modal Form is the easiest way to add a great looking search form to your site.

Modal Search Form **does not require SearchWP**, so it is [available on wordpress.org](http://wordpress.org/plugins/searchwp-modal-search-form/) and will work with any WordPress site. If you have SearchWP installed and activated, Modal Form will automatically utilize it. If you donât, native WordPress search results will be provided.

The plugin repo is available as well  if you have any ideas for templates please open a PR ?

[![Screenshots of Modal Form in various themes](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-modal-form-examples.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-modal-form-examples.gif)SearchWP Modal Form adapts to your active theme

## What makes it different?

The philosophy behind SearchWP Modal Search Form is to make it easy to implement accessible and lightweight modal search forms but perhaps even more important make it a great developer (and user) experience.

SearchWP Modal Search Form directly integrates with WordPress in the way you use it, and the default modal form theme builds upon the styles put in place by your active theme, making the overhead as small as possible. Thereâs a full template loader built in as well, allowing you to *fully customize* your SearchWP Modal Search Form with minimal hassle.

Check out the [default template](https://github.com/jchristopher/searchwp-modal-form/blob/master/templates/default.php) for an example of how easy it is to customize ?

## Usage

SearchWP Modal Search Form makes it easy to implement modal search forms anywhere in your WordPress website. To implement any modal you need both (1) a trigger and (2) the modal itself.

With this plugin, implementing both pieces is extremely easy! The following methods are built in and available to you:

1. As a [Menu Item](#menu-item)
2. Using a [Shortcode](#shortcode)
3. As a [Gutenberg Block](#block)
4. Within your template(s) [programmatically (code)](#code)

You can add *any number of search form modals* to your site, each using its own template and configuration options (e.g. SearchWP engine) ?

### Menu Item search form modal

Adding a search form to your main site navigation is a great idea, and SearchWP Modal Search Form makes it easy to add in just a moment.

The plugin adds a new group available to you when editing your existing Menus:

[![Screenshot of SearchWP Modal Form's Menu customization options](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/screenshot-4.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/screenshot-4.png)

Once added, you can treat SearchWP Modal Search Form Menu Items as you would any other Menu Item, including additional customizations you may wish to put in place.

### Shortcode search form modal

If youâd prefer, you can use a [Shortcode](https://codex.wordpress.org/Shortcode_API) to output a modal form trigger which when clicked will display your search form modal.

`[searchwp_modal_search_form]`

You can apply additional customizations that control various attributes of the trigger and the modal itself, for example:

`[searchwp_modal_search_form engine="my_searchwp_engine" template="My Custom Template" text="Open Search" type="button"]`

Which renders like so:

  Open Search

The available Shortcode attributes are as follows:

**`engine`**The `name` of the SearchWP engine to use (applies only if SearchWP is active)**`template`**The modal template name, please see [the template documentation](#templates)**`text`**Controls the text of the trigger**`type`**Defines the trigger type, which can be either a link `` or a ``Using these attributes you can fully customize both the trigger and modal template in use.

### Gutenberg block search form modal

SearchWP Modal Search Form supplies you with the option to insert a modal search form by using a Gutenberg block if youâd like:

[![Screenshot of adding a modal search form as a Gutenberg block](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/screenshot-5-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/screenshot-5-1.png)Insert and customize modal search forms in the block editor

Using the Block Properties panel you can customize various attributes of the block, including:

**Text**Controls the text of the trigger**Engine**The `name` of the SearchWP engine to use (applies only if SearchWP is active)**Template**The modal template name, please see [the template documentation](#templates)**Type**Defines the trigger type, which can be either a link `` or a ``Changing any of the Block Properties will allow you to see what the trigger will look like in real time.

### Programmatic search form modal

You have the option of directly inserting a SearchWP Modal Form into your theme templates anywhere youâd like, using the following syntax:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        searchwp\_modal\_form\_trigger();  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/efe2e133b5a1183b688b8d249c7e401f/raw/17b49b00b4098958335afc64073e462ff671dc33/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/efe2e133b5a1183b688b8d249c7e401f#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

There are a number of arguments that can be passed as an array when calling `searchwp_modal_form_trigger()`:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        searchwp\_modal\_form\_trigger( array(     'echo' => true, // Whether to echo     'type' => 'button', // Either 'link' or 'button'     'text' => 'Open Search', // Text of the trigger     'engine' => 'my\_searchwp\_engine', // SearchWP engine (when applicable)     'template' => 'My Custom Template', // Modal template to use (see docs)     'class' => array(),    ) );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/1652ebdf43ea9bd275e22888e0cdb6e5/raw/4bc5f373470feb4e877ee86ab7a1948f11108bb1/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/1652ebdf43ea9bd275e22888e0cdb6e5#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

You can implement any number of SearchWP Modal Search Forms throughout your site, each with its own configuration arguments (or sharing the same!)

## Customizing and creating modal templates

SearchWP Modal Search Form ships with a template loader, allowing you to easily customize (and create) the available templates. SearchWP Modal Form Templates encompass the content and style of the modal itself, not the trigger.

SearchWP Modal Search Form triggers inherit styles from your active theme, and can be customized by adding any custom CSS youâd like.

? **Important** ? When customizing or creating SearchWP Modal Form templates, you should create a folder within your (Child) theme titled `searchwp-modal-form` which will contain all of your custom templates that will ***not*** be overwritten when the plugin is updated.

SearchWP Modal Search Form templates are file based, and collected by checking the following directories:

- `~/wp-content/plugins/searchwp-modal-form/templates/*.php` (**do not** edit these, they will be overwritten in subsequent plugin updates!)
- `~/wp-content/themes/your-child-theme/searchwp-modal-form/*.php` (if applicable)
- `~/wp-content/themes/your-theme/searchwp-modal-form/*.php`

In order for a SearchWP Modal Form Template to be included, ***it must have the following header comment block***:

```
/**
 * SearchWP Modal Form Name: My Custom Template
 */
```

This defines the modal template name, which is used when defining the template to use when inserting a modal form trigger. It is suggested that any custom templates you create *have a unique name*, so as to be easily identifiable.

You should use [the default template](https://gist.github.com/searchwpgists/fe187c7fec79ca065a06cd7d6f105409) as a starting point when customizing or creating modal templates. It is fully documented for your reference.

## Hooks

The following hooks have been made available for you to customize the behavior of SearchWP Modal Form:

**`searchwp_modal_form_template_dir`**
Customize the folder name used to store your custom templates (default is `searchwp-modal-form`)

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_modal\_form\_template\_dir', function( $dir ) {     return 'my-searchwp-modal-forms';    } );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/6b9de04f4cfcaf4758ebba9467f98c1f/raw/7d9f16c78afbd81cda302e3557d7e4a82772ab0a/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/6b9de04f4cfcaf4758ebba9467f98c1f#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

*Need a new hook?* [Create an Issue](https://github.com/jchristopher/searchwp-modal-form/issues) so we can all work on it ?

## Developer notes

There is a build process for all JavaScript bundles contained within a single command:

`npm run watch`

This will execute four concurrent processes that watch for file changes and subsequently generate the following:

1. Production version of the SearchWP Modal Search Form bundle
2. Development version of the SearchWP Modal Search Form bundle
3. Production version of the SearchWP Modal Search Form block
4. Development version of the SearchWP Modal Search Form block

You can run each process individually if youâd prefer:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       \# Build development version of SearchWP Modal Search Form bundle    npm run dev        \# Build production version of SearchWP Modal Search Form bundle    npm run build        \# Build development version of SearchWP Modal Search Form block    npm run blockdev        \# Build production version of SearchWP Modal Search Form block    npm run blockbuild

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [bbPress Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/bbpress-integration/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** bbPress normally prevents searching by excluding itâs own post type. The bbPress Integration Extension allows SearchWP to index forum content and include it in search results. Best of all you can have Reply weight count toward parent Topics!

**Content:**

By default, bbPress registers itâs post types to be excluded from search. Use the bbPress Integration Extension to allow SearchWP to **include bbPress content in search results**.

Whatâs most useful about this Extension is the ability to have search weight normally found in Replies actually count toward the Topic by enabling post parent attribution when configuring Replies:

[![SearchWP bbPress Integration](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-bbpress.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-bbpress.png)

When enabled, search results will **point to the parent Topic instead of the single Reply**, which is awesome.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Diagnostics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/diagnostics/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** With this Extension you can easily retrieve various data and information from under the hood of the SearchWP index. New features being added regularly.

**Content:**

Diagnostics helps you to verify which content was indexed for any given entry and check if any entry is unindexed.

After activating Diagnostics, you can access its page from the SearchWP > Tools menu.

From there you can use all of the features integrated in Diagnostics.

[![SearchWP Diagnostics page](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-diagnostics.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-diagnostics.png)

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Redirects](https://searchwp.com/extensions/redirects/)

**Published:** May 16, 2017
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Automatically redirect to a specific page when certain searches are performed!

**Content:**

**Automatically redirect to a specific page when certain searches are performed!** While SearchWP will find the most relevant search results for you, there are times where you simply want to help your visitor move directly to a page of your choosing, saving them a click in the process.

## Setup

SearchWP Redirects is a standalone WordPress plugin that is installed and activated alongside SearchWP. Once activated, youâll see a new entry on the SearchWP > Settings.

Clicking through to the Redirects settings screen, youâre given full control over how Redirects should automatically guide your visitors depending on search query.

## Configuration

SearchWP Redirects allows you to define:

- The query string
- Whether partial matches should be accepted
- Applicable engine(s) for the redirect
- Destination URL

This allows you to define a comprehensive set of automatic redirections in response to various searches that can improve your visitor experience:

[![SearchWP Redirects settings screen](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-redirect.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-redirect.png)

You can add as many Redirects as youâd like, theyâre put in place as soon as you save the settings!

### Troubleshooting

If after activating Redirects you see an error message that looks like the following:

`Warning: Cannot modify header information - headers already sent by (output started at /var/www/wp-content/plugins/searchwp-redirects/includes/SearchWP_Redirects.php:56) in /var/www/wp-includes/pluggable.php on line 1195`

That means that either your theme or an active plugin has sent output to the browser before Redirects could handle the request. If you are using a supplemental engine please try editing your supplemental engine template so that your call to `get_header()` occurs ***after*** your SearchWP logic. If that doesnât work, please open a support ticket.

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Metrics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/)

**Published:** July 19, 2018
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Metrics collects comprehensive data for on-site search, and provides you with actionable advice about refinements you can make to your content

**Content:**

An effective on-site search implementation provides unique insight into your visitors. By observing what visitors are searching for you can make educated decisions about areas of improvement when it comes to your content.

SearchWPâs Metrics extension collects comprehensive data for on-site search, and provides you with actionable advice about refinements you can make to your content as time goes on.

[![Screenshot of SearchWP Metrics main interface](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-browser-trim.png)SearchWP Metrics main interface

[Watch Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WvMr0u8qzys)

### Documentation â Table of Contents

- [Installation & Setup](#installation)
- [Click Tracking](#tracking)
- [Dashboard Widget](#dashboard-widget)
- [Customizing Data](#data)
- [Reviewing Data](#review)
    - [Engine Statistics](#engine-statistics)
    - [Popular Searches](#popular-searches)
    - [Insights](#insights)
- [Exporting Data](#export)
- [Filters](#filters)

## Installation & Setup

Metrics was built to be a turnkey Extension for SearchWP. Metrics is a standalone WordPress plugin that should be installed and activated alongside SearchWP itself. Once installed and activated Metrics will immediately begin tracking searches and clicks. Metrics will begin displaying data as soon as enough has been collected.

Metrics was designed to supersede SearchWPâs core statistics feature. As a result, both the SearchWP Statistics page and Dashboard Widget will no longer display, and counterparts from Metrics will take their place.

### Click Tracking

By default, Metrics will *automatically* track clicks for the Default engine, assuming that your search results template uses The Loop. Any link within The Loop that is generated using `get_permalink()` (or variant thereof) will automatically be set up for click tracking. If youâre using a standard search results template you should be all set without needing to make any edits.

To integrate click tracking in your **supplemental engine(s)** you need to add two Hooks to your results template, one before your own loop of results, and one afterward.

Begin Metrics link tracking:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Initiate Metrics link tracking    do\_action( 'searchwp\_metrics\_click\_tracking\_start' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/6007dc68ca47471936496cb2214959e4/raw/b36d8fa74d184971740a9abcbb489eee214ec977/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/6007dc68ca47471936496cb2214959e4#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

Stop tracking links:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Stop Metrics link tracking    do\_action( 'searchwp\_metrics\_click\_tracking\_stop' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/cc62ae38c0358404ff9c6f6477b0b9f5/raw/f03657b698114910ccf07c28c4f2e904aa4faa1c/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/cc62ae38c0358404ff9c6f6477b0b9f5#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

All links generated with `get_permalink()` (or variant thereof) within those two actions will be processed by the click tracking within Metrics.

### Dashboard Widget

Metrics ships with its own Dashboard Widget that allows you to take a quick look at basic data when you log in to your site.

[![Screenshot of SearchWP Metrics Dashboard Widget](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-dashboard-widget.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-dashboard-widget.png)SearchWP Metrics Dashboard Widget

The Widget updates in real time as you visit your Dashboard, and you can customize the [number of popular searches](#searchwp_metrics_dashboard_widget_max_popular_searches) and [number of popular posts](#searchwp_metrics_dashboard_widget_max_popular_posts) shown.

### Customizing Data

Metrics allows you to easily customize the data being displayed. You can control the date range, the search queries, and the engines to display:

[![Screenshot of the controls for Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-controls-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-controls-1.png)Customize what Metrics displays

**Date Range:** Customize the date range shown in the main chart and engine details.

**Search Query Controls:** Limit the data displayed to a set of search queries. You can also control what queries have been ignored.

**Engines to Display:** Control which engine(s) are displayed in the charts

### Reviewing Data

The line chart at the top of the page shows the searches over the chosen time frame for each selected engine. Below are details for each engine that provide specific metrics for further examination:

[![Screenshot of engine details from Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-engine-details-1.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-engine-details-1.png)Details for one of the chosen engines

A details area is shown for each selected engine.

##### Engine Statistics

On the left are **Engine Statistics**:

Total SearchesThe total number of searches for the chosen time frameNo Results SearchesThe total number of searches that generated zero results (click the icon for more details)Total Results ViewedThe total number of conversions (clicks) for the chosen time frameSearches Per UserThe average number of searches per user (based on only users who have searched)Clicks Per SearchThe average number of clicks per searchAverage Click RankThe average rank of a clicked search resultWhen clicking the icon next to the **No Results Searches** youâre able to review a list of all of the search queries that yielded zero results, and how many searches there were. This list can be reviewed for accuracy as time goes on.

[![Screenshot of failed searches within Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-failed-searches.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-failed-searches.png)A list of no results searches within Metrics

Clicking the `Ã` icon next to a failed search query will add it to the list of ignored searches.

##### Popular Searches

The **Popular Searches** area lists the most popular searches on your site. Clicking either a listed popular search or the View More button brings up even more detail to check out:

[![Screenshot of Popular Searches details in Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-popular-searches.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-popular-searches.png)Details view for Popular Searches in Metrics

For each listed popular search you can expand additional details including which entries were clicked as a result of that search query and how many times. Using the controls at the top you can control how many popular searches are returned, and also export the data to CSV.

##### Insights

Metrics not only collects this data for you but it also implements some analysis to let you know where your content can potentially be improved. By analyzing conversion rates, popular searches, frequency of searches, and results rank positions, Metrics can let you know when:

- Content is ranking low but getting a lot of clicks: *can this content be optimized to rank higher?*
- Content is getting a high conversion rate: *should this content be more prominently linked because so many visitors are searching for it?*
- Searches are converting on a variety of posts: *should new content be created for these search queries?*

Insights can call out some areas of improvement based on actual visitor searches:

[![Screenshot of Insights within Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-insights.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-insights.png)Insights give actionable advice based on collected data

### Exporting Data

Within each engine details pane you can export any of the data being displayed:

[![Screenshot of the various export options in Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-export.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-export.png)The export features in Metrics

Exports are formatted as CSV files, which can then be used in any way youâd like.

### Settings

Metrics integrates some shortcuts and settings that can help you customize things a bit.

[![Screenshot of the settings in Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-settings.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-settings.png)Settings menu in Metrics

You can use **Clear Metrics Data** to remove the data logged by Metrics, and **Remove All Ignored Queries** will reset your ignored queries leaving nothing ignored. These operations *cannot be undone* so make sure you have a backup if necessary!

##### Modify Logging Rules

Metrics comes with some basic controls to make excluding certain searches a bit easier:

[![Screenshot of logging rules in Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-logging-rules.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-logging-rules.png)Easily blacklist User IDs/Roles or IPs to prevent searches from being logged

Here you can add any combination of WordPress User IDs or Roles that should have their searches ignored. You can also maintain a list of IPs to blacklist as well. This makes excluding internal searches (so as to avoid polluting data) a bit easier.

##### General Settings

There are a couple of general items to consider when using Metrics:

[![Screenshot of general settings for Metrics](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-general-settings.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-metrics-general-settings.png)General settings for Metrics

Metrics is able to use its tracking data to provide a buoy of sorts for search results. If youâd like results with more clicks to rank higher over time than those with fewer clicks, you can tick the checkbox to **Influence search results using click data**. This feature requires SearchWP version 2.9.14 or higher.

Metrics can also clean up after itself. With the **Remove all Metrics data on uninstallation** checkbox ticked, Metrics will remove its custom database tables, all click tracking data, and any stored options upon deletion.

### Filters

There are a couple of filters to note when using Metrics:

**`searchwp_metrics_dashboard_widget_max_popular_posts`**
Set the number of popular posts to show in the Dashboard Widget. Default is **5**, to customize:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. ---

### [Exclude UI](https://searchwp.com/extensions/exclude-ui/)

**Published:** April 28, 2015
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Add a checkbox to every Publish meta box allowing you to easily exclude an entry from search

**Content:**

SearchWP allows you to exclude content from search in a number of ways on the main SearchWP settings screen. Exclude UI adds this functionality to edit screens so you can easily exclude specific entries from search with the tick of a checkbox.

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-exclude-ui-gutenberg-3.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-exclude-ui-gutenberg-3.png)

**Note:** When working in the block editor with a post type that *does not* support `custom-fields` the Exclude from Search checkbox will display in a meta box

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [WooCommerce Product Table Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/woocommerce-product-table-integration/)

**Published:** January 13, 2021
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

[![Barn2 Plugins logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/extension-banner-woocommerce-product-tables.png)](https://barn2.co.uk/wordpress-plugins/woocommerce-product-table/)

The SearchWP WooCommerce Product Table Integration provides integration between SearchWP and [WooCommerce Product Table](https://barn2.co.uk/wordpress-plugins/woocommerce-product-table/), the easy way to speed up shopping by listing your products in a quick order form. It upgrades the basic search box which comes with WooCommerce Product Table so that your customers can benefit from SearchWPâs advanced search features.

Simply install the integration extension along with SearchWP and WooCommerce Product Table, and enable the [lazy load](https://barn2.co.uk/kb/lazy-load/) option in WooCommerce Product Table. The search box above your product tables will be powered by SearchWP.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [BetterDocs Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/betterdocs-integration/)

**Published:** August 25, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Use SearchWP to power BetterDocs' live search functionality!

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/betterdocs-card.png)

[BetterDocs](https://wordpress.org/plugins/betterdocs/) calls itself the âBest Documentation & Knowledge Base Pluginâ. Use this Extension to use SearchWP to power BetterDocâs live search implementation.

By default your **Default** Engine will be used for results, but you can customize the Engine used for BetterDocs searches with this hook:

 All hooks should be added to *your custom* [SearchWP Customizations Plugin](/documentation/knowledge-base/creating-searchwp-customizations-plugin/).

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Use a supplemental SearchWP Engine for BetterDocs searches.    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_betterdocs\_engine', function( $engine, $params ) {     return 'my\_engine\_name';    }, 20, 2 );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/fdb15189c7cb88be9520fb3fe4307b43/raw/b561b27266ef9d25228ee717e6920d73d384640a/searchwp-customizations.php) [ searchwp-customizations.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/fdb15189c7cb88be9520fb3fe4307b43#file-searchwp-customizations-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com)

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Flatsome Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/flatsome-integration/)

**Published:** July 10, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-card-flatsome-integration.png)

[Flatsome](https://flatsome3.uxthemes.com/) is a very popular WooCommerce & Business theme that has a great looking live search feature. Using the Flatsome Integration Extension for SearchWP you can directly utilize SearchWP for searches performed using that feature of Flatsome.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Offsite PDF Parsing](https://searchwp.com/extensions/offsite-pdf-parsing/)

**Published:** June 12, 2019
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

**Currently in beta!**

Offsite PDF parsing will utilize a service made available for active SearchWP license holders designed to remotely parse the contents of your PDFs.

By using this Extension you acknowledge that your PDFs will be downloaded by the service (hosted on a server in the USA), parsed for text content, and then immediately deleted once the request is complete.

*Additional documentation coming soon.*

---

### [MyListing Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/mylisting-integration/)

**Published:** April 21, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate MyListing and SearchWP to improve keyword search!

**Content:**

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/banner-mylisting.png)](https://mylistingtheme.com/)

[MyListing](https://mylistingtheme.com/) is a very popular theme designed to facilitate a custom directory website. If youâre using MyListing and SearchWP this Integration Extension will greatly improve the keyword search in MyListing!

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/listing-type-1030x603.jpg)](https://mylistingtheme.com/)

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Legacy Data Removal](https://searchwp.com/extensions/legacy-data-removal/)

**Published:** March 12, 2020
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

Use this Extension to remove legacy SearchWP data after upgrading to a new major version.

This utility will ***permanently remove*** the legacy data of your choice.

Once installed and activated, there will be an entry for **SearchWP Legacy Data Removal** in the **Tools** menu of your WordPress Admin.

![Screenshot of plugin UI](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2020-03-12-at-8.25.25-AM.png)

---

### [German Keyword Stemmer](https://searchwp.com/extensions/german-keyword-stemmer/)

**Published:** June 2, 2015
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Utilize German keyword stemming (as opposed to the default English)

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of SearchWP version 3.1 as it has been bundled into SearchWP itself â ï¸

By default SearchWP applies English stemming when enabled on the main SearchWP settings screen. This extension will instead apply German keyword stemming when that option is enabled.

---

**Published:** April 28, 2015

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of SearchWP version 3.1 as it has been bundled into SearchWP itself â ï¸

By default SearchWP applies English stemming when enabled on the main SearchWP settings screen. This extension will instead apply Dutch keyword stemming when that option is enabled.

---

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of SearchWP version 3.1 as it has been bundled into SearchWP itself â ï¸

### [Meta Box Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/meta-box-integration/)

**Published:** August 28, 2019
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/meta-box-integration-card.png)

With **hundreds of thousands of active installations** [Meta Box](https://metabox.io/) is one of the premier methods of managing Custom Fields on your WordPress website.

Meta Box takes a code-focused approach to custom fields, and developers have latched on because of its unique approach to implementing meta boxes.

## Meta Box and SearchWP

With SearchWPâs Meta Box Integration, youâll be able to easily add your Meta Box fields to your search engines, theyâre organized by the Meta Boxes you set up:

[![Animated GIF adding Meta Box fields to a SearchWP engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-01.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-01.gif)Easily add Meta Box fields to SearchWP engines

Further, Meta Box Integration also makes your [MB Custom Table](https://metabox.io/plugins/mb-custom-table/) content searchable as well!

[![Animated GIF adding MB Custom Table fields to a SearchWP engine](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-02.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-meta-box-integration-02.gif)Make MB Custom Table content searchable

SearchWP groups your MB Custom Table content together and makes it possible to search the data stored in MB Custom Table fields instantly!

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [BigCommerce Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/bigcommerce-integration/)

**Published:** August 28, 2019
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Content:**

![BigCommerce logo](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/bigcommerce-integration-card.png)

[BigCommerce](https://wordpress.org/plugins/bigcommerce/) is a popular e-commerce platform that includes powerful features. SearchWPâs BigCommerce Integration Extension makes your online store even more powerful by directly integrating BigCommerce product attributes into your on-site search.

The primary benefit to using SearchWPâs BigCommerce Integration is the ability to individually integrate BigCommerce Product Custom Fields into your WordPress search, allowing you to customize your search engine and make it a powerful asset to your customers!

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Manage Ignored](https://searchwp.com/extensions/manage-ignored/)

**Published:** May 25, 2017
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Manage ignored searches in SearchWP Statistics

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of [SearchWP version 3.0](https://searchwp.com/news/searchwp-3-0-is-here/) and will be removed soon â ï¸

SearchWP allows you to ignore search queries when running statistics. This tool allows you to un-ignore them!

Once installed, youâll see an Extensions dropdown on the SearchWP settings screen with an entry to Manage Ignored:

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-manage-ignored-settings.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-manage-ignored-settings.png)Use the Extensions dropdown on the SearchWP settings screen to Manage Ignored

You can use that screen to un-ignore ignored search queries.

---

### [Term Synonyms](https://searchwp.com/extensions/term-synonyms/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Manually define synonyms for common search terms that don't yield the results you're looking for.

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of [SearchWP version 3.0](https://searchwp.com/news/searchwp-3-0-is-here/) and will be removed soon â ï¸

SearchWP offers insight into searches taking place on your site via the Search Stats link under the Dashboard menu in the WordPress admin. You might notice that a lot of people are searching for slight variants of keywords or abbreviations that arenât getting the results youâre looking for. The Term Synonyms Extension allows you to manually define synonym relationships in your index.

Whatâs more is you can have the synonyms completely replace the search term. If people are commonly searching for terms that simply arenât (and never will be) in your index, you can replace them before searches are performed, getting the results your visitors are looking for.

After activating Term Synonyms, you can find the management of synonyms by navigating to the SearchWP Settings screen and clicking the link under the Extensions menu:

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/2018-08-14-at-9.39-AM.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/2018-08-14-at-9.39-AM.png)

From there you can manage your Term Synonyms and add an extra level of relevancy to *your* content.

[![](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-term-synonyms.png)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-term-synonyms.png)

---

### [LIKE Terms](https://searchwp.com/extensions/like-terms/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** LIKE Terms only applies partial term matching (compared to Fuzzy Matches which also performs primitive spell checking) which is useful when partial spell checking could wreak havoc on performance. Think partial matching for SKUs, for example.

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of [SearchWP version 3.0](https://searchwp.com/news/searchwp-3-0-is-here/) and will be removed soon â ï¸

**Unlike Fuzzy Matches**, LIKE Terms does not perform primitive spell checking at all. This is useful if youâre looking to allow partial matching of terms, without exposing close spelling variants which can cause performance issues in the wrong circumstances.

For example, you should use LIKE Terms instead of Fuzzy Matches if youâre looking to incorporate SKU searching and want to allow for partial term matches without exposing thousands of similarly âspelledâ SKUs that would be included with Fuzzy Matches.

### Filters

There are a number of filters to help with your LIKE Terms implementation:

**`searchwp_like_min_length`**
Customize the minimum length of a word for LIKE matching to be applied. Default is **4**, to customize:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // Apply LIKE matching for terms 2 or more characters in length.    function searchwp\_like\_min\_length( $length ) {     return 2;    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_like\_min\_length', 'my\_searchwp\_like\_min\_length' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/db2c8a4e105b52716485ed283868bae2/raw/60356dfd3100569af1010f1b79aa7633e349f887/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/db2c8a4e105b52716485ed283868bae2#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_like_stem`**
Customize whether SearchWP applies LIKE matching to the keyword stem regardless of engine setting. Default is **false**, to customize:

To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

### [Fuzzy Matches](https://searchwp.com/extensions/fuzzy-matches/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Fuzzy matching aims to make SearchWP a bit more lenient with search queries. Use this extension to include search terms that are close in spelling to terms within your index.

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of [SearchWP version 3.0](https://searchwp.com/news/searchwp-3-0-is-here/) and will be removed soon â ï¸

Fuzzy matching aims to make SearchWP a bit more lenient with search queries. Use this extension to include search terms that are close in spelling to terms within your index. There are some filters available for this Extension that let you fine-tune how loose the fuzziness is.

**Note:** Please read these notes before installing Fuzzy Matches

Using Fuzzy Matches is recommended only in circumstances that deem it *necessary*. This warning is given because Fuzzy Matches essentially reduces the effectiveness of SearchWPâs algorithm. *Fuzzy Matches broadens search terms which can essentially bring in unwanted results.*

It is recommended that you first monitor your **actual** search queries using SearchWPâs built-in statistics. Using this data you can evaluate what search terms are yielding zero results. The first step here would be to install [Term Synonyms](https://searchwp.com/extensions/term-synonyms/) if these failed searches are due to common misspellings.

If there are too many common misspellings to make managing Term Synonyms feasible, then (and only then) would Fuzzy Matches be a recommended solution.

**`searchwp_fuzzy_min_length`**
Set the minimum word length to include in fuzzy matches (default is 5)

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_fuzzy\_word\_length()    {     return 5;    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_fuzzy\_min\_length', 'my\_fuzzy\_word\_length' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/5300b22da025f1b76ae3/raw/54771c0057855551cdc83c8ca4f14a08e38167c3/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/5300b22da025f1b76ae3#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_fuzzy_threshold`**
Beyond basic fuzzy matching, SearchWP will apply some primitive spell checking to submitted terms. This filter lets you specify what minimum percentage should be used to constitute a valid match. *Must be 0 â 100.* (default is 80)

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function my\_fuzzy\_threshold()    {     return 85;    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_fuzzy\_threshold', 'my\_fuzzy\_threshold' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/b5250ad6298a9a311c2f/raw/9ea7ffe015ee8e35a4d808bf2c1c4ca0cffbc1f9/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/b5250ad6298a9a311c2f#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_fuzzy_digit_threshold`**
Set the maximum percentage of digits in search term to perform spell checking (default is 10)
