### [Term Highlight](https://searchwp.com/extensions/term-highlight/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Automatically highlight search terms in results within The Loop. Programmatically highlight search terms for content outside of The Loop. Generate an excerpt containing at least on search term, automatically!

**Content:**

â ï¸ **Note:** This extension is **DEPRECATED** as of [SearchWP version 3.0](https://searchwp.com/news/searchwp-3-0-is-here/) and will be removed soon â ï¸

Please see this documentation: 

When active, Term Highlight will automatically scrub the Title and Excerpt returned from searches and wrap search terms with a `span`, allowing you to highlight them in such a way that matches your site design well.

Term Highlight also includes a function allowing you to retrieve an excerpt from the `post_content` or from a Custom Field that contains at least one of the search terms. See usage below.

## Usage

Installation for this Extension is the same as all other Extensions. Download the zip file from your Account, upload and install as you would any other WordPress plugin. Term Highlight will show up in your Plugins list in the WordPress administration area, and you can activate it.

Once activated, Term Highlight will automatically highlight terms within the Title and Excerpt for native WordPress searches. Highlights are applied by wrapping terms in ``, which you can target in your CSS.

Term Highlight will also automatically override calls to `the_excerpt()` by replacing that content with an excerpt that contains at least one search term from the main content (e.g. the main content field, ***not*** Custom Fields). It will also highlight terms in this content. If you would like to disable or customize this functionality, please see the filters below.

You can take it one step further by utilizing Term Highlight to generate an excerpt from a number of potential sources (including Custom Fields) by automatically filtering calls to `the_excerpt()` on search results pages like so:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        function searchwp\_term\_highlight\_auto\_excerpt( $excerpt ) {     global $post;         if ( ! is\_search() ) {     return $excerpt;     }         // prevent recursion     remove\_filter( 'get\_the\_excerpt', 'searchwp\_term\_highlight\_auto\_excerpt' );         $global\_excerpt = searchwp\_term\_highlight\_get\_the\_excerpt\_global( $post->ID, null, get\_search\_query() );         add\_filter( 'get\_the\_excerpt', 'searchwp\_term\_highlight\_auto\_excerpt' );         return wp\_kses\_post( $global\_excerpt );    }        add\_filter( 'get\_the\_excerpt', 'searchwp\_term\_highlight\_auto\_excerpt' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/d3f435c0d2759dd7c0ae60197f5ad787/raw/4618379f251202074bd32fa0af2f08bac647ab6b/functions.php) [ functions.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/d3f435c0d2759dd7c0ae60197f5ad787#file-functions-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

If instead you want to manually replace the calls you can use something like this:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        /\* SearchWP Term Highlight offers an alternative to the\_excerpt()     If you're looking to generate any sort of excerpt based on one of the following:     - the actual excerpt     - the generated excerpt from the post content     - the post content itself     - string-based custom field values     this function is for you. It will try to find one of the above (in that order)     and output something with at least one search term in it (if possible)    \*/        // echo the excerpt (designed to be used IN PLACE OF the\_excerpt    if( function\_exists( 'searchwp\_term\_highlight\_the\_excerpt\_global' ) ) {     searchwp\_term\_highlight\_the\_excerpt\_global();    }        // return the excerpt (designed to be used IN PLACE OF get\_the\_excerpt    if( function\_exists( 'searchwp\_term\_highlight\_get\_the\_excerpt\_global' ) ) {     $excerpt = searchwp\_term\_highlight\_get\_the\_excerpt\_global();    }  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9667991/raw/ae60eaa35cbbcc9f88f60957561b930457daf3b0/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9667991#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

### Supplemental Search Engines

**Term Highlight does not automatically highlight terms for Supplemental Search Engine searches!** You will need to customize your template by sending your content through the highlighter before outputting it, like so:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // this snippet is based on SearchWP's sample Supplemental Search Engine results template found here:    // https://searchwp.com/docs/configuration/#supplemental            // \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* NOTE \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*    // this snippet is just a portion of your search results template    // \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* NOTE \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*        // these are the search terms    $query = isset( $\_REQUEST\['swpquery'\] ) ? sanitize\_text\_field( $\_REQUEST\['swpquery'\] ) : '';        $highlighter = false;    if( class\_exists( 'SearchWP\_Term\_Highlight' ) ) {     $highlighter = new SearchWP\_Term\_Highlight();    }        ?>        <?php foreach ( $posts as $post ): setup\_postdata( $post ); ?>     <div class="post">     <h2>     <a href="<?php echo get\_permalink(); ?>">     <?php     // highlight the title     $title = get\_the\_title();     if( $highlighter ) {     $title = $highlighter->apply\_highlight( $title, $query );     }     echo wp\_kses\_post( $title );     ?>     </a>     </h2>     <?php      // output an excerpt     $excerpt = get\_the\_excerpt();     if( $highlighter ) {     $excerpt = $highlighter->apply\_highlight( $excerpt, $query );     }     echo wp\_kses\_post( $excerpt );     ?>     <div class="custom-field-content">     <?php      // output highlighted content from a Custom Field     $custom\_field = get\_post\_meta( get\_the\_ID(), 'my\_custom\_field\_key', true );     if( $highlighter ) {     $custom\_field = $highlighter->apply\_highlight( $custom\_field, $query );     }     echo wp\_kses\_post( $custom\_field );     ?>     </div>     </div>    <?php endforeach; ?>  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/8356774/raw/824cd9c5984e06dc57bdde7f6cab2a7c63c9d43f/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/8356774#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**NOTE:** the snippet above is meant to represent the customization you would need to do to the sample Supplemental Search Engine results template *replacing the search results output ONLY*, it is not an entire page template.

### Filters

There are a couple of filters to note when using Term Highlight:

**`searchwp_th_auto_filter_excerpt`**
Set whether Term Highlight *automatically* replaces `the_excerpt()` with an excerpt containing search terms. Default is **true**, to disable:

To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

Define the number of words Term Highlight will include when building an excerpt that includes at least one search term. Default is **55**.

Tell Term Highlight to *skip term highlighting* for certain search engines. Default is **none**.

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. ### [Custom Results Order](https://searchwp.com/extensions/custom-results-order/)

**Published:** May 3, 2019
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Customize the order of search results for particular queries, ensuring that certain entries appear first!

**Content:**

Using the Custom Results Order extension you can apply your own, hand curated influence on search results sets.

SearchWPâs algorithm does a great job returning results based on relevance, but there are times when you may want certain entries to appear at the top of search results for specific search queries. Custom Results Order can do just that!

[![SearchWP Custom Results Order in action!](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-cro-1.1.gif)](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/searchwp-cro-1.1.gif)

Youâre able to add a search query and engine âtriggerâ which will then display the results for that search. Using the available controls you can promote an entry to the top spot. With that promotion in place that result will *always* be the top result, regardless of calculated weight.

You can promote multiple results for each search query if youâd like, and you can also re-promote an entry if things get out of order.

Thereâs no limit to the number of triggers you can add when using Custom Results Order, if there are cases where you want to be sure that a certain result is the top result, this extension will get the job done!

### A note about partial matches

There is support for partial matches in Custom Results Order, but it is not recommended. When partial matching is enabled, Custom Results Order will apply custom sorting if the search query trigger appears *anywhere* in the search query itself. This can have influence on search queries you would rather not have influenced, and there is no way for Custom Results Order to take that into consideration. Please disable exact matching with caution ?

## Why is it necessary?

*If SearchWP was originally built to return the most relevant results, why is this Extension necessary at all?*

SearchWP **does** return the most relevant results it can find, but those results are directly tied to SearchWPâs algorithm used to find those results in the first place. As with all algorithms, there are edge cases that go against the norm, and finding search results is no different.

You are the most knowledgeable of your site content, and you know what your visitors are looking for. Using SearchWPâs [Metrics](https://searchwp.com/extensions/metrics/) you can gain insight not only into what your visitors are searching for, but **also which results theyâre clicking**.

There will be times that visitors are clicking results that donât rank highest in the results set. You *could* manually edit the engine configuration to compensate, or stuff some keywords in your content, or even create a âhiddenâ Custom Field to stuff keywords to get the overall rank higher.

Those solutions can work, but Custom Results Order removes the burden of having to set up and manage some sort of undocumented system. It provides you with a single screen containing all of these customizations you have in place.

### Make your search even better

I hope Custom Results Order helps you improve your on-site search to an even higher degree. Itâs already in use on this site, making sure that customers can find the documentation theyâre looking for!

You can take advantage of Custom Results Order on the front end of your site too by [styling promoted entries](https://www.billerickson.net/manually-promote-featured-content-in-search-results/)!

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Polylang Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/polylang-integration/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Polylang is an awesome WordPress plugin that allows your content to go multilingual. The Polylang Integration Extension shows SearchWP how to limit search results to the active language at the time of searching.

**Content:**

[Polylang](http://wordpress.org/plugins/polylang/) is a fantastic plugin that adds multilingual support to WordPress:

> Polylang allows you to create a bilingual or multilingual WordPress site. You write posts, pages and create categories and post tags as usual, and then define the language for each of them. The translation is optional. The plugin does not integrate automatic or professional translation.

After installing and activating this Extension, SearchWP will limit search results to the active language of your Polylang-powered content segmentation.

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Give Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/give-integration/)

**Published:** December 26, 2018
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate SearchWP and Give allowing you to effectively search your forms by title or metadata!

**Content:**

[Give](https://givewp.com/) is the leader when it comes to facilitating donation collection and management within WordPress. The Give Integration extension for SearchWP better integrates the two systems, allowing you to effectively search all of your Give forms by title or content. Searching entries is not currently supported but if you are in need of that please submit a request, thank you!

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [Enable Media Replace Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/enable-media-replace-integration/)

**Published:** May 25, 2017
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate SearchWP with Enable Media Replace to automatically update file content when changes are made

**Content:**

[Enable Media Replace](https://wordpress.org/plugins/enable-media-replace/) is a popular plugin that allows you to replace existing files within your WordPress Media library. This process is really handy because it helps you to avoid having to update existing links used throughout your site that reference Media library items.

The Enable Media Replace Integration Extension for SearchWP augments this process by telling SearchWP to update file content when Enable Media Replace is used to update a file within your Media library.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [HeroThemes Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/herothemes-integration/)

**Published:** June 29, 2016
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate SearchWP results in HeroThemes products

**Content:**

[![Screen Shot 2016-06-29 at 2.16.14 PM](https://searchwp.com/wp-content/uploads/Screen-Shot-2016-06-29-at-2.16.14-PM-300x103.png)](https://herothemes.com/ref/39/)

[HeroThemes](https://herothemes.com/ref/39/) makes fantastic products to help set up an awesomely usable knowledge base full of content. If youâd like to integrate SearchWPâs results, all you need to do is activate and install this extension.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

### [Co-Authors Plus Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/co-authors-plus-integration/)

**Published:** April 7, 2016
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate with Automattic's Co-Authors Plus plugin and include Author details in searches

**Content:**

[Co-Authors Plus](https://wordpress.org/plugins/co-authors-plus/) is a plugin built and maintained by Automattic that allows you to define multiple Authors for your content. This extension will index and make searchable all multiple Authors.

Once the extension has been activated, you **must** rebuild your index on the Advanced tab of the SearchWP settings screen, and then trigger a reindex.

While the index is rebuilding, youâll notice a number of new Custom Fields in the SearchWP settings dropdown for each post type, they all start with `coauthorplus_`

By default the extension will index:

- `user_nicename`
- `display_name`
- `nickname`
- `first_name`
- `last_name`
- `biography`

and there are separate entries for each of those, but the extension also bundles all of those fields into a `coauthorsplus_any` Custom Field if you want to match any of those.

Once the index has rebuilt you will be able to search by Author data as defined by Co-Authors Plus. Searching for configured Author data will return the post(s) written by that author, not the Author archive or a User profile.

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [WP Document Revisions Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/wp-document-revisions-integration/)

**Published:** June 4, 2015
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** Integrate SearchWP with WP Document Revisions

**Content:**

Integrate SearchWP with [WP Document Revisions](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-document-revisions/), a popular plugin used by many to help keep track of documents within their WordPress install. Using this extension SearchWP will properly parse and index the content from the latest revision of a Document so as to make it searchable.

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [PrivateContent Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/privatecontent-integration/)

**Published:** December 1, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** PrivateContent is a popular plugin that allows you to restrict your WordPress content based on user groups you have defined. This integration ensures that results are limited to the appropriate group(s).

**Content:**

[PrivateContent](http://codecanyon.net/item/privatecontent-multilevel-content-plugin/1467885) is a plugin that allows you to restrict content based on user groups. PrivateContent Integration allows for the indexing of this content and also ensures that search results are limited to the appropriate user group(s).

**Extension Types:** Pro

---

### [WP Job Manager Integration](https://searchwp.com/extensions/wp-job-manager-integration/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** WP Job Manager is a great plugin that allows you to implement a job board on your site. It comes with a feature-rich search but if youâd like to further enhance the keyword implementation you can integrate SearchWP with it directly.

**Content:**

[WP Job Manager](https://wpjobmanager.com/) is a popular, well written job board plugin with a thriving add-on library. WP Job Manager offers various search implementations for itself and add-ons. If you would like to enhance the keyword (*only*) aspect of those search fields, WP Job Manager Integration will do the trick.

Just by activating WP Job Manager Integration youâll automatically enable SearchWP to handle the keyword search field for both WP Job Manager core and the Resume add-on. All of the other filters will remain functional an untouched, this extension merely restricts the keyword-provided results.

### Filters

There are a number of filters to help with your WP Job Manager Integration implementation:

**`searchwp_wpjmi_hijack_job_listing_search`**
Whether the extension will integrate with Job Listing searches. Default is **true**, to disable:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // disable SearchWP integration with WP Job Manager Job Listing searches    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_hijack\_job\_listing\_search', '\_\_return\_false' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793075/raw/23e7f24095eee2e5c93be4e4cccd41c9101d0fbf/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793075#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_job_engine`**
Which search engine configuration to use for Job Listing searches. Default is `default`, but you can easily use a Supplemental Search Engine:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // use my\_search\_engine instead of the default search engine when performing    // Job Listing searches in WP Job Manager    function my\_searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine() {     return 'my\_search\_engine';    }        add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine', 'my\_searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_engine' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793116/raw/27443be11e635f6d996f67c9442b6d1739b9cd0e/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793116#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_job_post_type_override`**
WP Job Manager appropriately limits Job Listing search results to the expected `post_type` but if you want to override that and force the search engine configuration to take precedence, you can do that:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

       <?php        // force SearchWP configuration to override WP Job Manager's when performing a Job Listing search    add\_filter( 'searchwp\_wpjmi\_job\_post\_type\_override', '\_\_return\_true' );  

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793149/raw/f0d9663762211ac8ac92fd509f401d19e3efaad7/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793149#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_hijack_resume_search`**
Whether the extension will integrate with Resume searches. Default is **true**, to disable:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

 [view raw](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793172/raw/d3a947c86b7b7d7ccd8195c1b8ee0e5543caed6a/gistfile1.php) [ gistfile1.php ](https://gist.github.com/jchristopher/9793172#file-gistfile1-php) hosted with â¤ by [GitHub](https://github.com) 

**`searchwp_wpjmi_resume_engine`**
Which search engine configuration to use for Resume searches. Default is `default`, but you can easily use a Supplemental Search Engine:

     This file contains hidden or bidirectional Unicode text that may be interpreted or compiled differently than what appears below. To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. [Learn more about bidirectional Unicode characters](https://github.co/hiddenchars)  [ Show hidden characters ](%7B%7B%20revealButtonHref%20%7D%7D)

WP Job Manager appropriately limits Resume search results to the expected `post_type` but if you want to override that and force the search engine configuration to take precedence, you can do that:

To review, open the file in an editor that reveals hidden Unicode characters. As of version `1.5.12` WP Job Manager Integration will bubble Featured listings to the top of results. This behavior can be prevented by adding the following to your themeâs `functions.php`:

### [Boolean Search](https://searchwp.com/extensions/boolean-search/)

**Published:** May 8, 2014
**Author:** Jonathan Christopher (main)

**Excerpt:** By default SearchWP pulls results using all keywords in a search and weights them accordingly, highest weight wins. This extension allows users to exclude keywords from search results, e.g. âcats -dogsâ would return search results including âcatsâ but not âdogsâ.

**Content:**

Boolean Search requires no configuration. It adds support for excluding results based on keyword. For instance, if the search query is âcats -dogs -birdsâ only entries with âcatsâ and without both âdogsâ and âbirdsâ will be included.

**Extension Types:** Standard

---

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

## Easily Create a Custom WordPress Document Search

SearchWPâs built-in integration with WP Document Revisions makes it easy to add a custom WordPress document search to your site. No tech skills, no code required.

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

SearchWPâs built-in integration with Enable Media Replace ensures your WordPress search auto-syncs with updated media files. No tech skills or coding required.

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin.    Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

## How do I enable infinite scroll in WordPress?

Itâs easy to add infinite scroll to your WordPress search with SearchWP and AJAX Load More. Simply add a filter to your functions.php file to connect the two plugins. Once done, your search results will display seamlessly as visitors scroll â no loading or clicking through other pages is needed.

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

## Live Instant Autofill Search For WordPress Made Easy

SearchWPâs Ajax live search extension makes adding autocomplete search to your WordPress site easy and code-free. Trusted by 50,000+ websites.

##  Frequently Asked Questions 

 Do you have a question about SearchWP? See the list below for our most frequently asked questions. If your question is not listed here, then please contact us.

   Can I Upgrade my License? Yes, you may upgrade your license at any time. Simply click the link in your Account area to Upgrade and pay the difference in cost. This **does not constitute a renewal**, so your license will expire on the same date.

   What are the Requirements for SearchWP? SearchWP is a WordPress plugin. You need to be running a version of WordPress that is within one minor release of the current release (e.g. if 5.4 is available, 5.3+ is supported). SearchWP inherits the same [requirements of WordPress](https://wordpress.org/about/requirements/).

   Does SearchWP work on WordPress.com? No it does not. SearchWP is a plugin for self-hosted WordPress(.org) installs.

   Are Search Results Highlighted? Yes, SearchWP can highlight search terms in results and generate excerpts surrounding highlights automatically. [More info](https://searchwp.com/documentation/setup/advanced/)

   Does SearchWP Index Author Details? Not by default, no. You can however teach SearchWP to [index any content you would like](https://searchwp.com/documentation/knowledge-base/adding-extra-data-to-indexed-entries/) (including Author information)

## Customize WordPress Search Rankings To Promote Your Best Content

Help visitors find high-value content faster by selecting the exact order and ranking of your WordPress search results. SearchWP makes it effortless and completely code-free.

- Left sport bottle pocket.
- Survival gear sold separately.

**Demo Item Category:** Bags

---

### [Overnight Duffle](https://searchwp.com/demo-items/overnight-duffle/)

**Published:** November 13, 2025
**Author:** Demo Item

**Excerpt:** This is a simple product called Overnight Duffle

**Content:**

For long weekends away, camping outings and business trips, the Overnight Duffle canât be beat. The dual handles make it a cinch to carry, while the durable waterproof exterior helps you worry less about the weather. With multiple organizational pockets inside and out, itâs the perfect travel companion.

- Dual top handles.
- W 15â³ x H 15â³ x D 9â³.

**Demo Item Category:** Bags, New Luma Yoga Collection

---

### [Push It Messenger Bag](https://searchwp.com/demo-items/push-it-messenger-bag/)

**Published:** November 13, 2025
**Author:** Demo Item

**Excerpt:** This is a simple product called Push It Messenger Bag

**Content:**

The name says so, but the Push It Messenger Bag is much more than a busy commuterâs tote. Itâs a closet away from home when youâre pedaling from class or work to gym and back or home again. Itâs the perfect size and shape for laptop, folded clothes, even extra shoes.

- Adjustable crossbody strap.
- Top handle.
- Zippered interior pocket.
- Secure clip closures.
- Durable fabric construction.

**Demo Item Category:** Bags, Performance Fabrics
